english_verse: “A virgin will have a baby boy, and he will be called Immanuel,” which means “God is with us.”
translation_verse: “Alebu ŋon ɔ te doo ta na baale see ya nɛ waa kyaŋ fulu na waa lola bu baale na baa baake e nyi Imanuɛle.” Keŋ nawolo nyi “Wurubuarɛ kyaa de man.”

english_verse: From David to the time of the exile in Babylonia, the ancestors of Jesus were: David, Solomon , Rehoboam, Abijah, Asa, Jehoshaphat, Jehoram; Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, Hezekiah, Manasseh, Amon, Josiah, and Jehoiachin and his brothers.
translation_verse: Gyosia lolana Gyekonia na ɔ naabia debaŋ keŋ bɔɔ kyaŋ Iseraetena yoo man mo gyu Babilɔn tɛɛle man nɛ.

english_verse: From the exile to the birth of Jesus, his ancestors were: Jehoiachin, Shealtiel, Zerubbabel, Abiud, Eliakim, Azor, Zadok, Achim; Eliud, Eleazar, Matthan, Jacob, and Joseph, the husband of Mary, the mother of Jesus, who is called the Messiah.
translation_verse: Gyekɔpo mɔ lolana Gyosɛfo. Te Gyosɛfo yala Mɛɛre ŋon ɔ ba lola Yesu ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Kristo nɛ. Ŋon yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ.

english_verse: more to the birth of the Messiah. The Birth of Jesus (Luke
translation_verse: Asɔ lolana Sadoki te Sadoki mɔ lola Akim te Akim mɔ lola Eliudi.

english_verse: This is how Jesus Christ was born. A young woman named Mary was engaged to Joseph from King David's family. But before they were married, she learned that she was going to have a baby by God's Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Yesu Kristo kalola wose man nombia yaa. Gyosɛfo na Yesu ɔ naa Mɛɛre yakowɔ see nyi baa yala dɔŋa. Mɔna Gyosɛfo ta kpeo ta e ya, te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ yela Mɛɛre kyaŋa fulu.

english_verse: Joseph was a good man and did not want to embarrass Mary in front of everyone. So he decided to quietly call off the wedding.
translation_verse: Na Gyosɛfo yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman, te ɔ bɛɛ gyae nyi waa do Mɛɛre desɛŋ balaŋ man dɔɔ ya nɛ, ɔ yɛɛ wa agyueŋ nyi waa yo bɛɛ e keŋ ŋolo na ŋolo ba gyem ya.

english_verse: While Joseph was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream. The angel said, “Joseph, the baby that Mary will have is from the Holy Spirit. Go ahead and marry her.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ Gyosɛfo na mo mena agyueŋ kei nɛ, te de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋolo kaa lese ɔ wose wola e dosɛɛ man, te ɔ yako e nyiaa, “Defidi ɔ naanabii Gyosɛfo, na yee Mɛɛre ke yala ya. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ feliŋ yele la te ɔ kyaŋa fulu kei.

english_verse: Then after her baby is born, name him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.”
translation_verse: Waa lola bu baale na n ka do e yele nyi Yesu. Nawolo nyi ŋon ne gyae la waa lese ɔ balaŋ lee be nombiakumɛɛ man.”

english_verse: After Joseph woke up, he and Mary were soon married, just as the Lord's angel had told him to do.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Gyosɛfo kefoŋ nɛ, ɔ moo Mɛɛre yala ŋgba mena keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon be yako e nɛ.

english_verse: But they did not sleep together before her baby was born. Then Joseph named him Jesus.
translation_verse: Mɔna ŋon na Mɛɛre te doo ŋgba alo na baale nɛ ya kaboena ɔ lola bu baale. Te Gyosɛfo doo e yele nyi Yesu.

english_verse: When Jesus was born in the village of Bethlehem in Judea, Herod was king. During this time some wise men from the east came to Jerusalem
translation_verse: Ba lola Yesu bo Bɛtelɛhɛm donɔɔ keŋ ke kyaa Gyudia tɛɛle man nɛ man. Debaŋ kenaŋ na gyoo Herode ne diina gyoori. Be ka lola Yesu nɛ, te di weeya ala ya te siakaralatena baale baŋ ba gyeŋ kyolobiise wose man nombia nɛ lee weese dɛɛlee yenaŋ kɔŋ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man,

english_verse: and said, “Where is the child born to be king of the Jews? We saw his star in the east and have come to worship him.”
translation_verse: kaa bɔɔse balaŋ nyiaa, “Aleŋ te ba lola bu ŋon waa kaa bese Gyudatena gyoo nɛ? Da te naa kyolobii ŋolo weese dɛɛlee yenaŋ keŋ na wola daa nyi be ta lola e, mena dɔɔ te de kɔŋawɔ de kaa som e.”

english_verse: When King Herod heard about this, he was worried, and so was everyone else in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Gyoo Herode na baŋ pou be kyaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, takaa wɔ nideli.

english_verse: Herod brought together the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses and asked them, “Where will the Messiah be born?”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ gyoo Herode baake Gyudatena saese kegyiise na ba mmaraa wolala pou yilaa te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Aleŋ te baa lola nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ?”

english_verse: They told him, “He will be born in Bethlehem, just as the prophet wrote,
translation_verse: Keŋte be tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Bɛtelɛhɛm donɔɔ keŋ ke kyaa Gyudia tɛɛle man nɛ.” Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre ŋolo yakowɔ see lee Bɛtelɛhɛm wose man nyiaa,

english_verse: ‘Bethlehem in the land of Judea, you are very important among the towns of Judea. From your town will come a leader, who will be like a shepherd for my people Israel.’ ”
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ kpa, “Ɛmɛɛ Bɛtelɛhɛmtena baŋ ɛ kyaa Gyudia tɛɛle man nɛ ɛ yɛɛ gyoorobiawɔ. Nawolo nyi ɛ man te gyoo dinaa ŋon waa kɛɛ me balaŋ Iseraetena dɔɔ nɛ waa lee.”

english_verse: Herod secretly called in the wise men and asked them when they had first seen the star.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ gyoo Herode moo Siakaralatena baŋ lee yenaŋ, te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ na waa gyeŋ debaŋ kitii keŋ kyolobii ŋon ba lee.

english_verse: He told them, “Go to Bethlehem and search carefully for the child. As soon as you find him, let me know. I also want to go and worship him.”
translation_verse: Be ke tiranɔɔ fa e taŋ nɛ, ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ gyu na ɛ ke kɛo bu ŋon nideli. Akpaa ɛ naa e na ɛ bese kaa yako maŋ, na maŋ mɔ ke gyu ka som e.” Keŋte ɔ lɔɔ wɔ gbɛɛ te be laŋa Bɛtelɛhɛm donɔɔ man.

english_verse: The wise men listened to what the king said and then left. And the star they had seen in the east went on ahead of them until it stopped over the place where the child was.
translation_verse: Be ke nyii gyoo ŋon nɔɔwoya taŋ nɛ, ba korowɔ mo be gbɛɛ bee gyu. Te kyolobii ŋon bɔɔ naa weese dɛɛlee yenaŋ nɛ, tɛɛwɔ be siaman menɛɛ te ke ka seŋawɔ botɔɔ keŋ bu ŋon doo nɛ adido.

english_verse: They were thrilled and excited to see the star.
translation_verse: Ba kena kyolobii ŋon mena nɛ, yɛɛ wɔ gyoŋ nideli.

english_verse: When the men went into the house and saw the child with Mary, his mother, they knelt down and worshiped him. They took out their gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh and gave them to him.
translation_verse: Be ke gyu ka gyoo dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man nɛ, be naa bu ŋon na ɔ naa Mɛɛre keŋte ba buŋawɔ som bu ŋon. Be gyuusu ba akpose te ba lese kɔba yayam, na oliifi na adudo kyɛɛ bu ŋon.

english_verse: Later they were warned in a dream not to return to Herod, and they went back home by another road. The Escape to Egypt
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, Wurubuarɛ faree wɔ dosɛɛ man nyi be na bese gyu ka mo gyoo Herode gyaŋ bela ya, mena dɔɔ ba moo gbɛɛ wɔle kpoo man gyu be dɛɛ. Be ka mo Yesu yeu gyu Igyipite tɛɛle man

english_verse: After the wise men had gone, an angel from the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, “Get up! Hurry and take the child and his mother to Egypt! Stay there until I tell you to return, because Herod is looking for the child and wants to kill him.”
translation_verse: Siakaralatena baŋ ke laŋ wɔle nɛ, de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋolo kaa lese ɔ wose wola Gyosɛfo dosɛɛ man te ɔ yako e nyiaa, “Koro mo bu ŋon na ɔ naa na ɛ ke yeu gyu Igyipite tɛɛle man, na ɛ ke kyaa botɔɔ kelii debaŋ keŋ maa yako ŋon nyi yaa koro botɔɔ. Nawolo nyi gyoo Herode waa kɛo bu ŋon waa ko.”

english_verse: That night, Joseph got up and took his wife and the child to Egypt,
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Gyosɛfo korowɔ nelim kenaŋ te ɔ moo bu ŋon na ɔ naa te be yeuwɔ gyu Igyipite tɛɛle man,

english_verse: where they stayed until Herod died. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “I called my son out of Egypt.” The Killing of the Children
translation_verse: te be kyaawɔ botɔɔ kaboena gyoo Herode yeŋawɔ. Kɛŋa pou kɔŋawɔ mena na kpene keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo be tɛɛ ɔ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre dɔɔ yako see nɛ ke kɔŋ mena ampaŋ. Ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Igyipite tɛɛle man te ma lese me bu lee.”

english_verse: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
translation_verse: Gyoo Herode kena nyi Siakaralatena baŋ ta mo adao di ɔ dɔɔ, keŋ be ta bese kɔŋ kaa mo ɔ gyaŋ bela ya nɛ ɔ gyeŋa baŋ nideli. Keŋte ɔ kpila ɔ yoonɔɔtena nyi baa gyu Bɛtelɛhɛm donɔɔ man, na ke donɔɔsebia ŋan benaa na ke nɛ, na baa koe bia baala baŋ pou be ta bee ta kela kulutooneŋ ala ya nɛ, nawolo nyi mena debaŋ kenaŋ man te Siakaralatena baŋ yako e nyi be naa kyolobii ŋon.

english_verse: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet Jeremiah had said,
translation_verse: Kei yele la kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Gyeremia be yako see nɛ kɔŋawɔ ampaŋ. Ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa,

english_verse: “In Ramah a voice was heard crying and weeping loudly. Rachel was mourning for her children, and she refused to be comforted, because they were dead.” The Return from Egypt
translation_verse: “De nyii yeesa na kewii na kayeyɛɛ dinaa Rama tɛɛle man. Rahɛle ne wii ɔ bia te ɔ te bɛɛ nyi be na kyɔlɛɛ e ya, nawolo nyi ɔ bia pou te munu.” Be ka bese kɔŋ Nasarɛte donɔɔ man

english_verse: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.
translation_verse: Gyoo Herode yeŋ wɔle nɛ, Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋolo kaa lese ɔ wose wola Gyosɛfo dosɛɛ man Igyipite tɛɛle man te ɔ yako e nyiaa,

english_verse: The angel said, “Get up and take the child and his mother back to Israel. The people who wanted to kill him are now dead.”
translation_verse: “Koro na n ka mo bu ŋon na ɔ naa, na ɛ ka bese gyu Iserae tɛɛle man. Nawolo nyi balaŋ baŋ nafɔ bɛɛ gyae bu ŋon baa ko nɛ ta yekee.”

english_verse: But when he heard that Herod's son Archelaus was now ruler of Judea, he was afraid to go there. Then in a dream he was told to go to Galilee,
translation_verse: Mɔna Gyosɛfo ke nyii nyi gyoo Herode bu Akelɔse kyaa la ɔ kya nawɔɔ man ɔ ne di gyoori Gyudia tɛɛle dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ yee botɔɔ ke gyu. Te keŋ Wurubuarɛ be faree e dosɛɛ man dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ kpasewɔ gyu Galelia tɛɛle man.

english_verse: and they went to live there in the town of Nazareth. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “He will be called a Nazarene.”
translation_verse: Te ɔ gyuuwɔ ke kyaa donɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi Nasarɛte nɛ man. Kei yela kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ be tɛɛ wa akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra dɔɔ yako see nɛ, kɔŋawɔ ampaŋ. Be yakowɔ nyiaa, “Baa baake e nyi Nasarɛte baale.”

english_verse: Do something to show you have really given up your sins.
translation_verse: Ɛ yɛɛ nombia kpaakpaa ŋan nawolo nyi ɛ te kyɛɛkee lee ɛ gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ.

english_verse: He said, “Turn back to God! The kingdom of heaven will soon be here.”
translation_verse: “Ɛ kyɛɛkee lee ɛ gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man na ɛ ke kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ ta benaa.”

english_verse: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
translation_verse: Kurumɔɔ tɔne te ba moowɔ ŋara Gyɔn kegba, te wonembu tɔne te ɔ moowɔ yɛɛ abɔsɔɔ kpaŋ ɔ teŋa man. Akpataana na teebɔɔ yɛna ɔ weenɛɛ.

english_verse: From Jerusalem and all Judea and from the Jordan River Valley crowds of people went to John.
translation_verse: Balaŋ lee Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man na Gyudia tɛɛlese man, na donɔɔse ŋan ŋe kyaa Gyɔɔdan boo keŋ yenaase nɛ kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ.

english_verse: They told how sorry they were for their sins, and he baptized them in the river.
translation_verse: Bɛɛ kaa tɔlɛɛ be nombiakumɛɛ ŋan pou bɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ fa e, na ɔ te sɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ Gyɔɔdan boo keŋ man.

english_verse: An ax is ready to cut the trees down at their roots. Any tree that doesn't produce good fruit will be chopped down and thrown into a fire.
translation_verse: Ɛ kɛɛ, Wurubuarɛ taŋ desina ɔ debeŋa see waa tuŋii daase lɔ. Mena dɔɔ daŋ kamasɛ keŋ ke bɛɛ se bia kpaakpaa ya na, waa tuŋ ke lɔ na baa keretii ke do boalaŋ man.

english_verse: His threshing fork is in his hand, and he is ready to separate the wheat from the husks. He will store the wheat in a barn and burn the husks in a fire that never goes out. The Baptism of Jesus (Mark
translation_verse: Ɔ dana ɔ pampaa ɔ nyiŋmaa man waa mo feefu wa amɔla na waa lese amɔla bia ŋan do ɔ kpee man, na waa kuu wura ŋan ka do boalaŋ keŋ ke bɛɛ yem ya nɛ man. Yesu Wurubuarɛ loŋkesɔ Maake

english_verse: Jesus left Galilee and went to the Jordan River to be baptized by John.
translation_verse: Gyɔn kɛɛ sɔ balaŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ keŋ nɛ, te Yesu leewɔ Galelia tɛɛle man kɔŋ Gyɔɔdan boo nɔɔman nyi Gyɔn waa sɔ e Wurubuarɛ loŋ.

english_verse: Jesus answered, “For now this is how it should be, because we must do all God wants us to do.” Then John agreed.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa Gyɔn nyiaa, “Debaŋ kei man nɛ, nyaŋ yɛɛ ke mena. De yɛɛ nnɛ na, Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii yaa dɛɛ yɛɛ baa.” Keŋte Gyɔn mɔ sɛɛwɔ.

english_verse: So Jesus was baptized. And as soon as he came out of the water, the sky opened, and he saw the Spirit of God coming down on him like a dove.
translation_verse: Gyɔn ke sɔ Yesu Wurubuarɛ loŋ taŋ, te Yesu ka mo waa lee loŋ keŋ man pɛ, te adido torowɔ. Keŋte ɔ naa Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon moo duwuluŋ dinɔɔ tisi kaa gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ.

english_verse: Then a voice from heaven said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te be nyii Wurubuarɛ kolosiwɔ lee adido yako nyiaa, “Kei yɛna me bu ŋon mɛɛ gyae lee me konɔɔ man, te ɔ na gyoo me sia nideli.”

english_verse: When Jesus heard that John had been put in prison, he went to Galilee.
translation_verse: Yesu ke nyii nyi ba te kyaŋ Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon tɔ deni nɛ, te ɔ besewɔ gyu Galelia tɛɛle man.

english_verse: But instead of staying in Nazareth, Jesus moved to Capernaum. This town was beside Lake Galilee in the territory of Zebulun and Naphtali.
translation_verse: Yesu ka lee Nasarɛte donɔɔ man nɛ, ɔ gyuuwɔ ke kyaa Kapanum donɔɔ man. Donɔɔ kei kyaa Galelia depaa nɔɔman, te ka benaa na Sebulɔn na Nafitali tɛɛlese.

english_verse: After Jesus had gone without eating for
translation_verse: Yesu ke bake ɔ nɔɔ weeya sɔŋola (

english_verse: days and nights, he was very hungry.
translation_verse: ) nelim na ka weese wɔle nɛ, tanam kyaŋa e nideli.

english_verse: Then the devil came to him and said, “If you are God's Son, tell these stones to turn into bread.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔbɔnsam kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ kaa yako e nyiaa, “Akpaa nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon na, yela boya kɛŋa ka bese weenɛɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “The Scriptures also say, ‘Don't try to test the Lord your God!’ ”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see na bese yako daa nyiaa, ‘Na kɛɛse n Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ ŋon kɛɛ ya.’ ”

english_verse: Finally, the devil took Jesus up on a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms on earth and their power.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔbɔnsam besewɔ mo Yesu gyu bula dinaa daale nyee dɔɔ, te ɔ wola e tɛɛle kei dɔɔ gyoorobiiri na ka kyeo pou.

english_verse: The devil said to him, “I will give all this to you, if you will bow down and worship me.”
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ yako Yesu nyiaa, “Akpaa n buŋ me nawɔɔ tɛɛ som maŋ na, maa mo abɔɔ kɛŋa pou fa neŋ.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Go away Satan! The Scriptures say: ‘Worship the Lord your God and serve only him.’ ”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Ɔbɔnsam ta lee me siaman, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako daa nyiaa, de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ waageŋ te kaboena nyi dee buŋ e na dɛɛ som e.”

english_verse: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said,
translation_verse: Kei yela kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya be yako see lee tɛɛle kenaŋ wose man nɛ kɔŋawɔ ampaŋ. Yesaya yakowɔ see nyiaa,

english_verse: “Listen, lands of Zebulun and Naphtali, lands along the road to the sea and across the Jordan. Listen Galilee, land of the Gentiles!
translation_verse: “Ɛmɛɛ balaŋ baŋ ɛ kyaa Sebulɔn na Nafitali tɛɛlese ŋan ŋe kyaa benaa na Galelia depaa, botɔɔ keŋ balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ boe nɛ, na Gyɔɔdan boo diŋgyiŋ nɛ, ɛ tei kɛbo.

english_verse: Right then the two brothers dropped their nets and went with him.
translation_verse: Debokenaŋ man te be tinaa ba asawuse ŋan yela te be kaa sila Yesu.

english_verse: While Jesus was walking along the shore of Lake Galilee, he saw two brothers. One was Simon, also known as Peter, and the other was Andrew. They were fishermen, and they were casting their net into the lake.
translation_verse: Wee daale Yesu keta Galelia boo nɔɔ nɔɔ nɛ, ɔ naa Simɔn ŋon ɔ yele daale bela yɛna Pita nɛ, na ɔ naabu Anderiase na bɛɛ lɔ asawu boo keŋ man, nawolo nyi be yɛɛ kpɛɛ kyaŋarawɔ.

english_verse: Jesus walked on until he saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat with their father, mending their nets. Jesus asked them to come with him.
translation_verse: Ɔ ke gyu ɔ siaman kyomii nɛ, ɔ naa Sebedeo bia Gyemisi na ɔ naabu Gyɔn na baŋ na be kya Sebedeo doo degbele man bee desina ba asawuse te Yesu baake wɔ,

english_verse: At once they left the boat and their father and went with Jesus. Jesus Teaches, Preaches, and Heals (Luke
translation_verse: debokenaŋ man te be tinaa degbele keŋ na be kya yela, te be sila Yesu laŋ. Yesu ke kyɔ kaweese Luka

english_verse: Jesus went all over Galilee, teaching in their synagogues and preaching the good news about God's kingdom. He also healed every kind of disease and sickness.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu kila Galelia tɛɛle keŋ pou, na ɔ na wola wɔ Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man. Ɔ kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia kpaakpaa lee Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ wose man, te ɔ kyɔɔ kaweese kpookpoo balaŋ wose man.

english_verse: News about him spread all over Syria, and people with every kind of sickness or disease were brought to him. Some of them had a lot of demons in them, others were thought to be crazy, and still others could not walk. But Jesus healed them all.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Yesu yele leewɔ Siria tɛɛle keŋ dɔɔ pou. Keŋte balaŋ moo kaweesetena kpookpoo, ŋgba baŋ bɛɛ wee keleuleuse na ɔtakasese na baŋ ba yenaase ba yekee nɛ kɔna e, te ɔ kyɔɔ wɔ te ɔ gberaa feliŋkumɛɛ lee baale mɔ man.

english_verse: Large crowds followed Jesus from Galilee and the region around the ten cities known as Decapolis. They also came from Jerusalem, Judea, and from across the Jordan River.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ balaŋ burum lee Galelia, na Dekapolise tɛɛle man na Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man na Gyudia tɛɛle man, na Gyɔɔdan diŋgyiŋ kaa sila e.

english_verse: You must obey God's commands better than the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law obey them. If you don't, I promise you will never get into the kingdom of heaven. Anger
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, akpaa ɛ gbɛɛneŋ ta tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman kela Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ, na Farasiitena dikpii keŋ balaŋ ya nɛ, ɛ be gyae ɛ ka gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man ya.” Yesu kawola lee baŋ wose man

english_verse: So if you are about to place your gift on the altar and remember that someone is angry with you,
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ nyi akpaa nyaŋ ŋon n mo kolo kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ kedeesa boe keŋ tɛɛ n kaa deesi, te n tɔɔse nyi ɛmɛɛ na ŋolo dana nombia na,

english_verse: God blesses those people who depend only on him. They belong to the kingdom of heaven!
translation_verse: “Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ ba mo ɛ wose pou lɔ Wurubuarɛ dɔɔ nɛ ɛmɛɛ na nyeebam, nawolo nyi ɛmɛɛ te Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ ɛ wui.

english_verse: God blesses those people who grieve. They will find comfort!
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ ne di walaŋsa nɛ ɛmɛɛ na nyeebam, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ waa kyɔlɛɛ ŋon.

english_verse: God blesses those people who are humble. The earth will belong to them!
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ ne yɔkɔse ɛ wose nɛ ɛmɛɛ na nyeebam, nawolo nyi ɛmɛɛ te tɛɛle kei gyae kaa bese ɛ wui.

english_verse: God blesses those people who are merciful. They will be treated with mercy!
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ ne wii balaŋ waraŋase nɛ ɛmɛɛ na nyeebam, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ waa wii ɛmɛɛ mɔ waraŋase.

english_verse: God blesses those people whose hearts are pure. They will see him!
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ baŋ dukum kamasɛ be doo ɛ konɔɔse man ya nɛ ɛmɛɛ na nyeebam, nawolo nyi ɛmɛɛ ne gyae la ɛ kena Wurubuarɛ.

english_verse: You are the light for the whole world. A city built on top of a hill cannot be hidden,
translation_verse: “Ɛ yɛɛ ŋgba keŋmaŋee nɛ fa balaŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ. Donɔɔ keŋ ke gyakaa bula dɔɔ nɛ bɛɛ weese ya.

english_verse: But I tell you not to swear by anything when you make a promise! Heaven is God's throne, so don't swear by heaven.
translation_verse: Mɔna maŋ ne yako ŋon baa nyiaa, ɛ na kaŋ ntam korakora gba ya. Ɛ na kaŋ adido ntam ya, nawolo nyi botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ kyaa ɔ ne di gyoori balaŋ pou dɔɔ.

english_verse: The earth is God's footstool, so don't swear by the earth. Jerusalem is the city of the great king, so don't swear by it.
translation_verse: Ɛ na kaŋ tɛɛle mɔ ntam ya, nawolo nyi botɔɔ yɛna Wurubuarɛ nae dɛɛ see. Ɛ na kaŋ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ mɔ ntam ya, nawolo nyi keŋ yɛna gyoo dinaa ŋon donɔɔ.

english_verse: and no one lights a lamp and puts it under a clay pot. Instead, it is placed on a lampstand, where it can give light to everyone in the house.
translation_verse: Ŋolo bɛɛ kyaŋee kanea na waa mo kolo wuu ke dɔɔ ya, mɔna ɔ na mo ke bo gyakaa kolo dɔɔ adido, na ke nyiŋ naa fa balaŋ baŋ pou be kyaa dekpaŋalaŋ man nɛ.

english_verse: Don't suppose I came to do away with the Law and the Prophets. I did not come to do away with them, but to give them their full meaning.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ na gyueŋ nyi me kɔŋae me kaa bɛɛ Wurubuarɛ mmaraase, yaa nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra be yako see nɛ ya. Ma te kɔŋ nyi me kaa bɛɛ ŋa ya, mɔna me kɔŋae na maa yela nombia ŋenaŋ pou ke kɔŋ ŋgba ba ke yako see nɛ.

english_verse: But I promise you if you are angry with someone, you will have to stand trial. If you call someone a fool, you will be taken to court. And if you say that someone is worthless, you will be in danger of the fires of hell.
translation_verse: Mɔna maŋ ne yako ŋon baa nyiaa, ‘Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ gyeŋ baŋ do ɔ dɔɔ na baa bu e fɔɔ, te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ tee ɔ dɔɔ nyi ɔ yɛɛ ohuhoo e na, baa mo e gyu kegyiise siaman na be kaa di ɔ nombia. Te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ baake ɔ dɔɔ nyi Diyiŋte na, ɔtenate benaa na boalaŋ man kagyoo.’

english_verse: leave your gift there in front of the altar. Make peace with that person, then come back and offer your gift to God.
translation_verse: tina nya abɔɔ ŋan yela kedeesa boe keŋ tɛɛ botɔɔ, na n ke gyu n dɔɔ ŋon gyaŋ na ɛ kaa desina ya nsana, pɛna n kaa mo nya abɔɔ ŋan deesi fa Wurubuarɛ.

english_verse: Before you are dragged into court, make friends with the person who has accused you of doing wrong. If you don't, you will be handed over to the judge and then to the officer who will put you in jail.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa ŋolo de samenaa neŋ na, yɛɛ kakyeŋ na ɛ ke desina ya nsana pɛna nombia kedi wee keŋ kelii. Na mena ya wee kenaŋ de lii na, waa mo neŋ ka do nombiadiiri ŋon nyiŋmaa man, na ŋon mɔ ka mo neŋ ka do kotɔtɔɔtena nyiŋmaa man, na baa mo neŋ ketɔ deni.

english_verse: You have been taught that a man who divorces his wife must write out divorce papers for her.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa “Ɛ nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia yakowɔ nyiaa, ‘Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon waa bɛɛ ɔ wɛɛle na, kaboena nyi waa fa e tɔne na kawola nyi ɔ te bɛɛ e.’

english_verse: You know our ancestors were told, “Don't use the Lord's name to make a promise unless you are going to keep it.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ nyiiwɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia besewɔ yako gyaŋgba keŋ nyiaa, walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ kaŋ ntam fa de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ na, waa yɛɛ kakyeŋ na waa di ke dɔɔ.

english_verse: But I tell you not to try to get even with a person who has done something to you. When someone slaps your right cheek, turn and let that person slap your other cheek.
translation_verse: Mɔna maŋ ne yako ŋon baa nyiaa, akpaa ŋolo de yɛɛ neŋ nombiikum na, nyaŋ na mo nombiikum tɔ e kom ya. Te akpaa ŋolo da ŋmera neŋ n dunoluŋ lakaa man na, besena n demena mɔ wui kpu e.

english_verse: If someone sues you for your shirt, give up your coat as well.
translation_verse: Nyi ŋolo de samenaa neŋ nyi waa lɛo n kegba wurikyim na, kpee n kegba atetate keŋ mɔ kpu e.

english_verse: If a soldier forces you to carry his pack one kilometer, carry it two kilometers.
translation_verse: Akpaa ŋolo da ŋere neŋ nyi ɛmɛɛ na e ke ta nawɔɔtɔɔse kufu na, sɛɛ na ɛmɛɛ na e ke ta nawɔɔtɔɔse sao.

english_verse: When people ask you for something, give it to them. When they want to borrow money, lend it to them. Love (Luke
translation_verse: Akpaa ŋolo da sola neŋ kolo na fa e, te akpaa ŋolo de kɔŋ ɔ kaa paa n kolo na, na bɛɛ ya.” Yesu kpa dɛɛ gyae balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ kɔla daa Luka

english_verse: You have heard people say, “Love your neighbors and hate your enemies.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako nyiaa, “Ɛ nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia yakowɔ nyiaa, gyae n dɔɔ na n ke kɔla ŋon ɔ ne kɔla neŋ nɛ.

english_verse: But I tell you to love your enemies and pray for anyone who mistreats you.
translation_verse: Mɔna maŋ ne yako ŋon baa nyiaa, ɛ gyae balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ kɔla ŋon nɛ, na ɛ ke fane fa baŋ bɛɛ naase ŋon diyem nɛ,

english_verse: Then you will be acting like your Father in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both good and bad people. And he sends rain for the ones who do right and for the ones who do wrong.
translation_verse: na kawola nyi ɛ yɛɛ ɛ kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ bia gbagbawɔ. Nawolo nyi ɔ ne yele weese ne gyɔɔ fa balaŋ kumɛɛ na balaŋ kpaakpaa pou, te ɔ ne yele ɔga ne ŋɛɛ fa balaŋ baŋ be gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ ɔ siaman, na baŋ be gbɛɛneŋ ba tenɛɛ ɔ siaman ya nɛ pou.

english_verse: If you love only those people who love you, will God reward you for this? Even tax collectors love their friends.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa ɛ ne gyae balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ gyae ŋon nɛ baageŋ bo na, ɛ na gyueŋ nyi ɛ ke nyiŋ kolo kpaakpaa lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ ya, nawolo nyi nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra mɔ koraŋ ne gyae baŋ bɛɛ gyae wɔ nɛ.

english_verse: If you greet only your friends, what's so great about this? Don't even unbelievers do that?
translation_verse: Te akpaa ɛ do ɛ gyoona baageŋ nɔɔ na, woŋ te ɛ ne yɛɛ kela balaŋ akaŋ baŋ? Na mena te balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ ya nɛ mɔ ne yɛɛ yee?

english_verse: When you do good deeds, don't try to show off. If you do, you won't get a reward from your Father in heaven.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa “Ɛ kɛɛ nideli nyi ɛ be yɛɛ deeli balaŋ man, na baa nyiŋ naa ŋon ya. Akpaa ɛ yɛɛ mena na, ɛ be gyae ɛ ke nyiŋ kom ketɔ kamasɛ lee ɛ kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ gyaŋ ya.

english_verse: When you give to the poor, don't let anyone know about it.
translation_verse: Mɔna akpaa n yɛɛ deeli fa diyemte ŋolo na, na yele n dɔɔ kpaakpaa gba koraŋ ke nyii ya.

english_verse: When you pray, don't be like those show-offs who love to stand up and pray in the synagogues and on the street corners. They do this just to look good. I can assure you that they already have their reward.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyi, “Akpaa ɛ ne fane nɛ, ɛ na yɛɛ ŋgba mena keŋ balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ beo ba wose ne yɛɛ nɛ ya. Nawolo nyi bɛɛ gyae Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man, na abɔnterese man keta seŋee fane na balaŋ kenyiŋ naa wɔ. Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi be taŋ tɔ wɔ ba kom pou.

english_verse: When you pray, go into a room alone and close the door. Pray to your Father in private. He knows what is done in private and will reward you.
translation_verse: Mɔna akpaa ɛ ne gyae ɛ ke fane na, ɛ gyu ɛ denɛɛ man na ɛ ketɔ disim na ɛ ke fane fa ɛ kya ŋon ɛ bɛɛ naa e na ɛ sia fonɛɛ ya nɛ. Akpaa ɛ yɛɛ mena na, ɛ kya ŋon ɔ ne naa kpene keŋ ɛ be yɛɛ weese nɛ, waa tɔ ŋon kom.

english_verse: When you pray, don't talk on and on as people do who don't know God. They think God likes to hear long prayers.
translation_verse: Akpaa ɛ ne fane na, ɛ na kolosi nɔɔwoya burum ŋan ŋɔɔ taŋ tɔnɔɔ, ŋgba mena keŋ balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ ya ne yɛɛ nɛ ya. Baŋ gyeŋ nyi akpaa be kolosi nɔɔwoya burum na, te Wurubuarɛ waa nyii wɔ.

english_verse: You should pray like this: Our Father in heaven, help us to honor your name.
translation_verse: “Ɛ ne gyae ɛ ke fane na, ɛ yako nyiaa, ‘De kya ŋon n kyaa adido, dɛɛ fane nyi balaŋ pou baa lese n yele.

english_verse: Come and set up your kingdom, so that everyone on earth will obey you, as you are obeyed in heaven.
translation_verse: Lese n gyoori debɔɔ, yela baa yɛɛ n kegyaebii tɛɛle dɔɔ, ŋgba mena keŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ adido nɛ.

english_verse: If you forgive others for the wrongs they do to you, your Father in heaven will forgive you.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi akpaa ɛ mo balaŋ nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ na, ɛ kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ mɔ waa mo ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ ŋon.

english_verse: But if you don't forgive others, your Father will not forgive your sins. Worshiping God by Going without Eating
translation_verse: Mɔna akpaa ɛ ta mo balaŋ nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ ya na, ɛ kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ mɔ be gyae waa mo ɛmɛɛ mɔ wui nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ ŋon ya. Yesu kawola lee nɔɔkebake wose man

english_verse: But more than anything else, put God's work first and do what he wants. Then the other things will be yours as well.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛ taŋgbɛɛ mo ɛ loo pou gyakaa Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ na ɔ gbɛɛneŋ ŋan tenɛɛ nɛ dɔɔ. Nyi akpaa ɛ yɛɛ mena na, Wurubuarɛ waa mo abɔɔ akaŋ ŋan pou kpu ŋon.

english_verse: Don't worry about tomorrow. It will take care of itself. You have enough to worry about today.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛ na yeyɛɛ na kyoo wui nombia ya, te ɛ na mo gyɛŋ wui nombia kpu kyoo wui man ya. Nawolo nyi wee kamasɛ na ke wui nombia.”

english_verse: Don't store up treasures on earth! Moths and rust can destroy them, and thieves can break in and steal them.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ na gyae abɔɔ see tɛɛle kei dɔɔ fa ɛ wose ya. Nawolo nyi tɛɛle kei dɔɔ na, paapaase na kandorɔse gyae ke tale wɔlɛɛ ŋa, yaa ŋmɛɛlatena mɔ baa tale kaa ŋmɛɛle ŋa.

english_verse: Instead, store up your treasures in heaven, where moths and rust cannot destroy them, and thieves cannot break in and steal them.
translation_verse: Mɔna ɛ gyae abɔɔ see ɛ wose Wurubuarɛ man, botɔɔ keŋ paapaase na kandorɔse be wɔlɛɛ ŋa ya, te ŋmɛɛlatena mɔ be gyae baa tale gyu ke ŋmɛɛle ŋa ya nɛ.

english_verse: Your heart will always be where your treasure is. Light (Luke
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi botɔɔ keŋ nya abɔɔ kpaakpaa doo nɛ, botɔɔ mɔ te n loo gyakaa.” Sia yɛna n wose keŋ keŋmaŋee Luka

english_verse: But when your eyes are bad, everything is dark. If the light inside you is dark, you surely are in the dark. Money (Luke
translation_verse: Mɔna n sia de wɔlɛɛ na, n wose pou doo ditiŋtɛɛ tuum man. Nyi akpaa kanea keŋ doo n man nɛ da gyoo ditiŋtɛɛ na, kenaŋ na ditiŋtɛɛ dinaa ta gyoo neŋ.” Ɛ na mo ɛ konɔɔ pou gyakaa ɛ wose man abɔɔ dɔɔ ya Luka

english_verse: You cannot be the slave of two masters! You will like one more than the other or be more loyal to one than the other. You cannot serve both God and money. Worry (Luke
translation_verse: “Walaŋ be kyaa keŋ waa tale som gbeŋgyoona bala ya. Dekɔŋ mena na, waa kɔla ŋolo na waa gyae ŋolo nombia, yaa waa som ŋolo nideli kela ŋolo. Ɛ be gyae ɛ ke tale som Wurubuarɛ na kɔba bom ya.

english_verse: Don't worry and ask yourselves, “Will we have anything to eat? Will we have anything to drink? Will we have any clothes to wear?”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛ na yeyɛɛ na kpene keŋ ɛ ke di, yaa kpene keŋ ɛ ke nyɔɔ yaa kpene keŋ ɛ ka do nɛ dɔɔ ya.

english_verse: I tell you not to worry about your life. Don't worry about having something to eat, drink, or wear. Isn't life more than food or clothing?
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ te mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, ɛ na yeyɛɛ na ɛ kekyaa man kedi na kenyɔɔ, na abɔɔ ŋan ɛ ka do ya. Nyeedoŋ ne hia kela weenɛɛ, te deniwalaŋ wose mɔ ne hia kela abɔɔ ŋan waa do.

english_verse: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
translation_verse: Ɛ kɛɛ gyebuse, bɔɔ ne duu abɔɔ ya te bɔɔ ne kɔɔ ya te bɔɔ dana kpeese yaa denɛɛ ŋan man baa see weenɛɛ ya. Mɔna ɛ kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ ne fa wɔ weenɛɛ. Kaboena ya gyeŋ nyi, ɛ wose ne hia kela gyebuse.

english_verse: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
translation_verse: Woŋ dɔɔ te ɛ na yeyɛɛ na abɔɔ ŋan ɛ ka do. Ɛ kɛɛ mena keŋ kyeŋkyeŋkɔɔse na bee, ŋe bɛɛ yɛɛ tom ya te ŋe bɛɛ loo kanyaŋse mɔ ya.

english_verse: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
translation_verse: Mɔna mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, Gyoo Solomɔn gba na ɔ wosekenyiŋ koraŋ nɛ, ɔ te nyiŋ kyeŋkyeŋkɔɔse kɛŋa man dokoloŋ kyeo ya.

english_verse: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. God will surely do even more for you! Why do you have such little faith?
translation_verse: Weera dɔɔ te ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di nideli ya. Nyi akpaa mena te Wurubuarɛ ne mia ŋalese ŋan ɛ kena ŋa gyɛŋ kyoo na be ta too ŋa na, kenaŋ na ɛ tɔɔse nyi Wurubuarɛ waa mia ŋon kela ŋalese ŋenaŋ.

english_verse: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father in heaven knows you need all of these.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi mena abɔɔ kɛŋa te tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ ya nɛ sia ne yɛɛ ŋe dɔɔ, mɔna ɛ kya Wurubuarɛ gyeŋ nyi mena abɔɔ kɛŋa ne hia ɛmɛɛ mɔ.

english_verse: God will be as hard on you as you are on others! He will treat you exactly as you treat them.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi gbɛɛ keŋ man ɛ ka mo di ɛ tebia nombia na, mena mɔ te Wurubuarɛ waa mo di ɛ nombia. Nombii keŋ ɛ ka mo yɛɛ ɛ tebia nɛ, keŋ ke te Wurubuarɛ mɔ waa mo yɛɛ ŋon.

english_verse: You can see the speck in your friend's eye, but you don't notice the log in your own eye.
translation_verse: Weera dɔɔ te n ne naa woŋsilibii keŋ doo n dɔɔ sibii man, te n be famenɛ na daakpee keŋ doo nyaŋ gbagba n sibii man ya?

english_verse: How can you say, “My friend, let me take the speck out of your eye,” when you don't see the log in your own eye?
translation_verse: Sena te n ke tale yako n dɔɔ nyi, ‘Me dɔɔ yela maa lese woŋsilibii lee n sibii man, keŋ nyaŋ gbagba be famenɛ na daakpee keŋ doo n sibii man ya?’

english_verse: You're nothing but show-offs! First, take the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye.
translation_verse: Nyaŋ walaŋ ŋon n na beo n wose, kaboena nyi nya taŋgbɛɛ lese daakpee keŋ doo n sibii man nɛ, pɛna n kena nideli na n ka lese woŋsilibii keŋ doo n dɔɔ sibii man nɛ.

english_verse: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.
translation_verse: Akpaa ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛrawɔ koraŋ gyeŋ abɔɔ kpaakpaa ka mo fa ɛ bia na, de yaa sena te ɛ kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ be gyae waa mo abɔɔ kpaakpaa ŋan kela kɛŋa nɛ fa baŋ bɛɛ sola e nɛ ya.

english_verse: Would any of you give your hungry child a stone, if the child asked for some bread?
translation_verse: Ɛ man woŋti kyaa la keŋ nyi ɔ bu da sola e weenɛɛ na waa mo boe fa e?

english_verse: Ask, and you will receive. Search, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened for you.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ sola Wurubuarɛ kolo na ɛ nyiŋmaa gyae ke kaŋ ke. Ɛ kɛo kolo lee ɔ gyaŋ na ɛ ke nyiŋ ke, te ɛ gyɔ e disim na waa toro fa ŋon.

english_verse: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ bɔɔse e kolo na ɔ nyiŋmaa ne kaŋ ke. Ŋon ɔ gyae kolo lee ɔ gyaŋ na ɔ ne nyiŋ ke, te ŋon mɔ ɔ gyɔ e disim na waa toro fa e.

english_verse: Would you give your child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
translation_verse: Yaa ɛ man woŋti kyaa la keŋ nyi ɔ bu da sola e kpebii na waa mo dom fa e?

english_verse: Treat others as you want them to treat you. This is what the Law and the Prophets are all about. The Narrow Gate (Luke
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ mmaraase na Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra nombia ŋan pou bɔɔ wolo nɛ ŋa nyee yɛna nyi, kpene keŋ n ne gyae nyi balaŋ baa yɛɛ fa neŋ na, keŋ te kaboena nyi nyaŋ mɔ nya yɛɛ fa wɔ.” Denanɔɔ bɔbɔɔkyee keŋ Luka

english_verse: Anyone who hears and obeys these teachings of mine is like a wise person who built a house on solid rock.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Mena dɔɔ walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne nyii me nɔɔwoya kɛŋa te ɔ ne di ŋe dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ yɛɛ ŋgba siakaralate ŋon ɔ be baŋ ɔ deni gyakaa boe dɔɔ nɛ.

english_verse: But the gate to life is very narrow. The road that leads there is so hard to follow that only a few people find it. A Tree and Its Fruit (Luke
translation_verse: Te denanɔɔ bɔbɔɔkyee keŋ ke gbɛɛnaa yɛɛ peperee te ke dɔɔ keta dana baŋ nɛ, na moona balaŋ gyu nyeedoŋ keŋ be taŋ ɔto ya nɛ man. Mɔna balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ naa ke te bɛɛ moo ke man gyoo nɛ, be ta boo ya.” Daŋ kamasɛ na ke bii keŋ kɛɛ see Luka

english_verse: Watch out for false prophets! They dress up like sheep, but inside they are wolves who have come to attack you.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose man nideli lee akɛrɛɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ wose man. Akpaa be kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ na, bɛɛ yɔkɔse ba wose ŋgba namense nɛ mɔna be konɔɔse man na be yɛɛ ŋgba gbougbouse nɛ.

english_verse: You can tell what they are by what they do. No one picks grapes or figs from thornbushes.
translation_verse: Be nombia ŋan bɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ te ɛ ka mo gyeŋ wɔ. Ŋolo bɛɛ kɔ mango bia lee akutu daŋ man ya, mena mɔ te bɔɔ ne kɔ apaŋ lee abotoo woŋse man ya.

english_verse: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit.
translation_verse: Mena mɔ te daŋ kpaakpaate kamasɛ na se bia kpaakpaa, te daŋ kum mɔ na see bia kumɛɛ.

english_verse: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
translation_verse: Daŋ kpaakpaate be gyae ke tale see bia kumɛɛ ya, te daŋ kum mɔ be gyae ke tale see bia kpaakpaa ya.

english_verse: Every tree producing bad fruit will be chopped down and burned.
translation_verse: Daŋ kamasɛ keŋ bɛɛ se bia kpaakpaa ya nɛ, bɛɛ kara ke na be ta mo do boalaŋ.

english_verse: You can tell who the false prophets are by their deeds. A Warning (Luke
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ nombia ŋan balaŋ benaŋ ne yɛɛ nɛ, te ɛ ka mo gyeŋ wɔ.” Walaŋ ŋon waa gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man

english_verse: Anyone who hears my teachings and doesn't obey them is like a foolish person who built a house on sand.
translation_verse: Mɔna walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne nyii me nɔɔwoya kɛŋa te ɔ bee di ŋe dɔɔ ya nɛ, ɔ yɛɛ ŋgba diyiŋte ŋon ɔ be ma ɔ deni gyakaa aŋasa dɔɔ nɛ.

english_verse: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and the winds blew and beat against that house. Finally, it fell with a crash.
translation_verse: Ɔga ŋɛɛwɔ te booneŋ wuuwɔ te feliŋ dinaa gyɔɔ deni keŋ te ka leewɔ yala worom tekerii pou.”

english_verse: Not everyone who calls me their Lord will get into the kingdom of heaven. Only the ones who obey my Father in heaven will get in.
translation_verse: “Na walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne baake maŋ nyi ‘Me Gbeŋgyoo, me Gbeŋgyoo’ nɛ, ne gyae la waa gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man ya. Mɔna walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne yɛɛ kpene keŋ me kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido ne gyae nɛ, ŋon waageŋ ne gyae la waa gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man.

english_verse: On the day of judgment many will call me their Lord. They will say, “We preached in your name, and in your name we forced out demons and worked many miracles.”
translation_verse: Nombia kedi wee dinaa keŋ man nɛ, balaŋ burum baa kaa yako nyiaa, ‘De Gbeŋgyoo, na n yele man te de kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia yee? Yaa na n yele te da moowɔ gegi feliŋkumɛɛ lee balaŋ man yee? Na n yele te da moowɔ yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia na nombia dinaana yee?’

english_verse: But I will tell them, “I will have nothing to do with you! Get out of my sight, you evil people!” Two Builders (Luke
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te maa toro yako wɔ nyiaa, ‘Mɔɔ gyeŋ ŋon ya, ɛ lee me siaman. Ɛmɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra!’ ” Denɛɛ maara bala Luka

english_verse: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and winds beat against that house. But it was built on solid rock, and so it did not fall.
translation_verse: Ɔga ŋɛɛwɔ te booneŋ wuuwɔ te feliŋ dinaa gyɔɔ deni keŋ, mɔna ka te yale ya. Nawolo nyi ɔ see ka mokɔɔ gyakaa boe dɔɔ.

english_verse: When Jesus finished speaking, the crowds were surprised at his teaching.
translation_verse: Yesu ke yako nombia kɛŋa taŋ nɛ, ɔ kawola keŋ doo balaŋ dikpii keŋ nɔɔ.

english_verse: He taught them like someone with authority, and not like their teachers of the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi ɔ wolawɔ ŋgba walaŋ ŋon ɔ dana doŋ nombia ŋan dɔɔ nɛ, be yɛɛ ŋgba mena keŋ ba mmaraa wolala baŋ na wolo nɛ ya.

english_verse: But the ones who should have been in the kingdom will be thrown out into the dark. They will cry and grit their teeth in pain.”
translation_verse: Te ɛmɛɛ Gyudatena baŋ nafɔ kaboena nyi yaa kyaa Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man nɛ, baa lese ŋon debɔɔ ka see ditiŋtɛɛ keŋ man. Botɔɔ te ɛ ke kyaŋ ɛ nyeeya wii nideli.

english_verse: That evening many people with demons in them were brought to Jesus. And with only a word he forced out the evil spirits and healed everyone who was sick.
translation_verse: Kelii balinɔɔ nɛ, ba moo balaŋ baŋ feliŋkumɛɛ doo be man nɛ kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ. Ɔ moo nombii dokoloŋ kooŋ bo gegi feliŋkumɛɛ ŋan pou, te ɔ kyɔɔ kaweesetena baŋ mɔ pou.

english_verse: Suddenly a man with leprosy came and knelt in front of Jesus. He said, “Lord, you have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
translation_verse: Keŋte akuŋtuŋte ŋolo kaa buŋawɔ ɔ nawɔɔ tɛɛ waase e nyiaa, “Me Gbeŋgyoo, akpaa n ne gyae na nyaŋ ke tale kyɔ maŋ me kawee.”

english_verse: Jesus put his hand on the man and said, “I want to! Now you are well.” At once the man's leprosy disappeared.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu tenee ɔ nyiŋmaa tina e te ɔ yako e nyiaa, “Mɛɛ gyae, n kawee dɛɛ taŋ.” Debokenaŋ man te balee ŋon kawee taŋawɔ.

english_verse: “I will go and heal him,” Jesus replied.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Maa kɔŋ na me kaa kyɔ e ɔ kawee.”

english_verse: When Jesus heard this, he was so surprised that he turned and said to the crowd following him, “I tell you in all of Israel I've never found anyone with this much faith!
translation_verse: Yesu ke nyii nɔɔwoya kɛŋa nɛ, doo ɔ nɔɔ te ɔ yako balaŋ dikpii keŋ be silana e nɛ nyiaa, “Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, Iserae tɛɛle kei dɔɔ pou nɛ ma te naa ta walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di ŋgba balee kei nɛ ya.”

english_verse: “Lord, my servant is at home in such terrible pain that he can't even move.”
translation_verse: “Me Gbeŋgyoo lɛɛ maŋ, nawolo nyi me tobaale te tekerii ɔ doo deni man ɔ ne naa diyem nideli.”

english_verse: Many people will come from everywhere to enjoy the feast in the kingdom of heaven with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
translation_verse: Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, balaŋ burum baa lee weese dɛɛlee na weese dɛɛyalae kɔŋ, na baŋ na Aberaham na Asiki na Gyekɔpo kaa kyaa Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man na baa di bom.

english_verse: Then Jesus said to the officer, “You may go home now. Your faith has made it happen.” Right then his servant was healed. Jesus Heals Many People (Mark
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako yoonɔɔtena kegyia ŋon nyiaa “Ta gyu dɛɛ, mena keŋ n be lɛɛ maŋ di nɛ dɔɔ gyae ke kɔŋ mena fa neŋ.” Debokenaŋ man te ɔ tobaale ŋon kawee taŋawɔ. Yesu kekyɔ balaŋ burum kaweese Maake

english_verse: Suddenly they shouted, “Jesus, Son of God, what do you want with us? Have you come to punish us before our time?”
translation_verse: Ba kena Yesu nɛ be fae yeesa nedoŋ nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ Bu, woŋ te n ne gyae de gyaŋ? N kɔŋae n kaa naase daa diyem pɛna debaŋ keŋ kelii?”

english_verse: Everyone in town came out to meet Jesus. When they saw him, they begged him to leave their part of the country.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ donɔɔ keŋ man balaŋ pou leewɔ kɔŋ kaa gyaŋee na Yesu. Ba kena e nɛ, be waase e nyi waa lee be tɛɛle dɔɔ.

english_verse: Jesus went to the home of Peter, where he found that Peter's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle te Yesu korowɔ gyu Pita dɛɛ. Ɔ ke gyu nɛ, na Pita wa awu doo ɔ na wee kpaŋalaŋ.

english_verse: He took her by the hand, and the fever left her. Then she got up and served Jesus a meal.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu kyaŋa e ɔ nyiŋmaa man te kpaŋalaŋ keŋ taŋawɔ. Keŋte ɔ korowɔ kɛɛ be dɔɔ.

english_verse: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said, “He healed our diseases and made us well.” Some Who Wanted To Go with Jesus (Luke
translation_verse: Ɔ ke yɛɛ kei yela nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya be yako see nɛ kɔŋawɔ ampaŋ. Yesaya yakowɔ nyiaa, “Ŋon lɛɛ na de kaweese pou, te ɔ moo de kaale mɔ seele.” Balaŋ baŋ be kaboena nyi baa sila Yesu Luka

english_verse: A teacher of the Law of Moses came up to him and said, “Teacher, I'll go anywhere with you!”
translation_verse: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ mmaraa wolale ŋolo kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ kaa yako e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, yenaŋ kamasɛ keŋ n ke gyu na, maa sila neŋ gyu.”

english_verse: Another disciple said to Jesus, “Lord, let me wait till I bury my father.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu silala baŋ man ŋolo mɔ yako e nyiaa, “Me gbeŋgyoo, yela maa taŋgbɛɛ gyu kewuu me kya pɛna me kaa sila neŋ.”

english_verse: so the disciples went over to him and woke him up. They said, “Lord, wake up! Save us before we drown!”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Yesu kaseela baŋ gyuuwɔ ke gyuusu e yako e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo lɛɛ daa, dɛɛ gyae dɛɛ munu.”

english_verse: After Jesus left in a boat with his disciples,
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu gyoowɔ degbele man, te ɔ kaseela baŋ mɔ gyoowɔ kpu na e.

english_verse: a terrible storm suddenly struck the lake, and waves started splashing into their boat. Jesus was sound asleep,
translation_verse: Karatetee dokoloŋ te feliŋ dinaa daale korowɔ ne gyɔɔ loŋ keŋ dɔɔ, te gyɔɔ loŋ do degbele keŋ man. Debaŋ kenaŋ na Yesu te doo dosoroŋ.

english_verse: The men in the boat were amazed and said, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him.” Two Men with Demons in Them (Mark
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ wose yeŋa wɔ te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, “Walaŋ woŋti yɛna kei, te feliŋ na loŋ koraŋ ne nyii e nɛ?” Yesu kekyɔ balaŋ baŋ feliŋkumɛɛ doo be man nɛ Maake

english_verse: After Jesus had crossed the lake, he came to shore near the town of Gadara and started down the road. Two men with demons in them came to him from the tombs. They were so fierce that no one could travel that way.
translation_verse: Yesu ka toŋ gyu boo keŋ diŋgyiŋ Gadaratena tɛɛle man nɛ, baala bala baale leewɔ amuseese man kɔŋ kaa gyaŋee na e. Na feliŋkumɛɛ doo balaŋ kɛwɔ man, te ba wose dana gyakoloŋ. Mena dɔɔ ŋolo bɛɛ tale mo gbɛɛ kenaŋ man ya.

english_verse: Not far from there a large herd of pigs was feeding.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ na, kekuiriise dikpii dinaa daale seŋɛɛ botɔɔ yenaŋ bee dii.

english_verse: So the demons begged Jesus, “If you force us out, please send us into those pigs!”
translation_verse: Keŋte feliŋkumɛɛ ŋan waase Yesu nyiaa, “Akpaa n ke gegi daa na, yela de kaa gyoo kekuiriise dikpii kei man.”

english_verse: Jesus told them to go, and they went out of the men and into the pigs. All at once the pigs rushed down the steep bank into the lake and drowned.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako feliŋkumɛɛ ŋan nyiaa, “Ɛ lee ta.” Mena dɔɔ feliŋkumɛɛ ŋan leewɔ balaŋ baŋ man gyu ka gyoo kekuiriise baŋ man, te be dikpii keŋ pou yeuwɔ tisi bula kyoloŋkyoloŋ keŋ gyu ka gyoo loŋ keŋ man te be munuwɔ pou.

english_verse: The people taking care of the pigs ran to the town and told everything, especially what had happened to the two men.
translation_verse: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ kɛɛ kekuiriise baŋ dɔɔ nɛ, yeuwɔ gyu donɔɔ man te be yako nombia ŋan be kɔŋ nɛ pou na kpene keŋ be kɔŋ balaŋ baŋ feliŋkumɛɛ doo be man dɔɔ nɛ fa balaŋ.

english_verse: Jesus got into a boat and crossed back over to the town where he lived.
translation_verse: Yesu gyoowɔ degbele man te ɔ toŋa boo keŋ gyu ŋon gbagba ɔ donɔɔ man.

english_verse: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined. New wine must be put into new wineskins. Both the skins and the wine will then be safe. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Mark
translation_verse: Mena mɔ te ŋolo bɛɛ mo soloŋ wɔle do wonembu tɔne ogele bene man ya. Ɔ yɛɛ mena na, soloŋ wɔle keŋ gyae ke yela wonembu tɔne ogele keŋ ke bɛɛ, na soloŋ keŋ ke leki na wonembu tɔne ogele keŋ ke wɔlɛɛ. Mena dɔɔ kaboena bo nyi baa mo soloŋ wɔle do wonembu tɔne ogele wɔle man, na ŋe popou be nyiŋ wɔlɛɛ ya.” Yesu kegyuusu alebubii ŋolo, na ɔ kekyɔ alo ŋon fatabo ne gyɔ e nɛ kawee Maake

english_verse: News about this spread all over that part of the country. Jesus Heals Two Blind Men
translation_verse: Mena nombia kɛŋa yaaseewɔ tɛɛle kenaŋ dɔɔ pou. Yesu kekyɔ kaweese

english_verse: Some teachers of the Law of Moses said to themselves, “Jesus must think he is God!”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ mmaraa wolala baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ ŋorokoteewɔ nyiaa, “Balee kei ne kolosi mmusuo nombia.”

english_verse: But Jesus knew what was in their minds, and he said, “Why are you thinking such evil things?
translation_verse: Na Yesu gyeŋ kpene keŋ bɛɛ gyueŋ ba nyeeya man nɛ te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Weera dɔɔ te ɛ na gyueŋ nombiakumɛɛ ɛ nyeeya man?

english_verse: But I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” So Jesus said to the man, “Get up! Pick up your mat and go on home.”
translation_verse: Mɔna mɛɛ gyae ɛ ka gyeŋ nyi, maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu dana doŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nyi maa mo dukum kyɛɛ.” Mena dɔɔ ɔ yako kaweete ŋon nyiaa, “Koro seŋ, gate n dofoŋoŋ na n ke ta gyu n dɛɛ.”

english_verse: The man got up and went home.
translation_verse: Keŋte balee ŋon korowɔ seŋ te ɔ laŋa ɔ dɛɛ.

english_verse: Go and learn what the Scriptures mean when they say, ‘Instead of offering sacrifices to me, I want you to be merciful to others.’ I didn't come to invite good people to be my followers. I came to invite sinners.” People Ask about Going without Eating (Mark
translation_verse: Mɔna ɛ gyu na ɛ kaa gyueŋ kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombii aŋmaraseŋ see kei na wolo nɛ man. Be ŋmarasewɔ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ kpa, ‘Na kedeesa te maŋ Wurubuarɛ ne gyae lee ɛ gyaŋ ya. Keŋ mɛɛ gyae yɛna nyi yaa wii dɔŋa waraŋase.’ Ma te kɔŋ nyi me kaa baake balaŋ baŋ be gbɛɛneŋ wulaa tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ ya, mɔna me kɔŋae me kaa baake nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra.” Be bɔɔse Yesu nɔɔkebake wose man nombia Maake

english_verse: As Jesus was leaving that place, two blind men began following him and shouting, “Son of David, have pity on us!”
translation_verse: Yesu ka lee botɔɔ ɔ ne gyu nɛ, siayɛlɛɛsatena bala baale sila e na bɛɛ fae yeesa baake nyiaa. “Gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii, wii de waraŋase.”

english_verse: As Jesus and his disciples were on their way, some people brought to him a man who could not talk because a demon was in him.
translation_verse: Be ka lee botɔɔ bee gyu nɛ, ba moo walaŋ ŋon feliŋkum doo ɔ man te ɔ bɛɛ tale kolosi ya nɛ kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ.

english_verse: As Jesus was leaving, he saw a tax collector named Matthew sitting at the place for paying taxes. Jesus said to him, “Follow me.” Matthew got up and went with him.
translation_verse: Yesu ka lee botɔɔ ɔ ne lam nɛ, ɔ naa balee ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Mateo nɛ, na ɔ kyaa ɔ lempoo dɛɛlɛe man. Te Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Koro sila maŋ” Keŋte Mateo korowɔ sila e.

english_verse: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Matthew's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners were also there.
translation_verse: Wee daale Yesu ke kyaa Mateo dɛɛ ɔ ne dii nɛ, lempoolɛɛra na nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra burum kɔŋawɔ kaa di bom na Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ.

english_verse: Some Pharisees asked Jesus' disciples, “Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and other sinners?”
translation_verse: Farasiitena baŋ kena mena nɛ be bɔɔse Yesu kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Weera dɔɔ te ɛ Gbeŋgyoo ŋon ne dii bom na lempoolɛɛra na nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra?”

english_verse: While Jesus was still speaking, an official came and knelt in front of him. The man said, “My daughter has just now died! Please come and place your hand on her. Then she will live again.”
translation_verse: Yesu kɛɛ kolosi nombia kɛŋa fa wɔ nɛ, Gyudatena ɔsom kegyia ŋolo kaa buŋawɔ ɔ nawɔɔ tɛɛ sola e nyiaa, “Me bu alebu ta yem debokeiman. Mɔna kɔŋ na n kaa mo n nyiŋmaa gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ, na waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ.”

english_verse: Jesus turned. He saw the woman and said, “Don't worry! You are now healed because of your faith.” At that moment she was healed.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu gɛɛsewɔ kɛɛ te ɔ naa alo ŋon te ɔ yako e nyiaa, “Me bu yela n wɔe ke fɛɛ neŋ. N kelɛɛ maŋ di dɔɔ te n nyiŋa kawee kekyɔ.” Debokenaŋ man te alo ŋon kawee taŋawɔ.

english_verse: After Jesus had forced the demon out, the man started talking. The crowds were so amazed they began saying, “Nothing like this has ever happened in Israel!”
translation_verse: Yesu ke gegi feliŋkum keŋ lee balee ŋon man nɛ, te ɔ kolosiwɔ. Balaŋ dikpii keŋ kena mena nɛ, nombia ŋan doo be nɔɔ te be yakowɔ nyiaa, “Da te naa ta mena nombia kɛŋa see Iserae tɛɛle kei dɔɔ ya.”

english_verse: Some followers of John the Baptist came and asked Jesus, “Why do we and the Pharisees often go without eating, while your disciples never do?”
translation_verse: Wee daale nɛ, Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon kaseela kɔŋawɔ kaa bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, “Weera dɔɔ te daa na Farasiitena ne bake de nɔɔ, te n kaseela baŋ dɔɔ baŋ bɛɛ bake be nɔɔ ya?”

english_verse: Jesus answered: The friends of a bridegroom aren't sad while he is still with them. But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Sena te balaŋ baŋ be kyaa alowɔletɛɛ nɛ baa wii walaŋsa keŋ alowɔle baale kyaa be gyaŋ? Mɔna debaŋ daale gyae kekɔŋ keŋ baa mo alowɔle baale ŋon lee be gyaŋ. Mena debaŋ kenaŋ man na baa bake be nɔɔ.

english_verse: No one uses a new piece of cloth to patch old clothes. The patch would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
translation_verse: Ŋolo bɛɛ kara kanyaŋ wɔle mo taa kakyeŋgbelaa keŋ ba yem nɛ ya. Akpaa ɔ yɛɛ mena na, kanyaŋ wɔle keŋ gyae ke wɔŋee bene keŋ tirii na bɔɔ keŋ ke wolose kpu.

english_verse: She had said to herself, “If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi alo ŋon yakowɔ ɔ nyeeman nyiaa, “Akpaa me nyiŋ tina ɔ kegba koraŋ na, me kawee gyae ke taŋ.”

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples got up and went with the man.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Yesu korowɔ sila e, te ɔ kaseela baŋ mɔ sila e gyu.

english_verse: A woman who had been bleeding for twelve years came up behind Jesus and barely touched his clothes.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ na te alo ŋolo ŋon fatabo ne gyɔ e kulutooneŋ kufu ala nɛ, moowɔ Yesu wɔle te ɔ moo ɔ nyiŋmaa tina Yesu kegba nɔɔbii.

english_verse: When Jesus went into the home of the official and saw the musicians and the crowd of mourners,
translation_verse: Yesu ke gyu ka gyoo Gyudatena ɔsom kegyia ŋon dɛɛ, te ɔ kena baŋ bɛɛ wola kefereese na baŋ bee wii lia ŋan tɛɛ nɛ,

english_verse: he said, “Get out of here! The little girl isn't dead. She is just asleep.” Everyone started laughing at Jesus.
translation_verse: ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ popou ɛ lee. Nawolo nyi alebu ŋon ta yeŋa ta ya, ɔ doo dosoroŋ bo.” Keŋte balaŋ baŋ pou boŋo Yesu.

english_verse: But after the crowd had been sent out of the house, Jesus went to the girl's bedside. He took her by the hand and helped her up.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ ka lee debɔɔ man nɛ, Yesu gyuuwɔ deni keŋ man woo ŋon doo nɛ, te ɔ kyaŋa e ɔ nyiŋmaa man te alebu ŋon foŋawɔ.

english_verse: After Jesus had gone indoors, the two blind men came up to him. He asked them, “Do you believe I can make you well?” “Yes, Lord,” they answered.
translation_verse: Yesu ke gyu ka gyoo dekpaŋalaŋ man nɛ, siayɛlɛɛsatena baŋ kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ ne lɛɛ maŋ di nyi maa tale gyuusu ɛ sia?” Keŋte be tiranɔɔ fa e nyi, “Ayi de Gbeŋgyoo.”

english_verse: Jesus touched their eyes and said, “Because of your faith, you will be healed.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu moo ɔ nyiŋmaa tina be sia te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Mena keŋ ɛ be lɛɛ di nɛ, dɛɛkɔŋ mena fa ŋon.” Keŋte be sia gyuusuwɔ.

english_verse: They were able to see, and Jesus strictly warned them not to tell anyone about him.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu faree wɔ nideli nyi, “Ɛ na yele ŋolo na ŋolo ke nyii nombia kɛŋa ya.”

english_verse: But they left and talked about him to everyone in that part of the country. Jesus Heals a Man Who Could Not Talk
translation_verse: Mɔna baŋ moo ɔ wose man nombia kpeŋkpeleŋ fa balaŋ tɛɛle kenaŋ pou dɔɔ.

english_verse: But the Pharisees said, “The leader of the demons gives him the power to force out demons.” Jesus Has Pity on People
translation_verse: Mɔna Farasiitena baŋ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Feliŋkumɛɛ pou gyoo ŋon doŋ yaa ɔ na mo gegi feliŋkumɛɛ nɛ.” Yesu kewii balaŋ waraŋase

english_verse: Jesus went to every town and village. He taught in their synagogues and preached the good news about God's kingdom. Jesus also healed every kind of disease and sickness.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tɛɛwɔ kila donɔɔse na akuraase ŋan pou man, na ɔ na wola wɔ Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man. Ɔ ne kolosi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ nombia kpaakpaa fa wɔ, te ɔ kyɔɔ kaweese kpookpoo kamasɛ mɔ.

english_verse: When he saw the crowds, he felt sorry for them. They were confused and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
translation_verse: Ɔ kena balaŋ dikpii keŋ nɛ, be nombia yɛɛ e waraŋase. Nawolo nyi bɛɛ yeyɛɛ te bɔɔ dana walaŋ ŋon waa lɛɛ wɔ ya. Be nombia yɛɛ ŋgba namense baŋ bɔɔ dana gbeŋgyoo ya nɛ.

english_verse: He said to his disciples, “A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔ yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Wɔɔman abɔɔ ŋan boe na kekɛrɛɛ, mɔna tomyɛɛra baŋ ta boo ya.

english_verse: Ask the Lord in charge of the harvest to send out workers to bring it in.”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛ fane fa wɔɔte ŋon, na waa lese balaŋ ke kɔŋ ɔ wɔɔ keŋ man kaa yɛɛ tom.”

english_verse: Philip, Bartholomew, Thomas, Matthew the tax collector, James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus were also apostles.
translation_verse: na Filipo na Batolomeo na Tomase na Mateo ŋon nafɔ ɔ yɛɛ lempoolɛɛre e nɛ, na Alfeose bu Gyemisi na Tadeose,

english_verse: But when someone arrests you, don't worry about what you will say or how you will say it. At that time you will be given the words to say.
translation_verse: Mɔna nyi akpaa be kyaŋ ŋon ka seŋ be siaman nɛ, ɛ na wɔlɛɛ na kpene keŋ ɛ ke yako, na mena keŋ ɛ ke yako ke ya. Debaŋ kenaŋ de lii nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa wola ŋon kpene keŋ ɛ ke yako.

english_verse: Go only to the people of Israel, because they are like a flock of lost sheep.
translation_verse: Mɔna ɛ taŋgbɛɛ gyu Iseraetena baŋ gyaŋ. Be yɛɛ ŋgba namense baŋ bɔɔ yoo nɛ.

english_verse: I am sending you like lambs into a pack of wolves. So be as wise as snakes and as innocent as doves.
translation_verse: Ɛ kɛɛ, mee kpila ŋon baa ŋgba namense nɛ, nyi yaa gyu gbougbouse man. Mena dɔɔ ɛ yela ɛ sia ke kara ŋgba doma nɛ, na ɛ ke yɔkɔse ɛ wose ŋgba awuluŋna nɛ.

english_verse: The others were Simon, known as the Eager One, and Judas Iscariot, who later betrayed Jesus. Instructions for the Twelve Apostles (Mark
translation_verse: na Simɔn ŋon ɔ ne gyae ɔ balaŋ nideli nɛ, na Gyudase Isekarɔte ŋon waa kaa lese Yesu fa nɛ.

english_verse: Jesus sent out the twelve apostles with these instructions: Stay away from the Gentiles and don't go to any Samaritan town.
translation_verse: Mena kpilala kufu bala kɛwɔ te Yesu kpilawɔ te ɔ faree wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ na gyu balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ gyaŋ ya, te ɛ na gyu Samariatena mɔ donɔɔ kamasɛ man ya.

english_verse: And don't carry a traveling bag or an extra shirt or sandals or a walking stick. Workers deserve their food.
translation_verse: Ɛ na mo akpo ya, te ɛ na mo kegba alate yaa nkyokota alate yaa gbutu alate tela ya. Nawolo nyi kaboena nyi baa tɔ tomyɛɛre kamasɛ kom.

english_verse: So when you go to a town or a village, find someone able and willing to have you as their guest and stay with them until you leave.
translation_verse: Donɔɔ yaa akuraa kamasɛ keŋ man ɛ gyoo nɛ, ɛ taŋgbɛɛ bɔɔse walaŋ ŋon ɔ kaboena te waa lɛɛ ŋon nɛ dɔɔ, na ɛ ke kyaa ɔ gyaŋ kaboena e ka mo lee donɔɔ kenaŋ man.

english_verse: I promise you the day of judgment will be easier for the towns of Sodom and Gomorrah than for that town. Warning about Trouble (Mark
translation_verse: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa di balaŋ nombia nɛ, waa naase mena balaŋ benaŋ diyem kela Sodom na Gomoratena diyem keŋ bɔɔ naa kaalaŋ nɛ.

english_verse: Brothers and sisters will betray one another and have each other put to death. Parents will betray their own children, and children will turn against their parents and have them killed.
translation_verse: Mena debaŋ kenaŋ nɛ, naa lolo bia baa lese dɔŋa fa yeŋ. Kya waa lese ɔ bu fa yeŋ, te bia mɔ baa koro seŋ ba lolala nyeeya dɔɔ na baa lese wɔ fa yeŋ.

english_verse: Watch out for people who will take you to court and have you beaten in their synagogues.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose man nideli lee balaŋ wose man. Nawolo nyi baa mo ŋon gyu kegyiise siaman na baa di ɛ nombia, na baa tuku ŋon bɔ ɔsom denɛɛ man.

english_verse: Because of me, you will be dragged before rulers and kings to tell them and the Gentiles about your faith.
translation_verse: Lee maŋ dɔɔ, baa mo ŋon gyu tɛɛle kegyiise na gyooneŋ siaman. Kei gyae ke yela ɛ ke nyiŋ gbɛɛ kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan fa wɔ, na baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ.

english_verse: But you will not really be the one speaking. The Spirit from your Father will tell you what to say.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi na ɛmɛɛ ne gyae la ɛ ke kolosi ya. Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ne gyae la waa ta ɛ dɔɔ kolosi.

english_verse: Everyone will hate you because of me. But if you remain faithful until the end, you will be saved.
translation_verse: Lee maŋ dɔɔ balaŋ pou baa kɔla ŋon. Mɔna walaŋ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ kakyeŋ seŋ keŋkeŋ ɔ kelɛɛdi man kelii ɔto nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa lɛɛ ɔ nyee.

english_verse: When people mistreat you in one town, hurry to another one. I promise you before you have gone to all the towns of Israel, the Son of Man will come.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa be naase ŋon diyem donɔɔ daale man na, ɛ yeu gyu donɔɔ wɔle man. Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, ɛ be gyae ɛ ke yɛɛ tom Iserae donɔɔse ŋan man pou taŋ, pɛte maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa bese kɔŋ ya.

english_verse: Students are not better than their teacher, and slaves are not better than their master.
translation_verse: Walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne kasee abɔɔ nɛ ɔ ba kela ŋon ɔ na wola e nɛ ya, te gbeŋi mɔ ba kela ɔ gbeŋgyoo ya.

english_verse: It is enough for students to be like their teacher and for slaves to be like their master. If people call the head of the family Satan, what will they say about the rest of the family? The One To Fear (Luke
translation_verse: Kaboena nyi kaseele waa yɛɛ ŋgba ɔ wolale nɛ, na gbeŋi mɔ ke yɛɛ ŋgba ɔ gbeŋgyoo nɛ. Te akpaa bɛɛ baake dekpaŋalaŋte ŋon nyi ŋon yɛna feliŋkumɛɛ gyoo Bɛlisɛbu na, woŋ dɔɔ te bɔɔ mo yela kumɛɛ gyakaa ɔ dekpaŋalaŋmantena baŋ dɔɔ ya? Walaŋ ŋon ɔ kaboena nyi yaa yee e Luka

english_verse: Don't be afraid of people. They can kill you, but they cannot harm your soul. Instead, you should fear God who can destroy both your body and your soul in hell.
translation_verse: Ɛ na yee balaŋ baŋ baa tale ko deniwalaŋ kaageŋ bo, mɔna bɔɔ tale yɛɛ kolo kpu bela nɛ ya. Mɔna ɛ yee Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ ko walaŋ taŋ na, ɔ dana doŋ nyi waa fuŋ e na ɔ woo pou ka do boalaŋ keŋ bɛɛ yem ya nɛ man.

english_verse: Aren't two sparrows sold for only a penny? But your Father knows when any one of them falls to the ground.
translation_verse: Akpelee koya yɛɛ aŋmoŋ? Na kaperɛ te bɛɛ yɔɔ akpeleese bala yee? Mɔna akpaa Wurubuarɛ te fa gbɛɛ ya na, be man dokoloŋ gba be gyae waa yala yeŋ ya.

english_verse: Even the hairs on your head are counted.
translation_verse: Ɛ nyeenyɔɔse koraŋ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ gyeŋ ŋe yaanɔɔle.

english_verse: So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Luke
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛ na yee ya, nawolo nyi ɛ dana tɔnɔɔ kela akpeleese burum.

english_verse: If you tell others you belong to me, I will tell my Father in heaven you are my followers.
translation_verse: Ɛ man walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ man do denibalaŋ siaman nyi me yɛɛ ɔ Gbeŋgyoo e nɛ, maŋ mɔ maa lɛɛ e do me kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ siaman.

english_verse: But if you reject me, I will tell my Father in heaven you don't belong to me. Not Peace, but Trouble (Luke
translation_verse: Mɔna walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ te lɛɛ maŋ do denibalaŋ siaman nyi me yɛɛ ɔ Gbeŋgyoo e ya nɛ, maŋ mɔ maa bɛɛ e me kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ siaman.

english_verse: I came to turn sons against their fathers, daughters against their mothers, and daughters-in-law against their mothers-in-law.
translation_verse: Bia baala baa koro seŋ tia be kyaɔ, na bia alebia mɔ ka koro seŋ tia be naɔ, na bia ala mɔ ka koro seŋ tia ba awuna.

english_verse: Your worst enemies will be in your own family.
translation_verse: Walaŋ dekpaŋalaŋ man balaŋ gbagba ne gyae la baa kɔla e.

english_verse: And unless you are willing to take up your cross and follow me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
translation_verse: Te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ta mo ɔ ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ ka seŋɛɛ fa kaale na yeŋ nɛ na waa sila maŋ ya na, ɔ tekaboena nyi waa yɛɛ me kaseele ya.

english_verse: If you try to save your life, you will lose it. But if you give it up for me, you will surely find it. Rewards (Mark
translation_verse: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ mo ŋon gbagba doŋ ɔ ne kɛo ɔ nyeedoŋ nɛ, ke gyae ka lee ɔ nyiŋmaa man. Mɔna walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ mo ɔ wose fa yeŋ lee maŋ dɔɔ nɛ, waa bese nyiŋ ɔ nyeedoŋ keŋ.

english_verse: Anyone who welcomes a prophet, just because that person is a prophet, will be given the same reward as a prophet. Anyone who welcomes a good person, just because that person is good, will be given the same reward as a good person.
translation_verse: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre, nyi keŋ ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre e dɔɔ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre kom te Wurubuarɛ waa mo tɔ walaŋ ŋonaŋ. Te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman nyi keŋ ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman dɔɔ nɛ, walaŋ ŋon ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ kom te Wurubuarɛ waa mo tɔ walaŋ ŋonaŋ.

english_verse: Anyone who welcomes you welcomes me. And anyone who welcomes me also welcomes the one who sent me.
translation_verse: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ ŋon na maŋ yaa ɔ lɛɛwɔ, te ŋon ɔ lɛɛ maŋ na Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ yaa ɔ lɛɛwɔ.

english_verse: And anyone who gives one of my most humble followers a cup of cool water, just because that person is my follower, will be rewarded.
translation_verse: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ fa balaŋ kɛwɔ man ŋolo loŋ defɛɛsa leenɔɔ tɔɔ nyi keŋ ɔ yɛɛ me kaseele e dɔɔ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa tɔ e kom.”

english_verse: As John's followers were going away, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What sort of person did you go out into the desert to see? Was he like tall grass blown about by the wind?
translation_verse: Gyɔn kpilala baŋ ka bese bee gyu nɛ, Yesu kolosi Gyɔn wose man nombia fa balaŋ dikpii keŋ te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ ke gyu Gyɔn gyaŋ depampaa dinaa keŋ man nɛ, woŋ te ɛ gyaewɔ nyi ɛ kena? Ŋale keŋ feliŋ ne gyɔ ke wosi nɛ?

english_verse: John the Baptist did not go around eating and drinking, and you said, “That man has a demon in him!”
translation_verse: Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon kɔŋawɔ na ɔ ne kyaa bake ɔ nɔɔ. Ɔ te nyɔɔ soloŋ kamasɛ ya te ɛ kpa nyi ‘Feliŋkumɛɛ doola ɔ man.’

english_verse: John was in prison when he heard what Christ was doing. So John sent some of his followers
translation_verse: Mena debaŋ kenaŋ na ba te kyaŋ Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre tɔ deni. Ɔ ke nyii kpene keŋ Yesu ne yɛɛ nɛ, ɔ kpila ɔ kaseela nyi

english_verse: to ask Jesus, “Are you the one we should be looking for? Or must we wait for someone else?”
translation_verse: baa gyu ke bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, “Nyaŋ yɛna walaŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi nyaŋ ke kɔŋ nɛ yaa, dɛɛ kɛɛ walaŋ wɔle gbɛɛ?”

english_verse: The blind are now able to see, and the lame can walk. People with leprosy are being healed, and the deaf can hear. The dead are raised to life, and the poor are hearing the good news.
translation_verse: Siayɛlɛɛsatena ne naa, ɔtakasese ne tɛɛ, baŋ bɛɛ wee kawee yayam nɛ kaweese te taŋ, te deŋela atɔɔsatena ne nyii. Woya ne foŋii, te bɛɛ yako Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan mɔ fa ayematena.

english_verse: God will bless everyone who doesn't reject me because of what I do.”
translation_verse: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ bɛɛ lɛɛ maŋ aŋmaareŋ nyi maŋ yɛna ŋon Wurubuarɛ be kpili ya nɛ, ŋon na nyeebam.”

english_verse: What kind of man did you go out to see? Was he someone dressed in fine clothes? People who dress like that live in the king's palace.
translation_verse: Akpaa na keŋ ya na, woŋ te ɛ gyuuwɔ ɛ kaa kɛɛ? Walaŋ ŋon ɔ doona abɔɔ kpaakpaa nyiŋan yaa? Baŋ bee mia mena nɛ, ne kyaa bo gyooneŋ dɛɛ.

english_verse: What did you really go out to see? Was he a prophet? He certainly was. I tell you that he was more than a prophet.
translation_verse: Ɛ yako maŋ maa nyii, woŋ te nafɔ ɛ gyuuwɔ nyi ɛ kaa kɛɛ? Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre? Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi, ŋon ɛ be naa nɛ gba kela Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre.

english_verse: I tell you no one ever born on this earth is greater than John the Baptist. But whoever is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than John.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ lolo tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, ŋolo be kyaa keŋ nyi ɔ kela Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ya. Mɔna Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man nɛ, walaŋ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ kemaasa e koraŋ nɛ, ɔ kela Gyɔn kei.

english_verse: From the time of John the Baptist until now, violent people have been trying to take over the kingdom of heaven by force.
translation_verse: Leenaŋ Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre debaŋ kaa lii na gyɛŋ kei nɛ, balaŋ na mo kale na doŋ nombia bo gyaŋee na Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ nombia ŋan, te balaŋ baŋ mɔ bɛɛ gyae ke nɛ, na mo siadoŋ bo baa gyoo ke man.

english_verse: And if you believe them, John is Elijah, the prophet you are waiting for.
translation_verse: Akpaa ɛ ke sɛɛ lɛɛ mena nombia kɛŋa di na, ŋon Gyɔn yɛna Elia ŋon Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre ŋolo be yako see nyi waa bese kɔŋ nɛ.

english_verse: “We played the flute, but you would not dance! We sang a funeral song, but you would not mourn!”
translation_verse: De kpee alowɔle nɔɔneŋse fa ŋon mɔna ɛ te sɔe ya! De kpee lia nɔɔneŋse mɔ fa ŋon, mɔna ɛ te wii ya.

english_verse: But the Son of Man goes around eating and drinking, and you say, “That man eats and drinks too much! He is even a friend of tax collectors and sinners.” Yet Wisdom is shown to be right by what it does. The Unbelieving Towns (Luke
translation_verse: Maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon kɔŋawɔ mee dii mɛɛ nyɔɔ, te ɛ kpa nyi, ‘Ɛ kɛɛ balee kei. Ɔ yɛɛ kedikumte na soloŋnyɔɔre e. Ɔ gyoona yɛna lempoolɛɛra na nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra.’ Mɔna walaŋ ŋon ɔ dana Wurubuarɛ nyansa nɛ, ɔ tom na wola la.” Donɔɔse ŋan man ŋe balaŋ te lɛɛ Yesu di ya nɛ Luka

english_verse: In the towns where Jesus had worked most of his miracles, the people refused to turn to God. So Jesus was upset with them and said:
translation_verse: Donɔɔse ŋan man Yesu be yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia nɛ, ŋe man balaŋ burum te lɛɛ e di na baa kyɛɛkee lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man, na baa kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ ya. Keŋte Yesu kolosiwɔ tia wɔ nyiaa,

english_verse: People of Capernaum, do you think you will be honored in heaven? You will go down to hell! If the miracles that took place in your town had happened in Sodom, it would still be standing.
translation_verse: Te nɛnɛɛ ɛmɛɛ Kapanumtena, ɛ gyeŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ waa kɛlɛɛ ŋon gyina ŋon adido? Koa, waa yɔkɔse ŋon, na waa mo ŋon do boalaŋ keŋ bɛɛ yem ya nɛ man, na ɛ kena diyem kekpaakekpaa. Nafɔ gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan mɔɔ yɛɛ ɛ man yaa meŋ yɛɛwɔ Sodom donɔɔ man nɛ, nafɔ gyɛŋ ke deŋ sɛɛ ke kyaa.

english_verse: So I tell you on the day of judgment the people of Sodom will get off easier than you. Come to Me and Rest (Luke
translation_verse: Mɔna mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyi wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa kaa di balaŋ pou nombia nɛ, waa wii Sodomtena waraŋase kela ŋon.” Yesu kpa balaŋ baa kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ na be kaa fɛɛfo Luka

english_verse: At that moment Jesus said: My Father, Lord of heaven and earth, I am grateful that you hid all this from wise and educated people and showed it to ordinary people.
translation_verse: Mena debaŋ kenaŋ man te Yesu fanewɔ nyiaa, “Me kya, adido na ateta pou Gbeŋgyoo, mɛɛ fa neŋ karaŋ nawolo nyi n ta mo abɔɔ kɛŋa weese nyansatena na baŋ ba gyeŋ nombia nɛ, te n lese ŋa wola balaŋ baŋ be dinɔɔ yɛɛ ŋgba bia nɛ.

english_verse: Yes, Father, this is what pleased you.
translation_verse: Taata, ma gyeŋ nyi kei yɛna nyaŋ gbagba n kegyaebii.”

english_verse: My Father has given me everything, and he is the only one who knows the Son. The only one who truly knows the Father is the Son. But the Son wants to tell others about the Father, so they can know him too.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako balaŋ baŋ nyiaa, “Me kya ta mo kpene kamasɛ do maŋ me nyiŋmaa man. Ŋolo ba gyeŋ maŋ ɔ bu ŋon ya, me kya waageŋ kooŋ gyeŋa na maŋ. Ŋolo mɔ ba gyeŋ kya ŋon ya, maŋ ɔ bu ŋon na baŋ maa lese me kya ŋon wola wɔ nɛ, baageŋ ne gyae la baa gyeŋ e.”

english_verse: If you are tired from carrying heavy burdens, come to me and I will give you rest.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako nyiaa, “Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ seelena kasolɔse yuŋnyiŋan te ne takaa ŋon nɛ, ɛ kɔŋ me gyaŋ na maa yela ɛ ke fɛɛfo.

english_verse: Take the yoke I give you. Put it on your shoulders and learn from me. I am gentle and humble, and you will find rest.
translation_verse: Ɛ kɔŋ na daa na ŋon ke yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ na ɛ ke kasee maŋ, na ɛ ke nyiŋ wosefɛɛreŋ. Nawolo nyi ma woɛ, te ma te yɔkɔse ma wose mɔ lee me konɔɔ man.

english_verse: Some Pharisees noticed this and said to Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do this!”
translation_verse: Farasiitena baŋ kena mena nɛ be yako Yesu nyiaa, “Kɛɛ! N kaseela ne yɛɛ kpene keŋ da mmaraa bɛɛ fa gbɛɛ nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ da kefɛɛfowee ya.”

english_verse: The crowds were so amazed they asked, “Could Jesus be the Son of David?”
translation_verse: Nombia kɛŋa doo balaŋ baŋ pou nɔɔ te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, “Na kei yɛna gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii ŋon baa yaa?”

english_verse: But when I force out demons by the power of God's Spirit, it proves that God's kingdom has already come to you.
translation_verse: Te akpaa Wurubuarɛ doŋ te mɛɛ mo gegi feliŋkumɛɛ na, kenaŋ na ɛ wulaa gyeŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ taŋ kekɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ.”

english_verse: Haven't you read in the Law of Moses that the priests are allowed to work in the temple on the Sabbath? But no one says they are guilty of breaking the law of the Sabbath.
translation_verse: Yaa ɛ ta bese ta kala lee Wurubuarɛ mmaraa tɔne keŋ man nyi, saese baŋ be kyaa Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man nɛ, deesiwɔ fa Wurubuarɛ kefɛɛfowee keŋ mɔna ŋolo te bu wɔ fɔɔ nyi ba te walaa ke yee?

english_verse: Don't you know what the Scriptures mean when they say, “Instead of offering sacrifices to me, I want you to be merciful to others?” If you knew what this means, you would not condemn these innocent disciples of mine.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ yakowɔ ɔ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see man nyiaa, ‘Mɛɛ gyae bo nyi yaa wii balaŋ waraŋase, na kedeesa te mɛɛ gyae ya. Nyi yeŋ nyii nombia kɛŋa man nɛ, nafɔ ɛ be gyae ɛ ke bu balaŋ baŋ be nombia dei nɛ fɔɔ ya.’

english_verse: Jesus left and went into one of their synagogues,
translation_verse: Yesu ka lee botɔɔ nɛ, ɔ gyuuwɔ Gyudatena ɔsom deni man.

english_verse: Then Jesus told the man, “Hold out your hand.” The man did, and it became as healthy as the other one.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako balee ŋon nyiaa, “Tenee n baam keŋ.” Keŋte balee ŋon mɔ tenee ke, te ɔ baam keŋ yɛɛ doŋ ŋgba alate keŋ nɛ.

english_verse: When Jesus found out what was happening, he left there and large crowds followed him. He healed all of their sick,
translation_verse: Yesu kena mena nɛ, ɔ wɔŋa ɔ wose lee yenaŋ. Balaŋ burum sila e te ɔ kyɔɔ be man kaweesetena baŋ pou,

english_verse: but warned them not to tell anyone about him.
translation_verse: mɔna ɔ faree wɔ nyi be na yako ɔ wose man nombia fa ŋolo na ŋolo ya.

english_verse: So God's promise came true, just as Isaiah the prophet had said,
translation_verse: Kei yele la Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan ɔ be tɛɛ ɔ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya dɔɔ yako nɛ kɔŋawɔ ampaŋ. Yesaya yakowɔ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ kpa

english_verse: “Here is my chosen servant! I love him, and he pleases me. I will give him my Spirit, and he will bring justice to the nations.
translation_verse: “Kei yɛna me tobaale ŋon mɔɔ lese e, ŋon te mɛɛ gyae te me sia na gye ɔ dɔɔ. Maa mo me feliŋ do ɔ man, na waa yako debiise pou nyi debaŋ keŋ maa lɛɛ ba nyee nɛ te lii.

english_verse: If you are not on my side, you are against me. If you don't gather in the harvest with me, you scatter it.
translation_verse: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ be kpuɛ na maŋ ya nɛ na ɔ ne kɔla maŋ, te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon mɔ ɔ bɛɛ kyɔ maŋ na dee yilaa balaŋ fa Wurubuarɛ ya nɛ, na ɔ ne yaasee wɔ bo.

english_verse: Some people brought to Jesus a man who was blind and could not talk because he had a demon in him. Jesus healed the man, and then he was able to talk and see.
translation_verse: Wee daale ba moo walaŋ ŋon feliŋkum doo ɔ man, te ɔ sia be yɛlɛɛ te ɔ bɛɛ tale kolosi mɔ ya nɛ kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ. Keŋte Yesu kyɔɔ e te ɔ kolosiwɔ te ɔ sia mɔ gyuusuwɔ.

english_verse: Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he said to them: Any kingdom where people fight each other will end up ruined. And a town or family that fights will soon destroy itself.
translation_verse: Mɔna Yesu gyeŋ kpene keŋ bɛɛ gyueŋ nɛ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Tɛɛle kamasɛ keŋ ke man balaŋ de kpase ba wose kpookpoo bee yoo na dɔŋa na, tɛɛle kenaŋ be gyae ke de ya. Mena mɔ te donɔɔ yaa dekpaŋalaŋ daale man balaŋ de kpase ba wose kpookpoo bee yoo na dɔŋa na, dekpaŋalaŋ kenaŋ ne yɛlɛɛ bo.

english_verse: If I use the power of Beelzebul to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? Your followers are the ones who will judge you.
translation_verse: Akpaa nyi Bɛlisɛbu doŋ te mɛɛ mo gegi feliŋkumɛɛ na, amɔte doŋ yaa ɛ balaŋ baŋ mɔ na mo gegi feliŋkumɛɛ? Mena na ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ balaŋ ne gyae la baa bu ŋon fɔɔ.

english_verse: When an evil spirit leaves a person, it travels through the desert, looking for a place to rest. But when the demon doesn't find a place,
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Feliŋkum da lee walaŋ man nɛ ɔ ne tɛɛ kila kɛo tɛɛle awolɛɛ ɔ ne gyae dekyae, mɔna ɔ bee nyim ya.

english_verse: it goes off and finds seven other evil spirits even worse than itself. They all come and make their home there, and the person ends up in worse shape than before. That's how it will be with you evil people of today. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Mark
