translation_verse: ɔ na bese gyu ka mo feliŋkumɛɛ nyetooro, ŋan ŋe dana nombiakumɛɛ kela e nɛ kpu na ɔ wose na be kaa kyaa walaŋ ŋonaŋ man. Dekɔŋ mena na, walaŋ ŋonaŋ dinɔɔ na bese wɔlɛɛ kela ɔ kekyaa gyaŋgba keŋ. Mena ne gyae la ke kɔŋ gyɛŋ wee kei balaŋ kumɛɛ dɔɔ.” Yesu ɔ naa na ɔ naabia Maake

english_verse: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit. You can tell what a tree is like by the fruit it produces.
translation_verse: N kɛɛ daŋ dɔɔ nideli te ke de nyiŋ doŋ nɛ, kɛɛ se bia kpaakpaa. Te akpaa n te kɛɛ ke dɔɔ nideli ya te kawee de kyaŋ ke na, kɛɛ se bia kumɛɛ bo. Nawolo nyi daŋ kamasɛ nɛ, ke bia te bɛɛ mo gyeŋ ke dinɔɔ.

english_verse: You are a bunch of evil snakes, so how can you say anything good? Your words show what is in your hearts.
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ dana nombiakumɛɛ ŋgba doma nɛ, sena te ɛmɛɛ balaŋ kumɛɛ kɛwɔ ɛ ke tale kolosi nombia kpaakpaa? Nawolo nyi kpene kamasɛ keŋ be wulu walaŋ konɔɔ man nɛ, keŋ na lee la ɔ nɔɔman.

english_verse: On that day they will be told they are either innocent or guilty because of the things they have said. A Sign from Heaven (Mark
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ nɔɔwoya te baa mo di ɔ nombia, na baa naa nyi ɔ dii deŋele, yaa ɔ dii fɔɔ.” Bɛɛ gyae nyi Yesu waa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia Maake

english_verse: Some Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses said, “Teacher, we want you to show us a sign from heaven.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Farasiitena na mmaraa wolala baŋ baale yako Yesu nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, dɛɛ gyae n ke yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia wola daa.”

english_verse: But Jesus replied: You want a sign because you are evil and won't believe! But the only sign you will get is the sign of the prophet Jonah.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛmɛɛ gyɛŋ wee kei balaŋ, ɛ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛrawɔ te ɛ be yee Wurubuarɛ ya. Ɛ ne gyae nyi maa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia na ɛ kena, mɔna mɔɔ gyae maa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombii kamasɛ kpu na keŋ be kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Gyona dɔɔ nɛ ya.

english_verse: He was in the stomach of a big fish for three days and nights, just as the Son of Man will be deep in the earth for three days and nights.
translation_verse: Mena keŋ Gyona be doo kpebii dinaa ŋon tɔɔman weeya atooro nelim na ka weese nɛ, mena te maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon mɔ maa doo tɛɛle tɔɔman weeya atooro nelim na ka weese.

english_verse: On the day of judgment the people of Nineveh will stand there with you and condemn you. They turned to God when Jonah preached, and yet here is something far greater than Jonah.
translation_verse: Wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa di balaŋ nombia nɛ, Niniwetena baa koro seŋ na baa bu gyɛŋ wee kei balaŋ fɔɔ, nawolo nyi debaŋ keŋ Gyona be kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan fa wɔ nɛ, be kyɛɛkeewɔ lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man te be kɔŋawɔ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ. Mɛɛ yako ŋon baa nyiaa, walaŋ ŋon ɔ kela Gyona nɛ kyaa kɛbo.

english_verse: The Queen of the South will also stand there with you and condemn you. She traveled a long way to hear Solomon's wisdom, and yet here is something much greater than Solomon. Return of an Evil Spirit (Luke
translation_verse: Mena mɔ te wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa di balaŋ nombia nɛ, Seba gyoo ŋon ɔ yɛɛ alo e te ɔ ba lee ata yenaŋ nɛ, waa koro seŋ na waa bu gyɛŋ wee kei balaŋ fɔɔ. Nawolo nyi ɔ ke nyii Solomɔn nyansa nombia ŋan nɛ, ɔ tɛɛwɔ leenaŋ tɛɛle ɔto kɔŋ kaa nyii ŋa. Mɛɛ yako ŋon baa nyiaa, walaŋ ŋon ɔ kela gyoo Solomɔn ŋonaŋ nɛ kyaa kɛbo.” Feliŋkumɛɛ kɛɛ bese kɔŋ Luka

english_verse: it says, “I will go back to the home I left.” When it gets there and finds the place empty, clean, and neat,
translation_verse: Akpaa ɔ te nyiŋ dekyae ya na ɔ ne yako ɔ wose nyiaa, maa bese gyu ma dekyae bene keŋ mɔɔ lee nɛ man. Akpaa ɔ kɔŋ kaa naa nyi ba te gbee botɔɔ desina ke nideli keŋ nyi ayimɛɛ kamasɛ be doo ya nɛ,

english_verse: While Jesus was still speaking to the crowds, his mother and brothers came and stood outside because they wanted to talk with him.
translation_verse: Yesu ke gyakaa ɔ ne kolosi fa balaŋ baŋ nɛ, ɔ naa na ɔ naabia kɔŋawɔ kaa seŋ debɔɔ man keŋ bɛɛ gyae gbɛɛ baa kolosi na e.

english_verse: Someone told Jesus, “Your mother and brothers are standing outside and want to talk with you.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ŋolo yako Yesu nyiaa, “N naa na n naabia seŋɛɛ debɔɔ man bɛɛ gyae baa naa neŋ.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Amɔte yɛna me naa, te bomɔ yɛna me naabia?”

english_verse: Then he pointed to his disciples and said, “These are my mother and my brothers!
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ tenee ɔ nyiŋmaa wola ɔ kaseela baŋ te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Kɛwɔ yɛna me naa na me naabia.

english_verse: Anyone who obeys my Father in heaven is my brother or sister or mother.”
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne yɛɛ me kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ kegyaebii na, ŋon yɛna me naabu na me deesi na me naa.”

english_verse: Such large crowds gathered around him that he had to sit in a boat, while the people stood on the shore.
translation_verse: Keŋte balaŋ burum kaa yilaawɔ kilisi e. Mena dɔɔ ɔ gyoowɔ ke kyaa degbele man, na balaŋ baŋ mɔ pou seŋɛɛ dekeŋkeŋ dɔɔ.

english_verse: Then he taught them many things by using stories. He said: A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔ yako wɔ nombia burum aduya man nyiaa, “Wɔɔfarale ŋolo gyuuwɔ ke ŋmatee abɔɔ bia ŋgba amɔla nɛ ɔ wɔɔman.

english_verse: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
translation_verse: Ŋaale mɔ yalawɔ kpanaŋse dɔɔ te ŋe tuuwɔ, mɔna tɛɛle ta boo botɔɔ ya.

english_verse: Jesus' disciples came to him and asked, “Why do you use stories to speak to the people?”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu kaseela baŋ kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ kaa bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Weera n ne kolosi fa balaŋ baŋ aduya man?”

english_verse: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said, “These people will listen and listen, but never understand. They will look and look, but never see.
translation_verse: Nombia akɔŋaŋ balaŋ kɛwɔ dɔɔ yela Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya nombia ŋan ɔ be yako see nɛ kɔŋawɔ ampaŋ. Yesaya yakowɔ nyiaa, “Balaŋ kɛwɔ baa tei tei, mɔna bɔɔ gyae baa nyii nombia ŋenaŋ man ya. Te baa kɛo kɛo, mɔna bɔɔ gyae baa naa kolo ya.

english_verse: Many prophets and good people were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear. Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Mark
translation_verse: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra na Wurubuarɛ balaŋ baŋ be gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ ɔ siaman baŋ bɔɔ kyaa kaalaŋ nɛ, gyaewɔ nyi baa naa kpene keŋ ɛ ne naa nɛ, mɔna ba te naa ya. Te be gyaewɔ nyi baa nyii kpene keŋ ɛ ne nyii nɛ, mɔna be te nyii ya.”

english_verse: The seeds that fell on rocky ground are the people who gladly hear the message and accept it at once.
translation_verse: Bia ŋan be yale kpanaŋse dɔɔ nɛ, seŋɛɛ fa balaŋ baŋ bee nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan, te bɛɛ lɛɛ ŋa na gyoŋ debokenaŋ man nɛ.

english_verse: The seeds that fell among the thornbushes are also people who hear the message. But they start worrying about the needs of this life and are fooled by the desire to get rich. So the message gets choked out, and they never produce anything.
translation_verse: Bia ŋan be yale kpalamse man nɛ, seŋɛɛ fa balaŋ baŋ bee nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan, te tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nombia, na kɔba kegyae beiŋ, na tɛɛle kei dɔɔ abɔɔ akaŋ ŋan kegyae da gyoo be man nɛ, na bee yeli baa bee Kristo man ya.

english_verse: Jesus then told them this story: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a farmer scattered good seed in a field.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ gyɔ dudu wɔle fa wɔ nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ ŋgba mena keŋ balee ŋolo be gyu ke ŋmatee abɔɔ bia kpaakpaa nyiŋan ɔ wɔɔman nɛ.

english_verse: But while everyone was sleeping, an enemy came and scattered weed seeds in the field and then left.
translation_verse: Mɔna nelim balaŋ pou kedokii nɛ, ɔ kɔlare kɔŋawɔ kaa ŋmatee ŋalese kumɛɛ bom na abɔɔ bia ŋan te ɔ laŋawɔ.

english_verse: When the plants came up and began to mature, the farmer's servants could see the weeds.
translation_verse: Abɔɔ bia ŋan ketu se bia nɛ, te ŋalese kumɛɛ ŋan mɔ lese ŋa wose debɔɔ.”

english_verse: The servants came and asked, “Sir, didn't you scatter good seed in your field? Where did these weeds come from?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te wɔɔte ŋon tewulɛɛ kɔŋawɔ kaa bɔɔse e nyiaa, “De gbeŋgyoo, na abɔɔ bia kpaakpaa nyiŋan te n ŋmateewɔ n wɔɔ keŋ man yee? Te aleŋ yaa ŋalese kumɛɛ kɛŋa leewɔ?”

english_verse: “An enemy did this,” he replied. His servants then asked, “Do you want us to go out and pull up the weeds?”
translation_verse: Keŋte balee ŋon yakowɔ nyiaa, “Me kɔlare ŋolo kaa yɛɛ laa mena nɛ.” Botɔɔ te ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ bɔɔse e nyiaa, “N ne gyae nyi dee gyu ke kulii ŋalese kumɛɛ ŋan?”

english_verse: Although it is the smallest of all seeds, it grows larger than any garden plant and becomes a tree. Birds even come and nest on its branches.
translation_verse: Daŋbii kei yɛna bii peperee daasebia pou man. Mɔna nyi akpaa ke da bee na, kɛɛ bee bese daŋ dinaa keŋ nyi gyebuse ne kɔŋ kaa loo ba asaase do ke tombia man.”

english_verse: Jesus told them another story: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when a farmer plants a mustard seed in a field.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ gyɔ dudu wɔle fa wɔ nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ ŋgba daŋ daale bii peperee keŋ ke yɛɛ ŋgba kpeli bii, keŋ balee ŋolo ba mo ke duu ɔ wɔɔman nɛ.

english_verse: Jesus used stories when he spoke to the people. In fact, he did not tell them anything without using stories.
translation_verse: Aduya man te Yesu kolosiwɔ fa balaŋ dikpii keŋ pou. Ɔ te kolosi nombii daale fa ŋolo keŋ ɔ te gyɔ dudu ya.

english_verse: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “I will use stories to speak my message and to explain things hidden since the creation of the world.” Jesus Explains the Story about the Weeds
translation_verse: Kei yele la Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan ɔ be tɛɛ ɔ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya dɔɔ yako see nɛ kɔŋawɔ ampaŋ. Ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Aduya man te maa kolosi fa wɔ na maa yako wɔ nombia aweeseŋ ŋan bɔɔ gyeŋ ya, leenaŋ tɛɛle kewalaŋ tɛɛ nɛ.” Yesu kalese ŋalese kumɛɛ dudu keŋ man

english_verse: After Jesus left the crowd and went inside, his disciples came to him and said, “Explain to us the story about the weeds in the wheat field.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu lɔɔ balaŋ dikpii keŋ gbɛɛ te ɔ gyuuwɔ dekpaŋalaŋ man. Ɔ kaseela baŋ kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ te be yako e nyiaa, “Wola daa dudu keŋ n be gyɔɔ lee ŋalese kumɛɛ ŋan ŋɔɔ tuu wɔɔ keŋ man nɛ asɛɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus answered: The one who scattered the good seed is the Son of Man.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Walaŋ ŋon ɔ be duu abɔɔ bia kpaakpaa ŋan nɛ yɛna maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon,

english_verse: The field is the world, and the good seeds are the people who belong to the kingdom. The weeds are those who belong to the evil one,
translation_verse: wɔɔ keŋ yɛna tɛɛle kei pou. Abɔɔ bia kpaakpaa ŋan yɛna Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man bia baŋ. Ŋalese kumɛɛ ŋan yɛna ɔbɔnsam ŋon bia.

english_verse: and the one who scattered them is the devil. The harvest is the end of time, and angels are the ones who bring in the harvest.
translation_verse: Te walaŋkum ŋon ɔ be duu ŋalese kumɛɛ ŋan yɛna ɔbɔnsam ŋon. Kekɛrɛɛ tɛɛ keŋ yɛna debaŋ ɔtomante keŋ. Baŋ baa kɛrɛɛ nɛ yɛna Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ.

english_verse: Weeds are gathered and burned. That's how it will be at the end of time.
translation_verse: Mena keŋ bɔɔ yilaa ŋalese kumɛɛ ŋan te bɔɔ too ŋa nɛ, mena ne gyae la ke kɔŋ debaŋ ɔtomante keŋ man.

english_verse: The Son of Man will send out his angels, and they will gather from his kingdom everyone who does wrong or causes others to sin.
translation_verse: Maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa kpila me kpilala lee adido na be kaa yilaa kpene kamasɛ keŋ ne yeli walaŋ ne yɛɛ dukum nɛ, na nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra pou lee me gyoori keŋ man.

english_verse: Then he will throw them into a flaming furnace, where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ baa fuŋ balaŋ kumɛɛ baŋ do boalaŋ degyapaa keŋ man. Botɔɔ te baa fae yeesa na baa tao be kela.

english_verse: But everyone who has done right will shine like the sun in their Father's kingdom. If you have ears, pay attention! A Hidden Treasure
translation_verse: Mɔna baŋ be gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ, baa ŋalakee ŋgba weese nɛ be kya ŋon gyoori keŋ man. Baŋ be dana deŋela anenyiiŋ dei na, baa tei nideli.” Yesu kekolosi lee Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ wose man

english_verse: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when someone finds a treasure hidden in a field and buries it again. Such a person is happy and goes and sells everything in order to buy that field. A Valuable Pearl
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ ŋgba kolo kpaakpaate keŋ ke dana koya doŋ te bɔɔ mo weese tɛɛle daale man nɛ. Balee ŋolo ke gyu kena ke nɛ, te ɔ desina ke weese nideli. Ke yɛɛ e gyoŋ gbaa mena dɔɔ ɔ gyuuwɔ ka mo kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɔ dana nɛ yɔɔ, te ɔ moowɔ ke lɛɛ tɛɛle kenaŋ.”

english_verse: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when a shop owner is looking for fine pearls.
translation_verse: Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ ŋgba kebudiiri ŋon ɔ dɔŋ ɔ ne gyae kegyaŋase kpaakpaa nyiŋan waa lɛɛ nɛ.

english_verse: After finding a very valuable one, the owner goes and sells everything in order to buy that pearl. A Fish Net
translation_verse: Ɔ ke gyu ke nyiŋ kegyaŋa kpaakpaate nɛ, ɔ gyuuwɔ ke yɔɔ kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɔ dana nɛ, te ɔ moowɔ ke lɛɛ ke.”

english_verse: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when a net is thrown into a lake and catches all kinds of fish.
translation_verse: Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ ŋgba asawu lɔɔra baŋ bɔɔ fuŋ ba asawu do loŋ man te bɔɔ kyaŋ kpebia kpookpoo nɛ.

english_verse: When the net is full, it is dragged to the shore, and the fishermen sit down to separate the fish. They keep the good ones, but throw the bad ones away.
translation_verse: Ba kena nyi asawu keŋ te wulu nɛ, be wɔŋee ke kɔŋ dekeŋkeŋ dɔɔ. Be tisiwɔ kyaa te be tɔɔsee kpebia kpaakpaa nyibaŋ do be doŋase man, te be fuŋii akumɛɛ baŋ pou leki.

english_verse: That's how it will be at the end of time. Angels will come and separate the evil people from the ones who have done right.
translation_verse: Mena ne gyae la ke kɔŋ wee ɔtomante keŋ man. Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ baa kɔŋ kaa lese balaŋ kumɛɛ baŋ lee balaŋ kpaakpaa baŋ man.

english_verse: Then those evil people will be thrown into a flaming furnace, where they will cry and grit their teeth in pain. New and Old Treasures
translation_verse: Na baa fuŋ balaŋ kumɛɛ baŋ do boalaŋ keŋ ke bɛɛ yem ya nɛ man. Botɔɔ te baa fae yeesa na baa tao be kela.”

english_verse: Jesus asked his disciples if they understood all these things. They said, “Yes, we do.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ nyii nombia kɛŋa pou asɛɛ?” Te be sɛɛwɔ nyi “Ayi.”

english_verse: So he told them, “Every student of the Scriptures who becomes a disciple in the kingdom of heaven is like someone who brings out new and old treasures from the storeroom.” The People of Nazareth Turn against Jesus (Mark
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Nombii kei nawolo baa nyiaa, mmaraa wolale kamasɛ ŋon ɔ be kasee Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ nombia gyeŋ ŋa nɛ, ɔ yɛɛ ŋgba dekpaŋalaŋ kegyia ŋon ɔ ne kyaa lese wa abɔɔ wɔlɛɛ na benɛɛ lee wa abɔɔ dɛɛsee man nɛ.” Ba ke bɛɛ Yesu Nasarɛte donɔɔ man Maake

english_verse: When Jesus had finished telling these stories, he left
translation_verse: Yesu ke gyɔ aduya kɛŋa taŋ nɛ, ɔ leewɔ botɔɔ laŋ.

english_verse: and went to his hometown. He taught in their synagogue, and the people were so amazed that they asked, “Where does he get all this wisdom and the power to work these miracles?
translation_verse: Ɔ ke gyu ka gyoo ɔ dɛɛ nɛ, ɔ gyuuwɔ ka wola wɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia bɔ ɔsom deni man, te ɔ kawola keŋ doo be popou nɔɔ, keŋte be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, “Aleŋ yaa ɔ nyiŋa nombia kagyeŋ kei, na doŋ keŋ ɔ na mo yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia na nombia dinaana kɛŋa lee?”

english_verse: Isn't he the son of the carpenter? Isn't Mary his mother, and aren't James, Joseph, Simon, and Judas his brothers?
translation_verse: Na kapenta ŋon bu yaa yee? Na ɔ naa yɛna Mɛɛre yee, na ɔ naabia yɛna Gyemisi na Gyosɛfo na Simɔn na Gyuda yee?

english_verse: Don't his sisters still live here in our town? How can he do all this?”
translation_verse: Na daa na ɔ desina kyaa la kɛbo yee? Aleŋ kitii yaa ɔ nyiŋa mena doŋ kei lee?

english_verse: So the people were upset because of what he was doing. But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their own family.”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ba te lɛɛ e ya. Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Balaŋ ne bu Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre yenaŋ kamasɛ, mɔna ŋon gbagba ɔ donɔɔ man balaŋ, na ɔ dekpaŋalaŋmantena bee buu na e ya.”

english_verse: And because the people did not have any faith, Jesus did not work many miracles there.
translation_verse: Te keŋ ba te lɛɛ Yesu di dɔɔ ya nɛ, ɔ te yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia burum ɔ dɛɛ botɔɔ ya.

english_verse: When Jesus got out of the boat, he saw the large crowd. He felt sorry for them and healed everyone who was sick.
translation_verse: Yesu ke tisi lee degbele keŋ man, te ɔ kena balaŋ dikpii keŋ nɛ, be nombia yɛɛ e waraŋase te ɔ kyɔɔ kaweesetena baŋ pou bɔɔ sila wɔ gyu nɛ.

english_verse: “Come on!” Jesus said. Peter then got out of the boat and started walking on the water toward him.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Kɔŋ” te Pita leewɔ degbele keŋ man ta loŋ keŋ dɔɔ ɔ ne gyu Yesu gyaŋ.

english_verse: and told his officials, “This is John the Baptist! He has come back from death, and that's why he has the power to work these miracles.”
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ yako ɔ tewulɛɛ nyiaa, gyae ke yɛɛ Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon bese la foŋ lee yeŋ man kɔŋ nɛ. Mena dɔɔ te ɔ ne tale yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia nɛ.

english_verse: Herod had earlier arrested John and had him chained and put in prison. He did this because John had told him, “It isn't right for you to take Herodias, the wife of your brother Philip.”
translation_verse: Kpene keŋ dɔɔ gyoo Herode be yɛɛ mena yɛna nyi, Gyɔn yako e nyi gbɛɛ be doo nyi waa mo ɔ kegyia Filipo wɛɛle Herodia yala ya.

english_verse: But the girl's mother told her to say, “Here on a serving plate I want the head of John the Baptist!”
translation_verse: Keŋte alebu ŋon ɔ naa feefuwɔ do ɔ bu nyi waa yako nyiaa, “Mɛɛ gyae n ke kara Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre nyee do tasa taŋtaŋalaŋ man fa maŋ debokeiman.”

english_verse: It was taken on a serving plate to the girl, and she gave it to her mother.
translation_verse: Keŋte ba moo Gyɔn nyee keŋ do tasa taŋtaŋalaŋ man kɔna alebu ŋon, te ɔ moo ke gyina ɔ naa.

english_verse: But when Peter saw how strong the wind was, he was afraid and started sinking. “Save me, Lord!” he shouted.
translation_verse: Mɔna Pita kena mena keŋ feliŋ keŋ ne gyɔɔ loŋ keŋ dɔɔ nɛ, gyakoloŋ gyoo e te ɔ fiasɛɛ ɔ na gyoo loŋ keŋ man. Keŋte ɔ fae yeesa nyiaa, “Me Gbeŋgyoo, lɛɛ maŋ!”

english_verse: But they said, “We have only five small loaves of bread and two fish.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ yako e nyiaa, “Bodobodose abiliŋ anoŋ na kpebia bala pɛ te de dana.”

english_verse: After Jesus heard about John, he crossed Lake Galilee to go to some place where he could be alone. But the crowds found out and followed him on foot from the towns.
translation_verse: Yesu ke nyii Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre yeŋ nɛ, ɔ gyoowɔ debgele man laŋ yenaŋ daale gyu ke kyaa waageŋ kooŋ. Balaŋ dikpii keŋ ke nyii nyi ɔ te lam nɛ, te ba leewɔ be donɔɔse man, mo na be nawɔɔ sila e.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “They don't have to leave. Why don't you give them something to eat?”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Tekaboena nyi baa ta ya. Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ gyae kolo fa wɔ na baa di.”

english_verse: Jesus asked his disciples to bring the food to him,
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ mo ŋa kɔna maŋ.”

english_verse: and he told the crowd to sit down on the grass. Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples, and they gave it to the people.
translation_verse: Te ɔ yela balaŋ baŋ tisiwɔ kyakee tɛɛle man. Keŋte Yesu moo bodobodose anoŋ na kpebia bala baŋ, te ɔ gyinaa ɔ nyee adido te ɔ fanewɔ do Wurubuarɛ nɔɔ, te ɔ kɛrɛɛ ŋa te ɔ moo ŋa fa ɔ kpilala baŋ, te baŋ mɔ moowɔ kpɛlɛɛ balaŋ baŋ.

english_verse: After everyone had eaten all they wanted, Jesus' disciples picked up twelve large baskets of leftovers.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ pou diiwɔ baa, te be tɔɔsee weenɛɛ buruburu akaŋ ŋan na, be nyiŋa doŋase kufu ala (

english_verse: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into a boat and start back across the lake. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
translation_verse: Debokenaŋ man te Yesu yela ɔ kaseela baŋ gyoowɔ degbele man kyaŋ gbɛɛ gyu boo keŋ diŋgyiŋ na waa nyiŋ lɔ balaŋ baŋ gbɛɛ.

english_verse: Then he went up on a mountain where he could be alone and pray. Later in the evening, he was still there.
translation_verse: Yesu ke lɔ balaŋ baŋ gbɛɛ taŋ nɛ, ɔ gyuuwɔ bula dɔɔ waageŋ ɔ kaa fane. Kelii balinɔɔ nɛ, na Yesu waageŋ kaa la botɔɔ.

english_verse: By this time the boat was a long way from the shore. It was going against the wind and was being tossed around by the waves.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ na degbele keŋ te lii lem, na feliŋ dinaa daale mɔ ne gyɔɔ degbele keŋ wosi loŋ keŋ dɔɔ.

english_verse: A little while before morning, Jesus came walking on the water toward his disciples.
translation_verse: Tɛɛ kɛɛ bee kena nɛ, Yesu tɛɛwɔ loŋ keŋ dɔɔ ɔ ne gyu ɔ kaseela baŋ gyaŋ.

english_verse: At once, Jesus said to them, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
translation_verse: Debokenaŋ man te Yesu kolosiwɔ fa wɔ nyi, “Ɛ nyiŋ konɔɔ, ɛ na yee ya, maŋ e.”

english_verse: Peter replied, “Lord, if it really is you, tell me to come to you on the water.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Pita yako e nyiaa, “Me Gbeŋgyoo, akpaa nyaŋ e na, yela maa ta loŋ keŋ dɔɔ kɔŋ n gyaŋ.”

english_verse: At once, Jesus reached out his hand. He helped Peter up and said, “You surely don't have much faith. Why do you doubt?”
translation_verse: Debokenaŋ man te Yesu tenee ɔ nyiŋmaa kyaŋ e te ɔ yako e nyiaa, “Baale, n kelɛɛdi ta boo ya. Weera dɔɔ te n lɛɛ maŋ aŋmaareŋ n konɔɔ man?”

english_verse: When Jesus and Peter got into the boat, the wind died down.
translation_verse: Yesu na Pita ka gyeŋ gyoo degbele keŋ man nɛ, te feliŋ keŋ tinaa kegyɔ yela.

english_verse: The men in the boat worshiped Jesus and said, “You really are the Son of God!” Jesus Heals Sick People in Gennesaret (Mark
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te baŋ be doo degbele keŋ man nɛ, korowɔ buŋ Yesu nawɔɔtɛɛ te be yako e nyiaa, “Nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon ampaŋ.”

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples crossed the lake and came to shore near the town of Gennesaret.
translation_verse: Be ka toŋ boo keŋ nɛ, be gyuuwɔ kelii tɛɛle keŋ bɛɛ baake ke Genesareti nɛ dɔɔ.

english_verse: The people found out he was there, and they sent word to everyone who lived in this part of the country. So they brought all the sick people to Jesus.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ be kyaa tɛɛle kenaŋ dɔɔ ka gyeŋ nyi Yesu ŋonaŋ nɛ, be kpiliiwɔ gyu ke yako donɔɔse ŋan pou kyaa botɔɔ yenaŋ nɛ. Mena dɔɔ balaŋ moo be kaweesetena pou kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ.

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Every plant that my Father in heaven did not plant will be pulled up by the roots.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Daŋ kamasɛ keŋ ka te yɛɛ nyi me kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ duu na ke ya na, waa kulu ke na ke lila pou ke leki.

english_verse: “Why don't your disciples obey what our ancestors taught us to do? They don't even wash their hands before they eat.”
translation_verse: “Weera dɔɔ te n kaseela baŋ bee di da beneŋ amanberɛse dɔɔ ya? Nawolo nyi bɔɔ ne faafo be nyiŋmaase ŋgba mena keŋ da beneŋ amanberɛse nawolo nɛ pɛte baa di ya!”

english_verse: Jesus answered: Why do you disobey God and follow your own teaching?
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Weera dɔɔ te ɛmɛɛ mɔ, lee ɛ beneŋ amanberɛse dɔɔ te ɛ bee di Wurubuarɛ mmaraase dɔɔ ya?”

english_verse: But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they have has been offered to God.
translation_verse: Mɔna ɛmɛɛ ne yako nyiaa, “Akpaa ŋolo de yako ɔ kya yaa ɔ naa nyi, ‘Kpene keŋ nafɔ me dana maa fa neŋ nɛ, yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ wui.’

english_verse: It is useless for you to worship me, when you teach rules made up by humans.” What Really Makes People Unclean (Mark
translation_verse: Bɛɛ som maŋ bo yakaa, nawolo nyi mmaraase ŋan denibalaŋ gbagba be kyaa yɛɛ nɛ, ŋan te bɛɛ mo wola balaŋ beo wɔ nyi ŋe yɛɛ maŋ Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋa.’ ” Kpene keŋ ne yɛɛ walaŋ ayimɛɛ Maake

english_verse: Stay away from those Pharisees! They are like blind people leading other blind people, and all of them will fall into a ditch.”
translation_verse: Ɛ tina Farasiitena benaŋ yela botɔɔ, be yɛɛ ŋgba siayɛlɛɛsatena baŋ bɛɛ wola siayɛlɛɛsatena gbɛɛ nɛ. Akpaa siayɛlɛɛsate na wola siayɛlɛɛsate gbɛɛ nɛ, be bala pou baa kaa yala bɔɔ man bo.”

english_verse: Jesus did not say a word. But the woman kept following along and shouting, so his disciples came up and asked him to send her away.
translation_verse: Mɔna Yesu te yako daale ya. Mena dɔɔ ɔ kaseela baŋ waase e nyiaa, “Lɔ alo ŋon gbɛɛ na waa ta, nawolo nyi ɔ ne fae yeesa tɔ daa deŋela.”

english_verse: The food you put into your mouth doesn't make you unclean and unfit to worship God. The bad words that come out of your mouth are what make you unclean.”
translation_verse: Na kpene keŋ na gyoo walaŋ nɔɔman nɛ ne yɛɛ na e ayimɛɛ ya, mɔna kpene keŋ na lee ɔ nɔɔman nɛ, keŋ ne yɛɛ na e ayimɛɛ.”

english_verse: Then his disciples came over to him and asked, “Do you know you insulted the Pharisees by what you said?”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu kaseela baŋ kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ kaa bɔɔse nyiaa, “Ŋgba n gyeŋ nyi debaŋ keŋ Farasiitena baŋ be nyii nombia ŋan na, te yɛɛ wɔ gyoŋ ya?”

english_verse: Peter replied, “What did you mean when you talked about the things that make people unclean?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Pita yako e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, lese dudu kei asɛɛ fa daa.”

english_verse: Jesus then said: Don't any of you know by now what I am talking about?
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Keŋ dɔɔ ɛmɛɛ mɔ nyeeya ta yem?

english_verse: Don't you know that the food you put into your mouth goes into your stomach and then out of your body?
translation_verse: Keŋ dɔɔ ɛ ba gyeŋ nyi kpene kamasɛ keŋ da gyoo walaŋ nɔɔman na, ne gyu ka gyoo ɔ dubulu man na ke ta bese lee ɔ wose man yee?

english_verse: But the words that come out of your mouth come from your heart. And they are what make you unfit to worship God.
translation_verse: Mɔna nombii kamasɛ keŋ na lee walaŋ nɔɔman nɛ, na lee bo ɔ konɔɔ man, te keŋ ne yɛɛ na e ayimɛɛ.

english_verse: Out of your heart come evil thoughts, murder, unfaithfulness in marriage, vulgar deeds, stealing, telling lies, and insulting others.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi walaŋ konɔɔ man te nombiakumɛɛ ŋgba, balaŋ kako, na yale kewɔlɛɛ na kafoŋ na ŋmɛɛleŋ na balaŋ nɔɔ kekɛrɛɛ na balaŋ yela kewɔlɛɛ na lee.

english_verse: These are what make you unclean. Eating without washing your hands will not make you unfit to worship God. A Woman's Faith (Mark
translation_verse: Mena abɔɔ kɛŋa ne yɛɛ na walaŋ ayimɛɛ, mɔna akpaa ŋolo te faafo ɔ nyiŋmaase pɛte waa di ya nɛ, ke bɛɛ yɛɛ e ayimɛɛ ya.” Alo ŋolo ke lɛɛ Yesu di Maake

english_verse: Jesus said, “I was sent only to the people of Israel! They are like a flock of lost sheep.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako alo ŋon nyiaa, “Iseraetena baŋ bɔɔ yoo Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛ nɛ baageŋ gyaŋ te Wurubuarɛ kpila maŋ.”

english_verse: Large crowds came and brought many people who were paralyzed or blind or lame or unable to talk. They placed them, and many others, in front of Jesus, and he healed them all.
translation_verse: Balaŋ burum moo be kaweesetena kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ. Kaweese ŋgba baŋ be nawɔɔ ba yekee, na baŋ be sia be yɛlɛɛ na ɔtakasese na omumuuse na kaweese kpookpoo nɛ. Keŋte Yesu kyɔɔ be popou.

english_verse: Jesus left and went to the territory near the towns of Tyre and Sidon.
translation_verse: Yesu ka lee botɔɔ nɛ, ɔ wɔŋa ɔ wose lee botɔɔ gyu Tae na Sidɔn donɔɔse ŋan yenaŋ.

english_verse: Suddenly a Canaanite woman from there came out shouting, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on me! My daughter is full of demons.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Kaanan alo ŋolo leewɔ botɔɔ yenaŋ kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ kaa wii fa e nyiaa, “Me Gbeŋgyoo, gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii, wii me waraŋase! Feliŋkum doo me bu alebubii man, te kɛɛ naase e diyem nideli.”

english_verse: The woman came closer. Then she knelt down and begged, “Please help me, Lord!”
translation_verse: Mɔna alo ŋon kɔŋawɔ kaa buŋ Yesu nawɔɔ tɛɛ waase e nyiaa, “Me Gbeŋgyoo, lɛɛ maŋ!”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “It isn't right to take food away from children and feed it to dogs.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako alo ŋon nyiaa, “Tekaboena nyi baa mo bia weenɛɛ fa babaase ya.”

english_verse: “Lord, this is true,” the woman said, “but even puppies get the crumbs that fall from their owner's table.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te alo ŋon mɔ sɛɛwɔ nyiaa, “Ayi, me Gbeŋgyoo, doo mena, mɔna babaase baa tale fiu weenɛɛ ŋan be gbeŋgyoo ne dii te ŋɛɛ yale tɛɛle man nɛ di.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Dear woman, you really do have a lot of faith, and you will be given what you want.” At that moment her daughter was healed. Jesus Heals Many People
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako alo ŋon nyiaa, “Alo n kelɛɛdi boe. Kpene keŋ n ne gyae nɛ, dɛɛkɔŋ mena fa neŋ.” Debokenaŋ man te ɔ bu ŋon kawee taŋawɔ. Yesu kekyɔ balaŋ burum kaweese

english_verse: From there, Jesus went along Lake Galilee. Then he climbed a hill and sat down.
translation_verse: Yesu ka lee botɔɔ nɛ, te ɔ tɛɛwɔ na Galelia boo keŋ nɔɔ nɔɔ. Keŋte ɔ gyuuwɔ ka gyeŋ bulabii daale gyu ke kyaa ke dɔɔ.

english_verse: Everyone was amazed at what they saw and heard. People who had never spoken could now speak. The lame were healed, the paralyzed could walk, and the blind were able to see. Everyone was praising the God of Israel. Jesus Feeds Four Thousand (Mark
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ kena nyi baŋ be nawɔɔ ba yekee ne tɛɛ, te omumuuse ne kolosi te ɔtakasese wose ne yɛɛ wɔ doŋ, te siayɛlɛɛsatena sia ne naa nɛ, doo be nɔɔ. Keŋte ba lese Iseraetena Wurubuarɛ ŋon yele. Yesu kefa balaŋ kakpoŋse banaara weenɛɛ Maake

english_verse: Jesus called his disciples together and told them, “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat. I don't want to send them away hungry. They might faint on their way home.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu baake ɔ kaseela baŋ kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Balaŋ kɛwɔ nombia dana maŋ waraŋase, nawolo nyi gyɛŋ weeya kpaakpaa atooro yaa ŋgba be kyaa me gyaŋ te be te dii ta kolo ya nɛ. Mɔɔ ne gyae maa lɔ wɔ gbɛɛ na tanam ya, nawolo nyi be sia de kaa biri wɔ gbɛɛnaa man.”

english_verse: His disciples said, “This place is like a desert. Where can we find enough food to feed such a crowd?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Aleŋ yaa dee nyiŋ weenɛɛ fa balaŋ burum kɛwɔ ŋale nsana kɛbo?”

english_verse: Jesus asked them how much food they had. They replied, “Seven small loaves of bread and a few little fish.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Bodobodose aŋmoŋ te ɛ dana?” Te be tiranɔɔ fa e nyi, “Bodobodose nyetooro na kpebia buruburu akalansɛɛ baale te de dana.”

english_verse: After Jesus had told the people to sit down,
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yela balaŋ baŋ tisiwɔ kyakee tɛɛle man.

english_verse: he took the seven loaves of bread and the fish and gave thanks. He then broke them and handed them to his disciples, who passed them around to the crowds.
translation_verse: Ɔ moo bodobodose nyetooro ŋan, na kpebia baŋ te ɔ fanewɔ fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ, te ɔ kɛrɛɛ ŋa mo fa ɔ kaseela baŋ. Keŋte ɔ kaseela baŋ mɔ moo ŋa kpɛlɛɛ balaŋ baŋ.

english_verse: Everyone ate all they wanted, and the leftovers filled seven large baskets.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ pou diiwɔ baa. Te ɔ kaseela baŋ tɔɔsee buruburu akaŋ ŋan na, be nyiŋa doŋase nyetooro.

english_verse: After Jesus had sent the crowds away, he got into a boat and sailed across the lake. He came to shore near the town of Magadan.
translation_verse: Yesu ke lɔ balaŋ baŋ gbɛɛ taŋ nɛ, ɔ gyoowɔ degbele man te ɔ gyuu tɛɛle keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi Magadan nɛ dɔɔ.

english_verse: Don't you know by now that I am not talking to you about bread? Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees!
translation_verse: Weera dɔɔ te ɛ ta gyem nyi na bodobodo wose man nombia yaa mee kolosi fa ŋon ya? Mɔna ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose man nideli lee Farasiitena na Sadukiitena wose man. Be dinɔɔ yɛɛ ŋgba tee keŋ bɛɛ mo do bodobodo man te kɛɛ koro nɛ.”

english_verse: When Jesus and his disciples were near the town of Caesarea Philippi, he asked them, “What do people say about the Son of Man?”
translation_verse: Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ ka gyoo Kaesaria Filipi tɛɛle man nɛ, ɔ bɔɔse ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Balaŋ ne kolosi ma wose man nombia na be kpa maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon yɛna amɔ?”

english_verse: He told them: If the sky is red in the evening, you say the weather will be good.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “De lii balinɔɔ te ŋɔma yasɛɛ na ɛ ne tale naa yako nyi, tɛɛ de naa na weese waa gyɔ.

english_verse: But if the sky is red and gloomy in the morning, you say it is going to rain. You can tell what the weather will be like by looking at the sky. But you don't understand what is happening now.
translation_verse: Te nebo mɔ na, ɛ ne tale kɛɛ ŋɔma dinɔɔ yako nyi gyɛŋ ɔga waa ŋɛɛ. Ɛ ne tale kɛɛ ŋɔma na ɛ kawola kpene keŋ gyae ke kɔŋ. Mɔna nombia ŋan ne yɛɛ debaŋ kei man nɛ, ɛ bɛɛ tale wola ŋe man ya.

english_verse: The disciples answered, “Some people say you are John the Baptist or maybe Elijah or Jeremiah or some other prophet.”
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ kaseela baŋ tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Balaŋ baale kpa nyaŋ yɛna Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon. Baale mɔ kpa nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Elia, te baale mɔ kpa nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Gyeremia, yaa Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ man ŋolo.”

english_verse: From then on, Jesus began telling his disciples what would happen to him. He said, “I must go to Jerusalem. There the nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses will make me suffer terribly. I will be killed, but three days later I will rise to life.”
translation_verse: Lee debaŋ kenaŋ pou nɛ, Yesu fiasɛɛ ɔ na lese nombia ŋan asɛɛ fa ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Kaboena nyi maa gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man, na me kaa naa diyem nideli Gyudatena kegyiise na Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ nyiŋmaa man. Baa ko maŋ, mɔna weeya atoorote keŋ man na, Wurubuarɛ waa gyuusu maŋ lee yeŋ man.”

english_verse: The disciples talked this over and said to each other, “He must be saying this because we didn't bring along any bread.”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɔ kaseela baŋ bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, “Keŋ de ta mo bodobodo kɔŋ dɔɔ ya te ɔ ne yako mena nombia kɛŋa yaa?”

english_verse: The Son of Man will soon come in the glory of his Father and with his angels to reward all people for what they have done.
translation_verse: Maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon na me kpilala dɛɛ lee adido kɔŋ me kya Wurubuarɛ gyoorobiiri man, na me kaa tɔ walaŋ kamasɛ kom lee ɔ tom keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ nɛ man.

english_verse: So I will call you Peter, which means “a rock.” On this rock I will build my church, and death itself will not have any power over it.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ yako neŋ nyiaa, nyaŋ yɛna Pita, te n yele kenaŋ asɛɛ yɛna nyi boe. Nyaŋ boe kei dɔɔ te maa baake me balaŋ pou yilaa. Te yeŋ gba koraŋ be gyae ke tale di be dɔɔ ya.

english_verse: Then Jesus asked, “But who do you say I am?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛmɛɛ mɔ de? Ɛ kpa maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon yɛna amɔ?”

english_verse: Simon Peter spoke up, “You are the Messiah, the Son of the living God.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Simɔn Pita tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Nyaŋ yɛna Kristo, nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ. Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa kekpaakekpaa nɛ Bu.”

english_verse: Jesus told him: Simon, son of Jonah, you are blessed! You didn't discover this on your own. It was shown to you by my Father in heaven.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Gyona bu Simɔn, nyaŋ na nyeebam, nawolo nyi na deniwalaŋ lese na nombia kɛŋa wola neŋ ya, mɔna me kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ lese na ŋa wola neŋ.

english_verse: I will give you the keys to the kingdom of heaven, and God in heaven will allow whatever you allow on earth. But he will not allow anything you don't allow.
translation_verse: Maa mo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ safowaase do n nyiŋmaa man. Kpene kamasɛ keŋ nyaŋ ke gyɔ ke dowoe tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, baa gyɔ ke dowoe adido, te kpene kamasɛ keŋ nyaŋ ke booli ke tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, baa booli ke adido mɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples not to tell anyone he was the Messiah. Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death (Mark
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu faree ɔ kaseela baŋ nyi, be na yako walaŋ kamasɛ nyi ŋon yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ ya. Yesu kekolosi lee ɔ yeŋ na ɔ kefoŋ wose man Maake

english_verse: Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that. He said, “God would never let this happen to you, Lord!”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Pita moo Yesu lee yenaŋ te ɔ tɔɔ e nyi, “Me Gbeŋgyoo, na yako mena ya. Wurubuarɛ dɛɛ mo kɛŋa gyu lem. Kɛŋa na kɔŋ n dɔɔ korakora ya.”

english_verse: Jesus turned to Peter and said, “Satan, get away from me! You're in my way because you think like everyone else and not like God.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu gɛɛsewɔ kɛɛ Pita te ɔ yako e nyiaa, “Ɔbɔnsam, ta lee me gyaŋ! N ne gyae n ke tɔ maŋ gbɛɛ. Nawolo nyi na Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii te n na gyueŋ ya, mɔna denibalaŋ kegyaebii te n na gyueŋ.”

english_verse: Then Jesus said to his disciples: If any of you want to be my followers, you must forget about yourself. You must take up your cross and follow me.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Akpaa ŋolo ne gyae waa sila maŋ na, ɔ na yɛɛ kpene keŋ ŋon gbagba ne gyae ya, mɔna waa mo ɔ ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ seele, na waa sila maŋ wee kamasɛ.

english_verse: If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me, you will find it.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne gyae waa lɛɛ ŋon gbagba nyeedoŋ na, ke gyae ka lee ɔ nyiŋmaa man. Mɔna walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ nyeedoŋ da lee ɔ nyiŋmaa man lee maŋ Yesu na me nombia dɔɔ nɛ, waa bese nyiŋ ke.

english_verse: What will you gain, if you own the whole world but destroy yourself? What would you give to get back your soul?
translation_verse: Akpaa ŋolo de nyiŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ abɔɔ pou te ɔ nyeedoŋ da lee ɔ nyiŋmaa man na, woŋ tɔnɔɔ te waa nyiŋ? Yaa woŋ te walaŋ waa tale mo kyɛɛkee na ɔ nyeedoŋ?

english_verse: I promise you some of those standing here will not die before they see the Son of Man coming with his kingdom.
translation_verse: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, baale seŋɛɛ ɛ man kɛbo keŋ bɔɔ gyae baa yeŋ ya, kelii debaŋ keŋ maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa lese ma wose wola me gyoorobiiri man.”

english_verse: There in front of the disciples, Jesus was completely changed. His face was shining like the sun, and his clothes became white as light.
translation_verse: Be ka seŋ botɔɔ nɛ, karatetee dokoloŋ te Yesu wose kyɛɛkeewɔ be siaman. Na ɔ sia gbɛɛ ne ŋalakee ŋgba weese nɛ, te wa abɔɔ ŋan ɔ doona nɛ bese abɔɔ wolomɛɛ futututu na ŋɛɛ ŋalakee polɛpolɛpolɛ.

english_verse: Then the disciples understood Jesus was talking to them about John the Baptist. Jesus Heals a Boy (Mark
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ gyeŋawɔ nyiaa, Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon wose man nombia yaa ɔ ne yako nɛ. Yesu kekyɔ tobaale ŋon ɔ na wee keleuleuse nɛ Maake

english_verse: Six days later Jesus took Peter and the brothers James and John with him. They went up on a very high mountain where they could be alone.
translation_verse: Weeya looro wɔle nɛ, Yesu moo Pita na Gyemisi na ŋon Gyemisi ɔ naabu Gyɔn kpu na ɔ wose te be gyuuwɔ bula dinaa daale dɔɔ baageŋ.

english_verse: All at once Moses and Elijah were there talking with Jesus.
translation_verse: Debokenaŋ man te Mosesi na Elia kaa bɛɛwɔ lee be dɔɔ, te be kolosiwɔ na Yesu.

english_verse: So Peter said to him, “Lord, it is good for us to be here! Let us make three shelters, one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Pita yako Yesu nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, dei ŋgba daa mɔ be kɔŋ kɛbo nɛ. Nyi akpaa n ne gyae na, yela dɛɛ baŋ kefɛɛse atooro kɛbo. N wui daale, Mosesi wui daale te Elia mɔ wui daale.”

english_verse: While Peter was still speaking, the shadow of a bright cloud passed over them. From the cloud a voice said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him. Listen to what he says!”
translation_verse: Pita ke gyakaa ɔ ne kolosi nɛ, te ŋɔma ŋɛlɛɛŋɛlɛɛ ŋaale kaa wuuwɔ be dɔɔ. Keŋte be nyii nɔɔwoya ŋaale lee ŋɔma ŋan man nyiaa, “Kei yɛna me bu ŋon mɛɛ gyae e lee me konɔɔ man te ɔ na gyoo me sia, ɛ nyii e.”

english_verse: When the disciples heard the voice, they were so afraid they fell flat on the ground.
translation_verse: Ɔ kaseela baŋ ke nyii nɔɔwoya kɛŋa nɛ, gyakoloŋ gyoo wɔ te be tisiwɔ buŋii tɛɛle man.

english_verse: But Jesus came over and touched them. He said, “Get up and don't be afraid!”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu kaa mo ɔ nyiŋmaa tina wɔ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ koro, ɛ na yee ya.”

english_verse: When they opened their eyes, they saw only Jesus.
translation_verse: Be ka koro gyina be sia nɛ, ba te naa ŋolo kpu na Yesu ya.

english_verse: The disciples asked Jesus, “Don't the teachers of the Law of Moses say Elijah must come before the Messiah does?”
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ kaseela baŋ bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Weera dɔɔ te Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ ne yako nyi, kaboena nyi Elia waa taŋgbɛɛ kɔŋ pɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ ke kɔŋ?”

english_verse: In fact, he has already come. But the people did not recognize him and treated him just as they wanted to. They will soon make the Son of Man suffer in the same way.”
translation_verse: Mɔna mɛɛ yako ŋon baa nyi Elia taŋ wulaa kɔŋ laŋ mɔna balaŋ ta gyeŋ e ya, te be yɛɛ e kpene kamasɛ keŋ bɛɛ gyae. Mena mɔ te maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa naa diyem be nyiŋmaa man.

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples returned to the crowd. A man knelt in front of him
translation_verse: Ba ke kɔŋ kaa gyoo balaŋ burum baŋ man nɛ, balee ŋolo kaa tisiwɔ buŋ Yesu nawɔɔtɛɛ,

english_verse: and said, “Lord, have pity on my son! He has a bad case of epilepsy and often falls into a fire or into water.
translation_verse: te ɔ yako e nyiaa, “Me Gbeŋgyoo, wii me bu kei waraŋase fa maŋ. Ɔ na wee keleuleuse, te ɔ ne naa diyem nideli mɔ. Ne kyaa kuu e lɔ boalaŋ yaa loŋ man.

english_verse: I brought him to your disciples, but none of them could heal him.”
translation_verse: Ma moo e kɔŋ n kaseela baŋ gyaŋ, mɔna ba te tale kyɔ e ya.”

english_verse: Jesus said, “You people are too stubborn to have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you? Bring the boy here.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ao! Ɛmɛɛ gyɛŋ balaŋ baŋ ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ maŋ di ya, te ɛ yɛɛ balaŋ kumɛɛwɔ. Weeya aŋmoŋ te kaboena nyi maa kyaa ɛ gyaŋ, na maa nyiŋ konɔɔ fa ŋon?” Keŋte ɔ yako balee ŋon nyiaa, “Mo n bu ŋon kɔna maŋ.”

english_verse: Then Jesus spoke sternly to the demon. It went out of the boy, and right then he was healed.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu fae feliŋkum keŋ te ka leewɔ bu ŋon man, te ɔ kawee taŋawɔ debokenaŋ man.

english_verse: Later the disciples went to Jesus in private and asked him, “Why couldn't we force out the demon?”
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle te Yesu kaseela baŋ gyuuwɔ ɔ gyaŋ, te be baake e lee yenaŋ bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Weera dɔɔ te daa te tale gegi feliŋkum keŋ lee bu ŋon man ya?”

english_verse: While Jesus and his disciples were going from place to place in Galilee, he told them, “The Son of Man will be handed over to people
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ be popou be kɔŋ kaa gyaŋee Galelia tɛɛle man nɛ, Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Baa lese maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon fa balaŋ,

english_verse: who will kill him. But three days later he will rise to life.” All of this made the disciples very sad. Paying the Temple Tax
translation_verse: na baa ko maŋ, mɔna weeya atoorote keŋ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa gyuusu maŋ lee yeŋ man.” Botɔɔ te dekɔkɔrɔkɔɔ kyaŋa ɔ kaseela baŋ.

english_verse: When Jesus and the others arrived in Capernaum, the collectors for the temple tax came to Peter and asked, “Does your teacher pay the temple tax?”
translation_verse: Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ ke kɔŋ kaa gyoo Kapanum donɔɔ man nɛ, balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ lɛɛ lempoo Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man nɛ, kɔŋawɔ kaa bɔɔse Pita nyiaa, “Ɛ gbeŋgyoo ŋon bɛɛ tɔ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa lempoo keŋ yee?”

english_verse: “Yes, he does,” Peter answered. After they had returned home, Jesus went up to Peter and asked him, “Simon, what do you think? Do the kings of this earth collect taxes and fees from their own people or from foreigners?”
translation_verse: Keŋte Pita tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, ɔ ne tɔɔ. Pita ke kɔŋ kaa gyoo dekpaŋalaŋ man pɛ nɛ, Yesu taŋgbɛɛ la kolosi na e te ɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Simɔn, sena yɛna nya agyueŋ? Balaŋ bomɔ gyaŋ te tɛɛle kei dɔɔ gyooneŋ ne lɛɛ lempoo lee? Donɔɔsetena gyaŋ yaa ŋɔɔla gyaŋ?”

english_verse: Peter answered, “From foreigners.” Jesus replied, “Then their own people don't have to pay.
translation_verse: Te Pita tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Ŋɔɔla gyaŋ te bɛɛ lɛɛ.” Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Akpaa mena na donɔɔsetena te nyiŋ ba wose.

english_verse: But we don't want to cause trouble. So go cast a line into the lake and pull out the first fish you hook. Open its mouth, and you will find a coin. Use it to pay your taxes and mine.”
translation_verse: Mɔna keŋ dɔɔ ne gyae nyi daa na wɔ ke yala dɔŋa dɔɔ ya nɛ, mo n darawaa na n ke gyu boo man ke lɔ. Kpebii gyaŋgbate ŋon n ke kyaŋ nɛ, n wola ɔ nɔɔ na n kena kɔba keŋ gyae ka yee daa na neŋ wui lempoo keŋ ketɔ. Lese ke na n ka mo gyina wɔ.”

english_verse: If your eye causes you to sin, poke it out and get rid of it. You would be better off to go into life with only one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into the fires of hell. The Lost Sheep (Luke
translation_verse: Te akpaa nyi n sibii ne yele la te n ne yɛɛ dukum na kulu ke leki. Dei nyi nya mo sibii dokoloŋ gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man, kela nyi baa mo neŋ na n sia ala ka do boalaŋ keŋ ke bɛɛ yem ya nɛ man. Nam ŋon ɔ ba yoo nɛ dudu Luka

english_verse: Don't you think you should show pity to someone else, as I did to you?”
translation_verse: Woŋ dɔɔ te nyaŋ mɔ n te wii n dɔɔ ŋon waraŋase, ŋgba mena keŋ maŋ be wii neŋ nɛ ya?’

english_verse: But if you are as humble as this child, you are the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ yɔkɔse ɔ wose ŋgba biikyaabii kei nɛ, ŋon yɛna walaŋ kegyia Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man.

english_verse: And when you welcome one of these children because of me, you welcome me. Temptations To Sin (Mark
translation_verse: Te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ biikyaabii kei dinɔɔ ma yele man na, maŋ yaa ɔ lɛɛwɔ.

english_verse: If the follower refuses to listen to them, report the matter to the church. Anyone who refuses to listen to the church must be treated like an unbeliever or a tax collector. Allowing and Not Allowing
translation_verse: Akpaa ɔ bɛɛ ɔ te nyii ŋon ya nɛ, kenaŋ na baake e n tebia Kristotena dikpii keŋ gyaŋ. Te akpaa ɔ bɛɛ ɔ te nyii baŋ mɔ ya nɛ, kenaŋ na kɛɛ e nyi ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ ba gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ ya, yaa ɔ yɛɛ dukumyɛɛre e.

english_verse: It will be terrible for people who cause even one of my little followers to sin. Those people would be better off thrown into the deepest part of the ocean with a heavy stone tied around their necks!
translation_verse: Mɔna walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ yeli bia buruburu kɛwɔ baŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ maŋ di nɛ man ŋolo de yɛɛ dukum nɛ, kenaŋ na nyi akpaa ba mo boe dinaa dinaa kpaŋ ɔ wɔŋe man, te be fuŋ e do ɛpo man na dei kela.

english_verse: If your hand or foot causes you to sin, chop it off and throw it away! You would be better off to go into life paralyzed or lame than to have two hands or two feet and be thrown into the fire that never goes out.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa n nyiŋmaa yaa n nae ne yele la te n ne yɛɛ dukum na kara ke leki. Nawolo nyi, dei nyi nya lee kadeŋ na n ka gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man, kela nyi baa mo neŋ nyiŋmaase ala yaa nawɔɔ ala ka do boalaŋ keŋ ke bɛɛ yem ya nɛ man.

english_verse: If one of my followers sins against you, go and point out what was wrong. But do it in private, just between the two of you. If that person listens, you have won back a follower.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, akpaa n dɔɔ Kristo baale de yɛɛ neŋ nombiikum na, gyu ɔ gyaŋ na n kaa bɔɔse e. Mɔna yela ka ke yɛɛ ɛmɛɛ na e ɛ bala nombia. Akpaa ɔ nyii neŋ na, n te tale baa besena e kɔŋ gbɛɛ kpaakpaate dɔɔ.

english_verse: Don't be cruel to any of these little ones! I promise you their angels are always with my Father in heaven.
translation_verse: Maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon kɔŋae me kaa lɛɛ baŋ bɔɔ yoo Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛ man nɛ nyee.

english_verse: Let me ask you this. What would you do if you had
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, sena te ɛ na gyueŋ lee nombia kɛŋa man? Akpaa ŋolo dana namense sɔŋonoŋ (

english_verse: silver coins. So he grabbed the man by the throat. He started choking him and said, “Pay me what you owe!”
translation_verse: ) te be man dokoloŋ da yoo na woŋ te ɔ ne yɛɛ? Ɔ be gyae waa tina sɔŋonaara na kufu kadaale (

english_verse: That's how it is with your Father in heaven. He doesn't want any of these little ones to be lost. When Someone Sins (Luke
translation_verse: Mena mɔ te ɛ kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ, bɛɛ gyae nyi balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ maŋ di nɛ, be man ŋolo dɛɛ yo ya. Mena keŋ ɛmɛɛ na n dɔɔ ŋon ɔ be yɛɛ neŋ dukum nɛ ɛ ke kyaa

english_verse: But if that one refuses to listen, take along one or two others. The Scriptures teach that every complaint must be proven true by two or more witnesses.
translation_verse: Mɔna nyi akpaa ɔ te nyii neŋ ya na, mo Kristo baale dokoloŋ yaa balaŋ bala kpu na n wose na ɛ ke gyu ɔ gyaŋ. Kei gyae ke yela n ke nyiŋ adansɛɛtena bala yaa batooro nombia ŋenaŋ man, ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia ne yako nɛ.

english_verse: I promise that when any two of you on earth agree about something you are praying for, my Father in heaven will do it for you.
translation_verse: “Te mɛɛ yako ŋon baa bela nyiaa, kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɛ man balaŋ bala de yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ seŋ ke dɔɔ fane tɛɛle kei dɔɔ na, me kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ waa yɛɛ ke fa ŋon.”

english_verse: Whenever two or three of you come together in my name, I am there with you. An Official Who Refused To Forgive
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi botɔɔ keŋ balaŋ bala yaa balaŋ batooro de gyaŋee ma yele man na, me kpuɛ na wɔ. Betaboŋmon te kaboena nyi baa mo walaŋ dukum kyɛɛ e

english_verse: Peter came up to the Lord and asked, “How many times should I forgive someone who does something wrong to me? Is seven times enough?”
translation_verse: Keŋte Pita kɔŋawɔ Yesu gyaŋ kaa bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Me Gbeŋgyoo, betaboŋmon te me dɔɔ de yɛɛ nombiikum me dɔɔ na te maa mo kyɛɛ e? Maa mo kyɛɛ e betabonyetooro?”

english_verse: This story will show you what the kingdom of heaven is like: One day a king decided to call in his officials and ask them to give an account of what they owed him.
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Nnɛ te Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ doo. Ke yɛɛ ŋgba gyoo ŋon ɔ ne gyae nyi ŋon na ɔ tewulɛɛ ke kyaa, na baa kɛɛ koma ŋan be dana e nɛ.

english_verse: As he was doing this, one official was brought in who owed him
translation_verse: Be ka beta fiasɛɛ bɛɛ gyoo ŋe man nɛ, te ba moo tobaale ŋon ɔ dana e kom kɔba na nsendee ya nɛ kɔna e.

english_verse: But he didn't have any money to pay what he owed. The king ordered him to be sold, along with his wife and children and all he owned, in order to pay the debt.
translation_verse: Keŋ tobaale ŋon te nyiŋ kɔba keŋ waa tɔ ya dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon yako wɔ nyi baa mo ŋon na ɔ wɛɛle na ɔ bia na wa abɔɔ ŋan pou ɔ dana nɛ ke yɔɔ, na baa mo kaa tɔ kom keŋ.

english_verse: The official got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you every cent I owe!”
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ tobaale ŋon tisiwɔ buŋ ɔ nawɔɔ tɛɛ waase e nyiaa, ‘Nyiŋ konɔɔ fa maŋ, maa tɔ neŋ kom keŋ pou.’

english_verse: The king felt sorry for him and let him go free. He even told the official that he did not have to pay back the money.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔ nombia yɛɛ ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon waraŋase, te ɔ moo kom keŋ pou kyɛɛ e te ɔ tina e yela nyi waa ta.

english_verse: The man got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you back.”
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ dɔɔ ŋon mɔ tisiwɔ buŋ ɔ nawɔɔ tɛɛ waase e nyiaa, ‘Me dɔɔ nyiŋ konɔɔ fa maŋ maa tɔ neŋ n kom keŋ pou.’

english_verse: But the first official refused to have pity. Instead, he went and had the other official put in jail until he could pay what he owed.
translation_verse: Mɔna tobaale ŋon bɛe, te ɔ yela ba moo ɔ dɔɔ ŋon ketɔ deni nyi waa doo botɔɔ, kelii debaŋ keŋ waa tɔ e ɔ kom keŋ pou.

english_verse: When some other officials found out what had happened, they felt sorry for the man who had been put in jail. Then they told the king what had happened.
translation_verse: Tewulɛɛ akaŋ baŋ kena mena nɛ takaa wɔ nideli, keŋte be gyuuwɔ ke tɔlɛɛ nombia ŋan pou fa be gbeŋgyoo ŋon.

english_verse: The king called the first official back in and said, “You're an evil man! When you begged for mercy, I said you did not have to pay back a cent.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te be gbeŋgyoo ŋon baake tobaale ŋon te ɔ yako e nyiaa, ‘N yɛɛ walaŋkum ŋolo e. Nyaŋ waase maŋ te ma moo n kom keŋ n dana maŋ nɛ pou kyɛɛ neŋ.

english_verse: The king was so angry that he ordered the official to be tortured until he could pay back everything he owed.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon gyeŋa baŋ, te ɔ yela ba moo tobaale ŋon ketɔ deni na waa naa diyem kelii debaŋ keŋ, ŋon mɔ waa tɔ ɔ koma ŋan ɔ dana e nɛ pou.

english_verse: That is how my Father in heaven will treat you, if you don't forgive each of my followers with all your heart.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ ta mo ɛ tebia nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ lee ɛ konɔɔse man ya nɛ, mena te me kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ waa yɛɛ ŋon.”

english_verse: When Jesus finished teaching, he left Galilee and went to the part of Judea east of the Jordan River.
translation_verse: Yesu kekolosi nombia kɛŋa pou taŋ nɛ, ɔ leewɔ Galelia tɛɛle man gyu Gyudia tɛɛle man, Gyɔɔdan boo keŋ diŋgyiŋ.

english_verse: Some people are unable to marry because of birth defects or because of what someone has done to their bodies. Others stay single in order to serve God better. Anyone who can accept this teaching should do so.” Jesus Blesses Little Children (Mark
translation_verse: Balaŋ baale kyaa keŋ nyi bɔɔ gyae baa tale yala ala ya, nawolo nyi mena te ba lola wɔ. Balaŋ baale mɔ dɔɔ, balaŋ yɛɛ na wɔ keŋ nyi bɔɔ gyae baa tale yala ala ya. Balaŋ baale mɔ dɔɔ, Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ dɔɔ te baŋ gbagba bɛɛwɔ nyi bɔɔ yale ya. Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon waa tale lɛɛ nombia kɛŋa do na, waa lɛɛ ŋa do.” Yesu ke hiraa bia Maake

english_verse: Large crowds followed him, and he healed their sick.
translation_verse: Balaŋ burum sila e gyu botɔɔ te ɔ kyɔɔ wɔ be kaweese pou.

english_verse: Some Pharisees wanted to test Jesus. They came up to him and asked, “Is it right for a man to divorce his wife for just any reason?”
translation_verse: Keŋte Farasiitena baale kɔŋawɔ be kaa teese ɔ nɔɔman te be bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Da mmaraa ne fa daa gbɛɛ nyi ŋolo waa tale bɛɛ ɔ wɛɛle lee nombii kamasɛ dɔɔ?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Don't you know in the beginning the Creator made a man and a woman?
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ te kala ta Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan nyi, lee kewalaŋ keŋ na, Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ alo na baale bo,

english_verse: That's why a man leaves his father and mother and gets married. He becomes like one person with his wife.
translation_verse: te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, ‘Lee kei dɔɔ nɛ baale waa tina ɔ kya na ɔ naa yela, na waa mo ɔ wose mataa na ɔ wɛɛle, na be balaŋ bala ka bese walaŋ dokoloŋ yee?’

english_verse: Then they are no longer two people, but one. And no one should separate a couple God has joined together.”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ be ta tekaa yɛɛ balaŋ bala bela ya, mɔna be ta bese walaŋ dokoloŋ. Mena dɔɔ kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo bom yɛɛ ke dokoloŋ nɛ, ŋolo na ŋolo na kpase ke man ya.”

english_verse: The Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why did Moses say a man could write out divorce papers and send his wife away?”
translation_verse: Keŋte Farasiitena baŋ bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, “Nyi akpaa mena te doo na, weera dɔɔ te de naana Mosesi yɛɛ mmaraa see nyi, baale waa tale ŋmarase yale kebɛɛ tɔne fa ɔ wɛɛle na waa bɛɛ e?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “You are so heartless! That's why Moses allowed you to divorce your wife. But from the beginning God did not intend it to be that way.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ konɔɔyuŋ dɔɔ te de naana Mosesi fa ŋon mena gbɛɛ kenaŋ. Mɔna lee kewalaŋ keŋ na, be doo mena ya.

english_verse: I say if your wife has not committed some terrible sexual sin, you must not divorce her to marry someone else. If you do, you are unfaithful.”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ yako ŋon baa nyiaa, nyi akpaa na alo lee ɔ baale wɔle bo ya, te ɔ baale de bɛɛ e gyu ke yala alo wɔle nɛ, Wurubuarɛ siaman na ɔtenate gyae ŋolo wɛɛle baa.”

english_verse: Jesus told them, “Only those people who have been given the gift of staying single can accept this teaching.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Nombia ŋan mɛɛ yako ŋon nɛ, na walaŋ kamasɛ ne gyae la waa tale lɛɛ ŋa do ya, gyisɛ balaŋ baŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo mena bɛrɛɛ kenaŋ boele wɔ nɛ baageŋ.

english_verse: Some people brought their children to Jesus, so he could place his hands on them and pray for them. His disciples told the people to stop bothering him.
translation_verse: Keŋte balaŋ baale moo bia kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ nyi waa mo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa be dɔɔ na waa fane fa wɔ. Mɔna ɔ kaseela baŋ kena mena nɛ, be ŋmanyaŋ balaŋ baŋ.

english_verse: A man came to Jesus and asked, “Teacher, what good thing must I do to have eternal life?”
translation_verse: Wee daale nɛ tobaale ŋolo kaa bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, woŋ deeli yaa maa yɛɛ na maa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ?”

english_verse: “Which ones?” the man asked. Jesus answered, “Do not murder. Be faithful in marriage. Do not steal. Do not tell lies about others.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te tobaale ŋon bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, “Mmaraase ŋemɔ?” Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Na ko walaŋ ya, na lee n wɛɛle yaa n baale wɔle ya, na ŋmɛɛle ya, na kara n dɔɔ nɔɔ ya.

english_verse: After Jesus had placed his hands on the children, he left. A Rich Young Man (Mark
translation_verse: Yesu ka mo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa bia baŋ dɔɔ taŋ nɛ, te ɔ leewɔ botɔɔ laŋ. Kɔbate ŋolo wose man nombia Maake

english_verse: Jesus said to him, “Why do you ask me about what is good? Only God is good. If you want to have eternal life, you must obey his commandments.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Weera te n ne bɔɔse maŋ lee deeli keyɛɛ wose man. Wurubuarɛ waageŋ dei la. Mena dɔɔ nyi akpaa n ne gyae n ke nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ nɛ, kenaŋ na kaboena nyi nya di Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ.”

english_verse: But Jesus said, “Let the children come to me, and don't try to stop them! People who are like these children belong to God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Yesu kena mena nɛ ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ yela bia baŋ ke kɔŋ me gyaŋ. Ɛ na tɔ wɔ gbɛɛ ya, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ yɔkɔse ba wose ŋgba bia kɛwɔ dinɔɔ nɛ wui.”

english_verse: But many who are now first will be last, and many who are last will be first.
translation_verse: Mɔna balaŋ burum baŋ be yɛɛ siamantenawɔ nɛ, baa kaa bese wɔle nyibaŋ, na balaŋ burum baŋ be yɛɛ wɔle nyibaŋwɔ nɛ kaa bese siamantena.”

english_verse: Respect your father and mother. And love others as much as you love yourself.”
translation_verse: Bu n kya na n naa, na n ke gyae n tebia ŋgba mena keŋ n ne gyae nyaŋ gbagba n wose nɛ.”

english_verse: The young man said, “I have obeyed all of these. What else must I do?”
translation_verse: Keŋte tobaale ŋon yakowɔ nyiaa, “Me wulaa mee di mmaraase kɛŋa pou dɔɔ. Woŋ kaa la nyi maa yɛɛ bela?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “If you want to be perfect, go sell everything you own! Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come and be my follower.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Nyi akpaa n ne gyae n ke de kelii ɔto na, gyu ke yɔɔ nya abɔɔ ŋan pou n dana nɛ, na n ka mo kɔba keŋ kpɛlɛɛ ayematena na n ke kɔŋ kaa sila maŋ. N yɛɛ mena na, n ke nyiŋ abɔɔ kpaakpaa see n wose Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ adido.”

english_verse: When the young man heard this, he went away sad, because he was very rich.
translation_verse: Tobaale ŋon ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, ɔ moo waraŋase lee botɔɔ nawolo nyi ɔ dana kɔba na nsendee ya.

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples, “I tell you, it's terribly hard for rich people to get into the kingdom of heaven!
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, gyae ke yɛɛ doŋ fa baŋ be dana kɔba nɛ nyi baa gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man.

english_verse: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Mɛɛ yako ŋon bela nyiaa, gyae ke yɛɛ doŋ fa baŋ be dana kɔba nɛ nyi baa gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man, kela nyi kurumɔɔ ka gyoo ŋarale bɔɔ man lee.”

english_verse: When the disciples heard this, they were greatly surprised and asked, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
translation_verse: Yesu kaseela baŋ ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ doo be nɔɔ te be bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Nyi nnɛ te doo na, amɔte ne gyae la waa nyiŋ nyeekelɛɛ baa?”

english_verse: Jesus looked straight at them and said, “There are some things people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu kɛɛ wɔ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Denibalaŋ gyaŋ na ŋolo be tale yɛɛ nombii kei ya, mɔna kpene kamasɛ be kyaa keŋ Wurubuarɛ be tale yɛɛ ya.”

english_verse: Jesus answered: Yes, all of you have become my followers. And so in the future world, when the Son of Man sits on his glorious throne, I promise you will sit on twelve thrones to judge the twelve tribes of Israel.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, debaŋ keŋ kpene kamasɛ de kaa bese wɔle te maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon de kyaa me gyooro gbeli keŋ dɔɔ me gyoorobiiri man nɛ, ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ be sila maŋ nɛ ɛ ke kyaa gyooro gbelɛɛ kufu ala dɔɔ, na ɛ ke di Iseraetena akpaŋalaŋse kufu ala ŋan balaŋ nombia.

english_verse: All who have given up home or brothers and sisters or father and mother or children or land for me will be given
translation_verse: Te ɛ man walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ be tina ɔ dekpaŋalaŋ yaa ɔ naabia yaa ɔ deesina, yaa ɔ kya yaa ɔ naa yaa ɔ bia yaa ɔ tɛɛlese yela lee maŋ dɔɔ nɛ, waa bese nyiŋ ŋa akpeŋ sɔŋonoŋ (

english_verse: times as much. They will also have eternal life.
translation_verse: ) kpu, na waa bese nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ mɔ.

english_verse: As Jesus was telling what the kingdom of heaven would be like, he said: Early one morning a man went out to hire some workers for his vineyard.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu gyɔɔ dudu lese nombia ŋan ɔ be yako nɛ man wola wɔ nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ ŋgba wɔɔfarale ŋon, ɔ ba koro nebo nebo fuu gyu ɔ ne gyae paasetena na be kaa yɛɛ tom ɔ wɔɔman nɛ.

english_verse: After he had agreed to pay them the usual amount for a day's work, he sent them off to his vineyard.
translation_verse: Balee ŋon na paasetena baŋ pou ke sɛɛ wee dokoloŋ koya dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ moo wɔ gyu ɔ wɔɔ keŋ man nyi ba ke yɛɛ tom.

english_verse: About nine that morning, the man saw some other people standing in the market with nothing to do.
translation_verse: Kegyɔ debaŋ kadaale nɛ, ɔ leewɔ gyu abɔntere man te ɔ kenaa tewulɛɛ baale mɔ seŋɛɛ yakaa bɔɔ ne yɛɛ kolo ya.

english_verse: He promised to pay them what was fair, if they would work in his vineyard.
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa ‘Ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ gyu me wɔɔ keŋ man ke yɛɛ tom. Maa tɔ ŋon kom keŋ kaboena nyi maa tɔ ŋon nɛ.’ ”

english_verse: So they went. At noon and again about three in the afternoon he returned to the market. And each time he made the same agreement with others who were loafing around with nothing to do.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ baŋ mɔ gyuuwɔ ɔ wɔɔ keŋ man. Weese ka seŋ dɛɛ, na ɔ ke gɛɛse nɛ, ɔ besewɔ ka mo balaŋ gyu ɔ wɔɔ keŋ man bela.

english_verse: Finally, about five in the afternoon the man went back and found some others standing there. He asked them, “Why have you been standing here all day long doing nothing?”
translation_verse: Ke lii balinɔɔ weese ŋgba debaŋ anoŋ nɛ, ɔ besewɔ gyu kena balaŋ baale mɔ seŋɛɛ yakaa. Keŋte ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Weera te gyɛŋ pou ɛ seŋɛɛ kɛbo yakaa ɛ bɛɛ yɛɛ kolo ya?”

english_verse: “Because no one has hired us,” they answered. Then he told them to go work in his vineyard.
translation_verse: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Nawolo nyi ŋolo te fa daa tom dɛɛ yɛɛ ya.” Botɔɔ te balee ŋon yako wɔ nyiaa, “Kenaŋ na ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ gyu ke yɛɛ tom me wɔɔman.”

english_verse: That evening the owner of the vineyard told the man in charge of the workers to call them in and give them their money. He also told the man to begin with the ones who were hired last.
translation_verse: Ke lii balinɔɔ nɛ, wɔɔte ŋon yako ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ man kegyia ŋon nyiaa, “Baake paasetena baŋ na n ke tɔ wɔ kom. Taŋgbɛɛ tɔ baŋ bɔɔ dɔɔ wɔle gyu wɔɔ keŋ man nɛ kom, pɛna n kaa tɔ baŋ bɔɔ taŋgbɛɛ gyu nɛ.”

english_verse: When the workers arrived, the ones who had been hired at five in the afternoon were given a full day's pay.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ dɔɔ wɔle gyu wɔɔ keŋ man balinɔɔ weese nɛ kɔŋawɔ, te be tɔɔ wɔ kom ŋgba mena keŋ wɔɔte ŋon be yako nyi waa tɔ gyaŋgba nyibaŋ baŋ nɛ.

english_verse: they began complaining to the owner of the vineyard.
translation_verse: Ba ke lɛɛ be kɔbase taŋ nɛ, be fiasɛɛ bɛɛ kolosi tia wɔɔte ŋon nyiaa,

english_verse: They said, “The ones who were hired last worked for only one hour. But you paid them the same that you did us. And we worked in the hot sun all day long!”
translation_verse: “Balaŋ kɛwɔ baŋ bɔɔ kɔŋ wɔle wɔle kei nɛ, yɛɛ tom bo debaŋ dokoloŋ kooŋ. Mɔna daa dɔɔ da seŋawɔ weese kpalala kei man leenaŋ nebo kaa lii balinɔɔ kei. Kenaŋ wɔle koraŋ na, n besewɔ tɔ de popou kom kyɛɛkyɛɛ.”

english_verse: The owner answered one of them, “Friend, I didn't cheat you. I paid you exactly what we agreed on.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te wɔɔte ŋon tiranɔɔ fa be man ŋolo nyiaa, “Me gyoo, me ta ŋere neŋ do ya na. Ŋgba daa na neŋ kolosiwɔ see nyi n yɛɛ tom wee dibiliŋ na, nnɛ te maa tɔ neŋ kom.

english_verse: Take your money now and go! What business is it of yours if I want to pay them the same that I paid you?
translation_verse: Mo n kɔba na n ke ta gyu n dɛɛ. Maŋ gbagba gyae la nyi maa tɔ baŋ bɔɔ dɔɔ wɔle kɔŋ nɛ kom ŋgba mena keŋ mɔɔ tɔ neŋ nɛ.

english_verse: Don't I have the right to do what I want with my own money? Why should you be jealous, if I want to be generous?”
translation_verse: Me dana gbɛɛ keŋ nyi maa mo maŋ gbagba me kɔba yɛɛ kpene keŋ mɛɛ gyae. Keŋ me dana wɔe dɔɔ te na wee neŋ nɛ?”

english_verse: Jesus then said, “So it is. Everyone who is now last will be first, and everyone who is first will be last.” Jesus Again Tells about His Death (Mark
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Mena mɔ te wɔle nyibaŋ baa kaa bese gyaŋgba nyibaŋ, na gyaŋgba nyibaŋ mɔ kaa bese wɔle nyibaŋ.” Yesu ka bese kolosi lee ɔ yeŋ wose man Maake

english_verse: When Jesus heard them, he stopped and asked, “What do you want me to do for you?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu dɔɔwɔ seŋ te ɔ baake wɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Woŋ te ɛ ne gyae nyi maa yɛɛ fa ŋon?”

english_verse: Jesus felt sorry for them and touched their eyes. At once they could see, and they became his followers.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu wii be waraŋase, te ɔ moo ɔ nyiŋmaa tinaa be sia. Debokenaŋ man te be sia gyuusuwɔ, te be sila Yesu laŋ.

english_verse: The crowd told them to be quiet, but they shouted even louder, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
translation_verse: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ ŋmanyaŋ wɔ nyi baa wu be nɔɔ. Mɔna nɛnɛɛ gba te be desina fae yeesa nedoŋ nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii, wii de waraŋase.”

english_verse: As Jesus was on his way to Jerusalem, he took his twelve disciples aside and told them in private:
translation_verse: Wee daale Yesu kee gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, ɔ baake ɔ kaseela kufu bala baŋ lee yenaŋ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa,

english_verse: and then they will hand him over to foreigners who will make fun of him. They will beat him and nail him to a cross. But on the third day he will rise from death. A Mother's Request (Mark
translation_verse: Baa mo maŋ do balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ nyiŋmaa man na baa kulu maŋ, na baa tuku maŋ na baa gyɔ maŋ sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ na maa yeŋ. Mɔna weeya atoorote na, Wurubuarɛ waa gyuusu maŋ lee yeŋ man.” Sebedeo bia Maake

english_verse: The mother of James and John came to Jesus with her two sons. She knelt down and started begging him to do something for her.
translation_verse: Keŋte Sebedeo bia, Gyemisi na Gyɔn be naa moo wɔ kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ te ɔ buŋawɔ ɔ nawɔɔ tɛɛ sola e nyi waa kyɔ e na nombii daale.

english_verse: Jesus asked her what she wanted, and she said, “When you come into your kingdom, please let one of my sons sit at your right side and the other at your left.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Woŋ te n ne gyae nyi maa yɛɛ fa neŋ?” Te alo ŋon tiranɔɔ fa Yesu nyiaa, “Mɛɛ gyae nyi n ke fa me bia bala kɛwɔ gbɛɛ, na ŋolo ke kyaa n dunoluŋ dɔɔ, na ŋolo mɔ ke kyaa n demena dɔɔ n gyoori keŋ man.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Not one of you knows what you are asking. Are you able to drink from the cup that I must soon drink from?” James and John said, “Yes, we are!”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ ba gyeŋ kpene ɛ na solo nɛ ya. Ɛ ke tale naa diyem keŋ maa naa nɛ?” Keŋte be tiranɔɔ fa e nyi, “Ayi, dɛɛ tale.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “You certainly will drink from my cup! But it isn't for me to say who will sit at my right side and at my left. This is for my Father to say.”
translation_verse: Te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ ke tale naa diyem keŋ maa naa nɛ, mɔna mɔɔ taŋ doŋ keŋ maa lese walaŋ ŋon waa kyaa ma dunoluŋ yaa ma demena dɔɔ ya. Mena akyaese kɛŋa doe fa balaŋ baŋ me kya gbagba be desina see wɔ nɛ.”

english_verse: When the ten other disciples heard this, they were angry with the two brothers.
translation_verse: Yesu kaseela kufu akaŋ baŋ ke nyii mena nombia kɛŋa nɛ, ba gyeŋa baŋ Gyemisi na Gyɔn dɔɔ.

english_verse: But Jesus called the disciples together and said: You know foreign rulers like to order their people around. And their great leaders have full power over everyone they rule.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu baake ɔ kaseela baŋ pou yilaa te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ gyeŋ nyi tɛɛle kei dɔɔ gyooneŋ ne di gyoori bo be balaŋ dɔɔ, te be kegyiise baŋ mɔ dana doŋ be dɔɔ.

english_verse: But don't act like them. If you want to be great, you must be the servant of all the others.
translation_verse: Mɔna ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ, na mena te kaboena nyi dɛɛ doo ɛ man ya. Ɛ man walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne gyae nyi waa bese ɛ kegyia nɛ, kaboena nyi waa bese ɛ kegbetibii.

english_verse: And if you want to be first, you must be the slave of the rest.
translation_verse: Te ɛ man walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne gyae nyi waa bese ɛ siamante nɛ, kaboena nyi waa bese ɛ popou ɛ kegbetibii.

english_verse: The Son of Man did not come to be a slave master, but a slave who will give his life to rescue many people. Jesus Heals Two Blind Men (Mark
translation_verse: Mena mɔ te maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon te kɔŋ nyi balaŋ baa som maŋ ya, mɔna me kɔŋae me kaa som balaŋ, na maa yeŋ lɛɛ balaŋ burum nyee.” Yesu kekyɔ siayɛlɛɛsatena bala baale Maake

english_verse: Jesus was followed by a large crowd as he and his disciples were leaving Jericho.
translation_verse: Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ kɛɛ lee Gyeriko donɔɔ man nɛ, balaŋ burum sila wɔ.

english_verse: Two blind men were sitting beside the road. And when they heard that Jesus was coming their way, they shouted, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
translation_verse: Na siayɛlɛɛsatena bala baale kyaa gbɛɛnaa nɔɔman botɔɔ. Be ke nyii nyi Yesu ne laŋa laa nɛ, be gyinaa be nɔɔ baake nyiaa “De Gbeŋgyoo, gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii, wii de waraŋase.”

english_verse: They answered, “Lord, we want to see!”
translation_verse: Keŋte be tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, dɛɛ gyae de sia ke gyuusu.”

english_verse: Jesus said: I will tell you a story about a man who had two sons. Then you can tell me what you think. The father went to the older son and said, “Go work in the vineyard today!”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ gyɔ dudu fa wɔ nyiaa, “Balee ŋolo kyaawɔ keŋ ɔ dana bia baala bala. Wee daale nɛ, ɔ gyuu ɔ bu kegyia ŋon gyaŋ ke yako e nyiaa, ‘Me bu, mɛɛ gyae nyi gyɛŋ n ke gyu me wɔɔman ke yɛɛ tom fa maŋ.’

english_verse: But when they saw the man's son, they said, “Someday he will own the vineyard. Let's kill him! Then we can have it all for ourselves.”
translation_verse: Mɔna paasetena baŋ kena ɔ bu ŋon nɛ be yako dɔŋa nyiaa, ‘Kei yɛna bu ŋon waa kaa di ɔ kya abɔɔ ŋan. Ɛ yela dɛɛ ko e na abɔɔ ŋan nafɔ waa di nɛ ka bese de wui!’

english_verse: If anyone asks why you are doing this, just say, ‘The Lord needs them.’ He will at once let you have the donkeys.”
translation_verse: Akpaa ŋolo de bɔɔse ŋon nombii daale nɛ, ɛ yako e nyiaa, ‘De Gbeŋgyoo Yesu ne gyae na wɔ.’ Ɛ yako mena nɛ, waa tina wɔ yela fa ŋon debokenaŋ man.”

english_verse: “Announce to the people of Jerusalem: ‘Your king is coming to you! He is humble and rides on a donkey. He comes on the colt of a donkey.’ ”
translation_verse: Ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ yako Gyerusalɛmtena nyiaa, ‘Ɛ kɛɛ! Ɛ gyoo ne kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ. Ɔ te yɔkɔse ɔ wose, te ɔ kyaa afurum dɔɔ. Ɔ kyae afurum bu dɔɔ ɔ ne kɔŋ.’ ”

english_verse: The disciples left and did what Jesus had told them to do.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu kaseela baŋ gyuuwɔ ke yɛɛ ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be yako wɔ nɛ.

english_verse: They brought the donkey and its colt and laid some clothes on their backs. Then Jesus got on.
translation_verse: Ba moo afurum ŋon na ɔ bu kɔŋ, te ba moo be kanyaŋse gyakaa be dɔɔ, te Yesu gyeŋawɔ kyaa.

english_verse: When Jesus came to Jerusalem, everyone in the city was excited and asked, “Who can this be?”
translation_verse: Yesu ke kɔŋ kaa gyoo Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, donɔɔ keŋ pou siŋiiwɔ te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, “Walaŋ woŋti yɛna kei?”

english_verse: But the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses were angry when they saw his miracles and heard the children shouting praises to the Son of David.
translation_verse: Mɔna saese kegyiise na mmaraa wola baŋ kena gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan Yesu be yɛɛ, na mena keŋ bia ne fae yeesa Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man nyiaa, “Gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii lɛɛ daa” nɛ, ba gyeŋa baŋ Yesu dɔɔ.

english_verse: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard these stories, they knew Jesus was talking about them.
translation_verse: Saese kegyiise na Farasiitena baŋ ke nyii Yesu aduya kɛŋa nɛ, ba gyeŋawɔ nyi ba wose man nombia yaa ɔ ne yako.

english_verse: He told them, “The Scriptures say, ‘My house should be called a place of worship.’ But you have turned it into a place where robbers hide.”
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako daa nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ kpa, ‘Me deni gyae ke yɛɛ botɔɔ keŋ baa fane,’ mɔna ɛmɛɛ ta besena ke yɛɛ ŋmɛɛlatena dɛɛgyaŋee.”

english_verse: If you have faith when you pray, you will be given whatever you ask for.” A Question about Jesus' Authority (Mark
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa ɛ lɛɛ maŋ di nɛ, kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɛ ke fane bɔɔse Wurubuarɛ nɛ, ɛ nyiŋmaa gyae ke kaŋ ke.” Ba ke bɔɔse Yesu ɔ doŋ wose man nombia Maake

english_verse: Then Jesus left the city and went out to the village of Bethany, where he spent the night. Jesus Puts a Curse on a Fig Tree (Mark
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu leewɔ be gyaŋ donɔɔ keŋ man, te ɔ gyuuwɔ Bɛtani donɔɔ man, te ɔ doowɔ botɔɔ. Yesu ke falaa fiigi daŋ keŋ Maake

english_verse: Jesus answered, “I have just one question to ask you. If you answer it, I will tell you where I got the right to do these things.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Maŋ mɔ ne gyae maa bɔɔse ŋon nombii dokoloŋ. Nyi akpaa ɛ tiranɔɔ fa maŋ nɛ kenaŋ na maŋ mɔ maa wola ŋon doŋ keŋ me dana mɛɛ yɛɛ mena abɔɔ kɛŋa pou.

english_verse: When Jesus got up the next morning, he was hungry. He started out for the city,
translation_verse: Tɛɛ kena nebo nebo fuu keŋ Yesu ba bese ɔ wɔle ɔ ne kɔŋ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, na tanam na ko e.

english_verse: But Jesus said to them, “If you have faith and don't doubt, I promise you can do what I did to this tree. And you will be able to do even more. You can tell this mountain to get up and jump into the sea, and it will.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, akpaa ɛ lɛɛ maŋ di nideli te ɛ te lɛɛ aŋmaareŋ ya nɛ, na kpene keŋ be kɔŋ fiigi daŋ kei dɔɔ nɛ kaageŋ te ɛ ke tale yɛɛ ya. Mɔna ɛ ke tale yako bula kei koraŋ nyiaa, ‘Koro ke yala ɛpo man’ te ke gyae ke kɔŋ mena.

english_verse: So they told Jesus, “We don't know.” Jesus said, “Then I won't tell you who gave me the right to do what I do.” A Story about Two Sons
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ be tiranɔɔ fa Yesu nyiaa, “Dɔɔ gyeŋ ya.” Keŋte Yesu mɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Akpaa mena na, maŋ mɔ ba wola ŋon me doŋ keŋ da lee ya.” Bia bala baale wose maŋ dudu

english_verse: Jesus told the chief priests and leaders to listen to this story: A land owner once planted a vineyard. He built a wall around it and dug a pit to crush the grapes in. He also built a lookout tower. Then he rented out his vineyard and left the country.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ nyii dudu daale bela. Wɔɔfarale ŋolo fara wɔɔ te ɔ duu daasebia ŋaale ŋan ŋe yɛɛ ŋgba akutuse te bɛɛ baake ŋa nyi gerepo nɛ. Ɔ dii abaŋ kilisi wɔɔ keŋ, te ɔ wula bɔɔ keŋ baa sara daasebia ŋan ke man na baa lese ŋa loŋ lee ŋe man nɛ. Te ɔ baŋa deni dɔɔloŋ dɔɔloŋ daale, keŋ walaŋ ke kyaa ke man na waa gyee wɔɔ kenaŋ. Te ɔ moo wɔɔ keŋ do paasetena baale nyiŋmaa man nyi baa kɛɛ ke dɔɔ, te ɔ korowɔ laŋ gbɛɛ.

english_verse: Jesus had gone into the temple and was teaching when the chief priests and the leaders of the people came up to him. They asked, “What right do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man, te ɔ kɛɛ wola wɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan nɛ, saese kegyiise na donɔɔ kegyiise kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ kaa bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Doŋ woŋti yaa n dana n ne yɛɛ mena abɔɔ kɛŋa pou? Amɔte faa na neŋ mena doŋ kenaŋ?”

english_verse: Who gave John the right to baptize? Was it God in heaven or merely some human being?” They thought it over and said to each other, “We can't say God gave John this right. Jesus will ask us why we didn't believe John.
