translation_verse: Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋkesɔ keŋ ɔ be sɔ balaŋ nɛ, ka lee aleŋ? Ka lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ yaa balaŋ gyaŋ?” Botɔɔ te ba gyoo nombia ŋan man te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, “Woŋ te dɛɛ yako? ‘Akpaa de yako nyi ka lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ na, waa bɔɔse daa nyi woŋ dɔɔ te da te lɛɛ Gyɔn nombia ŋan di ya?’

english_verse: On the other hand, these people think John was a prophet, and we are afraid of what they might do to us. That's why we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize.”
translation_verse: Te akpaa de yako nyi ka lee balaŋ gyaŋ na, dee yee balaŋ burum kɛwɔ mɔ. Nawolo nyi be popou ne lɛɛ di nideli nyi Gyɔn yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre e.”

english_verse: His son told him he would not do it, but later he changed his mind and went.
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ bu ŋon yako e nyiaa, mɔɔ gyae maa gyu ya, mɔna wɔle wɔle nɛ, ɔ kaa kyɛɛkee wa agyueŋ te ɔ gyuuwɔ.

english_verse: The man then told his younger son to go work in the vineyard. The boy said he would, but he didn't go.
translation_verse: Keŋte balee ŋon gyuuwɔ ɔ bu kemaasa ŋon gyaŋ ke yako ŋon mɔ mena ke. Ɔ bu kemaasa ŋon sɛɛwɔ nyi, ‘Yoo taata me nyiiwɔ, maa gyu,’ mɔna ɔ te gyu ya.

english_verse: Which one of the sons obeyed his father? “The older one,” the chief priests and leaders answered. Then Jesus told them: You can be sure tax collectors and prostitutes will get into the kingdom of God before you ever will!
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, ɛ wola maŋ ya agyueŋ. Bia bala kɛwɔ man na, woŋti yɛɛna ɔ kya ŋon kegyaebii? Te balaŋ baŋ tiranɔɔ fae e nyiaa, ‘Bu kegyia ŋon.’ Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, ‘Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, lempoolɛɛra na kafoŋtena te taŋgbɛɛ ŋon bɛɛ gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man.’

english_verse: When John the Baptist showed you how to do right, you would not believe him. But these evil people did believe. And even when you saw what they did, you still would not change your minds and believe. Renters of a Vineyard (Mark
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon kɔŋawɔ kaa wola ŋon gbɛɛ kpaakpaate keŋ ɛ ka mo, mɔna ɛ te lɛɛ e di ya. Lempoolɛɛra na kafoŋtena baŋ dɔɔ, be lɛɛ e di. Kenaŋ wɔle ɛ kena mena koraŋ nɛ, ɛ te kyɛɛkee ya agyueŋ na ɛ ke lɛɛ e di ya.” Paasetena baale dudu Maake

english_verse: When it was harvest time, the owner sent some servants to get his share of the grapes.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kelii nyi baa kɔ daasebia ŋan nɛ, ɔ kpila ɔ tewulɛɛ gyu paasetena baŋ gyaŋ nyi baa fa e ɔ wui nɔɔ.

english_verse: But the renters grabbed those servants. They beat up one, killed one, and stoned one of them to death.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te paasetena baŋ kyaŋa ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ, te be tuku ŋolo te ba koo ŋolo te be fuŋii ŋolo mɔ boya.

english_verse: He then sent more servants than he did the first time. But the renters treated them in the same way.
translation_verse: Keŋte wɔɔte ŋon besewɔ kpila ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ ba boe kela gyaŋgba nyibaŋ baŋ gyu paasetena baŋ gyaŋ, mɔna be yɛɛ baŋ mɔ mena ke.

english_verse: Finally, the owner sent his own son to the renters, because he thought they would respect him.
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, wɔɔte ŋon kpila ŋon gbagba ɔ bu te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, ‘Mberem ŋon na baa bu e.’

english_verse: So they grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ be wɔŋee e lee wɔɔ keŋ man ka ko.

english_verse: Jesus asked, “When the owner of that vineyard comes, what do you suppose he will do to those renters?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Mena dɔɔ wee keŋ wɔɔte ŋon de kɔŋ na, woŋ te waa yɛɛ paasetena baŋ?”

english_verse: The chief priests and leaders answered, “He will kill them in some horrible way. Then he will rent out his vineyard to people who will give him his share of grapes at harvest time.”
translation_verse: Keŋte be tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Waa koe mena balaŋ kumɛɛ benaŋ pou, na waa mo wɔɔ keŋ do paasetena wɔlɛɛ baŋ debaŋ de lii na, baa fa e ɔ wui nɔɔ nɛ nyiŋmaa man.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “You surely know that the Scriptures say, ‘The stone the builders tossed aside is now the most important stone of all. This is something the Lord has done, and it is amazing to us.’
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ te kala ta Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan na ɛ kena kpene keŋ ne yako nɛ yee? Be ŋmarasewɔ see nyiaa, ‘Boe keŋ deni mmaraa baŋ be bɛɛ nɛ, keŋ kaa bese na mokɔɔ kasee boe keŋ ne yeli deni keŋ ne yɛɛ doŋ. De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ la te kɔŋawɔ mena, te ke yɛɛ kpene keŋ ke dana gyakoloŋ de siaman’ ”

english_verse: I tell you God's kingdom will be taken from you and given to people who will do what he demands.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ waa lɛo ɔ gyoori keŋ lee ɛ gyaŋ, na waa mo fa balaŋ baŋ baa yɛɛ ɔ kegyaebii nɛ.

english_verse: Anyone who stumbles over this stone will be crushed, and anyone it falls on will be smashed to pieces.”
translation_verse: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ yale mena boe kei dɔɔ na, gyae ke keretii e kpetekpetekpete. Te nyaŋ walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon mɔ boe keŋ de yale n dɔɔ na, nyaŋ ke pɛtɛɛ.”

english_verse: So they looked for a way to arrest Jesus. But they were afraid to, because the people thought he was a prophet.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ be gyae gbɛɛ keŋ baa mo kyaŋ e, mɔna be yee balaŋ dikpii keŋ. Nawolo nyi bɛɛ lɛɛ di nideli nyi Yesu yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre e.

english_verse: The Pharisees got together and planned how they could trick Jesus into saying something wrong.
translation_verse: Keŋte Farasiitena baŋ korowɔ lee yenaŋ gyu ke bake Yesu nyeeman, mena keŋ baa kaa teese ɔ nɔɔman na baa mo ŋon gbagba ɔ nɔɔwoya kyaŋ e.

english_verse: Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother.
translation_verse: “De Gbeŋgyoo, de naana Mosesi ŋmarase mmaraa kei fa daa nyi, akpaa ŋolo da yem tina ɔ wɛɛle yela te be ta lolo bia ya na, kaboena nyi ɔ naabu waa yala lialo ŋon na baa lola bia ɔ kegyia ŋon yele man.

english_verse: Once again Jesus used stories to teach the people:
translation_verse: Yesu besewɔ kolosi fa balaŋ baŋ aduya man nyiaa,

english_verse: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a king gave a wedding banquet for his son.
translation_verse: “Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ ŋgba gyoo ŋon ɔ ba do kesaa fa ɔ bu ŋon ɔ ba kpeo alo nɛ.

english_verse: The king sent some servants to tell the invited guests to come to the banquet, but the guests refused.
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ kpila ɔ tewulɛɛ nyiaa, baa gyu ke baake balaŋ baŋ ɔ be lɔ yele baake wɔ nɛ nyi baa kɔŋ na be kaa di, mɔna be bɛe ba te kɔŋ ya.

english_verse: But the guests did not pay any attention. Some of them left for their farms, and some went to their places of business.
translation_verse: Mɔna balaŋ baŋ ɔ be lɔ yele baake nɛ ta mo ɔ nombia ŋan yɛɛ kolo ya. Kei da koro na ɔ te laŋ ɔ wɔɔ, kei mɔ da koro na ɔ te laŋ ɔ tom man.

english_verse: Others grabbed the servants, then beat them up and killed them.
translation_verse: Balaŋ akaŋ baŋ kyaŋa gyoo ŋon tewulɛɛ baŋ, te be tuku wɔ te ba koe wɔ.

english_verse: This made the king so furious that he sent an army to kill those murderers and burn down their city.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ gyoo ŋon gyeŋa baŋ nideli te ɔ kpila ɔ yoonɔɔtena te be ka koe balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ koe ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ nɛ, te ba too be donɔɔ keŋ mɔ.

english_verse: Then he said to the servants, “It is time for the wedding banquet, and the invited guests don't deserve to come.
translation_verse: Keŋte gyoo ŋon baake ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ bɔɔ kaa nɛ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, ‘Ba te saa ma alowɔle weenɛɛ ŋan taŋ, mɔna balaŋ baŋ mɔɔ lɔ yele baake wɔ nɛ, ba tekaboena nyi baa kɔŋ kaa di ya.

english_verse: Go out to the street corners and tell everyone you meet to come to the banquet.”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛ ŋere gyoo donɔɔ keŋ abɔnterese man, na ɛ ke baake walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɛ kena nɛ, nyi waa kɔŋ alowɔle keŋ tɛɛ.’

english_verse: When the king went in to meet the guests, he found that one of them wasn't wearing the right kind of clothes for the wedding.
translation_verse: Mɔna gyoo ŋon ke kɔŋ kaa gyoo dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man ɔ kaa kɛɛ balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ baake nɛ, ɔ naa baale ŋolo kyaa be man keŋ ɔ ba doona alowɔletɛɛ kegba keŋ kaboena nyi walaŋ kamasɛ waa do nɛ ya.

english_verse: Tell us what you think! Should we pay taxes to the Emperor or not?”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ yako daa nya agyueŋ. Dei nyi dɛɛ tɔ lempoo fa Romantena gyoo dinaa ŋon, yaa de na tɔɔ ya?”

english_verse: They sent some of their followers and some of Herod's followers to say to him, “Teacher, we know that you are honest. You teach the truth about what God wants people to do. And you treat everyone with the same respect, no matter who they are.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ be kpila be kaseela baŋ man baale kpu na gyoo Herode balaŋ gyu Yesu gyaŋ. Keŋte be yako e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, da gyeŋ nyi n nombia doo na ŋe gbɛɛ, te n na wola balaŋ Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛneŋ kpaakpaa ŋan bo na anokoare. Da gyeŋ nyi ŋolo be tale beo neŋ ya, nawolo nyi n bɛɛ kɛɛ balaŋ siaman ya.

english_verse: Let me see one of the coins used for paying taxes.” They brought him a silver coin,
translation_verse: Ɛ mo kɔbabii keŋ ɛ na mo tɔ lempoo nɛ kɔŋ maa kɛɛ.” Keŋte ba moo kɔbabii keŋ daale kɔna e.

english_verse: “The Emperor's,” they answered. Then Jesus told them, “Give the Emperor what belongs to him and give God what belongs to God.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te be tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Romantena gyoo dinaa ŋon.” Mena dɔɔ Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Mena na kpene keŋ yɛɛ Gyoo dinaa ŋon wui na ɛ mo fa e, te kpene keŋ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ mɔ wui na ɛ mo fa e.”

english_verse: His answer surprised them so much that they walked away. Life in the Future World (Mark
translation_verse: Be ke nyii Yesu nɔɔwoya kɛŋa nɛ, doo be nɔɔ. Mena dɔɔ be laŋawɔ tina e yela. Yesu kawola lee yeŋ wɔle kefoŋ wose man Maake

english_verse: At last the woman died.
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, te alo ŋon mɔ yeŋawɔ.

english_verse: All the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets are based on these two commandments. About David's Son (Mark
translation_verse: Mosesi mmaraase ŋan pou na Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ pou nombia ŋan bɔɔ wolo nɛ, seŋae mmaraase ala kɛŋa dɔɔ.” Amɔte yɛna nyeelɛɛre Kristo ŋon? Maake

english_verse: The Sadducees did not believe people would rise to life after death. So that same day some of the Sadducees came to Jesus and said:
translation_verse: Mena wee kenaŋ ke nɛ, Sadukiitena baŋ bɛɛ yako nyi yeŋ wɔle kefoŋ be kyaa ya nɛ kɔŋawɔ Yesu gyaŋ kaa bɔɔse e nyiaa,

english_verse: Jesus answered: You are completely wrong! You don't know what the Scriptures teach. And you don't know anything about the power of God.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ ta yoo, nawolo nyi ɛ ba gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ya, te ɛ ba gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ doŋ mɔ ya.

english_verse: And as for people being raised to life, God was speaking to you when he said,
translation_verse: Ɛ te kala ta Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan, na ɛ kena kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yako lee kefoŋ lee yeŋ man wose man nombia yee? Ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa,

english_verse: “I am the God worshiped by Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” He isn't the God of the dead, but of the living.
translation_verse: ‘Maŋ yɛna Aberaham na Asiki na Gyekɔpo Wurubuarɛ.’ Wurubuarɛ te yɛɛ woya Wurubuarɛ e ya, mɔna ɔ yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ be kyaa na be sia nɛ Wurubuarɛ e.”

english_verse: The crowds were surprised to hear what Jesus was teaching. The Most Important Commandment (Mark
translation_verse: Balaŋ dikpii keŋ ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, ɔ kawola keŋ doo be nɔɔ. Mmaraa dinaate Maake

english_verse: After Jesus had made the Sadducees look foolish, the Pharisees heard about it and got together.
translation_verse: Farasiitena baŋ ke nyii nyi Yesu ta mo ɔ nyansa di Sadukiitena baŋ dɔɔ nɛ, be popou kaa gyaŋeewɔ debodokoloŋ.

english_verse: “Teacher, what is the most important commandment in the Law?”
translation_verse: “De Gbeŋgyoo, Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan pou na, woŋti kela la?”

english_verse: This is the first and most important commandment.
translation_verse: Kei yɛna mmaraa siamante na keŋ ka kela ŋe popou.

english_verse: The second most important commandment is like this one. And it is, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”
translation_verse: Alate keŋ mɔ yɛɛ ŋgba gyaŋgbate keŋ nɛ. Keŋ yɛna nyi, ‘Gyae n dɔɔ ŋgba nyaŋ gbagba n wose nɛ.’

english_verse: While the Pharisees were still there, Jesus asked them,
translation_verse: Farasiitena baŋ ke yilaa debodokoloŋ nɛ, Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa,

english_verse: ‘The Lord said to my Lord: Sit at my right side until I make your enemies into a footstool for you.’
translation_verse: ‘De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ ŋon yako me Gbeŋgyoo nyi, tisi kyaa me dunoluŋ dɔɔ, kelii debaŋ keŋ maa mo n kɔlala baŋ do n tɛɛ.’

english_verse: “What do you think about the Messiah? Whose family will he come from?” They answered, “He will be a son of King David.”
translation_verse: “Sena te ɛ na gyueŋ lee nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ wose man? Ɔ yɛɛ amɔte ɔ naanabii e?” Keŋte be tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Ɔ yɛɛ gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii e.”

english_verse: If David called the Messiah his Lord, how can the Messiah be a son of King David?”
translation_verse: Akpaa nyi gyoo Defidi baake nyeelɛɛre ŋon nyi ɔ ‘Gbeŋgyoo’ na, kenaŋ na sena te nyeelɛɛre ŋon waa bese gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii?”

english_verse: No one was able to give Jesus an answer, and from that day on, no one dared ask him any more questions.
translation_verse: Be man ŋolo te tale tiranɔɔ fa Yesu ya. Te lee mena wee kenaŋ ne gyu mɔ na, ŋolo te tale gyɔ ɔ konɔɔ bɔɔse e nombii daale bela ya.

english_verse: If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.
translation_verse: Walaŋ ŋon ɔ gyina ɔ wose adido na, Wurubuarɛ waa yɔkɔse e, te ŋon ɔ yɔkɔse ɔ wose na, Wurubuarɛ waa gyina adido.

english_verse: Whoever is the greatest should be the servant of the others.
translation_verse: Walaŋ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ kegyia e ɛ man nɛ, waa bese ɛ kegbetibii.

english_verse: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! Your people have killed the prophets and have stoned the messengers who were sent to you. I have often wanted to gather your people, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you wouldn't let me.
translation_verse: O Gyerusalɛmtena! O Gyerusalɛmtena! Ɛmɛɛ koona Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra, te ɛ fuŋii balaŋ baŋ Wurubuarɛ be kpilii ɛ gyaŋ nɛ boya. Ma te yɛɛ kakyeŋ nyi maa yilaa ŋon do me tɛɛ, ŋgba mena keŋ kegyim talabo ne fɛlɛɛ ɔ beŋkelɛɛ wuu ɔ bia dɔɔ nɛ, mɔna ɛ te fa maŋ gbɛɛ ya.

english_verse: Jesus said to the crowds and to his disciples:
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu kolosiwɔ fa balaŋ dikpii keŋ na ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa,

english_verse: The Pharisees and the teachers of the Law are experts in the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: “Gyudatena mmaraa wolala na Farasiitena baŋ yɛna balaŋ baŋ be dana gbɛɛ nyii baa tale lese Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan man.

english_verse: So obey everything they teach you, but don't do as they do. After all, they say one thing and do something else.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ kaboena nyi yaa tei wɔ, na ɛ ke sila kpene kamasɛ keŋ bɛɛ wola ŋon nɛ. Mɔna ɛ na yɛɛ kpene keŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ ya, nawolo nyi bɔɔ ne di be kawola keŋ dɔɔ ya.

english_verse: They pile heavy burdens on people's shoulders and won't lift a finger to help.
translation_verse: Bɛɛ bake kasolɔse ŋan kela balaŋ nɛ yere wɔ, mɔna baŋ gbagba bɛɛ sɛɛ yɛɛ kolo kyomii kyɔ wɔ na baa tale seele be kasolɔse ŋenaŋ ya.

english_verse: Everything they do is just to show off in front of others. They even make a big show of wearing Scripture verses on their foreheads and arms, and they wear big tassels for everyone to see.
translation_verse: Kpene kamasɛ keŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ, bɛɛ yɛɛ bo na balaŋ ke nyiŋ naa wɔ. Wonembu tɔne adakaasebia ŋan bɛɛ yɛɛ te bɛɛ ŋmarase Wurubuarɛ nombia do ŋe man te bɛɛ mo mataa be tirinɔɔse na be baamense dɔɔ nɛ, bee yeli be wui ŋan na keeree. Te ŋmeeseŋ ŋan bɛɛ mo gbili ba kegba wurikyimse ŋan nɔɔsebia te ŋee wuluŋu nɛ, bee yeli be wui ne yɛɛ dɔɔloŋ dɔɔloŋ.

english_verse: They love the best seats at banquets and the front seats in the synagogues.
translation_verse: Bɛɛ gyae akyaese kpaakpaa be dɛɛgyaŋee di gyoŋ, na Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man.

english_verse: And when they are in the market, they like to have people greet them as their teachers.
translation_verse: Bɛɛ gyae nyi balaŋ ke buŋii do wɔ nɔɔ balaŋ dɛɛgyaŋee man, te bɛɛ gyae mɔ nyi balaŋ ke baake wɔ nyi ‘Wolala.’

english_verse: But none of you should be called a teacher. You have only one teacher, and all of you are like brothers and sisters.
translation_verse: Mɔna ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ, ɛ na yele baa baake ŋon nyi ‘Wolala’ ya, nawolo nyi ɛ popou dana wolale dokoloŋ kooŋ bo, te ɛ yɛɛ walaŋ dokoloŋ mɔ biawɔ.

english_verse: Don't call anyone on earth your father. All of you have the same Father in heaven.
translation_verse: Te ɛ na baake ŋolo mɔ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nyi ɛ ‘Kya’ ya, nawolo nyi ɛ dana kya dokoloŋ kooŋ bo. Ŋon yɛna ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You lock people out of the kingdom of heaven. You won't go in yourselves, and you keep others from going in.
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ mmaraa wolala na Farasiitena baŋ ɛ na beo ɛ wose, ɛ laako. Nawolo nyi ɛ na beo liala lɛo ba abɔɔ ŋan be dana nɛ. Ɛ ne fane kyare na ɛ ka mo beo balaŋ nyi ɛ yɛɛ balaŋ kpaakpaawɔ. Mena dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ waa wɔŋ ɛ deŋela nideli kela walaŋ kamasɛ.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You travel over land and sea to win one follower. And when you have done so, you make that person twice as fit for hell as you are.
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ mmaraa wolala na Farasiitena baŋ ɛ na beo ɛ wose, ɛ laako. Nawolo nyi ɛ na mo ɛpo dɔɔ, kila tɛɛle dɔɔ pou nyi ɛ ke nyiŋ walaŋ dokoloŋ na ɔ kaa sila Wurubuarɛ. Mɔna akpaa ɛ nyiŋ walaŋ ŋonaŋ na, ɛ ne yeli ɔ dinɔɔ na bese wɔlɛɛ koraŋ keŋ nyi diyem keŋ waa naa boalaŋ man nɛ, kela ɛmɛɛ gbagba wui betabola.

english_verse: You are in for trouble! You are supposed to lead others, but you are blind. You teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the temple. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gold in the temple.
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ siayɛlɛɛsatena baŋ ɛ na wola balaŋ gbɛɛ nɛ, ɛ laako nawolo nyi ɛ kpa akpaa ŋolo de kaŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ ntam na ka te yɛɛ kolo ya, mɔna akpaa ŋolo de kaŋ kɔba yayam abɔɔ ŋan doo Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man ntam na, kaboena nyi waa di ntam kenaŋ dɔɔ.

english_verse: You blind fools! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold sacred?
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ siayɛlɛɛsatena ayiŋtena, woŋti kela na ke dɔɔ? Kɔba yayam abɔɔ ŋan yaa Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ ne yele kɔba abɔɔ ŋan na bese Wurubuarɛ wui nɛ?

english_verse: You also teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the altar. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gift on the altar.
translation_verse: Ɛ na bese yako nyi, akpaa ŋolo de kaŋ Wurubuarɛ kedeesa boe keŋ ntam na te yɛɛ kolo ya, mɔna akpaa ɔ kaŋ abɔɔ ŋan gyakaa kedeesa boe keŋ dɔɔ nɛ ntam na, kaboena nyi waa di ntam keŋ dɔɔ.

english_verse: Are you blind? Which is more important, the gift or the altar that makes the gift sacred?
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ siayɛlɛɛsatena, woŋti kela na ke dɔɔ? Abɔɔ ŋan gyakaa kedeesa boe keŋ dɔɔ nɛ, yaa kedeesa boe keŋ ne yele kedeesa abɔɔ ŋan na bese Wurubuarɛ wui nɛ?

english_verse: And anyone who swears by the temple also swears by God, who lives there.
translation_verse: Te walaŋ ŋon ɔ kaŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ ntam na, nawolo baa nyi ɔ te kaŋ keŋ na Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa ke man nɛ ntam.

english_verse: To swear by heaven is the same as swearing by God's throne and by the one who sits on that throne.
translation_verse: Mena mɔ te walaŋ ŋon ɔ kaŋ adido ntam na, nawolo baa nyi ɔ te kaŋ Wurubuarɛ gyoorogbeli keŋ, na Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa ke dɔɔ nɛ ntam.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your garden, such as mint, dill, and cumin. Yet you neglect the more important matters of the Law, such as justice, mercy, and faithfulness. These are the important things you should have done, though you should not have left the others undone either.
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ mmaraa wolala na Farasiitena baŋ ɛ na beo ɛ wose, ɛ laako. Nawolo nyi ɛ na mo ɛ wɔɔman abɔɔ buruburu gba kufu man dekpeŋ dokoloŋ fa Wurubuarɛ, mɔna ɛ te tina kpene keŋ ne hia nyi yaa yɛɛ mmaraa keŋ man nɛ yela. Ɛ bee di nombia na ŋe gbɛɛ ya, te ɛ bee wii balaŋ waraŋase ya, te ɛ bee di anokoare mɔ ya. Nafɔ kaboena nyi yaa yɛɛ mena abɔɔ kɛŋa pou, na ɛ ka bese fa ɛ wɔɔman abɔɔ buruburu ŋan kufu man dekpeŋ dokoloŋ bela kpu.

english_verse: You blind leaders! You strain out a small fly but swallow a camel.
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ siayɛlɛɛsatena baŋ ɛ dɔŋ balaŋ siaman. Ɛ ne tale kyɔsee lese wonembia buruburu lee ɛ soloŋse man, mɔna ɛ ne lilii wonembia dinaana.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You wash the outside of your cups and dishes, while inside there is nothing but greed and selfishness.
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ mmaraa wolala na Farasiitena baŋ ɛ na beo ɛ wose, ɛ laako. Nawolo nyi ɛ ne faafo ɛ leenɔɔse na ɛ nyefalɛɛ wɔle bo, mɔna ŋe man dana ayimɛɛ. Nawolo nyi abɔɔ ŋan doo ŋe man nɛ, anibiri na doŋ te ɛ na mo ŋere balaŋ lɛo ŋa.

english_verse: You blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of a cup, and then the outside will also be clean.
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ Farasiitena siayɛlɛɛsatena, ɛ taŋgbɛɛ faafo ɛ leenɔɔse na ɛ nyefalɛɛ ŋan tɔɔman na, ŋe wɔle mɔ gyae ka lee.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You're like tombs that have been whitewashed. On the outside they are beautiful, but inside they are full of bones and filth.
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ mmaraa wolala na Farasiitena baŋ ɛ na beo ɛ wose, ɛ laako! Nawolo nyi ɛ yɛɛ ŋgba yebɔɔse ŋan bɔɔ mo kolo wolom gyilaa ŋe wɔle na ke yɛɛ kyeo nɛ. Mɔna ŋe tɔɔman na, denibalaŋ ŋɔɔ na ba wosenaane adalɛɛ te wulu ŋe man.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are nothing but show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You build monuments for the prophets and decorate the tombs of good people.
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ mmaraa wolala na Farasiitena baŋ ɛ na beo ɛ wose, ɛ laako. Nawolo nyi ɛ ne ma Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra na balaŋ baŋ be gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ yebɔɔse nideli, na ŋa ke yɛɛ kyeo.

english_verse: And you claim you would not have taken part with your ancestors in killing the prophets.
translation_verse: Te ɛ ne yako nyi, ‘Daa deŋ kyaa la de naanaɔ debaŋ keŋ nɛ, nafɔ deŋ te kpuu na wɔ na baa koe Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ ya.’

english_verse: But you prove you really are the relatives of the ones who killed the prophets.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛ nɔɔwoya kɛŋa nawola la nyi, ɛ te sɛɛ nyi balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ koe Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ nɛ, ɛ yɛɛ be duuluŋ man balaŋwɔ.

english_verse: You are nothing but snakes and the children of snakes! How can you escape going to hell?
translation_verse: Ɛ dana nombiakumɛɛ ŋgba doma nɛ. Ɛ be gyae ɛ ke tale yeu lee Wurubuarɛ nyiŋmaa man, keŋ nyi ɔ be bu ŋon fɔɔ na waa yela ɛ ka gyoo boalaŋ man ya.

english_verse: I can promise that you people living today will be punished for all these things! Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Luke
translation_verse: Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, kɛŋa pou deŋele kewɔŋ gyae ke kɔŋ ɛmɛɛ gyɛŋ wee kei balaŋ dɔɔ. Mena keŋ Yesu ne gyae Gyerusalɛm

english_verse: After Jesus left the temple, his disciples came over and said, “Look at all these buildings!”
translation_verse: Yesu ka lee Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man ɔ ne gyu nɛ, ɔ kaseela baŋ wɔŋa wa agyueŋ kɔŋ denɛɛ ŋan seŋɛɛ botɔɔ nɛ kyeo dɔɔ.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Do you see these buildings? They will certainly be torn down! Not one stone will be left in place.” Warning about Trouble (Mark
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Ŋgba ɛ ne naa abɔɔ kɛŋa pou. Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, baa tekerii ŋe popou lɔ keŋ nyi ɛ be gyae ɛ kena boe na boe gyakaa ke dɔɔ dɔɔ ya.”

english_verse: Many will come and claim to be me. They will say they are the Messiah, and they will fool many people.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi balaŋ burum baa kɔŋ ma yele man, na be kaa mo ba wose nyi baŋ yɛna maŋ nyeelɛɛre ŋon, na baa beo balaŋ burum na baa yo gbɛɛ.

english_verse: You will soon hear about wars and threats of wars, but don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but that isn't the end.
translation_verse: Ɛ nyii yoo nombia na, ɛ na yele ɛ wɔe ke lɔ ŋon ya. Nawolo nyi kaboena nyi nombia kɛŋa pou dɛɛkɔŋ, mɔna kɛŋa bɛɛ wolo nyi ɔto keŋ te lii ya.

english_verse: Later, as Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, his disciples came to him in private and asked, “When will this happen? What will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the world?”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu gyuuwɔ ke kyaa oliifi bula keŋ dɔɔ. Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ baageŋ korowɔ gyu ɔ gyaŋ te be bɔɔse e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo yako daa, debaŋ woŋti te kɛŋa pou gyae ke kɔŋ. Woŋ te dɛɛ mo gyeŋ nyi n kabese kɔŋ, na debaŋ ɔtomante keŋ ta benaa?”

english_verse: Nations and kingdoms will go to war against each other. People will starve to death, and in some places there will be earthquakes.
translation_verse: Tɛɛlese baa koro seŋ tia dɔŋa, na gyooneŋ mɔ ka koro seŋ tia dɔŋa. Tanam dinaa gyae ke yala, te tɛɛle mɔ gyae kewosi yenaase burum.

english_verse: It will be a terrible time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children.
translation_verse: Weeya ŋenaŋ man na, fulɛɛtena na talabose baa laako.

english_verse: But this is just the beginning of troubles.
translation_verse: Kɛŋa pou yɛɛ yaa ŋgba fulute kadoŋee kewalaŋ nɛ.

english_verse: You will be arrested, punished, and even killed. Because of me, you will be hated by people of all nations.
translation_verse: Lee maŋ dɔɔ balaŋ pou baa kɔla ŋon, na baa kyaŋee ŋon na baa naase ŋon diyem na baa koe ŋon.

english_verse: Many will give up and will betray and hate each other.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ man te balaŋ burum baa tina maŋ kelɛɛdi yela. Baa kɔla dɔŋa, na baa lese dɔŋa fa.

english_verse: Many false prophets will come and fool a lot of people.
translation_verse: Akɛrɛɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baa kɔŋ, na be kaa beo balaŋ burum.

english_verse: Someone may say, “Here is the Messiah!” or “There he is!” But don't believe it.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ de lii te ŋolo de yako ŋon nyi ‘Ɛ kɛɛ Kristo ŋon kyaa kɛbo yaa ɔ kyaa yenaŋ daale na, ɛ na lɛɛ ke di ya.’

english_verse: And pray that you won't have to escape in winter or on a Sabbath.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛ fane fa Wurubuarɛ nyi, ɛ keyeu wee kenaŋ na kɔŋ waaretɛɛ yaa kefɛɛfowee ya.

english_verse: Someday you will see that “Horrible Thing” in the holy place, just as the prophet Daniel said. Everyone who reads this must try to understand!
translation_verse: Akpaa ɛ naa mmusuo nombii Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man, ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ be tɛɛ ɔ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Daniɛle dɔɔ kolosi see lee ka wose man nɛ, na kaboena nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon waa kala mena nɔɔwoya ŋenaŋ na, waa yɛɛ kakyeŋ na waa nyii ŋe man nideli.

english_verse: If you are living in Judea at that time, run to the mountains.
translation_verse: De kɔŋ mena na, balaŋ baŋ be kyaa Gyudia tɛɛle man nɛ, baa yeu gyu bulase dɔɔ.

english_verse: If you are out in the field, don't go back for your coat.
translation_verse: Te akpaa ŋolo mɔ kyaa wɔɔman na, ɔ na bese gyu dɛɛ nyi ɔ kaa mo ɔ kegba ya.

english_verse: If God doesn't make the time shorter, no one will be left alive. But because of God's chosen ones, he will make the time shorter.
translation_verse: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ taŋ wula kɔkee kayeyɛɛ weeya kɛŋa. Na waa yɛɛ mena ya nɛ, nafɔ ŋolo na ŋolo be gyae waa ka ya. Mɔna lee ɔ balaŋ baŋ ɔ ba lese see dɔɔ nɛ, te waa kɔkee weeya ŋenaŋ.

english_verse: So when you see all these things happening, you will know the time has almost come.
translation_verse: Mena mɔ te akpaa ɛ naa mena nombia kɛŋa pou ne kɔŋ na, ɛ gyeŋ nyi maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon kekɔŋ ta benaa taŋ.

english_verse: When the Son of Man appears, things will be just as they were when Noah lived.
translation_verse: Mena keŋ be kɔŋ Nowa debaŋ keŋ nɛ, mena te maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon mɔ kabese kɔŋ gyae ke yɛɛ.

english_verse: No one knows the day or hour. The angels in heaven don't know, and the Son himself doesn't know. Only the Father knows.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, ŋolo ba gyeŋ wee na debaŋ keŋ kɛŋa pou gyae ke kɔŋ ya. Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ be kyaa adido, na maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon koraŋ ba gyeŋ ya. Gyisɛ me kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ waageŋ gyeŋa la.

english_verse: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day the flood came and Noah went into the big boat.
translation_verse: Weeya ŋenaŋ man pɛte loŋ keŋ ke kɔŋ kaa wɔlɛɛ tɛɛle nɛ, na balaŋ ne dii na bɛɛ nyɔɔ. Baala ne yala ala na bɛɛ mo ala mɔ fa yale, kelii wee keŋ Nowa ba gyoo adakaa dinaa keŋ man nɛ.

english_verse: They didn't know anything was happening until the flood came and swept them all away. This is how it will be when the Son of Man appears.
translation_verse: Be ta gyeŋ kpene keŋ ne kɔŋ ya, kelii debaŋ keŋ loŋ be kaa waale kuu wɔ pou laŋ nɛ. Mena te maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon ke kɔŋ gyae ke yɛɛ.

english_verse: Two men will be in the same field, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ man na, baala bala baa kyaa wɔɔman bɛɛ yɛɛ tom. Wurubuarɛ waa mo ŋolo tina ŋolo yela.

english_verse: Two women will be together grinding grain, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
translation_verse: Ala bala baa seŋ bɛɛ naŋ abɔɔ. Wurubuarɛ waa mo ŋolo tina ŋolo yela.

english_verse: So be on your guard! You don't know when your Lord will come.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛ doo ɛ wose dɔɔ, nawolo nyi ɛ ba gyeŋ wee keŋ ɛ gbeŋgyoo ŋon waa kɔŋ ya.

english_verse: Homeowners never know when a thief is coming, and they are always on guard to keep one from breaking in.
translation_verse: Ɛ tɔɔse nyiaa, nyi dekpaŋalaŋte deŋ gyeŋ debaŋ keŋ ŋmɛɛlate waa kɔŋ kaa ŋmɛɛle e nɛ, nafɔ waa deke ɔ dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ nyi ŋmɛɛlate be nyiŋ gbɛɛ kaa gyoo kuu wa abɔɔ ya.

english_verse: Always be ready! You don't know when the Son of Man will come. Faithful and Unfaithful Servants (Luke
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ doo ɛ wose, nawolo nyi maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon mɔ maa kɔŋ debaŋ keŋ ɛ loo be gyakaa ya.

english_verse: But suppose one of the servants thinks the master won't return until late.
translation_verse: Mɔna akpaa nyi tobaale ŋonaŋ yɛɛ tobaale kum e, te ɔ yɛɛ wa agyueŋ nyi ɔ gbeŋgyoo be gyae waa kɔŋ bileŋ ya,

english_verse: Who are faithful and wise servants? Who are the ones the master will put in charge of giving the other servants their food supplies at the proper time?
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ besewɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, amɔte yɛna tobaale ŋon ɔ sia be kare te ɔ ne di ɔ gbeŋgyoo mmaraase dɔɔ? Ŋon yɛna tobaale ŋon ɔ gbeŋgyoo waa mo ɔ tebia akaŋ baŋ do ɔ nyiŋmaa man nyi waa kɛɛ be dɔɔ, na waa fa wɔ weenɛɛ debaŋ keŋ kaboena nɛ.

english_verse: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
translation_verse: Akpaa tobaale ŋonaŋ seŋɛɛ ɔ tom man ɔ ne yɛɛ ke nideli mena, te ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon de kɔŋ kaa naa e na, ŋon na nyeebam.

english_verse: You may be sure a servant who is always faithful will be put in charge of everything the master owns.
translation_verse: Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, mena tobaale ŋonaŋ ɔ gbeŋgyoo waa mo wa abɔɔ pou do e ɔ nyiŋmaa man nyi waa kɛɛ ŋe dɔɔ.

english_verse: Suppose this evil servant starts beating the other servants and eats and drinks with people who are drunk.
translation_verse: te ɔ gyoo ɔ tebia akaŋ baŋ ketuku, te ŋon na soloŋ nyɔɔra ne dii bɛɛ nyɔɔ na,

english_verse: If that happens, the master will surely come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him.
translation_verse: ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon waa kaa lee ɔ dɔɔ wee keŋ ɔ loo be gyakaa ya nɛ.

english_verse: This servant will then be punished and thrown out with the ones who only pretended to serve their master. There they will cry and grit their teeth in pain.
translation_verse: Ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon de kɔŋ kaa naa e mena na, waa wɔŋ ɔ deŋele nideli na waa gegi e lee ɔ gyaŋ, na waa yela ɔ kaa kyaa balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ beo ba wose nɛ gyaŋ. Botɔɔ te waa kyaŋ ɔ nyee wii na waa tao ɔ kela.”

english_verse: The ones who were wise took along extra oil for their lamps.
translation_verse: Mɔna siakaralatena baŋ dɔɔ, baŋ moo nuŋ do porontiase man tela kpu na ŋan doo be kanease man nɛ.

english_verse: Then in the middle of the night someone shouted, “Here's the groom! Come to meet him!”
translation_verse: Ke lii tɛɛ nsana nɛ te akelelee yalawɔ nyiaa, ‘Alowɔle baale ŋon te kɔŋ! Ɛ lee kaa gyaŋee na e!’

english_verse: When the women got up and started getting their lamps ready,
translation_verse: Keŋte alebia kufu baŋ pou korowɔ te be gyinaa be kanea ŋmeeseŋ ŋan.

english_verse: the foolish ones said to the others, “Let us have some of your oil! Our lamps are going out.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ayiŋtena baŋ yako siakaralatena baŋ nyiaa, ‘De kanease na yekee, mena dɔɔ ɛ fa daa ɛ nuŋse ŋan kyomii.’

english_verse: Those who were wise answered, “There's not enough oil for all of us! Go and buy some for yourselves.”
translation_verse: Keŋte siakaralatena baŋ yako wɔ nyiaa, ‘Koa de nuŋse ŋan be gyae ka yee dɛɛ fa ŋon ya.’ Mena dɔɔ ɛ gyu donɔɔ man na ɛ kaa gyae ŋaale lɛɛ.

english_verse: But the groom replied, “I don't even know you!”
translation_verse: Keŋte alowɔle baale ŋon yako wɔ nyiaa, ‘Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa mɔɔ gyeŋ ŋon ya.’

english_verse: So, my disciples, always be ready! You don't know the day or the time when all this will happen. A Story about Three Servants (Luke
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, ‘Ɛ doo ɛ wose dɔɔ nideli, nawolo nyi ɛ ba gyeŋ wee yaa debaŋ keŋ maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa bese kɔŋ ya.’ ” Tewulɛɛ batooro baale dudu Luka

english_verse: Some time later the master of those servants returned. He called them in and asked what they had done with his money.
translation_verse: Be gbeŋgyoo ŋon kyaawɔ botɔɔ kyare pɛte ɔ kɔŋawɔ. Ɔ ke kɔŋ nɛ te ɔ baake ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ gyaŋee nyi baa gyoo be kɔbase ŋan man.

english_verse: You could have at least put my money in the bank, so I could have earned interest on it.”
translation_verse: weera n ta moo me kɔba ka see maŋ kɔba dɛɛsee man ya. Nafɔ ma ke kɔŋ kei na, ŋgba maa gyu ke lɛɛ keŋ na ka loŋ. Keŋte ɔ yako ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ nyiaa,

english_verse: The kingdom is also like what happened when a man went away and put his three servants in charge of all he owned.
translation_verse: Yesu besewɔ gyɔ dudu fa wɔ nyiaa, “Debaŋ kenaŋ man na Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ gyae ke yɛɛ ŋgba balee ŋon ɔ ne gyae waa gyu gbɛɛ, te ɔ be baake ɔ tewulɛɛ te ɔ be kpɛlɛɛ wa abɔɔ do wɔ be nyiŋmaa man nɛ.

english_verse: The man knew what each servant could do. So he handed
translation_verse: Ɔ faa walaŋ kamasɛ ŋgba mena keŋ waa tale tela nɛ. Ɔ faa ŋolo kɔba kakpoŋse anoŋ (

english_verse: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon yako e nyiaa, ‘Ao baale kpaakpaa anokoarete, karaŋ na keyɛɛ. N te di anokoare lee kyomii maa moo fa neŋ kei man, mena dɔɔ maa mo abɔɔ burum do n nyiŋmaa man. Kɔŋ na dee di gyoŋ keŋ mee dii nɛ.’

english_verse: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon yako e nyiaa, ‘Tobaale kpaakpaa anokoarete, karaŋ na ke yɛɛ. N te di anokoare lee kyomii maa moo fa neŋ kei man, mena dɔɔ maa mo abɔɔ burum do n nyiŋmaa man. Kɔŋ na dee di gyoŋ keŋ mee dii nɛ.’

english_verse: I was frightened and went out and hid your money in the ground. Here is every single coin!”
translation_verse: Me yee neŋ mena dɔɔ ma moo n kɔba keŋ ka weese bɔɔ man. Kɛɛ, n kɔba yaa, lɛɛ n woni.’

english_verse: The master of the servant told him, “You are lazy and good-for-nothing! You know I harvest what I don't plant and gather crops where I haven't scattered seed.
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon yako e nyiaa, ‘N yɛɛ tobaale kum na kebɔŋalete e. N gyeŋ nyi mɛɛ kɔ abɔɔ botɔɔ keŋ me te duu ya, te mee yilaa abɔɔ botɔɔ keŋ ma te ŋmatee ya na,

english_verse: Then the master said, “Now your money will be taken away and given to the servant with
translation_verse: ɛ lɛo kɔba keŋ lee ɔ gyaŋ na ɛ ka mo kpu ŋon ɔ be nyiŋ kakpoŋse kufu nɛ.

english_verse: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi walaŋ ŋon ɔ dana kolo na baa kpu e na waa nyiŋ burum, te ŋon ɔ be dana ya na, kyomii keŋ koraŋ ɔ dana nɛ baa lɛo ke lee ɔ gyaŋ.

english_verse: You are a worthless servant, and you will be thrown out into the dark where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.” The Final Judgment
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛ fuŋ tobaale ohuhoo kei ŋon ɔ be dana tɔnɔɔ ya nɛ leki ditiŋtɛɛ keŋ man. Botɔɔ te waa kyaŋ ɔ nyee wii na waa tao ɔ kela.’ ” Nombia kedi ɔtomante keŋ

english_verse: When the Son of Man comes in his glory with all his angels, he will sit on his royal throne.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Debaŋ keŋ maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa bese kɔŋ na, daa na Wurubuarɛ kpilala ne gyae la dɛɛ kɔŋ. Maa kɔŋ me gyoorobiiri man kaa kyaa me gyooro gbeli keŋ dɔɔ.

english_verse: The people of all nations will be brought before him, and he will separate them, as shepherds separate their sheep from their goats.
translation_verse: Tɛɛle kei balaŋ pou baa kaa yilaa me gyaŋ, na maa kpase be man akpeŋ ala ŋgba mena keŋ wonembia kɛɛlere ne kpase asoose lee namense man nɛ.

english_verse: He will place the sheep on his right and the goats on his left.
translation_verse: Maa mo namense baŋ see me dunoluŋ dɔɔ, na maa mo asoose baŋ see ma demena dɔɔ.

english_verse: Then the king will say to those on his right, “My father has blessed you! Come and receive the kingdom that was prepared for you before the world was created.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te maŋ gyoo ŋon maa yako baŋ be kyaa ma dunoluŋ dɔɔ nɛ nyiaa, ‘Ɛmɛɛ baŋ me kya be hiraa ŋon. Ɛ kɔŋ kaa kyaa Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ ɔ be wulaa desina see ŋon leenaŋ debaŋ keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ tɛɛle kei nɛ man.

english_verse: When I was hungry, you gave me something to eat, and when I was thirsty, you gave me something to drink. When I was a stranger, you welcomed me,
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi debaŋ keŋ tanam na ko maŋ na, ɛ faa maŋ weenɛɛ me diiwɔ. Debaŋ keŋ loŋboaneŋ na ko maŋ na ɛ faa maŋ loŋ me nyɔɔwɔ. Debaŋ keŋ me yɛɛ ŋɔɔle e na, ɛ lɛɛ maŋ gyu ya akpaŋalaŋse man,

english_verse: and when I was naked, you gave me clothes to wear. When I was sick, you took care of me, and when I was in jail, you visited me.”
translation_verse: debaŋ keŋ me dɔŋɛɛ diteŋteli na, ɛ faa maŋ abɔɔ ma doowɔ. Debaŋ keŋ mɛɛ wee na ɛ kaa kɛɛ me dɔɔ, debaŋ keŋ bɔɔ kyaŋ maŋ ketɔ deni na, ɛ ka doo maŋ nɔɔ.’

english_verse: When did we welcome you as a stranger or give you clothes to wear
translation_verse: Woromɔ te de naa neŋ nyi n yɛɛ ŋɔɔle te de lɛɛ neŋ gyu da akpaŋalaŋse man, yaa n dɔŋɛɛ diteŋteli te de faa neŋ abɔɔ n doowɔ?

english_verse: The king will answer, “Whenever you did it for any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you did it for me.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te maŋ gyoo ŋon maa tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, ‘Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɛ be yɛɛ fa me balaŋ kɛwɔ man kemaasabii koraŋ na, maŋ yaa ɛ yɛɛwɔ fa!’

english_verse: Then the king will say to those on his left, “Get away from me! You are under God's curse. Go into the everlasting fire prepared for the devil and his angels!
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te maa yako baŋ be kyaa ma demena dɔɔ nɛ nyiaa, ‘Ɛ seŋ lee me gyaŋ, ɛmɛɛ baŋ Wurubuarɛ be falaa ŋon nyi ya gyu ka gyoo boalaŋ degyapaa keŋ ɔ be desina see fa ɔbɔnsam na ɔ kpilala baŋ nɛ man.

english_verse: I was hungry, but you did not give me anything to eat, and I was thirsty, but you did not give me anything to drink.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi tanam koo maŋ na, ɛ te fa maŋ weenɛɛ ya, loŋboaneŋ koo maŋ na ɛ te fa maŋ loŋ ya.

english_verse: I was a stranger, but you did not welcome me, and I was naked, but you did not give me any clothes to wear. I was sick and in jail, but you did not take care of me.”
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ me yɛɛ ŋɔɔle e na ɛ ta mo maŋ gyu ya akpaŋalaŋse man ya. Me tɛɛwɔ diteŋteli na, ɛ te fa maŋ kolo maa do ya. Ma weewɔ mɔna ɛ te kaa do maŋ nɔɔ ya, te be kyaŋa maŋ ketɔ deni, mɔna ɛ te kaa do maŋ nɔɔ ya.’

english_verse: Then the people will ask, “Lord, when did we fail to help you when you were hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in jail?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te balaŋ baŋ baa bɔɔse maŋ gyoo ŋon nyiaa, ‘De gbeŋgyoo woromɔ te de naa neŋ nyi tanam na ko neŋ, yaa loŋboaneŋ na ko neŋ, yaa n yɛɛ ŋɔɔle e yaa n dɔŋ diteŋteli yaa n weewɔ yaa be kyaŋa neŋ ketɔ deni te da te kyɔ neŋ ya?’

english_verse: The king will say to them, “Whenever you failed to help any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you failed to do it for me.”
translation_verse: Keŋte maa tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, ‘Kpene keŋ ɛ be bɛɛ ɛ te yɛɛ fa me balaŋ kɛwɔ man kemaasabii koraŋ ya nɛ, na maŋ yaa ɛ bɛɛwɔ ɛ te yɛɛ fa ya.’

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “Those people will be punished forever. But the ones who pleased God will have eternal life.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te baa mo balaŋ benaŋ gyu botɔɔ keŋ baa naa diyem kekpaakekpaa nɛ, mɔna balaŋ baŋ be gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ dɔɔ, baa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaakekpaa.”

english_verse: Corinthians
translation_verse: Korintotena

english_verse: “You know two days from now will be Passover. This is when the Son of Man will be handed over to his enemies and nailed to a cross.”
translation_verse: “Ŋgba ɛ gyeŋ nyi teka weeya ala na nyeekelɛɛwee kedi keŋ te lii. Debaŋ kenaŋ man te baa lese maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon fa na baa gyɔ maŋ sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ.”

english_verse: Finally, Jesus returned to his disciples and said, “Are you still sleeping and resting? The time has come for the Son of Man to be handed over to sinners.
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ besewɔ kɔŋ ɔ kaseela baŋ gyaŋ te ɔ kaa bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ sɛɛ ɛ doo ɛ ne fɛɛfo? Ɛ kɛɛ, debaŋ keŋ te lii. Be ta lese maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon do balaŋ kumɛɛ nyiŋmaa man.

english_verse: They planned how they could sneak around and have Jesus arrested and put to death.
translation_verse: Keŋte be bakewɔ nyi baa yo kyaŋ Yesu na baa ko e.

english_verse: We could have sold this perfume for a lot of money and given it to the poor.”
translation_verse: “Nafɔ beŋ yɔɔ nuŋ kei na, baa nyiŋ kɔba burum na baa mo fa ayematena.”

english_verse: But when his disciples saw this, they became angry and complained, “Why such a waste?
translation_verse: Ɔ kaseela baŋ kena kpene keŋ alo ŋon be yɛɛ nɛ, yɛɛ wɔ baŋ te be yakowɔ nyiaa, “Weera dɔɔ te ɔ ne wɔlɛɛ nuŋ kei mena.”

english_verse: Then they sang a hymn and went out to the Mount of Olives. Peter's Promise (Mark
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, be kpee nɔɔneŋ lese Wurubuarɛ yele te ba leewɔ laŋ oliifi bula keŋ dɔɔ. Yesu keyako nyi Pita waa bɛɛ e Maake

english_verse: and from then on he started looking for a good chance to betray Jesus. Jesus Eats the Passover Meal with His Disciples (Mark
translation_verse: Lee debaŋ kenaŋ te Gyudase fiasɛɛ ɔ ne gyae gbɛɛ keŋ waa mo lese Yesu fa wɔ. Yesu na ɔ kaseela kedi nyeekelɛɛwee weenɛɛ ŋan

english_verse: When Jesus was eating with his twelve disciples that evening, he said, “One of you will surely hand me over to my enemies.”
translation_verse: Ba ke kyaa bee dii nɛ Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyi, ɛ man ŋolo waa lese maŋ fa.”

english_verse: On the first day of the Festival of Thin Bread, Jesus' disciples came to him and asked, “Where do you want us to prepare the Passover meal?”
translation_verse: Nyeekelɛɛwee kedi wee gyaŋgbate keŋ bee di bodobodo keŋ tee be doo ke man ya nɛ, Yesu kaseela baŋ kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ kaa bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Aleŋ te n ne gyae nyi de kaa desina see fa nyeekelɛɛwee weenɛɛ kedi keŋ?”

english_verse: During the meal Jesus took some bread in his hands. He blessed the bread and broke it. Then he gave it to his disciples and said, “Take this and eat it. This is my body.”
translation_verse: Be kee dii nɛ, Yesu moo bodobodo te ɔ fanewɔ fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ, te ɔ kara ke mo fa ɔ kaseela baŋ. Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ lɛɛ di, kei yɛna ma wosenaane keŋ.”

english_verse: This is my blood, and with it God makes his agreement with you. It will be poured out, so that many people will have their sins forgiven.
translation_verse: “Kei yɛna me fatabo keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo tɔ ŋon na ɔ balaŋ nɔɔkebakesee keŋ nɔɔ. Kei yɛna fatabo keŋ gyae ke kyɛɛkee leki, na Wurubuarɛ ka mo balaŋ burum nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ.

english_verse: From now on I am not going to drink any wine, until I drink new wine with you in my Father's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi mɔɔ gyae maa tekaa nyɔɔ soloŋ kei bela ya, kelii wee keŋ daa na ŋon dɛɛ nyɔɔ soloŋ wɔle me kya Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man.”

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples, “During this very night, all of you will reject me, as the Scriptures say, ‘I will strike down the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.’
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Gyɛŋ nelim kei nɛ ɛ popou ɛ ke yeu yela maŋ. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ yakowɔ ɔ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan man lee ma wose man nyiaa, ‘Maa gyɔ namensekɛɛlere ŋon lɔ, na ɔ namense baŋ pou ke yaasee.’

english_verse: Jesus replied, “I promise you before a rooster crows tonight, you will say three times that you don't know me.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako Pita nyiaa, “Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako neŋ nyi gyɛŋ nelim kei pɛte kegyim kelɔ woya nɛ, nyaŋ ke bɛɛ betabotooro nyi n ba gyeŋ maŋ korakora ya.”

english_verse: Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane. When they got there, he told them, “Sit here while I go over there and pray.”
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ gyuu yenaŋ daale keŋ bɛɛ baake botɔɔ nyi Gɛkyɛmane nɛ. Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ kyaa kɛbo, mee gyu siaman me kaa fane maa kɔŋ.”

english_verse: and he said to them, “I am so sad that I feel as if I am dying. Stay here and keep awake with me.”
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Dekɔkɔrɔkɔɔ te kyaŋ maŋ gbaa keŋ nyi ne gyae ka ko maŋ. Ɛ kyaa kɛbo na ɛ ka deke.”

english_verse: Peter spoke up, “Even if all the others reject you, I never will!”
translation_verse: Keŋte Pita yako e nyiaa, “Akpaa be popou de yeu tina neŋ yela koraŋ na, maŋ dɔɔ mɔɔ gyae maa tina neŋ yela korakora ya.”

english_verse: But Peter said, “Even if I have to die with you, I will never say I don't know you.” All the others said the same thing. Jesus Prays (Mark
translation_verse: Keŋte Pita tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Akpaa nyi ke yɛɛ yeŋ ke koraŋ na, mɔɔ gyae maa bɛɛ neŋ korakora ya.” Te ɔ kaseela akaŋ baŋ pou mɔ yakowɔ mena. Yesu kefane Gɛkyɛmane Maake

english_verse: Again Jesus went to pray and said, “My Father, if there is no other way, and I must suffer, I will still do what you want.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ lee ɔ kaseela baŋ gyaŋ bolate gyu ke fane nyiaa, “Taata, nyi be gyae ke de nyi mena kayeyɛɛ kei ka lee me dɔɔ ya nɛ, kenaŋ na yɛɛ nyaŋ gbagba n kegyaebii”

english_verse: Jesus walked on a little way. Then he knelt with his face to the ground and prayed, “My Father, if it is possible, don't make me suffer by drinking from this cup. But do what you want, and not what I want.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu tuuwɔ gyu siaman kyomii te ɔ tisiwɔ buŋ wuluŋu ɔ nyee te ɔ fanewɔ fa Wurubuarɛ nyiaa, “Taata, akpaa nyi gyae ke de na, yela mena kayeyɛɛ kei ke laŋ me dɔɔ. Mɔna na maŋ kegyaebii ya. Yɛɛ kpene keŋ nyaŋ gbagba ne gyae.”

english_verse: Get up! Let's go. The one who will betray me is already here.” Jesus Is Arrested (Mark
translation_verse: Ɛ koro dee gyu, ɛ kɛɛ walaŋ ŋon waa lese maŋ fa nɛ te tuŋ daa taŋ.” Ba kekyaŋ Yesu Maake

english_verse: Jesus took along Peter and the two brothers, James and John. He was very sad and troubled,
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ moo Pita na Sebedeo bia bala baŋ kpu na ɔ wose. Botɔɔ te dekɔkɔrɔkɔɔ kyaŋa e keŋ nyi ɔ bɛɛ gyeŋ mena keŋ waa yɛɛ ɔ wose ya.

english_verse: He came back and found his disciples sleeping. So he said to Peter, “Can't any of you stay awake with me for just one hour?
translation_verse: Yesu ka bese kɔŋ ɔ kaseela baŋ gyaŋ nɛ, ɔ kaa naawɔ nyi ba te doo dosoroŋ. Keŋte ɔ bɔɔse Pita nyiaa, “Mena dɔɔ ɛ te tale deke debaŋ dokoloŋ kooŋ keŋ nyi ɛ be doo yee?”

english_verse: He left them and prayed the same prayer once more.
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ besewɔ lee be gyaŋ gyu ɔ kaa fane botoorote. Te ɔ fanewɔ yako nombii dokoloŋ kenaŋ ke bela.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “My friend, do what you came for.” The men grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Me gyoo, yɛɛ kpene keŋ dɔɔ n be kɔŋ kɛbo nɛ.” Botɔɔ te balaŋ baŋ kaa tɔɔ Yesu do te be kyaŋa e.

english_verse: Jesus was still speaking, when Judas the betrayer came up. He was one of the twelve disciples, and a large mob armed with swords and clubs was with him. They had been sent by the chief priests and the nation's leaders.
translation_verse: Yesu ke gyakaa ɔ ne kolosi nɛ, ɔ kaseela kufu bala baŋ maŋ ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Gyudase nɛ kaa leewɔ botɔɔ. Ŋon na balaŋ dikpii keŋ bɔɔ lee saese kegyiise na donɔɔ kegyiise gyaŋ nɛ kɔŋala. Na be man baale dana yoosilase na akpokpose.

english_verse: Judas walked right up to Jesus and said, “Hello, teacher.” Then Judas kissed him.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Gyudase tɛɛwɔ teeŋ gyu Yesu gyaŋ te ɔ baake e nyi “De Gbeŋgyoo, te ɔ pukaa e doo e nɔɔ.”

english_verse: One of Jesus' followers pulled out a sword. He struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his ear.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu kaseela baŋ maŋ ŋolo lese ɔ yoosila, te ɔ moowɔ kara saese pou kegyia ŋon tobaale deŋele lese ke lɔ.

english_verse: But Jesus told him, “Put your sword away. Anyone who lives by fighting will die by fighting.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako ɔ kaseele ŋon nyiaa, “Mo n yoosila keŋ do ke bɔɔlee man. Nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne wɔŋ yoosila na, waa yeŋ bo yoosila nɔɔman.

english_verse: The high priest then tore his robe and said, “This man claims to be God! We don't need any more witnesses! You have heard what he said.
translation_verse: Saese kegyia ŋon ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, ɔ kyaŋa ɔ kegba tirii te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Ɔ te kolosi mmusuo nombia! Woŋ nombii yaa dɛɛ gyae dɛɛ tekaa nyii kpu bela? Nɛnɛɛ ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ nyii mmusuo nombia ŋan ɔ be kolosi nɛ.

english_verse: But Jesus did not answer. So the high priest said, “With the living God looking on, you must tell the truth. Are you the Messiah, the Son of God?”
translation_verse: Mɔna Yesu te gyuusu ɔ nɔɔ ya. Keŋte saese kegyia ŋon yako Yesu nyiaa, “Ma te kɛɛ Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa kekpaakekpaa nɛ nae fa neŋ, akpaa nyaŋ yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ, ŋon ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ bu dokoloŋ kooŋ ŋon e na, yako daa”

english_verse: When Peter had gone out to the gate, another servant girl saw him and said to some people there, “This man was with Jesus from Nazareth.”
translation_verse: Te ɔ leewɔ gyu dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ denanɔɔ man. Botɔɔ te alebubii ŋolo mɔ besewɔ naa e te ɔ yako ɔ tebia baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ nyiaa, “Balee kei mɔ kpuɛ na Yesu ŋon ɔ ba lee Nasarɛte donɔɔ man nɛ.”

english_verse: After Jesus had been arrested, he was led off to the house of Caiaphas the high priest. The nation's leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses were meeting there.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ kyaŋ Yesu nɛ moo e gyu saese pou kegyia Kaifase dekpaŋalaŋ man, botɔɔ keŋ mmaraa wolala na donɔɔ kegyiise pou be gyaŋee nɛ.

english_verse: But Peter followed along at a distance and came to the courtyard of the high priest's palace. He went in and sat down with the guards to see what was going to happen.
translation_verse: Pita sila Yesu wɔle wɔle menɛɛ kelii saese kegyia ŋon dekpaŋalaŋ man. Ɔ gyoowɔ ke man te ɔ gyuuwɔ ke kyaa tewulɛɛ baŋ bee gyee dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ nɛ gyaŋ, na waa naa kpene gyae ke kɔŋ Yesu dɔɔ.

english_verse: The chief priests and the whole council wanted to put Jesus to death. So they tried to find some people who would tell lies about him in court.
translation_verse: Saese kegyiise na donɔɔ kegyiise baŋ pou bɔɔ gyaŋee botɔɔ nɛ, ne gyae nombii keŋ baa mo gyakaa Yesu dɔɔ na baa nyiŋ gbɛɛ ko e.

english_verse: But they could not find any, even though many did come and tell lies. At last, two men came forward
translation_verse: Balaŋ burum kɔŋawɔ kaa kɛrɛɛ Yesu nɔɔse, mɔna be te nyiŋ nombiikum keŋ baa mo gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ ya. Wɔle wɔle na te baala bala baale kɔŋawɔ kaa yako nyi,

english_verse: and said, “This man claimed he could tear down God's temple and build it again in three days.”
translation_verse: “De nyiiwɔ balee kei yakowɔ nyi ‘Waa tale tekerii Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa kei lɔ, na waa mo weeya atooro ma ke bela.’ ”

english_verse: “That is what you say!” Jesus answered. “But I tell all of you, ‘Soon you will see the Son of Man sitting at the right side of God All-Powerful and coming on the clouds of heaven.’ ”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Keŋ yaa n yakowɔ nɛ. Mɔna mɛɛ yako ɛ popou ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, wee daale nɛ ɛ kena maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon kyaa Wurubuarɛ doŋ pou gbeŋgyoo ŋon dunoluŋ dɔɔ, na ɛ ka bese naa maŋ na me ta lee ŋɔma man adido mɛɛ kɔŋ.”

english_verse: Then they spit in his face and hit him with their fists. Others slapped him
translation_verse: Keŋte be tɔɔ nɔɔloŋse woli ɔ siagbɛɛ te be takee e akɔtase. Baale mɔ ŋmeree e ɔ lakaase man,

english_verse: and said, “You think you are the Messiah! So tell us who hit you!” Peter Says He Doesn't Know Jesus (Mark
translation_verse: te be yako e nyiaa “Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Kristo, wola daa ŋon ɔ be gyɔ neŋ.” Pita kebɛɛ Yesu Maake

english_verse: Again Peter denied it, and this time he swore, “I don't even know that man!”
translation_verse: Keŋte Pita besewɔ lɛɛ aŋmaareŋ te ɔ kaŋa ntam nyiaa, “Mɔɔ gyeŋ balee kei korakora ya.”

english_verse: While Peter was sitting out in the courtyard, a servant girl came up to him and said, “You were with Jesus from Galilee.”
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ na Pita kyaa dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man. Keŋte saese kegyia ŋon alebu ŋolo kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ kaa yako e nyiaa, “Nyaŋ mɔ n kpuɛ na Yesu ŋon ɔ ba lee Galelia tɛɛle man nɛ.”

english_verse: But in front of everyone Peter said, “That isn't so! I don't know what you are talking about!”
translation_verse: Keŋte Pita lɛɛ aŋmaareŋ balaŋ baŋ pou siaman botɔɔ nyiaa, “Mɔɔ gyeŋ nombia ŋan n ne yako nɛ man korakora ya.”

english_verse: A little while later some people standing there walked over to Peter and said, “We know you are one of them. We can tell it because you talk like someone from Galilee.”
translation_verse: Te kyare ya nɛ, balaŋ baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ kɔŋawɔ Pita gyaŋ kaa yako e nyiaa, “Kei dɔɔ ke yɛɛ ampaŋ ke kaeŋ nyi nyaŋ mɔ n kpuɛ na balee kei. N kekolosi koraŋ nawolo nyi n yɛɛ be man ŋolo e.”

english_verse: Peter began to curse and swear, “I don't know that man!” Right then a rooster crowed,
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Pita fiasɛɛ ɔ ne falaa ɔ wose te ɔ kaŋa ntam nyiaa, “Mɔɔ gyeŋ balee kei korakora ya.” Debokenaŋ man te kegyim lɔɔ woya.

english_verse: and Peter remembered that Jesus had said, “Before a rooster crows, you will say three times you don't know me.” Then Peter went out and cried bitterly.
translation_verse: Keŋte Pita tɔɔse nombia ŋan Yesu be yako e nyiaa, “Pɛte kegyim ke lɔ woya na, nyaŋ ke bɛɛ betabotooro nyi n ba gyeŋ maŋ ya” nɛ. Te Pita leewɔ debɔɔman ke kyaŋ ɔ nyee wii.

english_verse: Pilate knew the leaders had brought Jesus to him because they were jealous.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi na Pilato gyeŋ nyi, keŋ balaŋ baŋ ne kɔla Yesu dɔɔ te ba lese e fa.

english_verse: On the way they met a man named Simon who was from Cyrene, and they forced him to carry Jesus' cross.
translation_verse: Be ka lee debɔɔ man bee gyu nɛ, te be gyaŋee na balee ŋolo ŋon ɔ ba lee Kirene te bɛɛ baake e nyi Simɔn nɛ. Keŋte ba ŋere e nyi waa lɛɛ Yesu ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ seele.

english_verse: Then they had a meeting and decided to buy a field that belonged to someone who made clay pots. They wanted to use it as a graveyard for foreigners.
translation_verse: Keŋte kegyiise baŋ moo ba nyeeya baŋ na dɔŋa te be sɛɛwɔ nyi, baa mo kɔba kenaŋ ke lɛɛ tɛɛle saabuse maare ŋolo gyaŋ, na baa kyaa wuu ŋɔɔla botɔɔ.

english_verse: This is why people still call that place “Field of Blood.”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ kaa lii naŋ gyɛŋ nɛ, bɛɛ baake tɛɛle kenaŋ bo nyi fatabo tɛɛle.

english_verse: So the words of the prophet Jeremiah came true, “They took the thirty silver coins, the price of a person among the people of Israel.
translation_verse: Kɛŋa pou yela Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan ɔ be tɛɛ ɔ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Gyeremia dɔɔ yako see nɛ kɔŋawɔ mena. Ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Ba moo kɔba wolom bia sao na kufu

english_verse: They paid it for a potter's field, as the Lord had commanded me.” Pilate Questions Jesus (Mark
translation_verse: ke lɛɛ tɛɛle saabuse maare gyaŋ ŋgba mena keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ ba wola maŋ nɛ.” Be kamo Yesu gyu Pilato gyaŋ Maake

english_verse: Jesus was brought before Pilate the governor, who asked him, “Are you the king of the Jews?” “Those are your words!” Jesus answered.
translation_verse: Keŋte ba moo Yesu kaa seŋ Pilato ŋon ɔ seŋɛɛ Romantena gyoo dinaa nawɔɔ man nɛ siaman. Te Pilato bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Nyaŋ yɛna Gyudatena gyoo ŋon?” Te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Keŋ yaa n yakowɔ nɛ.”

english_verse: And when the chief priests and leaders brought their charges against him, he did not say a thing.
translation_verse: Mɔna debaŋ keŋ saese kegyiise na donɔɔ kegyiise ba mo nombia gyakaa Yesu dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ te gyuusu ɔ nɔɔ ya.

english_verse: But Jesus did not say anything, and the governor was greatly amazed. The Death Sentence (Mark
translation_verse: Mɔna Yesu bɛe ɔ te gyuusu ɔ nɔɔ yako nombii dokoloŋ kooŋ gba ya. Nombii kei doo Pilato nɔɔ nideli. Be kalese Yesu fa yeŋ Maake

english_verse: People who passed by said terrible things about Jesus. They shook their heads and
translation_verse: Na balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ lam botɔɔ nɛ na tee e na bee kpiisi ba nyeeya,

english_verse: But the chief priests and the leaders convinced the crowds to ask for Barabbas to be set free and for Jesus to be killed.
translation_verse: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na kegyiise akaŋ baŋ feefuwɔ do balaŋ dikpii keŋ nyi, baa sola Pilato na waa tina Barabase yela na baa ko Yesu.

english_verse: Pilate saw that there was nothing he could do and that the people were starting to riot. So he took some water and washed his hands in front of them and said, “I won't have anything to do with killing this man. You are the ones doing it!”
translation_verse: Pilato kena nyi balaŋ baŋ nɔɔwoya na kela e te ɔ be gyae waa tale yɛɛ kolo lee Yesu wose man ya nɛ, ɔ kyee loŋ faafo ɔ nyiŋmaase balaŋ dikpii keŋ siaman te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Maŋ nyiŋmaa be doo balee kei yeŋ man korakora ya. Ke yɛɛ ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ nombia ŋa.”

english_verse: Everyone answered, “We and our own families will take the blame for his death!”
translation_verse: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ pou sɛɛwɔ yako nyiaa, “Deŋele kewɔŋ keŋ gyae ke kɔŋ lee ɔ kako wose man nɛ, dɛɛkɔŋ daa na de bia dɔɔ.”

english_verse: They made a crown out of thorn branches and placed it on his head, and they put a stick in his right hand. The soldiers knelt down and pretended to worship him. They made fun of him and shouted, “Hey, you king of the Jews!”
translation_verse: Keŋte ba moo sɔɔse loo gyooro kotɔtɔɔ wuu e, te ba moo gbutu do e ɔ dunoluŋ nyiŋmaa man na bɛɛ buŋ ɔ siaman kulu e nyiaa, “Gyudatena gyoo nyaŋ na ke yɛɛ. N nyee dɛɛ wolose.”

english_verse: He trusted God, so let God save him, if he wants to. He even said he was God's Son.”
translation_verse: Ɔ na mo ɔ wose lɔ Wurubuarɛ dɔɔ te ɔ ne yako nyi, ‘Ŋon yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu. Akpaa Wurubuarɛ ne gyae e na, ɛ yela dɛɛ kɛɛ nyi waa lɛɛ e.’ ”

english_verse: When the soldiers had finished making fun of Jesus, they took off the robe. They put his own clothes back on him and led him off to be nailed to a cross.
translation_verse: Be ke kulu e taŋ nɛ, te ba kpee kegba yayam keŋ bɔɔ mo do nɛ te ba moo ŋon gbagba wui abɔɔ do e. Te ba moo lee be kaa gyɔ e sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ. Ba kegyɔ Yesu ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ Maake

english_verse: There they gave Jesus some wine mixed with a drug to ease the pain. But when Jesus tasted what it was, he refused to drink it.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ba moo tee ŋaŋte daale mo kyakale na soloŋ fa Yesu nyi waa nyɔɔ. Mɔna ɔ ke daa ke kɛɛ nɛ te ɔ bɛɛwɔ nyi ɔ be nyɔɔ ya.

english_verse: The soldiers nailed Jesus to a cross and gambled to see who would get his clothes.
translation_verse: Ba ke gyɔ e sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ nɛ, te be kpɛlɛɛ ɔ kegbase buŋii te be lɔɔ boe ŋe dɔɔ na baa naa keŋ walaŋ kamasɛ waa nyiŋ.

english_verse: Then they sat down to guard him.
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, te be kyaawɔ botɔɔ bɛɛ deke e.

english_verse: Above his head they put a sign that told why he was nailed there. It read, “This is Jesus, the King of the Jews.”
translation_verse: Keŋte be ŋmarase nombii keŋ dɔɔ bɔɔ gyɔ e ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ nɛ mataa ɔ nyee gbɛɛ nyiaa, “kei yɛna yesu, gyudatena gyoo ŋon.”

english_verse: shouted, “So you're the one who claimed you could tear down the temple and build it again in three days! If you are God's Son, save yourself and come down from the cross!”
translation_verse: na bɛɛ yako e nyiaa, “Nyaŋ ŋon n kpa n ke tekerii Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ na n ka mo weeya atooro ma ke nɛ, lɛɛ n wose na dɛɛ kɛɛ. Akpaa nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon na, lɛɛ n wose na n ke tisi lee ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ dɔɔ.”

english_verse: Others said, “Wait! Let's see if Elijah will come and save him.”
translation_verse: Keŋte balaŋ akaŋ baŋ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ yela dɛɛ kɛɛ nyi Elaegya waa kɔŋ kaa lɛɛ e?”

english_verse: At noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until three o'clock.
translation_verse: Lee weese kaseŋ dɛɛ kelii ɔ ke gɛɛse nɛ, ditiŋtɛɛ kaa lɛɛ tɛɛle keŋ dɔɔ pou.

english_verse: Then about that time Jesus shouted, “Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani?” which means, “My God, my God, why have you deserted me?”
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ nɛ, te Yesu fae yeesa keŋkeŋ nyiaa, “Eli, Eli lama sabatani?” Keŋ nawolo nyi, “Me Wurubuarɛ me Wurubuarɛ, weera te n lese n sia yela maŋ?”

english_verse: One of them at once ran and grabbed a sponge. He soaked it in wine, then put it on a stick and held it up to Jesus.
translation_verse: Debokenaŋ man te be man ŋolo yeuwɔ ka mo kolo ŋgba kokyaa keŋ gyae ke tale lɛɛ loŋ nɛ do soloŋ denyaŋesa daale man, te ɔ moo ke tooso na daŋ te ɔ gyinaa ke adido fa Yesu nyi waa nyɔɔ.

english_verse: At once the curtain in the temple was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth shook, and rocks split apart.
translation_verse: Kekɔŋ mena nɛ, kanyaŋ keŋ bɔɔ mo kpase Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man nɛ bɛɛsewɔ kyɛɛkyɛɛ ala lee adido kaa lii ka mokɔɔ man. Tɛɛle wosiwɔ, te boya dinaana mɔ yɛlɛɛwɔ.

english_verse: They left their graves, and after Jesus had risen to life, they went into the holy city, where they were seen by many people.
translation_verse: Yesu kefoŋ lee yeŋ man nɛ, Wurubuarɛ balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ foŋii lee ba yebɔɔse man nɛ, gyuu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man ka lese ba wose wola balaŋ burum.

english_verse: The officer and the soldiers guarding Jesus felt the earthquake and saw everything else that happened. They were frightened and said, “This man really was God's Son!”
translation_verse: Romantena yoonɔɔtena siamante ŋon na baŋ ba seŋɛɛ bɛɛ gyee Yesu nɛ kena tɛɛle kewosi na gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan be yɛɛ pou nɛ, ba wose yeŋa wɔ te be yakowɔ nyiaa, “Balee kei yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon ampaŋ.”

english_verse: Many women had come with Jesus from Galilee to be of help to him, and they were there, looking on at a distance.
translation_verse: Na ala burum baale mɔ seŋɛɛ yenaŋ botɔɔ lemlem bɛɛ kɛɛ. Be wulaa be silana Yesu leenaŋ Galelia donɔɔ man bɛɛ kɛɛ ɔ dɔɔ.

english_verse: went and asked for Jesus' body. Pilate gave orders for it to be given to Joseph,
translation_verse: Ɔ gyuu Pilato gyaŋ ka sola e nyi waa fa e gbɛɛ na waa mo Yesu woo ŋon ke wuu. Keŋte Pilato sɛɛwɔ nyi baa moo Yesu woo ŋon fa e.

english_verse: who took the body and wrapped it in a clean linen cloth.
translation_verse: Keŋte Gyosɛfo moo woo ŋon, te ɔ moo akaralɛɛ wɔle wɔle daale milii e.

english_verse: Then Joseph put the body in his own tomb that had been cut into solid rock and had never been used. He rolled a big stone against the entrance to the tomb and went away.
translation_verse: Te ɔ moo e ke wuu boe bɔɔ wɔle keŋ ŋon Gyosɛfo gbagba be kare see ɔ wose nɛ man. Keŋte ɔ biliŋi boe dinaa daale tɔ yebɔɔ keŋ nɔɔ te ɔ laŋawɔ.

english_verse: All this time Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were sitting across from the tomb.
translation_verse: Na Mɛɛre ŋon ɔ ba lee Magedala nɛ, na Mɛɛre balate ŋon baŋɛɛ botɔɔ be sia tee na yebɔɔ keŋ.

english_verse: On the next day, which was a Sabbath, the chief priests and the Pharisees went together to Pilate.
translation_verse: Tɛɛ kena keŋ ke yɛɛ Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee ke nɛ, Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na Farasiitena baŋ gyuuwɔ ke gyaŋee Pilato dekpaŋalaŋ man.

english_verse: They said, “Sir, we remember what this liar said while he was still alive. He claimed in three days he would come back from death.
translation_verse: Keŋte be yako Pilato nyiaa, “Kegyia dɛɛ tɔɔse nyi Yesu dekarate ŋon ke kyaa keŋ nɛ ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, weeya atoorote wɔle na waa foŋ lee yeŋ man.

english_verse: So please order the tomb to be carefully guarded for three days. If you don't, his disciples may come and steal his body. They will tell the people he has been raised to life, and this last lie will be worse than the first one.”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ yela be kaa deke yebɔɔ keŋ na baa gyee ke nideli kelii weeya atoorote keŋ. Na mena ya na ɔ kaseela baŋ baa yo gyu ke ŋmɛɛle e lese na baa yako nyi, ɔ foŋae lee yeŋ man. Na mena ya na, dekara alate kei gyae ka kela gyaŋgba te keŋ.”

english_verse: Pilate said to them, “All right, take some of your soldiers and guard the tomb as well as you know how.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Pilato yako wɔ nyiaa, ɛ lese ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ balaŋ na baa gyu ka deke yebɔɔ keŋ nideli fa ŋon ŋgba mena keŋ baa tale nɛ.

english_verse: Jesus' eleven disciples went to a mountain in Galilee, where Jesus had told them to meet him.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Yesu kaseela kufu daale akaŋ baŋ gyuuwɔ Galelia tɛɛle man, te be gyuu bula keŋ dɔɔ Yesu ba wola wɔ nyi baa gyu nɛ.

english_verse: So the chief priests met with the leaders and decided to bribe the soldiers with a lot of money.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na donɔɔ kegyiise baŋ gyaŋeewɔ, te be bakewɔ te be faa yoonɔɔtena baŋ bɛɛ deke yebɔɔ keŋ nɛ kɔba burum daale,

english_verse: and teach them to do everything I have told you. I will be with you always, even until the end of the world.
translation_verse: Na ɛ ka wola wɔ na baa di kpene kamasɛ keŋ mɔɔ faree ŋon nɛ dɔɔ. Te mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, me kpuɛ na ŋon debaŋ kamasɛ kelii tɛɛle kei ɔto.”

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “Don't be afraid! Tell my followers to go to Galilee. They will see me there.” Report of the Guard
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ na yee ya, ɛ gyu ke yako me tebia baŋ nyiaa, baa gyu Galelia tɛɛle man. Botɔɔ te baa naa maŋ.”

english_verse: Suddenly a strong earthquake struck, and the Lord's angel came down from heaven. He rolled away the stone and sat on it.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ nɛ tɛɛle wosiwɔ nawolo nyi de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ kpilale te tisi lee adido kɔŋ kaa biliŋi boe keŋ see yenaŋ, te ɔ tisiwɔ kyaa ke dɔɔ.

english_verse: The angel looked as bright as lightning, and his clothes were white as snow.
translation_verse: Na Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon ne ŋalakee ŋgba ɔga keŋmaale nɛ, te ɔ kegba wolosɛɛ futututu ŋgba akaralɛɛ nɛ.

english_verse: The guards shook from fear and fell down, as though they were dead.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ deke yebɔɔ keŋ kena Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon nɛ, gyakoloŋ gyoo wɔ nideli te be dɔɔwɔ ba seŋɛɛ botɔɔ ŋgba woya nɛ.

english_verse: The angel said to the women, “Don't be afraid! I know you are looking for Jesus, who was nailed to a cross.
translation_verse: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon yako ala baŋ nyiaa, “Ɛ na yee ya, ma gyeŋ nyi Yesu ŋon bɔɔ gyɔɔ sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ nɛ yaa ɛ ne kɛo.

english_verse: Now hurry! Tell his disciples he has been raised to life and is on his way to Galilee. Go there, and you will see him. This is what I came to tell you.”
translation_verse: na ɛ ke ta bileŋbileŋ ke yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa ‘Ɔ te fom lee woya man te ɔ te kyaŋ ŋon gbɛɛ ɔ ne gyu Galelia tɛɛle man. Botɔɔ te ɛ kena e. Kei yɛna nombii keŋ me dana maa yako ŋon.’ ”

english_verse: Suddenly Jesus met them and greeted them. They went near him, held on to his feet, and worshiped him.
translation_verse: Karatetee dokoloŋ te Yesu gyaŋee na ala baŋ te ɔ doo wɔ nɔɔ nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ waa fa ŋon wosefɛɛreŋ.” Keŋte ala baŋ tuuwɔ benaa e te be tisiwɔ buŋ kyaŋ ɔ nawɔɔ som e.

english_verse: While the women were on their way, some soldiers who had been guarding the tomb went into the city. They told the chief priests everything that had happened.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ ala baŋ ne gyuu nɛ, yoonɔɔtena baŋ bee gyee yebɔɔ keŋ nɛ man baale gyuuwɔ donɔɔ man ke yako Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise kpene kamasɛ keŋ be kɔŋ nɛ.

english_verse: They said to the soldiers, “Tell everyone that Jesus' disciples came during the night and stole his body while you were asleep.
translation_verse: na baa yako nyi nelim ba ke doo na, te Yesu kaseela baŋ kɔŋawɔ kaa ŋmɛɛle ɔ woo ŋon.

english_verse: The soldiers took the money and did what they were told. The people of Judea still tell each other this story. What Jesus' Followers Must Do (Mark
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ yoonɔɔtena baŋ lɛɛ kɔba keŋ te be gyuuwɔ ke yɛɛ ŋgba mena keŋ kegyiise baŋ be yako wɔ nɛ. Mena nombia kɛŋa te yaasee Gyudatena tɛɛle man kaa lii na gyɛŋ. Tom dinaa keŋ Yesu ba ma do ɔ kaseela nyiŋmaa man Maake

english_verse: Jesus came to them and said: I have been given all authority in heaven and on earth!
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tuuwɔ benaa wɔ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ te fa maŋ doŋ adido na tɛɛle kei pou dɔɔ.

english_verse: Go to the people of all nations and make them my disciples. Baptize them in the name of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit,
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛ gyu tɛɛle kei pou dɔɔ na ɛ kaa wola balaŋ na baa bese me kaseela. Ɛ sɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ lee Wurubuarɛ kya ŋon na ɔ Bu ŋon na ɔ Feliŋ ŋon yele man.

english_verse: They saw him and worshiped him, but some of them doubted.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu ka lese ɔ wose wola wɔ te be buŋawɔ som e, mɔna be man baale te lɛɛ di ya.

english_verse: Right then the two brothers dropped their nets and went with him.
translation_verse: Debokenaŋ man te be tinaa ba asawuse ŋan yela, te be kaa sila Yesu.

english_verse: Jesus walked on and soon saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat, mending their nets.
translation_verse: Ɔ ke gyu ɔ siaman kyomii nɛ, ɔ naa Sebedeo bia Gyemisi na ɔ naabu Gyɔn na be doo ba degbele man bee desina ba asawuse.

english_verse: News about Jesus quickly spread all over Galilee. Jesus Heals Many People (Matthew
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Yesu wose man nombia yaaseewɔ gyu Galelia tɛɛle man na ka yenaase pou. Yesu kekyɔ balaŋ burum kaweese Mateo

english_verse: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
translation_verse: Na kurumɔɔ tɔne te ba moowɔ ŋara Gyɔn kegba, te wonembu tɔne te ɔ moowɔ yɛɛ abɔsɔɔ kpaŋ ɔ teŋa man. Akpataana na teebɔɔ yɛna ɔ weenɛɛ ŋan ɔ ne dii.

english_verse: John also told the people, “Someone more powerful is going to come. And I am not good enough even to stoop down and untie his sandals.
translation_verse: Keŋte Gyɔn yako balaŋ baŋ nyiaa, “Walaŋ ŋolo dɔŋ me wɔle ɔ ne kɔŋ keŋ ɔ kela maŋ. Maŋ tekaboena nyi maa buŋ booli ɔtenate nkyokota ŋmeeseŋ gba ya.

english_verse: He stayed there for
translation_verse: Yesu kyaawɔ depampaa kenaŋ man weeya sɔŋola (

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples went to the town of Capernaum. Then on the next Sabbath he went into the synagogue and started teaching.
translation_verse: Keŋte be gyuuwɔ Kapanum donɔɔ man. Te Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee kelii nɛ, Yesu gyuuwɔ bɔ ɔsom deni man ɔ kaa wola wɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia.

english_verse: About that time Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
translation_verse: Gyɔn kɛɛ sɔ balaŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ nɛ, te Yesu leewɔ Nasarɛte donɔɔ keŋ ke kyaa Galelia tɛɛle man nɛ kɔŋ, te Gyɔn sɔɔ e Wurubuarɛ loŋ Gyɔɔdan boo man.

english_verse: As soon as Jesus came out of the water, he saw the sky open and the Holy Spirit coming down to him like a dove.
translation_verse: Yesu ka mo waa lee loŋ keŋ man nɛ, karatetee dokoloŋ te ɔ naawɔ na adido ta toro, te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon moo duwuluŋ dinɔɔ tisi lee adido kaa gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ.

english_verse: A man with leprosy came to Jesus and knelt down. He begged, “You have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
translation_verse: Te akuŋtuŋte ŋolo kaa buŋawɔ ɔ doona dɔɔ Yesu nawɔɔtɛɛ waase e nyiaa, “Me Gbeŋgyoo, akpaa n ne gyae na n ke tale kyɔ maŋ me kawee kei.”

english_verse: He said, “The time has come! God's kingdom will soon be here. Turn back to God and believe the good news!” Jesus Chooses Four Fishermen (Matthew
translation_verse: “Debaŋ keŋ te lii, te Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ mɔ ta benaa. Ɛ kyɛɛkee lee ɛ gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ, na ɛ ke lɛɛ nombia kpaakpaa ŋan di!”

english_verse: At once Jesus asked them to come with him. They left their father in the boat with the hired workers and went with him. A Man with an Evil Spirit (Luke
translation_verse: Debokenaŋ man te Yesu baake wɔ, te be tinaa be kya Sebedeo na be paasetena baŋ pou yela degbele keŋ man te be kaa sila Yesu. Yesu kegegi feliŋkum lee balee ŋolo man Luka

english_verse: Jesus went to her. He took hold of her hand and helped her up. The fever left her, and she served them a meal.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu gyuuwɔ alo ŋon gyaŋ, te ɔ kyaŋa e ɔ nyiŋmaa man gate e kyaase. Debokenaŋ man te alo ŋon kawee keŋ taŋawɔ, te ɔ kɛɛ be dɔɔ.

english_verse: And when they found him, they said, “Everyone is looking for you!”
translation_verse: Ba kena e nɛ te be yako e nyiaa, “Balaŋ baŋ pou ne kɛo neŋ.”

english_verse: Suddenly a man with an evil spirit in him entered the synagogue and yelled,
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ na balee ŋolo kyaa bɔ ɔsom deni man botɔɔ keŋ feliŋkum doo ɔ man. Karatetee dokoloŋ te ɔ fae yeesa keŋkeŋ nyiaa,

english_verse: “Jesus from Nazareth, what do you want with us? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are! You are God's Holy One.”
translation_verse: “Nyaŋ Yesu Nasarɛte baale, woŋ yaa n ne gyae de gyaŋ? N kɔŋae n kaa wɔlɛɛ daa? Ma gyeŋ neŋ nyi nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ walaŋ ŋon ayimɛɛ be doo n man ya nɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus told the evil spirit, “Be quiet and come out of the man!”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu faree feliŋkum keŋ nyiaa, “Wu n nɔɔ, na n ka lee ɔ man.”

english_verse: The spirit shook him. Then it gave a loud shout and left.
translation_verse: Keŋte feliŋkum keŋ wosi balee ŋon nideli, te ke fae yeesa keŋkeŋ te ka leewɔ ɔ man laŋ.

english_verse: Everyone was completely surprised and kept saying to each other, “What is this? It must be some new kind of powerful teaching! Even the evil spirits obey him.”
translation_verse: Nombii kei doo balaŋ baŋ pou nɔɔ te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, “Woŋ nombii yaa? Kawola wɔle? Ɔ dana doŋ feliŋkumɛɛ dɔɔ, keŋ nyi ɔ ne kolosi fa ŋa na ŋee nyii ɔ dei.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “We must go to the nearby towns, so that I can tell the good news to those people. This is why I have come.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ yela dee gyu donɔɔse ŋan ŋe kyaa siaman nɛ man, na me kaa kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan botɔɔ mɔ. Nawolo nyi keŋ dɔɔ te me kɔŋawɔ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus felt sorry for the man. So he put his hand on him and said, “I want to! Now you are well.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ nɛ, ɔ nombia yɛɛ Yesu waraŋase te ɔ moo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ yako e nyiaa, “Mɛɛ gyae, n kawee dɛɛ taŋ.”

english_verse: As soon as Jesus left the synagogue with James and John, they went home with Simon and Andrew.
translation_verse: Be ka lee bɔ ɔsom deni keŋ man pɛ, te baŋ na Gyemisi na Gyɔn gyuuwɔ Simɔn na Anderiase dɛɛ.

english_verse: When they got there, Jesus was told that Simon's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
translation_verse: Be ke gyu na Simɔn wa awu doo ɔ na wee kpaŋalaŋ, keŋte be yako Yesu.

english_verse: That evening after sunset, all who were sick or had demons in them were brought to Jesus.
translation_verse: Mena wee kenaŋ balinɔɔ weese kagyoo nɛ, balaŋ moo be kaweesetena na baŋ pou feliŋkumɛɛ doo be man nɛ kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ.

english_verse: In fact, the whole town gathered around the door of the house.
translation_verse: Keŋte donɔɔ keŋ man balaŋ pou kɔŋawɔ kaa wuu dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ denanɔɔ man.

english_verse: Jesus healed all kinds of terrible diseases and forced out a lot of demons. But the demons knew who he was, and he did not let them speak.

english_verse: Very early the next morning before daylight, Jesus got up and went to a place where he could be alone and pray.
translation_verse: Ke tɛɛ kena nebo nebo fuu nɛ, Yesu karatɛɛwɔ lee dekpaŋalaŋ man gyu yenaŋ daale keŋ botɔɔ yɛɛ yididi nɛ, te ɔ ke fanewɔ fa Wurubuarɛ.

english_verse: Simon and the others started looking for him.
translation_verse: Keŋte Simɔn na ɔ tebia baŋ gyuuwɔ bɛɛ kɛo e.

translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɔ tɛɛwɔ kila Galelia tɛɛle pou dɔɔ, te ɔ kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan fa wɔ bɔ ɔsom denɛɛ man pou, te ɔ gegi feliŋkumɛɛ lee balaŋ man. Yesu kekyɔ akuŋtuŋte ŋolo kawee Mateo

english_verse: At once the man's leprosy disappeared, and he was well.
translation_verse: Debokenaŋ man te ɔ kawee keŋ taŋawɔ, te ɔ nyiŋa ɔ wose.
