translation_verse: Pɛ Yesu ke lɔ balee ŋon gbɛɛ nɛ, ɔ faree e nideli nyiaa,

english_verse: He said, “Don't tell anyone about this. Just go and show the priest that you are well. Then take a gift to the temple as Moses commanded, and everyone will know that you have been healed.”
translation_verse: “Kɛɛ nyi walaŋ na walaŋ na nyii kpene keŋ be kɔŋ nɛ ya. Mɔna mo n wose gyu kawola Wurubuarɛ sae ŋon, na n ketɔ kedeesa abɔɔ ŋan de naana Mosesi ba wolo nyi yaa tɔ nɛ. Kei gyae ke yela walaŋ kamasɛ kena nyi n kawee keŋ te taŋ.”

english_verse: The man talked about it so much and told so many people, that Jesus could no longer go openly into a town. He had to stay away from the towns, but people still came to him from everywhere.
translation_verse: Mɔna balee ŋon ka lee debɔɔ man pɛ te ɔ gyoo nombia ŋan dekpeŋkpeŋ kegyɔ, te walaŋ kamasɛ nyiiwɔ. Lee mena dɔɔ Yesu te tale gyoo donɔɔ kamasɛ man na balaŋ kena e ya. Ɔ ne gyu bo ke kyaa donɔɔ keŋ nɔɔman, botɔɔ keŋ balaŋ be gyae baa naa e ya nɛ. Mɔ keŋ koraŋ na, balaŋ na lee yenaŋ kamasɛ kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ.

english_verse: It was during the time of Abiathar the high priest. David went into the house of God and ate the sacred loaves of bread that only priests are allowed to eat. He also gave some to his followers.”
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ Abiata yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ sae kegyia e nɛ, de naana Defidi gyoowɔ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man, te ɔ dii bodobodo keŋ bɔɔ mo deesi fa Wurubuarɛ keŋ ŋolo be dana gbɛɛ waa di ke kpu na Wurubuarɛ saese ya nɛ. Keŋte ɔ moo ŋaale mɔ fa ɔ tebia baŋ be kpuɛ na e nɛ, te baŋ mɔ diiwɔ.”

english_verse: Then so many of them came to the house that there wasn't even standing room left in front of the door. Jesus was still teaching
translation_verse: Keŋte balaŋ burum kaa wuuwɔ botɔɔ, keŋ nyi nae daseŋae be doo ya. Mena dɔɔ gbɛɛ be doo keŋ ŋolo waa mo gyoo dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man gba ya. Te Yesu kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia fa wɔ.

english_verse: when four people came up, carrying a man on a mat because he could not walk.
translation_verse: Keŋte balaŋ banaara baale seele ɔtakase ŋolo bɛɛ kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ.

english_verse: But because of the crowd, they could not get him to Jesus. So they made a hole in the roof above him and let the man down in front of everyone.
translation_verse: Keŋ balaŋ boe dɔɔ be te nyiŋ gbɛɛ mo e ta denanɔɔ man ya. Mena dɔɔ ba gyeŋa deni keŋ man Yesu kyaa nɛ nyee dɔɔ te be wolee yenaŋ daale, te be tisinaa kaweete ŋon na ɔ dofoŋoŋ keŋ pou dɛɛdɛɛ see Yesu siaman.

english_verse: “Why would he say such a thing? He must think he is God! Only God can forgive sins.”
translation_verse: “Weera dɔɔ te balee kei ne kolosi mena? Ɔ te kolosi mmusuo nombia. Lee Wurubuarɛ wɔle na, amɔte kyaa la keŋ waa tale mo dukum kyɛɛ?”

english_verse: I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” So Jesus said to the man,
translation_verse: Mɔna mɛɛ gyae ɛ ka gyeŋ nyi, Maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu dana doŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nyi maa mo dukum kyɛɛ.” Mena dɔɔ ɔ yako kaweete ŋon nyiaa,

english_verse: “Get up! Pick up your mat and go on home.”
translation_verse: “Koro seŋ. Gate n dofoŋoŋ, na n ke ta gyu n dɛɛ.”

english_verse: Once again, Jesus went to the shore of Lake Galilee. A large crowd gathered around him, and he taught them.
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle te Yesu besewɔ gyu Galelia boo nɔɔman, te balaŋ burum kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ te ɔ wola wɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia.

english_verse: Jesus finished by saying, “People were not made for the good of the Sabbath. The Sabbath was made for the good of people.
translation_verse: Te Yesu torowɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ kefɛɛfowee bo fa walaŋ. Ɔ te yɛɛ walaŋ fa kefɛɛfowee ya.

english_verse: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Levi's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners had become followers of Jesus, and they were also guests at the dinner.
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, Lewi baake Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ gyu ɔ dɛɛ be kaa di. Lempoolɛɛra na nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra burum sila Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ gyu botɔɔ, te baŋ na wɔ pou ke diiwɔ bom.

english_verse: Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees, and they saw Jesus eating with sinners and tax collectors. So they asked his disciples, “Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
translation_verse: Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ bɔɔ lee Farasiitena dikpii keŋ man kena ŋgba Yesu ne dii bom na lempoolɛɛra na nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra nɛ be bɔɔse ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Weera dɔɔ te Yesu ne dii bom na lempoolɛɛra na nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra?”

english_verse: The followers of John the Baptist and the Pharisees often went without eating. Some people came and asked Jesus, “Why do the followers of John and those of the Pharisees often go without eating, while your disciples never do?”
translation_verse: Wee daale balaŋ baale naawɔ na Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre silala na Farasiitena ne bake be nɔɔ. Keŋte be bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, “Weera dɔɔ te Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre silala na Farasiitena ne bake be nɔɔ, te nyaŋ wui kɛwɔ dɔɔ bɔɔ ne bake be nɔɔ ya?”

english_verse: But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.
translation_verse: Mɔna debaŋ daale gyae kekɔŋ keŋ baa mo alowɔle baale ŋon lee be gyaŋ. Mena wee kenaŋ man te baa bake be nɔɔ.

english_verse: No one patches old clothes by sewing on a piece of new cloth. The new piece would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
translation_verse: Ŋolo bɛɛ kara kanyaŋ wɔle mo taa kakyeŋgbelaa keŋ ba yem nɛ bɔɔ ya. Akpaa ɔ yɛɛ mena nɛ, kanyaŋ wɔle keŋ gyae ke tao kakyeŋgbelaa keŋ wolose ke bɔɔ keŋ yɛɛ ke dinaa.

english_verse: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined. New wine must be put into new wineskins. A Question about the Sabbath (Matthew
translation_verse: Mena mɔ te ŋolo bɛɛ mo soloŋ wɔle do wonembu tɔne ogele bene man ya. Akpaa ɔ yɛɛ mena na soloŋ keŋ gyae ke tao tɔne keŋ lii, na soloŋ keŋ na wonembu tɔne ogele keŋ pou ke wɔlɛɛ. Bɛɛ mo soloŋ wɔle bo do wonembu tɔne ogele wɔle man.” Yesu kawola lee kefɛɛfowee wose man Mateo

english_verse: One Sabbath Jesus and his disciples were walking through some wheat fields. His disciples were picking grains of wheat as they went along.
translation_verse: Kelii Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee nɛ, Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ gyuuwɔ ka mo wɔɔ daale man. Na weenɛɛ ŋan seŋɛɛ wɔɔ kenaŋ man nɛ yɛɛ ŋgba kefaa nɛ. Keŋte ba kpera ŋaale posaa bɛɛ tao.

english_verse: Some Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do that!”
translation_verse: Keŋte Farasiitena bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, “Weera te n kaseela baŋ ne yɛɛ kpene keŋ dɛɛ kɔle nɛ da kefɛɛfowee?”

english_verse: So the Son of Man is Lord over the Sabbath.”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon yɛna kefɛɛfowee keŋ gba Gbeŋgyoo.”

english_verse: He even had to tell his disciples to get a boat ready to keep him from being crushed by the crowds.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ booroŋ dɔɔ ɔ yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyi baa gyae degbele fa e, na balaŋ baŋ be nyiŋ tɔ e do ya.

english_verse: The Pharisees wanted to accuse Jesus of doing something wrong, and they kept watching to see if Jesus would heal him on the Sabbath.
translation_verse: Na balaŋ baŋ baale na deke Yesu nyi waa kyɔ balee ŋon kawee ba kefɛɛfowee keŋ, na baa nyiŋ mo nombia gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ.

english_verse: Jesus told the man to stand up where everyone could see him.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako baam dayeŋasate ŋon nyiaa, “Lee kaa seŋ siaman kɛbo.”

english_verse: Jesus was angry as he looked around at the people. Yet he felt sorry for them because they were so stubborn. Then he told the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He did, and his bad hand was healed.
translation_verse: Te Yesu kɛɛ wɔ pou gyaŋee baŋ man, te takaa e nideli lee ba deŋele doŋ keŋ dɔɔ. Keŋte ɔ yako kaweete ŋon nyiaa, “Tenee n baam.” Botɔɔ te balee ŋon tenee ɔ baam keŋ, te kawee keŋ taŋawɔ te ɔ baam keŋ yɛɛ doŋ.

english_verse: Jesus led his disciples down to the shore of the lake. Large crowds followed him from Galilee, Judea,
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle te Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ wɔŋa ba wose lee botɔɔ gyu Galelia boo nɔɔman, te balaŋ burum baŋ bɔɔ lee Galelia nɛ sila e gyu botɔɔ.

english_verse: Simon was one of the twelve, and Jesus named him Peter.
translation_verse: Baala kufu bala baŋ Yesu ba lese nɛ yela yaa, Simɔn te ɔ doo e yele nyi Pita, na

english_verse: Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus were also apostles. The others were Simon, known as the Eager One,
translation_verse: na Anderiase na Filipo na Batolomeo na Mateo na Tomase na Alfeose bu Gyemisi na Tadeose na Kaanan baale Simɔn ŋon ɔ ne gyae ɔ balaŋ nideli nɛ,

english_verse: Some teachers of the Law of Moses came from Jerusalem and said, “This man is under the power of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons! He is even forcing out demons with the help of Beelzebul.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ bɔɔ lee Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man kɔŋ botɔɔ nɛ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Feliŋkumɛɛ pou gyoo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Bɛlisɛbu nɛ doola Yesu man. Ŋon faa na e doŋ te ɔ na mo gberaa feliŋkumɛɛ lee balaŋ man nɛ.”

translation_verse: Na balaŋ burum kyaa kilisi e te be yako e nyiaa, “Kɛɛ, n naa na n naabia seŋɛɛ debɔɔman bɛɛ gyae neŋ.”

english_verse: Jesus told the people to gather around him. Then he spoke to them in riddles and said: How can Satan force himself out?
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu baake balaŋ baŋ kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ yako wɔ aduya man nyiaa, “Sena te ɔbɔnsam waa tale gegi ɔ wose?

english_verse: Jesus went back home, and once again such a large crowd gathered that there was no chance even to eat.
translation_verse: Yesu ka lese ɔ kpilala baŋ taŋ nɛ te ɔ gyuuwɔ ɔ dɛɛ. Keŋte balaŋ burum kɔŋawɔ kaa wuu ɔ dɔɔ, keŋ ŋon na ɔ kaseela bee nyiŋ gbɛɛ baa di weenɛɛ paŋpaŋ gba ya.

english_verse: A nation whose people fight each other won't last very long.
translation_verse: Akpaa tɛɛle daale man balaŋ de kpase ba wose bee yoo na dɔŋa nɛ, tɛɛle kenaŋ be gyae ke seŋ ya.

english_verse: How can anyone break into the house of a strong man and steal his things, unless he first ties up the strong man? Then he can take everything.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, ŋolo be tale gyu doŋte ŋolo dɛɛ na ɔ kaa mo ɔ kolo, keŋ nyi ɔ te di doŋte ŋon dɔɔ ya. Nyi akpaa ɔ di doŋte ŋon dɔɔ te ɔ bake e see nɛ, kenaŋ na waa tale kuu wa abɔɔ pou.

english_verse: I promise you that any of the sinful things you say or do can be forgiven, no matter how terrible those things are.
translation_verse: Mɛɛ yako ŋon anokoare baa ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ waa tale mo balaŋ pou nombiakumɛɛ na mmusuo nombia ŋan bɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ kyɛɛ wɔ.

english_verse: But if you speak against the Holy Spirit, you can never be forgiven. That sin will be held against you forever.
translation_verse: Mɔna walaŋ ŋon waa kolosi mmusuo nombia tia Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon nɛ, ɔ be gyae waa mo kyɛɛ ɔtenate korakora ya. Nawolo nyi walaŋ ŋonaŋ te yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛ ŋan ŋe doo kekpaakekpaa.

english_verse: Jesus said this because the people were saying that he had an evil spirit in him. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Matthew
translation_verse: Keŋ dɔɔ te Yesu be yako nombia kɛŋa yɛna nyiaa, na balaŋ ne yako nyi feliŋkum doo la ɔ man. Yesu ɔ naa na ɔ naabia Mateo

english_verse: Jesus' mother and brothers came and stood outside. Then they sent someone with a message for him to come out to them.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu ɔ naa na ɔ naabia kɔŋawɔ kaa seŋ debɔɔman kpila nyi baa baake e fa wɔ.

english_verse: Jesus asked, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Bomɔ yɛna me naa na me naabia?”

english_verse: Then he looked at the people sitting around him and said, “Here are my mother and my brothers.
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ kɛɛ baŋ be kyaa kilisi e nɛ gyaŋee te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Kɛɛ, me naa na me naabia yɛna kɛwɔ.

english_verse: Anyone who obeys God is my brother or sister or mother.”
translation_verse: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii nɛ, ŋon yɛna me naabu na me deesi na me naa.”

english_verse: The next time Jesus taught beside Lake Galilee, a big crowd gathered. It was so large that he had to sit in a boat out on the lake, while the people stood on the shore.
translation_verse: Wee daale Yesu besewɔ ɔ na wola Wurubuarɛ nombia Galelia boo nɔɔman. Te mena keŋ balaŋ burum be kaa wuu ɔ dɔɔ dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ gyoowɔ ke kyaa degbele man gyu loŋ keŋ dɔɔ na balaŋ akaŋ baŋ pou seŋɛɛ dekeŋkeŋ dɔɔ.

english_verse: While the farmer was scattering the seed, some of it fell along the road and was eaten by birds.
translation_verse: Ɔ kɛɛ ŋmatee ŋa nɛ, ŋaale yalawɔ gbɛɛneŋbia ŋan doo wɔɔ keŋ man nɛ man, te gyebuse kaa tɔɔsee ŋa di.

english_verse: He used stories to teach them many things, and this is part of what he taught:
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔ wola wɔ nombia aduya man.

english_verse: Now listen! A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
translation_verse: Te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ tei kɛbo, wɔɔfarale ŋolo gyuuwɔ ke ŋmatee abɔɔbia ŋgba amɔla nɛ ɔ wɔɔman.

english_verse: But when the sun came up, the plants were scorched and dried up, because they did not have deep roots.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɔga kaa seŋawɔ na te ŋa botowɔ. Te keŋ ŋe te nyiŋ lila burum dɔɔ ya nɛ, ŋe popou yekeewɔ.

english_verse: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked out the plants. So they did not produce any grain.
translation_verse: Bia ŋan ŋaale mɔ yalawɔ kpalamse man te ŋe tuuwɔ, te kpalamse ŋan mɔ tuuwɔ milii ŋa. Mena dɔɔ ŋe te nyiŋ ŋa wose se bia ya.

english_verse: Finally, Jesus said: What is God's kingdom like? What story can I use to explain it?
translation_verse: Yesu besewɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Woŋ te dɛɛ mo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ maa na? Yaa dudu woŋti te dɛɛ gyɔ na dɛɛ mo lese ke man?

english_verse: times as much as was planted. Light (Luke
translation_verse: ), nɛ.” Yesu kegyɔ dudu lee kanea wose man Mateo

english_verse: When Jesus was alone with the twelve apostles and some others, they asked him about these stories.
translation_verse: Keka Yesu waageŋ nɛ, ɔ kaseela kufu bala baŋ na balaŋ baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ kpu na wɔ nɛ yako e nyi waa wola wɔ dudu keŋ asɛɛ.

english_verse: But they don't have roots, and they don't last very long. As soon as life gets hard or the message gets them in trouble, they give up.
translation_verse: Mɔna keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan te nyiŋ lila be man dɔɔ ya nɛ, bɔɔ ne tale seŋ keŋkeŋ kelii ɔto ya. Mena dɔɔ akpaa de kɔŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan dɔɔ te bɛɛ naase wɔ diyem na, be ta bese be wɔle bileŋ.

english_verse: The reason is, “These people will look and look, but never see. They will listen and listen, but never understand. If they did, they would turn to God and be forgiven.” Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Matthew
translation_verse: na ke yela, ‘Baa kɛo kɛo mɔna bɔɔ gyae baa naa kolo ya, te baa tei tei mɔna bɔɔ gyae baa nyii nombia ŋenaŋ man ya. Na mena ya nɛ, baa kyɛɛkee lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man, na Wurubuarɛ ka mo be nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ.’ ” Yesu kalese dudu keŋ asɛɛ Mateo

english_verse: What the farmer is spreading is really the message about the kingdom.
translation_verse: Abɔɔbia ŋan wɔɔfarale ŋon be ŋmatee nɛ seŋɛɛ fa Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan.

english_verse: Jesus also said: You don't light a lamp and put it under a clay pot or under a bed. Don't you put a lamp on a lampstand?
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ŋolo bɛɛ kyaŋee kanea na waa mo kolo wuu ke dɔɔ yaa waa mo ke do mpa ateta ya. Mɔna ɔ na mo ke bo gyakaa kolo dɔɔ adido.

english_verse: Again Jesus said: God's kingdom is like what happens when a farmer scatters seed in a field.
translation_verse: Yesu besewɔ gyɔ dudu fa wɔ nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ ŋgba mena keŋ walaŋ ne ŋmatee abɔɔbia ŋaale ŋgba amɔla ɔ wɔɔman nɛ.

english_verse: It is the ground that makes the seeds sprout and grow into plants that produce grain.
translation_verse: Tɛɛle keŋ gbagba ne yele la te bia ŋan ne tuu. Ne taŋgbɛɛ yɛɛ faareŋse na te fulu, pɛte kaa yɛɛ bia.

english_verse: Then when harvest season comes and the grain is ripe, the farmer cuts it with a sickle. A Mustard Seed (Matthew
translation_verse: Amɔla bia ŋan de yɛɛ pɛ, na wɔɔte ŋon ta mo ɔ sakawa gyu ke kara ŋa, nawolo nyi amɔla ŋan kekara debaŋ te lii.” Daŋbii keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi Mastade nɛ dudu Mateo

english_verse: It is like what happens when a mustard seed is planted in the ground. It is the smallest seed in all the world.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ ŋgba daŋbii peperee daale keŋ bɛɛ baake nyi Mastade nɛ. Wɔɔ man abɔɔ ŋan bee duu pou nɛ, daŋbii kei yɛna ŋe man kemaasa.

english_verse: But once it is planted, it grows larger than any garden plant. It even puts out branches that are big enough for birds to nest in its shade. The Reason for Teaching with Stories (Matthew
translation_verse: Mɔna nyi akpaa be duu ke nɛ, kɛɛ bee bese kolo dinaa kela daasebia akaŋ ŋan pou, keŋ nyi gyebuse ne kɔŋ kaa loo ba asaase do ke tombia man.”

english_verse: He did not tell them anything without using stories. But when he was alone with his disciples, he explained everything to them. A Storm (Matthew
translation_verse: Ɔ te kolosi nombii yakaa fa wɔ keŋ ɔ te gyɔ dudu ya. Mɔ nyi akpaa de ka ŋon na ɔ kaseela baŋ baageŋ nɛ, ɔ na lese aduya ŋan pou man wola wɔ. Yesu keyɔkɔse feliŋ ɛpo dɔɔ Mateo

english_verse: That evening, Jesus said to his disciples, “Let's cross to the east side.”
translation_verse: Mena wee kenaŋ balinɔɔ nɛ, Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Ɛ yela dɛɛ toŋ gyu boo keŋ diŋgyiŋ.”

english_verse: Jesus used many other stories when he spoke to the people, and he taught them as much as they could understand.
translation_verse: Aduya burum ŋgba kei dinɔɔ nɛ te Yesu moowɔ kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia fa balaŋ baŋ, ŋgba mena keŋ baa tale nyii ŋe man nideli nɛ.

english_verse: So they left the crowd, and his disciples started across the lake with him in the boat. Some other boats followed along.
translation_verse: Keŋte be tinaa balaŋ dikpii keŋ yela, te ŋon na ɔ kaseela baŋ gyoowɔ degbele man te be laŋawɔ. Na agbelese ŋaale mɔ silana wɔ.

english_verse: Suddenly a storm struck the lake. Waves started splashing into the boat, and it was about to sink.
translation_verse: Be kee gyu na te feliŋ dinaa daale korowɔ ne gyɔɔ, te gyɔɔ loŋ do degbele keŋ man te ke yela ke munu.

english_verse: Jesus was in the back of the boat with his head on a pillow, and he was asleep. His disciples woke him and said, “Teacher, don't you care that we're about to drown?”
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ na Yesu te doo dosoroŋ degbele keŋ wɔle mo ɔ nyee gyakaa keputu dɔɔ. Keŋte ɔ kaseela baŋ ke gyuusu e bɔɔse e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, n be famenɛ ŋgba dɛɛ gyae dee munu yee?”

english_verse: Jesus got up and ordered the wind and the waves to be quiet. The wind stopped, and everything was calm.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu korowɔ te ɔ fae feliŋ keŋ te ɔ yako loŋ keŋ nyiaa, “Yɛɛ yididi!” Keŋte feliŋ keŋ tinaa kegyɔ yela, te loŋ keŋ mɔ yɛɛwɔ yididi.

english_verse: Jesus asked his disciples, “Why were you afraid? Don't you have any faith?”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Woŋ dɔɔ te ɛ dana gyakoloŋ mena? Ɛ sɛɛ ɛ te lɛɛ ta maŋ di yee?”

english_verse: Now they were more afraid than ever and said to each other, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him!”
translation_verse: Gyakoloŋ kyaŋa ɔ kaseela baŋ te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, “Walaŋ woŋti yaa kei, te feliŋ na loŋ gba ne nyii ɔ dei nɛ?”

english_verse: Jesus said to the woman, “You are now well because of your faith. May God give you peace! You are healed, and you will no longer be in pain.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Me bu, n kelɛɛ maŋ di dɔɔ te n nyiŋa kawee kekyɔ. Ta wosefɛɛreŋ man, nawolo nyi n kaale keŋ pou te taŋ.”

english_verse: She had gone to many doctors, and they had not done anything except cause her a lot of pain. She had paid them all the money she had. But instead of getting better, she only got worse.
translation_verse: Ɔ te naa diyem nideli teeneŋtena nyiŋmaa man, te ɔ wɔlɛɛ ɔ kɔba keŋ pou ɔ dana nɛ nyi waa nyiŋ kawee kekyɔ. Mɔna ɔ kawee keŋ na bese bo yɛɛ doŋ kpu.

english_verse: Then Jesus went inside and said to them, “Why are you crying and carrying on like this? The child isn't dead. She is just asleep.”
translation_verse: Yesu ka gyoo dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man nɛ te ɔ bɔɔse balaŋ baŋ nyiaa, “Weera te ɛ ne wii te ɛ ne fae yeesa? Bu ŋon ta yeŋa ta ya, ɔ doo dosoroŋ bo.”

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples crossed Lake Galilee and came to shore near the town of Gerasa.
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle te Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ toŋa boo keŋ gyu Gerasenetena tɛɛle man.

english_verse: from the graveyard where he had been living. No one was able to tie the man up anymore, not even with a chain.
translation_verse: Na mena balee ŋonaŋ kyae amusee man botɔɔ, te ŋolo na ŋolo bɛɛ tekaa tale kyaŋ e do agbaraagbaraa bela ya.

english_verse: He had often been put in chains and leg irons, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one could control him.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi nafɔ bɛɛ mo agbaraagbaraa do e ɔ nyiŋmaase na ɔ nawɔɔ man, mɔna ɔ ne tirii ŋa pou leki. Te ŋolo be dana doŋ keŋ waa tale yɔkɔse e ya.

english_verse: Night and day he was in the graveyard or on the hills, yelling and cutting himself with stones.
translation_verse: Nelim na ka weese na ɔ dɔŋ amusee man na bulase dɔɔ botɔɔ ɔ ne fae yeesa, na ɔ na mo boya kɛrɛɛ ɔ wose.

english_verse: When the man saw Jesus in the distance, he ran up to him and knelt down.
translation_verse: Balee ŋonaŋ kena Yesu ne kɔŋ lemlem nɛ, te ɔ yeuwɔ ke buŋ ɔ siaman.

english_verse: He then begged Jesus not to send them away.
translation_verse: Keŋte balee ŋon waase Yesu agbaatee agbaatee nyi, ɔ na gegi feliŋkumɛɛ ŋan lee depuŋ kenaŋ man ya.

english_verse: Over on the hillside a large herd of pigs was feeding.
translation_verse: Kekɔŋ mena na kekuiriise dikpii daale seŋɛɛ bula keŋ teŋa man bee dii.

english_verse: So the evil spirits begged Jesus, “Send us into those pigs! Let us go into them.”
translation_verse: Keŋte feliŋkumɛɛ ŋan doo Yesu teree nyi, “Waaseda, fa daa gbɛɛ na de kaa gyoo kekuiriise kɛwɔ man.”

english_verse: Jesus let them go, and they went out of the man and into the pigs. The whole herd of about
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu sɛɛwɔ te ba leewɔ ka gyoo kekuiriise baŋ man. Kekuiriise baŋ baa yɛɛ ŋgba kakpoŋse ala nɛ, te be yeuwɔ tisi dekeŋkeŋ kyoloŋkyoloŋ keŋ gyu ka gyoo loŋ man, te be popou munuwɔ.

english_verse: The men taking care of the pigs ran to the town and the farms to spread the news. Then the people came out to see what had happened.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ kɛɛ kekuiriise baŋ dɔɔ nɛ yeuwɔ, te be gyuuwɔ ke yako kpene keŋ be kɔŋ nɛ fa balaŋ baŋ be kyaa donɔɔ keŋ man nɛ na akuraase ŋan man pou. Keŋte balaŋ kɔŋawɔ kaa kɛɛ kpene keŋ be kɔŋ nɛ.

english_verse: When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had once been full of demons. He was sitting there with his clothes on and in his right mind, and they were terrified.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ te kyare ya te balaŋ kɔŋawɔ Yesu gyaŋ te be naa walaŋ ŋon nafɔ feliŋkumɛɛ doo ɔ man nɛ baŋɛɛ, na ɔ doona wa abɔɔ na ɔ diteŋ keŋ te taŋ. Ba kena nyi balee ŋon diteŋ te taŋ nɛ, gyakoloŋ gyoo wɔ nideli.

english_verse: Then the people started begging Jesus to leave their part of the country.
translation_verse: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ waase Yesu nyi waa lee be tɛɛle dɔɔ.

english_verse: But Jesus would not let him. Instead, he said, “Go home to your family and tell them how much the Lord has done for you and how good he has been to you.”
translation_verse: Mɔna Yesu te sɛɛ ya te ɔ yako e nyiaa, “Ta gyu n dɛɛ, na n kaa yako n loŋtona kpene keŋ n Gbeŋgyoo be yɛɛ fa neŋ, na mena keŋ ɔ be wii n waraŋase nɛ.”

english_verse: The man went away into the region near the ten cities known as Decapolis and began telling everyone how much Jesus had done for him. Everyone who heard what had happened was amazed. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Matthew
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ balee ŋon laŋawɔ, te ɔ gyuuwɔ donɔɔse kufu ŋan ŋe kyaa dupuŋ kenaŋ man nɛ ɔ ne yako kpene keŋ Yesu be yɛɛ fa e nɛ. Te gyakoloŋ gyoo balaŋ baŋ pou. Yesu kegyuusu Gyariose bu alebubii ŋolo, na ɔ kekyɔ alo ŋon fatabo ne gyɔ e nɛ kawee Mateo

english_verse: But the people laughed at him. After Jesus had sent them all out of the house, he took the girl's father and mother and his three disciples and went to where she was.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te balaŋ baŋ boŋo Yesu. Keŋte ɔ gegi balaŋ baŋ pou lee te ɔ moo bu ŋon ɔ kya na ɔ naa, na ɔ kaseela batooro baŋ be silana e nɛ, te be gyuuwɔ deni keŋ man woo ŋon doo nɛ.

english_verse: Once again Jesus got into the boat and crossed Lake Galilee. Then as he stood on the shore, a large crowd gathered around him.
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle te Yesu besewɔ gyoo degbele man toŋ boo keŋ gyu diŋgyiŋ. Keŋte balaŋ burum kɔŋawɔ kaa wuu ɔ dɔɔ boo nɔɔman botɔɔ.

english_verse: The person in charge of the synagogue was also there. His name was Jairus, and when he saw Jesus, he went over to him. He knelt at Jesus' feet
translation_verse: Keŋte Gyudatena ɔsom deni man kegyia ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Gyariose nɛ kɔŋawɔ botɔɔ. Ɔ kena Yesu nɛ, ɔ ke buŋawɔ ɔ nawɔɔ tɛɛ,

english_verse: and started begging him for help. He said, “My little daughter is about to die! Please come and touch her, so she will get well and live.”
translation_verse: te ɔ waase e nideli nyiaa, “Me bu alebubii ne gyae waa yeŋ. Kɔŋ na n kaa mo n nyiŋmaa gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ na waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ.”

english_verse: Jesus went with Jairus. Many people followed along and kept crowding around.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Yesu sila e laŋ. Be kee gyu na balaŋ burum silana wɔ, na bɛɛ soko e.

english_verse: In the crowd was a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years.
translation_verse: Na alo ŋolo kyaa keŋ fatabo ne gyɔ e kulutooneŋ kufu ala yaa.

english_verse: As soon as she touched them, her bleeding stopped, and she knew she was healed.
translation_verse: Karatetee dokoloŋ te fatabo keŋ ne gyɔ e nɛ karawɔ, te ɔ naawɔ ɔ wose man nyi ɔ kawee te taŋ.

english_verse: At that moment Jesus felt power go out from him. He turned to the crowd and asked, “Who touched my clothes?”
translation_verse: Debokenaŋ man te Yesu naawɔ nyi doŋ daale ta lee ɔ man. Keŋte ɔ besenawɔ kɛɛ balaŋ baŋ gyaŋee te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Amɔte tinaa na ma kegba?”

english_verse: His disciples said to him, “Look at all these people crowding around you! How can you ask who touched you?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ yako e nyiaa, “Nyaŋ gbagba n naa mena keŋ balaŋ be wuu n dɔɔ, na n na bese bɔɔse nyi ‘Amɔte tinaa na maŋ?’ ”

english_verse: But Jesus turned to see who had touched him.
translation_verse: Mɔna Yesu gyakaa ɔ ne kɛo nyi waa naa walaŋ ŋon ɔ be tina ɔ kegba nɛ.

english_verse: The woman knew what had happened to her. So she came trembling with fear and knelt down in front of Jesus. Then she told him the whole story.
translation_verse: Alo ŋon kena mena keŋ ɔ wose be yɛɛ e nɛ, ɔ moo gyakoloŋ na wose keseree kaa buŋ Yesu nawɔɔtɛɛ te ɔ yako e kpene keŋ be kɔŋ nɛ pou.

english_verse: While Jesus was still speaking, some people came from Jairus' home and said, “Your daughter has died! Why bother the teacher anymore?”
translation_verse: Yesu ka seŋ botɔɔ ɔ ne kolosi nɛ, baale leewɔ Gyudatena ɔsom deni man kegyia Gyariose ŋon dɛɛ kaa yako e nyiaa, “N bu alebu ŋon ta yem. Weera te n ne naase de Gbeŋgyoo ŋon diyem bela”

english_verse: Jesus did not let anyone go with him except Peter and the two brothers, James and John.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Yesu te yela ŋolo ke sila e kpu na Pita na Gyemisi, na Gyemisi ɔ naabu Gyɔn baageŋ ya.

english_verse: They went home with Jairus and saw the people crying and making a lot of noise.
translation_verse: Be ka benaa Gyudatena ɔsom deni man kegyia Gyariose ŋon dɛɛ nɛ, Yesu nyiiwɔ na balaŋ ne wii na bɛɛ fae yeesa keŋkeŋ.

english_verse: He took the twelve-year-old girl by the hand and said, “Talitha, koum!” which means, “Little girl, get up!” The girl got right up and started walking around. Everyone was greatly surprised.
translation_verse: Karatetee dokoloŋ te alebu ŋon korowɔ seŋ ɔ ne tɛɛ. Keŋte gyakoloŋ gyoo wɔ nideli. Na bu ŋon te di kulutooneŋ kufu ala.

english_verse: But Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened. Then he said, “Give her something to eat.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu faree wɔ nideli nyi be na yeli walaŋ na walaŋ ke nyii nombii kei ya. Te ɔ yako wɔ nyi baa gyae kolo fa bu ŋon ke di.

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples crossed the lake and brought the boat to shore near the town of Gennesaret.
translation_verse: Keŋte ba toŋa boo keŋ gyu Genesareti tɛɛle man, te be kpaŋa ba degbele keŋ botɔɔ.

english_verse: But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their relatives and their own family.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Balaŋ ne bu Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre yenaŋ kamasɛ, mɔna ŋon gbagba ɔ donɔɔ man balaŋ, na ɔ loŋtona na ɔ dekpaŋalaŋmantena bee buu na e ya.”

english_verse: But when Herod heard about Jesus, he said, “This must be John! I had his head cut off, and now he has come back to life.”
translation_verse: Gyoo Herode kenyii mena nɛ ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Gyɔn ŋon mɔɔ kara ɔ nyee nɛ bese la foŋ nɛ!”

english_verse: After the apostles returned to Jesus, they told him everything they had done and taught.
translation_verse: Yesu kpilala kufu bala baŋ ɔ be kpilii nɛ besewɔ kɔŋ kaa yako e nombia ŋan pou bɔɔ yɛɛ, na ŋan pou bɔɔ wola balaŋ nɛ.

english_verse: The next Sabbath he taught in the synagogue. Many of the people who heard him were amazed and asked, “How can he do all this? Where did he get such wisdom and the power to work these miracles?
translation_verse: Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee kelii nɛ, ɔ korowɔ gyu bɔ ɔsom deni man ɔ na wola wɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia. Ɔ nombia ŋan ɔ na wolo nɛ doo balaŋ baŋ pou nɔɔ te be bɔɔsewɔ nyiaa, “Aleŋ yaa balee kei nyiŋa abɔɔ kɛŋa pou lee? Aleŋ yaa ɔ nyiŋa mena nyansa kei lee? Kɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan ɔ ne yɛɛ?

english_verse: Isn't he the carpenter, the son of Mary? Aren't James, Joseph, Judas, and Simon his brothers? Don't his sisters still live here in our town?” The people were upset because of what he was doing.
translation_verse: Na kapenta ŋon yaa yee? Na ɔ naa yɛna Mɛɛre yee? Na ɔ naabia yɛna Gyemisi na Gyosɛfo na Simɔn na Gyuda yee? Na daa na ɔ desina kyaa la kɛbo yee?” Mena dɔɔ ba te lɛɛ e do ya.

english_verse: He told them, “You may take along a walking stick. But don't carry food or a traveling bag or any money.
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ na mo kolo tela kpu na ɛ gbutuse ya. Ɛ na mo weenɛɛ, yaa akpo yaa kɔba tela ya.

english_verse: If any place won't welcome you or listen to your message, leave and shake the dust from your feet as a warning to them.”
translation_verse: Te akpaa ɛ gyu yenaŋ daale te ba te lɛɛ ŋon yaa be te nyii ŋon ya nɛ, ɛ lee botɔɔ na ɛ kpiisi ya nkyokotase aŋasa lɔ wɔ na ke di adansɛɛ tia wɔ nyi Wurubuarɛ be gyae waa tina wɔ yela ya.”

english_verse: Herod had earlier married Herodias, the wife of his brother Philip. But John had told him, “It isn't right for you to take your brother's wife!” So, in order to please Herodias, Herod arrested John and put him in prison.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi na Gyɔn gyakaa ɔ ne yako gyoo Herode nyi, “Gbɛɛ be doo nyi waa mo ɔ kegyia Filipo wɛɛle yala ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ nɛ ya.” Mena dɔɔ gyoo Herode gbagba yela be kyaŋa Gyɔn te ba doo e agbaraagbaraase mo e ketɔ deni.

english_verse: Herodias had a grudge against John and wanted to kill him. But she could not do it
translation_verse: Nombii kei dɔɔ Herodia moo Gyɔn do ɔ tɔɔ man, te ɔ gyae gbɛɛ keŋ waa mo ko e mɔna ɔ bɛɛ tale ya.

english_verse: because Herod was afraid of John and protected him. He knew that John was a good and holy man. Even though Herod was confused by what John said, he was glad to listen to him. And he often did.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi gyoo Herode gyeŋ nyi Gyɔn yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman, te ɔ ta mo ɔ wose mɔ fa Wurubuarɛ. Mena dɔɔ gyoo Herode na weese Gyɔn keŋ nyi nombiikum daale be yɛɛ e ya. Debaŋ kamasɛ keŋ gyoo Herode de nyii Gyɔn nɔɔwoya na bɛɛ seŋ e nideli ya, mɔna ɔ ne gyae nyi waa tei e.

english_verse: The girl left and asked her mother, “What do you think I should ask for?” Her mother answered, “The head of John the Baptist!”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te alebu ŋon leewɔ yenaŋ gyu ke bɔɔse ɔ naa nyiaa, “Nna, woŋ te maa bɔɔse?” Botɔɔ te ɔ naa yako e nyiaa, “Yako nyi n ne gyae Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon nyee.”

english_verse: The girl hurried back and told Herod, “Here and now on a serving plate I want the head of John the Baptist!”
translation_verse: Debokenaŋ man te alebu ŋon barasewɔ gyu gyoo Herode gyaŋ ke yako e nyiaa, “Mɛɛ gyae n ke kara Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre nyee do tasa taŋtaŋalaŋ man fa maŋ.”

english_verse: Herod was very sorry for what he had said. But he did not want to break the promise he had made in front of his guests.
translation_verse: Nombii kei takaa gyoo Herode nideli, mɔna ntam keŋ ɔ be kam ŋɔɔla baŋ siaman dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ te tale bɛɛ ya.

english_verse: The guard put the head on a serving plate and took it to the girl. Then she gave it to her mother.
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ moo ke do tasa taŋtaŋalaŋ man kɔna gyoo Herode te ɔ moowɔ fa alebu ŋon, te ŋon mɔ moo ke fa ɔ naa.

english_verse: When John's followers learned that he had been killed, they took his body and put it in a tomb. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Matthew
translation_verse: Gyɔn kaseela baŋ kenyii ɔ yeŋ nɛ, be gyuuwɔ ka mo e ke wuu. Yesu kefa balaŋ kakpoŋse banoŋ weenɛɛ Mateo

english_verse: But so many people were coming and going that Jesus and the apostles did not even have a chance to eat. Then Jesus said, “Let's go to a place where we can be alone and get some rest.”
translation_verse: Na mena keŋ balaŋ na gyem tisi be dɔɔ beiŋ dɔɔ nɛ, be te nyiŋ gbɛɛ baa di weenɛɛ gba ya. Mena dɔɔ Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ yela dɛɛ wɔŋ da wose lee yenaŋ gyu botɔɔ keŋ balaŋ be kyaa ya nɛ, na ɛ kaa fɛɛfo.”

english_verse: They left in a boat for a place where they could be alone.
translation_verse: Keŋte ba gyoowɔ degbele man te be laŋa botɔɔ keŋ balaŋ be kyaa ya nɛ.

english_verse: That evening the disciples came to Jesus and said, “This place is like a desert, and it's already late.
translation_verse: Mena debaŋ kenaŋ na te lii balinɔɔ, mena dɔɔ ɔ kaseela baŋ kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ kaa yako e nyiaa, “Kɛbo yɛɛ ŋale nsana ke, te tɛɛ mɔ te yuu.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “You give them something to eat.” But they asked him, “Don't you know it would take almost a year's wages to buy all of these people something to eat?”
translation_verse: Mɔ Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ gyae kolo fa wɔ na baa di.” Keŋte be bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Da mo tomyɛɛre kulutoo dokoloŋ kɔba lɛɛ weenɛɛ koraŋ na, gyae ka yee balaŋ burum kɛwɔ pou?”

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “How much bread do you have? Go and see!” They found out and answered, “We have five small loaves of bread and two fish.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu mɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Bodobodose aŋmoŋ te ɛ dana? Ɛ gyu ke kɛɛ.” Be ke gyu ke kɛɛ nɛ te be kaa yako e nyiaa, “Bodobodose abiliŋ anoŋ na kpebia bala pɛ te de dana.”

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples to tell the people to sit down on the green grass.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyi baa yela balaŋ baŋ ke kyakee akpii akpii ŋalese nalaŋ ŋan dɔɔ.

english_verse: All of them saw him and were terrified. But at this same time he said, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi be popou naa e te gyakoloŋ kyaŋa wɔ. Debokenaŋ man te Yesu kolosiwɔ na wɔ nyi, “Ɛ nyiŋ konɔɔ! Ɛ na yee ya! Maŋ e.”

english_verse: Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples to give to the people. He also divided the two fish, so everyone could have some.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu moo bodobodose anoŋ na kpebia bala baŋ, te ɔ gyinaa ɔ nyee kɛɛ adido te ɔ fanewɔ do Wurubuarɛ nɔɔ. Te ɔ kɛrɛɛ ŋa fa ɔ kaseela baŋ nyi baa kpɛlɛɛ fa balaŋ baŋ.

english_verse: After everyone had eaten all they wanted,
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ pou diiwɔ baa ba kegyaebii.

english_verse: Jesus' disciples picked up twelve large baskets of leftover bread and fish.
translation_verse: Be ke di taŋ nɛ, ɔ kaseela baŋ tɔɔsee bodobodose na kpebia buruburu ŋan bɔɔ dii kaase na, be nyiŋa doŋase kufu ala (

english_verse: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into the boat and start back across to Bethsaida. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
translation_verse: Debokenaŋ man te Yesu yela ɔ kaseela baŋ gyoowɔ degbele man kyaŋ gbɛɛ bee gyu Bɛtesaida donɔɔ keŋ ke kyaa boo keŋ diŋgyiŋ nɛ, na ŋon ke lɔ balaŋ baŋ gbɛɛ.

english_verse: Then he told them goodbye and went up on the side of a mountain to pray.
translation_verse: Ɔ ke lɔ balaŋ baŋ gbɛɛ taŋ nɛ, ɔ gyuuwɔ bula dɔɔ ɔ kaa fane.

english_verse: Later in the evening he was still there by himself, and the boat was somewhere in the middle of the lake.
translation_verse: Kelii balinɔɔ nɛ, na degbele keŋ te lii boo keŋ nsana nsana, te kaa Yesu waageŋ boo keŋ diŋgyiŋ.

english_verse: He could see that the disciples were struggling hard, because they were rowing against the wind. Not long before morning, Jesus came toward them. He was walking on the water and was about to pass the boat.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu kɛɛwɔ na feliŋ ne gyɔɔ gyaŋee na ɔ kaseela baŋ, mena dɔɔ bɛɛ kaa na degbele keŋ kesaa. Keŋte ɔ karatɛɛwɔ ta loŋ keŋ dɔɔ gyu be gyaŋ, te ɔ yɛɛwɔ ŋgba ɔ ne gbaa wɔ nɛ.

english_verse: When the disciples saw Jesus walking on the water, they thought he was a ghost, and they started screaming.
translation_verse: Ɔ kaseela baŋ kena e ɔ dɔŋ loŋ keŋ dɔɔ ɔ ne kɔŋ nɛ, na ba gyeŋ nyi ɔ yɛɛ woo e te be fae yeesa.

english_verse: He then got into the boat with them, and the wind died down. The disciples were completely confused.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔ gyoowɔ kyaa degbele keŋ man kpu na wɔ, te feliŋ ŋon nɔɔ yɔkɔsewɔ. Nombia kɛŋa doo ɔ kaseela baŋ nɔɔ nideli.

english_verse: Their minds were closed, and they could not understand the true meaning of the loaves of bread. Jesus Heals Sick People in Gennesaret (Matthew
translation_verse: Yesu ka mo bodobodose anoŋ kpala balaŋ kakpoŋse banoŋ baŋ nɛ, ɔ kaseela baŋ te nyii ka asɛɛ ya, nawolo nyi na be sɛɛ be ta gyeŋa walaŋ ŋon dinɔɔ Yesu yɛɛ ya. Yesu kekyɔ kaweese Genesareti tɛɛle man Mateo

english_verse: As soon as they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus.
translation_verse: Be ka betaa lee degbele keŋ man pɛ, te balaŋ naa Yesu te ba gyeŋawɔ nyi ŋon ŋonaŋ.

english_verse: So they ran all over that part of the country to bring their sick people to him on mats. They brought them each time they heard where he was.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ balaŋ yekiiwɔ lee tɛɛle kenaŋ yenaŋ kamasɛ man kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ. Yenaŋ kamasɛ keŋ be nyii nyi Yesu kyaa na, bɛɛ kɔŋ na ba seele be kaweesetena ba afoŋoŋse dɔɔ bɛɛ kɔŋ botɔɔ.

english_verse: In every village or farm or marketplace where Jesus went, the people brought their sick to him. They begged him to let them just touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
translation_verse: Te akuraase yaa donɔɔse kamasɛ ŋan man Yesu de gyu nɛ, balaŋ na mo be kaweesetena kaa see abɔntere man, na ba te waase e nyi waa yela kaweesetena baŋ ka mo be nyiŋmaase tina ɔ kegba nɔɔbii paŋpaŋ bo gba. Te baŋ pou bɔɔ tina ɔ kegba keŋ nɛ, wose yɛɛ wɔ doŋ.

english_verse: Some Pharisees and several teachers of the Law of Moses from Jerusalem came and gathered around Jesus.
translation_verse: Wee daale nɛ, Farasiitena na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baale baŋ bɔɔ lee Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, kaa yilaawɔ Yesu gyaŋ.

english_verse: You are good at rejecting God's commands so that you can follow your own teachings!
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ gyeŋ adao gbɛɛneŋ ŋan ɛ na mo bɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan, na ɛ ke nyiŋ di ɛ beneŋ amanberɛse ŋan dɔɔ.

english_verse: The Pharisees and many others obey the teachings of their ancestors. They always wash their hands in the proper way before eating.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi Farasiitena na Gyudatena baŋ pou bee tina ba beneŋ amanberɛse ŋan yela ya. Mena dɔɔ nyi akpaa ba te faafo be nyiŋmaase ŋgba mena keŋ be naanaɔ be yako see ya na bɔɔ ne dii ya.

english_verse: None of them will eat anything they buy in the market until it is washed. They also follow a lot of other teachings, such as washing cups, pitchers, and bowls.
translation_verse: Mena ke mɔ te akpaa ba lee kebu man kɔŋ na, bɛɛ saase ba wose pɛte baa di weenɛɛ. Ba beneŋ amanberɛse boe nideli. Be dana gbɛɛneŋ ŋan bɛɛ mo saase ba leenɔɔse na tasase na osuŋse mɔ.

english_verse: The Pharisees and teachers asked Jesus, “Why don't your disciples obey what our ancestors taught us to do? Why do they eat without washing their hands?”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Farasiitena na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, “Weera dɔɔ te n kaseela baŋ bee di da beneŋ amanberɛse dɔɔ ya? Nawolo nyi bɔɔ ne faafo be nyiŋmaase ŋgba mena keŋ da beneŋ amanberɛ nawolo nɛ pɛ baa di ya!”

english_verse: It is useless for you to worship me, when you teach rules made up by humans.”
translation_verse: Bɛɛ som maŋ bo yakaa, nawolo nyi mmaraase ŋan denibalaŋ be yɛɛ nɛ te be dana bɛɛ wola balaŋ nombia.’

english_verse: You disobey God's commands in order to obey what humans have taught.
translation_verse: Ɛ te tina Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan yela, te ɛ dana ɛ beneŋ amanberɛse ŋan balaŋ na wolo nɛ man.”

english_verse: But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they own has been offered to God.
translation_verse: Mɔna ɛmɛɛ ne yako nyiaa, ‘Akpaa ŋolo de yako ɔ kya yaa ɔ naa nyi, “Kpene keŋ nafɔ me dana maa mo kyɔ neŋ nɛ yɛɛ Koriban ke.” ’ .

english_verse: And you ignore God's commands in order to follow your own teaching. You do a lot of other things just as bad. What Really Makes People Unclean (Matthew
translation_verse: Mena yaa ɛ na mo ɛ beneŋ amanberɛse ŋan wɔlɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan, te ɛ yɛɛ ŋa see ɛ bia mɔ. Te ɛ ne yɛɛ nombia burum ŋan ŋe yɛɛ ŋgba kɛŋa dinɔɔ nɛ kpu.” Kpene keŋ ne yɛɛ walaŋ ayimɛɛ Mateo

english_verse: Then Jesus said: What comes from your heart is what makes you unclean.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Kpene kamasɛ keŋ na lee walaŋ konɔɔ man nɛ, keŋ ne yɛɛ na e ayimɛɛ.

english_verse: Jesus called the crowd together again and said, “Pay attention and try to understand what I mean.
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, Yesu besewɔ baake balaŋ baŋ pou kaa yilaa ɔ gyaŋ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ popou ɛ tei maŋ, na ɛ ke nyii nombia kɛŋa man nideli.

english_verse: After Jesus and his disciples had left the crowd and gone into the house, they asked him what these sayings meant.
translation_verse: Yesu ka lee balaŋ baŋ man gyu dekpaŋalaŋ man nɛ, ɔ kaseela baŋ bɔɔse e dudu keŋ asɛɛ.

english_verse: He answered, “Don't you know what I am talking about by now? You surely know that the food you put into your mouth cannot make you unclean.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Mena dɔɔ ɛmɛɛ mɔ bee nyii nombia asɛɛ yee? Ɛ ba gyeŋ nyi kpene kamasɛ keŋ na lee yenaŋ gyoo walaŋ wose man na, ke bɛɛ yɛɛ e ayimɛɛ yee?

english_verse: It doesn't go into your heart, but into your stomach, and then out of your body.” By saying this, Jesus meant that all foods were fit to eat.
translation_verse: Ɛ ba gyeŋ nyi na ɔ konɔɔ man te ŋee gyu ka gyoo yee? Ŋee gyu bo ka gyoo ɔ dubulu man, na ta lee ɔ wose man.” Yesu ke yako kei nɛ, na ɔ nawolo baa nyi weenɛɛ kamasɛ dei na kedi.

english_verse: All of these come from your heart, and they are what make you unfit to worship God. A Woman's Faith (Matthew
translation_verse: Nombiakumɛɛ kɛŋa pou na lee bo walaŋ konɔɔ man, te ŋan ne yɛɛ na e ayimɛɛ.” Siria alo ŋolo ke lɛɛ Yesu di Mateo

english_verse: The woman replied, “Lord, even puppies eat the crumbs that children drop from the table.”
translation_verse: Keŋte alo ŋon mɔ yako e nyiaa, “Ayi, me Gbeŋgyoo, doo mena. Mɔ babaase baa tale fiu weenɛɛ ŋan bia ne dii te ŋɛɛ yale tɛɛle man nɛ di.”

english_verse: Jesus left and went to the region near the town of Tyre, where he stayed in someone's home. He did not want people to know he was there, but they found out anyway.
translation_verse: Lee botɔɔ te Yesu korowɔ gyu Tae na Sidɔn donɔɔse ŋan ŋe yɛɛ Iseraetena donɔɔse ŋa nɛ yenaŋ. Te ɔ ka gyoowɔ dekpaŋalaŋ daale man keŋ ɔ te gyae nyi ŋolo ka gyeŋ nyi ɔ kyaa botɔɔ ya, mɔna balaŋ naa e.

english_verse: A woman whose daughter had an evil spirit in her heard where Jesus was. And at once she came and knelt down at his feet.
translation_verse: Te alo ŋolo ŋon feliŋkum doo ɔ bu alebubii man ka betaa nyii nyi Yesu kyaa botɔɔ nɛ, te ɔ kaa buŋawɔ ɔ nawɔɔ tɛɛ.

english_verse: The woman was Greek and had been born in the part of Syria known as Phoenicia. She begged Jesus to force the demon out of her daughter.
translation_verse: Na alo ŋonaŋ yɛɛ Giriki alo e, te ba lola e bo Fonisia keŋ ke kyaa Siria tɛɛle man nɛ. Keŋte ɔ sola Yesu nyi waa gegi feliŋkum keŋ lee ɔ bu ŋon man.

english_verse: But Jesus said, “The children must first be fed! It isn't right to take away their food and feed it to dogs.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Nyi n dana bia na, n ke sɛɛ mo be weenɛɛ fa babaase? Koa, n ke taŋgbɛɛ mo weenɛɛ ŋan fa bia kedi pɛ. Te akpaa be dii kaase na, pɛna na n ka mo fa babaase.”

english_verse: When the woman got back home, she found her child lying on the bed. The demon had gone. Jesus Heals a Man Who Was Deaf and Could Hardly Talk
translation_verse: Alo ŋon ka lee botɔɔ gyu ɔ dɛɛ nɛ, te ɔ kenaawɔ nyi ɔ bu ŋon doo mpa dɔɔ na feliŋkum keŋ ta lee ɔ man. Yesu kekyɔ omumuu ŋolo kawee Mateo

english_verse: Jesus left the region around Tyre and went by way of Sidon toward Lake Galilee. He went through the land near the ten cities known as Decapolis.
translation_verse: Yesu ka lee Tae donɔɔ man nɛ, ɔ tɛɛwɔ Sidɔn donɔɔ man ka lee Galelia boo nɔɔ nɔɔ kɔŋ donɔɔse kufu ŋaale tɛɛle man.

english_verse: Some people brought to him a man who was deaf and could hardly talk. They begged Jesus just to touch him.
translation_verse: Keŋte balaŋ baale moo balee ŋolo ŋon ɔ deŋela be tɔɔ te ɔ bɛɛ tale kolosi nideli ya nɛ kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ. Te ba sola e nyi waa mo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ, na waa kyɔ e ɔ kawee.

english_verse: After Jesus had taken him aside from the crowd, he stuck his fingers in the man's ears. Then he spit and put it on the man's tongue.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu moo kaweete ŋon lee balaŋ baŋ man gyu yenaŋ. Te ɔ moo ɔ nyiŋmaasebia do kaweete ŋon deŋela man, te ɔ tɔɔ nɔɔloŋ mo tina ɔ laakem.

english_verse: Jesus looked up toward heaven, and with a groan he said, “Effatha!” which means “Open up!”
translation_verse: Te Yesu kɛɛ adido te ɔ lee weesɛɛ te ɔ yako e nyiaa, “Efata!” Keŋ nawolo nyi toro!

english_verse: At once the man could hear, and he had no more trouble talking clearly.
translation_verse: Debokenaŋ man te ɔ deŋela torowɔ te ɔ laakem booliwɔ, te ɔ talewɔ kolosi nideli.

english_verse: Jesus told the people not to say anything about what he had done. But the more he told them, the more they talked about it.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu faree balaŋ baŋ nideli nyi be na yako walaŋ kamasɛ ya. Mɔna keŋ na ɔ ke faree wɔ koraŋ nɛ, keŋ gba te be yela balaŋ nyiiwɔ beiŋ.

english_verse: They were completely amazed and said, “Everything he does is good! He even heals people who cannot hear or talk.”
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ wose yeŋawɔ nideli te be yakowɔ nyiaa, “Yesu te yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ nideli. Ɔ te yela deŋela atɔɔsatena ne nyii dei, te omumuuse mɔ ne kolosi.”

english_verse: Then Jesus explained clearly what he meant. Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that.
translation_verse: Yesu ka toro nombia kɛŋa yako fa ɔ kaseela baŋ nɛ, te Pita moo e lee yenaŋ te ɔ faree e nyi ɔ na kolosi mena ya.

english_verse: “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat.
translation_verse: “Balaŋ kɛwɔ nombia dana maŋ waraŋase. Nawolo nyi gyɛŋ weeya kpaakpaa atooro yaa ŋgba be kyaa me gyaŋ, te kolo be doo baa di ya nɛ.

english_verse: Some of them live a long way from here. If I send them away hungry, they might faint on their way home.”
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa me lɔ wɔ gbɛɛ na tanam na, be sia gyae kaa biri wɔ gbɛɛnaa man. Nawolo nyi be man baale lee lemlem”

english_verse: After Jesus told the crowd to sit down, he took the seven loaves and gave thanks. He then broke the loaves and handed them to his disciples, who passed them out to the crowd.
translation_verse: Te Yesu yela balaŋ baŋ kyakeewɔ tɛɛle man, te ɔ moo bodobodose nyetooro ŋan te ɔ fanewɔ fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ, te ɔ kɛrɛɛ ŋa mo fa ɔ kaseela baŋ nyi baa mo kpɛlɛɛ balaŋ baŋ. Keŋte baŋ mɔ moo ŋa kpɛlɛɛ balaŋ baŋ.

english_verse: They also had a few little fish, and after Jesus had blessed these, he told the disciples to pass them around.
translation_verse: Na ɔ kaseela baŋ dana kpebia kyomii baale mɔ. Te Yesu moo kpebia baŋ mɔ te ɔ fanewɔ fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ te ɔ moo ŋa fa wɔ nyi baa mo kpɛlɛɛ fa balaŋ baŋ.

english_verse: he got into the boat with the disciples and crossed to the territory near Dalmanutha. A Sign from Heaven (Matthew
translation_verse: te ŋon na ɔ kaseela baŋ gyoowɔ degbele man gyu Dalimanuta tɛɛle man. Farasiitena ne gyae nyi Yesu waa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia wola wɔ Mateo

english_verse: The Pharisees came out and started an argument with Jesus. They wanted to test him by asking for a sign from heaven.
translation_verse: Keŋte Farasiitena baale kɔŋawɔ Yesu gyaŋ bɛɛ bɔɔse e nombia na baa mo teese ɔ nɔɔ man. Be yako e nyi waa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia na kawola nyi ɔ lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ ampaŋ.

english_verse: The disciples had forgotten to bring any bread, and they had only one loaf with them in the boat.
translation_verse: Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ kee gyu nɛ, na ɔ kaseela baŋ te wolee be ta mo bodobodo ŋaale tela kpu na dokoloŋ keŋ doo ba degbele keŋ man nɛ ya.

english_verse: Jesus knew what they were thinking and asked, “Why are you talking about not having any bread? Don't you understand? Are your minds still closed?
translation_verse: Yesu ka gyeŋ kpene keŋ bɛɛ yako nɛ te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Weera dɔɔ te ɛ ne kolosi nyi ɛ ta mo bodobodo tela ya? Mena dɔɔ ɛ ta gyeŋa ta nombia man yee? Ya agyueŋse man ta toro ta yee?

english_verse: Jesus took him by the hand and led him out of the village, where he spit into the man's eyes. He placed his hands on the blind man and asked him if he could see anything.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu kyaŋa siayɛlɛɛsate ŋon nyiŋmaa man te ɔ moo e lee donɔɔ keŋ man, te ɔ tɔɔ nɔɔloŋ woli ɔ sia te ɔ moo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ te ɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, “N ne naa kolo?”

english_verse: The man looked up and said, “I see people, but they look like trees walking around.”
translation_verse: Keŋte balee ŋon gyinaa ɔ sia kɛɛ adido te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Mɛɛ naa balaŋ, mɔna be yɛɛ ŋgba daase ne tɛɛ la nɛ.”

english_verse: Once again Jesus placed his hands on the man's eyes, and this time the man stared. His eyes were healed, and he saw everything clearly.
translation_verse: Te Yesu besewɔ mo ɔ nyiŋmaa tina balee ŋon sia bela. Keŋte ɔ sia gyuusuwɔ, te ɔ naa kpene kamasɛ nideli.

english_verse: Jesus said to him, “You may return home now, but don't go into the village.” Who Is Jesus? (Matthew
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu lɔɔ balee ŋon gbɛɛ te ɔ faree e nyiaa, “Na bese gyu donɔɔ keŋ man bela ya. Ta gyu n dɛɛ.” Pita kpa Yesu yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Mateo

english_verse: Then Jesus asked, “But who do you say I am?” “You are the Messiah!” Peter replied.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛmɛɛ mɔ de? Ɛ kpa maŋ yɛna amɔ?” Botɔɔ te Pita tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Nyaŋ yɛna Kristo, nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ”

english_verse: Jesus warned the disciples not to tell anyone about him. Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death (Matthew
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu faree wɔ nyi be na yako ɔ wose man nombia fa ŋolo na ŋolo ya. Yesu kekolosi lee ɔ yeŋ na ɔ kefoŋ wose man Mateo

english_verse: Jesus began telling his disciples what would happen to him. He said, “The nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses will make the Son of Man suffer terribly. He will be rejected and killed, but three days later he will rise to life.”
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle te Yesu fiasɛɛ ɔ na wola ɔ kaseela baŋ nombia nyiaa, “Kaboena nyi maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa naa diyem nideli, na Gyudatena kegyiise na Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ ke bɛɛ maŋ nyi na maŋ yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon ya. Baa ko maŋ, mɔ weeya atoorote keŋ na maa foŋ lee yeŋ man.”

english_verse: But when Jesus turned and saw the disciples, he corrected Peter. He said to him, “Satan, get away from me! You are thinking like everyone else and not like God.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu gɛɛsewɔ kɛɛ ɔ kaseela baŋ te ɔ fae Pita nyiaa, “Ta lee me gyaŋ, ɔbɔnsam! Na Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii te n na gyueŋ ya, mɔna denibalaŋ kegyaebii te n na gyueŋ.”

english_verse: Jesus then told the crowd and the disciples to come closer, and he said: If any of you want to be my followers, you must forget about yourself. You must take up your cross and follow me.

translation_verse: Lee botɔɔ te Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ moo gbɛɛ bee gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man, na Yesu dɔŋɛɛ la be siaman. Be kee gyu nɛ, ɔ kaseela baŋ wose yeŋa wɔ, te gyakoloŋ mɔ kyaŋa balaŋ baŋ be silana wɔ nɛ. Keŋte ɔ baake ɔ kaseela kufu bala baŋ lee yenaŋ te ɔ yako wɔ kpene keŋ gyae ke kɔŋ ɔ dɔɔ nɛ nyiaa,

english_verse: We are now on our way to Jerusalem where the Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses. They will sentence him to death and hand him over to foreigners,
translation_verse: “Ɛ kɛɛ! Dee gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man baa. Baa lese maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon fa Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ. Baa di me nombia na baa bu maŋ fɔɔ nyi baa ko maŋ, na baa mo maŋ do balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ nyiŋmaa man.

english_verse: and they answered, “When you come into your glory, please let one of us sit at your right side and the other at your left.”
translation_verse: Keŋte be yako e nyiaa, “Dɛɛ gyae nyi de man ŋolo ke kyaa n dunoluŋ dɔɔ, na ŋolo mɔ ke kyaa n demena dɔɔ n gyoori keŋ man.”

english_verse: Jesus told them, “You don't really know what you're asking! Are you able to drink from the cup that I must soon drink from or be baptized as I must be baptized?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ ba gyeŋ kpene keŋ ɛ ne bɔɔse nɛ ya. Ɛ ke tale nyiŋ konɔɔ naa diyem keŋ maa naa nɛ, yaa ɛ ke tale gyoo kayeyɛɛ keŋ man maa gyoo nɛ?”

english_verse: “Yes, we are!” James and John answered. Then Jesus replied, “You certainly will drink from the cup from which I must drink. And you will be baptized just as I must!
translation_verse: Keŋte be tiranɔɔ fa e nyi, “Ayi, dɛɛ tale.” Te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ ke tale naa diyem keŋ maa naa, na ɛ ka gyoo kayeyɛɛ keŋ maa gyoo nɛ man.

english_verse: But it isn't for me to say who will sit at my right side and at my left. This is for God to decide.”
translation_verse: Mɔna mɔɔ taŋ doŋ keŋ maa lese walaŋ ŋon waa kyaa ma dunoluŋ yaa ma demena dɔɔ ya. Mena akyaese kɛŋa doe fa balaŋ baŋ me kya Wurubuarɛ gbagba be desina see wɔ nɛ.”

english_verse: When the ten other disciples heard this, they were angry with James and John.
translation_verse: Yesu kaseela kufu akaŋ baŋ kenyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, ba gyeŋa baŋ Gyemisi na Gyɔn dɔɔ.

english_verse: But Jesus called the disciples together and said: You know that those foreigners who call themselves kings like to order their people around. And their great leaders have full power over the people they rule.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu baake ɔ kaseela baŋ pou yilaa te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ gyeŋ nyi tɛɛle kei dɔɔ gyooneŋ ne di gyoori bo be balaŋ dɔɔ, te be kegyiise baŋ mɔ dana doŋ be dɔɔ.

english_verse: And if you want to be first, you must be everyone's slave.
translation_verse: Te ɛ man walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne gyae nyi waa bese ɛ siamante nɛ, kaboena nyi waa bese ɛ popou ɛ kegbetibii.

english_verse: The Son of Man did not come to be a slave master, but a slave who will give his life to rescue many people. Jesus Heals Blind Bartimaeus (Matthew
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon te kɔŋ nyi balaŋ baa som maŋ ya, mɔna me kɔŋae me kaa som balaŋ, na maa yeŋ mo ma nyeedoŋ lɛɛ balaŋ burum nyee.” Yesu kegyuusu Batimeo sia Mateo

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples went to Jericho. And as they were leaving, they were followed by a large crowd. A blind beggar by the name of Bartimaeus son of Timaeus was sitting beside the road.
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle te Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ gyuuwɔ Gyeriko donɔɔ man. Ŋon na ɔ kaseela na balaŋ burum baŋ ka lee botɔɔ bee gyu nɛ, be naa Timeo bu Batimeo ŋon ɔ sia be yɛlɛɛ nɛ na ɔ kyaa gbɛɛnaa nɔɔman ɔ na solee abɔɔ.

english_verse: When he heard that it was Jesus from Nazareth, he shouted, “Jesus, Son of David, have pity on me!”
translation_verse: Balee ŋon kenyii nyi Yesu Nasarɛte baale ŋon ne kɔŋ nɛ, ɔ fae yeesa yako nyiaa, “Yesu, gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii, wii me waraŋase.”

english_verse: Many people told the man to stop, but he shouted even louder, “Son of David, have pity on me!”
translation_verse: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ man burum too be nɔɔ gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ nyi waa wu ɔ nɔɔ. Mɔna nɛnɛɛ gba te ɔ ne fae yeesa keŋkeŋ nyiaa, “Gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii, wii me waraŋase.”

english_verse: Jesus stopped and said, “Call him over!” They called out to the blind man and said, “Don't be afraid! Come on! He is calling for you.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu seŋawɔ yako wɔ nyi, “Ɛ baake e fa maŋ.” Te be baake siayɛlɛɛsate ŋon yako e nyiaa, “Yela ke yɛɛ neŋ gyoŋ na n ka koro seŋ. Ɔ ne baake neŋ.”

english_verse: The man threw off his coat as he jumped up and ran to Jesus.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔ fuŋa ɔ kegba wurikyim lɔ yenaŋ, te ɔ toŋawɔ seŋ ta kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ.

english_verse: Jesus asked, “What do you want me to do for you?” The blind man answered, “Master, I want to see!”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Woŋ te n ne gyae nyi maa yɛɛ fa neŋ?” Botɔɔ te siayɛlɛɛsate ŋon yako e nyiaa, “Me Gbeŋgyoo, mɛɛ gyae me sia ke gyuusu”

english_verse: Jesus told him, “You may go. Your eyes are healed because of your faith.” At once the man could see, and he went down the road with Jesus.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Ta, n ke lɛɛ maŋ di dɔɔ n kawee te taŋ.” Debokenaŋ man te ɔ sia gyuusuwɔ, te ɔ sila Yesu laŋ.

english_verse: After Jesus had gone to Jerusalem, he went into the temple and looked around at everything. But since it was already late in the day, he went back to Bethany with the twelve disciples. Jesus Puts a Curse on a Fig Tree (Matthew
translation_verse: Yesu kelii Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, ɔ gyoo Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man te ɔ kɛɛ abɔɔ ŋan pou gyaŋee. Na tɛɛ te lam dɔɔ, ŋon na ɔ kaseela kufu bala baŋ gyuu Bɛtani donɔɔ man. Yesu kefalaa fiigi daŋ daale Mateo

english_verse: That evening, Jesus and the disciples went outside the city. A Lesson from the Fig Tree (Matthew
translation_verse: Kelii balinɔɔ nɛ, te ba leewɔ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man laŋ. Yesu kawola fiigi daŋ keŋ yeŋ asɛɛ Mateo

english_verse: They asked, “What right do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?”
translation_verse: Te be bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Doŋ woŋti yaa n dana n ne yɛɛ mena abɔɔ kɛŋa pou? Amɔte faa na neŋ mena doŋ kenaŋ?”

english_verse: If anyone asks why you are doing this, say, ‘The Lord needs it and will soon bring it back.’ ”
translation_verse: Akpaa ŋolo de bɔɔse ŋon nyi, Weera dɔɔ te ɛ ne booli e na? Ɛ yako e nyiaa, De Gbeŋgyoo ne gyae na e, mɔna be gyae ke kyare ya waa besena e kɔŋ.”

english_verse: The disciples left and found the donkey tied near a door that faced the street. While they were untying it,
translation_verse: Keŋte be gyuuwɔ kena afurum bu ŋon kpaŋɛɛ denanɔɔ man benaa na gbɛɛ te be booli e.

english_verse: some of the people standing there asked, “Why are you untying the donkey?”
translation_verse: Be kɛɛ booli e nɛ, balaŋ baŋ ba seŋɛɛ botɔɔ nɛ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Woŋ yaa ɛ ne booli e ɛ ke yɛɛ?”

english_verse: They told them what Jesus had said, and the people let them take it.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te be tiranɔɔ ŋgba mena keŋ Yesu be yako wɔ nɛ, te balaŋ baŋ yela ba moo e laŋ.

english_verse: The disciples led the donkey to Jesus. They put some of their clothes on its back, and Jesus got on.
translation_verse: Keŋte ba moo e kɔna Yesu te ba moo be kanyaŋse gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ, te Yesu gyeŋawɔ kyaa ɔ dɔɔ.

english_verse: In front of Jesus and behind him, people went along shouting, “Hooray! God bless the one who comes in the name of the Lord!
translation_verse: Na balaŋ baŋ be dɔŋɛɛ Yesu siaman na baŋ be dɔŋ ɔ wɔle nɛ ne fae yeesa kɛlɛɛ e akelegyi yako nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ waa hiraa walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne kɔŋ De Gbeŋgyoo yele man nɛ.

english_verse: God bless the coming kingdom of our ancestor David. Hooray for God in heaven above!”
translation_verse: De naana Defidi gyoori keŋ bese la kɛɛ kɔŋ. Wurubuarɛ dee yela ka kekɔŋ. Mena dɔɔ ɛ yela dɛɛ lese Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ yele.”

english_verse: The chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses heard what Jesus said, and they started looking for a way to kill him. They were afraid of him, because the crowds were completely amazed at his teaching.
translation_verse: Saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ ke nyii kpene keŋ ɔ be yako nɛ, be gyae gbɛɛ keŋ baa mo koe e. Mɔna gyakoloŋ gyoo wɔ, nawolo nyi ɔ kawola keŋ te yeli balaŋ baŋ pou wose ta yeŋ wɔ.

english_verse: So Jesus said to the tree, “Never again will anyone eat fruit from this tree!” The disciples heard him say this. Jesus in the Temple (Matthew
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔ falaa daŋ keŋ nyiaa, “Lee gyɛŋ ne gyu n be gyae n ka tekaa se bia na ŋolo ke di ya.” Te ɔ kaseela baŋ nyii kpene keŋ ɔ be yako nɛ. Yesu kegegi abɔɔyɔɔra Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man Mateo

english_verse: Then he taught the people and said, “The Scriptures say, ‘My house should be called a place of worship for all nations.’ But you have made it a place where robbers hide!”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔ bɔɔse balaŋ baŋ nyiaa, “Ɛ te kala ta Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako nyi, Wurubuarɛ kpa, ‘Me deni gyae ke yɛɛ botɔɔ keŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ pou baa kɔŋ kaa fane, mɔna ɛmɛɛ ta besena ke yɛɛ ŋmɛɛlatena dɛɛgyaŋee.’ ”

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples: Have faith in God!
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di.

english_verse: After Jesus and his disciples reached Jerusalem, he went into the temple and began chasing out everyone who was selling and buying. He turned over the tables of the moneychangers and the benches of those who were selling doves.
translation_verse: Be kelii Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, ɔ gyuuwɔ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ dekpaŋalaŋ man. Ɔ ke gyu botɔɔ nɛ, na balaŋ baŋ ta mo botɔɔ besena kebu dedii. Keŋte ɔ gegi balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ yɔɔ abɔɔ na baŋ bɛɛ lɛɛ abɔɔ botɔɔ nɛ. Ɔ besena baŋ bɛɛ yɛlɛɛ kɔbase nɛ tebuluse buŋii, te ɔ tɛɛse baŋ bɛɛ yɔɔ awuluŋna nɛ gbelɛɛ mɔ lɔ.

english_verse: Jesus would not let anyone carry things through the temple.
translation_verse: Te ɔ te fa ŋolo gbɛɛ waa seele kolo aneyɔɔŋ kaalaŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ dekpaŋalaŋ man ya.

english_verse: Whenever you stand up to pray, you must forgive what others have done to you. Then your Father in heaven will forgive your sins. A Question about Jesus' Authority (Matthew
translation_verse: Mɔna akpaa ɛ ta mo ɛ tebia nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ ya nɛ, ɛ kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido mɔ be gyae waa mo ɛ wui nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ ŋon ya.” Ba ke bɔɔse Yesu ɔ doŋ wose man nombia Mateo

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples returned to Jerusalem. And as he was walking through the temple, the chief priests, the nation's leaders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses came over to him.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ besewɔ kɔŋ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man. Te Yesu ke ta Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ dekpaŋalaŋ man ɔ ne lam nɛ, Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala na Gyudatena kegyiise kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ.

english_verse: Jesus answered, “I have just one question to ask you. If you answer it, I will tell you where I got the right to do these things.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Maŋ mɔ ne gyae maa bɔɔse ŋon nombii dokoloŋ. Nyi akpaa ɛ tiranɔɔ fa maŋ nɛ, kenaŋ na maa wola ŋon doŋ keŋ me dana mɛɛ yɛɛ mena abɔɔ kɛŋa pou.

english_verse: Who gave John the right to baptize? Was it God in heaven or merely some human being?”
translation_verse: Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋkesɔ keŋ ɔ be sɔ balaŋ nɛ, ka lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ yaa ka lee balaŋ gyaŋ? Ɛ tiranɔɔ fa maŋ.”

english_verse: They thought it over and said to each other, “We can't say that God gave John this right. Jesus will ask us why we didn't believe John.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ba gyoo nombia ŋan man te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, “Akpaa de yako nyi ka lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ na waa bɔɔse daa nyi, ‘Kenaŋ na woŋ dɔɔ te da te lɛɛ Gyɔn nombia ŋan di ya?’

english_verse: On the other hand, these people think that John was a prophet. So we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize.” They were afraid of the crowd
translation_verse: Te akpaa de yako nyi ‘Ka lee balaŋ gyaŋ’ na, dee yee balaŋ burum kɛwɔ mɔ. Nawolo nyi be popou ne lɛɛ di nideli nyi Gyɔn yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre e.”

english_verse: and told Jesus, “We don't know.” Jesus replied, “Then I won't tell you who gave me the right to do what I do.”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ be tiranɔɔ fa Yesu nyiaa, “Dɔɔ gyeŋ ya.” Keŋte Yesu mɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Akpaa mena na maŋ mɔ ba wola ŋon me doŋ keŋ me dana mɛɛ yɛɛ tom nɛ dalee ya.”

english_verse: It is also true that we must love God with all our heart, mind, and strength, and that we must love others as much as we love ourselves. These commandments are more important than all the sacrifices and offerings that we could possibly make.”
translation_verse: Te n besewɔ yako nyi kaboena nyi walaŋ waa mo ɔ konɔɔ pou na ɔ doŋ pou na wa agyueŋ pou gyae Wurubuarɛ, na waa bese gyae ɔ dɔɔ ŋgba ŋon gbagba nɛ. Mmaraase kɛŋa dɔɔ kedi dei kela keŋ walaŋ ka mo abɔɔ ke deesi fa Wurubuarɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus asked, “What do you think the owner of the vineyard will do? He will come and kill those renters and let someone else have his vineyard.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Woŋ te wɔɔte ŋon waa yɛɛ? Waa kɔŋ kaa koe mena paasetena benaŋ, na waa mo wɔɔ keŋ do paasetena wɔlɛɛ nyiŋmaa man.”

english_verse: Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother.
translation_verse: “De Gbeŋgyoo, de naana Mosesi ŋmarase mmaraa fa daa nyi, akpaa walaŋ da yem tina ɔ wɛɛle yela te be ta lola bia ya na, ɔ naabu waa yala lialo ŋon na baa lola bia ɔ kegyia ŋon yele man.

english_verse: When it was harvest time, he sent a servant to get his share of the grapes.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kelii nyi baa kɔ daasebia ŋan nɛ, balee ŋon kpila ɔ tobaale ŋolo gyu paasetena baŋ gyaŋ nyi baa fa e ɔ wui nɔɔ.

english_verse: The renters grabbed the servant, beat him up, and sent him away without a thing.
translation_verse: Mɔna paasetena baŋ kyaŋa ɔ tobaale ŋon, te be tuku e te be yela ɔ laŋawɔ nyiŋmaase kufu nawɔɔ kufu.

english_verse: Then the man sent another servant, and they killed him. He kept sending servant after servant. They beat some of them and killed some.
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle wɔɔte ŋon besewɔ kpila ɔ tobaale ŋolo bela, mɔna paasetena baŋ koo e bo. Ɔ kpila tewulɛɛ burum, mɔna be tuku baale te ba koe baale.

english_verse: The owner had a son he loved very much. Finally, he sent his son to the renters because he thought they would respect him.
translation_verse: Walaŋ ŋon ɔ be kaa keŋ wɔɔte ŋon waa kpila yɛna, ŋon gbagba bu dokoloŋ kooŋ ŋon ɔ bɛɛ sɛɛ ɔ dɔɔ ya nɛ. Wɔle wɔle nɛ, ɔ kaa kpila ŋon mɔ gyu paasetena baŋ gyaŋ te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, ‘Mberem me bu na, baa bu e.’

english_verse: But they said to themselves, “Someday he will own this vineyard. Let's kill him! That way we can have it all for ourselves.”
translation_verse: Mɔ paasetena baŋ kena ɔ bu ŋon nɛ be yako dɔŋa nyiaa, ‘Kei yɛna bu ŋon waa kaa di ɔ kya abɔɔ ŋan. Ɛ yela dɛɛ ko e, na abɔɔ ŋan nafɔ waa di nɛ ka bese de wui!’

english_verse: So they grabbed the owner's son, killed him, and threw his body out of the vineyard.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ be kyaŋa bu ŋon ko, te be wɔŋee e lee wɔɔ keŋ man ke leki.”

english_verse: The leaders knew that Jesus was really talking about them, and they wanted to arrest him. But because they were afraid of the crowd, they let him alone and left. Paying Taxes (Matthew
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ gyae gbɛɛ baa kyaŋ Yesu, nawolo nyi be naawɔ nyi baŋ dɔɔ te ɔ gyɔɔ dudu kei. Mɔna bee yee balaŋ baŋ, mena dɔɔ be tinaa e yela te be laŋawɔ. Ba ke bɔɔse Yesu lee lempoo ketɔ wose man Mateo

english_verse: Jesus knew what they were up to, and he said, “Why are you trying to test me? Show me a coin!”
translation_verse: Mɔ Yesu kena ba agyueŋ kumɛɛ ŋan nɛ ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ na beo ɛ wose, woŋ dɔɔ te ɛ ne gyae ɛ ka teese me nɔɔman. Ɛ mo kɔbabii keŋ kɔŋ maa kɛɛ”

english_verse: You surely know about people being raised to life. You know that in the story about Moses and the burning bush, God said, “I am the God worshiped by Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.”
translation_verse: Lee kefoŋ lee yeŋ man wose man nombia na, ɛ te kala ta de naana Mosesi tɔne man mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ be kolosi na ŋon Mosesi depoŋfa keŋ ne kpaa nɛ man yee? Ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, ‘Maŋ yɛna Aberaham na Asiki na Gyekɔpo Wurubuarɛ.’ Wurubuarɛ keyako kei nɛ, na nawolo baa nyi ɔ siaman na balaŋ kɛwɔ sɛɛ be kyaa

english_verse: The Pharisees got together with Herod's followers. Then they sent some men to trick Jesus into saying something wrong.
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle te Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ gyuuwɔ ke kpila gyoo Herode balaŋ baale, na Farasiitena baŋ baale nyi baa kɔŋ kaa teese Yesu nɔɔman na baa nyiŋ gbɛɛ kyaŋ e.

english_verse: They went to him and said, “Teacher, we know that you are honest. You treat everyone with the same respect, no matter who they are. And you teach the truth about what God wants people to do. Tell us, should we pay taxes to the Emperor or not?”
translation_verse: Baŋ mɔ kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ kaa bɔɔse e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, da gyeŋ nyi n bee di dekara ya, te n be famenɛ na kpene keŋ balaŋ ne yako nɛ ya. N bɛɛ kɛɛ walaŋ siaman ya, te n na wola Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛneŋ kpaakpaa ŋan ŋe gbɛɛ dɔɔ. Dei nyi dɛɛ tɔ lempoo fa Romantena gyoo dinaa Kaesa, yaa de na tɔɔ ya?”

english_verse: They brought him a silver coin, and he asked, “Whose picture and name are on it?” “The Emperor's,” they answered.
translation_verse: Keŋte ba moo kɔbabii dokoloŋ kɔna e. Botɔɔ te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Amɔte nyee na ɔ yele gyakaa la ke dɔɔ?” Te be yako e nyi gyoo dinaa Kaesa wui.

english_verse: Then Jesus told them, “Give the Emperor what belongs to him and give God what belongs to God.” The men were amazed at Jesus. Life in the Future World (Matthew
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Kpene keŋ yɛɛ gyoo Kaesa wui na ɛ mo fa e, te kpene keŋ mɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ wui nɛ, ɛ mo ke fa e.” Botɔɔ te gyakoloŋ gyoo balaŋ baŋ lee nɔɔ keŋ Yesu be tiri fa wɔ nɛ dɔɔ. Yesu kawola lee yeŋ wɔle kefoŋ wose man Mateo

english_verse: He isn't the God of the dead, but of the living. You Sadducees are all wrong. The Most Important Commandment (Matthew
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ te yɛɛ woya Wurubuarɛ e ya, ɔ yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ be kyaa na be sia nɛ Wurubuarɛ e. Mena dɔɔ ɛ ta yoo nideli ŋgba ɛ ne yako nyi yeŋ wɔle kefoŋ be kyaa ya nɛ.” Mmaraa dinaate Mateo

english_verse: But they cheat widows out of their homes and pray long prayers just to show off. They will be punished most of all. A Widow's Offering (Luke
translation_verse: Bɛɛ beo liala lɛo ba abɔɔ kamasɛ ŋan be dana nɛ. Te bɛɛ fane balaŋ man kyare mo wola ba wose. Wurubuarɛ waa wɔŋ mena balaŋ benaŋ dinɔɔ deŋela nideli.” Lialo diyemte ŋolo kɔba kado Luka

english_verse: The Sadducees did not believe that people would rise to life after death. So some of them came to Jesus and said:
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle te Sadukiitena baŋ bɔɔ ne lɛɛ di nyi walaŋ da yem na waa bese foŋ ya nɛ, man baale kɔŋawɔ Yesu gyaŋ kaa bɔɔse e nyiaa,

english_verse: Jesus answered: You are completely wrong! You don't know what the Scriptures teach. And you don't know anything about the power of God.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ ta yoo, nawolo nyi ɛ ba gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ya, te ɛ ba gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ doŋ keŋ mɔ ya.

english_verse: When Jesus saw that the man had given a sensible answer, he told him, “You are not far from God's kingdom.” After this, no one dared ask Jesus any more questions. About David's Son (Matthew
translation_verse: Yesu kena nyi Gyudatena mmaraa wolale ŋon te tiranɔɔ nideli nɛ ɔ yako e nyiaa, “Ɛmɛɛ na Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ nsana be dana lem ya.” Lee kei wɔle nɛ, ŋolo te tale gyɔ ɔ konɔɔ bɔɔse e nombii daale bela ya. Amɔte yɛna Kristo nyeelɛɛre ŋon? Mateo

english_verse: One of the teachers of the Law of Moses came up while Jesus and the Sadducees were arguing. When he heard Jesus give a good answer, he asked him, “What is the most important commandment?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ man ŋolo kɔŋawɔ botɔɔ. Ɔ ke kɔŋ kaa nyii mena keŋ bɛɛ wɔŋee nombia ŋan, na ɔ kena nyi Yesu ke tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nideli nɛ ɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan pou na, woŋti yɛna keŋ ne hia kela?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “The most important one says: ‘People of Israel, you have only one Lord and God.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Iseraetena pou ɛ nyii, ‘De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ ŋon yɛɛ dokoloŋ.

english_verse: You must love him with all your heart, soul, mind, and strength.’
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ kaboena nyi nya mo n konɔɔ pou na nya agyueŋ pou na n doŋ pou gyae n Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ.’

english_verse: The second most important commandment says: ‘Love others as much as you love yourself.’ No other commandment is more important than these.”
translation_verse: Alate keŋ mɔ yɛna nyi, ‘Gyae n dɔɔ ŋgba nyaŋ gbagba n wose nɛ.’ Mmaraa daale be kyaa keŋ kela kɛŋa ya.”

english_verse: The man replied, “Teacher, you are certainly right to say there is only one God.
translation_verse: Keŋte Gyudatena mmaraa wolale ŋon yako Yesu nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, ampaŋ yaa n yakowɔ nyi Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ dokoloŋ, te ŋolo be kyaa kpu na e ya.

english_verse: Everyone else gave what they didn't need. But she is very poor and gave everything she had. Now she doesn't have a cent to live on.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi balaŋ baŋ pou lese kɔba kyomii bo lee burum ŋan be dana nɛ man kaa do. Mɔ lialo diyemte kei dɔɔ, keŋ na ɔ diyem koraŋ nɛ kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɔ dana nɛ yaa ɔ moowɔ kaa do nɛ.”

english_verse: As Jesus was teaching in the temple, he said, “How can the teachers of the Law of Moses say the Messiah will come from the family of King David?
translation_verse: Yesu ke gyakaa ɔ na wolo Gyudatena ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ dekpaŋalaŋ man nɛ ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Sena dɔɔ te Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ ne tale yako nyi, Kristo ŋon Wurubuarɛ ba lese nɛ yɛɛ gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii e?

english_verse: If David called the Messiah his Lord, how can the Messiah be his son?” The large crowd enjoyed listening to Jesus teach. Jesus Condemns the Pharisees and the Teachers of the Law of Moses (Matthew
translation_verse: Te akpaa nyi gyoo Defidi baake nyeelɛɛre ŋon nyi ɔ ‘Gbeŋgyoo’ na, kenaŋ na sena te nyeelɛɛre ŋon waa bese gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii?” Keŋte yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ gyaŋee bee tei nɛ gyoŋ. Yesu kefaree ɔ silala lee Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ wose man Mateo

english_verse: As Jesus was teaching, he said: Guard against the teachers of the Law of Moses! They love to walk around in long robes and be greeted in the market.
translation_verse: Yesu kɛɛ wolo nɛ ɔ faree wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose nideli na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ. Bɛɛ gyae kegbase dɔɔloŋ nyiŋan kado na be dɔŋ na kebu agantaŋ man, na balaŋ kenyiŋ do wɔ nɔɔ.

english_verse: They like the front seats in the synagogues and the best seats at banquets.
translation_verse: Akpaa be gyaŋee Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man, na botɔɔse ŋan bɛɛ gyaŋee di be nɔɔ nɛ, na bɛɛ gyae akyaese kpaakpaa baa kyaa.

english_verse: Finally, a poor widow came up and put in two coins worth only a few pennies.
translation_verse: Keŋte lialo diyemte ŋolo moo kɔbabia ala, ŋan ŋe gyae ke yɛɛ ŋgba kotoku dokoloŋ nɛ kaa do adakaa keŋ man.

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples to gather around him. Then he said: I tell you that this poor widow has put in more than all the others.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu baake ɔ kaseela baŋ gyaŋee te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi lialo diyemte kei ta do kɔba kela balaŋ akaŋ baŋ pou.

english_verse: As Jesus was leaving the temple, one of his disciples said to him, “Teacher, look at these beautiful stones and wonderful buildings!”
translation_verse: Yesu kɛɛ lee Gyudatena ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man nɛ, ɔ kaseela baŋ man ŋolo yako e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, kɛɛ boya ŋan bɔɔ mo ma deni kei, na deni keŋ mɔ kyeo!”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Do you see these huge buildings? They will certainly be torn down! Not one stone will be left in place.” Warning about Trouble (Matthew
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu mɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ŋgba ɛ ne naa denɛɛ dinaana kɛŋa? Baa tekerii ŋe popou lɔ keŋ nyi ɛ be gyae ɛ kena boe na boe gyakaa ke dɔɔ dɔɔ ya.” Yesu kawola lee diyem keŋ gyae kekɔŋ nɛ wose man Mateo

english_verse: Jesus answered: Watch out and don't let anyone fool you!
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose man nideli, na ŋolo ba beo ŋon ya.

english_verse: Many will come and claim to be me. They will use my name and fool many people.
translation_verse: Balaŋ burum baa kɔŋ ma yele man na be kaa yako nyi, ‘Baŋ yɛna maŋ Nyeelɛɛre ŋon,’ na baa beo balaŋ burum na baa yo gbɛɛ.

english_verse: Later, as Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives across from the temple, Peter, James, John, and Andrew came to him in private.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu gyuuwɔ ke kyaa oliifi bula keŋ dɔɔ, te ɔ moo ɔ sia tee Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ. Ɔ kekyaa botɔɔ te Pita na Gyemisi na Gyɔn na Anderiase baageŋ kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ kaa yako e nyiaa,

english_verse: Someday you will see that “Horrible Thing” where it should not be. Everyone who reads this must try to understand! If you are living in Judea at that time, run to the mountains.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa ɛ naa kpene keŋ bɛɛ kɔle te ke dana duwaare nɛ de kaa seŋ botɔɔ keŋ tekaboena nyi ke dɛɛ seŋ ya nɛ, . Dekɔŋ mena na, balaŋ baŋ be kyaa Gyudia tɛɛle dɔɔ nɛ, baa yeu gyu bulase dɔɔ.

english_verse: When you hear about wars and threats of wars, don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but that isn't the end.
translation_verse: Akpaa ɛ nyii yoo na yoose nombia nɛ, ɛ na yeli ɛ wɔe ke lɔ ŋon ya. Kaboena nyi mena abɔɔ kɛŋa pou dɛɛkɔŋ mena, mɔna kei bɛɛ wolo nyi debaŋ ɔtomante keŋ te lii ya.

english_verse: This will be the worst time of suffering since God created the world, and nothing this terrible will ever happen again.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi weeya ŋenaŋ man nɛ, balaŋ baa naa diyem keŋ leenaŋ debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yɛɛ tɛɛle kei kaa lii na gyɛŋ nɛ, mena diyem kenaŋ te kɔŋa ta see ya, te ke dinɔɔ mɔ be gyae kekɔŋ mɔ see ya.

english_verse: They asked, “When will these things happen? What will be the sign that they are about to take place?”
