translation_verse: “Wola daa debaŋ keŋ nombia kɛŋa pou gyae ke kɔŋ. Woŋ te dɛɛ mo naa nyi kɛŋa pou gyae ke kɔŋ?”

english_verse: Nations and kingdoms will go to war against each other. There will be earthquakes in many places, and people will starve to death. But this is just the beginning of troubles.
translation_verse: Tɛɛlese baa koro seŋ tia dɔŋa, na gyooneŋ mɔ ka koro seŋ tia dɔŋa. Tɛɛle gyae ke wosi yenaase burum, te tanam mɔ gyae ke yala tɛɛle dɔɔ. Mɔna kɛŋa pou yɛɛ yaa ŋgba fulute kadoŋee kewalaŋ nɛ.

english_verse: Be on your guard! You will be taken to courts and beaten with whips in their synagogues. And because of me, you will have to stand before rulers and kings to tell about your faith.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose dɔɔ nideli, nawolo nyi baa mo ŋon gyina donɔɔ kegyiise na baa tuku ŋon Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man. Lee maŋ dɔɔ, baa mo ŋon gyu tɛɛle kegyiise na gyooneŋ siaman. Kei gyae ke yela ɛ ke nyiŋ gbɛɛ kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan fa wɔ.

english_verse: But before the end comes, the good news must be preached to all nations.
translation_verse: Mɔna kaboena nyi me silala baa taŋgbɛɛ kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia kpaakpaa ŋan fa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ pou pɛ.

english_verse: When you are arrested, don't worry about what you will say. You will be given the right words when the time comes. But you will not really be the ones speaking. Your words will come from the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa be kyaŋ ŋon gyu nombia dedii man nɛ, ɛ na wɔlɛɛ na kpene keŋ ɛ keyako ya. Kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa mo do ɛ nɔɔse man debaŋ kenaŋ nɛ, keŋ te kaboena nyi yaa yako. Nawolo nyi na ɛmɛɛ ne gyae la ɛ kekolosi ya, mɔna Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ne gyae la waa kolosi fa ŋon.

english_verse: Brothers and sisters will betray each other and have each other put to death. Parents will betray their own children, and children will turn against their parents and have them killed.
translation_verse: Naa lolo bia baa lese dɔŋa fa yeŋ, na kya mɔ ka lese ɔ bu fa yeŋ. Bia baa koro seŋ tia ba lolala, na baa lese wɔ fa yeŋ.

english_verse: Everyone will hate you because of me. But if you keep on being faithful right to the end, you will be saved. The Horrible Thing (Matthew
translation_verse: Maŋ dɔɔ balaŋ pou baa kɔla ŋon. Mɔ walaŋ ŋon ɔ kyaŋ ɔ wose seŋ keŋkeŋ kelii ɔto nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa lɛɛ ɔ nyee.

english_verse: If you are on the roof of your house, don't go inside to get anything.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa ŋolo kyaa ɔ deni nyee dɔɔ na, ɔ na tisi kɔŋ tɛɛle man nyi ɔ kaa mo ɔ kolo ya.

english_verse: If the Lord doesn't make the time shorter, no one will be left alive. But because of his chosen and special ones, he will make the time shorter.
translation_verse: Na Wurubuarɛ ke kɔkee kayeyɛɛ weeya kɛŋa ya nɛ, nafɔ ŋolo na ŋolo be gyae waa ka ya. Mɔna lee ɔ balaŋ baŋ ɔ ba lese see nɛ dɔɔ, te ɔ kɔkee weeya ŋenaŋ.

english_verse: If you are out in the field, don't go back for your coat.
translation_verse: Te akpaa ŋolo mɔ kyaa wɔɔman na, ɔ na bese gyu dɛɛ nyi ɔ ka mo ɔ kegba ya.

english_verse: It will be an awful time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children.
translation_verse: Mena weeya ŋenaŋ man nɛ, fulɛɛtena na talabose baa laako.

english_verse: Pray that it won't happen in winter.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛ fane fa Wurubuarɛ nyi, mena nombia kɛŋa naa kɔŋ waaretɛɛ ya.

english_verse: But be on your guard! That's why I am telling you these things now. When the Son of Man Appears (Matthew
translation_verse: Mɔna ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ ɛ doo ɛ wose dɔɔ, nawolo nyi me taŋ wulaa yako ŋon nombia kɛŋa.” Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon kekɔŋ Mateo

english_verse: So when you see all these things happening, you will know that the time has almost come.
translation_verse: Mena mɔ te akpaa ɛ naa mena nombia kɛŋa ne kɔŋ na, ɛ gyeŋ nyi maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon kekɔŋ ta benaa taŋ.

english_verse: The sky and the earth will not last forever, but my words will. No One Knows the Day or Time (Matthew
translation_verse: Adido na tɛɛle kei pou gyae ke gyu ɔto, mɔna me nɔɔwoya dɔɔ ŋan gyae ke kyaa kekpaakekpaa.” Ŋolo ba gyeŋ mena debaŋ keŋ nombia kɛŋa gyae kekɔŋ ya Mateo

english_verse: The stars will fall, and the powers in the sky will be shaken.”
translation_verse: Kyolobiise baa lee adido kaa yala. Te abɔɔ ŋan pou ŋe kyaa adido nɛ, gyae ke wosi.

english_verse: Learn a lesson from a fig tree. When its branches sprout and start putting out leaves, you know summer is near.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ kɛɛ fiigi daŋ na ɛ ke kasee kolo lee ka wose man. Akpaa ɛ naa ŋgba ke tombia da potee faareŋse wɔlɛɛ nɛ, na ɛ gyeŋ nyi awuluŋtɛɛ ta benaa baa.

english_verse: Then the Son of Man will be seen coming in the clouds with great power and glory.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ nɛ, balaŋ baa naa maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon ta mo doŋ na gyoorobiiri lee ŋɔma man adido mɛɛ kɔŋ.

english_verse: He will send his angels to gather his chosen ones from all over the earth. A Lesson from a Fig Tree (Matthew
translation_verse: Te maa kpila me kpilala lee adido na be kaa yilaa me balaŋ baŋ Wurubuarɛ ba lese wɔ see tɛɛle kei dɔɔ yenaŋ kamasɛ nɛ.

english_verse: No one knows the day or the time. The angels in heaven don't know, and the Son himself doesn't know. Only the Father knows.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ŋolo ba gyeŋ wee na debaŋ keŋ kɛŋa pou gyae ke kɔŋ ya. Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ be kyaa adido, na maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon koraŋ ba gyeŋ ya. Gyisɛ me kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ waageŋ gyeŋa la.

english_verse: So be alert! You don't know when the master of the house will come back. It could be in the evening or at midnight or before dawn or in the morning.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ doo ɛ wose dɔɔ, nawolo nyi ɛ ba gyeŋ wee keŋ dekpaŋalaŋte ŋon waa kɔŋ ya. Waa tale kɔŋ balinɔɔ yaa tɛɛnsana, yaa kegyim kɛɛ lɔ woya, yaa nebo nebo.

english_verse: So watch out and be ready! You don't know when the time will come.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛ kɛɛ nideli na ɛ ke desina ɛ wose see, nawolo nyi ɛ ba gyeŋ wee keŋ debaŋ kenaŋ gyae ke kɔŋ ya.

english_verse: You can be sure that some of the people of this generation will still be alive when all this happens.
translation_verse: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi balaŋ baŋ be kyaa gyɛŋ kei nɛ, be duuluŋ be gyae ke munu taŋ ya, pɛte nombia kɛŋa kekɔŋ.

english_verse: But if he comes suddenly, don't let him find you asleep.
translation_verse: Ɛ doo ɛ wose dɔɔ na ɔ be kɔŋ kaa futaa ŋon nyi ɛ doo dosoroŋ man ya.

english_verse: It is like what happens when a man goes away for a while and places his servants in charge of everything. He tells each of them what to do, and he orders the guard to keep alert.
translation_verse: Ke yɛɛ ŋgba walaŋ ŋon ɔ ba koro ɔ ne gyu gbɛɛ, te ɔ ba mo ɔ dekpaŋalaŋ do ɔ tewulɛɛ nyiŋmaa man nɛ. Ɔ faa be man walaŋ kamasɛ tom keŋ waa yɛɛ, te ɔ yako ŋon ɔ na deke denanɔɔ keŋ nɛ nyi waa deke ke nideli.

english_verse: I tell everyone just what I have told you. Be alert!
translation_verse: Nombii kei mɛɛ yako ŋon nɛ, mɛɛ yako baa fa balaŋ pou. Ɛ doo ɛ wose dɔɔ.”

english_verse: Corinthians
translation_verse: Korintotena

english_verse: Judas walked right up to Jesus and said, “Teacher!” Then Judas kissed him,
translation_verse: Gyudase ka betaa lii Yesu gyaŋ nɛ, ɔ baake e nyi, “Me Gbeŋgyoo,” te ɔ pukaa e.

english_verse: Jesus was led off to the high priest. Then the chief priests, the nation's leaders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses all met together.
translation_verse: Keŋte ba moo Yesu gyu Wurubuarɛ saese kegyia ŋon gyaŋ, te Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise baŋ pou na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala, na Gyudatena kegyiise kaa gyaŋeewɔ botɔɔ.

english_verse: She has done all she could by pouring perfume on my body to prepare it for burial.
translation_verse: Ɔ te yɛɛ kpene keŋ waa tale. Ɔ te taŋgbɛɛ kyɛɛkee nuŋ woli maŋ, na kawola nyi ɔ te desina maŋ fa me ke wuu pɛna maa yeŋ.

english_verse: This made some of the guests angry, and they complained, “Why such a waste?
translation_verse: Te balaŋ baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ baale gyeŋa baŋ bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, “Weera dɔɔ te ɔ wɔlɛɛ nuŋ kei mena.

english_verse: You may be sure that wherever the good news is told all over the world, people will remember what she has done. And they will tell others. Judas and the Chief Priests (Matthew
translation_verse: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, tɛɛle kei dɔɔ yenaŋ kamasɛ keŋ baa kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia kpaakpaa ŋan nɛ, baa yako kpene keŋ alo kei be yɛɛ nɛ na baa mo tɔɔse e.” Gyudase te sɛɛ nyi waa lese Yesu fa Mateo

english_verse: The two disciples went into the city and found everything just as Jesus had told them. So they prepared the Passover meal.
translation_verse: Ɔ kaseela baŋ ke gyu kelii donɔɔ keŋ man nɛ, be naa kpene kamasɛ keŋ Yesu be yako wɔ nɛ pɛpɛɛpɛ, te be desina nyeekelɛɛ weenɛɛ ŋan see botɔɔ.

english_verse: Judas Iscariot was one of the twelve disciples. He went to the chief priests and offered to help them arrest Jesus.
translation_verse: Keŋte Gyudase Isekarɔte ŋon ɔ yɛɛ Yesu kaseela kufu bala baŋ man ŋolo e nɛ, gyuuwɔ Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise baŋ gyaŋ nyi waa lese Yesu fa wɔ.

english_verse: The Son of Man will die, just as the Scriptures say. But it is going to be terrible for the one who betrays me. That man would be better off if he had never been born.” The Lord's Supper (Matthew
translation_verse: Maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa yeŋ ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako lee ma wose man nɛ, mɔna walaŋ ŋon waa lese maŋ fa nɛ waa laako. Nyi beŋ ta lola walaŋ ŋonaŋ ya na, nafɔ dei.” Yesu na ɔ kaseela kedi nyeekelɛɛwee dinaa weenɛɛ ŋan Mateo

english_verse: Jesus picked up a cup of wine and gave thanks to God. He gave it to his disciples, and they all drank some.
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle te ɔ moo leenɔɔ keŋ soloŋ doo ke man nɛ te ɔ fanewɔ fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ, te ɔ moo ke fa ɔ kaseela baŋ. Keŋte be popou nyɔɔwɔ lee ke man.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “This very night before a rooster crows twice, you will say three times that you don't know me.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako Pita nyiaa, “Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako neŋ nyi gyɛŋ nelim kei pɛte kegyim kelɔ woya betabola nɛ, nyaŋ ke bɛɛ betabotooro nyi n ba gyeŋ maŋ korakora ya.”

english_verse: But Peter was so sure of himself that he said, “Even if I have to die with you, I will never say I don't know you!” All the others said the same thing. Jesus Prays (Matthew
translation_verse: Keŋte Pita yakowɔ seŋ ke dɔɔ keŋkeŋ nyiaa, “Akpaa nyi ke yɛɛ yeŋ ke koraŋ na, mɔɔ gyae maa bɛɛ nyi mɔɔ gyeŋ neŋ korakora ya.” Te ɔ kaseela akaŋ baŋ pou mɔ yakowɔ mena. Yesu kefane Gɛkyɛmane Mateo

english_verse: told them, “I am so sad that I feel as if I am dying. Stay here and keep awake with me.”
translation_verse: Te ɔ yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Nombia ne takaa maŋ nideli keŋ mɛɛ gyae maa yeŋ. Ɛ kyaa kɛbo, ɛ na doo ya.”

english_verse: Jesus walked on a little way. Then he knelt down on the ground and prayed, “Father, if it is possible, don't let this happen to me! Father, you can do anything. Don't make me suffer by drinking from this cup. But do what you want, and not what I want.”
translation_verse: Te ɔ yakowɔ nyi, “Me kya, kpene kamasɛ be taŋ neŋ doŋ ya. Lese kaale kei me dɔɔ. Mɔ na yɛɛ kpene keŋ maŋ ne gyae ya. Yɛɛ kpene keŋ nyaŋ gbagba ne gyae.”

english_verse: But after I am raised to life, I will go ahead of you to Galilee.”
translation_verse: Mɔna me fom lee yeŋ man na, maa kyaŋ ŋon gbɛɛ gyu Galelia tɛɛle man.”

english_verse: Peter spoke up, “Even if all the others reject you, I never will!”
translation_verse: Keŋte Pita yako e nyiaa, “Akpaa be popou de yeu tina neŋ yela koraŋ na, maŋ dɔɔ mɔɔ gyae maa tina neŋ yela korakora ya.”

english_verse: and the men grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te balaŋ baŋ kyaŋa e.

english_verse: Jesus went back and prayed the same prayer.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ gyu ke fane yako nombii dokoloŋ kenaŋ bela.

english_verse: Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane, and he told them, “Sit here while I pray.”
translation_verse: Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ kelii botɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake nyi Gɛkyɛmane nɛ ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ kyaa kɛbo na me kaa fane.”

english_verse: When Jesus came back and found the disciples sleeping, he said to Simon Peter, “Are you asleep? Can't you stay awake for just one hour?
translation_verse: Ɔ ka bese kɔŋ nɛ, ɔ kaa naawɔ na ba te doo te ɔ gyuusu wɔ bɔɔse Simɔn Pita nyiaa, “Simɔn, ɛ te doo? Ɛ te tale kyaa na ɛ ka deke debaŋ dokoloŋ kooŋ keŋ nyi ɛ be doo yee?

english_verse: But when he returned to the disciples, he found them sleeping again. They simply could not keep their eyes open, and they did not know what to say.
translation_verse: Ɔ ka bese kɔŋ be gyaŋ na be sɛɛ be doo, nawolo nyi dosoroŋ doo be siaman te be ta gyeŋ kpene keŋ baa yako ya.

english_verse: When Jesus returned to the disciples the third time, he said, “Are you still sleeping and resting? Enough of this! The time has come for the Son of Man to be handed over to sinners.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ kɔŋ be gyaŋ betabotoorote te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyi, “Ɛ sɛɛ ɛ gyakaa dosoroŋ kei dɔɔ ɛ ne fɛɛfo? Tekaboe mena, debaŋ keŋ te lii. Be ta lese maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon fa nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra.

english_verse: Get up! Let's go. The one who will betray me is already here.” Jesus Is Arrested (Matthew
translation_verse: Ɛ koro dee gyu de kaa gyaŋee na wɔ. Ŋon ɔ na lese maŋ waa fa nɛ te tuŋ daa taŋ.” Ba kekyaŋ Yesu Mateo

english_verse: Someone standing there pulled out a sword. He struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his ear.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu kaseela baŋ man ŋolo ŋon ɔ seŋɛɛ botɔɔ nɛ lese ɔ yoosila kara Wurubuarɛ saese kegyia ŋon tobaale deŋele lese ke lɔ.

english_verse: Jesus was still speaking, when Judas the betrayer came up. He was one of the twelve disciples, and a mob of men armed with swords and clubs were with him. They had been sent by the chief priests, the nation's leaders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Yesu ke gyakaa ɔ ne kolosi nɛ, Gyudase ŋon ɔ yɛɛ kaseela kufu bala baŋ man ŋolo e nɛ, kaa bɛɛwɔ lee be dɔɔ. Na balaŋ dikpii keŋ be dana yoosilase na akpokpose, na Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ Gyudatena kegyiise ba lese do na e nɛ silana e.

english_verse: Judas had told them ahead of time, “Arrest the man I greet with a kiss. Tie him up tight and lead him away.”
translation_verse: Na Gyudase ŋon ɔ ba lese e fa nɛ taŋ taŋgbɛɛ yako balaŋ baŋ nyiaa, “Walaŋ ŋon maa pukaa e nɛ ŋon ŋonaŋ, ɛ kyaŋ e keŋkeŋ na ɛ ka mo e ta.”

english_verse: Jesus said to the mob, “Why do you come with swords and clubs to arrest me like a criminal?
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Me yɛɛ walaŋkum e te ɛ moo yoosilase na akpokpose ɛ ne kɔŋ ɛ kaa kyaŋ maŋ?

english_verse: Day after day I was with you and taught in the temple, and you didn't arrest me. But what the Scriptures say must come true.”
translation_verse: Wee kamasɛ keŋ me kyaa ɛ gyaŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man mɛɛ wola ŋon nɛ, ɛ te kyaŋ maŋ ya. Mɔna nombia kɛŋa pou kɔŋae, na ke yela Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ke kɔŋ mena.”

english_verse: All of Jesus' disciples ran off and left him.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu kaseela baŋ pou yeuwɔ tina e yela.

english_verse: One of them was a young man who was wearing only a linen cloth. And when the men grabbed him,
translation_verse: Na tobaale ŋolo ŋon ɔ wuuna kanyaŋbii nɛ silana Yesu. Be ka mo baa kyaŋ e nɛ,

english_verse: he left the cloth behind and ran away naked. Jesus Is Questioned by the Council (Matthew
translation_verse: te ɔ leewɔ be nyiŋmaa man yeu diteŋteli tina ɔ kanyaŋbii keŋ yela. Be kamo Yesu gyu kegyiise siaman Mateo

english_verse: Finally, some men stood up and lied about him. They said,
translation_verse: Te baale korowɔ kɛrɛɛ ɔ nɔɔse nyiaa,

english_verse: Peter replied, “That isn't true! I don't know what you're talking about. I don't have any idea what you mean.” He went out to the gate, and a rooster crowed.
translation_verse: Mɔ Pita lɛɛ aŋmaareŋ te ɔ yako e nyi, “Mɔɔ gyeŋ kpene keŋ n ne yako nɛ man korakora ya.” Botɔɔ te Pita leewɔ gyu denanɔɔ keŋ man.

english_verse: Peter had followed at a distance, and when he reached the courtyard of the high priest's house, he sat down with the guards to warm himself beside a fire.
translation_verse: Na Pita silana Yesu wɔle wɔle lemlem gyu Wurubuarɛ saese kegyia ŋon dekpaŋalaŋ man. Botɔɔ te Pita gyuuwɔ ke kyaa kotɔtɔɔtena baŋ bee gyee dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ nɛ gyaŋ bee woli boalaŋ.

english_verse: The chief priests and the whole council tried to find someone to accuse Jesus of a crime, so they could put him to death. But they could not find anyone to accuse him.
translation_verse: Na Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise, na Gyudatena kegyiise akaŋ baŋ ne kɛo nombii keŋ baa mo gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ na baa nyiŋ gbɛɛ ko e, mɔ be te nyim ya.

english_verse: At once the high priest ripped his robe apart and shouted, “Why do we need more witnesses?
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ saese kegyia ŋon tirii ŋon gbagba kegba te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Woŋ adansɛɛ yaa dɛɛ gyae dee nyii bela?”

english_verse: This time Peter began to curse and swear, “I don't even know the man you're talking about!”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Pita moo nɔɔdoŋ kolosi te ɔ kaŋa ntam kpu nyiaa, “Mɔɔ gyeŋ balee kei ŋon ɛ ne kolosi ɔ wose man nombia nɛ korakora ya”

english_verse: “We heard him say he would tear down this temple that we built. He also claimed that in three days he would build another one without any help.”
translation_verse: “De te nyii nyi ɔ kpa waa tekerii Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ balaŋ be maa nɛ, te weeya atooro wɔle na waa ma wɔle keŋ te yɛɛ walaŋ maa na ke ya.”

english_verse: But even then they did not agree on what they said.
translation_verse: Baŋ mɔ nombia ŋan bɔɔ kaa yako nɛ, te kyaa na dɔŋa ya.

english_verse: The high priest stood up in the council and asked Jesus, “Why don't you say something in your own defense? Don't you hear the charges they are making against you?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ saese kegyia ŋon korowɔ seŋ kebu keŋ nsana nsana te ɔ bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, “N be tiranɔɔ yee? Woŋ dɔɔ te balaŋ kɛwɔ ne kɛrɛɛ n nɔɔse, te n be tiranɔɔ ya?”

english_verse: But Jesus kept quiet and did not say a word. The high priest asked him another question, “Are you the Messiah, the Son of the glorious God?”
translation_verse: Mɔ Yesu te gyuusu ɔ nɔɔ ya. Botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ saese kegyia ŋon bɔɔse e nyi, “Nyaŋ yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi nyaŋ ke kɔŋ nɛ? Nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ ŋon kaboena nyi baa lese ɔ yele nɛ bu dokoloŋ ŋon?”

english_verse: You heard him claim to be God! What is your decision?” They all agreed he should be put to death.
translation_verse: Ɛ te nyii mmusuo nombia ŋan ɔ be yako yee? Sena yaa ɛ na gyueŋ lee ke man? Te kegyiise baŋ pou too be nɔɔ gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ bu e fɔɔ nyi, “Kaboena nyi baa ko e.”

english_verse: Some of the people started spitting on Jesus. They blindfolded him, hit him with their fists, and said, “Tell us who hit you!” Then the guards took charge of Jesus and beat him. Peter Says He Doesn't Know Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ baale fiasɛɛ bɛɛ tɔ nɔɔloŋ woli e, te be wuu ɔ sia na bɛɛ takee e akɔtase. Te be bɔɔse e nyi, “Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre, wola daa ŋon ɔ be gyɔ neŋ.” Keŋte ba moo e fa kotɔtɔɔtena baŋ te baŋ mɔ ke tuku e. Pita kebɛɛ Yesu Mateo

english_verse: The servant girl saw Peter again and said to the people standing there, “This man is one of them!”
translation_verse: Alebu ŋon ka bese naa Pita botɔɔ nɛ ɔ yako balaŋ baŋ ba seŋɛɛ botɔɔ nɛ nyiaa, “Balee kei mɔ kpuɛ na wɔ.”

english_verse: While Peter was still in the courtyard, a servant girl of the high priest came up
translation_verse: Pita ke kyaa dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man nɛ, Wurubuarɛ saese kegyia ŋon alebu ŋolo kɔŋawɔ botɔɔ.

english_verse: and saw Peter warming himself by the fire. She stared at him and said, “You were with Jesus from Nazareth!”
translation_verse: Ɔ kena Pita kyaa ɔ ne woli boalaŋ nɛ, ɔ kɛɛ e yididi te ɔ yako e nyiaa, “Nyaŋ mɔ n kpuɛ na Yesu Nasarɛte baale ŋon.”

english_verse: “No, I'm not!” Peter replied. A little while later some of the people said to Peter, “You certainly are one of them. You're a Galilean!”
translation_verse: Mɔ Pita besewɔ bɛɛ ke bela. Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, balaŋ baŋ ba seŋɛɛ botɔɔ nɛ besewɔ yako Pita nyiaa, “Ke yɛɛ ampaŋ nyi n kpuɛ na wɔ, nawolo nyi n yɛɛ Galelia baale e.”

english_verse: At once the rooster crowed a second time. Then Peter remembered that Jesus had told him, “Before a rooster crows twice, you will say three times that you don't know me.” So Peter started crying.
translation_verse: Debokenaŋ man te kegyim lɔɔ woya betabolate. Keŋte Pita tɔɔse nombia ŋan Yesu be yako e nɛ nyiaa, “Pɛ kegyim kelɔ woya betabola na, nyaŋ ke bɛɛ betabotooro nyi n ba gyeŋ maŋ ya.” Keŋte ɔ yɛlɛɛwɔ do ɔ ne wii.

english_verse: Early the next morning the chief priests, the nation's leaders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses met together with the whole Jewish council. They tied up Jesus and led him off to Pilate.
translation_verse: Tɛɛ kena nebo nebo fuu nɛ, saese kegyiise na donɔɔ kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala gyaŋeewɔ te be bake Yesu nyeeman. Mena dɔɔ be bake e te ba moo e gyu ka do Pilato ŋon ɔ seŋɛɛ Romantena gyoo dinaa nawɔɔ man nɛ, nyiŋmaa man.

english_verse: But the chief priests told the crowd to ask Pilate to free Barabbas.
translation_verse: Mɔ Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise baŋ feefuwɔ do balaŋ dikpii keŋ nyi, baa yako Pilato nyi waa lese Barabase fa wɔ.

english_verse: Pilate asked, “But what crime has he done?” “Nail him to a cross!” they yelled even louder.
translation_verse: Keŋte Pilato bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Woŋ dukum te ɔ yɛɛwɔ?” Mɔna be gyakaa bo bɛɛ fae yeesa keŋkeŋ nyi, “Gyɔ e nyeelabia sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ”

english_verse: But Jesus did not answer, and Pilate was amazed. The Death Sentence (Matthew
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te nombia ŋan doo gyoo Pilato nɔɔ, nawolo nyi Yesu te yako daale ya. Be kalese Yesu fa yeŋ Mateo

english_verse: They made fun of Jesus and shouted, “Hey, you king of the Jews!”
translation_verse: Na bee kulu e nyiaa, “Gyudatena gyoo, n nyee dɛɛ wolose”

english_verse: Then Pilate questioned him again, “Don't you have anything to say? Don't you hear what crimes they say you have done?”
translation_verse: Keŋte Pilato bɔɔse e nyi, “Be ta mo nombia kɛŋa bɛɛ gyakaa n dɔɔ na, n ba lese n nɔɔse yee?”

english_verse: Pilate wanted to please the crowd, so he set Barabbas free. Then he ordered his soldiers to beat Jesus with a whip and nail him to a cross. Soldiers Make Fun of Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Keŋ Pilato ne gyae mena keŋ be wɔe ke fɛɛ wɔ dɔɔ ɔ lese Barabase fa wɔ, te ɔ yela ɔ yoonɔɔtena tuku Yesu te ɔ moo e fa nyi baa gyu ke gyɔ e nyeelabia sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ. Be kekulu Yesu Mateo

english_verse: On it was a sign that told why he was nailed there. It read, “This is the King of the Jews.”
translation_verse: Keŋte be ŋmarase dukum keŋ be kpa ɔ be yɛɛ nɛ mataa ɔ nyee gbɛɛ nyiaa, “gyudatena gyoo ŋon yaa.”

english_verse: It was about nine o'clock in the morning when they nailed him to the cross.
translation_verse: Gyae ke yɛɛ ŋgba nebo weese debaŋ atoorote man nɛ, te be gyɔɔ Yesu sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ.

english_verse: A Roman army officer was standing in front of Jesus. When the officer saw how Jesus died, he said, “This man really was the Son of God!”
translation_verse: Yoonɔɔtena kegyia ŋon ɔ seŋɛɛ botɔɔ kena Yesu ke fae yeesa kara weesi nɛ, ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Balee kei yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon ampaŋ.”

english_verse: The soldiers led Jesus inside the courtyard of the fortress and called together the rest of the troops.
translation_verse: Keŋte yoonɔɔtena baŋ moo Yesu gyu Pilato dekpaŋalaŋ man botɔɔ keŋ bee di nombia nɛ, te be baake be tebia yoonɔɔtena akaŋ baŋ pou gyaŋee.

english_verse: And at that time there was a prisoner named Barabbas. He and some others had been arrested for murder during a riot.
translation_verse: Mena debaŋ kenaŋ na ba te kyaŋ Barabase tɔ deni. Barabase kei kpuɛ balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ koro seŋ tia gyoo dinaa, te bɔɔ ko balaŋ nɛ man.

english_verse: The crowd now came and asked Pilate to set a prisoner free, just as he usually did.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te balaŋ dikpii keŋ kɔŋa Pilato gyaŋ nyi waa yɛɛ kpene keŋ ɔ ne taŋ yɛɛ debaŋ kamasɛ nɛ fa wɔ.

english_verse: Pilate asked them, “Do you want me to free the king of the Jews?”
translation_verse: Te Pilato bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ ne gyae nyi maa lese Gyudatena gyoo ŋon fa ŋon?”

english_verse: Pilate knew that the chief priests had brought Jesus to him because they were jealous.
translation_verse: Keŋ dɔɔ Pilato be bɔɔse mena yɛna nyi, ɔ gyeŋ nyi saese kegyiise baŋ ne kɔla Yesu dɔɔ te ba lese e fa yeŋ.

english_verse: Then Pilate asked the crowd, “What do you want me to do with this man you say is the king of the Jews?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Pilato bɔɔse wɔ nyi, “Sena te ɛ ne gyae maa yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɛ ne baake e nyi Gyudatena gyoo nɛ?”

english_verse: When the soldiers had finished making fun of Jesus, they took off the purple robe. They put his own clothes back on him and led him off to be nailed to a cross.
translation_verse: Be ke kulu e taŋ nɛ, ba kpee kegba yayam keŋ te ba moo ŋon gbagba wui keŋ do e. Te ba moo e lee be kaa gyɔ e sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ. Ba kegyɔ Yesu ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ Mateo

english_verse: Simon from Cyrene happened to be coming in from a farm, and they forced him to carry Jesus' cross. Simon was the father of Alexander and Rufus. Jesus Is Nailed to a Cross (Matthew
translation_verse: Be ka lee donɔɔ keŋ man bee gyu nɛ, te be gyaŋee na Simɔn ŋon ɔ ba lee Kirene donɔɔ man nɛ na ɔ ne kɔŋ. Simɔn kei bia yɛna Alɛkesanda na Rufuse. Keŋte ba ŋere e nyi waa lɛɛ Yesu ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ seele.

english_verse: The chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses also made fun of Jesus. They said to each other, “He saved others, but he can't save himself.
translation_verse: Mena mɔ te Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ mɔ kulu e. Te be yako dɔŋa nyiaa, “Ɔ ne tale lɛɛ baale nyee, mɔna ɔ be tale lɛɛ ŋon gbagba nyee ya.”

english_verse: Pilate was surprised to hear that Jesus was already dead, and he called in the army officer to find out if Jesus had been dead very long.
translation_verse: Pilato ke nyii nyi Yesu ta yem nɛ, yɛɛ e gyakoloŋ. Mena dɔɔ ɔ baake ɔ yoonɔɔtena kegyia ŋon bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Yesu ŋon wulaa yeŋawɔ?”

english_verse: A man named Joseph from Arimathea was brave enough to ask Pilate for the body of Jesus. Joseph was a highly respected member of the Jewish council, and he was also waiting for God's kingdom to come.
translation_verse: balee ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Gyosɛfo ŋon ɔ ba lee Arimatia donɔɔ man nɛ gyɔɔ ɔ konɔɔ gyu Pilato gyaŋ ka sola e nyi waa mo Yesu woo ŋon fa e ɔ kaa wuu. Gyosɛfo kei yɛɛ donɔɔ kegyia ŋolo e, te ŋon mɔ ne gyeki Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ.

english_verse: The soldiers took Jesus to Golgotha, which means “Place of a Skull.”
translation_verse: Te ba moo Yesu gyu botɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake nyi “Golgata” keŋ nawolo nyi “Nyeepookoo” dɛɛ nɛ.

english_verse: They nailed Jesus to a cross and gambled to see who would get his clothes.
translation_verse: Ba ke gyɔ e sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ nɛ, te be kpɛlɛɛ ɔ kegbase buŋii te be lɔɔ boe ŋe dɔɔ, na baa naa keŋ walaŋ kamasɛ waa nyiŋ.

english_verse: People who passed by said terrible things about Jesus. They shook their heads and shouted, “Ha! So you're the one who claimed you could tear down the temple and build it again in three days.
translation_verse: Na balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ lam botɔɔ nɛ, na tee e na bee kpiisi ba nyeeya yako nyiaa, “Nyaŋ ŋon n kpa n ke tekerii Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ na n ka mo weeya atooro maa ke nɛ,

english_verse: If he is the Messiah, the king of Israel, let him come down from the cross! Then we will see and believe.” The two criminals also said cruel things to Jesus. The Death of Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa ŋon yɛna nyeedoŋ Gbeŋgyoo na Iseraetena gyoo ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ, kenaŋ na waa lɛɛ ɔ wose lee ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ dɔɔ na dɛɛ naa na dɛɛ lɛɛ e di. Te balaŋ bala baŋ bɔɔ gyɔ wɔ sakaa ɔpaŋdaase dɔɔ kpu na e nɛ mɔ tee e mena. Yesu yeŋ Mateo

english_verse: About noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until around three o'clock.
translation_verse: Weese ka seŋ dɛɛ nɛ, ditiŋtɛɛ kaa lɛɛ tɛɛle keŋ dɔɔ pou kelii ɔ ke gɛɛse.

english_verse: Then about that time Jesus shouted, “Eloi, Eloi, lema sabachthani?” which means, “My God, my God, why have you deserted me?”
translation_verse: Te weese ke gɛɛse kenaŋ nɛ Yesu fae yeesa keŋkeŋ nyiaa, “Eli, Eli, lama sabatani?” Keŋ nawolo nyi, “Me Wurubuarɛ, Me Wurubuarɛ, weera te n lese n sia yela maŋ?”

english_verse: Some of the people standing there heard Jesus and said, “He is calling for Elijah.”
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ ba seŋɛɛ botɔɔ ke nyii mena nɛ be yakowɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ nyii, ɔ ne baake Elaegya.”

english_verse: One of them ran and grabbed a sponge. After he had soaked it in wine, he put it on a stick and held it up to Jesus. He said, “Let's wait and see if Elijah will come and take him down!”
translation_verse: Debokenaŋ man te be man ŋolo yeuwɔ ka mo kolo ŋgba kokyaa keŋ gyae ke tale lɛɛ loŋ nɛ do soloŋ denyaŋesa daale man, te ɔ moo ke tooso na daŋ te ɔ gyinaa ke adido fa Yesu nyi waa nyɔɔ. Keŋte ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ seŋ dɛɛ kɛɛ nyi Elaegya waa kɔŋ kaa lese e lee ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ dɔɔ.”

english_verse: It was now the evening before the Sabbath, and the Jewish people were getting ready for that sacred day.
translation_verse: Na wee kenaŋ yɛɛ wee keŋ Gyudatena na mo desina ba wose fa ba kefɛɛfowee keŋ kedi. Ke lii balinɔɔ nɛ,

english_verse: Joseph bought a linen cloth and took the body down from the cross. He had it wrapped in the cloth, and he put it in a tomb that had been cut into solid rock. Then he rolled a big stone against the entrance to the tomb.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Gyosɛfo gyuuwɔ ke lɛɛ akaralɛɛ te ɔ lese woo ŋon lee ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ dɔɔ te ɔ moo akaralɛɛ keŋ milii e. Keŋte ɔ moo e ke wuu yebɔɔ keŋ bɔɔ kare boe daale man nɛ man. Te ɔ biliŋi boe dinaa daale tɔ yebɔɔ keŋ nɔɔ.

english_verse: Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joseph were watching and saw where the body was placed.
translation_verse: Na Mɛɛre ŋon ɔ ba lee Magedala nɛ, na Gyose ɔ naa Mɛɛre seŋɛɛ botɔɔ. Mena dɔɔ be naa botɔɔ keŋ bɔɔ wuu e nɛ.

english_verse: Later, Jesus appeared in another form to two disciples, as they were on their way out of the city.
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, Yesu lese ɔ wose dinɔɔ wɔle daale man wola ɔ kaseela bala baale debaŋ keŋ bee gyu donɔɔ daale man nɛ.

english_verse: Then the disciples left and preached everywhere. The Lord was with them, and the miracles they worked proved that their message was true. ANOTHER OLD ENDING TO MARK'S GOSPEL
translation_verse: Yesu kaseela baŋ ka lee botɔɔ nɛ, be gyuuwɔ tɛɛle kei pou dɔɔ kekolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan. Te de Gbeŋgyoo seŋawɔ be wɔle te be yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia, na kawola nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan yɛɛ ampaŋ.

english_verse: Very early on Sunday morning, just as the sun was coming up, they went to the tomb.
translation_verse: Kosida nebo nebo weese ke bɛɛ nɛ, ala baŋ korowɔ bee gyu yebɔɔ keŋ tɛɛ.

english_verse: On their way, they were asking one another, “Who will roll the stone away from the entrance for us?”
translation_verse: Be kee gyu na bɛɛ bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, “Amɔte ne gyae la waa biliŋi boe keŋ lee yebɔɔ keŋ nɔɔman fa daa?”

english_verse: But when they looked, they saw that the stone had already been rolled away. And it was a huge stone!
translation_verse: Mɔ be ke gyina be sia kɛɛ na, be naawɔ nyi be te biliŋi boe dinaa dinaa keŋ lee yebɔɔ keŋ nɔɔman.

english_verse: The women went into the tomb, and on the right side they saw a young man in a white robe sitting there. They were alarmed.
translation_verse: Be ka mo baa gyoo yebɔɔ keŋ man nɛ, te be naa tobaale ŋolo na ɔ doona kegba wolom wurikyim na ɔ baŋɛɛ dunoluŋ dɔɔ te be ŋeŋawɔ.

english_verse: Now go and tell his disciples, and especially Peter, that he will go ahead of you to Galilee. You will see him there, just as he told you.”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛ gyu ke yako ɔ kaseela baŋ na Pita nyiaa, ‘Ɔ te kyaŋ ŋon gbɛɛ ɔ ne gyu Galelia donɔɔ man. Botɔɔ te ɛ kena e ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be yako ŋon nɛ.’ ”

english_verse: They will handle snakes and will drink poison and not be hurt. They will also heal sick people by placing their hands on them. Jesus Returns to Heaven (Luke
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa be gate doma na be nyiŋmaase yaa be nyɔɔ teekum daale koraŋ na, be gyae ke yɛɛ wɔ kolo ya. Baa mo be nyiŋmaase gyakaa kaweesetena dɔɔ, na ba wose ke yɛɛ wɔ doŋ.”

english_verse: But when these disciples told what had happened, the others would not believe either. What Jesus' Followers Must Do (Matthew
translation_verse: Keŋte baŋ mɔ besewɔ kɔŋ kaa yako be tebia akaŋ baŋ. Mɔ baŋ mɔ te lɛɛ wɔ di ya. Yesu kalese ɔ wose wola ɔ kaseela kufu daale baŋ Mateo

english_verse: Anyone who believes me and is baptized will be saved. But anyone who refuses to believe me will be condemned.
translation_verse: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ nombia ŋan di te be sɔ e Wurubuarɛ loŋ na, waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ. Mɔ walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ te lɛɛ nombia ŋan di ya nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa bu e fɔɔ.

english_verse: After the Lord Jesus had said these things to the disciples, he was taken back up to heaven where he sat down at the right side of God.
translation_verse: De Gbeŋgyoo Yesu kekolosi fa wɔ taŋ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ moo e gyu adido te ɔ ke kyaawɔ Wurubuarɛ dunoluŋ dɔɔ.

english_verse: Afterwards, Jesus appeared to his eleven disciples as they were eating. He scolded them because they were too stubborn to believe the ones who had seen him after he had been raised to life.
translation_verse: Kɛŋa pou wɔle nɛ, ɔ kaseela kufu daale baŋ yilaawɔ bee dii te Yesu besewɔ kaa lese ɔ wose wola wɔ. Keŋte ɔ gyeŋa baŋ do wɔ lee mena keŋ balaŋ be kɔŋ kaa yako wɔ nyi ɔ te fom, te ba lɛɛ wɔ di ya nɛ dɔɔ.

english_verse: Then he told them: Go and preach the good news to everyone in the world.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ gyu tɛɛle kei pou dɔɔ na ɛ kaa kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan fa balaŋ pou.

english_verse: Everyone who believes me will be able to do wonderful things. By using my name they will force out demons, and they will speak new languages.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ baa lɛɛ maŋ di nɛ, baa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia kɛŋa. Baa gberaa feliŋkumɛɛ lee balaŋ man lee ma yele man, na baa kolosi deise wɔlɛɛ kpookpoo ŋan bɔɔ gyeŋ ya.

english_verse: Many people have tried to tell the story of what God has done among us.
translation_verse: Kegyia kpaakpaa Teyofilose, balaŋ burum te yɛɛ kakyeŋ ŋmarase nombia ŋan be kɔŋ de gyaŋ kɛbo lee Yesu Kristo wose man nɛ see.

english_verse: They wrote what we had been told by the ones who were there in the beginning and saw what happened.
translation_verse: Nombia ŋan bɔɔ ŋmarase nɛ, mena nombia ŋenaŋ ke te baŋ bɔɔ mo be sia naa ŋa leenaŋ kewalaŋ, te bɔɔ kolosi de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo wose man nombia fa daa nɛ yakowɔ.

english_verse: So I made a careful study of everything and then decided to write and tell you exactly what took place. Honorable Theophilus,
translation_verse: Kegyia Teyofilose, lee mena dɔɔ maŋ gbagba gyoo nombia kɛŋa man nideli leenaŋ ŋa kewalaŋ keŋ na, me naawɔ nyi kaboena nyi maa tɔlɛɛ ŋa kekɔŋ bii dokoloŋ dokoloŋ fa neŋ,

english_verse: I have done this to let you know the truth about what you have heard. An Angel Tells about the Birth of John
translation_verse: na n ke nyiŋ gyeŋ nyi nombia ŋan pou bɔɔ wola neŋ nɛ yɛɛ ampaŋ. Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon kalola wose man nombia

english_verse: When Herod was king of Judea, there was a priest by the name of Zechariah from the priestly group of Abijah. His wife Elizabeth was from the family of Aaron.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ gyoo Herode ne di gyoori Gyudia tɛɛle man nɛ, na Wurubuarɛ sae ŋolo kyaa keŋ bɛɛ baake e nyi Sakaria. Na ɔ kpuɛ Abigya saere dikpii man, te ɔ wɛɛle Elisabɛte mɔ lee Wurubuarɛ sae Aarɔn duuluŋ man.

english_verse: Both of them were good people and pleased the Lord God by obeying all that he had commanded.
translation_verse: Sakaria na ɔ wɛɛle gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman. Te bee di de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ mmaraase na ɔ nombia ŋan pou dɔɔ nideli keŋ ba wose man be dana nombiikum daale ya.

english_verse: But they did not have children. Elizabeth could not have any, and both Zechariah and Elizabeth were already old.
translation_verse: Mɔna bɔɔ taŋ bu ya nawolo nyi Elisabɛte yɛɛ ɔlamanbo e, te ŋon na ɔ baale Sakaria mɔ pou te ŋmɛɛ.

english_verse: One day Zechariah's group of priests were on duty, and he was serving God as a priest.
translation_verse: Wee daale nɛ, kaa liiwɔ Sakaria saese dikpii keŋ dɔɔ nyi baa gyu Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man ke fa Wurubuarɛ kedi.

english_verse: According to the custom of the priests, he had been chosen to go into the Lord's temple that day and to burn incense,
translation_verse: Be lɔɔ boe ŋgba mena keŋ ba amanberɛ na wolo nɛ, te yalawɔ Sakaria dɔɔ nyi waa gyu Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man ɔ kaa too oliifi.

english_verse: All at once an angel from the Lord appeared to Zechariah at the right side of the altar.
translation_verse: Sakaria kɛɛ too oliifi keŋ nɛ, botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ kpilale kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ kaa seŋ kedeesa boe keŋ ɔ na too oliifi ke dɔɔ nɛ dunoluŋ dɔɔ.

english_verse: Zechariah was confused and afraid when he saw the angel.
translation_verse: Sakaria kena e nɛ, ɔ wɔe lɔɔ e te gyakoloŋ gyoo e.

english_verse: But the angel told him: Don't be afraid, Zechariah! God has heard your prayers. Your wife Elizabeth will have a son, and you must name him John.
translation_verse: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon yako e nyiaa, “Sakaria na yee ya, Wurubuarɛ te nyii n kefane mena dɔɔ n wɛɛle Elisabɛte waa lola bu baale fa neŋ, na n ka do e yele nyi Gyɔn.

english_verse: His birth will make you very happy, and many people will be glad.
translation_verse: Ba lola bu ŋon na, gyae ke yɛɛ neŋ gyoŋ nideli te balaŋ burum mɔ sia gyae ka gye ɔ dɔɔ,

english_verse: Your son will be a great servant of the Lord. He must never drink wine or beer, and the power of the Holy Spirit will be with him from the time he is born.
translation_verse: nawolo nyi bu ŋon waa yɛɛ walaŋ dinaa Wurubuarɛ siaman, mena dɔɔ ɔ na mo soloŋ na soloŋ daa ɔ nɔɔ ya. Wurubuarɛ waa mo ɔ feliŋ do ɔ man leenaŋ ɔ naa tɔɔman pɛna baa lola e.

english_verse: John will lead many people in Israel to turn back to the Lord their God.
translation_verse: Te waa kyɛɛkee Iseraetena balaŋ burum agyueŋ kɔŋ be Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ.

english_verse: He will go ahead of the Lord with the same power and spirit that Elijah had. And because of John, parents will be more thoughtful of their children. And people who now disobey God will begin to think as they ought to. This is how John will get people ready for the Lord.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ waa mo ɔ feliŋ do ɔ man, na waa fa e doŋ ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be fa ɔ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Elia nɛ. Waa ta de Gbeŋgyoo ŋon siaman na waa desina kyaɔ na be bia nsana, te balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ ne bu Wurubuarɛ ya nɛ, waa besena wɔ kɔŋ gbɛɛ kpaakpaate keŋ dɔɔ na waa desina de Gbeŋgyoo balaŋ see e.”

english_verse: Zechariah said to the angel, “How will I know this is going to happen? My wife and I are both very old.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Sakaria bɔɔse Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon nyiaa, “Sena te maa yɛɛ na maa naa nyi nombia kɛŋa yɛɛ ampaŋ? Nawolo nyi daa na me wɛɛle pou te ŋmɛɛ.”

english_verse: The angel answered, “I am Gabriel, God's servant, and I was sent to tell you this good news.
translation_verse: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Maŋ yɛna Gaberɛle, Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon ma seŋɛɛ ɔ siaman debaŋ kamasɛ. Ŋon kpila na maŋ nyi me kaa yako neŋ mena nombia kpaakpaa kɛŋa.

english_verse: You have not believed what I have said. So you will not be able to say a thing until all this happens. But everything will take place when it is supposed to.”
translation_verse: Nombia ŋan mɔɔ yako neŋ nɛ, gyae kekɔŋ ŋgba mena keŋ mɔɔ yako see nɛ. Mɔna keŋ n te lɛɛ me nombia kɛŋa di ya dɔɔ nɛ, n be gyae n ke tale kolosi ya kaboena nombia ŋan mɛɛ yako neŋ nɛ kekɔŋ ampaŋ debaŋ keŋ mɔɔ see neŋ nɛ.”

english_verse: The crowd was waiting for Zechariah and kept wondering why he was staying in the temple so long.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ pou man na, balaŋ baŋ kyaa debɔɔ man bɛɛ daa Sakaria, na bɛɛ gyueŋ kpene keŋ dɔɔ ɔ be dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man mena.

english_verse: When he did come out, he could not speak, and they knew he had seen a vision. He motioned to them with his hands, but did not say a thing.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ ɔ ba lee kɔŋ balaŋ baŋ gyaŋ nɛ, ɔ te tale kolosi na wɔ ya, mena dɔɔ ba gyeŋawɔ nyi ɔ te naa kalesewola daale Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man. Ɔ nyiŋmaase te ɔ moowɔ kolosi na wɔ, nawolo nyi ɔ ta bese omumuu.

english_verse: When Zechariah's time of service in the temple was over, he went home.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ ɔ saere tom keŋ taŋ nɛ, te ɔ besewɔ gyu ɔ dɛɛ.

english_verse: Soon after this, his wife was expecting a baby, and for five months she did not leave the house. She said to herself,
translation_verse: Nombia kɛŋa kekɔŋ wɔle nɛ, te kyare ya te ɔ wɛɛle Elisabɛte kyaŋa fulu, te ɔ weese ɔ wose gyaalaŋna banoŋ keŋ ɔ bɛɛ lee debɔɔ ya.

english_verse: “What the Lord has done for me will keep people from looking down on me.” An Angel Tells about the Birth of Jesus
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ te wii me waraŋase ŋgba ɔ ba lese desɛŋ dinaa kei lee me dɔɔ balaŋ siaman nɛ.” Yesu kalola dekpeŋkpeŋ kegyɔ

english_verse: One month later God sent the angel Gabriel to the town of Nazareth in Galilee
translation_verse: Elisabɛte fulu keŋ ke di gyaalaŋna looro nɛ, Wurubuarɛ kpila ɔ kpilale Gaberɛle gyu Nasarɛte keŋ kyaa Galelia tɛɛle dɔɔ nɛ.

english_verse: The angel greeted Mary and said, “You are truly blessed! The Lord is with you.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon gyuuwɔ ke yako e nyiaa, “Mɛɛre nyaŋ na nyeebam, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ kpuɛ na neŋ, te ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa n dɔɔ nideli.”

english_verse: Mary was confused by the angel's words and wondered what they meant.
translation_verse: Mɛɛre kenyii nɔɔwoya kɛŋa nɛ, ɔ wose gbirisi e nawolo nyi ɔ te nyii nombia ŋan man nideli ya.

english_verse: Then the angel told Mary, “Don't be afraid! God is pleased with you,
translation_verse: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon yako e nyiaa, “Mɛɛre na yee ya, Wurubuarɛ ta mo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele neŋ.

english_verse: and you will have a son. His name will be Jesus.
translation_verse: Nyaŋ ke kyaŋ fulu na n ka lola bu baale na n ka do e yele nyi Yesu.

english_verse: He will be great and will be called the Son of God Most High. The Lord God will make him king, as his ancestor David was.
translation_verse: Waa yɛɛ walaŋ dinaa Wurubuarɛ siaman, na baa baake e nyi Wurubuarɛ Kpatakpere ŋon bu. De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ waa yela waa di gyoori ŋgba ɔ naana Defidi nɛ.

english_verse: He will rule the people of Israel forever, and his kingdom will never end.”
translation_verse: Waa di gyoori Gyekɔpo duuluŋ man balaŋ dɔɔ kekpaakekpaa, te ɔ gyoori kenaŋ betaŋ ɔto ya.”

english_verse: Mary asked the angel, “How can this happen? I am not even married!”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Mɛɛre bɔɔse Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon nyiaa, “Sena te nombia kɛŋa gyae ke tale kɔŋ mena, debaŋ keŋ mɔɔ gyeŋ baale na baale ya?”

english_verse: Your relative Elizabeth is also going to have a son, even though she is old. No one thought she could ever have a baby, but in three months she will have a son.
translation_verse: Kɛɛ! N loŋto Elisabɛte ŋon be kpa ɔ yɛɛ ɔlamanbo e te ɔ be ŋmɛɛ koraŋ mɔ nɛ, te kyaŋ fulu gyaalaŋna looro yaa.

english_verse: Mary said, “I am the Lord's servant! Let it happen as you have said.” And the angel left her. Mary Visits Elizabeth
translation_verse: Keŋte Mɛɛre yakowɔ nyiaa, “Ma nyee tesɛɛ bo na Wurubuarɛ. Ŋan pou n be yako maŋ nɛ dɛɛkɔŋ mena.” Keŋte Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon laŋawɔ yela e. Mɛɛre kegyu Elisabɛte gyaŋ

english_verse: She went into Zechariah's home, where she greeted Elizabeth.
translation_verse: Ɔ ka gyoo donɔɔ keŋ man nɛ, ɔ gyuuwɔ Sakaria dɛɛ te ɔ doo Elisabɛte nɔɔ.

english_verse: When Elizabeth heard Mary's greeting, her baby moved within her. The Holy Spirit came upon Elizabeth.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ Elisabɛte be nyii Mɛɛre nɔɔ kado keŋ nɛ, ɔ bu ŋon wosi ɔ wose ɔ tɔɔman, te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon kaa gyoowɔ Elisabɛte man nideli.

english_verse: Then in a loud voice she said to Mary: God has blessed you more than any other woman! He has also blessed the child you will have.
translation_verse: Keŋte Elisabɛte nyaŋeewɔ fae yeesa nyiaa, “Nyaŋ yɛna alo ŋon Wurubuarɛ ba mo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa n dɔɔ nideli ala pou man, te ɔ ta mo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa n bu ŋon nyaŋ ka lola nɛ dɔɔ.”

english_verse: Why should the mother of my Lord come to me?
translation_verse: Woŋ nombia dinaana yaa Wurubuarɛ yɛɛwɔ fa maŋ nɛ, nyi me Gbeŋgyoo ɔ naa waa kɔŋ kaa do maŋ nɔɔ?

english_verse: As soon as I heard your greeting, my baby became happy and moved within me.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi me ka betaa nyii n nɔɔ kado pɛ, te me bu ŋon ɔ doo me tɔɔman nɛ nyaŋeewɔ wosi ɔ wose.

english_verse: The Lord has blessed you because you believed that he will keep his promise. Mary's Song of Praise
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ te hiraa neŋ, ŋgba n be lɛɛdi nyi de Gbeŋgyoo nombia ŋan ɔ be yako nɛ, gyae kekɔŋ mena fa neŋ nɛ.

english_verse: Mary said: With all my heart I praise the Lord,
translation_verse: Keŋte Mɛɛre yakowɔ nyiaa, “Mɛɛ lese de Gbeŋgyoo yele lee me konɔɔ man.

english_verse: and I am glad because of God my Savior.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ yɛna ma nyeelɛɛre, mena dɔɔ te mɛɛ nyaŋee,

english_verse: God All-Powerful has done great things for me, and his name is holy.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ Kpatakpere ŋon te yɛɛ nombia dinaana fa maŋ. Ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ ŋon ayimɛɛ be doo ɔ man ya.

english_verse: He always shows mercy to everyone who worships him.
translation_verse: Deniwalaŋ duuluŋ kamasɛ man nɛ, ɔ ne wii baŋ pou bee yee e nɛ waraŋase.

english_verse: God drags strong rulers from their thrones and puts humble people in places of power.
translation_verse: Ɔ te yɔkɔse gyooneŋ dinaana tɛɛle man, te ɔ gyinaa balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ yɔkɔse ba wose nɛ adido.

english_verse: God gives the hungry good things to eat, and sends the rich away with nothing.
translation_verse: Ɔ te yeli baŋ tanam na ko wɔ nɛ te nyiŋ abɔɔ kpaakpaa, te ɔ yela baŋ be dana kɔba nɛ laŋawɔ nyiŋmaase kufu nawɔɔ kufu.

english_verse: God helps his servant Israel and is always merciful to his people.
translation_verse: Ɔ te di ntam keŋ ɔ be kaŋ fa de naanaɔ nɛ dɔɔ, te ɔ kɔŋawɔ kaa kyɔ ɔ tewulɛɛ Iseraetena,

english_verse: The Lord made this promise to our ancestors, to Abraham and his family forever!
translation_verse: ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be yako see nyi, waa wii Aberaham na ɔ duuluŋ man balaŋ pou waraŋase kekpaakekpaa nɛ.”

english_verse: Mary stayed with Elizabeth about three months. Then she went back home. The Birth of John the Baptist
translation_verse: Mɛɛre kyaawɔ Elisabɛte gyaŋ ŋgba gyaalaŋna batooro nɛ, te ɔ besewɔ gyu ɔ dɛɛ. Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre kalola

english_verse: her neighbors and relatives heard how kind the Lord had been to her, and they too were glad.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ be kyaa benaa na e, na ɔ loŋtona nyii mena keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo be wii ɔ waraŋase nideli nɛ, be popou nyaŋeewɔ kpu na e.

english_verse: Eight days later they did for the child what the Law of Moses commands. They were going to name him Zechariah, after his father.
translation_verse: Bu ŋon kedi weeya gyanaara nɛ, be kɔŋawɔ be kaa kara ɔ baala wose na baa mo ɔ kya Sakaria yele do e,

english_verse: But Elizabeth said, “No! His name is John.”
translation_verse: mɔna ɔ naa bɛɛwɔ te ɔ kpa “Baa baake e nyi Gyɔn.”

english_verse: The people argued, “No one in your family has ever been named John.”
translation_verse: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ yako e nyiaa, “Yele kei te yɛɛ n dekpaŋalaŋ man yele ya.”

english_verse: So they motioned to Zechariah to find out what he wanted to name his son.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ba moo be nyiŋmaase kolosi na ɔ kya bɔɔse e yele keŋ ɔ ne gyae nyi baa mo do ɔ bu ŋon.

english_verse: Zechariah asked for a writing tablet. Then he wrote, “His name is John.” Everyone was amazed.
translation_verse: Keŋte Sakaria yako wɔ nyiaa baa fa e kolo waa ŋmarase ke dɔɔ, te ɔ ŋmarasewɔ nyi, “Ɔ yele yɛna Gyɔn.” Nombii kei yɛɛ be popou gyakoloŋ.

english_verse: At once, Zechariah started speaking and praising God.
translation_verse: Debokenaŋ man te Sakaria nɔɔ gyuusuwɔ te ɔ fiasɛɛ kolosi lese Wurubuarɛ yele.

english_verse: All the neighbors were frightened because of what had happened, and everywhere in the hill country people kept talking about these things.
translation_verse: Keŋte gyakoloŋ gyoo be tebia baŋ pou, te nombia ŋenaŋ yaaseewɔ gyu Gyudia tɛɛle man donɔɔse ŋan ŋe kyaa bulase dɔɔ nɛ pou man.

english_verse: Everyone who heard about this wondered what this child would grow up to be. They knew the Lord was with him. Zechariah Praises the Lord
translation_verse: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ be nyii nombii kei nɛ bɔɔse ɔ wose nyiaa, “Walaŋ woŋti dinɔɔ yaa bu kei waa kaa yɛɛ baa?” Nawolo nyi be naawɔ nyi, de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ nyiŋmaa gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ nideli. Sakaria ke kpee nɔɔneŋ lese Wurubuarɛ yele

english_verse: The Holy Spirit came upon Zechariah, and he began to speak:
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ moo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do Gyɔn ɔ kya Sakaria man, te ɔ kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia see nyiaa,

english_verse: Praise the Lord, the God of Israel! He has come to save his people.
translation_verse: “De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ, dɛɛ lese n yele nawolo nyi n te kɔŋ kaa lɛɛ n balaŋ Iseraetena nyee.

english_verse: Our God has given us a mighty Savior from the family of David his servant.
translation_verse: N te fa daa nyeelɛɛre dinaa ŋolo, ŋon ɔ ba lee n tobaale Defidi duuluŋ man.

english_verse: Long ago the Lord promised by the words of his holy prophets
translation_verse: Ŋgba mena keŋ n be tɛɛ nya akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ ayimɛɛ be doo be man ya nɛ dɔɔ kolosi see nɛ nyiaa,

english_verse: to save us from our enemies and from everyone who hates us.
translation_verse: waa lɛɛ daa lee de kɔlala, na baŋ bɔɔ ne naa de sia laŋ ya nɛ nyiŋmaa man.

english_verse: God said he would be kind to our people and keep his sacred promise.
translation_verse: N yakowɔ nyi nyaŋ ke wii de naanaɔ waraŋase, na n ke tɔɔse nɔɔkebakesee keŋ ɛmɛɛ na wɔ be bake see nɛ.

english_verse: that he would rescue us from our enemies. Then we could serve him without fear,
translation_verse: n ka lese daa lee de kɔlala nyiŋmaase man, na ke yela dɛɛ tale gyɔ de konɔɔ som neŋ keŋ dɔɔ yee gyakoloŋ ya,

english_verse: You, my son, will be called the prophet of God Most High. You will go ahead of the Lord to get everything ready for him.
translation_verse: Sakaria ke yako kɛŋa pou taŋ nɛ te ɔ kolosiwɔ fa bu ŋon nyiaa, nɛnɛɛ nyaŋ me bu Gyɔn, baa baake neŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ Kpatakpere ŋon dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre, nawolo nyi nyaŋ keta de Gbeŋgyoo ŋon siaman na n ke desina ɔ gbɛɛneŋ fa e.

english_verse: You will tell his people that they can be saved when their sins are forgiven.
translation_verse: Na n ke yela ɔ balaŋ ka gyeŋ nyi ba sola Wurubuarɛ te ɔ mo be nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ na, baa nyiŋ nyeekelɛɛ.

english_verse: God's love and kindness will shine upon us like the sun that rises in the sky.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ waraŋasete e, te ɔ te kpila nyeelɛɛre ŋon nyi waa kɔŋ kaa lɛɛ da nyee. Ɔ kekɔŋ gyae ke yɛɛ ŋgba keŋmaŋee nɛ,

english_verse: On us who live in the dark shadow of death this light will shine to guide us into a life of peace.
translation_verse: keŋmaŋee kei gyae ka lee adido kɔŋ baŋ pou be kyaa ditiŋtɛɛ man na yeŋ gbɛɛneŋ man nɛ dɔɔ, na kawola daa gbɛɛ kpaakpaa keŋ dɛɛ ta wosefɛɛreŋ man.”

english_verse: As John grew up, God's Spirit gave him great power. John lived in the desert until the time he was sent to the people of Israel.
translation_verse: Keŋte bu ŋon beewɔ te de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ moo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do ɔ man te ɔ nyiŋa doŋ. Ɔ kyaawɔ depampaa dinaa man kaboena wee keŋ ɔ ba lese ɔ wose debɔɔ wola Iseraetena nɛ liiwɔ.

english_verse: About that time Emperor Augustus gave orders for the names of all the people to be listed in record books.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ man te Roman gyoo dinaa ŋon bɛɛ baake e Agustuse nɛ, faa mmaraa nyi baa kala balaŋ baŋ pou be kyaa ɔ tɛɛle man nɛ.

english_verse: At this time a man named Simeon was living in Jerusalem. Simeon was a good man. He loved God and was waiting for him to save the people of Israel. God's Spirit came to him
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ na balee ŋolo kyaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man keŋ ɔ yele yɛna Simeɔn. Ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman te ɔ ne yee Wurubuarɛ. Wurubuarɛ feliŋ doo ɔ man te ɔ ne daa wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa kaa lɛɛ Iseraetena nyee nɛ.

english_verse: These first records were made when Quirinius was governor of Syria.
translation_verse: Kei yɛna balaŋ kekala gyaŋgbate, debaŋ keŋ Kireniuse kyaa Romantena gyoo dinaa nawɔɔ man Siria tɛɛle man nɛ.

english_verse: Everyone had to go to their own hometown to be listed.
translation_verse: Lee mmaraa keŋ gyoo ŋon be faa dɔɔ, walaŋ kamasɛ gyuu ɔ dɛɛ ɔ kaa ŋmarase ɔ yele.

english_verse: So Joseph had to leave Nazareth in Galilee and go to Bethlehem in Judea. Long ago Bethlehem had been King David's hometown, and Joseph went there because he was from David's family.
translation_verse: Keŋte Gyosɛfo mɔ lee Nasarɛte keŋ kyaa Galelia tɛɛle man nɛ, gyu Bɛtelɛhɛm keŋ ke kyaa Gyudia tɛɛle man nɛ. Bɛtelɛhɛm donɔɔ kei man te ba lola gyoo Defidi. Gyosɛfo gyuu botɔɔ nawolo nyi ɔ lee gyoo Defidi duuluŋ man.

english_verse: Mary was engaged to Joseph and traveled with him to Bethlehem. She was soon going to have a baby,
translation_verse: Ŋon na Mɛɛre ŋon, ŋon na e be yako see nyi baa yala dɔŋa nɛ, gyuu la be kaa ŋmarase ba yela. Debaŋ kenaŋ man na Mɛɛre dana fulu.

english_verse: and while they were there,
translation_verse: Ba kekyaa Bɛtelɛhɛm botɔɔ nɛ te kadoŋee kyaŋa Mɛɛre.

english_verse: she gave birth to her first-born son. She dressed him in baby clothes and laid him on a bed of hay, because there was no room for them in the inn. The Shepherds
translation_verse: Keŋ be te nyiŋ deni ŋɔɔla dasoe keŋ man ya dɔɔ, be gyue ka gyoo wonembia dedoi man. Botɔɔ te ɔ lola ɔ bu baale gyaŋgbate te ɔ mo kakyeŋgbelaase milii e mo e doosi adakaa keŋ wonembia ne dii ke man nɛ man. Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ kekolosi na namensekɛɛlera baale

english_verse: That night in the fields near Bethlehem some shepherds were guarding their sheep.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ man na, namensekɛɛlera baale kyaa ŋale man benaa na botɔɔ yenaŋ bee gyee be namense nelim kenaŋ.

english_verse: All at once an angel came down to them from the Lord, and the brightness of the Lord's glory flashed around them. The shepherds were frightened.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋolo kaa bɛɛwɔ lee be dɔɔ te de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoorobiiri keŋ ŋalakeewɔ kilisi wɔ, keŋte gyakoloŋ gyoo wɔ.

english_verse: But the angel said, “Don't be afraid! I have good news for you, which will make everyone happy.
translation_verse: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ naa yee ya! Ɛ kɛɛ! Me ta mo nombia kpaakpaa ŋaale mɛɛ kɔna ŋon. Nombia kɛŋa gyae ke yɛɛ balaŋ pou gyoŋ.

english_verse: This very day in King David's hometown a Savior was born for you. He is Christ the Lord.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi gyɛŋ wee kei nɛ, be ta lola ɛ nyeelɛɛre fa ŋon gyoo Defidi donɔɔ Bɛtelɛhɛm man. Ŋon yɛna de Gbeŋgyoo Kristo, .

english_verse: You will know who he is, because you will find him dressed in baby clothes and lying on a bed of hay.”
translation_verse: Kpene keŋ gyae kawola nyi nombia ŋan mɛɛ yako nɛ yɛɛ ampaŋ yɛna nyi, ɛ kena kyokyoo ŋon doo adakaa keŋ wonembia ne dii ke man nɛ man, na be ta mo kakyeŋgbelaase milii e.”

english_verse: Suddenly many other angels came down from heaven and joined in praising God. They said:
translation_verse: Debokenaŋ man te Wurubuarɛ kpilala burum baale kɔŋawɔ kaa kpu na kpilale gyaŋgbate ŋon, te be kpee nɔɔneŋ lese Wurubuarɛ yele nyiaa,

english_verse: “Praise God in heaven! Peace on earth to everyone who pleases God.”
translation_verse: “Wurubuarɛ ŋon n kyaa adido, n gyoorobiiri kaboena neŋ. N wosefɛɛreŋ dɛɛkɔŋ balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ gyoo n sia tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ man.”

english_verse: After the angels had left and gone back to heaven, the shepherds said to each other, “Let's go to Bethlehem and see what the Lord has told us about.”
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ ka bese lee be gyaŋ gyu adido nɛ, namensekɛɛlera baŋ yako dɔŋa nyiaa, “Ɛ fa dee gyu Bɛtelɛhɛm, na de kaa kɛɛ kpene keŋ be kɔŋ te de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ ba lese wola daa nɛ.”

english_verse: They hurried off and found Mary and Joseph, and they saw the baby lying on a bed of hay.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ be yɛɛwɔ bileŋ te be gyuuwɔ te ba ke naa Mɛɛre na Gyosɛfo na kyokyoo ŋon, na ɔ doo adakaa keŋ wonembia ne dii ke man nɛ man.

english_verse: When the shepherds saw Jesus, they told his parents what the angel had said about him.
translation_verse: Namensekɛɛlera baŋ kena kyokyoo ŋon nɛ, be yako wɔ mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon be kolosi kyokyoo ŋon wose man nombia fa wɔ nɛ.

english_verse: But Mary kept thinking about all this and wondering what it meant.
translation_verse: Mɔna Mɛɛre kyaŋa nombia kɛŋa pou do ɔ konɔɔ man, na ɔ na gyueŋ ŋe man nideli.

english_verse: As the shepherds returned to their sheep, they were praising God and saying wonderful things about him. Everything they had seen and heard was just as the angel had said.
translation_verse: Namensekɛɛlera baŋ besewɔ laŋ, na bee kpee nɔɔneŋ lese Wurubuarɛ yele lee ŋan pou bɔɔ nyii te bɔɔ naa nɛ dɔɔ, nawolo nyi nombia ŋan pou bɔɔ nyii te bɔɔ naa nɛ, kyaawɔ na ŋan Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon be yako wɔ nɛ. Be kado Yesu yele

english_verse: Eight days later Jesus' parents did for him what the Law of Moses commands. And they named him Jesus, just as the angel had told Mary when he promised she would have a baby. Simeon Praises the Lord
translation_verse: Weeya gyanaarate keŋ kelii nyi baa kara bu ŋon baala wose nɛ, ba doo e yele nyi Yesu. Mena yele kei te Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon yakowɔ nyi baa baake e pɛte Mɛɛre kyaŋa ɔ fulu. Gyosɛfo na Mɛɛre kamo Yesu gyu Gyerusalɛm

english_verse: The time came for Mary and Joseph to do what the Law of Moses says a mother is supposed to do after her baby is born. They took Jesus to the temple in Jerusalem and presented him to the Lord,
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ ba ayimɛɛ kegberaa weeya ŋan be gyaŋee ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan ɔ be yeli Mosesi be ŋmarase see nawolo nɛ, Gyosɛfo na Mɛɛre moo Yesu gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man be kaa do de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ nyiŋmaa man,

english_verse: The Law of the Lord also says parents have to offer a sacrifice, giving at least a pair of doves or two young pigeons. So that is what Mary and Joseph did.
translation_verse: Keŋte Gyosɛfo na Mɛɛre gyuuwɔ ke deesi fa Wurubuarɛ ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan ɔ be yeli Mosesi be ŋmarase see ne yako nɛ nyiaa, “Baa mo donɔɔ man yaa ŋale man awuluŋna bala deesi nɛ.”

english_verse: and told him that he would not die until he had seen Christ the Lord.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon lese la wola e nyi, waa naa nyeelɛɛre ŋon de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ pɛna ŋon Simeɔn ka yeŋ.

english_verse: When Mary and Joseph brought Jesus to the temple to do what the Law of Moses says should be done for a new baby, the Spirit told Simeon to go into the temple.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon yela Simeɔn gyuu Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man. Gyosɛfo na Mɛɛre ka mo be bu Yesu ŋon kɔŋ be kaa do de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ nyiŋmaa man, ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan ne yako nɛ,

english_verse: Simeon took the baby Jesus in his arms and praised God,
translation_verse: te Simeɔn tee ɔ nyiŋmaase lɛɛ bu ŋon te ɔ lese Wurubuarɛ yele nyiaa,

english_verse: “Lord, I am your servant, and now I can die in peace, because you have kept your promise to me.
translation_verse: “De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ, nombii keŋ n be yako see nɛ n te di ke dɔɔ. Mena dɔɔ nyaŋ ke tale lɔ n tobaale gbɛɛ wosefɛɛreŋ man.

english_verse: With my own eyes I have seen what you have done to save your people,
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi maŋ gbagba ta mo me sibii naa nyeelɛɛre ŋon n be kpili nɛ,

english_verse: and foreign nations will also see this.
translation_verse: n kpila e nyi waa kɔŋ na balaŋ pou kena e.

english_verse: Your mighty power is a light for all nations, and it will bring honor to your people Israel.”
translation_verse: Ŋon yɛna keŋmaŋee keŋ gyae ke yela baŋ bɔɔ yɛɛ Iseraetenawɔ ya nɛ, kena n gbɛɛneŋ na baa lese n balaŋ Iseraetena yele.”

english_verse: Then he blessed them and told Mary, “This child of yours will cause many people in Israel to fall and others to stand. The child will be like a warning sign. Many people will reject him,
translation_verse: Keŋte Simeɔn fanewɔ fa wɔ nyi Wurubuarɛ waa hiraa wɔ te ɔ yako bu ŋon ɔ naa Mɛɛre nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ lese na bu kei. Iseraetena burum baŋ baa bɛɛ e na baa naa diyem, te baŋ mɔ baa lɛɛ e di na baa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ. Bu kei waa yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ gbagba tobaale, mɔna balaŋ baa koro seŋ tia e,

english_verse: and you, Mary, will suffer as though you had been stabbed by a dagger. But all this will show what people are really thinking.” Anna Speaks about the Child Jesus
translation_verse: na ka lese ŋan balaŋ burum na gyueŋ nɛ pou debɔɔ. Te nyaŋ Mɛɛre n kena diyem ŋgba sila te ba moowɔ sɔɔ neŋ n konɔɔ man nɛ.” Ana kekolosi lee Yesu wose man

english_verse: The prophet Anna was also there in the temple. She was the daughter of Phanuel from the tribe of Asher, and she was very old. In her youth she had been married for seven years, but her husband died.
translation_verse: Alo kegyia ŋolo kyaawɔ ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Ana. Ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre e te ɔ kya yele yɛna Fanuwɛli. Fanuwɛli kei lee Asa dekpaŋalaŋ man. Ana kei yalawɔ te ŋon na ɔ baale kyaawɔ kulutooneŋ nyetooro, te ɔ baale yeŋawɔ yela e.

english_verse: years old. Night and day she served God in the temple by praying and often going without eating.
translation_verse: ). Nelim na ka weese pou na ɔ kyae Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man ɔ ne bake ɔ nɔɔ fane.

english_verse: At this time Anna came in and praised God. She spoke about the child Jesus to everyone who hoped for Jerusalem to be set free. The Return to Nazareth
translation_verse: Simeɔn kɛɛ kolosi nɛ, te ŋon Ana mɔ kɔŋawɔ. Ɔ fanewɔ fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ, te ɔ kolosiwɔ lee bu ŋon wose man fa balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ daa nyeelɛɛre ŋon waa kɔŋ kaa lese Gyerusalɛmtena lee ba diyem man nɛ.

english_verse: After Joseph and Mary had done everything that the Law of the Lord commands, they returned home to Nazareth in Galilee.
translation_verse: Gyosɛfo na Mɛɛre keyɛɛ kpene kamasɛ taŋ ŋgba mena keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan nawolo nɛ, ba besewɔ gyu be dɛɛ Nasarɛte keŋ kyaa Galelia tɛɛle man nɛ.

english_verse: The child Jesus grew. He became strong and wise, and God blessed him. The Boy Jesus in the Temple
translation_verse: Bu ŋon beewɔ yɛɛ doŋ te ɔ sia karawɔ nideli, te Wurubuarɛ moo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele e. Yesu kegyu Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa man

english_verse: Every year Jesus' parents went to Jerusalem for Passover.
translation_verse: Kulutoo kamasɛ nɛ, Yesu ɔ kya na ɔ naa ne gyu Gyerusalɛm ke di ba nyeekelɛɛwee dinaa keŋ.

english_verse: And when Jesus was twelve years old, they all went there as usual for the celebration.
translation_verse: Yesu ke di kulutooneŋ kufu ala nɛ, be gyuu Gyerusalɛm be kaa di ba nyeekelɛɛwee dinaa kei ŋgba mena keŋ bɛɛ taŋ gyu nɛ.

english_verse: After Passover his parents left, but they did not know that Jesus had stayed on in the city.
translation_verse: Be ke di wee keŋ taŋ bese bee gyu nɛ, Yesu dɔɔwɔ Gyerusalɛm botɔɔ mɔna ɔ kya na ɔ naa ta gyem ya.

english_verse: They thought he was traveling with some other people, and they went a whole day before they started looking for him.
translation_verse: Na baŋ gyeŋ nyi ɔ kpuɛ na be balaŋ dikpii keŋ mena dɔɔ be tɛɛwɔ wee debiliŋ pou, pɛte ba gyoo e kekɛo be tebia na ba loŋtona man.

english_verse: When they could not find him with their relatives and friends, they went back to Jerusalem and started looking for him there.
translation_verse: Keŋ ba te naa e ya dɔɔ, ba besewɔ gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man be kaa kɛo e.

english_verse: Three days later they found Jesus sitting in the temple, listening to the teachers and asking them questions.
translation_verse: Ka weeya atoorote te ba ke naa e Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man na ŋon na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ kyaa la. Ɔ ne tei be nombia, na ŋon mɔ ne bɔɔse wɔ nombia.

english_verse: Everyone who heard him was surprised at how much he knew and at the answers he gave.
translation_verse: Mena keŋ ɔ ne nyii nombia ŋan asɛɛ na mena keŋ ɔ ne tira ŋe nɔɔ fa wɔ nɛ, doo baŋ pou be tei nɛ nɔɔ.

english_verse: When his parents found him, they were amazed. His mother said, “Son, why have you done this to us? Your father and I have been very worried, and we have been searching for you!”
translation_verse: Ɔ kya na ɔ naa ke gyu kena e botɔɔ nɛ, ba wose gbirisi wɔ. Keŋte ɔ naa bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Me bu, weera te n yɛɛ daa mena? Kɛɛ, daa na n kya sia te yɛɛ, de dɔŋɛɛ dɛɛ kɛo neŋ.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Why did you have to look for me? Didn't you know that I would be in my Father's house?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Weera dɔɔ te ɛ ne kɛo maŋ? Ɛ ba gyeŋ nyi kaboena nyi maa kyaa me kya dɛɛ yee?”

english_verse: Jesus went back to Nazareth with his parents and obeyed them. His mother kept on thinking about all that had happened.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu korowɔ sila wɔ gyu Nasarɛte donɔɔ man, te ɔ nyii ɔ kya na ɔ naa dei. Ɔ naa moo nombia kɛŋa pou do ɔ konɔɔ man, na ɔ na gyueŋ ŋe man nideli.

english_verse: Jesus became wise, and he grew strong. God was pleased with him and so were the people.
translation_verse: Yesu beewɔ nideli te ɔ sia karawɔ kpu, te ɔ gyoo Wurubuarɛ na balaŋ pou sia.

english_verse: When tax collectors came to be baptized, they asked John, “Teacher, what should we do?”
translation_verse: Lempoolɛɛra baale mɔ kɔŋawɔ nyi Gyɔn dɛɛ sɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ te be bɔɔse e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, woŋ te kaboena nyi daa mɔ dɛɛ yɛɛ?”

english_verse: Do something to show that you really have given up your sins. Don't start saying you belong to Abraham's family. God can turn these stones into children for Abraham.
translation_verse: Ɛ yɛɛ nombia kpaakpaa ŋan nawolo nyi ɛ te kyɛɛkee lee ɛ gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man nɛ. Ɛ na yɛɛ ya agyueŋ nyi keŋ ɛ yɛɛ Aberaham ɔ naanasewɔ dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ lɛɛ ŋon ya. Mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi Wurubuarɛ waa tale yela boya adoŋ kɛŋa ka bese naanase fa Aberaham.

english_verse: But to Herod the ruler, he said, “It was wrong for you to take Herodias, your brother's wife.” John also said Herod had done many other bad things.
translation_verse: Gyɔn lese nombiakumɛɛ burum ŋan Herode ŋon ɔ seŋɛɛ Romantena gyoo dinaa nawɔɔ man be yɛɛ nɛ si e, na mena keŋ ɔ be lɛo ŋon gbagba ɔ kegyia wɛɛle Herodia yala nɛ.

english_verse: Isaiah the prophet wrote about John when he said, “In the desert someone is shouting, ‘Get the road ready for the Lord! Make a straight path for him.
translation_verse: Ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre ŋon bɛɛ baake nyi Yesaya be ŋmarase ɔ tɔne man lee Gyɔn wose man nɛ nyiaa, “Ŋolo ne fae yeesa depampaa dinaa man nyiaa, ɛ desina de Gbeŋgyoo gbɛɛneŋ, na ɛ ke yela ɔ gbɛɛneŋ pou ka tenee.

english_verse: Fill up every valley and level every mountain and hill. Straighten the crooked paths and smooth out the rough roads.
translation_verse: Ɛ tɔ boŋobii kamasɛ na ɛ ke yɛlɛɛ bulase na bulasebia pou, na ŋa ke doo kyɛɛkyɛɛ. Ɛ tenee gbɛɛneŋ ŋan be gokii nɛ, na ɛ ke kyalase gbɛɛneŋ ŋan ŋa be kyalasɛɛ ya nɛ.

english_verse: Then everyone will see the saving power of God.’ ”
translation_verse: Kɛŋa pou wɔle na, balaŋ pou baa naa nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ ba lese nyi ɔ kaa lɛɛ daa nɛ.”

english_verse: Crowds of people came out to be baptized, but John said to them, “You bunch of snakes! Who warned you to run from the coming judgment?
translation_verse: Balaŋ burum kɔŋawɔ Gyɔn gyaŋ nyi waa sɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ dana nombiakumɛɛ ŋgba doma nɛ, amɔte faree na ŋon nyi yaa yeu lee Wurubuarɛ baŋ dinaa keŋ gyae kekɔŋ balaŋ dɔɔ nɛ man?

english_verse: Some soldiers asked him, “And what about us? What do we have to do?” John told them, “Don't force people to pay money to make you leave them alone. Be satisfied with your pay.”
translation_verse: Yoonɔɔtena baale mɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Daa mɔ de? Woŋ te kaboena nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ?” Keŋte Gyɔn yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ na mo siadoŋ lɛo ŋolo kɔba ya, yaa ɛ na mo nombiakumɛɛ gyakaa ŋolo dɔɔ ya. Ɛ yela kɔba keŋ bɛɛ tɔ ŋon nɛ ka gyoo ɛ sia.”

english_verse: John said, “I am just baptizing with water. But someone more powerful is going to come, and I am not good enough even to untie his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire.
translation_verse: Keŋte Gyɔn tiranɔɔ fa be popou nyiaa, “Maŋ dɔɔ loŋ te mɛɛ mo sɔ ŋon Wurubuarɛ loŋ, mɔna ŋon ɔ kela maŋ nɛ waa kɔŋ keŋ maŋ tekaboe gba nyi maa buŋ booli ɔ nkyokota ŋmeeseŋ koraŋ gba ya. Ŋon dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ na boalaŋ, te waa mo sɔ ŋon Wurubuarɛ loŋ.

english_verse: In many different ways John preached the good news to the people.
translation_verse: Keŋte Gyɔn tɛɛwɔ gbɛɛneŋ burum dɔɔ kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia fa wɔ, te ɔ doo wɔ kakyeŋ nyi baa kyɛɛkee lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man.

english_verse: Finally, Herod put John in jail, and this was the worst thing he had done. The Baptism of Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Te nombiikum keŋ Herode be yɛɛ tɔ nɔɔ koraŋ yɛna nyi, ɔ yela be kyaŋa Gyɔn ke tɔ deni. Yesu Wurubuarɛ loŋkesɔ Mateo

english_verse: While everyone else was being baptized, Jesus himself was baptized. Then as he prayed, the sky opened up,
translation_verse: Gyɔn ke sɔ balaŋ dikpii keŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ taŋ nɛ, ɔ sɔɔ Yesu mɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ. Ɔ ke sɔ e taŋ na te Yesu moowɔ ɔ ne fane. Botɔɔ te adido torowɔ,

english_verse: and the Holy Spirit came down upon him in the form of a dove. A voice from heaven said, “You are my own dear Son, and I am pleased with you.” The Ancestors of Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: keŋte Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon moo duwuluŋ dinɔɔ te ɔ kaa gyakaawɔ Yesu dɔɔ. Botɔɔ te be nyii nɔɔwoya ŋaale lee adido nyiaa, “Nyaŋ yɛna me bu ŋon mɛɛ gyae neŋ lee me konɔɔ man, te n na gyoo me sia nideli.” Yesu ɔ naanaɔ

english_verse: Enosh, and Seth. The family of Jesus went all the way back to Adam and then to God.
translation_verse: Kainan mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Enose te Enose mɔ ɔ kya yɛna Sɛɛte, te Sɛɛte ɔ kya yɛna Adam, te Wurubuarɛ mɔ yɛɛna Adam.

english_verse: The Scriptures say: ‘God will tell his angels to take care of you. They will catch you in their arms, and you will not even hurt your feet on the stones.’ ”
translation_verse: na baa kɔlee neŋ keŋ nyi, n be gyae n ka mo n nae gyɔ na boe koraŋ gba ya.’ ”

english_verse: After the sun had set, people with all kinds of diseases were brought to Jesus. He put his hands on each one of them and healed them.
translation_verse: Kelii balinɔɔ nɛ, balaŋ burum moo kaweesetena baŋ bɛɛ wee kaweese kpookpoo nɛ kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ, te ɔ moo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa kaweete kamasɛ dɔɔ te ɔ kyɔɔ wɔ.

english_verse: The devil said to Jesus, “If you are God's Son, tell this stone to turn into bread.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔbɔnsam yako e nyiaa, “Akpaa nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon na, yela boe kei ka bese weenɛɛ.”

english_verse: Then the devil led Jesus up to a high place and quickly showed him all the nations on earth.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔbɔnsam moo Yesu gyu bula dinaa daale nyee dɔɔ, te ɔ lese tɛɛle kei dɔɔ gyoorobiiri pou wola e karatetee dokoloŋ.

english_verse: Finally, the devil took Jesus to Jerusalem and had him stand on top of the temple. The devil said, “If you are God's Son, jump off.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔbɔnsam moo Yesu gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man te ɔ mo e gyu Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ nyee man adido adido te ɔ yako e nyiaa, “Akpaa nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon na, fuŋ n wose lee kɛbo ke lɔ tɛɛle man,

english_verse: He taught in the Jewish synagogues, and everyone praised him. The People of Nazareth Turn against Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Yesu tɛɛwɔ wola Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man, te walaŋ kamasɛ kɛlɛɛ e. Ba kebɛɛ Yesu Nasarɛte donɔɔ man Mateo

english_verse: Jesus went back to Nazareth, where he had been brought up, and as usual he went to the synagogue on the Sabbath. When he stood up to read from the Scriptures,
translation_verse: Yesu gyuuwɔ Nasarɛte botɔɔ keŋ bɔɔ kɛɛle e nɛ, te Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee kelii nɛ, ɔ gyuuwɔ Gyudatena ɔsom deni man ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ ne taŋ yɛɛ nɛ. Keŋte ɔ korowɔ seŋ nyi waa kala Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan,

english_verse: “The Lord's Spirit has come to me, because he has chosen me to tell the good news to the poor. The Lord has sent me to announce freedom for prisoners, to give sight to the blind, to free everyone who suffers,
translation_verse: “De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ feliŋ doo me man, nawolo nyi ɔ ta lese maŋ see nyi maa kolosi ɔ nombia kpaakpaa ŋan fa ayematena. Ɔ te kpila maŋ nyi maa yako yoo gbeŋa nyi Wurubuarɛ waa lese wɔ lee be gbeŋiri man, na maa yako siayɛlɛɛsatena nyi be sia gyae ke gyuusu, na baŋ mɔ bee wuritii wɔ nɛ ke nyiŋ ba wose,

english_verse: and to say, ‘This is the year the Lord has chosen.’ ”
translation_verse: na maa yela balaŋ pou ka gyeŋ nyi, debaŋ keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ waa kaa lɛɛ ɔ balaŋ nyee nɛ ta benaa.”

english_verse: Jesus closed the book, then handed it back to the man in charge and sat down. Everyone in the synagogue looked straight at Jesus.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔ tɔne keŋ te ɔ besena ke fa tobaale ŋon ɔ ba mo ke fa e nɛ, te ɔ tisiwɔ kyaa. Balaŋ baŋ pou be kyaa bɔ ɔsom deni keŋ man nɛ, fɛɛ be sia bɛɛ kɛɛ e nɛ fee.

english_verse: All the people started talking about Jesus and were amazed at the wonderful things he said. They kept on asking, “Isn't he Joseph's son?”
translation_verse: Nombia kpaakpaa ŋan Yesu be yako nɛ, doo balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ gyaŋee botɔɔ nɛ nɔɔ te be kɛlɛɛ e. Keŋte be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, “Na Gyosɛfo bu ŋon yaa yee?”

english_verse: Jesus answered: You will certainly want to tell me this saying, “Doctor, first make yourself well.” You will tell me to do the same things here in my own hometown that you heard I did in Capernaum.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ma gyeŋ nyi ɛ ke gyɔ maŋ dudu kei nyi ‘Nyaŋ ŋon n ne kyɔ balaŋ nɛ, kyɔ n wose na dɛɛ kɛɛ.’ Te ɛ ka bese yako maŋ nyi, ‘Gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan dɔɔ nyii n be yɛɛ Kapanum donɔɔ man nɛ, yɛɛ ŋa nyaŋ gbagba n donɔɔ man kɛbo mɔ na dɛɛ kɛɛ.’ ”

english_verse: But you can be sure that no prophets are liked by the people of their own hometown.
translation_verse: Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ kpu nyiaa, “Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre ŋolo be kyaa keŋ bɛɛ lɛɛ e ŋon gbagba ɔ dɛɛ ya.”

english_verse: Once during the time of Elijah there was no rain for three and a half years, and people everywhere were starving. There were many widows in Israel,
translation_verse: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi, debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Elia be kyaa nɛ, na liala burum kyaa Iserae tɛɛle dɔɔ, mɔna debaŋ keŋ ɔga ba sem kulutooneŋ atooro na yaale, te tanam dinaa be yale tɛɛle keŋ pou dɔɔ nɛ,

english_verse: During the time of the prophet Elisha, many men in Israel had leprosy. But no one was healed, except Naaman who lived in Syria.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Elisa mɔ ke kyaa nɛ, na akuŋtuŋtena burum kyaa Iserae tɛɛle dɔɔ mɔna Wurubuarɛ te kyɔ be man ŋolo ya. Siria baale Naaman ŋon waageŋ te ɔ kyɔɔwɔ.

english_verse: But Jesus slipped through the crowd and got away. A Man with an Evil Spirit (Mark
translation_verse: Mɔna Yesu moowɔ balaŋ baŋ pou nsana nsana te ɔ laŋawɔ. Yesu kegegi feliŋkum lee balee ŋolo man Maake

english_verse: Jesus went to the town of Capernaum in Galilee and taught the people on the Sabbath.
translation_verse: Yesu gyuuwɔ Kapanum donɔɔ keŋ ke kyaa Galelia tɛɛle man nɛ man. Te Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee kelii na, ɔ gyuuwɔ ka wola wɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia.

english_verse: There in the synagogue was a man with an evil spirit. He yelled out,
translation_verse: Na balee ŋolo kyaa Gyudatena ɔsom deni keŋ man keŋ feliŋkum doo ɔ man. Ɔ fae yeesa keŋkeŋ nyiaa,

english_verse: Jesus ordered the evil spirit to be quiet and come out. The demon threw the man to the ground in front of everyone and left without harming him.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yase ɔ sia yako feliŋkum keŋ nyiaa, “Wu n nɔɔ, na n ka lee ɔ man.” Keŋte feliŋkum keŋ fuŋa balee ŋon lɔ balaŋ baŋ pou siaman te ka leewɔ ɔ man laŋ, mɔna balee ŋon te gooli ya.

english_verse: They all were amazed and kept saying to each other, “What kind of teaching is this? He has power to order evil spirits out of people!”
translation_verse: Nombii kei doo balaŋ baŋ pou nɔɔ te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, “Woŋ kawola yɛna kei? Ɔ na mo doŋ kolosi fa feliŋkumɛɛ na ŋɛɛ lee balaŋ man.”

english_verse: News about Jesus spread all over that part of the country. Jesus Heals Many People (Matthew
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu wose man nombia yaaseewɔ gyu debiise ŋan ŋa benaa na botɔɔ nɛ pou man. Yesu kekyɔ balaŋ burum kaweese Mateo

english_verse: Jesus left the synagogue and went to Simon's home. When Jesus got there, he was told that Simon's mother-in-law was sick with a high fever.
translation_verse: Yesu ka lee Gyudatena ɔsom deni keŋ man na, te ɔ gyuuwɔ Simɔn dɛɛ. Ɔ ke gyu botɔɔ na Simɔn wa awu doo ɔ na wee kpaŋalaŋ. Keŋte be waase Yesu nyi waa kyɔ e kawee fa wɔ.

english_verse: So Jesus went over to her and ordered the fever to go away. Right then she was able to get up and serve them a meal.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔ ka seŋawɔ alo ŋon yenaŋ te ɔ fae kpaŋalaŋ keŋ, te ɔ kawee keŋ taŋawɔ. Debokenaŋ man te alo ŋon korowɔ kyaa te ɔ kɛɛ be dɔɔ.

english_verse: Demons went out of many people and shouted, “You are the Son of God!” But Jesus ordered the demons not to speak because they knew he was the Messiah.
translation_verse: Feliŋkumɛɛ mɔ leewɔ balaŋ burum man na ŋɛɛ fae yeesa nyi, “Nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon.” Mɔna ɔ fae ŋa keŋ ɔ te fa ŋa gbɛɛ ŋa ke kolosi ya, nawolo nyi ŋa gyeŋ nyi ŋon yɛna Kristo, nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ. Yesu kolosi nombia burum Galelia tɛɛle man

english_verse: The next morning Jesus went out to a place where he could be alone, and crowds came looking for him. When they found him, they tried to stop him from leaving.
translation_verse: Tɛɛ kena nɛ, Yesu wɔŋa ɔ wose lee yenaŋ. Botɔɔ na balaŋ baŋ te fiasɛɛ bɛɛ kɛo e. Ba kena e nɛ, ba te fa e gbɛɛ waa lee be gyaŋ ya.

english_verse: But Jesus said, “People in other towns must hear the good news about God's kingdom. This is why I was sent.”
translation_verse: Mɔna ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Kaboena nyi maa kolosi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ wose man nombia kpaakpaa ŋan fa donɔɔse akaŋ ŋan mɔ, nawolo nyi keŋ dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ kpila maŋ.”

english_verse: So he kept on preaching in the synagogues in Judea.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Yesu tɛɛwɔ kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man Galelia tɛɛle dɔɔ pou.

english_verse: But Jesus would often go to some place where he could be alone and pray. Jesus Heals a Man Who Could Not Walk (Matthew
translation_verse: Mɔna Yesu ne kyaa wɔŋ ɔ wose lee yenaŋ gyu kefane. Yesu kekyɔ yenaŋ dayeŋasate ŋolo kawee Mateo

english_verse: When Jesus saw how much faith they had, he said to the man, “My friend, your sins are forgiven.”
translation_verse: Yesu kena mena keŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ e di nɛ, ɔ yako kaweete ŋon nyiaa, “Me gyoo, me ta mo n nombiakumɛɛ pou kyɛɛ neŋ.”

english_verse: Jesus got into the boat that belonged to Simon and asked him to row it out a little way from the shore. Then Jesus sat down in the boat to teach the crowd.
