translation_verse: Yesu gyoowɔ degbele keŋ daale man, keŋ ke yɛɛ Simɔn wui nɛ. Te ɔ yako e nyi waa tɛɛse e gyu loŋ keŋ man kyomii. Yesu kyaawɔ degbele keŋ man botɔɔ te ɔ wola balaŋ dikpii keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia.

english_verse: “Master,” Simon answered, “we have worked hard all night long and have not caught a thing. But if you tell me to, I will let the nets down.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Simɔn tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, da te yɛɛ tom nelim pou da te kyaŋ kolo ya! Mɔna keŋ nyaŋ be yako dɔɔ, dɛɛ lɔ da asawuse.”

english_verse: They did this and caught so many fish that their nets began ripping apart.
translation_verse: Ba ke lɔ ba asawuse ŋan nɛ, be kyaŋa kpɛɛ burum keŋ nyi ba asawuse ŋan ne lɛɛ gbɛɛ ŋe ke tirii.

english_verse: Then they signaled for their partners in the other boat to come and help them. The men came, and together they filled the two boats so full that they both began to sink.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ be baake be tebia baŋ be doo degbele daale man nɛ nyi be kaa kyɔ wɔ. Ba kekɔŋ nɛ, be kaa lese kpɛɛ wulu agbelese ala ŋan pou keŋ nyi, ŋe lɛɛ gbɛɛ nafɔ ŋe ke munu.

english_verse: His partners James and John, the sons of Zebedee, were surprised too. Jesus told Simon, “Don't be afraid! From now on you will bring in people instead of fish.”
translation_verse: Mena mɔ te doo ɔ tebia Sebedeo bia Gyemisi na Gyɔn baŋ baŋ na wɔ ne yɛɛ tom nɛ nɔɔ. Botɔɔ te Yesu yako Simɔn nyiaa, “Na yee ya, lee gyɛŋ ne gyu nɛ, balaŋ te maa yela n ke kyaŋ.”

english_verse: The men pulled their boats up on the shore. Then they left everything and went with Jesus. Jesus Heals a Man (Matthew
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ba ke saa ba agbelese ŋan kɔŋ dekeŋkeŋ dɔɔ nɛ, be tinaa kpene kamasɛ yela botɔɔ te be sila Yesu laŋ. Yesu kekyɔ akuŋtuŋte ŋolo kawee Mateo

english_verse: News about Jesus kept spreading. Large crowds came to listen to him teach and to be healed of their diseases.
translation_verse: Mɔna nɛnɛɛ gba te Yesu wose man nombia yaaseewɔ gyu yenaŋ kamasɛ, te balaŋ burum kɔŋawɔ be kaa nyii e na waa kyɔ wɔ be kaweese.

english_verse: But because of the crowd, they could not get him to Jesus. So they went up on the roof, where they removed some tiles and let the mat down in the middle of the room.
translation_verse: mɔna be te nyiŋ gbɛɛ mo e ta denanɔɔ man ya, nawolo nyi balaŋ boe. Mena dɔɔ ba gyeŋawɔ gyu deni keŋ nyee dɔɔ te be wolee yenaŋ daale, te be tisinaa e na ɔ dofoŋoŋ keŋ pou dɛɛdɛɛ see Yesu siaman balaŋ baŋ nsana nsana.

english_verse: Is it easier for me to tell this man that his sins are forgiven or to tell him to get up and walk?
translation_verse: Nombia kɛŋa man woŋti woɛla na keyako? ‘Me ta mo n dukum kyɛɛ neŋ,’ yaa ‘Koro seŋ na n ke ta?’

english_verse: But now you will see that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” Jesus then said to the man, “Get up! Pick up your mat and walk home.”
translation_verse: Mɔna mɛɛ gyae ɛ ka gyeŋ nyi maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu dana doŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nyi maa mo dukum kyɛɛ. Mena dɔɔ ɔ yako kaweete ŋon nyiaa, Koro seŋ, gate n dofoŋoŋ na n ke ta gyu n dɛɛ!”

english_verse: At once the man stood up in front of everyone. He picked up his mat and went home, giving thanks to God.
translation_verse: Debokenaŋ man te kaweete ŋon korowɔ seŋ ɔ nawɔɔ dɔɔ be siaman te ɔ gate ɔ dofoŋoŋ keŋ dɔɔ ɔ doo nɛ, te ɔ laŋa ɔ dɛɛ. Ɔ ne gyu na ɔ na lese Wurubuarɛ yele.

english_verse: Everyone was amazed and praised God. What they saw surprised them, and they said, “We have seen a great miracle today!” Jesus Chooses Levi (Matthew
translation_verse: Nombia kɛŋa doo balaŋ baŋ pou nɔɔ nideli te ba lese Wurubuarɛ yele. “Gyakoloŋ gyoo wɔ te be yakowɔ nyiaa, gyɛŋ dɔɔ da te naa nombia ŋan te kɔŋa ta see ya.” Yesu ke baake Lewi Mateo

english_verse: Later, Jesus went out and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at the place for paying taxes. Jesus said to him, “Follow me.”
translation_verse: Nombia kɛŋa wɔle nɛ Yesu leewɔ te ɔ ke naa lempoolɛɛre ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Lewi nɛ, na ɔ kyaa ɔ lempoo delɛe man. Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Koro sila maŋ.”

english_verse: Levi left everything and went with Jesus.
translation_verse: Debokenaŋ man te Lewi tinaa kpene kamasɛ yela te ɔ korowɔ sila Yesu.

english_verse: In his home Levi gave a big dinner for Jesus. Many tax collectors and other guests were also there.
translation_verse: Keŋte Lewi moo Yesu gyu ŋon Lewi dɛɛ te ɔ ke saa e weenɛɛ ampaŋ ampaŋ. Lempoolɛɛra burum na balaŋ yakaa nyibaŋ kɔŋawɔ kaa di bom na wɔ.

english_verse: The Pharisees and some of their teachers of the Law of Moses grumbled to Jesus' disciples, “Why do you eat and drink with these tax collectors and other sinners?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Farasiitena na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ bɔɔ lee Farasiitena dikpii keŋ man nɛ ŋorokoteewɔ bɔɔse Yesu kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Weera dɔɔ te ɛmɛɛ na lempoolɛɛra na nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra ne dii te ɛ ne nyɔɔ bom?”

english_verse: I didn't come to invite good people to turn to God. I came to invite sinners.” People Ask about Going without Eating (Matthew
translation_verse: Ma te kɔŋ nyi me kaa baake balaŋ baŋ be gbɛɛneŋ wulaa tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ ya, mɔna me kɔŋae me kaa baake nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra na baa kyɛɛkee lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man na baa kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ.” Ba ke bɔɔse Yesu nɔɔkebake wose man nombia Mateo

english_verse: Some people said to Jesus, “John's followers often pray and go without eating, and so do the followers of the Pharisees. But your disciples never go without eating or drinking.”
translation_verse: Keŋte be yako Yesu nyiaa, “Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon silala ne kyaa bake be nɔɔ fane, te mena mɔ yaa Farasiitena silala mɔ ne yɛɛ. Mɔna nyaŋ wui kɛwɔ dɔɔ bee dii bɛɛ nyɔɔ debaŋ kamasɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus told them, “The friends of a bridegroom don't go without eating while he is still with them.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ ke tale yela balaŋ baŋ be kyaa alowɔletɛɛ nɛ ke bake be nɔɔ keŋ alowɔle baale kyaa be gyaŋ?

english_verse: But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.”
translation_verse: Mɔna debaŋ daale gyae kekɔŋ keŋ baa mo alowɔle baale ŋon lee be gyaŋ. Mena weeya ŋenaŋ man te baa bake be nɔɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus then told them these sayings: No one uses a new piece of cloth to patch old clothes. The patch would shrink and make the hole even bigger.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu gyɔɔ wɔ dudu kei nyiaa, “Ŋolo bɛɛ kara kanyaŋ wɔle mo taa kakyeŋgbelaa keŋ ba yem nɛ ya. Akpaa ɔ yɛɛ mena na ɔ te wɔlɛɛ kanyaŋ wɔle keŋ, nawolo nyi kanyaŋ wɔle keŋ be gyae ke kyaa na bene keŋ ya.

english_verse: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The new wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined.
translation_verse: Mena mɔ te ŋolo bɛɛ mo soloŋ wɔle do wonembu tɔne ogele bene man ya. Ɔ yɛɛ mena na soloŋ wɔle keŋ gyae ke tao wonembu tɔne ogele keŋ lii na soloŋ keŋ ke leki, na wonembu tɔne ogele keŋ mɔ ke wɔlɛɛ.

english_verse: New wine must be put only into new wineskins.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ kaboena bo nyi baa mo soloŋ wɔle do wonembu tɔne ogele wɔle man.

english_verse: No one wants new wine after drinking old wine. They say, “The old wine is better.”
translation_verse: Te ŋolo be kyaa keŋ nyi ɔ nyɔɔ soloŋ apaesa na, waa yako nyi ɔ ne gyae ntoŋkum waa nyɔɔ bela ya. Nawolo nyi ɔ ne yako nyi apaesate keŋ yɛna kpaakpaate.”

english_verse: Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he told the man to stand up where everyone could see him. And the man stood up.
translation_verse: Mɔna na Yesu gyeŋ kpene keŋ bɛɛ gyueŋ te ɔ yako baam dayeŋasate ŋon nyiaa, “Koro seŋ na n ke kɔŋ kaa seŋ balaŋ kɛwɔ pou siaman.” Keŋte balee ŋon korowɔ kaa seŋ be siaman.

english_verse: Some Pharisees said, “Why are you picking grain on the Sabbath? You're not supposed to do that!”
translation_verse: Keŋte Farasiitena baale naa wɔ te be bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Weera dɔɔ te ɛ ne yɛɛ kpene keŋ da mmaraa bɛɛ fa gbɛɛ nyi baa yɛɛ da kefɛɛfowee ya nɛ?”

english_verse: Ask God to bless anyone who curses you, and pray for everyone who is cruel to you.
translation_verse: Ɛ sola Wurubuarɛ na waa hiraa baŋ bɛɛ falaa ŋon nɛ, na ɛ kefane fa baŋ bɛɛ naase ŋon diyem nɛ.

english_verse: Then Jesus asked, “On the Sabbath should we do good deeds or evil deeds? Should we save someone's life or destroy it?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Woŋ te da mmaraa ne fa gbɛɛ nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ kefɛɛfowee keŋ? Da mmaraa ne fa daa gbɛɛ nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ kolo kpaakpaa yaa dɛɛ yɛɛ kolo kum? Dɛɛ lɛɛ walaŋ nyee yaa dee yela waa yeŋ?”

english_verse: One was Simon, and Jesus named him Peter. Another was Andrew, Peter's brother. There were also James, John, Philip, Bartholomew,
translation_verse: Baala kufu bala baŋ Yesu ba lese nɛ yela yɛna Simɔn, ŋon Yesu ba do e yele nyi Pita nɛ, na ɔ naabu Anderiase na Gyemisi na Gyɔn na Filipo na Batolomeo na

english_verse: The next morning he called his disciples together and chose twelve of them to be his apostles.
translation_verse: Tɛɛ kena nɛ ɔ baake ɔ kaseela kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ te ɔ lese baala kufu bala lee be man nyi baa yɛɛ ɔ kpilala.

english_verse: Everyone was trying to touch Jesus, because power was going out from him and healing them all. Blessings and Troubles (Matthew
translation_verse: Na balaŋ baŋ pou ne gyae nyi baa tina e, nawolo nyi doŋ na lee ɔ man na ne kyɔ wɔ be kaweese pou. Yesu kawola lee siakagye na waraŋase wose man Mateo

english_verse: Matthew, Thomas, and James the son of Alphaeus. The rest of the apostles were Simon, known as the Eager One,
translation_verse: Mateo na Tomase na Alfeose bu Gyemisi na Simɔn ŋon ɔ ne gyae ɔ balaŋ nideli nɛ

english_verse: You well-fed people are in for trouble. You will go hungry! You people who are laughing now are in for trouble. You are going to cry and weep!
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ be dii baa debaŋ kei nɛ ɛ laako, nawolo nyi tanam gyae kaa ko ŋon. Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ na boŋo debaŋ kei nɛ ɛ laako, nawolo nyi yaa kaa wii na ɛ ke di walaŋsa.

english_verse: These people had come to listen to Jesus and to be healed of their diseases. All who were troubled by evil spirits were also healed.
translation_verse: Be kɔŋawɔ be kaa nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia na waa kyɔ wɔ be kaweese. Baŋ feliŋkumɛɛ ne haa wɔ nɛ, ɔ kyɔɔ baŋ mɔ.

english_verse: God will bless you hungry people. You will have plenty to eat! God will bless you people who are now crying. You will laugh!
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ baŋ tanam dana ŋon debokeiman nɛ ɛmɛɛ na nyeebam, nawolo nyi ɛmɛɛ ne gyaela ɛ kaa baa. Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ ne wii debokeiman nɛ ɛmɛɛ na nyeebam, nawolo nyi ɛmɛɛ ne gyaela ɛ kaa boŋo.

english_verse: You are in for trouble when everyone says good things about you. That is what your own people said about those prophets who told lies. Love for Enemies (Matthew
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ baŋ balaŋ pou ne kolosi ɛ wose man nombia kpaakpaa nɛ ɛ laako, nawolo nyi mena mɔ te be kyaɔ yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ dii akɛrɛɛ nyi be yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔrawɔ nɛ. Yesu kpa dɛɛ gyae baŋ bɛɛ kɔla daa nɛ nombia Mateo

english_verse: If you give to others, you will be given a full amount in return. It will be packed down, shaken together, and spilling over into your lap. The way you treat others is the way you will be treated.
translation_verse: Ɛ yɛɛ deeli fa balaŋ na Wurubuarɛ mɔ ke yɛɛ deeli fa ŋon. Ɛ yɛɛ mena na, kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa fa ŋon nɛ te yɛɛ nsendee ya. Waa nyiŋ konɔɔ maa ke nideli na ke wulu gba leki, na waa mo do ɛ nyiŋmaa man. Nawolo nyi mena keŋ ɛ ke maa fa ɛ tebia nɛ, mena ke te Wurubuarɛ mɔ waa maa fa ŋon.”

english_verse: is like someone who dug down deep and built a house on solid rock. When a flood came and the river rushed against the house, it was built so well that it didn't even shake.
translation_verse: Ɔ yɛɛ ŋgba walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne ma deni te ɔ be wula bɔɔ te ɔ ba see ka mokɔɔ nideli boe dɔɔ nɛ. Keŋte boo wuuwɔ kaa mo ke dɔɔ mɔna ka te yale ya, nawolo nyi ɔ see ka mokɔɔ nideli.

english_verse: If someone slaps you on one cheek, don't stop that person from slapping you on the other cheek. If someone wants to take your coat, don't try to keep back your shirt.
translation_verse: Akpaa ŋolo de gyɔ n lakaa man na besena alate keŋ mɔ fa e. Mena mɔ te akpaa ŋolo da mo n kegba wurikyim na mo atetate keŋ mɔ kpu e.

english_verse: Give to everyone who asks and don't ask people to return what they have taken from you.
translation_verse: Akpaa ŋolo da sola neŋ kolo na fa e te akpaa ŋolo da mo n kolo na, na gyu ke bɔɔse ke dɔɔ bela ya.

english_verse: Treat others just as you want to be treated.
translation_verse: Kpene keŋ n ne gyae nyi walaŋ waa yɛɛ fa neŋ nɛ nyaŋ mɔ yɛɛ ke fa e.

english_verse: If you love only someone who loves you, will God praise you for that? Even sinners love people who love them.
translation_verse: Akpaa ɛ ne gyae balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ gyae ŋon nɛ baageŋ bo na, ɛ na gyueŋ nyi ɛ ke nyiŋ kolo kpaakpaa lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ ya, nawolo nyi nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra mɔ koraŋ ne gyae baŋ bɛɛ gyae wɔ nɛ.

english_verse: Have pity on others, just as your Father has pity on you. Judging Others (Matthew
translation_verse: Ɛ wii balaŋ waraŋase ŋgba mena keŋ ɛ kya Wurubuarɛ ne wii balaŋ waraŋase nɛ.” Ɛ na mo ɛ wose yɛɛ nombiadiira ya Mateo

english_verse: Jesus said: Don't judge others, and God won't judge you. Don't be hard on others, and God won't be hard on you. Forgive others, and God will forgive you.
translation_verse: “Ɛ na mo ɛ wose yɛɛ balaŋ nombiadiira na ɛ ke bu wɔ fɔɔ, na Wurubuarɛ mɔ be nyiŋ di ɛ nombia na waa bu ŋon fɔɔ ya. Ɛ mo balaŋ nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ, na Wurubuarɛ mɔ ka mo ɛ nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ ŋon.

english_verse: Jesus also used some sayings as he spoke to the people. He said: Can one blind person lead another blind person? Won't they both fall into a ditch?
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu gyɔɔ wɔ dudu kei nyiaa, “Siayɛlɛɛsate waa tale wola ɔ dɔɔ siayɛlɛɛsate gbɛɛ? Akpaa ɔ yɛɛ mena na, be balaŋ bala pou be gyae be kaa gyoo bɔɔ man yee?

english_verse: Are students better than their teacher? But when they are fully trained, they will be like their teacher.
translation_verse: Kaseele bɛɛ kela ɔ gbeŋgyoo ya, mɔna kaseele kamasɛ ŋon ɔ kasee tom nideli nɛ waa yɛɛ ŋgba ɔ gbeŋgyoo nɛ.

english_verse: You can see the speck in your friend's eye, but you don't notice the log in your own eye.
translation_verse: Weera dɔɔ te n ne naa woŋsilibii keŋ doo n dɔɔ sibii man, te n be famenɛ na daakpee keŋ doo nyaŋ gbagba n sibii man ya?

english_verse: How can you say, “My friend, let me take the speck out of your eye,” when you don't see the log in your own eye? You show-offs! First, get the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye. A Tree and Its Fruit (Matthew
translation_verse: Sena te n ke tale yako n dɔɔ nyi, ‘Me dɔɔ, yela maa lese woŋsilibii lee n sibii man,’ keŋ nyaŋ gbagba be famenɛ na daakpee keŋ doo n sibii man ya? Nyaŋ walaŋ ŋon n na beo n wose, kaboena nyi nya taŋgbɛɛ lese daakpee keŋ doo n sibii man nɛ, pɛna n kena nideli lese woŋsilibii keŋ doo n dɔɔ sibii man nɛ.” Daŋ kamasɛ na ke bii keŋ kɛɛ see Mateo

english_verse: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
translation_verse: Daŋ kpaakpaate kamasɛ bɛɛ se bia kumɛɛ ya, mena mɔ te daŋ kum bɛɛ se bia kpaakpaa ya.

english_verse: You can tell what a tree is like by the fruit it produces. You cannot pick figs or grapes from thornbushes.
translation_verse: Daŋ kamasɛ nɛ, ke bia te bɛɛ mo gyeŋ ke. Bɔɔ ne kɔ akutu bia lee mango daŋ man ya, mena mɔ te bɔɔ ne kɔ apaŋ lee abotoo woŋse man ya.

english_verse: Good people do good things because of the good in their hearts, but bad people do bad things because of the evil in their hearts. Your words show what is in your heart. Two Builders (Matthew
translation_verse: Mena ke te denibalaŋ mɔ doo. Walaŋ kpaakpaate kamasɛ na lese abɔɔ kpaakpaa ŋan doo ɔ konɔɔ man nɛ wola, te walaŋkum kamasɛ mɔ na lese nombiakumɛɛ ŋan doo ɔ konɔɔ man nɛ wola. Kpene kamasɛ keŋ be wulu walaŋ konɔɔ man nɛ, keŋ na lee la ɔ nɔɔman. Yesu kekolosi lee deni maara bala wose man Mateo

english_verse: Why do you keep on saying that I am your Lord, when you refuse to do what I say?
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Woŋ dɔɔ te ɛ ne baake maŋ nyi, ‘Ɛ Gbeŋgyoo,’ te ɛ bɛɛ yɛɛ kpene keŋ mɔɔ yako ŋon nyi yaa yɛɛ nɛ ya?

english_verse: But anyone who hears what I say and doesn't obey me is like someone whose house wasn't built on solid rock. As soon as the river rushed against that house, it was smashed to pieces!
translation_verse: Mɔna walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne nyii me nɔɔwoya te ɔ bee di ŋe dɔɔ ya nɛ, ɔ yɛɛ ŋgba walaŋ ŋon ɔ be ma deni te ɔ ta see ka mokɔɔ nideli ya nɛ. Boo wuuwɔ kaa mo ke dɔɔ, te ke yalawɔ tekerii pou keŋ n be naa ka mokɔɔ gba ya.”

english_verse: I have officers who give orders to me, and I have soldiers who take orders from me. I can say to one of them, ‘Go!’ and he goes. I can say to another, ‘Come!’ and he comes. I can say to my servant, ‘Do this!’ and he will do it.”
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi maŋ gbagba mɔ yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon me doo baale tɛɛ, te yoonɔɔtena mɔ doo me tɛɛ. Me yako ŋolo nyi waa gyu yenaŋ daale na ɔ ne gyu, te me yako ŋolo nyi waa kɔŋ na ɔ ne kɔŋ. Kpene kamasɛ keŋ me yako be man ŋolo nyi waa yɛɛ na, keŋ te ɔ ne yɛɛ.”

english_verse: He loves our nation and even built us a synagogue.”
translation_verse: nawolo nyi ɔ ne gyae de tɛɛle kei deeli, te ŋon gba maana dɔ ɔsom deni fa daa.”

english_verse: When the Lord saw the woman, he felt sorry for her and said, “Don't cry!”
translation_verse: De Gbeŋgyoo Yesu kena alo ŋon nɛ waraŋase kyaŋa e te ɔ yako e nyiaa, “Alo na wii ya.”

english_verse: The leaders went to Jesus and begged him to do something. They said, “This man deserves your help!
translation_verse: Keŋte Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ kɔŋawɔ Yesu gyaŋ kaa waase e nideli nyiaa, “Yɛɛ kakyeŋ na n ke kyɔ balee kei fa daa,

english_verse: Simon answered, “I suppose it would be the one who had owed more and didn't have to pay it back.” “You are right,” Jesus said.
translation_verse: Keŋte Simɔn tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Mɛɛ lɛɛdi nyi ŋon ɔ kom boe nɛ.” Te Yesu yako e nyiaa, “N tiranɔɔ nideli.”

english_verse: And when he heard about Jesus, he sent some Jewish leaders to ask him to come and heal the servant.
translation_verse: Yoonɔɔtena kegyia kei ke nyii Yesu nkaŋ nɛ, ɔ kpila Gyudatena kegyiise baale gyu ɔ gyaŋ nyi waa kɔŋ kaa kyɔ ɔ tobaale ŋon fa e.

english_verse: So Jesus went with them. When Jesus wasn't far from the house, the officer sent some friends to tell him, “Lord, don't go to any trouble for me! I am not good enough for you to come into my house.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Yesu sila wɔ gyu. Ɔ ka benaa yoonɔɔtena kegyia ŋon dɛɛ nɛ, ɔ kpila ɔ gyoona gyu keyako Yesu nyiaa, “Me Gbeŋgyoo, na haa n wose ya nawolo nyi ma tekaboena nyi nya kɔŋ me dɛɛ ya,

english_verse: And I am certainly not worthy to come to you. Just say the word, and my servant will get well.
translation_verse: mena ke te ma gyeŋ nyi maŋ gbagba mɔ tekaboena nyi maa kɔŋ n gyaŋ ya. Mena dɔɔ nyaŋ yako nombii dokoloŋ bo, me tobaale ŋon waa koro.

english_verse: When Jesus heard this, he was so surprised that he turned and said to the crowd following him, “In all of Israel I've never found anyone with this much faith!”
translation_verse: Yesu ke nyii nɔɔwoya kɛŋa nɛ, doo ɔ nɔɔ te ɔ gɛɛsewɔ kɛɛ balaŋ dikpii keŋ be silana e nɛ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, Iserae tɛɛle kei dɔɔ pou nɛ ma te naa ta walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di ŋgba balee kei nɛ ya.”

english_verse: The officer's friends returned and found the servant well. A Widow's Son
translation_verse: Keŋte yoonɔɔtena kegyia ŋon kpilala baŋ besewɔ gyu be dɛɛ. Be gyuuwɔ na ɔ tobaale ŋon ta koro. Yesu kegyuusu lialo ŋolo bu

english_verse: As they came near the gate of the town, they saw people carrying out the body of a widow's only son. Many people from the town were walking along with her.
translation_verse: Yesu ka betaa benaa donɔɔ keŋ nɔɔman nɛ, te ɔ naa ba seelena woo ŋolo bɛɛ kɔŋ be kaa wuu. Na woo ŋonaŋ waageŋ yɛna bu baale ŋon ɔ naa ba lolo te ɔ naa ŋon mɔ yɛɛ lialo e. Na donɔɔtena baŋ burum silana alo ŋon.

english_verse: Jesus went over and touched the stretcher on which the people were carrying the dead boy. They stopped, and Jesus said, “Young man, get up!”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu gyuuwɔ ke tina woo ŋon, te baŋ ba seelena e nɛ seŋawɔ. Keŋte Yesu yakowɔ nyiaa, “Tobaale, koro.”

english_verse: The boy sat up and began to speak. Jesus then gave him back to his mother.
translation_verse: Keŋte woo ŋon korowɔ kyaa te ɔ fiasɛɛ ɔ ne kolosi, te Yesu besena e fa ɔ naa.

english_verse: Everyone was frightened and praised God. They said, “A great prophet is here with us! God has come to his people.”
translation_verse: Nombia kɛŋa yɛɛ walaŋ kamasɛ gyakoloŋ te ba lese Wurubuarɛ yele yako nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre dinaa ŋolo te kaa bɛɛ lee de gyaŋ,” te ba besewɔ yako nyiaa “Wurubuarɛ te kɔŋ kaa kyɔ ɔ balaŋ.”

english_verse: News about Jesus spread all over Judea and everywhere else in that part of the country. John the Baptist (Matthew
translation_verse: Yesu wose man nombia kɛŋa yaaseewɔ Gyudia tɛɛle dɔɔ pou na tɛɛlese ŋan pou benaa na botɔɔ nɛ. Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre kekpila ɔ kaseela gyu Yesu gyaŋ Mateo

english_verse: John's followers told John everything that was being said about Jesus. So he sent two of them to ask the Lord, “Are you the one we should be looking for? Or must we wait for someone else?”
translation_verse: Keŋte Gyɔn kpila ɔ kaseela bala baale nyi baa gyu ke bɔɔse de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu nyiaa, “Nyaŋ yɛna walaŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi nyaŋ kekɔŋ nɛ yaa dɛɛ kɛɛ walaŋ wɔle gbɛɛ?”

english_verse: When these messengers came to Jesus, they said, “John the Baptist sent us to ask, ‘Are you the one we should be looking for? Or are we supposed to wait for someone else?’ ”
translation_verse: Gyɔn kaseela baŋ ke gyu Yesu gyaŋ nɛ be yako e nyiaa, “Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon kpila na daa n gyaŋ nyi de kaa bɔɔse neŋ nyiaa, ‘Nyaŋ yɛna walaŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi nyaŋ kekɔŋ nɛ yaa dɛɛ kɛɛ walaŋ wɔle siaman?’ ”

english_verse: At that time Jesus was healing many people who were sick or in pain or were troubled by evil spirits, and he was giving sight to a lot of blind people.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ man na Yesu te kyɔ balaŋ burum kaweese lese wɔ lee be kayeyɛɛ man. Ɔ gegi feliŋkumɛɛ lee baale mɔ man, te ɔ kyɔɔ siayɛlɛɛsatena burum mɔ.

english_verse: Jesus said to the messengers sent by John, “Go and tell John what you have seen and heard. Blind people are now able to see, and the lame can walk. People who have leprosy are being healed, and the deaf can now hear. The dead are raised to life, and the poor are hearing the good news.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Yesu tiranɔɔ fa Gyɔn kpilala baŋ nyiaa, “Ɛ bese gyu keyako Gyɔn kpene keŋ ɛ be naa na ɛ sia, na ŋan ɛ be nyii na ɛ deŋela. Siayɛlɛɛsatena ne naa, ɔtakasese ne tɛɛ, baŋ bɛɛ wee kawee yayam nɛ kaweese te taŋ, te deŋele atɔɔsatena ne nyii. Woya ne foŋii, te bɛɛ yako Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan mɔ fa ayematena.

english_verse: God will bless everyone who doesn't reject me because of what I do.”
translation_verse: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ bɛɛ lɛɛ maŋ aŋmaareŋ nyi maŋ yɛna ŋon Wurubuarɛ be kpili ya nɛ, ŋon na nyeebam.”

english_verse: After John's messengers had gone, Jesus began speaking to the crowds about John: What kind of person did you go out to the desert to see? Was he like tall grass blown about by the wind?
translation_verse: Gyɔn kpilala baŋ ka bese laŋ nɛ, Yesu kolosi Gyɔn wose man nombia fa balaŋ dikpii keŋ te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ ke gyu Gyɔn gyaŋ depampaa dinaa keŋ man nɛ, woŋ te ɛ gyaewɔ nyi ɛ kena? Ŋale keŋ feliŋ ne gyɔ ke wosi nɛ?

english_verse: What kind of man did you really go out to see? Was he someone dressed in fine clothes? People who wear expensive clothes and live in luxury are in the king's palace.
translation_verse: Akpaa na keŋ ya na, woŋ te ɛ gyuuwɔ ɛ kaa kɛɛ? Walaŋ ŋon ɔ doona abɔɔ kpaakpaa nyiŋan yaa? Baŋ bee mia mena te bee di gyoŋ nɛ ne kyaa bo gyooneŋ akpaŋalaŋ man.

english_verse: What then did you go out to see? Was he a prophet? He certainly was! I tell you that he was more than a prophet.
translation_verse: Ɛ yako maŋ maa nyii, woŋ te nafɔ ɛ gyuuwɔ nyi ɛ kaa kɛɛ? Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre? Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi, ŋon ɛ be naa nɛ gba kela Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre.

english_verse: In the Scriptures, God calls John his messenger and says, “I am sending my messenger ahead of you to get things ready for you.”
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi Gyɔn yɛna Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ kpa, ‘Mee kpila me kpilale keta n siaman, ŋon ne gyae la waa desina n gbɛɛ fa neŋ.’ ”

english_verse: Everyone had been listening to John. Even the tax collectors had obeyed God and had done what was right by letting John baptize them.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ balaŋ baŋ pou na lempoolɛɛra be nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, be lɛɛwɔ do nyi Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ, nawolo nyi be sɛɛwɔ Gyɔn sɔɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ.

english_verse: But the Pharisees and the experts in the Law of Moses refused to obey God and be baptized by John.
translation_verse: Mɔna Farasiitena na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ bɛɛ kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ ne gyae nyi waa yɛɛ fa wɔ nɛ, nawolo nyi be bɛɛwɔ nyi Gyɔn na sɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ ya.

english_verse: Jesus went on to say: What are you people like? What kind of people are you?
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔsewɔ nyiaa, “Woŋ te maa mo gyɛŋ wee kei balaŋ maa na? Sena te be doo?

english_verse: You are like children sitting in the market and shouting to each other, “We played the flute, but you would not dance! We sang a funeral song, but you would not cry!”
translation_verse: Be yɛɛ ŋgba bia kyaa la kebu man bee yoi nɛ, na bɛɛ fae yeesa yako dɔŋa nyiaa, ‘De kpee alowɔle nɔɔneŋse fa ŋon, mɔna ɛ te sɔe ya! De kpee lia nɔɔneŋse mɔ, mɔna ɛ te wii ya!’

english_verse: John the Baptist did not go around eating and drinking, and you said, “John has a demon in him!”
translation_verse: Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon kɔŋawɔ na ɔ ne kyaa bake ɔ nɔɔ keŋ ɔ te nyɔɔ soloŋ kamasɛ ya. Te ɛ kpa nyi, ‘Feliŋkumɛɛ doola ɔ man.’

english_verse: But because the Son of Man goes around eating and drinking, you say, “Jesus eats and drinks too much! He is even a friend of tax collectors and sinners.”
translation_verse: Maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon mɔ kɔŋawɔ mee dii mɛɛ nyɔɔ, te ɛ kpa nyi, ‘Ɛ kɛɛ balee kei! Ɔ yɛɛ kedikumte na soloŋnyɔɔre e. Ɔ gyoona yɛna lempoolɛɛra na nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra.’

english_verse: Yet Wisdom is shown to be right by what its followers do. Simon the Pharisee
translation_verse: Mɔna baŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di nɛ, ta lese wola nyi Wurubuarɛ dana nyansa nideli.” Alo dukumyɛɛre ŋolo ke tiu Yesu nuŋ

english_verse: A Pharisee invited Jesus to have dinner with him. So Jesus went to the Pharisee's home and got ready to eat.
translation_verse: Wee daale nɛ Farasii baale ŋolo baake Yesu nyi waa kɔŋ ɔ dɛɛ na ŋon na e ke di bom. Yesu sɛɛwɔ te ɔ gyuuwɔ ke kyaa bee dii.

english_verse: When a sinful woman in that town found out that Jesus was there, she bought an expensive bottle of perfume.
translation_verse: Na alo ŋolo kyaa donɔɔ kenaŋ man keŋ ɔ yɛɛ futooli alo e. Ɔ kenyii nyi Yesu kyaa Farasii baale ŋon dɛɛ ɔ ne dii nɛ, ɔ moo nuŋ ofoŋofoŋte daale porontia tɔɔ kɔŋ botɔɔ,

english_verse: Then she came and stood behind Jesus. She cried and started washing his feet with her tears and drying them with her hair. The woman kissed his feet and poured the perfume on them.
translation_verse: keŋte ɔ kaa seŋawɔ Yesu wɔle benaa na ɔ nawɔɔ. Keŋte ɔ wii siasaraŋ woli Yesu nawɔɔ. Te ɔ moo ɔ nyeenyɔɔse feefo Yesu nawɔɔ ŋenaŋ te ɔ kyaŋa ŋa obuo man, te ɔ toosi nuŋ ofoŋofoŋte keŋ woli ŋa.

english_verse: The Pharisee who had invited Jesus saw this and said to himself, “If this man really were a prophet, he would know what kind of woman is touching him! He would know that she is a sinner.”
translation_verse: Farasii baale ŋon ɔ be baake Yesu gyu ɔ dɛɛ kena mena nɛ, ɔ yakowɔ ɔ nyeeman nyiaa, “Akpaa balee kei deŋ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre gbagba e na, nafɔ waa gyeŋ alo ŋon ɔ ne tina e nɛ dinɔɔ. Nawolo nyi ɔ yɛɛ dukumyɛɛre e.”

english_verse: Jesus said to the Pharisee, “Simon, I have something to say to you.” “Teacher, what is it?” Simon replied.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Simɔn, me dana kolo maa yako neŋ.” Keŋte ɔ yako e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, yako maŋ.”

english_verse: But Jesus told the woman, “Because of your faith, you are now saved. May God give you peace!”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako alo ŋon nyiaa, “N kelɛɛdi te yeli n te nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa, ta wosefɛɛreŋ man.”

english_verse: Since neither of them could pay him back, the moneylender said that they didn't have to pay him anything. Which one of them will like him more?”
translation_verse: Be man kamasɛ bɛɛ tale waa tɔ ɔ kom ya, mena dɔɔ balee ŋon moo ba koma ŋan pou kyɛɛ wɔ. Yako maŋ! Balaŋ bala kɛwɔ na, be man woŋti ne gyae la waa gyae kɔbate ŋon nombia nideli?”

english_verse: He turned toward the woman and said to Simon, “Have you noticed this woman? When I came into your home, you didn't give me any water so I could wash my feet. But she has washed my feet with her tears and dried them with her hair.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu besena ɔ sia tee alo ŋon te ɔ yako Simɔn nyiaa, “N naa alo kei? Me kɔŋa n dɛɛ na n te fa maŋ loŋ maa faafo me nawɔɔ ya. Mɔna alo kei dɔɔ ŋon ta mo ɔ siasaraŋ faafo me nawɔɔ te ɔ moo ɔ nyeenyɔɔse feefo ŋa.

english_verse: You didn't greet me with a kiss, but from the time I came in, she has not stopped kissing my feet.
translation_verse: N te pukaa maŋ ya, mɔna lee debaŋ keŋ mɔɔ kɔŋ kɛbo nɛ, alo kei te tinaa ta me nawɔɔ ke pukaa yela ya.

english_verse: You didn't even pour olive oil on my head, but she has poured expensive perfume on my feet.
translation_verse: N ta mo nuŋ paŋpaŋ gba tiu ma nyee ya, mɔna alo kei dɔɔ ŋon kyɛɛkee nuŋ woli me nawɔɔ.

english_verse: So I tell you that all her sins are forgiven, and that is why she has shown great love. But anyone who has been forgiven for only a little will show only a little love.”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ yako neŋ n ke nyii nyi ɔ nombiakumɛɛ ŋan pou ɔ be yɛɛ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ ta mo ŋa kyɛɛ e, mena dɔɔ te ɔ ne gyae me nombia nideli. Nyaŋ ŋon ŋolo da mo n dukum kyomii kyɛɛ neŋ na, n ne gyae ɔtenate nombia bo kyomii.”

english_verse: Then Jesus said to the woman, “Your sins are forgiven.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako alo ŋon nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ ta mo n nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ neŋ.”

english_verse: Some other guests started saying to one another, “Who is this who dares to forgive sins?”
translation_verse: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ bee dii nɛ bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, “Amɔte yɛna kei, ŋon ɔ na mo nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ?”

english_verse: and so were some women who had been healed of evil spirits and all sorts of diseases. One of the women was Mary Magdalene, who once had seven demons in her.
translation_verse: Na ala baale baŋ ɔ be kyɔ wɔ be kaweese te ɔ be gegi feliŋkumɛɛ lee be baale man nɛ mɔ kpuɛ na e. Be man baale yɛna Mɛɛre ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Magedala, ŋon Yesu be gegi feliŋkumɛɛ nyetooro lee ɔ man nɛ,

english_verse: The seeds that fell on rocky ground are the people who gladly hear the message and accept it. But they don't have deep roots, and they believe only for a little while. As soon as life gets hard, they give up.
translation_verse: Bia ŋan be yale kpanaŋse dɔɔ nɛ seŋɛɛ fa balaŋ baŋ bee nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan te bɛɛ lɛɛ ŋa na gyoŋ, mɔna ŋe bee nyiŋ lila be man ya. Bɛɛ lɛɛ ŋa di debaŋ kyomii bo, te akpaa ɔbɔnsam de kɛɛse wɔ kɛɛ na ba te yale.

english_verse: Other seeds fell on rocky ground and started growing. But the plants did not have enough water and soon dried up.
translation_verse: Ŋaale mɔ yalawɔ kpanaŋse dɔɔ te ŋe tuuwɔ, mɔna te kyare ya te ŋa yekeewɔ. Nawolo nyi tɛɛle ta boo kpanaŋse ŋan dɔɔ na ke lɛɛ loŋ see ya.

english_verse: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked the plants.
translation_verse: Bia ŋan ŋaale mɔ yalawɔ kpalamse man te ŋe tuuwɔ, te kpalamse ŋan mɔ tuuwɔ milii ŋa.

english_verse: So he answered: I have explained the secrets about God's kingdom to you. But for others I use stories, so they will look, but not see, and they will hear, but not understand. Jesus Explains the Story about a Farmer (Matthew
translation_verse: Te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ ta toro ya agyueŋse na ɛ ka gyeŋ nombia ŋan weesɛɛ lee ɔ gyoori keŋ wose man. Mɔna balaŋ akaŋ baŋ dɔɔ aduya kaageŋ man te maa kolosi fa wɔ. ‘Baa kɛo na baa kɛo mɔna bɔɔ gyae baa naa ya, te baa tei na baa tei mɔna bɔɔ gyae baa nyii asɛɛ ya.’ ”

english_verse: There is nothing hidden that will not be found. There is no secret that will not be well known.
translation_verse: Kolo ba weesɛɛ keŋ be gyae ka lee debɔɔ ya, te kolo mɔ be wuɛ keŋ nyi be gyae ka lee debɔɔ ya.

english_verse: and while they were sailing across, he went to sleep. Suddenly a storm struck the lake, and the boat started sinking. They were in danger.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ be kyaa degbele keŋ man bee gyu nɛ te dosoroŋ moo Yesu. Keŋte feliŋ dinaa daale korowɔ ne gyɔɔ loŋ keŋ dɔɔ, mena dɔɔ ke gyɔɔ loŋ do degbele keŋ man te Yesu kaseela baŋ sia yɛɛ wɔ nideli.

english_verse: This is what the story means: The seed is God's message,
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, dudu keŋ mɔɔ gyɔɔ nɛ ka asɛɛ yaa. “Abɔɔ bia ŋan seŋɛɛ fa Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan.

english_verse: The seeds that fell among the thornbushes are also people who hear the message. But they are so eager for riches and pleasures that they never produce anything.
translation_verse: Bia ŋan be yale kpalamse man nɛ seŋɛɛ fa balaŋ baŋ bee nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan, te tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nombia, na kɔba kegyae beiŋ, na tɛɛle kei dɔɔ abɔɔ akaŋ ŋan kegyae da gyoo be man nɛ, na bee yeli baa bee Kristo man ya.

english_verse: Those seeds that fell on good ground are the people who listen to the message and keep it in good and honest hearts. They last and produce a harvest. Light (Mark
translation_verse: Abɔɔ bia ŋan be yale tɛɛle kpaakpaa dɔɔ nɛ seŋɛɛ fa balaŋ baŋ bee nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan, te bɛɛ lɛɛ ŋa do konɔɔ kpaakpaa man, te bɛɛ tela ŋa nideli te bɛɛ yɛɛ kakyeŋ di ŋe dɔɔ kelii debaŋ keŋ nombia ŋan ne yeli bɛɛ yɛɛ abɔɔ kpaakpaa nɛ.” Yesu kegyɔ dudu lee kanea wose man Maake

english_verse: Then Jesus asked the disciples, “Don't you have any faith?” But they were frightened and amazed. They said to each other, “Who is this? He can give orders to the wind and the waves, and they obey him!” A Man with Demons in Him (Matthew
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Weera dɔɔ te ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ maŋ di ya?” Gyakoloŋ kyaŋa wɔ te ba wose yeŋa wɔ te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, “Walaŋ woŋti yɛna kei? Ɔ ne kolosi fa feliŋ na loŋ koraŋ na ŋee nyii e.” Yesu kekyɔ walaŋ ŋon feliŋkumɛɛ doo ɔ man nɛ Mateo

english_verse: No one lights a lamp and puts it under a bowl or under a bed. A lamp is always put on a lampstand, so people who come into a house will see the light.
translation_verse: Yesu besewɔ gyɔ dudu fa wɔ nyiaa, “Ŋolo bɛɛ kyaŋee kanea na waa mo kolo wu ke dɔɔ yaa waa mo ke do mpa ateta ya. Mɔna ɔ na mo ke bo gyakaa kolo dɔɔ adido na kena dekpaŋalaŋ pou, na baŋ bɛɛ kɔŋ na baa nyiŋ naa.

english_verse: But Jesus answered, “Someone touched me, because I felt power going out from me.”
translation_verse: Mɔna Yesu yakowɔ nyiaa, “Ŋolo te tina maŋ. Nawolo nyi ma te naa nyi doŋ daale ta lee me man.”

english_verse: When Jesus heard this, he told Jairus, “Don't worry! Have faith, and your daughter will get well.”
translation_verse: Yesu ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ ɔ yako Gyariose nyiaa, “Na yee ya, nyaŋ lɛɛ maŋ di. N bu ŋon waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ.”

english_verse: They begged Jesus not to send them to the deep pit, where they would be punished.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te feliŋkumɛɛ ŋan waase Yesu nyi, ɔ na gegi wɔ ka do bɔɔ keŋ betaŋ ɔto ya nɛ man ya.

english_verse: The people went out to see what had happened, and when they came to Jesus, they also found the man. The demons had gone out of him, and he was sitting there at the feet of Jesus. He had clothes on and was in his right mind. But the people were terrified.
translation_verse: Keŋte balaŋ yeuwɔ gyu be kaa kɛɛ kpene keŋ be kɔŋ nɛ. Ba ke kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ nɛ, be naa walaŋ ŋon nafɔ feliŋkumɛɛ doo ɔ man nɛ baŋɛɛ Yesu nawɔɔ tɛɛ, na ɔ wose te fɛɛ e keŋ ɔ doona abɔɔ. Te gyakoloŋ gyoo balaŋ baŋ pou.

english_verse: Jesus' mother and brothers went to see him, but because of the crowd they could not get near him.
translation_verse: Yesu ɔ naa na ɔ naabia kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ, mɔna balaŋ dikpii keŋ dɔɔ be te nyiŋ baa benaa e ya.

english_verse: As Jesus was getting out of the boat, he was met by a man from this town. The man had demons in him. He had gone naked for a long time and no longer lived in a house, but in the graveyard.
translation_verse: Yesu ka betaa lee degbele keŋ man pɛ, te baale ŋolo ŋon feliŋkumɛɛ doo ɔ man nɛ leewɔ donɔɔ keŋ man kɔŋ kaa gyaŋee na e. Ɔ te tɛɛ diteŋteli kyare te ɔ bɛɛ kyaa dɛɛ ya. Ɔ kyae amuseese man.

english_verse: Just then the man in charge of the synagogue came and knelt down in front of Jesus. His name was Jairus, and he begged Jesus to come to his home
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te balee ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake nyi Gyariose, ŋon ɔ yɛɛ Gyudatena ɔsom deni man kegyia e nɛ kɔŋawɔ kaa buŋ Yesu nawɔɔ tɛɛ sola e nyi waa kɔŋ ɔ dɛɛ.

english_verse: One day, Jesus and his disciples got into a boat, and he said, “Let's cross the lake.” They started out,
translation_verse: Wee daale nɛ Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Ɛ yela dɛɛ toŋ gyu boo keŋ diŋgyiŋ.” Mena dɔɔ ba gyoowɔ degbele man te be laŋawɔ.

english_verse: So they went to Jesus and woke him up, “Master, Master! We are about to drown!” Jesus got up and ordered the wind and waves to stop. They obeyed, and everything was calm.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Yesu kaseela baŋ gyuuwɔ ke gyuusu e yako e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, de Gbeŋgyoo dɛɛ gyae dee munu.” Botɔɔ te Yesu korowɔ te ɔ fae feliŋ keŋ na loŋ keŋ pou te ŋe yɛɛwɔ yididi.

english_verse: The man saw Jesus and screamed. He knelt down in front of him and shouted, “Jesus, Son of God Most High, what do you want with me? I beg you not to torture me!”
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ ɔ be naa Yesu nɛ ɔ fae yeesa te ɔ leewɔ yala ɔ nawɔɔ tɛɛ, na ɔ ne fae yeesa keŋkeŋ nyiaa, “Yesu Wurubuarɛ Adidote Bu, woŋ te n ne gyae me gyaŋ? Mɛɛ waase neŋ bo, na naase maŋ diyem ya.”

english_verse: When the men taking care of the pigs saw this, they ran to spread the news in the town and on the farms.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ kɛɛ kekuiriise baŋ dɔɔ kena kpene keŋ be kɔŋ nɛ, ba korowɔ yeu gyu ke yako kpene keŋ be kɔŋ nɛ fa balaŋ baŋ be kyaa donɔɔ keŋ man nɛ na akuraase ŋan man pou.

english_verse: He said this because Jesus had already told the evil spirit to go out of him. The man had often been attacked by the demon. And even though he had been bound with chains and leg irons and kept under guard, he smashed whatever bound him. Then the demon would force him out into lonely places.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi na Yesu taŋ ke fae feliŋkum keŋ nyi ke dɛɛ lee ɔ man. Debaŋ kamasɛ na feliŋkum keŋ ne naase e diyem, na ba te kyaŋ e bake e agbaraagbaraase ɔ nyiŋmaase na ɔ nawɔɔ man na balaŋ ne gyee e. Keŋ koraŋ na ɔ ne tirii ŋa, na feliŋkum keŋ ta mo e gyu kefula man.

english_verse: Jesus asked the man, “What is your name?” He answered, “My name is Lots.” He said this because there were “lots” of demons in him.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse e nyiaa, “N yele yɛna sena?” Te balee ŋon tiranɔɔ nyi ma yele yɛna “Dikpii” Ɔ tiranɔɔ mena nawolo nyi feliŋkumɛɛ burum doo ɔ man.

english_verse: A large herd of pigs was feeding there on the hillside. So the demons begged Jesus to let them go into the pigs, and Jesus let them go.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ na kekuiriise dikpii daale seŋɛɛ bula daale dɔɔ bee dii. Keŋte feliŋkumɛɛ ŋan waase Yesu nyi waa yela be kaa gyoo kekuiriise baŋ man, te Yesu faa wɔ gbɛɛ.

english_verse: Then the demons left the man and went into the pigs. The whole herd rushed down the steep bank into the lake and drowned.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ feliŋkumɛɛ ŋan leewɔ balee ŋon man ka gyoo kekuiriise baŋ man, te ba dikpii keŋ pou yeuwɔ tisi bula kyoloŋkyoloŋ keŋ gyu ka gyoo loŋ keŋ man te be munuwɔ pou.

english_verse: Then all who had seen the man healed told about it.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ naa kpene keŋ be kɔŋ nɛ yako baŋ bɔɔ kɔŋ nɛ mena keŋ Yesu be kyɔ balee ŋon kawee.

english_verse: Everyone from around Gerasa begged Jesus to leave, because they were so frightened. When Jesus got into the boat to start back,
translation_verse: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ pou be kyaa Gerasene tɛɛle dɔɔ nɛ waase Yesu nyi waa lee be gyaŋ nawolo nyi gyakoloŋ dinaa ta gyoo wɔ. Mena dɔɔ Yesu gyoowɔ degbele keŋ man te ɔ laŋawɔ.

english_verse: the man who had been healed begged to go with him. But Jesus sent him off and said,
translation_verse: Walaŋ ŋon Yesu be gegi feliŋkumɛɛ lee ɔ man nɛ waase Yesu nyi waa sila e. Mɔna Yesu bɛɛwɔ te ɔ yako e nyiaa,

english_verse: “Go back home and tell everyone how much God has done for you.” The man then went all over town, telling everything that Jesus had done for him. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Matthew
translation_verse: “Bese gyu n dɛɛ, na n kaa yako kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yɛɛ fa neŋ nɛ fa balaŋ.” Mena dɔɔ balee ŋon gyuuwɔ donɔɔ keŋ man ke yako balaŋ pou kpene keŋ Yesu be yɛɛ fa e nɛ. Yesu kegyuusu Gyariose bu alebu lee yeŋ man Mateo

english_verse: In the crowd was a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years. She had spent everything she had on doctors, but none of them could make her well.
translation_verse: Na alo ŋolo kpuɛ balaŋ burum baŋ be silana Yesu nɛ man keŋ fatabo ne gyɔ e kulutooneŋ kufu ala yaa, mɔna ŋolo te tale kyɔ e ya.

english_verse: Everyone had been waiting for Jesus, and when he came back, a crowd was there to welcome him.
translation_verse: Yesu ka bese lee boo keŋ diŋgyiŋ kɔŋ nɛ, balaŋ burum kɔŋawɔ kaa gyaŋee na e. Nawolo nyi na be popou ne kɛɛ ɔ gbɛɛ.

english_verse: As soon as she came up behind Jesus and barely touched his clothes, her bleeding stopped.
translation_verse: Alo ŋon moowɔ balaŋ baŋ man ka lee Yesu wɔle te ɔ tinaa ɔ kegba nɔɔbii. Debokenaŋ man te fatabo keŋ ne gyɔ e nɛ karawɔ.

english_verse: “Who touched me?” Jesus asked. While everyone was denying it, Peter said, “Master, people are crowding all around and pushing you from every side.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔsewɔ nyiaa, “Amɔte tinaa na maŋ?” Balaŋ baŋ pou kebɛɛ nyi be te tina e ya nɛ te Pita yako e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, balaŋ burum ke kilisi neŋ gyaŋee te bɛɛ tɛɛse dɔŋa kei na, n ne bɔɔse nyi amɔte tinaa na neŋ?”

english_verse: The woman knew that she could not hide, so she came trembling and knelt down in front of Jesus. She told everyone why she had touched him and that she had been healed at once.
translation_verse: Alo ŋon kena nyi ɔ be gyae waa tale weese ɔ wose ya nɛ, ɔ moo gyakoloŋ na wose keseree kaa buŋ Yesu nawɔɔ tɛɛ. Botɔɔ te ɔ yako kpene keŋ dɔɔ ɔ be tina Yesu kegba, na mena keŋ ɔ kawee keŋ be taŋ debokenaŋ man nɛ balaŋ baŋ pou siaman.

english_verse: Jesus said to the woman, “You are now well because of your faith. May God give you peace!”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako alo ŋon nyiaa, “Me bu, n kelɛɛ maŋ di dɔɔ te n nyiŋa kawee kekyɔ. Ta wosefɛɛreŋ man.”

english_verse: While Jesus was speaking, someone came from Jairus' home and said, “Your daughter has died! Why bother the teacher anymore?”
translation_verse: Yesu ke gyakaa ɔ ne kolosi nɛ, walaŋ ŋolo lee Gyudatena ɔsom deni man kegyia Gyariose ŋon dɛɛ kaa yako e nyiaa, “N bu alebu ŋon ta yem. Na tekaa naase de Gbeŋgyoo ŋon diyem bela ya.”

english_verse: Jesus went into the house, but he did not let anyone else go with him, except Peter, John, James, and the girl's father and mother.
translation_verse: Yesu ke gyu ka gyoo Gyariose dɛɛ nɛ, ɔ te yeli ŋolo ke sila e gyoo deni keŋ man ya. Pita na Gyɔn na Gyemisi na bu ŋon ɔ kya na ɔ naa baageŋ te ɔ moowɔ sila ɔ wose.

english_verse: Everyone was crying and weeping for the girl. But Jesus said, “The child isn't dead. She is just asleep.”
translation_verse: Na balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ yilaa botɔɔ nɛ pou ne wii na bee di walaŋsa. Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ na wii ya, alebu kei ta yeŋa ta ya, ɔ doo dosoroŋ bo.”

english_verse: The people laughed at him because they knew she was dead.
translation_verse: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ pou boŋo Yesu, nawolo nyi ba gyeŋ nyi bu ŋon ta yem.

english_verse: Jesus took hold of the girl's hand and said, “Child, get up!”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu kyaŋa alebu ŋon nyiŋmaa man te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Me bu! Koro seŋ.”

english_verse: She came back to life and got right up. Jesus told them to give her something to eat.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te bu ŋon woo besewɔ kɔŋ kaa gyoo ɔ man te ɔ korowɔ seŋ debokenaŋ man. Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyi baa gyae kolo fa bu ŋon na waa di.

english_verse: Her parents were surprised, but Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened.
translation_verse: Gyakoloŋ gyoo bu ŋon ɔ kya na ɔ naa, te Yesu tɔɔ wɔ nideli nyi be na yako walaŋ na walaŋ kpene keŋ be kɔŋ nɛ ya.

english_verse: The apostles came back and told Jesus everything they had done. He then took them with him to the village of Bethsaida, where they could be alone.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ kpilala kufu bala baŋ ba bese kɔŋ nɛ, be yako Yesu kpene kamasɛ keŋ bɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ. Keŋte Yesu baake wɔ te ŋon na wɔ gyuu donɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi Bɛtesaida nɛ man.

english_verse: They did this, and all the people sat down.
translation_verse: Keŋte kpilala baŋ yela balaŋ baŋ pou kyakeewɔ ŋgba mena keŋ Yesu be yako nɛ.

english_verse: Herod the ruler heard about all that was happening, and he was worried. Some people were saying John the Baptist had come back to life.
translation_verse: Galeliatena Gyoo dinaa Herode ke nyii abɔɔ ŋan pou Yesu be yɛɛ nɛ ŋe haa e ɔ konɔɔ man, nawolo nyi balaŋ baale ne yako nyi, “Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon ɔ ba yem nɛ foŋala lee yeŋ man.

english_verse: He told them, “Don't take anything with you! Don't take a walking stick or a traveling bag or food or money or even a change of clothes.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ ne gyu na ɛ na mo kolo na kolo tela ya. Ɛ na mo gbutu yaa akpo yaa weenɛɛ, yaa kɔba, yaa kegba alate gba tela ya.

english_verse: When you are welcomed into a home, stay there until you leave that town.
translation_verse: Dekpaŋalaŋ kamasɛ keŋ man ɛ soo te be lɛɛ ŋon atuu na, ɛ kyaa botɔɔ kaboena ɛ ka mo lee donɔɔ kenaŋ man.

english_verse: Jesus strictly warned his disciples not to tell anyone about this. Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death (Matthew
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu tɔɔ wɔ nideli nyi be na yako nombii kei fa walaŋ na walaŋ ya. Yesu kekolosi lee ɔ yeŋ na ɔ kefoŋ wose man Mateo

english_verse: “Pay close attention to what I am telling you! The Son of Man will be handed over to his enemies.”
translation_verse: “Ɛ nyii nombia ŋan mɛɛ gyae maa yako ŋon nɛ nideli. Baa lese maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon do balaŋ nyiŋmaa man.”

english_verse: But a lot of people found out about this and followed him. Jesus welcomed them. He spoke about God's kingdom and healed everyone who was sick.
translation_verse: Balaŋ dikpii keŋ kenyii nyi ɔ te lam botɔɔ nɛ be sila ɔ gbɛɛ. Be ke gyu kena nɛ ɔ lɛɛ wɔ atuu te ɔ kolosi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ wose man nombia fa wɔ, te ɔ kyɔɔ baŋ be hia kawee kekyɔ nɛ.

english_verse: “Don't stop him!” Jesus said. “Anyone who isn't against you is for you.” A Samaritan Village Refuses To Receive Jesus
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Ɛ na tɔ e gbɛɛ ya, nawolo nyi ŋon ɔ bee yoo na ŋon ya nɛ, na ɔ seŋɛɛ ɛ wɔle.” Samariatena kebɛɛ Yesu

english_verse: About eight days later Jesus took Peter, John, and James with him and went up on a mountain to pray.
translation_verse: Yesu keyako nombia kɛŋa weeya gyanaara wɔle nɛ, ɔ moo Pita na Gyemisi na Gyɔn sila ɔ wose gyu bula dɔɔ ɔ kaa fane.

english_verse: If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me, you will save it.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne gyae waa lɛɛ ŋon gbagba nyeedoŋ na, ke gyae ka lee ɔ nyiŋmaa man. Mɔna walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ nyeedoŋ da lee ɔ nyiŋmaa man lee maŋ Yesu dɔɔ nɛ waa bese nyiŋ ke.

english_verse: What will you gain, if you own the whole world but destroy yourself or waste your life?
translation_verse: Akpaa ŋolo de nyiŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ abɔɔ pou te ɔ nyeedoŋ da lee ɔ nyiŋmaa man na, woŋ tɔnɔɔ te waa nyiŋ?

english_verse: If you are ashamed of me and my message, the Son of Man will be ashamed of you when he comes in his glory and in the glory of his Father and the holy angels.
translation_verse: Akpaa maŋ Yesu na me nombia de yɛɛ ŋolo desɛŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, kenaŋ na wee keŋ maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa bese kɔŋ daa na me kya Wurubuarɛ na Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ gyoorobiiri man nɛ, walaŋ ŋonaŋ mɔ nombia gyae ke yɛɛ maŋ desɛŋ.

english_verse: They appeared in heavenly glory and talked about all that Jesus' death in Jerusalem would mean.
translation_verse: Be kɔŋawɔ gyoorobiiri man kaa yako Yesu mena keŋ waa yeŋ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man, ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ be bake see nɛ.

english_verse: ) While everyone was still amazed at what Jesus was doing, he said to his disciples,
translation_verse: Mena debaŋ kenaŋ na Pita na ɔ tebia baŋ bɔɔ sila Yesu gyu nɛ te doo dosoroŋ. Ba ke foŋii nɛ, be naa Yesu gyoorobiiri man na baala bala seŋɛɛ ɔ gyaŋ.

english_verse: While Peter was still speaking, a shadow from a cloud passed over, and they were frightened as the cloud covered them.
translation_verse: Pita kɛɛ kolosi keŋ nɛ te ŋɔma kaa wuuwɔ be dɔɔ, keŋte gyakoloŋ kyaŋa kaseela baŋ ŋgba ŋɔma ŋan be kaa wuu be dɔɔ nɛ.

english_verse: From the cloud a voice spoke, “This is my chosen Son. Listen to what he says!”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te be nyii nɔɔwoya ŋaale lee ŋɔma ŋan man nyiaa, “Kei yɛna me bu ŋon mɔɔ lese e, ɛ nyii e.”

english_verse: After the voice had spoken, Peter, John, and James saw only Jesus. For some time they kept quiet and did not say anything about what they had seen. Jesus Heals a Boy (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɔɔwoya ŋan kekolosi taŋ nɛ, be kɛɛ na Yesu waageŋ seŋɛɛ la te kaseela baŋ wuu be nɔɔ. Ba te yako walaŋ kamasɛ kpene keŋ bɔɔ naa weeya ŋenaŋ man nɛ ya. Yesu kekyɔ tobaale ŋon ɔ na wee keleuleuse nɛ Mateo

english_verse: A demon often attacks him and makes him scream. It shakes him until he foams at the mouth, and it won't leave him until it has completely worn the boy out.
translation_verse: Feliŋkum ne kyaa ɔ dɔɔ na ɔ te peti fae yeesa na te fuŋ e ŋmera na ɔ nɔɔ ne tɔ afofose. Ne yɛɛ doŋ pɛte kɛɛ koro ɔ dɔɔ, mɔna ke gyakaa kɛɛ wɔlɛɛ e.

english_verse: Jesus said to them, “You people are stubborn and don't have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you?” Then Jesus said to the man, “Bring your son to me.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Ao! Ɛ mɛɛ gyɛŋ balaŋ baŋ ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ maŋ di ya te ɛ gbɛɛneŋ ba tenɛɛ ya. Weeya aŋmoŋ te kaboena nyi maa kyaa ɛ gyaŋ na maa nyiŋ konɔɔ fa ŋon?” Keŋte ɔ yako balee ŋon nyi, “Mo n bu ŋon kɔna maŋ.”

english_verse: While the boy was being brought, the demon attacked him and made him shake all over. Jesus ordered the demon to stop. Then he healed the boy and gave him back to his father.
translation_verse: Bu ŋon kɛɛ kɔŋ nɛ, feliŋkum keŋ doo ɔ man nɛ kara e lɔ na ne wɔŋee e tokii. Mɔna Yesu fae feliŋkum keŋ te ɔ kyɔɔ bu ŋon kawee te ɔ besena e fa ɔ kya.

english_verse: But the disciples did not know what he meant. The meaning was hidden from them. They could not understand it, and they were afraid to ask. Who Is the Greatest? (Matthew
translation_verse: Mɔna ɔ kaseela baŋ te nyii kpene keŋ ɔ ne yako wɔ nɛ man ya. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ ta lese wola wɔ na baa nyii asɛɛ ya, mɔna bee yee nyi baa bɔɔse e nombii kenaŋ man. Amɔte kela na ɔ dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man? Mateo

english_verse: Jesus' disciples were arguing about which one of them was the greatest.
translation_verse: Keŋte aŋmaareŋ gyoo Yesu kaseela baŋ man nyi be man woŋti kela na ɔ dɔɔ.

english_verse: Jesus knew what they were thinking, and he had a child stand there beside him.
translation_verse: Na Yesu gyeŋ kpene keŋ bɛɛ gyueŋ nɛ te ɔ kyaŋa biikyaabii ŋolo kaa seŋ ɔ yenaŋ,

english_verse: Then he said to his disciples, “When you welcome even a child because of me, you welcome me. And when you welcome me, you welcome the one who sent me. Whichever one of you is the most humble is the greatest.” For or against Jesus (Mark
translation_verse: keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Walaŋ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ biikyaabii kei ma yele man na ɔ te lɛɛ maŋ, te ŋon ɔ lɛɛ maŋ na ɔ te lɛɛ ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ. Nawolo nyi walaŋ ŋon ɛ bɛɛ mo e yɛɛ kolo ɛ man ya nɛ, ŋon kela na ɛ popou.”

english_verse: John said, “Master, we saw a man using your name to force demons out of people. But we told him to stop, because he isn't one of us.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Gyɔn yako Yesu nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, de naa balee ŋolo na ɔ ne gegi feliŋkumɛɛ lee n yele man te de tɔɔ e, nawolo nyi ɔ be kpuɛ de man ya.”

english_verse: Not long before it was time for Jesus to be taken up to heaven, he made up his mind to go to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Weeya ŋan Wurubuarɛ waa mo Yesu gyu adido kɛɛ benaa nɛ, ɔ yɛɛ wa gyueŋ nyi waa gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man.

english_verse: He sent some messengers on ahead to a Samaritan village to get things ready for him.
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ kpila ɔ kaseela baale nyi baa kyaŋ gbɛɛ gyu Samaria donɔɔ daale man na be kaa desina botɔɔ see e.

english_verse: But he was on his way to Jerusalem, so the people there refused to welcome him.
translation_verse: Mɔna balaŋ baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ bɛe nyi bɔɔ lɛɛ e ya, nawolo nyi na ɔ sia gyakae nyi ɔ ne gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man.

english_verse: When the disciples James and John saw what was happening, they asked, “Lord, do you want us to call down fire from heaven to destroy these people?”
translation_verse: Ɔ kaseela Gyemisi na Gyɔn kena mena nɛ be bɔɔse e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, n ne gyae dɛɛ baake boalaŋ lee adido na kaa kpa wɔ wɔlɛɛ?”

english_verse: But Jesus turned and corrected them for what they had said.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu besenawɔ kɛɛ wɔ te ɔ faree wɔ nyi be naa yɛɛ mena ya.

english_verse: Then they all went on to another village. Three People Who Wanted To Be Followers (Matthew
translation_verse: Keŋte be popou gbaawɔ gyu akuraa wɔle man. Balaŋ baŋ be kaboena nyi baa sila Yesu Mateo

english_verse: Jesus said, “Foxes have dens, and birds have nests, but the Son of Man doesn't have a place to call his own.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Nyanyase dana bɔɔneŋ te gyebuse mɔ dana asaase, mɔna Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon betaŋ botɔɔ keŋ waa mo ɔ nyee teeli ya.”

english_verse: Jesus told someone else to come with him. But the man said, “Lord, let me wait until I bury my father.”
translation_verse: Walaŋ wɔle ŋolo mɔ kɔŋawɔ Yesu gyaŋ te Yesu yako e nyi, “Sila maŋ.” Mɔna walaŋ ŋon yako e nyiaa, “Me Gbeŋgyoo, yela maa taŋgbɛɛ gyu kewuu me kya pɛna me kaa sila neŋ.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Let the dead take care of the dead, while you go and tell about God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Yela woya kewuu ba woya, mɔna nyaŋ dɔɔ gyu na n kaa kolosi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ wose man nombia.”

english_verse: Then someone said to Jesus, “I want to follow you, Lord, but first let me go back and take care of things at home.”
translation_verse: Ŋolo mɔ yako Yesu nyiaa, “Me Gbeŋgyoo maa sila neŋ, mɔna yela maa gyu kekalaa ma loŋtona pɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Anyone who starts plowing and keeps looking back isn't worth a thing to God's kingdom!”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Nyaŋ walaŋ ŋon n buŋɛɛ fare dɔɔ te n ne gɛɛse kɛɛ n wɔle nɛ, n tekaboena Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man ka gyoo ya.”

english_verse: followers returned, they were excited and said, “Lord, even the demons obeyed when we spoke in your name!”
translation_verse: ) te ɔ kpila wɔ bala bala nyi, baa kyaŋ gbɛɛ gyu donɔɔse na yenaase ŋan pou man ŋon gbagba ne gyae waa gyu nɛ.

english_verse: He said to them: A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers. Ask the Lord in charge of the harvest to send out workers to bring it in.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Wɔɔman weenɛɛ ŋan boe na ke tekerii, mɔna balaŋ baŋ baa tekerii nɛ ta boo ya. Mena dɔɔ ɛ waase wɔɔte ŋon na waa kpila balaŋ burum kɔŋ wɔɔ keŋ man na be kaa tekerii.

english_verse: Now go, but remember, I am sending you like lambs into a pack of wolves.
translation_verse: Ɛ ta! Mee kpila ŋon baa ŋgba namensebia ne gyuula gbougbouse man nɛ.

english_verse: Don't take along a moneybag or a traveling bag or sandals. And don't waste time greeting people on the road.
translation_verse: Ɛ ne gyu na ɛ na mo kɔba yaa akpo yaa nkyokota ya. Te ɛ na wɔlɛɛ debaŋ na balaŋ nɔɔ kado gbɛɛnaa man ya.

english_verse: As soon as you enter a home, say, “God bless this home with peace.”
translation_verse: Dekpaŋalaŋ kamasɛ keŋ man ɛ gyoo na, ɛ taŋgbɛɛ yako dekpaŋalaŋmantena baŋ nyi, ‘Wosefɛɛreŋ dɛɛkɔŋ dekpaŋalaŋ kei man.’

english_verse: If the people living there are peace-loving, your prayer for peace will bless them. But if they are not peace-loving, your prayer will return to you.
translation_verse: Akpaa walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne gyae wosefɛɛreŋ kyaa dekpaŋalaŋ kenaŋ man na, ɛ wosefɛɛreŋ gyae ke kyaa na e. Te akpaa walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne gyae wosefɛɛreŋ be kyaa botɔɔ ya na, ɛ wosefɛɛreŋ keŋ gyae ka bese kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ.

english_verse: Stay with the same family, eating and drinking whatever they give you, because workers are worth what they earn. Don't move around from house to house.
translation_verse: Nyi be lɛɛ ŋon na ɛ kyaa dekpaŋalaŋ kenaŋ man na ɛ ke di na ɛ ke nyɔɔ kpene kamasɛ keŋ baa fa ŋon nɛ, nawolo nyi kaboena nyi baa tɔ tomyɛɛre kamasɛ kom. Ɛ na tɛɛ na akpaŋalaŋ yakaa yakaa ya.

english_verse: If the people of a town welcome you, eat whatever they offer.
translation_verse: Akpaa ɛ gyu donɔɔ daale man te be lɛɛ ŋon atuu na, ɛ di kpene kamasɛ keŋ balaŋ baŋ baa fa ŋon nɛ.

english_verse: Heal their sick and say, “God's kingdom will soon be here!”
translation_verse: Ɛ kyɔ kaweesetena kawee donɔɔ kenaŋ man, na ɛ keyako donɔɔsetena benaŋ nyi, ‘Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ ta benaa ŋon taŋ.’

english_verse: But if the people of a town refuse to welcome you, go out into the street and say,
translation_verse: Mɔna donɔɔ keŋ man ɛ gyu te ba te lɛɛ ŋon ya nɛ, ɛ gyu ka abɔnterese ŋan man na ɛ ke yako nyiaa,

english_verse: I tell you that on the day of judgment the people of Sodom will get off easier than the people of that town! The Unbelieving Towns (Matthew
translation_verse: Mɛɛ yako ŋon na ɛ ke nyii nyi wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa kɔŋ kaa di balaŋ nombia nɛ, waa wii Sodomtena waraŋase koraŋ kela mena donɔɔ kenaŋ man balaŋ.” Donɔɔse ŋan ŋe balaŋ te lɛɛ Yesu di ya nɛ Mateo

english_verse: But don't be happy because evil spirits obey you. Be happy that your names are written in heaven! Jesus Thanks His Father (Matthew
translation_verse: Ɛ na yela keyɛɛ ŋon gyoŋ nyi feliŋkumɛɛ ne nyii ŋon ya, mɔna ɛ yela keyɛɛ ŋon gyoŋ nyi ba te ŋmarase ɛ yela adido.” Yesu kefane fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ Mateo

english_verse: Many prophets and kings were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear.” The Good Samaritan
translation_verse: Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra na gyooneŋ gyaewɔ baa naa kpene keŋ ɛ ne naa nɛ mɔna ba te naa ya, te be gyaewɔ baa nyii kpene keŋ ɛ ne nyii nɛ mɔna be te nyii ya.” Yesu kegyɔ dudu lee Samaria baale ŋolo wose man

english_verse: On the day of judgment the people of Tyre and Sidon will get off easier than you will.
translation_verse: Mɔna wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa kaa di balaŋ nombia nɛ, waa wii Taetena na Sidɔntena waraŋase kela ŋon.

english_verse: People of Capernaum, do you think you will be honored in heaven? Well, you will go down to hell!
translation_verse: Te nɛnɛɛ ɛmɛɛ Kapanumtena! Ɛ gyeŋ nyi baa gyina ŋon adido gyu Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ? Koa, baa tisina ŋon ateta ateta kɔŋ botɔɔ keŋ ɛ kena diyem kekpaakekpaa.”

english_verse: Jesus told them: I saw Satan fall from heaven like a flash of lightning.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Me naa ɔbɔnsam lee adido kaa yala ŋgba ɔga ŋmaalela nɛ.

english_verse: I have given you the power to trample on snakes and scorpions and to defeat the power of your enemy Satan. Nothing can harm you.
translation_verse: Ɛ nyii, ma te fa ŋon doŋ nyi yaa mo sakatee doma na kanase dɔɔ na ɛ ke di ɛ kɔlare ŋon doŋ dɔɔ, te kolo be gyae ke tale yɛɛ ŋon ya.

english_verse: An expert in the Law of Moses stood up and asked Jesus a question to see what he would say. “Teacher,” he asked, “what must I do to have eternal life?”
translation_verse: Wee daale nɛ Gyudatena mmaraa wolale ŋolo kɔŋawɔ ɔ kaa teese Yesu nɔɔman te ɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, woŋ te maa yɛɛ na maa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ?”

english_verse: The man replied, “The Scriptures say, ‘Love the Lord your God with all your heart, soul, strength, and mind.’ They also say, ‘Love your neighbors as much as you love yourself.’ ”
translation_verse: Keŋte balee ŋon tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Be ŋmarasewɔ nyi mo n konɔɔ pou na n wose pou na n doŋ pou na nya agyueŋ pou gyae n Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ, na n ke gyae n dɔɔ ŋgba nyaŋ gbagba n wose nɛ.”

english_verse: At that same time, Jesus felt the joy that comes from the Holy Spirit, and he said: My Father, Lord of heaven and earth, I am grateful that you hid all this from wise and educated people and showed it to ordinary people. Yes, Father, this is what pleased you.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ man te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon yela gyoŋ kaa gyoowɔ Yesu man nideli te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Me kya adido na ateta pou Gbeŋgyoo! Mɛɛ fa neŋ karaŋ nawolo nyi n ta mo abɔɔ kɛŋa weese nyansatena na baŋ ba gyeŋ nombia nɛ, te n lese ŋa wola baŋ be dinɔɔ yɛɛ ŋgba bia nɛ. Taata, ma gyeŋ nyi kei yɛna nyaŋ gbagba n kegyaebii.”

english_verse: My Father has given me everything, and he is the only one who knows the Son. The only one who really knows the Father is the Son. But the Son wants to tell others about the Father, so they can know him too.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako balaŋ baŋ nyiaa, “Me kya ta mo kpene kamasɛ do maŋ me nyiŋmaa man. Ŋolo ba gyeŋ maŋ ɔ Bu ŋon ya, me kya waageŋ kooŋ gyeŋa na maŋ. Ŋolo mɔ ba gyeŋ kya ŋon ya, maŋ ɔ Bu ŋon na baŋ maa lese me kya ŋon wola wɔ nɛ daageŋ ne gyae la dɛɛ gyeŋ e.”

english_verse: Jesus then turned to his disciples and said to them in private, “You are really blessed to see what you see!
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besenawɔ kɛɛ ɔ kaseela baŋ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ te yɛɛ nyeebam ŋgba ɛ ne naa mena abɔɔ ŋan ɛ ne naa nɛ!

english_verse: Jesus answered, “What is written in the Scriptures? How do you understand them?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse e nyi, “Woŋ te be ŋmarasewɔ do Wurubuarɛ mmaraa tɔne keŋ man? Sena te ɛ ne nyii mmaraase ŋan man?”

english_verse: Jesus said, “You have given the right answer. If you do this, you will have eternal life.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, “N tiranɔɔ nideli, yɛɛ ŋgba mena keŋ n be yako nɛ na n ke nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ.”

english_verse: But the man wanted to show that he knew what he was talking about. So he asked Jesus, “Who are my neighbors?”
translation_verse: Mɔna keŋ mmaraa wolale ŋon ne gyae waa wola nyi ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ bɔɔse Yesu nyi, “Amɔte yɛna me dɔɔ?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: As a man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho, robbers attacked him and grabbed everything he had. They beat him up and ran off, leaving him half dead.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu gyɔɔ dudu daale tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Wee daale balee ŋolo lee Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man tisi ɔ ne gyu Gyeriko donɔɔ man. Keŋte ɔ gyuuwɔ kegyaŋee na ŋmɛɛlatena, te be lɛo ɔ kpene kamasɛ te be tuku e yeŋ yeŋ, te be tinaa e yela diteŋteli.

english_verse: A priest happened to be going down the same road. But when he saw the man, he walked by on the other side.
translation_verse: Te kɔŋawɔ nyi Wurubuarɛ Sae ŋolo tisiwɔ mo gbɛɛ kenaŋ ɔ ne gyu. Ɔ kena balee ŋon doo nɛ ɔ kpasewɔ mo yenaŋ laŋ.

english_verse: Later a temple helper came to the same place. But when he saw the man who had been beaten up, he also went by on the other side.
translation_verse: Mena mɔ te Lewi baale ŋolo mɔ kɔŋawɔ kaa mo botɔɔ te ɔ kena balee ŋon doo nɛ, ŋon mɔ kpasewɔ mo yenaŋ laŋ.

english_verse: A man from Samaria then came traveling along that road. When he saw the man, he felt sorry for him
translation_verse: Mɔna Samaria baale ŋolo ŋon ɔ ne gyu ɔ gbɛɛ nɛ, kaa leewɔ balee ŋon dɔɔ. Ɔ kena e keŋ nɛ ɔ tɔɔ nyakee e, te waraŋase kyaŋa e fa balee ŋon.

english_verse: and went over to him. He treated his wounds with olive oil and wine and bandaged them. Then he put him on his own donkey and took him to an inn, where he took care of him.
translation_verse: Ɔ tuuwɔ benaa e te ɔ doo e tee ɔ gyamɛɛ ŋan man te ɔ bake ŋa. Botɔɔ te ɔ moo balee ŋon gyakaa ŋon gbagba ɔ wonembu ŋon ɔ kyaa nɛ dɔɔ, te ɔ moo e gyu ŋɔɔla dɛɛsoe keŋ man nyi baa kɛɛ ɔ dɔɔ fa e.

english_verse: The next morning he gave the innkeeper two silver coins and said, “Please take care of the man. If you spend more than this on him, I will pay you when I return.”
translation_verse: Tɛɛ kena nɛ, ɔ lese kɔba fa kegyia ŋon ɔ ne kɛɛ ŋɔɔla dɛɛsoe keŋ dɔɔ nɛ te ɔ yako e nyiaa, ‘Kɛɛ balee kei dɔɔ fa maŋ. Ma bese kɔŋ na me kaa tɔ neŋ kom kamasɛ keŋ n be wɔlɛɛ ɔ dɔɔ nɛ kpu.’ ”

english_verse: Then Jesus asked, “Which one of these three people was a real neighbor to the man who was beaten up by robbers?”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse balee ŋon nyiaa, “Nya agyueŋ man na, balaŋ batooro kɛwɔ man woŋti yɛna balee ŋon ŋmɛɛlatena be tuku e nɛ ɔ dɔɔ?”

english_verse: The expert in the Law of Moses answered, “The one who showed pity.” Jesus said, “Go and do the same!” Martha and Mary
translation_verse: Keŋte mmaraa wolale ŋon tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Walaŋ ŋon ɔ be wii ɔ waraŋase nɛ.” Botɔɔ te Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Nyaŋ mɔ gyu na n kaa yɛɛ wɔe mena.” Yesu kegyu Maata na Mɛɛre dɛɛ

english_verse: The Lord and his disciples were traveling along and came to a village. When they got there, a woman named Martha welcomed him into her home.
translation_verse: Yesu na ɔ kaseela kegyakaa be gbɛɛ dɔɔ bee gyu nɛ, ɔ keliiwɔ akuraa daale man te alo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Maata nɛ moo e gyu ɔ dɛɛ.

english_verse: She had a sister named Mary, who sat down in front of the Lord and was listening to what he said.
translation_verse: Na Maata dana naabu ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Mɛɛre. Keŋte Mɛɛre kɔŋawɔ kaa kyaa de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu gyaŋ ɔ ne nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia.

english_verse: Martha was worried about all that had to be done. Finally, she went to Jesus and said, “Lord, doesn't it bother you that my sister has left me to do all the work by myself? Tell her to come and help me!”
translation_verse: Maata kɛɛ ka ɔ wose na ŋɔɔla baŋ kedi man nɛ, ɔ kɔŋawɔ kaa bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, n be famenɛ ŋgba me naabu be tina maŋ yela maageŋ yee? Yako e nyi waa kɔŋ kaa kyɔ maŋ.”

english_verse: The Lord answered, “Martha, Martha! You are worried and upset about so many things,
translation_verse: Keŋte de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Maata, Maata, n ne naase n wose diyem abɔɔ burum dɔɔ,

english_verse: but only one thing is necessary. Mary has chosen what is best, and it will not be taken away from her.”
translation_verse: mɔna kolo dokoloŋ ne hia la keŋ kaboena nyi nya yɛɛ. Mena kolo kpaakpaa kenaŋ yaa Mɛɛre moowɔ, te bɔɔ gyae baa lɛo ke lee ɔ nyiŋmaa man ya.”

english_verse: So I tell you to ask and you will receive, search and you will find, knock and the door will be opened for you.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ yako ŋon baa nyi ɛ sola Wurubuarɛ kolo na, ɛ nyiŋmaa gyae ke kaŋ ke. Ɛ kɛo kolo lee ɔ gyaŋ na ɛ ke nyiŋ ke, te ɛ gyɔ e disim mɔ na waa toro fa ŋon.

english_verse: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give the Holy Spirit to anyone who asks. Jesus and the Ruler of Demons (Matthew
translation_verse: Akpaa ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛrawɔ koraŋ gyeŋ abɔɔ kpaakpaa ka mo fa ɛ bia na, de yaa sena te ɛ kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ, ba mo ɔ feliŋ do baŋ bɛɛ sola e nɛ man nideli ya?” Yesu na Bɛlisɛbu Mateo

english_verse: And suppose your friend answers, “Don't bother me! The door is bolted, and my children and I are in bed. I cannot get up to give you something.”
translation_verse: Na n gyoo ŋon te tiranɔɔ deni tɔɔman nyi, ‘Na haa maŋ ya, me taŋ ketɔ me disim te daa na me bia mɔ taŋ kedoo. Mɔɔ gyae maa tale koro kaa fa neŋ kolo ya.’ ”

english_verse: Which one of you fathers would give your hungry child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
translation_verse: Lolala, ɛ man woŋti kyaa la keŋ nyi ɔ bu da sola e kpebii na, waa mo dom fa e?

english_verse: So Jesus told them, “Pray in this way: ‘Father, help us to honor your name. Come and set up your kingdom.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ ne gyae ɛ ke fane na ɛ yako nyiaa, ‘De kya, dɛɛ fane nyi balaŋ pou baa lese n yele Lese n gyoori debɔɔ.

english_verse: Everyone who asks will receive, everyone who searches will find, and the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ bɔɔse e kolo na ɔ nyiŋmaa ne kaŋ ke, te ŋon ɔ gyae kolo lee ɔ gyaŋ na ɔ ne nyiŋ ke, te ŋon mɔ ɔ gyɔ e disim na waa toro fa e.

english_verse: Just as Jonah was a sign to the people of Nineveh, the Son of Man will be a sign to the people of today.
translation_verse: Mena keŋ Gyona nombia ŋan ba bese kolo kekasee fa Niniwetena nɛ, mena mɔ te maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon mɔ me nombia gyae ka bese kolo kekasee fa gyɛŋ wee kei balaŋ.

english_verse: While Jesus was still talking, a woman in the crowd spoke up, “The woman who gave birth to you and nursed you is blessed!”
translation_verse: Yesu kɛɛ kolosi nombia kɛŋa nɛ, alo ŋolo gyinaa ɔ nɔɔ balaŋ baŋ man yako nyiaa, “Alo ŋon ɔ ba lola neŋ te ɔ be kɛɛle neŋ nɛ, ŋon na nyeebam.”

english_verse: Jesus forced a demon out of a man who could not talk. And after the demon had gone out, the man started speaking, and the crowds were amazed.
translation_verse: Wee daale Yesu gberaa feliŋkum keŋ ne yeli walaŋ bɛɛ tale kolosi ya nɛ lee balee ŋolo man. Feliŋkum keŋ ka lee nɛ, balee ŋon kolosiwɔ te doo balaŋ dikpii keŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ pou nɔɔ.

english_verse: Others wanted to put Jesus to the test. So they asked him to show them a sign from God.
translation_verse: Keŋ baale mɔ ne gyae baa teese Yesu nɔɔman dɔɔ be yako e nyi waa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia, na kawola nyi ɔ lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ.

english_verse: Jesus knew what they were thinking, and he said: A kingdom where people fight each other will end up in ruin. And a family that fights will break up.
translation_verse: Mɔna Yesu gyeŋ kpene keŋ bɛɛ gyueŋ nɛ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Tɛɛle kamasɛ keŋ ke man balaŋ de kpase ba wose kpookpoo bee yoo na dɔŋa na, tɛɛle kenaŋ be gyae ke de ya. Mena mɔ te dekpaŋalaŋ daale man balaŋ de kpase ba wose kpookpoo bee yoo na dɔŋa na, dekpaŋalaŋ kenaŋ ne yɛlɛɛ bo.

english_verse: If I use his power to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? They are the ones who will judge you.
translation_verse: Akpaa nyi Bɛlisɛbu doŋ te mɛɛ mo gegi feliŋkumɛɛ na, amɔte doŋ yaa ɛ silala baŋ na mo gegi feliŋkumɛɛ? Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ silala ne gyae la baa bu ŋon fɔɔ.

english_verse: When a strong man arms himself and guards his home, everything he owns is safe.
translation_verse: Akpaa doŋte ŋolo de desina ɔ wose mo yoo abɔɔ ɔ na deke ɔ dɛɛ nɛ, ŋolo be tale kaa mo ɔ kolo ya.

english_verse: You Pharisees are in for trouble! You love the front seats in the synagogues, and you like to be greeted with honor in the market.
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ Farasiitena ɛ laako! Nawolo nyi ɛ ne gyae akyaese kpaakpaa Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man, te ɛ ne gyae mɔ nyi ɛ lii balaŋ man na balaŋ dee buŋ do ŋon nɔɔ.

english_verse: A teacher of the Law of Moses spoke up, “Teacher, you said cruel things about us.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te mmaraa wolala baŋ man ŋolo bɔɔse e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, nya ayakoŋ kɛŋa nɛ ŋgba n tee daa mɔ baa.”

english_verse: When an evil spirit leaves a person, it travels through the desert, looking for a place to rest. But when it doesn't find a place, it says, “I will go back to the home I left.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Feliŋkum da lee walaŋ man nɛ, ɔ ne tɛɛ kila kɛo tɛɛle awolɛɛ ɔ ne gyae dekyae. Akpaa ɔ te nyiŋ dekyae ya na ɔ ne yako ɔ wose nyiaa, ‘Maa bese gyu ma dekyae bene keŋ mɔɔ lee nɛ man.’

english_verse: When it gets there and finds the place clean and fixed up,
translation_verse: Akpaa ɔ kɔŋ kaa naa nyi ba te gbee botɔɔ desina ke nideli keŋ nyi ayimɛɛ kamasɛ be doo ya nɛ.

english_verse: it goes off and finds seven other evil spirits even worse than itself. They all come and make their home there, and that person ends up in worse shape than before. Being Really Blessed
translation_verse: Ɔ na bese gyu ka mo feliŋkumɛɛ nyetooro ŋan ŋe dana nombiakumɛɛ kela e nɛ kpu na ɔ wose na be kaa kyaa walaŋ ŋonaŋ man. Dekɔŋ mena na, walaŋ ŋonaŋ dinɔɔ na bese wɔlɛɛ kela ɔ kekyaa gyaŋgba keŋ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “That's true, but the people who are really blessed are the ones who hear and obey God's message!” A Sign from God (Matthew
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Balaŋ baŋ bee nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan te bee di ŋe dɔɔ nɛ, baŋ gba te kaboena nyi be sia dɛɛ gye.” Bɛɛ gyae nyi Yesu waa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia Mateo

english_verse: The Pharisee was surprised that he did not wash his hands before eating.
translation_verse: Farasii baale ŋon kena nyi Yesu te faafo ɔ nyiŋmaase ŋgba mena keŋ ba beneŋ mmaraa nawolo ya nɛ, doo ɔ nɔɔ.

english_verse: You Pharisees are in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your gardens, such as mint and rue. But you cheat people, and you don't love God. You should be fair and kind to others and still give a tenth to God.
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ Farasiitena ɛ laako! Nawolo nyi ɛ na mo ɛ wɔɔman abɔɔ buruburu gba kufu man dekpeŋ dokoloŋ fa Wurubuarɛ, mɔna ɛ bee di nombia na ŋe gbɛɛ ya te ɛ bɛɛ mo Wurubuarɛ mɔ yɛɛ kolo ya. Kaboena nyi nafɔ yaa yɛɛ mena abɔɔ kɛŋa pou, na ɛ ka bese fa ɛ wɔɔman abɔɔ buruburu ŋan kufu man dekpeŋ dokoloŋ bela kpu.

english_verse: As crowds were gathering around Jesus, he said: You people of today are evil! You keep looking for a sign from God. But what happened to Jonah is the only sign you will be given.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ ke kɔŋ kaa wuku nɛ Yesu besewɔ yako nyiaa, “Gyɛŋ wee kei balaŋ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛrawɔ. Bɛɛ gyae bo nyi baa naa gyakoloŋ nombia, mɔna mɔɔ gyae maa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia kpu na ŋan be kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Gyona dɔɔ nɛ ya.

english_verse: No one lights a lamp and then hides it or puts it under a clay pot. A lamp is put on a lampstand, so everyone who comes into the house can see the light.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa “Ŋolo bɛɛ kyaŋee kanea na waa mo ke weese yaa waa mo kolo wu ke ya. Mɔna ɔ na mo ke bo gyakaa kolo dɔɔ adido na balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ kɔŋ dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man nɛ ke nyiŋ naa.

english_verse: Your eyes are the lamp for your body. When your eyes are good, you have all the light you need. But when your eyes are bad, everything is dark.
translation_verse: Deniwalaŋ kanea yɛna ɔ sia. N sia ne naa na n wose pou yɛɛ keŋmaŋee ke, mɔna n sia de wɔlɛɛ na n wose pou doo ditiŋtɛɛ tuum man.

english_verse: You fools! Didn't God make both the outside and the inside?
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ ayiŋtena; Ɛ ba gyeŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ be yɛɛ kolo wɔle nɛ, ŋon yɛna ke tɔɔman mɔ yee?

english_verse: Yes, you are really in for trouble. You build monuments to honor the prophets your own people murdered long ago.
translation_verse: Ɛ laako! Nawolo nyi ɛ ne desina Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ yebɔɔse, mɔna ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ naanaɔ koona wɔ.

english_verse: When Jesus finished speaking, a Pharisee invited him home for a meal. Jesus went and sat down to eat.
translation_verse: Yesu kekolosi taŋ nɛ Farasii baale ŋolo baake e gyu ɔ dɛɛ ɔ kaa di, mena dɔɔ ɔ gyuuwɔ kekyaa weenɛɛ ŋan tɛɛ.

english_verse: So the Lord said to him: You Pharisees clean the outside of cups and dishes, but on the inside you are greedy and evil.
translation_verse: Keŋte de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Ɛmɛɛ Farasiitena ne faafo ɛ leenɔɔse na ɛ nyefalɛɛ wɔle bo, mɔna ŋe man dana ayimɛɛ. Mena te ɛmɛɛ mɔ konɔɔse man doo, ɛ dana sibii te nombiakumɛɛ mɔ te wulu ɛ man.

english_verse: If you would only give what you have to the poor, everything you do would please God.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛ yɛɛ deeli fa ayematena, na kawola nyi ɛ wose betaŋ ayimɛɛ ya.

english_verse: But you are in for trouble! You are like unmarked graves that people walk on without even knowing it.
translation_verse: Ɛ laako! Nawolo nyi ɛ yɛɛ ŋgba yebɔɔse ŋan ba yoo te balaŋ ne tɛɛ ŋe dɔɔ keŋ bɔɔ gyeŋ nyi ŋe yɛɛ yebɔɔse ŋa ya nɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied: You teachers are also in for trouble! You load people down with heavy burdens, but you won't lift a finger to help them carry the loads.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Ɛmɛɛ mmaraa wolala mɔ ɛ laako! Nawolo nyi ɛ ne bake kasolɔse ŋan kela balaŋ nɛ yere wɔ, mɔna ɛmɛɛ gbagba bɛɛ yɛɛ kolo kyomii kyɔ wɔ na baa tale seele be kasolɔse ŋenaŋ ya.

english_verse: You must think that was the right thing for your people to do, or else you would not have built monuments for the prophets they murdered.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ kei nawolo baa nyiaa, ɛmɛɛ gbagba te lɛɛ nombia ŋan ɛ naanaɔ be yɛɛ nɛ do. Nawolo nyi ɛ naanaɔ koona Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ, te ɛmɛɛ mɔ kɔŋawɔ ɛ ne desina ba yebɔɔse.

english_verse: Because of your evil deeds, the Wisdom of God said, “I will send prophets and apostles to you. But you will murder some and mistreat others.”
translation_verse: Kei dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ nyansate ŋon kolosiwɔ nyiaa, ‘Maa kpila ma akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra na me kpilala be gyaŋ. Baa ko be man baale, na baa naase be man baale mɔ diyem.’

english_verse: You people living today will be punished for all the prophets who have been murdered since the beginning of the world.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ bɔɔ koo lee debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yɛɛ tɛɛle kei nɛ, ka deŋele kewɔŋ gyae ke kɔŋ ɛmɛɛ gyɛŋ balaŋ kɛwɔ dɔɔ,

english_verse: This includes every prophet from the time of Abel to the time of Zechariah, who was murdered between the altar and the temple. You people will certainly be punished for all of this.
translation_verse: lee Abɛle kako man kelii Sakaria dɔɔ. Sakaria kei te ba koowɔ Wurubuarɛ kedeesa boe keŋ, na Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ nsana nsana nɛ. Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, kɛŋa pou deŋele kewɔŋ gyae ke kɔŋ ɛmɛɛ gyɛŋ balaŋ kɛwɔ dɔɔ.

english_verse: You teachers of the Law of Moses are really in for trouble! You carry the keys to the door of knowledge about God. But you never go in, and you keep others from going in.
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ mmaraa wolala ɛ laako! Ɛ ta mo Wurubuarɛ kagyeŋ gbɛɛ weese balaŋ te ɛmɛɛ gbagba mɔ bɛɛ tɛɛ gbɛɛ kenaŋ dɔɔ ya, te ɛ ne tɔ baŋ bɛɛ gyae baa ta gbɛɛ kenaŋ dɔɔ mɔ nɛ gbɛɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus was about to leave, but the teachers and the Pharisees wanted to get even with him. They tried to make him say what he thought about other things,
translation_verse: Yesu ka lee botɔɔ nɛ, mmaraa wolala na Farasiitena baŋ moo nombia gyu ke tuŋ e baŋ man te be bɔɔse e nombia burum,

english_verse: so they could catch him saying something wrong.
translation_verse: mo teese ɔ nɔɔman na baa nyiŋ kyaŋ e.

english_verse: As thousands of people crowded around Jesus and were stepping on each other, he told his disciples: Be sure to guard against the dishonest teaching of the Pharisees! It is their way of fooling people.
