translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ man nɛ, balaŋ burum kɔŋawɔ kaa yilaa keŋ nae daseŋae be doo ya. Keŋte Yesu fiasɛɛ ɔ ne yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose man nideli na Farasiitena dinɔɔ keŋ ke yɛɛ ŋgba tee keŋ bɛɛ mo do kolo man te ne wuku nɛ. Nawolo nyi bɛɛ yɛɛ ba wose ŋgba be yɛɛ balaŋ kpaakpaawɔ nɛ mɔna bɛɛ beo bo.

english_verse: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. Won't he do even more for you? You have such little faith!
translation_verse: Weera dɔɔ te ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di nideli ya? Nyi akpaa mena te Wurubuarɛ ne desina ŋalese ŋan ɛ kena ŋa gyɛŋ kyoo na be ta too ŋa na, kenaŋ na ɛ tɔɔse nyi Wurubuarɛ waa tale desina ŋon kela ŋalese ŋenaŋ.

english_verse: But put God's work first, and these things will be yours as well. Treasures in Heaven (Matthew
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛ taŋgbɛɛ mo ɛ loo pou gyakaa Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ dɔɔ. Nyi akpaa ɛ yɛɛ mena na, Wurubuarɛ waa mo abɔɔ akaŋ ŋan pou kpu ŋon.’ ”

english_verse: My friends, don't be afraid of people. They can kill you, but after that, there is nothing else they can do.
translation_verse: Me tebia mɛɛ yako ŋon baa nyi ɛ na yee balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ ko wosenaane kaageŋ bo, mɔna bɔɔ tale yɛɛ kolo kpu bela nɛ ya.

english_verse: Even the hairs on your head are counted. So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Matthew
translation_verse: Ɛ nyeenyɔɔse koraŋ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ gyeŋ ŋe yaanɔɔle. Mena dɔɔ ɛ na yee ya, nawolo nyi ɛ dana tɔnɔɔ kela akpeleese burum.”

english_verse: Sell what you have and give the money to the poor. Make yourselves moneybags that never wear out. Make sure your treasure is safe in heaven, where thieves cannot steal it and moths cannot destroy it.
translation_verse: Ɛ yɔɔ ya abɔɔ na ɛ ka mo ŋe kɔba pou fa ayematena. Kei gyae ke yela ɛ ke nyiŋ abɔɔ kpaakpaa see ɛ wose Wurubuarɛ man. Ŋmɛɛlate be gyae waa tale gyu botɔɔ ke ŋmɛɛle ya, te kolo be gyae ke tale wɔlɛɛ ŋa mɔ ya.

english_verse: At that time the Holy Spirit will tell you what to say. A Rich Fool
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi debaŋ kenaŋ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ne gyae la waa wola ŋon kpene keŋ ɛ ke yako.” Yesu kegyɔ dudu lee kɔbate diyiŋte ŋolo wose man

english_verse: But God said to him, “You fool! Tonight you will die. Then who will get what you have stored up?”
translation_verse: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ yako e nyiaa, “Nyaŋ diyiŋte! Gyɛŋ nelim kei maa lɛo n nyeedoŋ lee n nyiŋmaa man. Na nya abɔɔ ŋan pou n be gyae see nɛ, gyae ka bese amɔte wui?”

english_verse: If you tell others that you belong to me, the Son of Man will tell God's angels that you are my followers.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu kpa, “Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ maŋ do denibalaŋ siaman nyi me yɛɛ ɔ Gbeŋgyoo e na, maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon mɔ maa lɛɛ e do Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ siaman adido.

english_verse: When you are brought to trial in the synagogues or before rulers or officials, don't worry about how you will defend yourselves or what you will say.
translation_verse: Akpaa be wɔŋee ŋon gyu Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man na gyooneŋ na donɔɔ kegyiise siaman nyi be kaa di ɛ nombia na, ɛ na wɔlɛɛ na mena keŋ ɛ ke tira ɛ nɔɔse yaa kpene keŋ ɛ ke yako ya.

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Who gave me the right to settle arguments between you and your brother?”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyi “Baale, amɔte lese na maŋ nyi maa yɛɛ ɛ nombiadiiri yaa ya abɔɔ kpalare?”

english_verse: Then he said to the crowd, “Don't be greedy! Owning a lot of things won't make your life safe.”
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ kɛɛ nideli na ɛ ka deke ɛ wose lee kedikum kamasɛ man, nawolo nyi deniwalaŋ nyeedoŋ be doo abɔɔ burum kenyiŋ man ya.”

english_verse: and he said to himself, “What can I do? I don't have a place large enough to store everything.”
translation_verse: Keŋte balee ŋonaŋ yako ɔ wose nyiaa, ‘Woŋ te maa yɛɛ, nawolo nyi mɔɔ taŋ botɔɔ keŋ maa see me weenɛɛ bela ya.’

english_verse: Later, he said, “Now I know what I'll do. I'll tear down my barns and build bigger ones, where I can store all my grain and other goods.
translation_verse: Keŋte balee ŋon yako ɔ wose nyi ‘Ma gyeŋ kpene keŋ maa yɛɛ, maa tekerii me kpeese benɛɛ ŋan pou na maa baŋ dinaana na maa see me weenɛɛ na ma abɔɔ pou ŋe man.’

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: I tell you not to worry about your life! Don't worry about having something to eat or wear.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Mena dɔɔ te mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi ‘Ɛ na naase ɛ wose diyem lee ɛ kekyaa man kedi na kenyɔɔ na abɔɔ ŋan ɛ ka do ya.

english_verse: Look how the wild flowers grow! They don't work hard to make their clothes. But I tell you Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of these flowers.
translation_verse: Ɛ kɛɛ mena keŋ kyeŋkyeŋkɔɔse na bee, ŋe bɛɛ yɛɛ tom ya te ŋe bɛɛ loo kanyaŋse mɔ ya. Mɔna mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyi Gyoo Solomɔn na ɔ wosekenyiŋ koraŋ nɛ, ɔ te nyiŋ kyeŋkyeŋkɔɔse kɛŋa man dokoloŋ kyeo ya.

english_verse: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father knows what you need.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi mena abɔɔ kɛŋa te tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ ya nɛ sia ne yɛɛ ŋe dɔɔ, mɔna ɛ kya Wurubuarɛ gyeŋ nyi mena abɔɔ kɛŋa ne hia ɛmɛɛ mɔ.

english_verse: But suppose one of the servants thinks that the master won't return until late. Suppose that servant starts beating all the other servants and eats and drinks and gets drunk.
translation_verse: Mɔna akpaa tobaale kei de yako ɔ wose nyiaa, “Me gbeŋgyoo te kyare ɔ bɛɛ kɔŋ ya, te ɔ gyoo tewulɛɛ na alebia akaŋ baŋ akpanyaŋnyaŋ keyɛɛ te ɔ ne dii te ɔ ne nyɔɔ dɛɛse nɛ,

english_verse: Do you think that I came to bring peace to earth? No indeed! I came to make people choose sides.
translation_verse: Ɛ gyeŋ nyi wosefɛɛreŋ yaa ma moowɔ kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ? Koa! Na wosefɛɛreŋ ya, mɔna me kɔŋae na balaŋ kekpase kpookpoo.

english_verse: Servants are fortunate if their master finds them awake and ready when he comes! I promise you he will get ready and let his servants sit down so he can serve them.
translation_verse: Tewulɛɛ baŋ be gbeŋgyoo waa kaa naa nyi ba delena nɛ, baŋ na nyeebam. Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, be gbeŋgyoo ŋon waa kpee wa abɔɔ desina ɔ wose na waa yela baa kyakee na waa fa wɔ weenɛɛ baa di.

english_verse: Those servants are really fortunate if their master finds them ready, even though he comes late at night or early in the morning.
translation_verse: Akpaa nyi be gbeŋgyoo ŋon da bese kɔŋ tɛɛ nsana yaa nebo fuu koraŋ te ɔ naa nyi ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ delena na, baŋ na nyeebam.

english_verse: You would surely not let a thief break into your home, if you knew when the thief was coming.
translation_verse: Mɔna ɛ tɔɔse nyiaa, akpaa dekpaŋalaŋte deŋ gyeŋ debaŋ keŋ ŋmɛɛlatena baa kɔŋ kaa ŋmɛɛle e nɛ, nafɔ waa doo ɔ wose dɔɔ na bɔɔ nyiŋ kɔŋ kaa kuu wa abɔɔ ya.

english_verse: Peter asked Jesus, “Did you say this just for us or for everyone?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Pita bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, “Me Gbeŋgyoo, daa yaa n gyɔɔ dudu kei fa yaa walaŋ kamasɛ?”

english_verse: The Lord answered: Who are faithful and wise servants? Who are the ones the master will put in charge of giving the other servants their food supplies at the proper time?
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyi, “Amɔte yɛna tobaale ŋon bɛɛ lɛɛ e di te ɔ sia be kare?” Ŋon yɛna walaŋ ŋon ɔ gbeŋgyoo ba mo e nyi waa kɛɛ ɔ tewulɛɛ akaŋ baŋ dɔɔ, na waa fa wɔ weenɛɛ debaŋ keŋ kaboena nɛ man.

english_verse: A servant who is always faithful will surely be put in charge of everything the master owns.
translation_verse: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon waa mo wa abɔɔ pou do tobaale ŋonaŋ nyiŋmaa man nyi waa kɛɛ ŋe dɔɔ.

english_verse: If that happens, the master will come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him. That servant will then be punished and thrown out with the servants who cannot be trusted.
translation_verse: ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon da bese kɔŋ wee yaa debaŋ keŋ tobaale ŋon loo be gyakaa ya nɛ, waa wɔŋ tobaale ŋonaŋ deŋele na waa gegi e na waa yela waa naa diyem ŋgba baŋ bɔɔ ne lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di ya nɛ.

english_verse: If servants are not ready or willing to do what their master wants them to do, they will be beaten hard.
translation_verse: Tobaale ŋon ɔ gyeŋ kpene keŋ ɔ gbeŋgyoo ne gyae te ɔ te desina ɔ wose see na waa yɛɛ ɔ gbeŋgyoo kegyaebii ya nɛ, baa kebii e dambiise nideli.

english_verse: But servants who don't know what their master wants them to do will not be beaten so hard for doing wrong. If God has been generous with you, he will expect you to serve him well. But if he has been more than generous, he will expect you to serve him even better. Not Peace, but Trouble (Matthew
translation_verse: Mɔna tobaale ŋon ɔ ba gyeŋ kpene keŋ ɔ gbeŋgyoo ne gyae ya te ɔ yɛɛ kpene keŋ kaboena ketuku na, baa tuku e bo kyomii. Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ba mo abɔɔ burum do ɔ nyiŋmaa man na, baa bɔɔse abɔɔ burum lee ɔ gyaŋ. Te ŋon mɔ ba mo abɔɔ ŋan ŋa boe nideli do e ɔ nyiŋmaa man na, mena ke mɔ te baa bɔɔse lee ɔ gyaŋ.” Yesu nombia ta mo kekpase kɔŋ Mateo

english_verse: I came to set fire to the earth, and I wish it were already on fire!
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Me kɔŋae me kaa do boalaŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ, te ma kegyaebii yɛna nyi nafɔ ke deŋ wulaa kɛɛ kpaa.

english_verse: I am going to be put to a hard test. And I will have to suffer a lot of pain until it is over.
translation_verse: Kaale daale doo daa na maŋ te mɛɛ yeyɛɛ kelii debaŋ keŋ kaale kenaŋ gyae ke taŋ.

english_verse: Jesus said to all the people: As soon as you see a cloud coming up in the west, you say, “It's going to rain,” and it does.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako balaŋ dikpii keŋ nyiaa, “Ɛ naa ŋɔma da lee weese dɛɛyalae man nɛ, debokenaŋ man na ɛ kpa ‘Ɔga ne kɔŋ’ na ampaŋ ɔga te ŋɛɛ.

english_verse: When the south wind blows, you say, “It's going to get hot,” and it does.
translation_verse: Te ɛ naa ŋgba feliŋ ne gyɔɔ lee ata yenaŋ na ɛ kpa ‘Weese waa seŋ’ na ampaŋ te kɔŋ mena.

english_verse: Are you trying to fool someone? You can predict the weather by looking at the earth and sky, but you don't really know what's going on right now.
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ na beo ɛ wose! Ɛ ne tale kɛɛ adido na ateta na ɛ ta wola kpene keŋ gyae ke yɛɛ. Woŋ dɔɔ te ɛ be tale kɛɛ abɔɔ ŋan ne kɔŋ gyɛŋ nɛ, na ɛ ka wola ŋe man ya?

english_verse: When someone accuses you of something, try to settle things before you are taken to court. If you don't, you will be dragged before the judge. Then the judge will hand you over to the jailer, and you will be locked up.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa ŋolo de samenaa neŋ te ɛ ne gyu ɛ kaa di nombia ŋan na, yɛɛ kakyeŋ na ɛ ke desina ya nsana gbɛɛnaa man. Na mena ya na waa mo neŋ gyu nombiadiiri ŋon gyaŋ na ŋon mɔ ka mo neŋ kefa kotɔtɔɔtena na baŋ mɔ kewɔŋee neŋ ketɔ deni.

english_verse: About this same time Jesus was told that Pilate had given orders for some people from Galilee to be killed while they were offering sacrifices.
translation_verse: Yesu ke gyakaa ɔ ne kolosi fa balaŋ baŋ nɛ balaŋ baale kɔŋawɔ kaa yako e nyiaa, Galeliatena baale gyuuwɔ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man be kaa deesi fa Wurubuarɛ. Keŋte gyoo dinaa Pilato yela be ka koo wɔ, te be fatabo kyakalewɔ na ba kedeesa fatabo keŋ.

english_verse: Jesus replied: Do you think that these people were worse sinners than everyone else in Galilee just because of what happened to them?
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ na gyueŋ nyi Galeliatena baŋ bɔɔ naa mena diyem kei gyu ka yeŋ nɛ, be nombiakumɛɛ kela Galeliatena akaŋ baŋ pou?

english_verse: Not at all! But you can be sure that if you don't turn back to God, every one of you will also be killed.
translation_verse: Koa be doo mena ya, mɛɛ yako ŋon baa nyiaa akpaa ɛ te kyɛɛkee lee ɛ gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man na ɛ ke kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ ya nɛ, ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ ka yeŋ.

english_verse: Jesus said, “What is God's kingdom like? What can I compare it with?
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Sena te Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ doo? Yaa woŋ te dɛɛ mo ke maa na?

english_verse: Not at all! But you can be sure that if you don't turn back to God, every one of you will also die. A Story about a Fig Tree
translation_verse: Koa be doo mena ya, mɛɛ yako ŋon baa nyiaa akpaa ɛ te kyɛɛkee lee ɛ gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man na ɛ ke kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ ya nɛ, ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ ka yeŋ.” Daŋ keŋ ke bɛɛ see ya nɛ dudu

english_verse: Jesus then told them this story: A man had a fig tree growing in his vineyard. One day he went out to pick some figs, but he didn't find any.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu gyɔɔ dudu kei fa wɔ nyiaa, “Balee ŋolo duu daŋ keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi fiigi nɛ ɔ wɔɔman, te ɔ gyuuwɔ ɔ kaa kɛɛ nyi waa nyiŋ ke bia di mɔna ke ta see ya.

english_verse: The gardener answered, “Master, leave it for another year. I'll dig around it and put some manure on it to make it grow.
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ paate ŋon tiranɔɔ nyiaa, ‘Me gbeŋgyoo, yela ke ka seŋ kulutoo dokoloŋ kpu na maa wula tɛɛle keŋ ke dei nɛ kaa do ka ateta na dɛɛ kɛɛ.

english_verse: Maybe it will have figs on it next year. If it doesn't, you can have it cut down.” Healing a Woman on the Sabbath
translation_verse: Akpaa ke da see bia kulutoo anekɔŋaŋ na dei, te akpaa ke ta see ya na dɛɛ kara ke leki.’ ” Yesu kekyɔ alo ŋolo kawee Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee

english_verse: One Sabbath, Jesus was teaching in a synagogue,
translation_verse: Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee daale nɛ, na Yesu na wolo Gyudatena ɔsom deni daale man.

english_verse: When Jesus saw the woman, he called her over and said, “You are now well.”
translation_verse: Yesu kena alo ŋon nɛ, ɔ baake e kaa seŋ balaŋ siaman te ɔ yako e nyiaa, “Alo, n te nyiŋ kawee kekyɔ.”

english_verse: The Lord replied, “Are you trying to fool someone? Won't any one of you untie your ox or donkey and lead it out to drink on a Sabbath?
translation_verse: Keŋte de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa “Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ na beo ɛ wose! Ɛ man woŋti bɛɛ booli na ɔ wonembu lee ɔ dekpaŋae man mo e gyu kefa e loŋ kefɛɛfowee ya?

english_verse: Jesus' words made his enemies ashamed. But everyone else in the crowd was happy about the wonderful things he was doing. A Mustard Seed and Yeast (Matthew
translation_verse: Yesu nɔɔ ketira keŋ yela ɔ kɔlala baŋ pou sia yeŋawɔ, te gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan ɔ be yɛɛ nɛ yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ pou gyoŋ. Yesu kegyɔ dudu lee Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ wose man Mateo

english_verse: At that time some Pharisees came to Jesus and said, “You had better get away from here, because Herod wants to kill you!”
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ man te Farasiitena baale kɔŋawɔ Yesu gyaŋ kaa yako e nyi “Lee kɛbo kei gyu yenaŋ daale, nawolo nyi gyoo Herode ne gyae neŋ waa ko.”

english_verse: But I am going on my way today and tomorrow and the next day. After all, Jerusalem is the place where prophets are killed. Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Matthew
translation_verse: Mɔna kaboena nyi maa mo me sia tee me dee gyue dɛɛdɛɛ gyɛŋ na kyoo na ke tɛɛ kena. Nawolo nyi yɛɛ senaa nɛ, Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man te kaboena nyi Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre waa yeŋ.

english_verse: There the ones who are now least important will be the most important, and those who are now most important will be least important. Jesus and Herod
translation_verse: Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ dɔɔ wɔle nɛ baa kaa laŋ siaman, te baŋ be kyaa siaman nɛ baa dɔɔ wɔle.” Mena keŋ Yesu ne gyae Gyerusalɛm Mateo

english_verse: Jesus said to them: Go tell that fox, “I am going to force out demons and heal people today and tomorrow, and three days later I'll be through.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ gyu ke yako mena adaote ŋonaŋ nyiaa, maa gberaa feliŋkumɛɛ na maa kyɔ balaŋ gyɛŋ na kyoo, te weeya atoorote man na maa taŋ ma tom.”

english_verse: It is like what happens when someone plants a mustard seed in a garden. The seed grows as big as a tree, and birds nest in its branches.”
translation_verse: Ke yɛɛ ŋgba daŋ daale bii peperee ŋgba kpeli bii keŋ balee ŋolo ba mo ke duu ɔ wɔɔman nɛ. Te ke tuuwɔ bese daŋ dinaa dinaa te gyebuse kɔŋawɔ kaa loo ba asaase do ke tombia man.”

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “What can I compare God's kingdom with?
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Woŋ te maa mo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ maa na?

english_verse: As Jesus was on his way to Jerusalem, he taught the people in the towns and villages.
translation_verse: Yesu ka mo ɔ nyee baŋ ɔ ne gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, ɔ tɛɛwɔ donɔɔse na akuraase man ɔ na wola wɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia.

english_verse: Do all you can to go in by the narrow door! A lot of people will try to get in, but will not be able to.
translation_verse: “Nyi akpaa ɛ ne gyae ɛ ka gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man nɛ, ɛ yɛɛ kakyeŋ na ɛ ka mo denanɔɔ bɔbɔɔkyee keŋ man gyoo. Nawolo nyi balaŋ burum baa peree nyi baa mo botɔɔ gyoo, mɔna bɔɔ gyae baa tale ya.

english_verse: Once the owner of the house gets up and locks the door, you will be left standing outside. You will knock on the door and say, “Sir, open the door for us!” But the owner will answer, “I don't know a thing about you!”
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ dekpaŋalaŋte ŋon waa koro na waa tɔ ɔ disim nɛ, debaŋ kenaŋ te ɛ ka seŋ debɔɔ man na ɛ ke gyɔ disim keŋ na ɛ ke waase e nyiaa, ‘De Gbeŋgyoo toro denanɔɔ keŋ fa daa.’ Keŋte waa tiranɔɔ fa ŋon nyi, ‘Mɔɔ gyeŋ ŋon ya te mɔɔ gyeŋ botɔɔ keŋ ɛ ba lee mɔ ya.’

english_verse: Then you will start saying, “We dined with you, and you taught in our streets.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɛ ke tiranɔɔ nyiaa, daa na neŋ doo nyiŋmaa di te de nyɔɔwɔ, te n wola nombia de debiise mɔ man.

english_verse: But he will say, “I really don't know who you are! Get away from me, you evil people!”
translation_verse: Mɔna dekpaŋalaŋte ŋon waa bese yako nyiaa, ‘Mɔɔ gyeŋ ŋon ya te mɔɔ gyeŋ botɔɔ keŋ ɛ ba lee mɔ ya. Ɛ ta lee me siaman, ɛmɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra!’

english_verse: Then when you have been thrown outside, you will weep and grit your teeth because you will see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in God's kingdom.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ ɛ kena de naanaɔ Aberaham na Asiki na Gyekɔpo na Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ pou Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man te ɛ be gyae ɛ ke tale ke kpu na wɔ ya nɛ, botɔɔ te ɛ ke kyaŋ ɛ nyeeya wii na ɛ ka doŋee ɛ nyimbia.

english_verse: People will come from all directions and sit down to feast in God's kingdom.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baa lee weese dɛɛlee na weese dɛɛyalae man, na ado na ata yenaŋ kɔŋ kaa kyaa Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man di gyoŋ.

english_verse: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! Your people have killed the prophets and have stoned the messengers who were sent to you. I have often wanted to gather your people, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you wouldn't let me.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yakowɔ nyiaa, “O Gyerusalɛmtena, O Gyerusalɛmtena, ɛmɛɛ koona Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ te ɛ fuŋii Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ ɔ be kpili ɛ gyaŋ nɛ boya. Ma te yɛɛ mena keŋ maa yilaa ŋon kɔŋ me gyaŋ ŋgba mena keŋ kegyim talabo ne yilaa ɔ bia do ɔ beŋkelɛɛ tɛɛ nɛ, mɔna ɛ te fa maŋ gbɛɛ ya.

english_verse: Now your temple will be deserted. You won't see me again until the time when you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
translation_verse: Ɛ kɛɛ, Wurubuarɛ ta lese ɔ sia lee ɛ donɔɔ keŋ dɔɔ. Te mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, ɛ be gyae ɛ kena maŋ bela ya kelii debaŋ keŋ ɛ keyako nyiaa, ‘Walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne kɔŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ yele man nɛ, ŋon na nyeebam.’ ”

english_verse: Jesus turned and asked the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses, “Is it right to heal on the Sabbath?”
translation_verse: te Yesu bɔɔse Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ na Farasiitena baŋ nyiaa, “Da mmaraa ne fa gbɛɛ nyi dɛɛ kyɔ walaŋ kawee kefɛɛfowee yaa bɛɛ fa daa gbɛɛ ya?”

english_verse: But they did not say a word. Jesus took hold of the man. Then he healed him and sent him away.
translation_verse: Mɔna be man ŋolo te gyuusu ɔ nɔɔ ya. Keŋte Yesu kyaŋa kaweete ŋon nyiŋmaa man te ɔ kyɔɔ e kawee te ɔ lɔɔ e gbɛɛ.

english_verse: There was nothing they could say. How To Be a Guest
translation_verse: Mɔna ba te tale tiranɔɔ fa e ya. Wose keyɔkɔse

english_verse: Jesus saw how the guests had tried to take the best seats. So he told them:
translation_verse: Yesu kena mena keŋ balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ baake wɔ kɔŋ weenɛɛ ŋan tɛɛ kɛɛ tɔɔsee akyaese kpaakpaa nyiŋan nɛ, ɔ gyɔɔ dudu kei fa wɔ nyiaa,

english_verse: When you are invited to a wedding feast, don't sit in the best place. Someone more important may have been invited.
translation_verse: “Akpaa ŋolo de baake neŋ alowɔlere weenɛɛ tɛɛ nɛ na peree dekyae kpaakpaate ya. Nawolo nyi gyae ke tale kɔŋ nyi ɔ te baake walaŋ ŋolo ŋon ɔ kela neŋ nɛ mɔ kɔŋ botɔɔ.

english_verse: If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ gyina ɔ wose na Wurubuarɛ waa yɔkɔse e, te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ yɔkɔse ɔ wose na Wurubuarɛ waa gyina e adido.”

english_verse: Then Jesus said to the man who had invited him: When you give a dinner or a banquet, don't invite your friends and family and relatives and rich neighbors. If you do, they will invite you in return, and you will be paid back.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako walaŋ ŋon ɔ be baake e gyu ɔ dɛɛ nɛ nyiaa, “Akpaa n do kesaa te n ne gyae n ke baake balaŋ kaa di be nɔɔ nɛ, na baake n gyoona yaa n naabia yaa n loŋtona yaa n tebia baŋ be dana kɔba nɛ ya. Nawolo nyi n yɛɛ mena na baŋ mɔ baa kaa baake neŋ wee daale tɔ kom.

english_verse: When the servant returned, he said, “Master, I've done what you told me, and there is still plenty of room for more people.”
translation_verse: Te kyare ya nɛ tobaale ŋon besewɔ kɔŋ kaa yako ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon nyiaa, ‘Me gbeŋgyoo, kpene keŋ n be kpila maŋ nyi maa yɛɛ nɛ, ma te yɛɛ mɔna akyaese sɛɛ doo burum.’

english_verse: After Jesus had finished speaking, one of the guests said, “The greatest blessing of all is to be at the banquet in God's kingdom!”
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ baŋ na wɔ kyaa bee di weenɛɛ ŋan man ŋolo kenyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ ɔ yako Yesu nyiaa, “Walaŋ ŋon waa di weenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man nɛ, ŋon na nyeebam!”

english_verse: Jesus told him: A man once gave a great banquet and invited a lot of guests.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Balee ŋolo kyaawɔ te wee daale ɔ doo kesaa, te ɔ baake balaŋ burum nyi be kaa di.

english_verse: When the banquet was ready, he sent a servant to tell the guests, “Everything is ready! Please come.”
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ weenɛɛ ŋan kedi be lii nɛ, ɔ kpila ɔ tobaale nyi waa ta baake balaŋ baŋ ɔ be lɔ yele baake wɔ nɛ nyiaa, ‘Ɛ kɔŋ, ba te taŋ kpene kamasɛ.’

english_verse: One guest after another started making excuses. The first one said, “I bought some land, and I've got to look it over. Please excuse me.”
translation_verse: Mɔna be popou wɔŋee ba wose lee wɔle dokoloŋ dokoloŋ. Walaŋ gyaŋgbate ŋon tobaale ŋon be gyu ɔ gyaŋ nɛ yakowɔ nyiaa, ‘Ma ke lɛɛ tɛɛle daale yaa te mee gyu me kaa kɛɛ ke mena dɔɔ mɛɛ waase neŋ bo mɔɔ gyae maa tale kɔŋ ya.’

english_verse: The servant told his master what happened, and the master became so angry he said, “Go as fast as you can to every street and alley in town! Bring in everyone who is poor or paralyzed or blind or lame.”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ tobaale ŋon besewɔ gyu ke yako ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon nombia ŋan bɔɔ yako e nɛ. Ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ ɔ gyeŋa baŋ nideli te ɔ yako ɔ tobaale ŋon nyiaa, ‘Bese gyu donɔɔ keŋ abɔnterese na ŋe gbɛɛneŋ nɔɔman, na n kaa baake ayematena na ɔtakasese na siayɛlɛɛsatena na abafaŋse nyi be popou baa kɔŋ.’

english_verse: His master then told him, “Go out along the back roads and make people come in, so my house will be full.
translation_verse: Keŋte balee ŋon yako ɔ tobaale ŋon nyiaa, ‘Gyu donɔɔ keŋ denɛɛ wɔla na ke gbɛɛneŋ pou man, na n kaa mo balaŋ kɔŋ na ma dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ kenyiŋ wulu.’

english_verse: Not one of the guests I first invited will get even a bite of my food!” Being a Disciple (Matthew
translation_verse: Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, ‘Balaŋ baŋ mɔɔ lɔ yele baake wɔ nɛ, be man ŋolo na ŋolo nɔɔ be gyae ke daa me weenɛɛ kɛŋa ya.’ ” Kaale keŋ doo Yesu kesila Mateo

english_verse: Large crowds were walking along with Jesus, when he turned and said:
translation_verse: Wee daale balaŋ burum baale kesila Yesu bee gyuu nɛ, ɔ besena ɔ sia tee wɔ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa,

english_verse: You cannot be my disciple, unless you love me more than you love your father and mother, your wife and children, and your brothers and sisters. You cannot follow me unless you love me more than you love your own life.
translation_verse: “Akpaa ŋolo ne kɔŋ me gyaŋ ɔ kaa sila maŋ te ɔ te gyae maŋ kela ɔ kya na ɔ naa na ɔ wɛɛle na ɔ bia na ɔ kegyiise na ɔ naabia na ɔ deesina na ŋon gbagba ɔ wose koraŋ ya nɛ, ɔ be gyae waa tale bese me kaseele ya.

english_verse: Suppose one of you wants to build a tower. What is the first thing you will do? Won't you sit down and figure out how much it will cost and if you have enough money to pay for it?
translation_verse: Akpaa ɛ man ŋolo ne gyae waa baŋ deni dinaa na, ɔ bɛɛ kyaa na waa bu deni keŋ abɔɔ koya kɛɛ nyi kɔba keŋ ɔ dana nɛ gyae ka yee deni kenaŋ kebaŋ yee?

english_verse: Otherwise, you will start building the tower, but not be able to finish. Then everyone who sees what is happening will laugh at you.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi akpaa ɔ see deni keŋ mokɔɔ te ɔ te tale taŋ ke ya na, baŋ pou baa naa ke nɛ baa boŋo e,

english_verse: They will say, “You started building, but could not finish the job.”
translation_verse: nyiaa ‘Balee kei korowɔ nyi ɔ ne baŋ deni mɔna ɔ nyiŋmaa te gyaŋee ya.’

english_verse: What will a king do if he has only
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako nyiaa, akpaa gyoo ŋolo dana yoonɔɔtena kakpoŋse kufu (

english_verse: If he thinks he won't be able to defend himself, he will send messengers and ask for peace while the other king is still a long way off.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa ɔ kɛɛ nyi ɔ be tale yoo na e ya nɛ, debaŋ keŋ ɔ ta benaa ta e ya na waa kpila teree gyu ke doo e nyi waa yela baa desina ba nsana na baa kyaa wosefɛɛreŋ man.

english_verse: So then, you cannot be my disciple unless you give away everything you own. Salt and Light (Matthew
translation_verse: Mena mɔ te ɛ man walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ be gyae waa tale tina kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɔ dana yela ya nɛ, ɔ be gyae waa tale bese me kaseele ya.”

english_verse: Salt is good, but if it no longer tastes like salt, how can it be made to taste salty again?
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako nyiaa, “Yabia dei mɔna akpaa ka gyoŋ keŋ da lee ke man nɛ, n be gyae n ke tale yela ka ke yɛɛ gyoŋ bela ya.

english_verse: It is no longer good for the soil or even for the manure pile. People simply throw it out. If you have ears, pay attention!
translation_verse: De kɔŋ mena na, yabia kenaŋ be dei fa kolo na kolo keyɛɛ bela ya, bee fuŋ ke bo leki. Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ dana deŋela anenyiiŋ dei na, waa tei nombia ŋan mɛɛ yako nɛ.”

english_verse: I will go to my father and say to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against God in heaven and against you.
translation_verse: Maa koro na maa bese gyu me kya gyaŋ na me kaa yako e nyiaa, ‘Taata, ma te yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛ tia Wurubuarɛ te ma te yɛɛ tia nyaŋ mɔ.

english_verse: The younger son said to his father, “Give me my share of the property.” So the father divided his property between his two sons.
translation_verse: Wee daale balee kei bu kemaasa ŋon yako ɔ kya nyiaa, ‘Taata, kpala nya abɔɔ ŋan n dana nɛ na n ka mo me wui fa maŋ.’ Mena dɔɔ ɔ kya ŋon kpala wa abɔɔ ŋan fa ɔ bia baŋ.

english_verse: He had spent everything, when a bad famine spread through that whole land. Soon he had nothing to eat.
translation_verse: Ɔ ke wɔlɛɛ kɔba keŋ pou taŋ nɛ, tanam dinaa daale yalawɔ tɛɛle kenaŋ pou dɔɔ te ɔ wose kyaŋa e nideli.

english_verse: Tax collectors and sinners were all crowding around to listen to Jesus.
translation_verse: Debaŋ daale nɛ, lempoolɛɛra burum na nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra kɔŋawɔ Yesu gyaŋ be kaa nyii nombia ŋan ɔ na wolo nɛ.

english_verse: So the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses started grumbling, “This man is friendly with sinners. He even eats with them.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Farasiitena na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala ŋorokosowɔ yako nyiaa, “Balee kei dɔɔ ɔ ne lɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra do ɔ wose man te ɔ ne dii bom na wɔ gba koraŋ.”

english_verse: Then Jesus told them this story:
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Yesu gyɔɔ dudu kei fa wɔ nyiaa,

english_verse: If any of you has
translation_verse: “Akpaa ɛ man ŋolo dana namense sɔŋonoŋ (

english_verse: sheep, and one of them gets lost, what will you do? Won't you leave the
translation_verse: ) te be man ŋolo da yoo na woŋ te ɔ ne yɛɛ?” Ɔ be gyae waa tina sɔŋonaara na kufu kadaale (

english_verse: And when you find it, you will be so glad that you will put it on your shoulder
translation_verse: Te akpaa ɔ naa e na, ɔ be gyae waa nyaŋee na waa gate e gyakaa ɔ degbaŋgbaŋ dɔɔ mo e gyu dɛɛ yee?

english_verse: and carry it home. Then you will call in your friends and neighbors and say, “Let's celebrate! I've found my lost sheep.”
translation_verse: Ɔ gyu dɛɛ nɛ waa baake ɔ gyoona na ɔ tebia gyaŋee na waa yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ yela dɛɛ nyaŋee nawolo nyi ma te naa me nam ŋon ɔ ba yoo nɛ.

english_verse: Jesus told the people another story: What will a woman do if she has ten silver coins and loses one of them? Won't she light a lamp, sweep the floor, and look carefully until she finds it?
translation_verse: Yesu besewɔ yako nyiaa, akpaa alo ŋolo dana kɔba wolom bia kufu te ŋe man dokoloŋ da yoo na woŋ te ɔ ne yɛɛ? Ɔ be gyae waa kyaŋee kanea na waa gbee ɔ deni man na waa kɛo yenaŋ kamasɛ nideli kaboena waa naa ke yee?

english_verse: Then she will call in her friends and neighbors and say, “Let's celebrate! I've found the coin I lost.”
translation_verse: Te akpaa ɔ naa ke na, ɔ ne baake ɔ gyoona na ɔ tebia gyaŋee na waa yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ yela dɛɛ nyaŋee nawolo nyi ma te naa me kɔba wolom bii keŋ kɔɔ yoo nɛ.

english_verse: Jesus told them yet another story: Once a man had two sons.
translation_verse: Yesu besewɔ gyɔ dudu kei fa wɔ nyiaa, “Balee ŋolo kyaawɔ na ɔ dana bia baala bala.

english_verse: He went to work for a man in that country, and the man sent him out to take care of his pigs.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɔ gyuuwɔ ɔ kaa di paa fa donɔɔte ŋolo, te donɔɔte ŋon moo e gyu nyi waa gyu ke kɛɛ ɔ kekuiriise dɔɔ.

english_verse: He would have been glad to eat what the pigs were eating, but no one gave him a thing.
translation_verse: Na nafɔ ɔ loo gyakaa nyi waa nyiŋ kekuiriise baŋ weenɛɛ ŋan ŋaale di, mɔna ŋolo te fa e ŋa ya.

english_verse: Finally, he came to his senses and said, “My father's workers have plenty to eat, and here I am, starving to death!
translation_verse: Ɔ sia ke ŋmaŋee e nɛ ɔ yako ɔ wose nyiaa, ‘Me kya tewulɛɛ pou ne dii baa kaase, te maŋ kyaa kɛbo keŋ tanam ne gyae ke kara ma nyee?’

english_verse: I am no longer good enough to be called your son. Treat me like one of your workers.’ ”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ma tekaboena nyi nya baake maŋ n bu bela ya, mo maŋ yɛɛ ŋgba n tewulɛɛ baŋ man ŋolo nɛ.’ ”

english_verse: The younger son got up and started back to his father. But when he was still a long way off, his father saw him and felt sorry for him. He ran to his son and hugged and kissed him.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɔ korowɔ mo gbɛɛ ɔ ne gyu ɔ kya gyaŋ. Debaŋ keŋ ɔ dɔŋɛɛ lemlem ɔ ne kɔŋ dɛɛ nɛ, ɔ kya naa e te ɔ bu ŋon nombia yɛɛ e waraŋase te ɔ yeuwɔ kegyaŋee na e te ɔ pukaa e nyaŋee na e.

english_verse: The son said, “Father, I have sinned against God in heaven and against you. I am no longer good enough to be called your son.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔ bu ŋon yako e nyiaa, “Taata, ma te yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛ tia Wurubuarɛ te ma te yɛɛ tia nyaŋ mɔ. Mena dɔɔ ma tekaboena nyi nya baake maŋ n bu bela ya. Mo maŋ yɛɛ ŋgba n tewulɛɛ baŋ man ŋolo nɛ.”

english_verse: But his father said to the servants, “Hurry and bring the best clothes and put them on him. Give him a ring for his finger and sandals for his feet.
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ kya ŋon baake ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ kaŋ ɛ wose na ɛ ka lese kegba dɔɔloŋ kpaakpaate keŋ kɔŋ kaa do e. Ɛ do e kɔlabia ɔ nyiŋmaa man na ɛ ka do e nkyokotase.

english_verse: Get the best calf and prepare it, so we can eat and celebrate.
translation_verse: Na ɛ ke gyu ka mo nunkyue baale dinaa ŋon kɔŋ kaa ko na dee di de nɔɔ.

english_verse: This son of mine was dead, but has now come back to life. He was lost and has now been found.” And they began to celebrate.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi yɛɛ yaa ŋgba me bu kei yeŋae te ɔ besewɔ foŋ nɛ. Nafɔ na ɔ ta yoo te nɛnɛɛ ma te naa e bela.” Keŋte be fiasɛɛ bɛɛ nyaŋee na bee di be nɔɔ.

english_verse: The older son had been out in the field. But when he came near the house, he heard the music and dancing.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ kɛŋa pou nɛ, na ɔ bu kegyia ŋon kyaa wɔɔman. Ɔ ka lee wɔɔ ɔ ne kɔŋ te ɔ ba benaa dɛɛ nɛ, ɔ nyii nɔɔneŋ na suya ne tao kela.

english_verse: So he called one of the servants over and asked, “What's going on here?”
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ baake ɔ kya tewulɛɛ baŋ man ŋolo bɔɔse e kpene keŋ be kɔŋ dekpaŋalaŋ man.

english_verse: The servant answered, “Your brother has come home safe and sound, and your father ordered us to kill the best calf.”
translation_verse: Te ɔ tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “N naabu ŋon ta bese kɔŋ donɔɔ te n kya koo nunkyue baale dinaa ŋon, nawolo nyi ɔ ta bese kɔŋ dɛɛ na nyeedoŋ.”

english_verse: The older brother got so angry that he would not even go into the house. His father came out and begged him to go in.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔ bu kegyia ŋon gyeŋa baŋ bɛɛ nyi ɔ ba gyoo dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man bela ya. Ɔ kya ke nyii mena na te ɔ leewɔ kɔŋ kaa waase e nyi waa gyoo dɛɛ.

english_verse: But he said to his father, “For years I have worked for you like a slave and have always obeyed you. But you have never even given me a little goat, so that I could give a dinner for my friends.
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ yako ɔ kya nyiaa, “Taata nyaŋ kɛɛ, kulutooneŋ kɛŋa pou me kyaa mɛɛ som neŋ ŋgba gbeŋi nɛ, te ma te yɛɛ deŋele doŋ n dɔɔ ya. Woŋ te n faa maŋ? N te faa ta maŋ asoobii paŋpaŋ gba na daa na me gyoona mɔ kedi de nɔɔ ya.

english_verse: This other son of yours wasted your money on prostitutes. And now that he has come home, you ordered the best calf to be killed for a feast.”
translation_verse: Mɔna n bu kei ŋon ɔ ba mo nya abɔɔ pou ke wɔlɛɛ futooli ala dɔɔ te ɔ ba bese kɔŋ donɔɔ nyiŋmaase wolo nɛ te n koo nunkyue baale dinaa ŋon fa.”

english_verse: His father replied, “My son, you are always with me, and everything I have is yours.
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ kya ŋon yako e nyiaa, “Me bu, debaŋ kamasɛ n kyae me gyaŋ te kpene kamasɛ keŋ me dana nɛ yɛɛ n wui.

english_verse: But we should be glad and celebrate! Your brother was dead, but he is now alive. He was lost and has now been found.”
translation_verse: Mɔna kaboena nyi dee di de nɔɔ na dɛɛ nyaŋee. Nawolo nyi yɛɛ yaa ŋgba n naabu kei yeŋae te ɔ besewɔ foŋ nɛ, nafɔ na ɔ ta yoo te nɛnɛɛ da te naa e.”

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: A rich man once had a manager to take care of his business. But he was told that his manager was wasting money.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu gyɔɔ dudu fa ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Kɔbate ŋolo kyaawɔ keŋ ɔ dana tobaale ŋon ɔ ne kɛɛ wa abɔɔ pou dɔɔ, te be yako balee ŋon nyi ɔ tobaale ŋon ne wɔlɛɛ ɔ kɔba.

english_verse: So the rich man called him in and said, “What is this I hear about you? Tell me what you have done! You are no longer going to work for me.”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ balee ŋon baake ɔ tobaale ŋon te ɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, woŋ nombia yaa mee nyii lee n wose man nɛ? Kɔŋ kaa bu abɔɔ ŋan pou doo n nyiŋmaa man nɛ akɔntaa fa maŋ, nawolo nyi n ba tekaa kɛɛ ma abɔɔ dɔɔ bela ya.”

english_verse: The manager said to himself, “What shall I do now that my master is going to fire me? I can't dig ditches, and I'm ashamed to beg.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te tobaale ŋon yakowɔ ɔ nyee man nyiaa, “Me gbeŋgyoo ne gyae waa gegi maŋ lee ma tom man. Sena te maa yɛɛ? Mɔɔ taŋ doŋ me kaa fara ya, te abɔɔ kasolee mɔ dana maŋ desɛŋ.”

english_verse: I know what I'll do, so that people will welcome me into their homes after I've lost my job.”
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ yako ɔ wose nyi me ta gyeŋ kpene keŋ maa yɛɛ keŋ nyi akpaa ma tom de wɔlɛɛ na, maa nyiŋ balaŋ baŋ baa lɛɛ maŋ be dɛɛ.

english_verse: The manager asked someone else who was in debt to his master, “How much do you owe?” “A thousand sacks of wheat,” the man replied. The manager said, “Take your bill and write ‘
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ besewɔ bɔɔse balate ŋon mɔ nyi, “Kom aŋmoŋ te nyaŋ dana me gbeŋgyoo?” Te ŋon mɔ tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Maŋ dana e kom kameese tasase kakpoŋ (

english_verse: The master praised his dishonest manager for looking out for himself so well. That's how it is! The people of this world look out for themselves better than the people who belong to the light.
translation_verse: Ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon ke nyii wa ayɛɛŋ nɛ, ɔ kɛlɛɛ ɔ tobaale nkontopoote ŋon nyi ɔ gyeŋ adao nideli. Nawolo nyi tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ gyeŋ adaosebia ŋan baa mo kyaa na be tebia kela balaŋ baŋ bee yee Wurubuarɛ nɛ.

english_verse: My disciples, I tell you to use wicked wealth to make friends for yourselves. Then when it is gone, you will be welcomed into an eternal home.
translation_verse: “Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyi ɛ mo tɛɛle kei dɔɔ abɔɔ yɛɛ deeli lese gyoori, na akpaa abɔɔ ŋan de taŋ nɛ, na ke yela Wurubuarɛ ke lɛɛ ŋon na ɛ ke kyaa ɔ gyaŋ kekpaakekpaa.

english_verse: So Abraham said, “If they won't pay attention to Moses and the prophets, they won't listen even to someone who comes back from the dead.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Aberaham yako e nyiaa, ‘Nyi akpaa be te nyii Mosesi na Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ nombia ya nɛ, mena mɔ te ŋolo de fom lee yeŋ man kɛbo gyu be gyaŋ koraŋ na bɔɔ gyae baa taŋ bua e ya.’ ”

english_verse: The Pharisees really loved money. So when they heard what Jesus said, they made fun of him.
translation_verse: Farasiitena baŋ be sia ne yɛɛ kɔba dɔɔ nideli ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, ba boŋo Yesu kulu e.

english_verse: But Jesus told them: You are always making yourselves look good, but God sees what is in your heart. The things that most people think are important are worthless as far as God is concerned.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛmɛɛ yɛna balaŋ baŋ ɛ ne desina ɛ wose balaŋ siaman, mɔna Wurubuarɛ gyeŋ ɛ konɔɔse man. Nawolo nyi kpene kamasɛ keŋ balaŋ ne bu ke nideli nɛ, ke yɛɛ kolo anekɔlaŋ ke Wurubuarɛ siaman.

english_verse: Until the time of John the Baptist, people had to obey the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets. But since God's kingdom has been preached, everyone is trying hard to get in.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan, na Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra nombia ŋan dɔɔ te balaŋ ne dii pɛte Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon kɔŋawɔ. Lee mena debaŋ keŋ Gyɔn be kɔŋ pou na, bee kolosi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ wose man nombia kpaakpaa ŋan, te walaŋ kamasɛ na peree nyi waa gyoo ke man.

english_verse: Heaven and earth will disappear before the smallest letter of the Law does.
translation_verse: Dana doŋ nyi kolo bii peperee ka yo lee Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan man, kela nyi adido na ateta ke ŋaalaa.

english_verse: It is a terrible sin for a man to divorce his wife and marry another woman. It is also a terrible sin for a man to marry a divorced woman. Lazarus and the Rich Man
translation_verse: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ bɛɛ ɔ wɛɛle te ɔ yala alo wɔle nɛ, Wurubuarɛ siaman na ɔtenate gyae ŋolo wɛɛle baa Te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon mɔ ɔ yala alo ŋon ɔ baale be bɛɛ e na, Wurubuarɛ siaman na ɔtenate mɔ gyae ŋolo wɛɛle baa.” Kɔbate ŋolo na Lasarose wose man nombia

english_verse: There was once a rich man who wore expensive clothes and every day ate the best food.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako nyiaa, “Kɔbate ŋolo kyaawɔ keŋ ɔ na do abɔɔ kpaakpaa nyiŋan ŋan ŋe dana koyadoŋ te ɔ ne di gyoŋ wee kamasɛ.

english_verse: But a poor beggar named Lazarus was brought to the gate of the rich man's house.
translation_verse: Wee na wee mɔ na, bɛɛ mo balee diyemte ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Lasarose nɛ, kaa see kɔbate ŋon denanɔɔ man. Na Lasarose kei wose pou te kulii gyamɛɛ.

english_verse: He was happy just to eat the scraps that fell from the rich man's table. His body was covered with sores, and dogs kept coming up to lick them.
translation_verse: Ɔ ne kɔŋ kaa kyaa botɔɔ gyakaa loo nyi waa nyiŋ kɔbate ŋon weenɛɛ ŋan ne yale tɛɛle man nɛ ŋaale di. Na babaase gba ne kɔŋ kaa deŋee ɔ gyamɛɛ ŋan.

english_verse: He went to hell and was suffering terribly. When he looked up and saw Abraham far off and Lazarus at his side,
translation_verse: Ɔ ke doo botɔɔ ɔ ne naa diyem nɛ, ɔ gyinaa ɔ sia kɛɛ adido na te ɔ naa Aberaham lemlem na Lasarose ŋon kyaa ɔ yenaŋ.

english_verse: he said to Abraham, “Have pity on me! Send Lazarus to dip his finger in water and touch my tongue. I'm suffering terribly in this fire.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te kɔbate ŋon gyinaa ɔ nɔɔ baake Aberaham nyiaa, ‘Me kya Aberaham! Wii me waraŋase na n ke yela Lasarose ka mo ɔ nyimbii ke sɔɔ loŋ man, na waa mo kaa tina me laakem na ma wose ke fɛɛ maŋ kyomii, nawolo nyi mɛɛ naa diyem nideli boalaŋ kei man!’

english_verse: Abraham answered, “My friend, remember that while you lived, you had everything good, and Lazarus had everything bad. Now he is happy, and you are in pain.
translation_verse: Keŋte Aberaham tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, ‘Me bu, tɔɔse nyi debaŋ keŋ n kyaa tɛɛle dɔɔ nɛ n nyiŋa abɔɔ kpaakpaa di gyoŋ, na Lasarose ŋon doo diyem man ɔ na yeyɛɛ, te nɛnɛɛ ŋon mɔ ne di gyoŋ kɛbo te nyaŋ mɔ n doo diyem man n na yeyɛɛ.

english_verse: But the rich man said, “Abraham, then please send Lazarus to my father's home.
translation_verse: Keŋte kɔbate ŋon yako e nyiaa, ‘Me kya, kenaŋ na mɛɛ sola neŋ bo, kpila Lasarose ka bese gyu me kya dɛɛ,

english_verse: Let him warn my five brothers, so they won't come to this horrible place.”
translation_verse: nawolo nyi me dana naabia baala banoŋ na ɔ kaa faree wɔ, na mena ya na baŋ mɔ baa kɔŋ kaa naa diyem boalaŋ kei man.’

english_verse: Abraham answered, “Your brothers can read what Moses and the prophets wrote. They should pay attention to that.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Aberaham yako kɔbate ŋon nyiaa, ‘Ba gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan ɔ be yeli Mosesi be ŋmarase see, na Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra ba wolo nɛ. Mena dɔɔ baa tei nombia ŋenaŋ nideli.’

english_verse: Then the rich man said, “No, that's not enough! If only someone from the dead would go to them, they would listen and turn to God.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te kɔbate ŋon tiranɔɔ nyiaa, ‘Koa me kya Aberaham! Akpaa ŋolo da lee woya man kɛbo gyu keyako wɔ nɛ, baa kyɛɛkee lee be nombiakumɛɛ keyɛɛ man na baa kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ.’

english_verse: Why was this foreigner the only one who came back to thank God?”
translation_verse: Weera dɔɔ te ŋɔɔle kei waageŋ yɛna walaŋ ŋon ɔ ba bese kɔŋ ɔ kaa fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: If you had faith no bigger than a tiny mustard seed, you could tell this mulberry tree to pull itself up, roots and all, and to plant itself in the ocean. And it would!
translation_verse: Keŋte de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Akpaa ɛ kelɛɛdi yɛɛ peperee ŋgba kefaabii koraŋ nɛ, ɛ ke tale yako daŋ nyi ‘Kulu na n lila pou na n kaa seŋ ɛpo man, te gyae ke kɔŋ mena.’

english_verse: If your servant comes in from plowing or from taking care of the sheep, would you say, “Welcome! Come on in and have something to eat”?
translation_verse: Dɛɛ mo ke nyi ɛ man ŋolo dana tobaale ŋon ɔ ne fare fa e yaa ɔ ne kɛɛ ɔ namense dɔɔ. Akpaa tobaale ŋon da lee ŋale man kɔŋ nɛ, ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon bɛɛ yako e nyi tisi kyaa bileŋ na n kedi ya.

english_verse: There is no use saying, ‘Look! Here it is’ or ‘Look! There it is.’ God's kingdom is here with you.”
translation_verse: Ŋolo be gyae waa yako ŋon nyiaa ‘Ɛ kɛɛ, keŋ yaa kɛbo,’ yaa ke kyaa yenaŋ daale ya! Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ kyaa ɛ man.”

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: The time will come when you will long to see one of the days of the Son of Man, but you will not.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Debaŋ daale gyae kekɔŋ keŋ ɛ ke gyae nyi ɛ ka bese naa maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon kekyaa man wee dokoloŋ gba, mɔna ɛ be gyae ɛ kena ya.”

english_verse: Servants don't deserve special thanks for doing what they are supposed to do.
translation_verse: Kaboena nyi balee ŋon waa do ɔ tobaale ŋon nɔɔ nyi ɔ te yɛɛ kpene keŋ ɔ be yako e nyi waa yɛɛ dɔɔ? Koa.

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: There will always be something that causes people to sin. But anyone who causes them to sin is in for trouble. A person who causes even one of my little followers to sin
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Abɔɔ ŋan ne yeli balaŋ ne yɛɛ dukum nɛ gyae ke kɔŋ, mɔna nyaŋ ŋon ŋe gyae ke ta n dɔɔ kɔŋ nɛ nya laako!

english_verse: So be careful what you do. Correct any followers of mine who sin, and forgive the ones who say they are sorry.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose nideli. Akpaa n dɔɔ de yɛɛ neŋ dukum na lese si e, te akpaa ɔ nyii ɔ wose asɛɛ te ɔ kyɛɛkee na mo kyɛɛ e.

english_verse: Even if one of them mistreats you seven times in one day and says, “I am sorry,” you should still forgive that person.
translation_verse: Akpaa ɔ yɛɛ nombiikum tia neŋ betabonyetooro wee dokoloŋ, te ɔ bese kɔŋ n gyaŋ betabonyetooro wee dokoloŋ kenaŋ kaa doo neŋ teree na mo kyɛɛ e.”

english_verse: The apostles said to the Lord, “Make our faith stronger!”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu kpilala kufu bala baŋ yako e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, yela da kelɛɛdi ka bo kpu.”

english_verse: No, you wouldn't say that. You would say, “Prepare me something to eat. Get ready to serve me, so I can have my meal. Then later on you can eat and drink.”
translation_verse: Mɔna ɔ ne yako e bo nyi, ‘Desina n wose na n ke saa me balinɔɔ weenɛɛ fa maŋ na n ke kyaa daa maŋ maa di na maa nyɔɔ, me taŋ na pɛna nyaŋ mɔ n kaa di na n ke nyɔɔ.’

english_verse: And that's how it should be with you. When you've done all you should, then say, “We are merely servants, and we have simply done our duty.” Ten Men with Leprosy
translation_verse: Mena te doo fa ɛmɛɛ mɔ, nyi akpaa ɛ yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ keŋ mɔɔ yako ŋon nyi yaa yɛɛ nɛ, kaboena yaa yako nyi de taŋ yɛɛ n tewulɛɛwɔ, tom keŋ kaboena nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ te de yɛɛwɔ.” Yesu kekyɔ balaŋ kufu baale kawee yayam

english_verse: On his way to Jerusalem, Jesus went along the border between Samaria and Galilee.
translation_verse: Yesu ka mo ɔ nyee baŋ ɔ ne gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, ɔ tɛɛwɔ Samaria na Galelia tɛɛlese wulu man.

english_verse: As he was going into a village, ten men with leprosy came toward him. They stood at a distance
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ ɔ na gyoo akuraa daale man nɛ, balaŋ kufu baale baŋ bɛɛ wee kawee yayam nɛ kɔŋawɔ be kaa gyaŋee na e te be dɔɔwɔ seŋ lemlem,

english_verse: and shouted, “Jesus, Master, have pity on us!”
translation_verse: keŋte be gyinaa be nɔɔ nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo Yesu! Waaseda wii de waraŋase!”

english_verse: Jesus looked at them and said, “Go show yourselves to the priests.” On their way they were healed.
translation_verse: Yesu kena wɔ keŋ nɛ, ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa “Ɛ mo ɛ wose kawola Wurubuarɛ saese baŋ.” Be kee gyuu nɛ te be kaweese ŋan taŋawɔ.

english_verse: When one of them discovered that he was healed, he came back, shouting praises to God.
translation_verse: Be man ŋolo kena nyi ɔ kawee te taŋ nɛ, ɔ besewɔ kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ na ɔ ne fae yeesa lese Wurubuarɛ yele.

english_verse: He bowed down at the feet of Jesus and thanked him. The man was from the country of Samaria.
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ kaa buŋawɔ Yesu nawɔɔtɛɛ do e nɔɔ. Na mena balee kei yɛɛ Samaria baale e.

english_verse: Jesus asked, “Weren't ten men healed? Where are the other nine?
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔsewɔ nyiaa, “Na balaŋ kufu te me kyɔɔwɔ yee? Te nde balaŋ kadaale akaŋ baŋ?

english_verse: Then Jesus told the man, “You may get up and go. Your faith has made you well.” God's Kingdom (Matthew
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Koro na n ke ta, n kelɛɛdi dɔɔ te n nyiŋa kawee kekyɔ.” Yesu kekolosi lee Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ wose man Mateo

english_verse: When people say to you, “Look there,” or “Look here,” don't go looking for him.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baa yako ŋon nyiaa, “Ɔ kyaa kɛbo, yaa ɔ kyaa yenaŋ daale! Mɔna ɛ na yekii sila wɔ ya.

english_verse: Then Jesus' disciples spoke up, “But where will this happen, Lord?” Jesus said, “Where there is a corpse, there will always be vultures.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ bɔɔse e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, aleŋ gbɛɛ te mena nombia kɛŋa pou gyae kekɔŋ?” Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Botɔɔ keŋ kolo punta doo nɛ, botɔɔ te bapɛtɛɛna ne yilaa.”

english_verse: But first he must suffer terribly and be rejected by the people of today.
translation_verse: Mɔna kaboena nyi maa taŋgbɛɛ naa diyem nideli, na gyɛŋ wee kei balaŋ ke bɛɛ maŋ pɛ.

english_verse: When the Son of Man comes, things will be just as they were when Noah lived.
translation_verse: Mena keŋ be kɔŋ Nowa kekyaa man nɛ, mena mɔ te gyae ke kɔŋ maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon ka bese kɔŋ man.

english_verse: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day when Noah went into the big boat. Then the flood came and drowned everyone on earth.
translation_verse: Mena Nowa weeya ŋenaŋ man nɛ, na balaŋ gyakaa bee dii na bɛɛ nyɔɔ, na ala na baala mɔ ne yala dɔŋa kelii debaŋ keŋ Nowa ba gyoo adakaa keŋ man te loŋ keŋ waalewɔ kaa koe balaŋ pou.

english_verse: But on the very day Lot left Sodom, fiery flames poured down from the sky and killed everyone.
translation_verse: Mɔna wee keŋ Lɔɔte ba lee Sodom donɔɔ man nɛ, Wurubuarɛ yela boalaŋ na kpatakpela leewɔ adido kɔŋ kaa kpa Sodom donɔɔ pou.

english_verse: The same will happen on the day when the Son of Man appears.
translation_verse: Mena mɔ te wee keŋ maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa bɛɛ lee nɛ gyae ke futaa balaŋ.

english_verse: At that time no one on a rooftop should go down into the house to get anything. No one in a field should go back to the house for anything.
translation_verse: Mena wee kenaŋ nɛ walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ be kyaa ɔ deni man ya te wa abɔɔ doo ɔ deni man nɛ, ɔ na bese gyu ka lese ŋa ya. Mena mɔ te walaŋ ŋon ɔ kyaa wɔɔman nɛ ɔ na bese gyu dɛɛ nyi ɔ kaa mo ɔ kolo ya.

english_verse: Remember what happened to Lot's wife.
translation_verse: Ɛ na wolee kpene keŋ be kɔŋ Lɔɔte wɛɛle dɔɔ nɛ ya.

english_verse: People who try to save their lives will lose them, and those who lose their lives will save them.
translation_verse: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne gyae nyi waa lɛɛ ŋon gbagba nyeedoŋ na ke gyae ka lee ɔ nyiŋmaa man, mɔna walaŋ ŋon ɔ nyeedoŋ da lee ɔ nyiŋmaa man lee maŋ dɔɔ nɛ waa bese nyiŋ ke.

english_verse: On that night two people will be sleeping in the same bed, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyi mena wee kenaŋ nelim nɛ balaŋ bala baa doo dofoŋoŋ dokoloŋ dɔɔ, mɔna Wurubuarɛ waa mo ŋolo tina ŋolo yela.

english_verse: Two women will be together grinding wheat, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
translation_verse: Mena mɔ te baala bala baa seŋ wɔɔman bɛɛ yɛɛ tom, mɔna Wurubuarɛ waa mo ŋolo tina ŋolo yela.”

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples a story about how they should keep on praying and never give up:
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu gyɔɔ dudu fa ɔ kaseela baŋ wola wɔ nyiaa, kaboena nyi baa fane wee kamasɛ keŋ ba wose na yɔkɔse wɔ ya.

english_verse: In a town there was once a judge who didn't fear God or care about people.
translation_verse: Ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa “Donɔɔ daale man nɛ nombiadiiri ŋolo kyaawɔ keŋ ɔ bee yee Wurubuarɛ ya, te ɔ be famenɛ na walaŋ mɔ ya.

english_verse: In that same town there was a widow who kept going to the judge and saying, “Make sure that I get fair treatment in court.”
translation_verse: Na lialo ŋolo mɔ kyaa donɔɔ kenaŋ man. Ɔ ne kyaa kɔŋ nombiadiiri ŋon gyaŋ kaa waase e wee kamasɛ nyiaa, ‘Kɛɛ na n ke di daa na me walaŋ ŋon daa na e be nyim nɛ nombia ŋan nideli fa maŋ.’

english_verse: For a while the judge refused to do anything. Finally, he said to himself, “Even though I don't fear God or care about people,
translation_verse: Nafɔ nombiadiiri ŋon bɛɛwɔ kelii debaŋ daale, mɔna wɔle wɔle nɛ ɔ yako ɔ wose nyiaa, ‘Yɛɛ ampaŋ nyi mɔɔ ne yee Wurubuarɛ ya te mɔɔ famenɛ na walaŋ mɔ ya.

english_verse: I will help this widow because she keeps on bothering me. If I don't help her, she will wear me out.”
translation_verse: Mɔna alo kei nombia te poro maŋ mena dɔɔ maa kɛɛ na maa di ɔ nombia ŋan nideli fa e, na ɔ be nyiŋ gyem tisi me dɔɔ naase maŋ diyem bela ya.’ ”

english_verse: The Lord said: Think about what that crooked judge said.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yakowɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ nyii kpene keŋ nombiadiiri kum kei be yako.

english_verse: He will surely hurry and help them. But when the Son of Man comes, will he find on this earth anyone with faith? A Pharisee and a Tax Collector
translation_verse: Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ waa di nombia ŋan anokoare man fa wɔ bileŋ na waa yela baa di deŋele. Te nyi akpaa maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon ma bese kɔŋ na, balaŋ baa kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ keŋ baa lɛɛ maŋ di?” Farasii baale ŋolo na lempoolɛɛre ŋolo wose man dudu

english_verse: Jesus told a story to some people who thought they were better than others and who looked down on everyone else:
translation_verse: Yesu besewɔ gyɔ dudu kei fa balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ kɛɛ ba wose gyɔ konɔɔ nyi be gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman, te bɔɔ na mo walaŋ kamasɛ yɛɛ kolo ya nɛ nyiaa!

english_verse: The Pharisee stood over by himself and prayed, “God, I thank you that I am not greedy, dishonest, and unfaithful in marriage like other people. And I am really glad that I am not like that tax collector over there.
translation_verse: Farasii baale ŋon korowɔ seŋ te ɔ fanewɔ kɛlɛɛ ɔ wose nyiaa, ‘Wurubuarɛ mɛɛ fa neŋ karaŋ nyi ma te yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛre e ŋgba balaŋ akaŋ baŋ nɛ ya. Mɔɔ yɛɛ ŋgba ŋmɛɛlatena na balaŋ kumɛɛ na kafoŋtena yaa ŋgba lempoolɛɛre kei nɛ ya.

english_verse: I go without eating for two days a week, and I give you one tenth of all I earn.”
translation_verse: Mɛɛ bake me nɔɔ betabola weeya nyetooro kamasɛ man, te mɛɛ kpase ma kpene kamasɛ keŋ mee nyim nɛ akpeŋ kufu na me ta mo dekpeŋ dokoloŋ fa nyaŋ Wurubuarɛ.’

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “When the two men went home, it was the tax collector and not the Pharisee who was pleasing to God. If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.” Jesus Blesses Little Children (Matthew
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yakowɔ nyiaa, “Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyi ba ke fane taŋ nɛ, lempoolɛɛre ŋon te Wurubuarɛ lɛɛwɔ do nyi ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ ŋon Wurubuarɛ siaman. Nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ gyina ɔ wose adido na Wurubuarɛ waa yɔkɔse e, te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ yɔkɔse ɔ wose na Wurubuarɛ waa gyina e adido.” Yesu kehiraa bia Mateo

english_verse: Some people brought their little children for Jesus to bless. But when his disciples saw them doing this, they told the people to stop bothering him.
translation_verse: Wee daale balaŋ baale moo be bia kɔŋ nyi Yesu waa mo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa be dɔɔ na waa hiraa wɔ. Ɔ kaseela baŋ kena mena nɛ, be ŋmanyaŋ balaŋ baŋ.

english_verse: will be given much more in this life. And in the future world they will have eternal life.” Jesus Again Tells about His Death (Matthew
translation_verse: waa bese nyiŋ ŋa burum kpu gyɛŋ weeya kɛŋa man, na waa bese nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa wee daale.” Yesu besewɔ kolosi lee ɔ yeŋ na ɔ kefoŋ wose man Mateo

english_verse: Jesus took the twelve apostles aside and said: We are now on our way to Jerusalem. Everything that the prophets wrote about the Son of Man will happen there.
translation_verse: Wee daale nɛ Yesu baake ɔ kaseela kufu bala baŋ lee yenaŋ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Dee gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man baa, te kpene kamasɛ keŋ Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra be ŋmarase lee maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon wose man nɛ, gyae ke kɔŋ mena.

english_verse: An important man asked Jesus, “Good Teacher, what must I do to have eternal life?”
translation_verse: Wee daale nɛ Gyudatena siamante ŋolo kaa bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa “De Gbeŋgyoo kpaakpaa, woŋ te maa yɛɛ na maa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ?”

english_verse: He told Jesus, “I have obeyed all these commandments since I was a young man.”
translation_verse: Keŋte balee ŋon mɔ tiranɔɔ nyiaa “Me wulaa mee di mmaraase kɛŋa pou dɔɔ leenaŋ me biatɛɛ”

english_verse: When Jesus heard this, he said, “There is one thing you still need to do. Go and sell everything you own! Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come and be my follower.”
translation_verse: Yesu ke nyii ɔ nɔɔwoya ŋan nɛ ɔ yako e nyiaa, “Ta bese ka neŋ kolo dokoloŋ kooŋ n ke yɛɛ. Gyu ke yɔɔ nya abɔɔ ŋan pou n dana nɛ na n ka mo kɔba keŋ kpɛlɛɛ ayematena na n ke kɔŋ kaa sila maŋ. N yɛɛ mena na n ke nyiŋ abɔɔ kpaakpaa see n wose Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ adido.”

english_verse: When the man heard this, he was sad, because he was very rich.
translation_verse: Balee ŋon ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, takaa e nideli nawolo nyi ɔ dana abɔɔ burum.

english_verse: Jesus saw how sad the man was. So he said, “It's terribly hard for rich people to get into God's kingdom!
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu gɛɛsewɔ kɛɛ e te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Gyae ke yɛɛ doŋ fa baŋ be dana kɔba nɛ nyi baa gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man.

english_verse: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, gyae ke yɛɛ doŋ fa baŋ be dana kɔba nɛ nyi baa gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man, kela nyi kurumɔɔ ka gyoo ŋarale bɔɔ man lee.”

english_verse: When the crowd heard this, they asked, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ be bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Nyi nnɛ te doo na, amɔte ne gyae la waa nyiŋ nyeekelɛɛ baa?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “There are some things that people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Kpene keŋ deniwalaŋ be gyae waa tale yɛɛ ya nɛ, Wurubuarɛ dɔɔ waa tale yɛɛ.”

english_verse: He will be handed over to foreigners, who will make fun of him, mistreat him, and spit on him.
translation_verse: Baa mo maŋ do balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ nyiŋmaa man, na baa kulu maŋ na baa tee maŋ na baa tɔ nɔɔloŋ woli maŋ na baa tuku maŋ na baa ko maŋ.

english_verse: They will beat him and kill him, but three days later he will rise to life.
translation_verse: Mɔna weeya atoorote keŋ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa gyuusu maŋ lee yeŋ man.”

english_verse: When Jesus was coming close to Jericho, a blind man sat begging beside the road.
translation_verse: Yesu kɛɛ benaa Gyeriko donɔɔ man nɛ, na siayɛlɛɛsate ŋolo kyaa gbɛɛnaa man ɔ na solee abɔɔ.

english_verse: The man heard the crowd walking by and asked what was happening.
translation_verse: Ɔ ke nyii balaŋ burum baŋ kɛɛ lam nɛ, ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyi woŋ kɔŋala?

english_verse: Some people told him that Jesus from Nazareth was passing by.
translation_verse: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ yako e nyiaa “Yesu Nasarɛte baale ŋon ne laŋa laa.”

english_verse: So the blind man shouted, “Jesus, Son of David, have pity on me!”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔ gyinaa ɔ nɔɔ baake nyiaa, “Yesu, Defidi ɔ naanabii! Wii me waraŋase!”

english_verse: The people who were going along with Jesus told the man to be quiet. But he shouted even louder, “Son of David, have pity on me!”
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ be dɔŋɛɛ siaman nɛ ŋmanyaŋ e nyi waa wu ɔ nɔɔ. Mɔna nɛnɛɛ gba te ɔ desinaa fae yeesa nedoŋ nyiaa, “Defidi ɔ naanabii! Wii me waraŋase.”

english_verse: Jesus stopped and told some people to bring the blind man over to him. When the blind man was getting near, Jesus asked,
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Yesu seŋawɔ te ɔ yakowɔ nyi baa mo siayɛlɛɛsate ŋon kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ. Ɔ ka benaa e nɛ, Yesu bɔɔse e nyiaa,

english_verse: “What do you want me to do for you?” “Lord, I want to see!” he answered.
translation_verse: “Woŋ te n ne gyae nyi maa yɛɛ fa neŋ?” Keŋte siayɛlɛɛsate ŋon tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Me Gbeŋgyoo, mɛɛ gyae me sia ke gyuusu.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Look and you will see! Your eyes are healed because of your faith.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, “N sia dee gyuusu na n kena. N ke lɛɛ maŋ di dɔɔ te n nyiŋa kawee kekyɔ.”

english_verse: At once the man could see, and he went with Jesus and started thanking God. When the crowds saw what happened, they praised God.
translation_verse: Debokenaŋ man te ɔ sia gyuusuwɔ, te ɔ naawɔ te ɔ sila Yesu na ɔ na lese Wurubuarɛ yele. Balaŋ dikpii keŋ pou kena nɛ, baŋ mɔ lese Wurubuarɛ yele.

english_verse: Jesus was heading his way, and Zacchaeus wanted to see what he was like. But Zacchaeus was a short man and could not see over the crowd. So he ran ahead and climbed up into a sycamore tree.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɔ kyaŋa gbɛɛ gyu siaman ka gyeŋ daŋ daale, na waa nyiŋ naa e nawolo nyi botɔɔ te Yesu waa kaa mo.

english_verse: When Jesus got there, he looked up and said, “Zacchaeus, hurry down! I want to stay with you today.”
translation_verse: Yesu kelii daŋ keŋ tɛɛ nɛ ɔ gyinaa ɔ sia kɛɛ adido te ɔ naa Sakio te ɔ baake e nyiaa, “Sakio tisi kɔŋ bileŋ, nawolo nyi gyɛŋ n dɛɛ te maa soo.”

english_verse: Zacchaeus hurried down and gladly welcomed Jesus.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Sakio mɔ tisiwɔ bileŋ kɔŋ te ɔ lɛɛ Yesu gyoŋ man.

english_verse: Everyone who saw this started grumbling, “This man Zacchaeus is a sinner! And Jesus is going home to eat with him.”
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ naa nɛ fiasɛɛ bɛɛ ŋorokotee nyi, “Balee kei ne gyu ɔ kaa soo nombiakumɛɛyɛɛre kei.”

english_verse: Later that day Zacchaeus stood up and said to the Lord, “I will give half of my property to the poor. And I will now pay back four times as much to everyone I have ever cheated.”
translation_verse: Be ke gyu ka gyoo Sakio dɛɛ nɛ, ɔ korowɔ kaa seŋ balaŋ baŋ siaman te ɔ yako Yesu nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo kɛɛ, maa kpase ma abɔɔ ŋan pou me dana nɛ yaale fa ayematena. Te akpaa me te sisii ŋolo na maa tɔ e betabonaara kpu”

english_verse: Jesus said to Zacchaeus, “Today you and your family have been saved, because you are a true son of Abraham.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako Sakio nyiaa, “Gyɛŋ Wurubuarɛ te lɛɛ ɛmɛɛ na n dekpaŋalaŋmantena pou nyee, nawolo nyi nyaŋ mɔ n yɛɛ Aberaham ɔ naanabii e.

english_verse: The Son of Man came to look for and to save people who are lost.” A Story about Ten Servants (Matthew
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu kɔŋae me kaa kɛo na maa lɛɛ baŋ bɔɔ yoo nɛ nyee.” Yesu kegyɔ dudu lee kɔba bia ŋaale wose man Mateo

english_verse: But they said, “Sir, he already has ten times as much!”
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ yako e nyiaa, “De gbeŋgyoo, ŋgba ŋon wulaa ɔ dana kɔba burum?”

english_verse: But the people of his country hated him, and they sent messengers to the foreign country to say, “We don't want this man to be our king.”
translation_verse: Mɔna ɔ dɛɛtena baŋ bɛɛ gyae ɔ nombia ya, mena dɔɔ be kpila balaŋ gyu keyako donɔɔ keŋ man ɔ ne gyu nɛ kegyiise nyiaa, ‘De bɛɛ gyae balee kei kaa bese de gyoo ya.’

english_verse: He told them, “Go into the next village, where you will find a young donkey that has never been ridden. Untie the donkey and bring it here.
translation_verse: nyiaa, “Ɛ gyu akuraa keŋ kyaa ɛ siaman nɛ man. Ɛ betaa ɛ na gyoo ke man pɛ nɛ, ɛ kena afurum bu ŋolo kpaŋɛɛ keŋ walaŋ na walaŋ te kyaa ta ɔ dɔɔ see ya. Ɛ booli e na ɛ ka mo e kɔna maŋ kɛbo.

english_verse: The first servant came and said, “Sir, with the money you gave me I have earned ten times as much.”
translation_verse: Ɔ tobaale gyaŋgbate ŋon kɔŋawɔ kaa yako e nyiaa, ‘Me gbeŋgyoo, kɔba keŋ n ba mo fa maŋ nɛ ta lola kufu kpu na bene keŋ.’

english_verse: The second one came and said, “Sir, with the money you gave me, I have earned five times as much.”
translation_verse: Ɔ tobaale balate ŋon mɔ kɔŋawɔ kaa yako e nyiaa, ‘Me gbeŋgyoo, n kɔba keŋ n ba mo fa maŋ nɛ ta lola anoŋ kpu na bene keŋ.’ ”

english_verse: Another servant came and said, “Sir, here is your money. I kept it safe in a handkerchief.
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ tobaale ŋolo mɔ kɔŋawɔ kaa yako e nyiaa, “Me gbeŋgyoo, n kɔba keŋ n ba mo fa maŋ nɛ yaa, ma moo ke kpaŋ na kakyeŋgbelaa ka weese.

english_verse: Why didn't you put my money in the bank? On my return, I could have had the money together with interest.”
translation_verse: woŋ dɔɔ te n ta mo me kɔba keŋ ka do kɔba dɛɛsee man na me kɔŋ na, me kaa lɛɛ keŋ na ka loŋ keŋ ya?”

english_verse: Then he said to some other servants standing there, “Take the money away from him and give it to the servant who earned ten times as much.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te be gbeŋgyoo ŋon yako baŋ ba seŋɛɛ botɔɔ nɛ nyiaa, “Ɛ lɛo kɔba keŋ lee ɔ gyaŋ na ɛ ka mo fa walaŋ ŋon ɔ kɔba ba lola kufu kpu nɛ.”

english_verse: Now bring me the enemies who didn't want me to be their king. Kill them while I watch!” Jesus Enters Jerusalem (Matthew
translation_verse: Te me kɔlala baŋ ba te gyae nyi maa di gyoori be dɔɔ ya nɛ, ɛ mo wɔ kɔŋ na ɛ kaa koe wɔ me siaman kɛbo.” Yesu kegyu Gyerusalɛm gyoorobiiri man Mateo

english_verse: When Jesus had finished saying all this, he went on toward Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Yesu keyako nombia kɛŋa taŋ nɛ, ɔ tɛɛwɔ balaŋ baŋ siaman te ba moo ba nyee baŋ bee gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man.

english_verse: As he was getting near Bethphage and Bethany on the Mount of Olives, he sent two of his disciples on ahead.
translation_verse: Ɔ kɛɛ benaa Bɛtifagi na Bɛtani donɔɔse ŋan kyaa oliifi bula keŋ teŋa man nɛ, ɔ kpila ɔ kaseela baŋ man balaŋ bala

english_verse: If anyone asks why you are doing this, just say, ‘The Lord needs it.’ ”
translation_verse: Akpaa ŋolo de bɔɔse ŋon nyi, ‘Weera dɔɔ te ɛ ne booli e?’ na ‘Ɛ yako e nyi de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu ne gyae na e.’ ”

english_verse: They went off and found everything just as Jesus had said.
translation_verse: Keŋte baŋ Yesu be kpili nɛ gyuuwɔ kena afurum bu ŋon kpaŋɛɛ ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be yako wɔ nɛ.

english_verse: While they were untying the donkey, its owners asked, “Why are you doing that?”
translation_verse: Be kee booli afurum bu ŋon nɛ ɔ gbeŋgyoo bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Weera dɔɔ te ɛ ne booli ma afurum bu ŋon?”

english_verse: Then they led the donkey to Jesus. They put some of their clothes on its back and helped Jesus get on.
translation_verse: Te ba moo afurum bu ŋon kɔna Yesu. Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ moo be kanyaŋse gyakaa afurum bu ŋon dɔɔ te be gate Yesu gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ.

english_verse: And as he rode along, the people spread clothes on the road in front of him.
translation_verse: Yesu kekyaa ɔ dɔɔ ɔ ne gyuu nɛ, baale fɛlɛɛ be kanyaŋse lɔ gbɛɛnaa man fa e.

english_verse: They shouted, “Blessed is the king who comes in the name of the Lord! Peace in heaven and glory to God.”
translation_verse: Be fae yeesa yako nyiaa, “Ɛ fa dɛɛ lese gyoo ŋon ɔ ne kɔŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ yele man nɛ yele. Wosefɛɛreŋ dɛɛ kyaa adido, te Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa adido mɔ nɛ gyoorobiiri dɛɛ kaboena e.”

english_verse: Some Pharisees in the crowd said to Jesus, “Teacher, make your disciples stop shouting!”
translation_verse: Na Farasiitena baale kpuɛ na balaŋ baŋ be silana e nɛ te be yako Yesu nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, tɔ n silala baŋ nyi baa wu be nɔɔ.”

english_verse: When Jesus came closer and could see Jerusalem, he cried
translation_verse: Yesu ka benaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ te ɔ kena ke nɛ, ɔ wiiwɔ fa ke,

english_verse: and said: It is too bad that today your people don't know what will bring them peace! Now it is hidden from them.
translation_verse: keŋte ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Nafɔ akpaa ɛmɛɛ Gyerusalɛmtena deŋ gyeŋ kpene keŋ gyae ke fa ŋon wosefɛɛreŋ gyɛŋ wee kei nɛ, nafɔ na dei. Mɔna ɛ ba gyeŋ ke ya!

english_verse: Jerusalem, the time will come when your enemies will build walls around you to attack you. Armies will surround you and close in on you from every side.
translation_verse: Te weeya ŋaale gyae kekɔŋ keŋ ɛ kɔlala baa kilisi ŋon, na baa tɔ ŋon do lee yenaŋ kamasɛ.

english_verse: They will level you to the ground and kill your people. Not one stone in your buildings will be left on top of another. This will happen because you did not see that God had come to save you. Jesus in the Temple (Matthew
translation_verse: Na baa wɔlɛɛ ɛmɛɛ na ɛ donɔɔ keŋ pou keŋ nyi boe na boe be gyae ke gyakaa ke dɔɔ dɔɔ ya, nawolo nyi debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ be kɔŋ nyi ɔ kaa lɛɛ ŋon nɛ ɛ bɛɛ e bo.” Yesu kegegi abɔɔyɔɔra Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man Mateo

english_verse: When Jesus entered the temple, he started chasing out the people who were selling things.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu gyuuwɔ ka gyoo Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ dekpaŋalaŋ man. Na balaŋ ne yɔɔ abɔɔ botɔɔ te ɔ gegi wɔ,

english_verse: He told them, “The Scriptures say, ‘My house should be a place of worship.’ But you have made it a place where robbers hide!”
translation_verse: te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako nyi Wurubuarɛ kpa, ‘Me deni dinaa keŋ gyae ke yɛɛ botɔɔ keŋ baa fane. Mɔna ɛmɛɛ ta besena ke yɛɛ ŋmɛɛlatena dɛɛgyaŋee!’ ”

english_verse: Each day, Jesus kept on teaching in the temple. So the chief priests, the teachers of the Law of Moses, and some other important people tried to have him killed.
translation_verse: Wee na wee na Yesu ne kyaa wola balaŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man. Te saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala na donɔɔ kegyiise baŋ gyae gbɛɛ nyi baa ko e.

english_verse: But they could not find a way to do it, because everyone else was eager to listen to him.
translation_verse: Mɔna be ta gyeŋ gbɛɛ keŋ baa mo kyaŋ e ya, nawolo nyi na balaŋ baŋ pou ne tei ɔ nombia ŋan ɔ na wolo nɛ nideli keŋ bɔɔ ne gyae ŋa ke laŋ wɔ ya.

english_verse: The spies said to him, “Teacher, we know you teach the truth about what God wants people to do. And you treat everyone with the same respect, no matter who they are.
translation_verse: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ gyuuwɔ ke bɔɔse e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, da gyeŋ nyi kpene keŋ n ne yako te n na wolo mɔ nɛ yɛɛ ampaŋ. Da gyeŋ nyi n bɛɛ kɛɛ walaŋ siaman ya, mɔna n na wola balaŋ Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛneŋ kpaakpaa ŋan bo na anokoare.

english_verse: The Sadducees did not believe that people would rise to life after death. So some of them came to Jesus
translation_verse: Keŋte Sadukiitena baŋ bɛɛ yako nyi yeŋ wɔle kefoŋ be kyaa ya nɛ man baale kɔŋawɔ Yesu gyaŋ kaa bɔɔse e nyiaa,

english_verse: asked him, “What right do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?”
translation_verse: kaa bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Yako daa, doŋ woŋti yaa n dana n ne yɛɛ mena abɔɔ kɛŋa pou? Amɔte faa na neŋ mena doŋ kenaŋ?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “I want to ask you a question.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Maŋ mɔ ne gyae maa bɔɔse ŋon nombii dokoloŋ.

english_verse: Who gave John the right to baptize? Was it God in heaven or merely some human being?”
translation_verse: Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋkesɔ keŋ ɔ be sɔ balaŋ nɛ, lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ yaa balaŋ gyaŋ?”

english_verse: They talked this over and said to each other, “We can't say God gave John this right. Jesus will ask us why we didn't believe John.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te be lɛɛ aŋmaareŋ na dɔŋa nyiaa, “Woŋ te dɛɛ yako? Akpaa de yako nyi ka lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ na, waa bɔɔse daa nyi woŋ dɔɔ te da te lɛɛ Gyɔn nombia ŋan di ya?

english_verse: And we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize. The crowd will stone us to death, because they think John was a prophet.”
translation_verse: Te akpaa de yako nyi ka lee balaŋ gyaŋ na, balaŋ dikpii kɛwɔ pou baa fuŋii daa boya. Nawolo nyi bɛɛ lɛɛ di nideli nyi Gyɔn yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre e.”

english_verse: So they told Jesus, “We don't know who gave John the right to baptize.”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ be tiranɔɔ fa Yesu nyiaa “Dɔɔ gyeŋ botɔɔ keŋ kɔɔ lee ya.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Then I won't tell you who gave me the right to do what I do.” Renters of a Vineyard (Matthew
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu mɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Akpaa mena na, maŋ mɔ ba wola ŋon me doŋ keŋ da lee ya.” Paasetena baale dudu Mateo

english_verse: Guard against the teachers of the Law of Moses! They love to walk around in long robes, and they like to be greeted in the market. They want the front seats in the synagogues and the best seats at banquets.
translation_verse: “Ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose nideli na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ. Bɛɛ gyae kegbase dɔɔloŋ nyiŋan keta do kila, te bɛɛ gyae balaŋ ke buŋii do wɔ nɔɔ balaŋ dɛɛgyaŋee man, te bɛɛ gyae akyaese kpaakpaa Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ na be dɛɛgyaŋee di gyoŋ man.

english_verse: When it was time to harvest the crop, he sent a servant to ask the renters for his share of the grapes. But they beat up the servant and sent him away without anything.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kelii nyi bɛɛ kɔ daasebia ŋan nɛ, ɔ kpila ɔ tobaale ŋolo gyu paasetena baŋ gyaŋ nyi baa fa e ɔ wui nɔɔ. Botɔɔ te paasetena baŋ tuku tobaale ŋon, te be tina e yela ɔ laŋawɔ nawɔɔ kufu nyiŋmaase kufu.

english_verse: When the renters saw the owner's son, they said to one another, “Someday he will own the vineyard. Let's kill him! Then we can have it all for ourselves.”
translation_verse: Mɔna paasetena baŋ kena bu ŋon nɛ be yako dɔŋa nyiaa, “Kei yɛna bu ŋon waa di ɔ kya abɔɔ ŋan. Ɛ yela dɛɛ ko e na wa abɔɔ ŋan ka bese de wui.”

english_verse: I'll tell you what. He will come and kill those renters and let someone else have his vineyard.” When the people heard this, they said, “This must never happen!”
translation_verse: “Waa kɔŋ na ɔ kaa ko paasetena benaŋ, na waa mo wɔɔ keŋ do balaŋ wɔlɛɛ nyiŋmaa man.” Debaŋ keŋ balaŋ baŋ be nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ be yakowɔ nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ na yeli kekɔŋ mena ya.”

english_verse: The chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses knew that Jesus was talking about them when he was telling this story. They wanted to arrest him right then, but they were afraid of the people. Paying Taxes (Matthew
translation_verse: Botɔɔ na saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ gyae gbɛɛ baa kyaŋ Yesu debokenaŋ man. Nawolo nyi ba gyeŋ nyi baŋ yaa ɔ gyɔɔ dudu keŋ, mɔna na bee yee balaŋ baŋ.

english_verse: Jesus' enemies kept watching him closely, because they wanted to hand him over to the Roman governor. So they sent some men who pretended to be good. But they were really spies trying to catch Jesus saying something wrong.
translation_verse: Keŋ dɔɔ ba moo be sia gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ. Botɔɔ te kegyiise baŋ gyae kɔba fa balaŋ te be kpila wɔ nyi baa yɛɛ ba wose ŋgba be yɛɛ balaŋ kpaakpaawɔ nɛ na baa deke Yesu, na be kaa teese ɔ nɔɔman na baa nyiŋ lese e fa gyoo dinaa ŋon ɔ ne di be dɔɔ nɛ.

english_verse: “Show me a coin.” Then he asked, “Whose picture and name are on it?” “The Emperor's,” they answered.
translation_verse: “Ɛ mo kɔbabii keŋ daale kɔŋ maa kɛɛ. Amɔte nyee na ɔ yele gyakaa la ke dɔɔ?” Botɔɔ te be tiranɔɔ nyi “Romantena gyoo dinaa ŋon.”

english_verse: Then he told them, “Give the Emperor what belongs to him and give God what belongs to God.”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Mena na kpene keŋ yɛɛ Gyoo dinaa ŋon wui na ɛ mo fa e, te kpene keŋ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ mɔ wui na ɛ mo fa e.”

english_verse: Jesus' enemies could not catch him saying anything wrong there in front of the people. They were amazed at his answer and kept quiet. Life in the Future World (Matthew
translation_verse: Kpene keŋ ɔ be yako nɛ dɔɔ ba te tale mo ɔ nɔɔwoya ŋan kyaŋ e balaŋ baŋ siaman ya. Mena dɔɔ ɔ nɔɔwoya yɛɛ wɔ gyakoloŋ te be wuu be nɔɔse. Yesu kawola lee yeŋ wɔle kefoŋ wose man Mateo

english_verse: and said: Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother.
translation_verse: “De Gbeŋgyoo, de naana Mosesi ŋmarase mmaraa kei fa daa nyi, ‘Akpaa walaŋ ŋolo ɔ kegyia yaa ɔ naabu da yem tina ɔ wɛɛle yela te be ta lola bia ya na, kaboena nyi ŋon ɔ kyaa nɛ waa mo lialo ŋon yala na baa lola bia yere ŋon yele man.’

english_verse: There were once seven brothers. The first one married, but died without having any children.
translation_verse: Kɔŋawɔ nyi baala nyetooro baale kyaawɔ te be kegyia ŋon yala alo te ɔ yeŋawɔ keŋ ɔ ta lola bia ya.

english_verse: The second one married his brother's widow, and he also died without having any children.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te balate ŋon mɔ yala alo ŋon,

english_verse: The same thing happened to the third one. Finally, all seven brothers married this woman and died without having any children.
translation_verse: te batoorote ŋon mɔ mena. Balaŋ nyetooro kɛwɔ pou yala alo kei te ba yekeewɔ, mɔna be man ŋolo na alo ŋon ta lolo ya.

english_verse: At last the woman died.
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle na te alo ŋon mɔ yeŋawɔ.

english_verse: Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses said, “Teacher, you have given a good answer!”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ baale yakowɔ nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, n tiranɔɔ nideli.”

english_verse: Jesus asked, “Why do people say that the Messiah will be the son of King David?
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Sena te baa tale yako nyi nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ yɛɛ gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii e?

english_verse: In the book of Psalms, David himself says, ‘The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at my right side
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi gyoo Defidi gbagba yakowɔ nɔɔneŋ tɔne keŋ man nyiaa, ‘De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ ŋon yako me Gbeŋgyoo nyi “Tisi kyaa me dunoluŋ dɔɔ,

english_verse: until I make your enemies into a footstool for you.’
translation_verse: kelii debaŋ keŋ maa mo n kɔlala baŋ do n tɛɛ.” ’

english_verse: David spoke of the Messiah as his Lord, so how can the Messiah be his son?” Jesus and the Teachers of the Law of Moses (Matthew
translation_verse: Gyoo Defidi baake nyeelɛɛre ŋonaŋ nyi ɔ Gbeŋgyoo. Akpaa mena na, sena te nyeelɛɛre ŋonaŋ waa bese gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii?” Yesu kefaree ɔ silala lee Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ wose man Mateo

english_verse: While everyone was listening to Jesus, he said to his disciples:
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ pou kee tei Yesu nɛ ɔ yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa,

english_verse: But they cheat widows out of their homes and then pray long prayers just to show off. These teachers will be punished most of all.
translation_verse: Bɛɛ beo liala lɛo ba abɔɔ ŋan be dana nɛ. Bɛɛ fane kyare na baa mo beo balaŋ nyi be yɛɛ balaŋ kpaakpaawɔ.” Wurubuarɛ waa wɔŋ mena balaŋ kɛwɔ dinɔɔ deŋela nideli kela walaŋ kamasɛ.

english_verse: Before all this happens, you will be arrested and punished. You will be tried in your synagogues and put in jail. Because of me you will be placed on trial before kings and governors.
translation_verse: Mɔna pɛte kɛŋa pou kekɔŋ nɛ, lee maŋ dɔɔ baa kyaŋ ŋon na baa naase ŋon diyem. Baa mo ŋon fa Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man kegyiise na gyooneŋ na gyooneŋ kegyiise na baa di ɛ nombia na baa do ŋon denɛɛ man.

english_verse: Some people were talking about the beautiful stones used to build the temple and about the gifts that had been placed in it. Jesus said,
translation_verse: Na Yesu kaseela baŋ man baale ne kolosi Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ kyeo na mena keŋ bɔɔ mo boya kpaakpaa nyiŋan ma ke, na abɔɔ kpaakpaa ŋan balaŋ ba mo kaa fa Wurubuarɛ te bɔɔ mo desina deni kenaŋ nɛ wose man nombia. Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa,

english_verse: “Do you see these stones? The time is coming when not one of them will be left in place. They will all be knocked down.” Warning about Trouble (Matthew
translation_verse: “Abɔɔ ŋan pou ɛ ne naa kɛbo nɛ, debaŋ daale gyae kekɔŋ keŋ baa tekerii ŋe popou lɔ keŋ nyi ɛ be gyae ɛ kena boe na boe gyakaa ke dɔɔ dɔɔ ya.”
