english_verse: Some people asked, “Teacher, when will all this happen? How can we know when these things are about to take place?”
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ kaseela baŋ bɔɔse e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, debaŋ woŋti te mena nombia kɛŋa gyae kekɔŋ? Woŋ ne gyae la ke yɛɛ na kawola nyi debaŋ keŋ nombia kɛŋa gyae ke kɔŋ nɛ te lii?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: Don't be fooled by those who will come and claim to be me. They will say, “I am Christ!” and “Now is the time!” But don't follow them.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose nideli nyi ŋolo na beo ŋon ya. Nawolo nyi balaŋ burum baa kɔŋ ma yele man nyi, ‘Baŋ yɛna maŋ nyeelɛɛre ŋon,’ na baa yako ŋon nyi ‘Debaŋ keŋ te lii.’ Akpaa ɛ nyii mena na ɛ na sila wɔ ya.

english_verse: When you hear about wars and riots, don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but this isn't the end.
translation_verse: Te akpaa ɛ nyii yoo na yoo nombia na ɛ na yeli gyakoloŋ kekyaŋ ŋon ya, nawolo nyi kaboena nyi mena nombia kɛŋa pou dɛɛkɔŋ. Mɔna kɛŋa bɛɛ wolo nyi debaŋ ɔtomante keŋ gyae ke kɔŋ debokenaŋ man ya.”

english_verse: Nations will go to war against one another, and kingdoms will attack each other.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Tɛɛlese gyae ka koro yokii na dɔŋa, te gyooneŋ mɔ baa yokii na dɔŋa.

english_verse: There will be great earthquakes, and in many places people will starve to death and suffer terrible diseases. All sorts of frightening things will be seen in the sky.
translation_verse: Tɛɛle gyae ke wosi, tanam dinaa gyae ke yala tɛɛlese dɔɔ te kaweese mɔ gyae ke gbiki tɛɛlese dɔɔ. Gyakoloŋ nombia na nombia dinaana gyae ka lee adido kɔŋ.

english_verse: You will be betrayed by your own parents, brothers, family, and friends. Some of you will even be killed.
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ kyaɔ na ɛ naɔ na ɛ naabia na ɛ loŋtona na ɛ gyoona ne gyae la baa lese ŋon fa, na baa koe ɛ man baale.

english_verse: Because of me, you will be hated by everyone.
translation_verse: Lee maŋ dɔɔ balaŋ pou baa kɔla ŋon.

english_verse: If you are living in Judea at this time, run to the mountains. If you are in the city, leave it. And if you are out in the country, don't go back into the city.
translation_verse: Akpaa de kɔŋ mena na balaŋ baŋ be kyaa Gyudia tɛɛle man nɛ, baa yeu gyu bulase dɔɔ te baŋ mɔ be kyaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ baa lee ke man, te baŋ mɔ be kyaa ka yenaase nɛ be na bese kɔŋ donɔɔ keŋ man ya.

english_verse: When you see Jerusalem surrounded by soldiers, you will know that it will soon be destroyed.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyi, “Debaŋ keŋ ɛ naa yoonɔɔtena de kaa kilisi Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ nɛ, botɔɔ te ɛ ka gyeŋ nyi ka kewɔlɛɛ ta benaa.

english_verse: This time of punishment is what is written about in the Scriptures.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi kei yɛna deŋele kewɔŋ keŋ gyae ke kɔŋ ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan be yako nɛ.

english_verse: It will be an awful time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children! Everywhere in the land people will suffer horribly and be punished.
translation_verse: Weeya ŋenaŋ man nɛ, fulɛɛtena na talabose baa laako. Nawolo nyi kayeyɛɛ dinaa gyae ke kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ, na Wurubuarɛ deŋele kewɔŋ kekɔŋ balaŋ kɛwɔ dɔɔ.

english_verse: Then Jesus told them a story: When you see a fig tree or any other tree
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu gyɔɔ dudu fa wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ kɛɛ fiigi daŋ keŋ na daase akaŋ ŋan pou.

english_verse: So, when you see these things happening, you know that God's kingdom will soon be here.
translation_verse: Mena mɔ te akpaa ɛ naa mena abɔɔ kɛŋa ne kɔŋ na, ɛ gyeŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ ta benaa.

english_verse: Then the Son of Man will be seen, coming in a cloud with power and great glory.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ nɛ, ɛ kena maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon ta mo doŋ na gyoorobiiri lee ŋɔma man adido mɛɛ kɔŋ.

english_verse: Strange things will happen to the sun, moon, and stars. The nations on earth will be afraid of the roaring sea and tides, and they won't know what to do.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yakowɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ kena gyakoloŋ nombia weese na gyaalaŋ na kyolobiise man, te gyakoloŋ gyae ka gyoo tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ pou te bɔɔ gyae baa gyeŋ kpene keŋ baa yɛɛ ya, lee mena keŋ ɛpo ne muusu te kɛɛ gyɔɔ nedoŋ nɛ dɔɔ.

english_verse: People will be so frightened that they will faint because of what is happening to the world. Every power in the sky will be shaken.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ bɛɛ wɔlɛɛ na nombia ŋan gyae ke yɛɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, gyakoloŋ gyae ke yela balaŋ woya ke yekii lee be man, nawolo nyi abɔɔ ŋan ŋe kyaa adido te ŋe dana doŋ nɛ pou gyae ke wosi.

english_verse: You can be sure that some of the people of this generation will still be alive when all of this takes place.
translation_verse: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, mena duuluŋ kei be gyae ke munu pɛte abɔɔ kɛŋa pou ke kɔŋ ya.

english_verse: like a trap. This day will surprise everyone on earth.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi mena wee kenaŋ gyae ke kɔŋ tɛɛle kei balaŋ pou dɔɔ.

english_verse: putting out leaves, you know that summer will soon come.
translation_verse: Akpaa ɛ naa ŋgba ŋe tombia ne pɔrɔkee na, ɛ gyeŋ nyi dekpao ta benaa.

english_verse: The sky and the earth won't last forever, but my words will. A Warning
translation_verse: Adido na tɛɛle kei pou gyae ke gyu ɔto, mɔna me nɔɔwoya ŋan dɔɔ ŋan gyae ke kyaa kekpaakekpaa.”

english_verse: Don't spend all of your time thinking about eating or drinking or worrying about life. If you do, the final day will suddenly catch you
translation_verse: “Ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose dɔɔ nideli! Ɛ na yeli tɛɛle kei dɔɔ abɔɔ kegyae ka bere ɛ sia ya. Ɛ na mo soloŋ kenyɔɔ do ɛ wose ya. Ɛ na gyueŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nombia beiŋ, na wee keŋ be nyiŋ kɔŋ kaa futaa ŋon ya.

english_verse: Watch out and keep praying that you can escape all that is going to happen and that the Son of Man will be pleased with you.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose dɔɔ nideli debaŋ kamasɛ na ɛ ke fane na ɛ kenyiŋ tale lese ɛ wose lee nombia ŋan pou gyae ke kɔŋ nɛ man, na ɛ ke nyiŋ tale seŋ maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon siaman.”

english_verse: Jesus taught in the temple each day, and he spent each night on the Mount of Olives.
translation_verse: Wee kamasɛ na, Yesu na wolo Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man te balinɔɔ de lii na ɔ ne gyu ke doo oliifi bula keŋ dɔɔ.

english_verse: Everyone got up early and came to the temple to hear him teach.
translation_verse: Tɛɛ de naa nebo nebo na, balaŋ baŋ pou ne gyu ke yilaa Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man na baa nyii ɔ nombia ŋan.

english_verse: They were very pleased and offered to pay Judas some money.
translation_verse: Yɛɛ kegyiise baŋ gyoŋ te be sɛɛwɔ nyi baa fa e kɔba.

english_verse: Jesus got up from praying and went over to his disciples. They were asleep and worn out from being so sad.
translation_verse: Ɔ ke fane taŋ keŋ nɛ te ɔ besewɔ gyu ɔ kaseela baŋ gyaŋ. Ɔ ke gyu keŋ na ɔ ke naawɔ ŋgba ɔ kaseela baŋ te dokii, nawolo nyi dekɔkɔrɔkɔɔ te kyaŋ wɔ.

english_verse: Judas went to talk with the chief priests and the officers of the temple police about how he could help them arrest Jesus.
translation_verse: Keŋte Gyudase gyuuwɔ te ŋon na Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ kotɔtɔɔtena kegyiise bakewɔ see mena keŋ waa lese Yesu fa wɔ.

english_verse: He agreed and started looking for a good chance to betray Jesus when the crowds were not around. Jesus Eats with His Disciples (Matthew
translation_verse: Keŋte Gyudase mɔ sɛɛwɔ te ɔ gyae gbɛɛ keŋ waa mo lese Yesu fa wɔ keŋ balaŋ ba gyem ya. Yesu na ɔ kaseela kedi nyeekelɛɛwee dinaa weenɛɛ ŋan Mateo

english_verse: Jesus took a cup of wine in his hands and gave thanks to God. Then he told the apostles, “Take this wine and share it with each other.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu moo leenɔɔ keŋ te ɔ fanewɔ do Wurubuarɛ nɔɔ te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ lɛɛ kei na ɛ ke nyɔɔ fa dɔŋa.

english_verse: The one who will betray me is here at the table with me!
translation_verse: Mɔna mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, walaŋ ŋon waa lese maŋ fa nɛ kpuɛ na maŋ weenɛɛ kɛŋa dedii man kɛbo.

english_verse: You will eat and drink with me in my kingdom, and you will each sit on a throne to judge the twelve tribes of Israel. Jesus' Disciples Will Be Tested (Matthew
translation_verse: Na daa na ŋon kedi na dɛɛ nyɔɔ me gyoori keŋ man, na ɛ ke kyaa me gyooro gbelɛɛ ŋan dɔɔ ɛ ke di Iseraetena akpaŋalaŋ kufu ala ŋan balaŋ nombia.” Yesu keyako nyi Pita waa bɛɛ e Mateo

english_verse: The day had come for the Festival of Thin Bread, and it was time to kill the Passover lambs.
translation_verse: Gyudatena wee dinaa keŋ bee di bodobodo keŋ bodobodo tee be doo ke man ya, te bɛɛ mo nyeekelɛɛ nambu ŋon mɔ deesi kelii nɛ,

english_verse: So Jesus said to Peter and John, “Go and prepare the Passover meal for us to eat.”
translation_verse: Yesu kpila Pita na Gyɔn nyiaa, “Ɛ gyu ke desina abɔɔ ŋan dɛɛ mo di nyeekelɛɛ weenɛɛ ŋan nɛ see.”

english_verse: But they asked, “Where do you want us to prepare it?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te be bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Aleŋ te n ne gyae de kaa desina see?”

english_verse: The Son of Man will die in the way that has been decided for him, but it will be terrible for the one who betrays him!”
translation_verse: Maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa yeŋ ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yako see nɛ, mɔna walaŋ ŋon waa lese maŋ fa nɛ waa laako.”

english_verse: Then the apostles started arguing about who would ever do such a thing. An Argument about Greatness
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ gyoo dɔŋa ke bɔɔse nyiaa, “De man walaŋ woŋti kitii ne gyae la waa yɛɛ mena nombii kei?” Amɔte yɛna kegyia? Mateo

english_verse: he said to them, “I have very much wanted to eat this Passover meal with you before I suffer.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Me gyaewɔ nideli me konɔɔ man nyi daa na ŋon ke di mena nyeekelɛɛ weenɛɛ kɛŋa pɛna me kaa naa diyem!

english_verse: You have stayed with me in all my troubles.
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ yɛna baŋ ɛ ba sem me wɔle lee me kayeyɛɛ man pou,

english_verse: So I will give you the right to rule as kings, just as my Father has given me the right to rule as a king.
translation_verse: mena dɔɔ mɛɛ see ŋon gyoori ŋgba mena keŋ me kya ba see maŋ gyoo nɛ.

english_verse: Jesus said, “Simon, listen to me! Satan has demanded the right to test each one of you, as a farmer does when he separates wheat from the husks.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako Simɔn Pita nyi “Simɔn, Simɔn nyii! Ɔbɔnsam ta sola gbɛɛ lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ waa kɛɛse ɛ popou kɛɛ na waa kpase ɛ man ŋgba mena keŋ bee feefu amɔla lese bia nɛ.

english_verse: Jesus asked his disciples, “When I sent you out without a moneybag or a traveling bag or sandals, did you need anything?” “No!” they answered.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Debaŋ keŋ mɔɔ kpila ŋon keŋ ɛ ta mo kɔba yaa akpo, yaa ɛ ta mo nkyokotase tela ya nɛ kolo hia ŋon?” Keŋte be tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Koa, kolo te hia daa ya.”

english_verse: The disciples said, “Lord, here are two swords!” “Enough of that!” Jesus replied. Jesus Prays (Matthew
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ yako e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo kɛɛ silase ala yaa.” Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyi, “Tekaboe mena.” Yesu kefane Gɛkyɛmane Mateo

english_verse: Peter said, “Lord, I am ready to go with you to jail and even to die with you.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Pita tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Me Gbeŋgyoo, nyi akpaa ke yɛɛ deni kedoo ke yaa yeŋ ke koraŋ na, ma te desina ma wose nyi maa sila neŋ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Peter, I tell you that before a rooster crows tomorrow morning, you will say three times that you don't know me.” Moneybags, Traveling Bags, and Swords
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Pita, mɛɛ yako neŋ nyi pɛte kegyim kelɔ woya gyɛŋ na, nyaŋ ke bɛɛ betabotooro nyi n ba gyeŋ maŋ korakora ya.”

english_verse: He said to them, “Why are you asleep? Wake up and pray that you won't be tested.” Jesus Is Arrested (Matthew
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Weera ɛ ne doo? Ɛ koro na ɛ ke fane na ɛ be nyiŋ gyoo kekɛɛsekɛɛ man ya.” Ba kekyaŋ Yesu Mateo

english_verse: “Father, if you will, please don't make me suffer by drinking from this cup. But do what you want, and not what I want.”
translation_verse: “Taata, akpaa ke yɛɛ n kegyaebii ke na, lese maŋ lee kaale kei man. Mɔna na yɛɛ maŋ kegyaebii ya, yɛɛ nyaŋ gbagba n kegyaebii.”

english_verse: Jesus went out to the Mount of Olives, as he often did, and his disciples went with him.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu leewɔ donɔɔ keŋ man gyu oliifi bula keŋ dɔɔ ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ ne kyaa gyu nɛ, te ɔ kaseela baŋ sila e gyu.

english_verse: When they got there, he told them, “Pray that you won't be tested.”
translation_verse: Be kelii botɔɔ nɛ Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Ɛ fane na ɛ be nyiŋ gyoo kekɛɛsekɛɛ man ya.”

english_verse: Jesus walked on a little way before he knelt down and prayed,
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ wɔŋa ɔ wose lee yenaŋ gyu siaman kyomii te ɔ ke buŋawɔ fane nyiaa,

english_verse: Jesus was in great pain and prayed so sincerely that his sweat fell to the ground like drops of blood.
translation_verse: Yesu kɛɛ yeyɛɛ keŋ nɛ, ɔ kyaŋa ɔ wose fane nideli te dufuŋ keŋ ba lee e nɛ kyɔseewɔ yala tɛɛle man ŋgba fatabo nɛ.

english_verse: While Jesus was still speaking, a crowd came up. It was led by Judas, one of the twelve apostles. He went over to Jesus and greeted him with a kiss.
translation_verse: Yesu ka seŋ ɔ ne kolosi nɛ balaŋ dikpii baale kaa bɛɛwɔ lee ɔ dɔɔ, na Gyudase ŋon ɔ yɛɛ Yesu kaseela kufu bala baŋ man ŋolo e nɛ dɔŋɛɛ la be siaman. Ɔ kena Yesu nɛ te ɔ kɔŋawɔ ɔ kaa pukaa e.

english_verse: and a servant girl saw him. Then after she had looked at him carefully, she said, “This man was with Jesus!”
translation_verse: Alebia baŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ tom dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man nɛ ŋolo naa Pita kyaa boalaŋ keŋ gyaŋ, te ɔ gyene e nideli te ɔ yakowɔ nyi “Na balee kei mɔ kpuɛ na Yesu!”

english_verse: One of the disciples even struck at the high priest's servant with his sword and cut off the servant's right ear.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu kaseela baŋ man ŋolo moo ɔ yoosila kara saese kegyia tobaale ŋolo dunoluŋ deŋele lese ke lɔ.

english_verse: Jesus asked Judas, “Are you betraying the Son of Man with a kiss?”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Gyudase, kepukaa te nyaŋ ka mo lese maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon fa?”

english_verse: When Jesus' disciples saw what was about to happen, they asked, “Lord, should we attack them with a sword?”
translation_verse: Yesu kaseela baŋ kena kpene keŋ ne gyae ke kɔŋ nɛ te be bɔɔse e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, dɛɛ mo de silase na dee yoo?”

english_verse: “Enough of that!” Jesus said. Then he touched the servant's ear and healed it.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ tina yela, ɛ na yɛɛ mena ya.” Te ɔ tinaa balee ŋon deŋele keŋ te ka towaawɔ bela.

english_verse: Jesus spoke to the chief priests, the temple police, and the leaders who had come to arrest him. He said, “Why do you come out with swords and clubs and treat me like a criminal?
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse saese kegyiise na Wurubuarɛ dɛɛ kotɔtɔɔtena kegyiise na kegyiise baŋ bɔɔ kɔŋ botɔɔ be kaa kyaŋ e nɛ nyiaa, “Weera te ɛ moo yoosilase na akpokpose ɛ ne kɔŋ me dɔɔ ŋgba me yɛɛ walaŋkum e nɛ?

english_verse: Peter said, “Woman, I don't even know that man!”
translation_verse: Keŋte Pita lɛɛ aŋmaareŋ nyiaa, “Alebu, mɔɔ gyeŋ e ya.”

english_verse: A little later someone else saw Peter and said, “You are one of them!” “No, I'm not!” Peter replied.
translation_verse: Te kyare ya te ŋolo mɔ naa e te ɔ yako e bela nyiaa, “Nyaŋ mɔ n yɛɛ be man ŋolo e!” Keŋte Pita tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Baale, mɔɔ kpuɛ na wɔ ya!”

english_verse: Jesus was arrested and led away to the house of the high priest, while Peter followed at a distance.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te be kyaŋa Yesu mo e gyu Wurubuarɛ saese kegyia ŋon dekpaŋalaŋ man, na Pita mɔ silana wɔ wɔle wɔle.

english_verse: At daybreak the nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses got together and brought Jesus before their council.
translation_verse: Tɛɛ kena keŋ nɛ, donɔɔ kegyiise na Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ gyaŋeewɔ te ba moo Yesu kɔŋ kaa seŋ be siaman.

english_verse: Peter replied, “I don't know what you are talking about!” Right then, while Peter was still speaking, a rooster crowed.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Pita tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Baale, mɔɔ gyeŋ nombia ŋan n ne yako nɛ man ya.” Pita ke gyakaa ɔ ne kolosi nɛ te kegyim lɔɔ woya.

english_verse: The Lord turned and looked at Peter. And Peter remembered that the Lord had said, “Before a rooster crows tomorrow morning, you will say three times that you don't know me.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu gɛɛsewɔ kɛɛ Pita, te Pita tɔɔse de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu nombia ŋan ɔ be yako e nɛ nyiaa, “Pɛ kegyim ke lɔ woya gyɛŋ na, nyaŋ ke bɛɛ betabotooro nyi n ba gyeŋ maŋ ya nɛ.”

english_verse: The men who were guarding Jesus made fun of him and beat him.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ kɛɛ Yesu dɔɔ nɛ fiasɛɛ bee kulu e na bee tuku e.

english_verse: They kept on insulting Jesus in many other ways. Jesus Is Questioned by the Council (Matthew
translation_verse: Te ba tee e tuubia kpaŋ kpaŋ kpu. Be kamo Yesu gyu donɔɔ kegyiise siaman Mateo

english_verse: And if I asked you a question, you wouldn't answer.
translation_verse: te akpaa me bɔɔse ŋon nombia mɔ nɛ ɛ be gyae ɛ ke tiranɔɔ fa maŋ ya.

english_verse: But from now on, the Son of Man will be seated at the right side of God All-Powerful.”
translation_verse: Mɔna lee debaŋ kei ne gyu nɛ, maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa kyaa Wurubuarɛ Kpatakpere ŋon dunoluŋ dɔɔ.”

english_verse: Then they asked, “Are you the Son of God?” Jesus answered, “You say I am!”
translation_verse: Keŋte be popou bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Akpaa mena na nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon?” Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ nyi, “Mena yaa ɛ ne yako nɛ.”

english_verse: They replied, “Why do we need more witnesses? He said it himself!”
translation_verse: Keŋte be yakowɔ nyiaa, “Woŋ nombia yaa dɛɛ gyae dɛɛ bese nyii kpu bela. Daa gbagba te nyii lee ŋon gbagba nɔɔman!”

english_verse: Everyone in the council got up and led Jesus off to Pilate.
translation_verse: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ gyaŋee botɔɔ nɛ pou korowɔ mo Yesu gyu Pilato ŋon ɔ seŋɛɛ Romantena gyoo dinaa nawɔɔ man nɛ gyaŋ,

english_verse: They started accusing him and said, “We caught this man trying to get our people to riot and to stop paying taxes to the Emperor. He also claims that he is the Messiah, our king.”
translation_verse: te be fiasɛɛ bɛɛ mo nombia gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ nyiaa, “De naa balee kei dɔŋ de balaŋ siaman ɔ na wola wɔ nombiakumɛɛ keyɛɛ. Ɔ ne bɛɛ nyi be na tɔ lempoo fa gyoo dinaa Kaesa ya, te ɔ ne gyɔ ɔ konɔɔ yako nyi ŋon yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ, te ɔ yɛɛ gyoo mɔ e.”

english_verse: Herod and his soldiers made fun of Jesus and insulted him. They put a fine robe on him and sent him back to Pilate.
translation_verse: Keŋte gyoo Herode na ɔ yoonɔɔtena baŋ kulu Yesu te ba do e desɛŋ. Ba doo e gyooro kegba te ɔ yela ba besena e gyina Pilato.

english_verse: He told them, “You brought Jesus to me and said he was a troublemaker. But I have questioned him here in front of you, and I have not found him guilty of anything that you say he has done.
translation_verse: te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛmɛɛ moona balee kei kɔŋ me gyaŋ te ɛ kpa ɔ na beo balaŋ baŋ. Maŋ gyoo ke man ɛ siaman kɛbo mɔna ma te naa nombiakumɛɛ ŋan ɔ be yɛɛ te ɛ ne kara ɔ nɔɔ ya.

english_verse: But they all kept on saying, “He has been teaching and causing trouble all over Judea. He started in Galilee and has now come all the way here.” Jesus Is Brought before Herod
translation_verse: Mɔna nɛnɛɛ gba te ba seŋɛɛ be nɔɔwoya dɔɔ keŋkeŋ nyi, “Ɔ ta mo ɔ kawola keŋ wɔlɛɛ balaŋ agyueŋ Gyudia tɛɛle kei dɔɔ pou. Ɔ fiasɛɛ lee Galelia tɛɛle dɔɔ te nɛnɛɛ ɔ te kaa lii de gyaŋ kɛbo.”

english_verse: Pilate asked Jesus, “Are you the king of the Jews?” “Those are your words,” Jesus answered.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Pilato bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, “Nyaŋ yɛna Gyudatena gyoo ŋon?” Te Yesu tiranɔɔ nyi, “Mena yaa n yakowɔ nɛ.”

english_verse: Pilate told the chief priests and the crowd, “I don't find him guilty of anything.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Pilato yako saese kegyiise baŋ na balaŋ dikpii keŋ nyiaa, “Ma te naa nombiikum keŋ balee kei be yɛɛ keŋ kaboena nyi maa bu e fɔɔ ya.”

english_verse: After Pilate learned that Jesus came from the region ruled by Herod, he sent him to Herod, who was in Jerusalem at that time.
translation_verse: Pilato ka gyeŋ nyi Yesu lee gyoo Herode tɛɛle man nɛ, ɔ yela ba mo e gyu gyoo Herode gyaŋ. Debaŋ kenaŋ na gyoo Herode mɔ kyaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man botɔɔ.

english_verse: For a long time Herod had wanted to see Jesus and was very happy because he finally had this chance. He had heard many things about Jesus and hoped to see him work a miracle.
translation_verse: Gyoo Herode kena Yesu nɛ yɛɛ e gyoŋ nideli, nawolo nyi ɔ wulaa nyii Yesu wose man nombia te ɔ ne gyae e mɔ waa naa. Lee mena keŋ ɔ be nyii Yesu wose man nombia dɔɔ nɛ, wa agyueŋ yɛna nyi waa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia wola e.

english_verse: Herod asked him a lot of questions, but Jesus did not answer.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ gyoo Herode bɔɔse Yesu nombia burum, mɔna ɔ te tira ŋe man dokoloŋ nɔɔ ya.

english_verse: Then the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses stood up and accused him of all kinds of bad things.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ na saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ seŋɛɛ keŋkeŋ bɛɛ kɛrɛɛ nombia dinaana gyakaa Yesu dɔɔ.

english_verse: That same day Herod and Pilate became friends, even though they had been enemies before this. The Death Sentence (Matthew
translation_verse: Lee wee kenaŋ te gyoo Herode na Pilato nsana besewɔ de, nafɔ na bɔɔ na gyoo ya.

english_verse: As Jesus was being led away, some soldiers grabbed hold of a man named Simon who was from Cyrene. He was coming in from the fields, but they put the cross on him and made him carry it behind Jesus.
translation_verse: Be ka mo Yesu lee Pilato gyaŋ bee gyuu nɛ, be gyaŋee na balee ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Simɔn ŋon ɔ ba lee Kirene donɔɔ man nɛ, na ɔ ta lee akuraa ɔ ne kɔŋ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man. Keŋte be kyaŋa e te ba ŋere e mo ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ seele e na ɔ silana Yesu wɔle wɔle.

english_verse: One of the criminals hanging there also insulted Jesus by saying, “Aren't you the Messiah? Save yourself and save us!”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra bala baŋ be sakaa ɔpaŋdaase dɔɔ nɛ man ŋolo tee Yesu nyiaa, “Na nyaŋ yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon yee? Lɛɛ n wose na n ke lɛɛ daa mɔ.”

english_verse: Pilate called together the chief priests, the leaders, and the people.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Pilato baake saese kegyiise baŋ na donɔɔ kegyiise na balaŋ baŋ pou gyaŋee,

english_verse: Pilate wanted to set Jesus free, so he spoke again to the crowds.
translation_verse: Keŋ Pilato ne gyae waa tina Yesu yela dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ besewɔ kolosi fa balaŋ baŋ.

english_verse: Pilate spoke to them a third time, “But what crime has he done? I have not found him guilty of anything for which he should be put to death. I will have him beaten with a whip and set free.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Pilato besewɔ kolosi fa wɔ betabotoorote nyiaa, “Woŋ nombiikum yaa balee kei yɛɛwɔ? Maŋ te naa kpene keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ keŋ kaboena nyi baa ko e ya. Mena dɔɔ maa yela baa tuku e na maa tina e yela.”

english_verse: The people kept on shouting as loud as they could for Jesus to be put to death.
translation_verse: Mɔna balaŋ baŋ seŋɛɛ keŋkeŋ bɛɛ fae yeesa nedoŋ nyi baa gyɔ e sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ, te be yeesa ŋan kela Pilato.

english_verse: He freed the man who was in jail for rioting and murder, because he was the one the crowd wanted to be set free. Then Pilate handed Jesus over for them to do what they wanted with him. Jesus Is Nailed to a Cross (Matthew
translation_verse: Keŋte Pilato tinaa Barabase ŋon bɔɔ kyam tɔ deni nɛ yela fa wɔ. Barabase kei tɛɛ la balaŋ baale siaman te ba korowɔ seŋ tia kegyiise te ɔ koo walaŋ mɔ. Ŋon yɛna walaŋ ŋon balaŋ baŋ be yako nyi waa tina e yela fa wɔ nɛ. Te ɔ moo Yesu fa wɔ nyi baa gyu ke yɛɛ e ba kegyaebii. Ba kegyɔ Yesu sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ Mateo

english_verse: If this can happen when the wood is green, what do you think will happen when it is dry?
translation_verse: Akpaa daŋ laŋte ne kpaa la nnɛ na, sena te daŋ dowolo gyae ke yɛɛ?”

english_verse: Around noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until the middle of the afternoon.
translation_verse: Weese ka seŋ dɛɛ nɛ, weese tinaa kegyɔ yela te ditiŋtɛɛ lɛɛ tɛɛle keŋ dɔɔ pou kelii debaŋ keŋ weese be gɛɛse nɛ.

english_verse: Two criminals were led out to be put to death with Jesus.
translation_verse: Keŋte ba moo nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra bala baale mɔ kaa kpu na Yesu na be kaa ko wɔ.

english_verse: Jesus said, “Father, forgive these people! They don't know what they're doing.” While the crowd stood there watching Jesus, the soldiers gambled for his clothes. The leaders insulted him by saying, “He saved others. Now he should save himself, if he really is God's chosen Messiah!”
translation_verse: Na balaŋ burum seŋɛɛ botɔɔ bɛɛ kɛɛ te Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ mɔ kulu e nyiaa, “Ɔ ne lɛɛ balaŋ nyee, ɛ yela waa lɛɛ ɔ wose na dɛɛ kɛɛ. Ŋgba ŋon kpa ŋon yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ.”

english_verse: The soldiers made fun of Jesus and brought him some wine.
translation_verse: Keŋte yoonɔɔtena baŋ mɔ kulu e te be faa e soloŋ keŋ kɔɔ nyaŋe nɛ,

english_verse: They said, “If you are the king of the Jews, save yourself!”
translation_verse: te be yakowɔ nyiaa, “Akpaa nyaŋ yɛna Gyudatena gyoo ŋon na, kenaŋ na lɛɛ n wose!”

english_verse: But the other criminal told the first one off, “Don't you fear God? Aren't you getting the same punishment as this man?
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ balate ŋon gyɔɔ ɔ nɔɔ dɔɔ nyiaa, “N bee yee Wurubuarɛ ŋgba de popou doo deŋele kewɔŋ dokoloŋ man yee?

english_verse: Jesus replied, “I promise that today you will be with me in paradise.” The Death of Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako neŋ nyiaa, gyɛŋ nyaŋ ke kyaa me gyaŋ Wurubuarɛ man.” Yesu yeŋ Mateo

english_verse: The sun stopped shining, and the curtain in the temple split down the middle.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te kanyaŋ keŋ kpaŋɛɛ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man nɛ bɛɛsewɔ kyɛɛkyɛɛ ala.

english_verse: A crowd had gathered to see the terrible sight. Then after they had seen it, they felt brokenhearted and went home.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ gyaŋee botɔɔ bɛɛ kɛɛ nɛ naa kpene keŋ be kɔŋ, te ba besewɔ gyu donɔɔ man na ba nyiŋmaase gyakaa ba nyeeya dɔɔ waraŋase man.

english_verse: All of Jesus' close friends and the women who had come with him from Galilee stood at a distance and watched. Jesus Is Buried (Matthew
translation_verse: Mɔna baŋ pou ba gyeŋ Yesu na ala baŋ bɔɔ sila e lee Galelia donɔɔ man nɛ, seŋawɔ lemlem bɛɛ kɛɛ nombia kɛŋa. Yesu kewuu Mateo

english_verse: There was a man named Joseph, who was from Arimathea in Judea. Joseph was a good and honest man, and he was eager for God's kingdom to come. He was also a member of the council, but he did not agree with what they had decided.
translation_verse: Balee ŋolo kyaawɔ na bɛɛ baake e nyi Gyosɛfo. Ɔ lee Gyudatena donɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi Arimatia nɛ man. Na ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman te bee bu e mɔ nideli. Ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne kɛɛ Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ siaman te ŋon mɔ kpuɛ Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ man, mɔna ɔ te lɛɛ kpene keŋ bɔɔ bake na kpene keŋ bɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ do ya.

english_verse: Joseph went to Pilate and asked for Jesus' body.
translation_verse: Mena balee kei gyuuwɔ Pilato gyaŋ ka sola gbɛɛ nyi waa mo Yesu woo ŋon ke wuu.

english_verse: He took the body down from the cross and wrapped it in fine cloth. Then he put it in a tomb that had been cut out of solid rock and had never been used.
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ ka lese woo ŋon lee ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ dɔɔ te ɔ moo akaralɛɛ milii e te ɔ moo e ke wuu yebɔɔ keŋ bɔɔ kare boe man te be te wuu ta walaŋ na walaŋ ke man see ya nɛ man.

english_verse: It was Friday, and the Sabbath was about to begin.
translation_verse: Wee kenaŋ yɛɛ wee keŋ Gyudatena na mo desina ba wose fa ba kefɛɛfowee keŋ kedi.

english_verse: The women who had come with Jesus from Galilee followed Joseph and watched how Jesus' body was placed in the tomb.
translation_verse: Ala baŋ bɔɔ sila Yesu lee Galelia kɔŋ nɛ, sila Gyosɛfo gyu te ba ke naa yebɔɔ keŋ na mena keŋ ɔ be wuu Yesu.

english_verse: Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and some other women were the ones who had gone to the tomb. When they returned, they told the eleven apostles and the others what had happened.
translation_verse: Mɛɛre Magedala na Gyoana na Gyemisi ɔ naa Mɛɛre, na ala akaŋ baŋ be kpuɛ na wɔ nɛ gyuu la ke yako nombia kɛŋa fa kpilala baŋ.

english_verse: When they found the stone rolled away from the entrance,
translation_verse: Be kelii botɔɔ nɛ be naawɔ ŋgba boe keŋ bɔɔ mo tɔ yebɔɔ keŋ nɔɔman nɛ te biliŋi laŋ yenaŋ,

english_verse: they went in. But they did not find the body of the Lord Jesus,
translation_verse: mena dɔɔ ba gyoowɔ yebɔɔ keŋ man mɔna ba te naa de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu woo ŋon ya.

english_verse: and they did not know what to think. Suddenly two men in shining white clothes stood beside them.
translation_verse: Be ka seŋ botɔɔ bɛɛ gyueŋ nombia kɛŋa man nɛ, karatetee dokoloŋ te baala bala baŋ ba doona kegbase wolomɛɛ ŋan ŋɛɛ ŋalakee ŋgba akaralɛɛ nɛ kaa bɛɛwɔ lee be dɔɔ.

english_verse: The women were afraid and bowed to the ground. But the men said, “Why are you looking in the place of the dead for someone who is alive?
translation_verse: Gyakoloŋ kyaŋa ala baŋ te be gɔŋase ba nyeeya. Keŋte baala baŋ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Weera dɔɔ te ɛ ne kɛo walaŋ ŋon ɔ delena nɛ woya dɛɛ?

english_verse: ‘The Son of Man will be handed over to sinners who will nail him to a cross. But three days later he will rise to life.’ ”
translation_verse: ‘Kaboena nyi baa lese maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon fa nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra, na baa gyɔ maŋ nyeelabia sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ na maa yeŋ. Mɔna weeya atoorote na Wurubuarɛ waa gyuusu maŋ lee yeŋ man.’ ”

english_verse: That same day two of Jesus' disciples were going to the village of Emmaus, which was about eleven kilometers from Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Mena wee kenaŋ nɛ, Yesu kaseela bala baale leewɔ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man bee gyu donɔɔbii daale keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi Emause nɛ man. Lee botɔɔ gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man gyae ke yɛɛ ŋgba gbɛɛkemaase nyetooro nɛ.

english_verse: Jesus came near and started walking along beside them.
translation_verse: Ba ke ta bɛɛ yɔɔ afa kɛŋa nɛ, Yesu gbagba kaa bɛɛwɔ lee be dɔɔ.

english_verse: Jesus asked them, “What were you talking about as you walked along?” The two of them stood there looking sad and gloomy.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Woŋ afa yaa ɛ dɔŋɛɛ ɛ ne yɔɔ mena?” Keŋte be dɔɔwɔ seŋ waraŋase man.

english_verse: We had hoped that he would be the one to set Israel free! But it has already been three days since all this happened.
translation_verse: Mɔna nafɔ ŋon te de loo gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ nyi waa kaa lɛɛ Iseraetena nyee. Gyɛŋ yɛna weeya atoorote ŋgba kɛŋa pou be kɔŋ nɛ.

english_verse: Some women in our group surprised us. They had gone to the tomb early in the morning,
translation_verse: Kɛŋa wɔle nɛ, ala baale baŋ be kpuɛ na daa nɛ kɔŋawɔ kaa yako daa gyakoloŋ nombia ŋaale. Be kpa be karatɛɛwɔ gyu yebɔɔ keŋ tɛɛ,

english_verse: Some men from our group went to the tomb and found it just as the women had said. But they didn't see Jesus either.
translation_verse: De man baale mɔ gyuu yebɔɔ keŋ tɛɛ na, ba ke naa kpene kamasɛ ŋgba mena keŋ ala baŋ be yako nɛ mɔna baŋ mɔ te naa Yesu ŋon ya.”

english_verse: Then Jesus asked the two disciples, “Why can't you understand? How can you be so slow to believe all that the prophets said?
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “O ɛmɛɛ ayiŋtena, weera dɔɔ te ɛ yɛɛ nyoɛnyoɛ na nombia ŋan pou Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ be yako see nɛ ke lɛɛ di mena.

english_verse: Didn't you know that the Messiah would have to suffer before he was given his glory?”
translation_verse: Tekaboena nyi nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ, waa naa diyem kei pɛna waa gyoo ɔ gyoori keŋ man yee?”

english_verse: Jesus then explained everything written about himself in the Scriptures, beginning with the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu fiasɛɛ ɔ na wola wɔ kpene keŋ bɔɔ ŋmarase Mosesi mmaraase ŋan man, na ŋan Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra be yako see lee ɔ wose man nɛ man.

english_verse: They begged him, “Stay with us! It's already late, and the sun is going down.” So Jesus went into the house to stay with them.
translation_verse: Mɔna be waase e nideli nyi, “Tɛɛ te yuu dɔɔ waa kɔŋ kaa doo be gyaŋ.” Mena dɔɔ ɔ gyuuwɔ kekyaa be gyaŋ.

english_verse: After Jesus sat down to eat, he took some bread. He blessed it and broke it. Then he gave it to them.
translation_verse: Ŋon na wɔ ke kyaa baa di nɛ, ɔ moo bodobodo te ɔ fanewɔ do Wurubuarɛ nɔɔ te ɔ kɛrɛɛ ke kpala wɔ.

english_verse: At once they knew who he was, but he disappeared.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te be sia gyuusuwɔ te ba gyeŋawɔ nyi Yesu ŋonaŋ, keŋte ɔ ŋaalaawɔ be siaman.

english_verse: They said to each other, “When he talked with us along the road and explained the Scriptures to us, didn't it warm our hearts?”
translation_verse: Keŋte be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, “Debaŋ keŋ ɔ ne kolosi na daa gbɛɛnaa man te ɔ ba lese Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan man wola daa nɛ, de wɔe te lɔ daa yee?”

english_verse: So they got up and returned to Jerusalem. The two disciples found the eleven apostles and the others gathered together.
translation_verse: Debokenaŋ man te ba korowɔ bileŋ bese gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man. Botɔɔ te ba ke naa kpilala kufu daale baŋ na balaŋ akaŋ baŋ te yilaa botɔɔ

english_verse: And they learned from the group that the Lord was really alive and had appeared to Peter.
translation_verse: na bɛɛ yako nyiaa, “Ampaŋ de Gbeŋgyoo ŋon te fom! Ɔ ta lese ɔ wose wola Simɔn!”

english_verse: Then the disciples from Emmaus told what happened on the road and how they knew he was the Lord when he broke the bread. What Jesus' Followers Must Do (Matthew
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te kaseela bala baŋ mɔ yako wɔ kpene keŋ be kɔŋ Emause gbɛɛnaa man nɛ, na mena keŋ Yesu be kɛrɛɛ bodobodo kpala wɔ te bɔɔ gyem nyi ŋon ŋonaŋ nɛ. Yesu kalese ɔ wose wola ɔ kaseela baŋ Mateo

english_verse: While Jesus' disciples were talking about what had happened, Jesus appeared and greeted them.
translation_verse: Ba ke gyakaa bee kolosi ɔ wose nombia kɛŋa nɛ, Yesu gbagba kaa seŋawɔ be gyaŋ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Wosefɛɛreŋ dɛɛ kyaa ɛ man.”

english_verse: They were frightened and terrified because they thought they were seeing a ghost.
translation_verse: Be ŋeŋawɔ te gyakoloŋ kyaŋa wɔ, na baŋ gyeŋ nyi woo yaa be naawɔ.

english_verse: But Jesus said, “Why are you so frightened? Why do you doubt?
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Woŋ dɔɔ te ɛ ne yee yaa woŋ dɔɔ te ɛ ne lɛɛ aŋmaareŋ ɛ nyeeya man?

english_verse: Look at my hands and my feet and see who I am! Touch me and find out for yourselves. Ghosts don't have flesh and bones as you see I have.”
translation_verse: Ɛ kɛɛ me nyiŋmaase na me nawɔɔ! Maŋ gbagba ŋonaŋ. Ɛ tina maŋ kɛɛ! Nawolo nyi woo be dana wosenaane na ŋɔɔ ŋgba mena keŋ ɛ ne naa maŋ dana nɛ ya.”

english_verse: After Jesus said this, he showed them his hands and his feet.
translation_verse: Ɔ kekolosi taŋ nɛ, ɔ moo ɔ nyiŋmaase na ɔ nawɔɔ wola wɔ.

english_verse: The disciples were so glad and amazed that they could not believe it. Jesus then asked them, “Do you have something to eat?”
translation_verse: Nombia ŋan doo be nɔɔ te yɛɛ wɔ gyoŋ gbaa keŋ nyi ba te lɛɛ di ya. Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ dana weenɛɛ ŋaale kɛbo?”

english_verse: They gave him a piece of broiled fish.
translation_verse: Keŋte ba moo kpebii keŋ bɔɔ ŋasee ke nɛ duguŋ fa e.

english_verse: He took it and ate it as they watched.
translation_verse: Te ɔ lɛɛ ke tao be siaman botɔɔ.

english_verse: Jesus said to them, “While I was still with you, I told you that everything written about me in the Law of Moses, the Books of the Prophets, and in the Psalms had to happen.”
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Debaŋ keŋ me kyaa ɛ gyaŋ nɛ me yako ŋon nyiaa, nombii kamasɛ keŋ bɔɔ ŋmarase Mosesi mmaraase ŋan man, na ŋan Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra be ŋmarase see na ŋan bɔɔ ŋmarase see nɔɔneŋ tɔne man lee ma wose man nɛ, kaboena nyi ŋe dɛɛ kɔŋ mena.”

english_verse: They also say that all people of every nation must be told in my name to turn to God, in order to be forgiven. So beginning in Jerusalem,
translation_verse: te baa kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ɔ yele man fa balaŋ pou tɛɛle kei dɔɔ, na baa kyɛɛkee lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man na baa kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ, na Wurubuarɛ ka mo be nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ. Baa fiasɛɛ lee Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man, pɛna baa gyu donɔɔse akaŋ ŋan man.

english_verse: you must tell everything that has happened.
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ yɛna balaŋ baŋ ɛ ke di mena nombia kɛŋa wose man adansɛɛ.

english_verse: I will send you the one my Father has promised, but you must stay in the city until you are given power from heaven. Jesus Returns to Heaven (Mark
translation_verse: Maa mo me kya Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ɔ be yako see nyi waa fa ŋon nɛ kaa do ɛ man. Mɔna ɛ kyaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man kɛbo kelii debaŋ keŋ waa mo mena doŋ kei lee adido kaa do ɛ man.” Yesu kegyu adido Maake

english_verse: Jesus led his disciples out to Bethany, where he raised his hands and blessed them.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu moo ɔ kaseela baŋ lee donɔɔ kenaŋ man gyu Bɛtani donɔɔ nɔɔman, botɔɔ te ɔ gyinaa ɔ nyiŋmaase adido te ɔ hiraa ɔ kaseela baŋ.

english_verse: As he was doing this, he left and was taken up to heaven.
translation_verse: Ɔ kee hiraa wɔ nɛ, ɔ tinaa wɔ yela te Wurubuarɛ moo e laŋ adido.

english_verse: After his disciples had worshiped him, they returned to Jerusalem and were very happy.
translation_verse: Keŋte ba som e te ba moo kenyaŋee dinaa bese kɔŋ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man,

english_verse: They spent their time in the temple, praising God.
translation_verse: te debaŋ kamasɛ na bee gyu ke kyaa Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man lese Wurubuarɛ yele.

english_verse: From the very beginning the Word was with God.
translation_verse: Ŋon na Wurubuarɛ kyaa la leenaŋ kewalaŋ kenaŋ man.

english_verse: received its life from him, and his life gave light to everyone.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ nombii kei ne faa na nyeedoŋ, te nyeedoŋ kenaŋ yɛna keŋmaŋee keŋ ne gyuusu balaŋ sia na baa naa Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛ.

english_verse: The light keeps shining in the dark, and darkness has never put it out.
translation_verse: Keŋmaŋee kenaŋ ne ŋmaŋee ditiŋtɛɛ man, mɔna ditiŋtɛɛ keŋ bɛɛ tale di ke dɔɔ ya.

english_verse: who came to tell about the light and to lead all people to have faith.
translation_verse: Ŋon kɔŋala ɔ kaa di adansɛɛ lee keŋmaŋee keŋ wose man fa balaŋ, na balaŋ pou kenyiŋ ta ɔ dɔɔ lɛɛ keŋmaŋee keŋ di.

english_verse: The true light that shines on everyone was coming into the world.
translation_verse: Ŋon bɛɛ baake e keŋmaŋee gbagba keŋ nɛ waa kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ, na ɔ kaa ŋmaŋee fa walaŋ kamasɛ.

english_verse: The Word was in the world, but no one knew him, though God had made the world with his Word.
translation_verse: Keŋmaŋee kenaŋ kaa kyaawɔ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ. Ɔ dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ tɛɛwɔ yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ, mɔna tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ ta gyeŋ e ya.

english_verse: He came into his own world, but his own nation did not welcome him.
translation_verse: Ɔ kɔŋawɔ ŋon gbagba ɔ balaŋ gyaŋ, mɔna ɔ balaŋ te lɛɛ e ya.

english_verse: They were not God's children by nature or because of any human desires. God himself was the one who made them his children.
translation_verse: Ba te yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ bia ŋgba mena keŋ alo na baale na lola bia ba kegyae man nɛ ya. Mɔna Wurubuarɛ gbagba kegyaebii man te ɔ lese wɔ nyi baa yɛɛ ɔ bia.

english_verse: The Word became a human being and lived here with us. We saw his true glory, the glory of the only Son of the Father. From him the complete gifts of undeserved grace and truth have come down to us.
translation_verse: Ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Wurubuarɛ nombii nɛ, kaa bese deniwalaŋ te ɔ kaa kyaawɔ de man weeya akalansɛɛ ŋaale. De naa ɔ gyoorobiiri keŋ nawolo nyi ŋon waageŋ yɛna ɔ kya Wurubuarɛ bu dokoloŋ ŋon nɛ. Ɔ man te Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ na anokoare keŋ doo.

english_verse: Because of all that the Son is, we have been given one blessing after another.
translation_verse: Lee ɔ bɛrɛɛ keŋ be wulu ɔ man nɛ dɔɔ, te ɔ ne hiraa daa agbaatee agbaatee.

english_verse: The religious authorities in Jerusalem sent priests and temple helpers to ask John who he was. He told them plainly, “I am not the Messiah.”
translation_verse: Mɔna ɔ torowɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Na maŋ yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ ya.”

english_verse: Then when they asked him if he were Elijah, he said, “No, I am not!” And when they asked if he were the Prophet, he also said “No!”
translation_verse: Keŋte be bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Kenaŋ na nyaŋ yɛna amɔte? Nyaŋ yɛna Elaegya?” Keŋte ɔ tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyi, “Koa, na maŋ yɛna Elaegya ya.” Ba besewɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre ŋon dɛɛ daa nɛ?” Keŋte Gyɔn tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyi “Koa, na maŋ ya.”

english_verse: Finally, they said, “Who are you then? We have to give an answer to the ones who sent us. Tell us who you are!”
translation_verse: Keŋte be bɔɔse e nyi “Te kenaŋ na nyaŋ yɛna amɔte? Yako daa nombii dokoloŋ na dee nyiŋ gyu ke yako baŋ bɔɔ kpila daa nɛ. Sena te n ne yako lee nyaŋ gbagba n wose man?”

english_verse: John answered in the words of the prophet Isaiah, “I am only someone shouting in the desert, ‘Get the road ready for the Lord!’ ”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Gyɔn moo nɔɔwoya ŋan Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya be yako see nɛ tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Maŋ yɛna woya ŋan ne fae yeesa depampaa dinaa keŋ man nɛ nyiaa, Ɛ tenee de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛ see e na ɔ kaa ta ke dɔɔ.”

english_verse: Some Pharisees had also been sent to John.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ kpili gyu Gyɔn gyaŋ nɛ, man baale yɛɛ Farasiitenawɔ,

english_verse: They asked him, “Why are you baptizing people, if you are not the Messiah or Elijah or the Prophet?”
translation_verse: keŋte be bɔɔse Gyɔn nyiaa, “Akpaa na nyaŋ yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ ya, yaa Elaegya yaa Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre ŋon ya na, woŋ dɔɔ te n ne sɔ balaŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ?”

english_verse: John told them, “I use water to baptize people. But here with you is someone you don't know.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Gyɔn tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Maŋ dɔɔ loŋ yakaa te me dana mɛɛ sɔ balaŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ, mɔna ŋolo kyaa ɛ man keŋ ɛ ba gyeŋ e ya.

english_verse: Even though I came first, I am not good enough to untie his sandals.”
translation_verse: Ŋon yɛna walaŋ ŋon ɔ dɔŋ me wɔle ɔ ne kɔŋ, keŋ ma te kaboe gba nyi maa buŋ booli ɔ nkyokota ŋmeeseŋ koraŋ gba ya.”

english_verse: John said this as he was baptizing east of the Jordan River in Bethany. The Lamb of God
translation_verse: Nombia kɛŋa pou kɔŋae Gyɔɔdan boo diŋgyiŋ donɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake ke Bɛtani nɛ man, botɔɔ keŋ Gyɔn ne sɔ balaŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ nɛ.

english_verse: The next day, John saw Jesus coming toward him and said: Here is the Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!
translation_verse: Tɛɛ kena nɛ, Gyɔn naa Yesu ne kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ te ɔ yakowɔ lee Yesu wose man nyiaa, “Ɛ kɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nambu ŋon. Ŋon na lesena balaŋ lee be dukum man.

english_verse: He is the one I told you about when I said, “Someone else will come, who is greater than I am, because he was alive before I was born.”
translation_verse: Kei yɛna walaŋ ŋon mɔɔ wulaa yako ŋon ɔ wose man nombia nyiaa, ‘Walaŋ ŋon ɔ dɔŋ me wɔle ɔ ne kɔŋ nɛ ɔ kela maŋ. Nawolo nyi ɔ wulaa ɔ kyaa pɛte ba lola maŋ.’

english_verse: I didn't know who he was. But I came to baptize you with water, so that everyone in Israel would see him.
translation_verse: Nafɔ na maŋ gbagba ba gyeŋ e ya, mɔna ŋon dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ kpila maŋ nyi me kaa sɔ balaŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ, na maa lese e debɔɔ wola Iseraetena na baa nyiŋ gyeŋ e.”

english_verse: I was there and saw the Spirit come down on him like a dove from heaven. And the Spirit stayed on him.
translation_verse: Keŋte Gyɔn dii adansɛɛ nyiaa, “Me naa Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon moo duwuluŋ dinɔɔ tisi lee adido kaa kyaa ɔ dɔɔ.

english_verse: Before this I didn't know who he was. But the one who sent me to baptize with water had told me, “You will see the Spirit come down and stay on someone. Then you will know that he is the one who will baptize with the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: Nafɔ maŋ gbagba ba gyeŋ e ya, mɔna Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nyi me kaa mo loŋ sɔ balaŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ nɛ yako na maŋ nyiaa, ‘Walaŋ ŋon n naa Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon de tisi kaa kyaa ɔ dɔɔ nɛ, ŋon ne gyaela waa mo Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon sɔ balaŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ.’

english_verse: I saw this happen, and I tell you that he is the Son of God. The First Disciples of Jesus
translation_verse: Ma te naa te mee di adansɛɛ nyiaa, ŋon yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon.” Yesu kpilala gyaŋgba nyibaŋ baŋ

english_verse: The next day, John was there again, and two of his followers were with him.
translation_verse: Tɛɛ kena nɛ, Gyɔn na ɔ kaseela bala besewɔ gyu boo keŋ nɔɔman.

english_verse: When he saw Jesus walking by, he said, “Here is the Lamb of God!”
translation_verse: Keŋte Gyɔn naa Yesu ne lam. Ɔ kena e nɛ te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ kɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nambu ŋon!”

english_verse: John's two followers heard him, and they went with Jesus.
translation_verse: Gyɔn kaseela bala baŋ kenyii Gyɔn nɔɔwoya kɛŋa nɛ, be tinaa Gyɔn yela gyu ke sila Yesu.

english_verse: When Jesus turned and saw them, he asked, “What do you want?” They answered, “Rabbi, where do you live?” The Hebrew word “Rabbi” means “Teacher.”
translation_verse: Yesu gɛɛsewɔ kɛɛ na te ɔ naa wɔ keŋte ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Woŋ yaa ɛ ne gyae?” Botɔɔ te baŋ mɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, aleŋ te n kyaa?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Come and see!” It was already about four o'clock in the afternoon when they went with him and saw where he lived. So they stayed on for the rest of the day.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ kɔŋ na ɛ kaa kɛɛ.” Mena dɔɔ be gyuuwɔ kena botɔɔ keŋ ɔ kyaa. Debaŋ kenaŋ na te gyɔ balinɔɔ debaŋ anaara te be kyaawɔ ɔ gyaŋ wee kenaŋ pou.

english_verse: One of the two men who had heard John and had gone with Jesus was Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter.
translation_verse: Balaŋ bala baŋ bɔɔ nyii Gyɔn nɔɔwoya te bɔɔ sila Yesu nɛ, be man ŋolo yɛna Simɔn Pita ɔ naabu Anderiase.

english_verse: The first thing Andrew did was to find his brother and tell him, “We have found the Messiah!” The Hebrew word “Messiah” means the same as the Greek word “Christ.”
translation_verse: Kpene keŋ Anderiase be taŋgbɛɛ yɛɛ yɛna nyi, ɔ gyuuwɔ ke kɛo ɔ kegyia Simɔn te ɔ yako e nyiaa, “Da te naa Mesaya ŋon, nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ.”

english_verse: Andrew brought his brother to Jesus. And when Jesus saw him, he said, “Simon son of John, you will be called Cephas.” This name can be translated as “Peter.” Jesus Chooses Philip and Nathanael
translation_verse: Keŋte Anderiase moo Simɔn kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ. Yesu kena Simɔn nɛ ɔ kɛɛ e yididi te ɔ yako e nyiaa, “Me gyeŋ nyi nyaŋ yɛna Gyɔn bu Simɔn ŋon. Baa baake neŋ nyi Kefase yaa Pita.” Ka asɛɛ yɛna nyi boe. Yesu kebaake Filipo na Natanɛle

english_verse: The next day Jesus decided to go to Galilee. There he met Philip, who was from Bethsaida, the hometown of Andrew and Peter. Jesus said to Philip, “Follow me.”
translation_verse: Filipo lee donɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake ke Bɛtesaida nɛ man. Donɔɔ kenaŋ man mɔ te Anderiase na Pita mɔ leewɔ.

english_verse: Philip then found Nathanael and said, “We have found the one that Moses and the Prophets wrote about. He is Jesus, the son of Joseph from Nazareth.”
translation_verse: Filipo mɔ naa Natanɛle te ɔ yako e nyiaa, “Da te naa walaŋ ŋon Mosesi be ŋmarase ɔ wose man nombia do mmaraa tɔne keŋ man, te Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ mɔ be ŋmarase ɔ wose man nombia nɛ. Bɛɛ baake e nyi Yesu. Ŋon yɛna Yesu, Gyosɛfo bu ŋon ɔ ba lee Nasarɛte nɛ.”

english_verse: Nathanael asked, “Can anything good come from Nazareth?” Philip answered, “Come and see.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Natanɛle bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Nasarɛte ya? Kolo kpaakpaa daale gyae ke tale lee botɔɔ?” Keŋte Filipo yako e nyiaa, “Nyaŋ kɔŋ na n kaa kɛɛ.”

english_verse: When Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him, he said, “Here is a true descendant of our ancestor Israel. And he isn't deceitful.”
translation_verse: Yesu kena Natanɛle ne kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ nɛ ɔ kolosiwɔ lee ɔ wose man nyiaa, “Ɛ kɛɛ, Iserae baale gbagba yaa kei keŋ dekara be doo ɔ man ya.”

english_verse: “How do you know me?” Nathanael asked. Jesus answered, “Before Philip called you, I saw you under the fig tree.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Natanɛle bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Sena te n yɛɛwɔ gyeŋ maŋ?” Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyi, “Me wulaa naa neŋ fiigi daŋ keŋ tɛɛ pɛte Filipo baake neŋ.”

english_verse: Nathanael said, “Rabbi, you are the Son of God and the King of Israel!”
translation_verse: Keŋte Natanɛle torowɔ yako nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon! Nyaŋ yɛna Iseraetena gyoo ŋon!”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Did you believe me just because I said that I saw you under the fig tree? You will see something even greater.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Keŋ mɔ yako neŋ nyi me naa neŋ fiigi daŋ keŋ tɛɛ dɔɔ te n ne lɛɛ maŋ di? N kena nombia dinaana ŋan kela kɛŋa.”

english_verse: I tell you for certain you will see heaven open and God's angels going up and coming down on the Son of Man.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, ɛ kena ŋgba adido ta toro na Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ na gyem tisi maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon dɔɔ.”

english_verse: Three days later Mary, the mother of Jesus, was at a wedding feast in the village of Cana in Galilee.
translation_verse: Ka weeya atoorote keŋ nɛ, ba kpeo alo ŋolo Kaanan donɔɔ keŋ ke kyaa Galelia tɛɛle man nɛ man. Na Yesu ɔ naa mɔ kyaa botɔɔ.

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples had also been invited and were there.
translation_verse: Be baake Yesu na ɔ kaseela mɔ kɔŋ alowɔle keŋ tɛɛ botɔɔ.

english_verse: When the wine was all gone, Mary said to Jesus, “They don't have any more wine.”
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ soloŋ ke taŋ nɛ, Yesu ɔ naa yako e nyi “Balaŋ baŋ soloŋ te taŋ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Mother, my time hasn't yet come! You must not tell me what to do.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Nna kei te yɛɛ daa na neŋ de nombia ya. Debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba see maŋ nɛ te lii ta ya.”

english_verse: Mary then said to the servants, “Do whatever Jesus tells you to do.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu ɔ naa yako dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ tewulɛɛ baŋ nyiaa, “Kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɔ yako ŋon nyi yaa yɛɛ na, ɛ yɛɛ ke.”

english_verse: At the feast there were six stone water jars that were used by the people for washing themselves in the way that their religion said they must. Each jar held about
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ na, osuŋse dinaana looro ŋaale baŋɛɛ botɔɔ. Gyudatena ɔsom man na, osuŋse kɛŋa man te bee kyekii loŋ do mo faafo ba wose. Dokoloŋ gyae ke lɛɛ loŋ ŋgba garawaase anoŋ yaa looro nɛ.

english_verse: Jesus told the servants to fill them to the top with water. Then after the jars had been filled,
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako tewulɛɛ baŋ nyiaa, “Ɛ kyekii loŋ do osuŋse ŋan man wulu.” Keŋte tewulɛɛ baŋ kyekii loŋ wulu ŋa belebelebele.

english_verse: he said, “Now take some water and give it to the man in charge of the feast.” The servants did as Jesus told them,
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Nɛnɛɛ ɛ kyee loŋ keŋ ŋaale gyina kegyia ŋon ɔ seŋɛɛ alowɔle keŋ siaman nɛ.” Te be kyeewɔ gyina e.

english_verse: and the man in charge drank some of the water that had now turned into wine. He did not know where the wine had come from, but the servants did. He called the bridegroom over
translation_verse: Kegyia ŋon daa loŋ keŋ ba bese soloŋ nɛ kɛɛ. Ɔ naawɔ nyi soloŋ keŋ dei, mɔna ɔ ba gyeŋ ke dalee ya, mɔna tewulɛɛ baŋ bɔɔ kyee loŋ keŋ kɔna e nɛ baŋ gyeŋ botɔɔ keŋ kɔɔ lee. Keŋte kegyia ŋon baake alowɔle baale ŋon lee yenaŋ,

english_verse: and said, “The best wine is always served first. Then after the guests have had plenty, the other wine is served. But you have kept the best until last!”
translation_verse: te ɔ yako e nyiaa, “Balaŋ degyaŋee na, bɛɛ taŋgbɛɛ bo mo soloŋ kpaakpaate fa wɔ, te akpaa be dɛɛse na te bɛɛ mo soloŋ keŋ kɔɔ dei ya nɛ kɔŋ. Mɔna nyaŋ moo soloŋ kpaakpaate keŋ bo weese kaa lii debaŋ kei pɛte n lese ke debɔɔ.”

english_verse: But Jesus was talking about his body as a temple.
translation_verse: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ Yesu ne kolosi ka wose man nombia nɛ, yɛna ŋon gbagba ɔ wose.

english_verse: Not long before the Jewish festival of Passover, Jesus went to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ Gyudatena nyeekelɛɛwee keŋ na benaa nɛ, Yesu gyuu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man.

english_verse: So he took some rope and made a whip. Then he chased everyone out of the temple, together with their sheep and cattle. He turned over the tables of the moneychangers and scattered their coins.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɔ moo ŋmeeseŋ ŋmɛ yɛɛ dambila te ɔ moowɔ gegi balaŋ baŋ na be nunkyuese na be namense pou lee dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man. Ɔ yaasee be kɔbasebia ŋan te ɔ besena be tebuluse ŋan pou buŋii.

english_verse: The disciples then remembered that the Scriptures say, “My love for your house burns in me like a fire.”
translation_verse: Nombia kɛŋa yela ɔ kaseela baŋ tɔɔse Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako nyi, “O Wurubuarɛ, n deni dinaa keŋ man nombia ne hia maŋ nideli, te ŋe te di ma nyee mɔ.”

english_verse: “Destroy this temple,” Jesus answered, “and in three days I will build it again!”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ yɛlɛɛ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa kei, weeya atoorote na maa ma ke bela.”

english_verse: There he found people selling cattle, sheep, and doves in the temple. He also saw moneychangers sitting at their tables.
translation_verse: Yesu ke gyu ka gyoo Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ dekpaŋalaŋ man nɛ, ɔ naawɔ nyi baale ne yɔɔ nunkyuese na namense na awuluŋna, na balaŋ baale mɔ kyakɛɛ bɛɛ yɛlɛɛ kɔba.

english_verse: Jesus said to the people who had been selling doves, “Get those doves out of here! Don't make my Father's house a marketplace.”
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ yako balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ yɔɔ awuluŋna nɛ nyiaa, “Ɛ mo wɔ lee kɛbo! Ɛ na mo me kya dɛɛ besena ɛ kebu dedii ya!”

english_verse: The Jewish leaders asked Jesus, “What miracle will you work to show us why you have done this?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Gyudatena kegyiise kaa bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Woŋ gyakoloŋ nombia te nyaŋ ke tale yɛɛ na kawola daa nyi Wurubuarɛ faa na neŋ doŋ nyi nya yɛɛ mena abɔɔ kɛŋa?”

english_verse: And when he was raised from death, his disciples remembered what he had told them. Then they believed the Scriptures and the words of Jesus. Jesus Knows What People Are Like
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ debaŋ keŋ Yesu be fom lee yeŋ man nɛ, ɔ kaseela baŋ tɔɔsewɔ nyi ɔ yako mena nombia kɛŋa. Te be lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see, na Yesu nombia ŋan ɔ be yako nɛ di nideli.

english_verse: In Jerusalem during Passover many people put their faith in Jesus, because they saw him work miracles.
translation_verse: Yesu ke kyaa Gyerusalɛm Gyudatena nyeekelɛɛweeya kedi ŋan man nɛ, balaŋ burum naa gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan ɔ be yɛɛ nɛ te be lɛɛ e di.

english_verse: But Jesus knew what was in their hearts, and he would not let them have power over him.
translation_verse: Mɔna Yesu ta mo ɔ wose lɔ be dɔɔ ya, nawolo nyi ɔ gyeŋ walaŋ kamasɛ dinɔɔ.

english_verse: No one had to tell him what people were like. He already knew.
translation_verse: Te yɛɛ keŋ nyi ŋolo keyako deniwalaŋ nombii fa e ya, nawolo nyi ɔ gyeŋ walaŋ kamasɛ nyeeman nombia.

english_verse: There was a man named Nicodemus who was a Pharisee and a Jewish leader.
translation_verse: Mena debaŋ kenaŋ man nɛ, na Gyudatena kegyia ŋolo kyaawɔ keŋ ɔ yele yɛna Nikodemose. Ɔ kpuɛ Farasiitena dikpii keŋ man.

english_verse: One night he went to Jesus and said, “Rabbi, we know that God has sent you to teach us. You could not work these miracles, unless God were with you.”
translation_verse: Wee daale nelim nɛ ɔ korowɔ gyu Yesu gyaŋ te ɔ yako e nyiaa, “Kegyia, da gyeŋ nyi n yɛɛ wolale e ŋon Wurubuarɛ be kpila neŋ. Nawolo nyi ŋolo be kyaa keŋ waa tale yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia na nombia dinaana ŋan n ne yɛɛ nɛ, keŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ be kpuɛ na e ya.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “I tell you for certain that you must be born from above before you can see God's kingdom!”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Mɛɛ yako neŋ anokoare n ke nyii nyiaa, akpaa be ta bese lola walaŋ ya nɛ, ɔ be gyae waa naa Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ ya.”

english_verse: Jesus answered: I tell you for certain that before you can get into God's kingdom, you must be born not only by water, but by the Spirit.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Mɛɛ yako neŋ anokoare n ke nyii nyiaa, akpaa ba te sɔ neŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ na Wurubuarɛ ka mo ɔ feliŋ do n man ya na, n be gyae n ke tale gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man ya.

english_verse: Humans give life to their children. Yet only God's Spirit can change you into a child of God.
translation_verse: Deniwalaŋ na lola ɔ dinɔɔ bo, te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon mɔ na lola walaŋ bo ŋgba Wurubuarɛ dinɔɔ nɛ.

english_verse: Don't be surprised when I say that you must be born from above.
translation_verse: Na yela keyɛɛ neŋ gyakoloŋ nyi me kpa, kaboena nyi ba bese lola neŋ bela ya.

english_verse: Only God's Spirit gives new life. The Spirit is like the wind that blows wherever it wants to. You can hear the wind, but you don't know where it comes from or where it is going.
translation_verse: Feliŋ ne gyɔɔ bo gyu botɔɔ keŋ kɛɛ gyae. N ne nyii mena keŋ kɛɛ gyɔɔ mɔna n ba gyeŋ ke dalee yaa ke dee gyue ya. Mena mɔ te doo fa walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ba lola e nɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied: How can you be a teacher of Israel and not know these things?
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Ŋgba n yɛɛ Gyudatena wolale kegyia e. N bee nyii mena nombia kɛŋa man yee?

english_verse: I tell you for certain we know what we are talking about because we have seen it ourselves. But none of you will accept what we say.
translation_verse: Mɛɛ yako neŋ anokoare n ke nyii nyiaa, nombia ŋan da gyeŋ nɛ te dɛɛ yako te ŋan dɛɛ naa mɔ nɛ te dee di ŋa wose man adansɛɛ, mɔna ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ nombia ŋan dɛɛ yako ŋon nɛ di ya.

english_verse: If you don't believe when I talk to you about things on earth, how can you possibly believe if I talk to you about things in heaven?
translation_verse: Mɛɛ yako ŋon tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nombia te ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ ŋa di ya na, sena te me yako ŋon Wurubuarɛ man nombia na ɛ kelɛɛ ŋa di?

english_verse: No one has gone up to heaven except the Son of Man, who came down from there.
translation_verse: Ŋolo na ŋolo te gyuu ta Wurubuarɛ man ya, Gyisɛ maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon mɔɔ lee botɔɔ kɔŋ nɛ maageŋ.

english_verse: Then everyone who has faith in the Son of Man will have eternal life.
translation_verse: Na walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ maŋ di na, waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ.

english_verse: God loved the people of this world so much that he gave his only Son, so that everyone who has faith in him will have eternal life and never really die.
translation_verse: Lee mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ ne gyae balaŋ dɔɔ te ɔ moo ɔ bu dokoloŋ kooŋ ŋon fa, nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ e di na ɔ be gyae waa yeŋ leki ya, mɔna waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa.”

english_verse: God did not send his Son into the world to condemn its people. He sent him to save them!
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ te kpila ɔ bu tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nyi ɔ kaa bu balaŋ fɔɔ ya, mɔna ɔ kpila e bo na balaŋ ke ta ɔ dɔɔ nyiŋ nyeedoŋ.

english_verse: No one who has faith in God's Son will be condemned. But everyone who doesn't have faith in him has already been condemned for not having faith in God's only Son.
translation_verse: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ e di na bɔɔ gyae baa bu e fɔɔ ya, te ŋon ɔ te lɛɛ e di ya na be wulaa bu e fɔɔ, nawolo nyi ɔ te lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ bu dokoloŋ kooŋ ŋon di ya.

english_verse: The light has come into the world, and people who do evil things are judged guilty because they love the dark more than the light.
translation_verse: Nombia ŋan bɔɔ mo bu tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ fɔɔ yɛna nyi, keŋmaŋee te kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ, mɔna balaŋ ne gyae ditiŋtɛɛ bo kela keŋmaŋee keŋ. Nawolo nyi be nombia ŋan bɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ yɛɛ dukum ke.

english_verse: People who do evil hate the light and won't come to the light, because it clearly shows what they have done.
translation_verse: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne yɛɛ dukum na, ɔ ne kɔla keŋmaŋee keŋ te ɔ bɛɛ benaa ke ya. Nawolo nyi ɔ bɛɛ gyae nyi ɔ nombiakumɛɛ ŋan ka lee debɔɔ ya.

english_verse: But everyone who lives by the truth will come to the light, because they want others to know that God is really the one doing what they do. Jesus and John the Baptist
translation_verse: Mɔna walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne yɛɛ nombia kpaakpaa nɛ, ɔ na benaa keŋmaŋee keŋ. Nawolo nyi keŋmaŋee keŋ gyae ka lese ɔ nombia ŋan ɔ ne yɛɛ nɛ wola nyi ɔ ne yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii. Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre kedi Yesu wose man adansɛɛ

english_verse: Later, Jesus and his disciples went to Judea, where he stayed with them for a while and was baptizing people.
translation_verse: Kɛŋa wɔle nɛ Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ gyuu Gyudia tɛɛle man. Yesu kyaawɔ botɔɔ weeya akalansɛɛ ŋaale te ɔ sɔɔ balaŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ.

english_verse: John's followers got into an argument with a Jewish man about a ceremony of washing.
translation_verse: Gyuda baale ŋolo na Gyɔn kaseela baŋ man baale lɛɛ aŋmaareŋ lee mena keŋ bɛɛ mo loŋ faafo ba wose lee ba amanberɛ gbɛɛ nɛ wose man.

english_verse: They went to John and said, “Rabbi, you spoke about a man when you were with him east of the Jordan. He is now baptizing people, and everyone is going to him.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Gyɔn kaseela baŋ kɔŋawɔ Gyɔn gyaŋ kaa yako e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, n ne tɔɔse balee ŋon nafɔ ɔ kpuɛ na daa Gyɔɔdan boo keŋ diŋgyiŋ, te n be di ɔ wose man adansɛɛ nideli nɛ? Kɛɛ, ɔ ne sɔ balaŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ, te balaŋ pou ne gyu ɔ gyaŋ.”

english_verse: John replied: No one can do anything unless God in heaven allows it.
translation_verse: Keŋte Gyɔn tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Nyi Wurubuarɛ te fa walaŋ gbɛɛ nyi waa yɛɛ kolo ya nɛ, ɔtenate be gyae waa tale yɛɛ ya.

english_verse: You surely remember how I told you that I am not the Messiah. I am only the one sent ahead of him.
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ ke tale di maŋ adansɛɛ nyi me yakowɔ nyiaa, Na maŋ yɛna Nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ ya, mɔna Wurubuarɛ kpila maŋ bo nyi maa kyaŋ gbɛɛ ta ɔ siaman kɔŋ.”

english_verse: At a wedding the groom is the one who gets married. The best man is glad just to be there and to hear the groom's voice. That's why I am so glad.
translation_verse: Keŋte Gyɔn gyɔɔ dudu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Walaŋ ŋon ɔ dana alowɔle na, ŋon yɛna alowɔle ŋon baale. Mɔna alowɔle baale ŋon ɔ gyoona dɔɔ bɛɛ sem bo yenaŋ tei, te akpaa be nyii alowɔle baale ŋon woya na ne yɛɛ wɔ gyoŋ nideli. Mena mɔ yaa yɛɛ maŋ gyoŋ nideli debokeiman ŋgba mɔɔ nyii Yesu nkaŋ nɛ.

english_verse: Jesus must become more important, while I become less important. The One Who Comes from Heaven
translation_verse: Kaboena nyi Yesu yele dɛɛ lee kela maŋ.

english_verse: God's Son comes from heaven and is above all others. Everyone who comes from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks about earthly things. The one who comes from heaven is above all others.
translation_verse: Walaŋ ŋon ɔ ba lee adido kɔŋ nɛ ɔ kela walaŋ kamasɛ, te walaŋ ŋon ɔ ba lee tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ ɔ yɛɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ walaŋ e te ɔ ne kolosi tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nombia bo. Walaŋ ŋon ɔ ba lee adido nɛ, ɔ kela kpene kamasɛ.

english_verse: He speaks about what he has seen and heard, and yet no one believes him.
translation_verse: Nombia ŋan ɔ be naa na ŋan ɔ be nyii nɛ wose man adansɛɛ te ɔ ne dii, mɔna ŋolo te lɛɛ wa adansɛɛ keŋ di ya.

english_verse: But everyone who does believe him has shown that God is truthful.
translation_verse: Te walaŋ ŋon ɔ be lɛɛ wa adansɛɛ keŋ di na, na ɔtenate te sɛɛ baa nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan yɛɛ ampaŋ.

english_verse: The Son was sent to speak God's message, and he has been given the full power of God's Spirit.
translation_verse: Walaŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ be kpila e na ɔ ne kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia bo, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ ta mo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do walaŋ ŋonaŋ man nideli.

english_verse: The Father loves the Son and has given him everything.
translation_verse: Kya ŋon ne gyae ɔ bu ŋon nombia, te ɔ moo kpene kamasɛ do e ɔ nyiŋmaa man.

english_verse: Everyone who has faith in the Son has eternal life. But no one who rejects him will ever share in that life, and God will be angry with them forever.
translation_verse: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ ɔ bu ŋon di na waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa, te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ bɛɛ ɔ bu ŋon na ɔ be gyae waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kei ya, mɔna Wurubuarɛ waa gyeŋ baŋ do e kekpaakekpaa.”

english_verse: But Jesus' disciples were really the ones doing the baptizing, and not Jesus himself. Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
translation_verse: Mɔna Yesu gbagba dɔɔ ɔ te sɔ ŋolo na ŋolo Wurubuarɛ loŋ ya, ɔ kaseela baŋ sɔɔ na wɔ.

english_verse: This time he had to go through Samaria,
translation_verse: Na kaboena nyi waa mo na Samaria tɛɛle man.

english_verse: “You are a Jew,” she replied, “and I am a Samaritan woman. How can you ask me for a drink of water when Jews and Samaritans won't have anything to do with each other?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Samaria alo ŋon yako Yesu nyiaa, “N yɛɛ Gyuda baale e te maŋ mɔ yɛɛ Samaria alo e. Woŋ dɔɔ te n na sola maŋ loŋ?” Keŋ dɔɔ ɔ be bɔɔse mena yɛna nyi, Gyudatena na Samariatena bɛɛ gyoo ya.

english_verse: “Sir,” the woman said, “you don't even have a bucket, and the well is deep. Where are you going to get this life-giving water?
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te alo ŋon yako e nyiaa, “Kegyia, ditiribuŋ keŋ dana siiliŋ te n be dana kpene keŋ n ka mo kyee loŋ keŋ ya. Aleŋ te nyaŋ kenyiŋ mena nyeedoŋ loŋ kei.

english_verse: Our ancestor Jacob dug this well for us, and his family and animals got water from it. Are you greater than Jacob?”
translation_verse: Yaa nyaŋ kela na de naana Gyekɔpo ŋon ɔ be wula ditiribuŋ kei fa daa nɛ? Ŋon gbagba na ɔ bia na ɔ wonembia pou nyɔɔ loŋ lee ditiribuŋ kei man.”

english_verse: But no one who drinks the water I give will ever be thirsty again. The water I give will become in that person a flowing fountain that gives eternal life.”
translation_verse: Mɔna walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon waa nyɔɔ loŋ keŋ maa fa e nɛ, loŋboaneŋ be gyae ka ko e bela ya. Loŋ keŋ maa fa e nɛ gyae ke doo ɔ man ŋgba ditiribuŋ nɛ, na ka ke fa e nyeedoŋ loŋ na waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaakekpaa.”

english_verse: The woman replied, “Sir, please give me a drink of that water! Then I won't get thirsty and have to come to this well again.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te alo ŋon yako e nyiaa, “Kegyia, akpaa mena na fa maŋ mena loŋ kei na loŋboaneŋ ba tekaa ko maŋ bela ya, na maa tina ditiribuŋ kei man kekɔŋ yela.”

english_verse: Jesus told her, “Go and bring your husband.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Gyu ke baake n baale na ɛ ke kɔŋ.”

english_verse: The woman answered, “I don't have a husband.” “That's right,” Jesus replied, “you're telling the truth. You don't have a husband. You have already been married five times, and the man you are now living with isn't your husband.”
translation_verse: N taŋ ke yala baala banoŋ, te ŋon gyaŋ n kyaa debokeiman nɛ ɔ te yɛɛ n baale e ya. Mena dɔɔ nombia ŋan n be yako maŋ nɛ yɛɛ ampaŋ.”

english_verse: The woman said, “Sir, I can see that you are a prophet.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te alo ŋon yako e nyiaa, “Kegyia ma te naa nyi n yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre e ampaŋ.

english_verse: My ancestors worshiped on this mountain, but you Jews say Jerusalem is the only place to worship.”
translation_verse: De naanaɔ som Wurubuarɛ bo bula kei dɔɔ, mɔna ɛmɛɛ Gyudatena kpa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man te kaboena nyi dɛɛ som Wurubuarɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus said to her: Believe me, the time is coming when you won't worship the Father either on this mountain or in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Alo lɛɛ maŋ di, debaŋ daale gyae kekɔŋ keŋ bɔɔ gyae baa tekaa som Wurubuarɛ bula kei dɔɔ yaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man ya.

english_verse: You Samaritans don't really know the one you worship. But we Jews do know the God we worship, and by using us, God will save the world.
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ Samariatena na som Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɛ ba gyeŋ e ya, te daa Gyudatena dɔɔ dɛɛ som Wurubuarɛ ŋon da gyeŋ e, nawolo nyi nyeekelɛɛ gyae ke ta bo Gyudatena dɔɔ kɔŋ.

english_verse: But a time is coming, and it is already here! Even now the true worshipers are being led by the Spirit to worship the Father according to the truth. These are the ones the Father is seeking to worship him.
translation_verse: Debaŋ daale ne kɔŋ te ke taŋ kekɔŋ, keŋ nyi baŋ bɛɛ som Wurubuarɛ anokoare man nɛ, baa mo Wurubuarɛ Feliŋ na anokoare bo som Wurubuarɛ nawolo nyi mena somra kɛwɔ dinɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ ne gyae.

english_verse: God is Spirit, and those who worship God must be led by the Spirit to worship him according to the truth.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ feliŋ e, mena dɔɔ baŋ baa som e nɛ kaboena nyi baa mo ɔ feliŋ na anokoare som e.”

english_verse: The woman said, “I know that the Messiah will come. He is the one we call Christ. When he comes, he will explain everything to us.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Samaria alo ŋon yako e nyiaa, “Ma gyeŋ nyi Nyeelɛɛre ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Kristo nɛ waa kɔŋ. Ɔ kɔŋ na waa kaa lese kpene kamasɛ man fa daa.”

english_verse: “I am that one,” Jesus told her, “and I am speaking to you now.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu torowɔ yako e nyiaa, “Maŋ ŋon mɛɛ kolosi na neŋ nɛ, maŋ yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon.”

english_verse: The disciples returned about this time and were surprised to find Jesus talking with a woman. But none of them asked him what he wanted or why he was talking with her.
translation_verse: Be kee kolosi kenaŋ nɛ, te Yesu kaseela baŋ leewɔ donɔɔ man kɔŋ. Ba kena ŋgba Yesu na alo ŋon seŋɛɛ bee kolosi nɛ, yɛɛ wɔ gyakoloŋ. Mɔna be man ŋolo te tale bɔɔse alo ŋon nyi woŋ te ɔ ne gyae ya, te ba te tale bɔɔse Yesu mɔ nyi weera ɔ ne kolosi na alo ŋon ya.

english_verse: The woman left her water jar and ran back into town, where she said to the people,
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te alo ŋon tina osuŋ keŋ yela, te ɔ yeuwɔ gyu donɔɔ keŋ man ke yako balaŋ nyiaa,

english_verse: “Come and see a man who told me everything I have ever done! Could he be the Messiah?”
translation_verse: “Ɛ kɔŋ kaa kɛɛ balee ŋolo ŋon ɔ be yako maŋ ma wose man nombia ŋan pou mɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ. Na ŋon yɛna Kristo, nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ baa yaa?”

english_verse: Everyone in town went out to see Jesus.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ balaŋ leewɔ donɔɔ keŋ man kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ.

english_verse: While this was happening, Jesus' disciples were saying to him, “Teacher, please eat something.”
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ na te ɔ kaseela baŋ waase e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo mo kolo tina n nɔɔ”

english_verse: But Jesus told them, “I have food you don't know anything about.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Me dana weenɛɛ ŋaale maa di keŋ ɛ ba gyeŋ ya.”

english_verse: His disciples started asking each other, “Has someone brought him something to eat?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, “Doona ŋolo saa weenɛɛ baa kɔna e?”

english_verse: Jesus said: My food is to do what God wants! He is the one who sent me, and I must finish the work that he gave me to do.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Me weenɛɛ yɛna nyi maa yɛɛ me kya ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ kegyaebii, na maa yɛɛ tom keŋ ɔ ba mo fa maŋ nɛ kelii ɔto.

english_verse: You may say there are still four months until harvest time. But I tell you to look, and you will see that the fields are ripe and ready to harvest.
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ne tale yako nyi ‘Te ka gyaalaŋna banaara na weenɛɛ kekɛrɛɛ te lii.’ Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, ɛ gyina ɛ sia na ɛ ke kɛɛ wɔɔse ŋan. Ɛ kena nyi abɔɔ ŋan te yɛɛ na kekɛrɛɛ.

english_verse: Even now the harvest workers are receiving their reward by gathering a harvest that brings eternal life. Then everyone who planted the seed and everyone who harvests the crop will celebrate together.
translation_verse: Walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne kɛrɛɛ weenɛɛ ŋan nɛ baa tɔ e kom, te ɔ ne yilaa abɔɔ ŋenaŋ bo see fa nyeedoŋ kekpaakekpaa keŋ. Mena dɔɔ walaŋ ŋon ɔ be fare na walaŋ ŋon ɔ be kɛrɛɛ nɛ pou baa bom di gyoŋ.

english_verse: So the saying proves true, “Some plant the seed, and others harvest the crop.”
translation_verse: Dudu keŋ bɔɔ gyɔɔ nyi, ‘Ŋolo ne duula te ŋolo mɔ ne karala’ nɛ yɛɛ ampaŋ.

english_verse: A lot of Samaritans in that town put their faith in Jesus because the woman had said, “This man told me everything I have ever done.”
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ be kyaa Samaria donɔɔ keŋ man nɛ, be man burum lɛɛ Yesu di nawolo nyi alo ŋon yakowɔ nyiaa, “Ɔ te yako maŋ ma wose man nombia ŋan pou weesɛɛ nɛ.”

english_verse: They came and asked him to stay in their town, and he stayed on for two days.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Samariatena baŋ kekɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ nɛ, be waase e nyi waa kyaa be gyaŋ, te Yesu dii weeya ala be gyaŋ.

english_verse: Many more Samaritans put their faith in Jesus because of what they heard him say.
translation_verse: Yesu nombia ŋan ɔ ba wolo nɛ yela balaŋ burum lɛɛ e di.

english_verse: They told the woman, “We no longer have faith in Jesus just because of what you told us. We have heard him ourselves, and we are certain that he is the Savior of the world!” Jesus Heals an Official's Son (Matthew
translation_verse: Keŋte be yako alo ŋon nyiaa, “Nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ nɛ na n nɔɔwoya ŋan dɔɔ te de lɛɛ e di ya, mɔna daa gbagba te nyii e te da te naa nyi ŋon yɛna balaŋ pou nyeelɛɛre gbagba ŋon.” Yesu kekyɔ tomyɛɛre kegyia ŋolo bu kawee

english_verse: Jesus had said, “Prophets are honored everywhere, except in their own country.” Then two days later he left
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi Yesu gbagba yakowɔ nyiaa, “Bɔɔ ne lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre kamasɛ do ŋon gbagba ɔ donɔɔ man ya.”

english_verse: and went to Galilee. The people there welcomed him, because they had gone to the festival in Jerusalem and had seen everything he had done.
translation_verse: Yesu kekɔŋ kaa gyoo Galelia nɛ, botɔɔtena lɛɛ e nideli, nawolo nyi be naa nombia ŋan pou ɔ be yɛɛ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man debaŋ keŋ bɔɔ gyu be kaa di ba nyeekelɛɛwee keŋ nɛ.

english_verse: While Jesus was in Galilee, he returned to the village of Cana, where he had turned the water into wine. There was an official in Capernaum whose son was sick.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ gyu Kaanan donɔɔ keŋ ke kyaa Galelia tɛɛle man nɛ man. Mena donɔɔ kenaŋ man te ɔ besena loŋ yɛɛ soloŋ nɛ. Debaŋ kenaŋ na tomyɛɛre kegyia ŋolo kyaa botɔɔ keŋ ɔ bu na wee Kapanum donɔɔ man.

english_verse: Jesus told the official, “You won't have faith unless you see miracles and wonders!”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Na ɛ kena gyakoloŋ nombia na nombia dinaana ya nɛ, nafɔ ɛ man walaŋ kamasɛ be gyae waa lɛɛ maŋ di ya.”

english_verse: Jesus then said, “Your son will live. Go on home to him.” The man believed Jesus and started back home.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Ta, n bu ŋon waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ.” Balee ŋon lɛɛ Yesu nɔɔwoya ŋan di te ɔ laŋawɔ.

english_verse: Some of the official's servants met him along the road and told him, “Your son is better!”
translation_verse: Balee ŋon keta ɔ ne gyu ɔ dɛɛ nɛ, ɔ gyaŋee na ɔ tewulɛɛ gbɛɛnaa man te be yako e nyiaa, “N bu ŋon wose te yɛɛ e doŋ.”

english_verse: The boy's father realized that at one o'clock the day before, Jesus had told him, “Your son will live!” So the man and everyone in his family put their faith in Jesus.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔ kya ŋon tɔɔsewɔ nyiaa, debaŋ kenaŋ man pɛpɛɛpɛ te Yesu yako e nyiaa, “N bu ŋon wose gyae ke yɛɛ e doŋ.” Mena dɔɔ ŋon na ɔ dekpaŋalaŋmantena pou lɛɛ Yesu di.

english_verse: This was the second miracle that Jesus worked after he left Judea and went to Galilee.
translation_verse: Yesu ka lee Gyudia kɔŋ Galelia tɛɛle man nɛ, kei yɛna gyakoloŋ nombia bolate keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ.

english_verse: In the city near the sheep gate was a pool with five porches, and its name in Hebrew was Bethzatha.
translation_verse: Na depaa daale kyaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man keŋ bɛɛ baake ke be dɛɛ dei man nyi Bɛtesaida. Mena depaa kei benaa na denanɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi “Namense Denanɔɔ” nɛ, te be baŋee kefɛɛse anoŋ kilisi ke.

english_verse: Beside the pool was a man who had been sick for
translation_verse: Na balee ŋolo ŋon ɔ be tekerii kulutooneŋ sao na kufu gyanaara nɛ doo botɔɔ.

english_verse: You have not believed his message, because you refused to have faith in the one he sent.
translation_verse: Ɔ nombia be doo ɛ konɔɔse man ya, nawolo nyi ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ be kpili nɛ di ya.

english_verse: When Jesus saw the man and realized that he had been crippled for a long time, he asked him, “Do you want to be healed?”
translation_verse: Yesu kena balee ŋonaŋ te ɔ ke nyii nyi ɔ ta wee kyare nɛ ɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, “N ne gyae n kawee ke taŋ?”

english_verse: The man answered, “Sir, I don't have anyone to put me in the pool when the water is stirred up. I try to get in, but someone else always gets there first.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te kaweete ŋon tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Me Gbeŋgyoo, akpaa be wosi loŋ keŋ na, mɔɔ ne nyiŋ ŋon waa gate maŋ ka do ke man ya. Akpaa ma mo maa yɛɛ kakyeŋ koro ka gyoo na ŋolo te taŋgbɛɛ maŋ.”

english_verse: Jesus told him, “Pick up your mat and walk!”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Koro! Gate n dofoŋoŋ na n ke ta.”

english_verse: Right then the man was healed. He picked up his mat and started walking around. The day on which this happened was a Sabbath.
translation_verse: Debokenaŋ man te balee ŋon kawee taŋawɔ te ɔ korowɔ gate ɔ dofoŋoŋ te ɔ tɛɛwɔ laŋ. Mena wee kenaŋ yɛɛ Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee ke.

english_verse: When the Jewish leaders saw the man carrying his mat, they said to him, “This is the Sabbath! No one is allowed to carry a mat on the Sabbath.”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Gyudatena kegyiise yako balee ŋon bɔɔ kyɔ e nɛ nyiaa, “Gyɛŋ yɛɛ da kefɛɛfowee ke, te da mmaraa bɛɛ fa gbɛɛ nyi nya seele n dofoŋoŋ ya.”

english_verse: But he replied, “The man who healed me told me to pick up my mat and walk.”
translation_verse: Keŋte balee ŋon tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Walaŋ ŋon ɔ be kyɔ maŋ me kawee nɛ, yako na maŋ nyi maa gate ma dofoŋoŋ na maa ta.”

english_verse: They asked him, “Who is this man that told you to pick up your mat and walk?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te be bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Walaŋ woŋti yako na neŋ nyi nya gate n dofoŋoŋ na n keta?”

english_verse: Later, Jesus met the man in the temple and told him, “You are now well. But don't sin anymore or something worse might happen to you.”
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, Yesu kena balee ŋonaŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man te ɔ yako e nyiaa, “Kɛɛ, nɛnɛɛ n wose te yɛɛ neŋ doŋ, na tekaa yɛɛ dukum bela ya. Na mena ya na kpene keŋ gyae kekɔŋ n dɔɔ nɛ, gyae ka kela kei.”

english_verse: The man left and told the leaders that Jesus was the one who had healed him.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te balee ŋon gyuuwɔ keyako Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ nyiaa, Yesu kyɔɔ na e ɔ kawee keŋ.

english_verse: They started making a lot of trouble for Jesus because he did things like this on the Sabbath.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ naase Yesu diyem, nawolo nyi ɔ te kyɔ kawee ba kefɛɛfowee.

english_verse: But Jesus said, “My Father has never stopped working, and this is why I keep on working.”
