translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Me kya Wurubuarɛ ne yɛɛ tom debaŋ kamasɛ kaa lii na gyɛŋ, mena dɔɔ kaboena nyi maŋ mɔ maa yɛɛ tom mena.”

english_verse: Now the leaders wanted to kill Jesus for two reasons. First, he had broken the law of the Sabbath. But even worse, he had said God was his Father, which made him equal with God. The Son's Authority
translation_verse: Lee mena nombia kɛŋa dɔɔ nɛ Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ yase sia gyae gbɛɛ keŋ baa mo ko Yesu. Nawolo nyi na keŋ ɔ be wɔlɛɛ ba kefɛɛfowee keŋ kaageŋ dɔɔ ya, mɔna ɔ kpa ɔ kya yɛna Wurubuarɛ keŋ nawolo nyi ɔ dokoso yɛna Wurubuarɛ.

english_verse: Jesus told the people: I tell you for certain the Son cannot do anything on his own. He can do only what he sees the Father doing, and he does exactly what he sees the Father do.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, kolo be kyaa keŋ maŋ Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon ne yɛɛ ma wose dɔɔ ya. Kpene keŋ me naa me kya ne yɛɛ na keŋ te mɛɛ yɛɛ. Kpene keŋ kya ŋon ne yɛɛ na, keŋ te maŋ ɔ bu ŋon mɔ ne yɛɛ.

english_verse: The Father loves the Son and has shown him everything he does. The Father will show him even greater things, and you will be amazed.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi me kya ne gyae maŋ te ɔ na wola maŋ kpene kamasɛ keŋ ŋon gbagba ne yɛɛ nɛ. Waa wola maŋ abɔɔ dinaana ŋan kela kɛŋa na maa yɛɛ, na ɛ wose ka yeŋ ŋon.

english_verse: Just as the Father raises the dead and gives life, so the Son gives life to anyone he wants to.
translation_verse: Mena keŋ kya ŋon ne gyuusu woya te ɔ ne fa wɔ nyeedoŋ nɛ, mena kee te maŋ ɔ bu ŋon mɔ maa fa balaŋ baŋ maŋ gbagba ne gyae nɛ nyeedoŋ.

english_verse: The Father doesn't judge anyone, but he has made his Son the judge of everyone.
translation_verse: Kya ŋon gbagba bee di balaŋ nombia ya. Ɔ ta mo balaŋ nombia kedi pou do maŋ ɔ bu ŋon nyiŋmaa man,

english_verse: The Father wants all people to honor the Son as much as they honor him. When anyone refuses to honor the Son, this is the same as refusing to honor the Father who sent him.
translation_verse: na balaŋ pou ke nyiŋ lese maŋ ɔ bu ŋon yele ŋgba mena keŋ bɛɛ lese me kya ŋon yele nɛ. Walaŋ ŋon ɔ bɛɛ lese maŋ ɔ bu ŋon yele ya na, ɔ bɛɛ lese me kya ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ ɔ bu ŋon nɛ yele ya.

english_verse: I tell you for certain that everyone who hears my message and has faith in the one who sent me has eternal life and will never be condemned. They have already gone from death to life.
translation_verse: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ nyii me nɔɔwoya te ɔ lɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ di na, waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa te bɔɔ gyae baa bu e fɔɔ ya. Kei nawolo baa nyi ɔ ta lee yeŋ man kɔŋ nyeedoŋ man.

english_verse: I tell you for certain the time will come, and it is already here, when all of the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God. And those who listen to it will live!
translation_verse: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, debaŋ daale ne kɔŋ te ke taŋ ke kɔŋ keŋ balaŋ baŋ be yɛɛ ŋgba woya nɛ, nawolo nyi ba te lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan di dɔɔ ya nɛ, baa nyii maŋ Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon dei. Balaŋ baŋ baa nyii me dei te baa yɛɛ kpene keŋ mɔɔ yako wɔ nɛ na baa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ.

english_verse: The Father has the power to give life, and he has given that same power to the Son.
translation_verse: Mena keŋ me kya ŋon ne fa balaŋ nyeedoŋ nɛ, mena mɔ te ɔ faa maŋ ɔ bu doŋ nyi maa fa balaŋ nyeedoŋ.

english_verse: And he has given his Son the right to judge everyone, because he is the Son of Man.
translation_verse: Ɔ te fa maŋ ɔ bu ŋon doŋ nyi maa di balaŋ nombia, nawolo nyi maŋ yɛna Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon.

english_verse: Don't be surprised! The time will come when all of the dead will hear the voice of the Son of Man,
translation_verse: Ɛ na yeli nombia kɛŋa ke yɛɛ ŋon gyakoloŋ ya, nawolo nyi debaŋ daale gyae kekɔŋ keŋ woya pou baa nyii ma woya,

english_verse: and they will come out of their graves. Everyone who has done good things will rise to life, but everyone who has done evil things will rise and be condemned.
translation_verse: na baa lee ba yebɔɔse man kɔŋ. Baŋ bɔɔ yɛɛ nombia kpaakpaa nɛ baa koro na baa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ, te baŋ bɔɔ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛ nɛ baa koro na baa bu wɔ fɔɔ.” Yesu adansɛɛtena

english_verse: I cannot do anything on my own. The Father sent me, and he is the one who told me how to judge. I judge with fairness, because I obey him, and I don't just try to please myself. Witnesses to Jesus
translation_verse: “Maŋ gbagba bɛɛ yɛɛ kolo me baam dɔɔ ya. Mena keŋ me kya Wurubuarɛ de yako maŋ nɛ, mena te mee di balaŋ nombia, te me nombia kedi mɔ doo na ke gbɛɛ. Nawolo nyi na maŋ gbagba kegyaebii te mɛɛ yɛɛ ya, mɔna me kya ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ kegyaebii te mɛɛ yɛɛ.

english_verse: If I speak for myself, there is no way to prove I am telling the truth.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa maŋ gbagba de di ma wose man adansɛɛ na, ɛ be gyae ɛ ke lɛɛ maŋ di ya.

english_verse: But there is someone else who speaks for me, and I know what he says is true.
translation_verse: Mɔna ŋolo kyaa keŋ ɔ ne di ma wose man adansɛɛ, te ma gyeŋ nyi wa adansɛɛ keŋ ɔ ne dii lee ma wose man nɛ yɛɛ ampaŋ.

english_verse: You sent messengers to John, and he told them the truth.
translation_verse: Ɛ kpila balaŋ gyu Gyɔn gyaŋ, te ɔ dii anokoare keŋ wose man adansɛɛ fa ŋon.

english_verse: I don't depend on what people say about me, but I tell you these things so that you may be saved.
translation_verse: Na mɛɛ gyae bo nyi denibalaŋ baa di ma wose man adansɛɛ ya, mɔna ɛ nyeedoŋ kenyiŋ dɔɔ te mɛɛ yako kɛŋa.

english_verse: John was a lamp that gave a lot of light, and you were glad to enjoy his light for a while.
translation_verse: Gyɔn yɛɛ ŋgba kanea keŋ kɛɛ naa te kɛɛ ŋalakee nɛ, te ɛ gyaewɔ nyi ɛ ke di kanea kenaŋ gyoŋ kyomii.

english_verse: But something more important than John speaks for me. I mean the things that the Father has given me to do! All of these speak for me and prove that the Father sent me.
translation_verse: Me dana ŋolo ŋon ɔ ne di ma wose man adansɛɛ, te wa adansɛɛ kenaŋ kela Gyɔn wui keŋ ɔ be dii lee ma wose man nɛ. Nawolo nyi tom keŋ me kya Wurubuarɛ ba mo do maŋ me nyiŋmaa man nyi maa yɛɛ ke kelii ɔto te mɛɛ yɛɛ ke nɛ, ne diina adansɛɛ lee ma wose man nyi me kya ŋonaŋ kpila na maŋ.

english_verse: The Father who sent me also speaks for me, but you have never heard his voice or seen him face to face.
translation_verse: Me kya ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ, ŋon gbagba mɔ ne di ma wose man adansɛɛ. Ɛ te nyii ta ɔ nɔɔwoya ya, te ɛ te naa ta ɔ dinɔɔ mɔ ya.

english_verse: You search the Scriptures, because you think you will find eternal life in them. The Scriptures tell about me,
translation_verse: Ɛ ne yase ɛ sia kasee Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan nawolo nyi na ya agyueŋ yɛna nyi ŋe man te ɛ ke nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ. Te mena Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see kɛŋa ne di ma wose man adansɛɛ.

english_verse: but you refuse to come to me for eternal life.
translation_verse: Mɔna ɛ bɛɛ gyae ɛ ke kɔŋ me gyaŋ na ɛ kaa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ ya.

english_verse: I don't care about human praise,
translation_verse: Na ma kegyaebii yɛna nyi balaŋ baa kɛlɛɛ maŋ ya.

english_verse: but I do know that none of you love God.
translation_verse: Mɔna ma gyeŋ ɛ dinɔɔ te ma gyeŋ nyi ɛ bɛɛ gyae Wurubuarɛ lee ɛ konɔɔse man ya.

english_verse: I have come with my Father's authority, and you have not welcomed me. But you will welcome people who come on their own.
translation_verse: Me kɔŋae me kya ŋon yele man mɔna ɛ te lɛɛ maŋ ya. Mɔna akpaa ŋolo de kɔŋ ŋon gbagba yele man na ɛ ke lɛɛ e.

english_verse: How could you possibly believe? You like to have your friends praise you, and you don't care about praise that the only God can give!
translation_verse: Ɛ ne gyae nyi ɛ ke kɛlɛɛ dɔŋa, mɔna ɛ bɛɛ gyae nyi Wurubuarɛ dokoloŋ ŋon ke kɛlɛɛ ŋon ya. Kenaŋ na sena te ɛ ke tale lɛɛ maŋ di?

english_verse: Don't think that I will be the one to accuse you to the Father. You have put your hope in Moses, yet he is the very one who will accuse you.
translation_verse: Ɛ na gyueŋ nyi maŋ ne gyaela me kaa yako ɛ nombiakumɛɛ fa me kya Wurubuarɛ ya. Mosesi ŋon ɛ ba mo ɛ loo gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ nɛ, ŋon ne gyaela waa mo ɛ nombiakumɛɛ gyu me kya siaman.

english_verse: Moses wrote about me, and if you had believed Moses, you would have believed me.
translation_verse: Nyi yeŋ lɛɛ Mosesi di na nafɔ ɛ kelɛɛ maŋ mɔ di, nawolo nyi ma wose man nombia te Mosesi ŋmarasewɔ.

english_verse: But if you don't believe what Moses wrote, how can you believe what I say?
translation_verse: Mɔna nyi akpaa ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ kpene keŋ ɔ be ŋmarase nɛ di ya na, sena te ɛ kelɛɛ kpene keŋ mɛɛ yako nɛ di?”

english_verse: The disciples gathered them up and filled twelve large baskets with what was left over from the five barley loaves.
translation_verse: Weenɛɛ ŋan balaŋ baŋ be dii kaase nɛ, be tɔɔsee ŋa buruburu akaŋ ŋan na be nyiŋa doŋase kufu ala.

english_verse: The disciples wanted to take him into the boat, but suddenly the boat reached the shore where they were headed. The Bread That Gives Life
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te be lɛɛ Yesu gyoŋ man do ba degbele keŋ man, debokenaŋ man te be kelii botɔɔ keŋ bee gyu nɛ.

english_verse: They replied, “What miracle will you work, so that we can have faith in you? What will you do?
translation_verse: Keŋte be bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, “Woŋ gyakoloŋ nombia te nyaŋ ke yɛɛ wola daa na dɛɛ lɛɛ neŋ di nyi Wurubuarɛ kpila na neŋ? Woŋ te nyaŋ ke yɛɛ?

english_verse: No one can come to me, unless the Father who sent me makes them want to come. But if they do come, I will raise them to life on the last day.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa me kya ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ te wɔŋ ŋolo benaa na maŋ ya nɛ, mena walaŋ ŋonaŋ be gyae waa tale kɔŋ me gyaŋ ya. Te wee ɔtomante keŋ nɛ, maa gyuusu mena walaŋ ŋonaŋ lee yeŋ man na waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ.

english_verse: “There is a boy here who has five small loaves of barley bread and two fish. But what good is that with all these people?”
translation_verse: “Bu ŋolo kyaa kɛbo keŋ ɔ dana bodobodose anoŋ na kpebia bala. Mɔna woŋ te kɛŋa gyae keyɛɛ balaŋ burum kɛwɔ.”

english_verse: They got into a boat and started across for Capernaum. Later that evening Jesus had still not come to them,
translation_verse: keŋte ba gyoowɔ degbele daale man te ba moo ba nyee baŋ bee gyu Kapanum donɔɔ man. Debaŋ kenaŋ na tɛɛ te biri mɔna Yesu sɛɛ ɔ te kɔŋa ta be gyaŋ ya.

english_verse: Jesus crossed Lake Galilee, which was also known as Lake Tiberias.
translation_verse: Weeya akalansɛɛ ŋaale wɔle nɛ, Yesu toŋa Galelia depaa keŋ bɛɛ baake ke bela nyi Tiberiase nɛ gyu ke diŋgyiŋ.

english_verse: A large crowd had seen him work miracles to heal the sick, and those people went with him.
translation_verse: Balaŋ burum sila ɔ gbɛɛ gyu botɔɔ, nawolo nyi be naa gyakoloŋ nombia na nombia dinaana ŋan ɔ be yɛɛ lee mena keŋ ɔ be kyɔ balaŋ kaweese nɛ dɔɔ.

english_verse: Philip answered, “Don't you know that it would take almost a year's wages just to buy only a little bread for each of these people?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Filipo tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Pɛ walaŋ kamasɛ kenyiŋ kyomii di koraŋ na, kaboena nyi dɛɛ mo tomyɛɛre kegyia ŋolo gyaalaŋna gyanaara kɔba bo lɛɛ weenɛɛ.”

english_verse: Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter, was one of the disciples. He spoke up and said,
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ kaseela baŋ ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Anderiase, ŋon ɔ kegyia yɛna Simɔn Pita nɛ yakowɔ nyiaa,

english_verse: Jesus took the bread in his hands and gave thanks to God. Then he passed the bread to the people, and he did the same with the fish, until everyone had plenty to eat.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu moo bodobodose ŋan te ɔ fanewɔ fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ, te ɔ moo ŋa kpɛlɛɛ fa balaŋ baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ. Mena ke te ɔ moo kpebia ŋan mɔ te ɔ fanewɔ fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ te ɔ kpɛlɛɛ ŋa fa balaŋ baŋ pou. Balaŋ baŋ diiwɔ baa ba kegyaebii.

english_verse: The people ate all they wanted, and Jesus told his disciples to gather up the leftovers, so that nothing would be wasted.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ pou kedi baa nɛ Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Ɛ tɔɔsee weenɛɛ akaŋ ŋan pou, ɛ na yeli ŋa kewɔlɛɛ ya.”

english_verse: Jesus realized that they would try to force him to be their king. So he went up on a mountain, where he could be alone. Jesus Walks on the Water (Matthew
translation_verse: Na Yesu taŋ kena nyi balaŋ baŋ ne gyae be kaa kyaŋ e, na baa ŋere e mo e see be gyoo mena dɔɔ ŋon waageŋ wɔŋa ɔ wose lee be man gyu ke kyaa bula keŋ dɔɔ bela. Yesu keta loŋ dɔɔ Mateo

english_verse: The people who had stayed on the east side of the lake knew that only one boat had been there. They also knew that Jesus had not left in it with his disciples. But the next day
translation_verse: Tɛɛ kena nɛ, balaŋ burum baŋ bɔɔ kaa depaa keŋ diŋgyiŋ nɛ naawɔ nyi degbele dokoloŋ doo la botɔɔ. Te ba gyeŋ mɔ nyi Yesu ta gyoo degbele kenaŋ man kpu na ɔ kaseela baŋ ya. Ɔ kaseela baŋ baageŋ gyoola ke man laŋ.

english_verse: Don't work for food that spoils. Work for food that gives eternal life. The Son of Man will give you this food, because God the Father has given him the right to do so.”
translation_verse: Ɛ na naase ɛ wose diyem gyae weenɛɛ ŋan gyae kewɔlɛɛ nɛ ya, mɔna ɛ yɛɛ kakyeŋ gyae weenɛɛ ŋan gyae kefa ŋon nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ nɛ. Weenɛɛ kɛŋa te maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa fa ŋon, nawolo nyi me kya Wurubuarɛ ta mo ɔ doŋ do me man keŋ nawolo nyi ɔ te lɛɛ maŋ do.”

english_verse: One of the prophets wrote, “God will teach all of them.” And so everyone who listens to the Father and learns from him will come to me.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ man ŋolo ŋmarasewɔ see nyiaa, ‘Wurubuarɛ ne gyaela waa wola walaŋ kamasɛ nombia.’ Mena dɔɔ walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ tei me kya Wurubuarɛ nombia te ɔ kasee ŋa nɛ, waa kɔŋ me gyaŋ.

english_verse: They started arguing with each other and asked, “How can he give us his flesh to eat?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Gyudatena baŋ gyoo aŋmaareŋ na dɔŋa nyiaa, “Sena te balee kei waa tale mo ɔ wosenaane fa daa na dee di?”

english_verse: That evening, Jesus' disciples went down to the lake.
translation_verse: Kelii balinɔɔ nɛ Yesu kaseela baŋ besewɔ gyu depaa keŋ nɔɔman,

english_verse: and a strong wind was making the water rough.
translation_verse: Te feliŋ dinaa daale korowɔ ne gyɔɔ loŋ keŋ dɔɔ keŋ te yɛɛ nsendee ya.

english_verse: When the disciples had rowed for five or six kilometers, they saw Jesus walking on the water. He kept coming closer to the boat, and they were terrified.
translation_verse: Ɔ kaseela baŋ kesaa degbele keŋ gyu siaman keŋ gyae keyɛɛ ŋgba gbɛɛkemaa atooro na yale nɛ, be naa Yesu dɔŋɛɛ loŋ keŋ dɔɔ ɔ ne kɔŋ ba degbele keŋ gyaŋ, keŋte gyakoloŋ gyoo wɔ.

english_verse: But he said, “I am Jesus! Don't be afraid!”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ na yee ya, maŋ ŋonaŋ.”

english_verse: some boats from Tiberias sailed near the place where the crowd had eaten the bread for which the Lord had given thanks.
translation_verse: Agbelese ŋaale mɔ lee Tiberiase donɔɔ man kɔŋ diŋgyiŋ botɔɔ, benaa na botɔɔ keŋ Yesu ba mo bodobodo fane fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ te ɔ ba mo fa balaŋ burum baŋ be dii nɛ.

english_verse: They saw that Jesus and his disciples had left. Then they got into the boats and went to Capernaum to look for Jesus.
translation_verse: Balaŋ burum baŋ kena nyi Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ ya nɛ, ba gyoowɔ agbelese ŋenaŋ man gyu Kapanum donɔɔ man bɛɛ kɛo e. Yesu yɛna nyeedoŋ weenɛɛ

english_verse: They found him on the west side of the lake and asked, “Rabbi, when did you get here?”
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ ke gyu kena Yesu boo keŋ diŋgyiŋ nɛ be bɔɔse e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, debaŋ woŋti yaa n taŋ kaa lii kɛbo?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “I tell you for certain that you are not looking for me because you saw the miracles, but because you ate all the food you wanted.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, na gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan ɛ be naa mɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ dɔɔ te ɛ silana me gbɛɛ ya, mɔna weenɛɛ ŋan ɛ be nyim di baa ɛ kegyaebii nɛ dɔɔ te ɛ silana me gbɛɛ.

english_verse: For example, when our ancestors were in the desert, they were given manna to eat. It happened just as the Scriptures say, ‘God gave them bread from heaven to eat.’ ”
translation_verse: De naanaɔ dii weenɛɛ ŋan bɛɛ baake ŋa nyi maana nɛ depampaa keŋ man, ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako daa nɛ nyiaa, ‘Ɔ faa wɔ weenɛɛ lee adido te be diiwɔ.’ ”

english_verse: Jesus then told them, “I tell you for certain that Moses wasn't the one who gave you bread from heaven. My Father is the one who gives you the true bread from heaven.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, na Mosesi faa na ŋon weenɛɛ lee adido ya, mɔna me kya yɛna walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne fa weenɛɛ gbagba ŋan ba lee adido nɛ.

english_verse: And the bread that God gives is the one who came down from heaven to give life to the world.”
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi weenɛɛ ŋan me kya Wurubuarɛ ne faa nɛ yɛna ŋon ɔ ba lee adido kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ, te ɔ ne fa balaŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa nɛ.”

english_verse: The people said, “Sir, give us this bread and don't ever stop!”
translation_verse: Keŋte be yako e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, akpaa mena na lee gyɛŋ ne gyu na fa daa weenɛɛ kɛŋa wee kamasɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied: I am the bread that gives life! No one who comes to me will ever be hungry. No one who has faith in me will ever be thirsty.
translation_verse: Te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Maŋ yɛna nyeedoŋ weenɛɛ ŋan. Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ kɔŋ me gyaŋ nɛ, tanam be gyae ka ko e ya. Te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ maŋ di na, loŋboaneŋ be gyae ka ko e ya.

english_verse: I have told you already that you have seen me and still do not have faith in me.
translation_verse: Ŋgba mena keŋ mɔ taŋ yako ŋon kaalaŋ nɛ, ɛ te naa maŋ mɔna ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ maŋ di ya.

english_verse: Everything and everyone that the Father has given me will come to me, and I won't turn any of them away.
translation_verse: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon me kya ba mo e fa maŋ nɛ, waa kɔŋ me gyaŋ. Te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ kɔŋ me gyaŋ nɛ, mɔɔ gyae maa gegi e ya.

english_verse: I didn't come from heaven to do what I want! I came to do what the Father wants me to do. He sent me,
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi me ta lee adido kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nyi me kaa yɛɛ maŋ gbagba kegyaebii ya, mɔna me kɔŋae me kaa yɛɛ ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ kegyaebii.

english_verse: and he wants to make certain that none of the ones he has given me will be lost. Instead, he wants me to raise them to life on the last day.
translation_verse: Ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ kegyaebii yɛna nyiaa, balaŋ baŋ pou ɔ ba mo do maŋ me nyiŋmaa man nɛ, me na yela be man dokoloŋ ka yo ya. Mɔna wee ɔtomante keŋ man nɛ, maa gyuusu wɔ pou lee yeŋ man kɔŋ nyeedoŋ man.

english_verse: My Father wants everyone who sees the Son to have faith in him and to have eternal life. Then I will raise them to life on the last day.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi me kya ŋon kegyaebii yɛna nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ mo ɔ loo gyakaa me dɔɔ te ɔ lɛɛ maŋ di na, waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ. Te wee ɔtomante keŋ man nɛ, maa gyuusu mena walaŋ ŋonaŋ lee yeŋ man kɔŋ nyeedoŋ man.”

english_verse: The people started grumbling because Jesus had said he was the bread that had come down from heaven.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Gyudatena baŋ fiasɛɛ bɛɛ ŋorokoso lee Yesu wose man nawolo nyi ɔ kpa, ŋon yɛna weenɛɛ ŋan ba lee adido kɔŋ nɛ.

english_verse: They were asking each other, “Isn't he Jesus, the son of Joseph? Don't we know his father and mother? How can he say that he has come down from heaven?”
translation_verse: Keŋte be yakowɔ nyiaa, “Na kei yɛna Gyosɛfo bu ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Yesu nɛ yee? Da gyeŋ ɔ kya na ɔ naa. Woŋ dɔɔ te nɛnɛɛ ɔ ne yako daa nyi ɔ lee adido bo kɔŋ?”

english_verse: The only one who has seen the Father is the one who has come from him. No one else has ever seen the Father.
translation_verse: Kei bɛɛ wolo nyi ŋolo te naa me kya ŋon ya. Walaŋ ŋon ɔ ba lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ kɔŋ nɛ, ŋon waageŋ naa na e.

english_verse: I tell you for certain that everyone who has faith in me has eternal life.
translation_verse: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, walaŋ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ maŋ di na waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaakekpaa.

english_verse: I am the bread that gives life!
translation_verse: Maŋ yɛna nyeedoŋ weenɛɛ ŋan.

english_verse: Your ancestors ate manna in the desert, and later they died.
translation_verse: Ɛ naanaɔ dii weenɛɛ ŋan bɛɛ baake ŋa nyi maana nɛ depampaa keŋ man, mɔna ba yekeewɔ.

english_verse: But the bread from heaven has come down, so that no one who eats it will ever die.
translation_verse: Mɔna nyeedoŋ weenɛɛ ŋan ba lee Wurubuarɛ man kɔŋ te mɛɛ yako ŋon ŋa wose man nombia nɛ dɔɔ, walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ di ke na ɔ be gyae waa yeŋ ya.

english_verse: I am that bread from heaven! Everyone who eats it will live forever. My flesh is the life-giving bread I give to the people of this world.
translation_verse: Maŋ yɛna nyeedoŋ weenɛɛ ŋan ba lee adido kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ. Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ di mena weenɛɛ kɛŋa na, waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaakekpaa. Weenɛɛ ŋan maa mo fa balaŋ na baa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ nɛ yɛna ma wosenaane.”

english_verse: Jesus answered: I tell you for certain that you won't live unless you eat the flesh and drink the blood of the Son of Man.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, nyi akpaa ɛ te di maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu wosenaane na ɛ kenyɔɔ me fatabo ya nɛ, ɛ be gyae ɛ ke nyiŋ nyeedoŋ ya.

english_verse: But if you do eat my flesh and drink my blood, you will have eternal life, and I will raise you to life on the last day.
translation_verse: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon waa di ma wosenaane na waa nyɔɔ me fatabo nɛ, waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ. Wee ɔtomante keŋ man nɛ, maa gyuusu mena walaŋ ŋonaŋ lee yeŋ man.

english_verse: My flesh is the true food, and my blood is the true drink.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi ma wosenaane yɛna weenɛɛ anediŋ gbagba, te me fatabo mɔ yɛna kolo anenyɔɔŋ gbagba.

english_verse: If you eat my flesh and drink my blood, you are one with me, and I am one with you.
translation_verse: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ di ma wosenaane te ɔ nyɔɔ me fatabo na, daa na e ta bese walaŋ dokoloŋ.

english_verse: The living Father sent me, and I have life because of him. Now everyone who eats my flesh will live because of me.
translation_verse: Me kya ŋon ɔ ne fa balaŋ nyeedoŋ nɛ kpila na maŋ, te wa adaworoma dɔɔ te me dana nyeedoŋ. Mena ke mɔ te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ di ma wosenaane na, maa fa e nyeedoŋ.

english_verse: The bread that comes down from heaven isn't like what your ancestors ate. They died, but whoever eats this bread will live forever.
translation_verse: Maŋ yɛna weenɛɛ ŋan ba lee adido kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ. Ma te yɛɛ ŋgba weenɛɛ ŋan ɛ naanaɔ be dii te bɔɔ yekee nɛ ya. Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon wa di mena weenɛɛ kɛŋa nɛ, waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaakekpaa.”

english_verse: Jesus was teaching in a synagogue in Capernaum when he said these things. The Words of Eternal Life
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ Yesu na wolo Gyudatena ɔsom deni man Kapanum donɔɔ man nɛ, te ɔ yako mena nombia kɛŋa.

english_verse: Many of Jesus' disciples heard him and said, “This is too hard for anyone to understand.”
translation_verse: Yesu kaseela baŋ kenyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ be yakowɔ nyiaa, “Mena kawola kɛŋa man dana doŋ. Amɔte ne gyaela waa tale lɛɛ ŋa do?”

english_verse: Jesus knew that his disciples were grumbling. So he asked, “Does this bother you?
translation_verse: Yesu ka gyeŋ nyi ɔ kaseela baŋ na ŋorokoso lee nombia kɛŋa wose man nɛ ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Nombia kɛŋa ne takaa ŋon?

english_verse: What if you should see the Son of Man go up to heaven where he came from?
translation_verse: Kenaŋ na akpaa ɛ naa maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon na gyem mee gyu adido, botɔɔ keŋ mɔɔ lee na sena te ɛ ke yɛɛ?

english_verse: The Spirit is the one who gives life! Human strength can do nothing. The words that I have spoken to you are from that life-giving Spirit.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ne faa na balaŋ nyeedoŋ. Kpene keŋ denibalaŋ na mo baŋ gbagba be doŋ yɛɛ nɛ be taŋ tɔnɔɔ ya. Nombia ŋan mɔɔ yako ŋon nɛ lee Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon man te ŋɛɛ fa nyeedoŋ.

english_verse: But some of you refuse to have faith in me.” Jesus said this, because from the beginning he knew who would have faith in him. He also knew which one would betray him.
translation_verse: Mɔna ɛ man baale bɛɛ lɛɛ di ya.” Yesu yako nombia kɛŋa nawolo nyi lee kewalaŋ keŋ na, ɔ wulaa ɔ gyeŋ baŋ bɔɔ gyae baa lɛɛ e di ya, na ŋon waa kaa lese e fa.

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “You cannot come to me, unless the Father makes you want to come. That is why I have told these things to all of you.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Kei dɔɔ te me yako ŋon nyiaa, akpaa me kya te fa ŋolo gbɛɛ nyi waa kɔŋ me gyaŋ ya na, ɔ be gyae waa tale kɔŋ ya nɛ.”

english_verse: Because of what Jesus said, many of his disciples turned their backs on him and stopped following him.
translation_verse: Kei wɔle nɛ, ɔ silala baŋ man balaŋ burum wɔŋa ba wose lee wɔle keŋ be te sila e bela ya.

english_verse: Jesus then asked his twelve disciples if they also were going to leave him.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse ɔ kaseela kufu bala baŋ mɔ nyiaa, “Ɛmɛɛ mɔ ne gyae ɛ ka bese ɛ wɔle yaa?”

english_verse: Simon Peter answered, “Lord, there is no one else that we can go to! Your words give eternal life.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Simɔn Pita tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, amɔte gyaŋ te dee gyu? Nyaŋ nyaageŋ danaana nombia ŋan ŋɛɛ fa nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ.

english_verse: We have faith in you, and we are sure that you are God's Holy One.”
translation_verse: Nɛnɛɛ da te lɛɛ neŋ di, te de ta gyem nyi nyaŋ yɛna walaŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ be kpili te ayimɛɛ be doo ɔ man ya nɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples, “I chose all twelve of you, but one of you is a demon!”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Na ɛmɛɛ balaŋ kufu bala te me baakewɔ yee? Mɔna ɛ man ŋolo yɛɛ walaŋkum e.”

english_verse: Jesus was talking about Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot. He would later betray Jesus, even though he was one of the twelve disciples.
translation_verse: Na Simɔn Isekarɔte bu Gyudase wose man nombia yaa Yesu ne yako nɛ. Nawolo nyi Gyudase yɛɛ ɔ kaseela kufu bala baŋ man ŋolo e, mɔna ŋon ne gyae la ɔ kaa lese Yesu fa yeŋ.

english_verse: Jesus decided to leave Judea and to start going through Galilee because the leaders of the people wanted to kill him.
translation_verse: Nombia kɛŋa wɔle nɛ Yesu tɛɛwɔ kila Galelia donɔɔse man. Ɔ te gyae nyi waa gyu Gyudia tɛɛle man ya, nawolo nyi Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ ne gyae gbɛɛ baa ko e.

english_verse: It was almost time for the Festival of Shelters,
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ na Gyudatena kefɛɛse man kekyaa wee dinaa kedi ta benaa.

english_verse: and Jesus' brothers said to him, “Why don't you go to Judea? Then your disciples can see what you are doing.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Yesu ɔ naabia yako e nyiaa, “Kaboena nyi nya lee kɛbo na n ke gyu Gyuda tɛɛle man na n kaseela baŋ ke nyiŋ naa gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan n ne yɛɛ nɛ.

english_verse: No one does anything in secret, if they want others to know about them. So let the world know what you are doing!”
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi walaŋ ne gyae ɔ yele ka lee na, ɔ bɛɛ yɛɛ kolo weese ya. Mena dɔɔ n keta n ne yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia kɛŋa nɛ, kaboena nyi nya lese n wose debɔɔ fa balaŋ pou.”

english_verse: Even Jesus' own brothers had not yet become his followers.
translation_verse: Ɔ naabia baŋ kolosi nombia kɛŋa nawolo nyi baŋ gbagba koraŋ te lɛɛ e di ya.

english_verse: Jesus answered, “My time hasn't yet come, but your time is always here.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba see fa maŋ nɛ te lii ta ya, mɔna ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ debaŋ kamasɛ dei fa ŋon.

english_verse: The people of this world cannot hate you. They hate me, because I tell them that they do evil things.
translation_verse: Tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ bɛɛ kɔla ŋon ya mɔna maŋ dɔɔ bɛɛ kɔla maŋ, nawolo nyi me gyakaa mɛɛ yako wɔ nyi be nombia ŋan bɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ be dei ya.

english_verse: Go on to the festival. My time hasn't yet come, and I am not going.”
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ ɛ ta gyu kedi wee dinaa keŋ, maŋ be gyae maa gyu ya nawolo nyi ma debaŋ keŋ te lii ta ya.”

english_verse: Jesus said this and stayed on in Galilee. Jesus at the Festival of Shelters
translation_verse: Ɔ ke yako nombia kɛŋa fa wɔ taŋ nɛ, te ŋon dɔɔwɔ Galelia tɛɛle man.

english_verse: After Jesus' brothers had gone to the festival, he went secretly, without telling anyone.
translation_verse: Yesu ɔ naabia ke laŋ ba wee dinaa keŋ dedii man nɛ, te ŋon mɔ korowɔ gyu botɔɔ. Mɔna ɔ weese ɔ wose bo.

english_verse: During the festival the leaders of the people looked for Jesus and asked, “Where is he?”
translation_verse: Na Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ dɔŋɛɛ bɛɛ kɛo e na bɛɛ bɔɔse nyiaa “Ɔ kyaa aleŋ?”

english_verse: The crowds even got into an argument about him. Some were saying, “Jesus is a good man,” while others were saying, “He is lying to everyone.”
translation_verse: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ man balaŋ burum gyoo kekolosi na dɔŋa lee Yesu wose man. “Baale kpa ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ kpaakpaa e, te baale mɔ kpa koa, ɔ na beo balaŋ bo na baa yo lee Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛneŋ ŋan man.”

english_verse: But the people were afraid of their leaders, and none of them talked in public about him.
translation_verse: Mɔna ŋolo te tale kolosi ɔ wose man nombia balaŋ man ya, nawolo nyi be yee Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ.

english_verse: When the festival was about half over, Jesus went into the temple and started teaching.
translation_verse: Be kedi ba wee dinaa keŋ kelii nsana nsana nɛ, Yesu gyuu Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man ɔ na wola balaŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia.

english_verse: The leaders were surprised and said, “How does this man know so much? He has never been taught!”
translation_verse: Ɔ kawola keŋ doo Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ nɔɔ te be bɔɔsewɔ nyiaa, “Sena te balee kei yɛɛwɔ gyeŋ nombia kɛŋa keŋ nyi ɔ te kasee lee ŋolo gyaŋ ya?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: I am not teaching something I thought up. What I teach comes from the one who sent me.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Kpene keŋ mɛɛ wolo nɛ ta lee maŋ gbagba gyaŋ ya, mɔna ka lee me kya Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ gyaŋ.

english_verse: If you really want to obey God, you will know if what I teach comes from God or from me.
translation_verse: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne gyae nyi waa di Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan dɔɔ nɛ, ɔtenate waa gyeŋ nyi kpene keŋ mɛɛ wolo nɛ, lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ yaa lee maŋ gbagba gyaŋ.

english_verse: If I wanted to bring honor to myself, I would speak for myself. But I want to honor the one who sent me. This is why I tell the truth and not a lie.
translation_verse: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne kolosi ŋon gbagba ɔ baam dɔɔ nɛ, na ɔ ne gyae yele bo wa fa ɔ wose. Te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne gyae bo nyi waa lese ŋon ɔ be kpila e nɛ yele na, ɔ nombia doo na ke gbɛɛ keŋ nyi dekara be doo ke man ya.

english_verse: Didn't Moses give you the Law? Yet none of you obey it! So why do you want to kill me?
translation_verse: Na Mosesi faa na ŋon mmaraase ŋan yee? Mɔna ɛ man ŋolo bee di ŋe dɔɔ ya. Woŋ dɔɔ te ɛ ne gyae ɛ ka ko maŋ?”

english_verse: The crowd replied, “You're crazy! What makes you think someone wants to kill you?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te balaŋ dikpii keŋ tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Feliŋkumɛɛ doo n man. Amɔte ne gyae la waa ko neŋ?”

english_verse: Jesus answered: I worked one miracle, and it amazed you.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Gyakoloŋ nombii dokoloŋ te me yɛɛwɔ ɛ kefɛɛfowee, keŋ na doo na ŋon gyakoloŋ nɛ.

english_verse: Moses commanded you to circumcise your sons. But it wasn't really Moses who gave you this command. It was your ancestors, and even on the Sabbath you circumcise your sons
translation_verse: Mosesi yako ŋon nyi ya kara ɛ bia baalawose. , mena dɔɔ ɛ ne kara ɛ bia baalawose ɛ kefɛɛfowee.

english_verse: in order to obey the Law of Moses. Why are you angry with me for making someone completely well on the Sabbath?
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa ɛ ne kara ɛ bia baalawose ɛ kefɛɛfo te keŋ bɛɛ wɔlɛɛ ya mmaraa ya na, weera dɔɔ te ɛ na gyeŋ baŋ do maŋ nyi ma te kyɔ walaŋ dibiliŋ kawee kefɛɛfowee?

english_verse: Don't judge by appearances. Judge by what is right.
translation_verse: Ɛ na barase bu balaŋ fɔɔ ya, ɛ di nombia na ŋe gbɛɛ.”

english_verse: Some of the people from Jerusalem were saying, “Isn't this the man they want to kill?
translation_verse: Keŋte Gyerusalɛmtena baale bɔɔsewɔ nyiaa, “Na balee kei te bɛɛ kɛo nyi baa ko nɛ yee?

english_verse: Yet here he is, speaking for everyone to hear. And no one is arguing with him. Do you suppose the authorities know he is the Messiah?
translation_verse: Ɛ kɛɛ ɔ ka seŋ balaŋ man ɔ ne kolosi keŋ ŋolo bɛɛ bɔɔse e daale ya. Yaa nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ kegyiise baŋ te lɛɛ e do nyi ŋon yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon?

english_verse: But how could that be? No one knows where the Messiah will come from, but we know where this man comes from.”
translation_verse: Balee kei dɔɔ de popou gyeŋ botɔɔ keŋ ɔ ba lee. Mɔna akpaa nyi nyeelɛɛre ŋon waa kɔŋ na, ŋolo be gyae waa gyeŋ botɔɔ keŋ waa lee ya.”

english_verse: As Jesus was teaching in the temple, he shouted, “Do you really think you know me and where I came from? I didn't come on my own! The one who sent me is truthful, and you don't know him.
translation_verse: Yesu kɛɛ wola abɔɔ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man nɛ, ɔ gyinaa ɔ woya bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Mena dɔɔ ɛ gyeŋ maŋ nideli na botɔɔ keŋ mɔɔ lee? Na maŋ gbagba me baam dɔɔ te me kɔŋawɔ ya. Walaŋ ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ ɔ yɛɛ anokoarete e, mɔna ŋon dɔɔ ɛ ba gyeŋ e ya.

english_verse: But I know the one who sent me, because I came from him.”
translation_verse: Mɔna maŋ dɔɔ ma gyeŋ e nawolo ɔ gyaŋ te ma leewɔ te ŋon kpila na maŋ.”

english_verse: Some of the people wanted to arrest Jesus right then. But no one even laid a hand on him, because his time had not yet come.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ be yela baa kyaŋ Yesu, mɔna ŋolo te tale mo ɔ nyiŋmaa tina e ya, nawolo nyi debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba see nyi waa yeŋ nɛ te lii ta ya.

english_verse: A lot of people in the crowd put their faith in him and said, “When the Messiah comes, he surely won't perform more miracles than this man has done!” Officers Sent To Arrest Jesus
translation_verse: Mɔna balaŋ burum lɛɛ e di te be bɔɔsewɔ nyiaa, “Akpaa nyeelɛɛre ŋon de kɔŋ na, waa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia kela balee kei aneyɛɛŋ kɛŋa?” Be kpila balaŋ nyi baa gyu ke kyaŋ Yesu

english_verse: When the Pharisees heard the crowd arguing about Jesus, they got together with the chief priests and sent some temple police to arrest him.
translation_verse: Farasiitena ke nyii mena keŋ balaŋ ne kolosi nombia kɛŋa adadawoya man lee Yesu wose man nɛ, baŋ na Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise kpila kotɔtɔɔtena baŋ bɛɛ deke Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ nɛ nyi baa gyu kekyaŋ e kɔŋ.

english_verse: But Jesus told them, “I will be with you a little while longer, and then I will return to the one who sent me.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yakowɔ nyiaa, “Maa kyaa ɛ gyaŋ bo weeya akalansɛɛ ŋaale na maa bese gyu walaŋ ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ gyaŋ.

english_verse: You will look for me, but you won't find me. You cannot go where I am going.”
translation_verse: Ɛ ke kɛo maŋ mɔna ɛ be gyae ɛ kena maŋ ya, te botɔɔ keŋ me kyaa mɔ nɛ, ɛ be gyae ɛ ke tale kɔŋ ya.”

english_verse: The people asked each other, “Where can he go to keep us from finding him? Is he going to some foreign country where our people live? Is he going there to teach the Greeks?
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, “Aleŋ yaa balee kei ne gyae waa yeu gyu te dɔɔ gyae dɛɛ naa e ya? Yaa ɔ ne gyae waa yeu ba gyu Girikitena gyaŋ botɔɔ keŋ de balaŋ be yaasee gyu nɛ ɔ kaa wola wɔ abɔɔ?”

english_verse: What did he mean by saying that we will look for him, but won't find him? Why can't we go where he is going?” Streams of Life-Giving Water
translation_verse: Ɔ ke yako nyi, “Ɛ ke kɛo maŋ mɔna ɛ be gyae ɛ kena maŋ ya, Te botɔɔ keŋ me gyu nɛ ɛ be gyae ɛ ke tale gyu ya nɛ, na sena yaa ɔ ne yako baa? Na woŋ nombia yɛna kɛŋa baa?” Nyeedoŋ nombia

english_verse: On the last and most important day of the festival, Jesus stood up and shouted, “If you are thirsty, come to me and drink!
translation_verse: Gyudatena kefɛɛse man kekyaa weekedi ɔtomante keŋ nɛ, Yesu korowɔ seŋ te ɔ gyinaa ɔ nɔɔ kolosi te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Akpaa nyaŋ ŋon loŋboaneŋ dana neŋ na kɔŋ me gyaŋ na n kaa nyɔɔ loŋ.

english_verse: Have faith in me, and you will have life-giving water flowing from deep inside you, just as the Scriptures say.”
translation_verse: ‘Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ maŋ di na, nyeedoŋ nombia gyae ka lee ɔ man kɔŋ ŋgba mena keŋ loŋ na lee ditiribuŋ man kɔŋ nɛ. Nnɛ te Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see yakowɔ.’ ”

english_verse: Jesus was talking about the Holy Spirit, who would be given to everyone that had faith in him. The Spirit had not yet been given to anyone, since Jesus had not yet been given his full glory. The People Take Sides
translation_verse: Yesu kekolosi kei nɛ, na Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon waa kaa mo do baŋ baa lɛɛ e di nɛ man nombia yaa ɔ ne kolosi nɛ. Debaŋ kenaŋ na ɔ ta moo ta Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon do ŋolo man ya, nawolo nyi Yesu gyoorobiiri keŋ ta lee ta debɔɔ ya. Balaŋ kekpase lee Yesu dɔɔ

english_verse: When the crowd heard Jesus say this, some of them said, “He must be the Prophet!”
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ ke nyii Yesu nɔɔwoya kɛŋa nɛ be man baale yakowɔ nyiaa, “Ampaŋ, balee kei yɛna Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ.”

english_verse: The Scriptures say that the Messiah will come from the family of King David. Doesn't this mean that he will be born in David's hometown of Bethlehem?”
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako nyiaa, “Nyeelɛɛre ŋon waa lee bo Bɛtelɛhɛm donɔɔ man botɔɔ keŋ Gyoo Defidi be kyaa nɛ, te waa lee bo gyoo Defidi loŋtoro man.”

english_verse: The people started taking sides against each other because of Jesus.
translation_verse: Lee Yesu dɔɔ, balaŋ baŋ man kpasewɔ.

english_verse: Some of them wanted to arrest him, but no one laid a hand on him. The Leaders Refuse To Have Faith in Jesus
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ baale gyaewɔ nyi baa kyaŋ Yesu, mɔna ŋolo te tale mo ɔ nyiŋmaa tina e ya. Gyudatena kegyiise te lɛɛ Yesu di ya

english_verse: They answered, “No one has ever spoken like this man!”
translation_verse: Keŋte be tiranɔɔ fa kegyiise baŋ nyiaa, “Ŋolo na ŋolo te kolosi ta see ŋgba balee kei nɛ ya.”

english_verse: The Pharisees said to them, “Have you also been fooled?
translation_verse: Te Farasiitena baŋ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Doona ɔ taŋ beo ɛmɛɛ mɔ?

english_verse: Not one of the chief priests or the Pharisees has faith in him.
translation_verse: Ɛ te nyii nyi donɔɔ kegyiise kɛwɔ man ŋolo yaa Farasiitena kɛwɔ man ŋolo te lɛɛ Yesu di?

english_verse: And these people who don't know the Law are under God's curse anyway.”
translation_verse: Mɔna mena balaŋ burum kɛwɔ baŋ bɔɔ gyeŋ Mosesi mmaraa keŋ ya nɛ, baŋ doola kefaala tɛɛ.”

english_verse: Nicodemus was there at the time. He was a member of the council, and was the same one who had earlier come to see Jesus. He said,
translation_verse: Nikodemose ŋon ɔ be kyaa gyu Yesu gyaŋ nɛ yɛɛ kegyiise baŋ man ŋolo e. Te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa,

english_verse: “Our Law doesn't let us condemn people before we hear what they have to say. We cannot judge them before we know what they have done.”
translation_verse: “Da mmaraa ne fa daa gbɛɛ nyi dɛɛ bu ŋolo fɔɔ keŋ de te nyii ta ɔ nɔɔman nombia yee?”

english_verse: Then they said, “Nicodemus, you must be from Galilee! Read the Scriptures, and you will find that no prophet is to come from Galilee.” A Woman Caught in Sin
translation_verse: Keŋte be tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Weera nyaŋ mɔ ne kolosi ŋgba n yɛɛ Galelia baale e nɛ? Nyaŋ kɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan man nideli, n kena nyi Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre ŋolo ta lee ta Galelia ya.”

english_verse: but Jesus walked out to the Mount of Olives.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ ke yaasee nɛ, Yesu gyuuwɔ oliifi bula keŋ dɔɔ.

english_verse: Then early the next morning he went to the temple. The people came to him, and he sat down and started teaching them.
translation_verse: Tɛɛ kena nɛ ɔ besewɔ kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man. Balaŋ baŋ pou be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ kaa wuuwɔ ɔ dɔɔ te ɔ tisiwɔ kyaa ɔ na wola wɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia.

english_verse: The Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses brought in a woman who had been caught in bed with a man who wasn't her husband. They made her stand in the middle of the crowd.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Gyudatena mmaraa wolala na Farasiitena baŋ moo alo ŋolo ŋon ɔ ba lee ɔ baale wɔle te bɔɔ kyaŋ e nyiŋmaa nyiŋmaa nɛ kɔŋ kaa seŋ Yesu na balaŋ baŋ pou siaman.

english_verse: Then they said, “Teacher, this woman was caught sleeping with a man who isn't her husband.
translation_verse: Keŋte be yako Yesu nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, da te kyaŋ alo kei ɔ ne gyae baala.

english_verse: The Law of Moses teaches that a woman like this should be stoned to death! What do you say?”
translation_verse: Mosesi mmaraa ŋan na wola daa nyi dee fuŋii mena alo kei dinɔɔ boya ko. Te nyaŋ mɔ n kpoo?”

english_verse: They asked Jesus this question, because they wanted to test him and bring some charge against him. But Jesus simply bent over and started writing on the ground with his finger.
translation_verse: Be yako nombia kɛŋa nyi baa mo teese Yesu nɔɔman, na baa nyiŋ mo nombia gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ. Mɔna Yesu gɔŋasewɔ te ɔ moo ɔ nyimbii ŋmarase kolo tɛɛle man.

english_verse: They kept on asking Jesus about the woman. Finally, he stood up and said, “If any of you have never sinned, then go ahead and throw the first stone at her!”
translation_verse: Be kaseŋ bɛɛ daa e nyi waa tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nɛ, ɔ korowɔ seŋ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Nyaŋ ŋon n gyeŋ nyi n te yɛɛ ta dukum see ya nɛ taŋgbɛɛ mo boe fuŋ e”

english_verse: Once again he bent over and began writing on the ground.
translation_verse: Te ɔ besewɔ gɔŋase ŋmarase kolo tɛɛle man.

english_verse: The people left one by one, beginning with the oldest. Finally, Jesus and the woman were there alone.
translation_verse: Be ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, be yaaseewɔ dokoloŋ dokoloŋ tina e yela. Be man kegyiise baŋ taŋgbɛɛ la lee botɔɔ, te kaa Yesu na alo ŋon na balaŋ dikpii keŋ baageŋ.

english_verse: Jesus stood up and asked her, “Where is everyone? Isn't there anyone left to accuse you?”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu korowɔ seŋ te ɔ bɔɔse alo ŋon nyiaa, “Alo nde wɔ? Ŋolo te tale bu neŋ fɔɔ yee?”

english_verse: “No sir,” the woman answered. Then Jesus told her, “I am not going to accuse you either. You may go now, but don't sin anymore.” Jesus Is the Light for the World
translation_verse: Te alo ŋon tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Me Gbeŋgyoo, ŋolo te bu maŋ fɔɔ ya.” Te Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Maŋ mɔ be gyae maa bu neŋ fɔɔ ya. Ta, na tekaa yɛɛ dukum bela ya.” Yesu yɛna keŋmaŋee fa balaŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ

english_verse: Once again Jesus spoke to the people. This time he said, “I am the light for the world! Follow me, and you won't be walking in the dark. You will have the light that gives life.”
translation_verse: Nombia kɛŋa wɔle nɛ Yesu besewɔ kolosi fa balaŋ baŋ nyiaa, “Maŋ yɛna keŋmaŋee fa balaŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ. Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ sila maŋ na ɔ sia gyae ke gyuusu na waa naa nyeedoŋ gbɛɛ keŋ. Ɔ be gyae waa ta ditiŋtɛɛ man ya.”

english_verse: The Pharisees objected, “You are the only one speaking for yourself, and what you say isn't true!”
translation_verse: Keŋte Farasiitena baŋ yako e nyiaa, “Nyaŋ gbagba ne diina n wose man adansɛɛ, mɔna nya adansɛɛ keŋ betaŋ tɔnɔɔ fa daa ya.”

english_verse: Jesus replied: Even if I do speak for myself, what I say is true! I know where I came from and where I am going. But you don't know where I am from or where I am going.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Nyi akpaa maŋ gbagba de di ma wose man adansɛɛ koraŋ nɛ, ke doo na ke gbɛɛ. Nawolo nyi ma gyeŋ botɔɔ keŋ mɔɔ lee na botɔɔ keŋ mee gyu. Ɛmɛɛ ba gyeŋ botɔɔ keŋ mɔɔ lee ya, te ɛ ba gyeŋ botɔɔ keŋ mee gyu ya.

english_verse: You judge in the same way that everyone else does, but I don't judge anyone.
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ ne bu balaŋ fɔɔ bo lee denibalaŋ nombia kedi gbɛɛ man, mɔna maŋ dɔɔ mɔɔ ne bu ŋolo fɔɔ ya.

english_verse: If I did judge, I would judge fairly, because I would not be doing it alone. The Father who sent me is here with me.
translation_verse: Mɔna nyi akpaa me yako nyi maa bu ŋolo fɔɔ koraŋ nɛ, ke doo na ke gbɛɛ nawolo nyi na maŋ maageŋ ne buu na fɔɔ keŋ ya. Daa na me kya ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ ne buu na fɔɔ kenaŋ.

english_verse: I am one of my witnesses, and the Father who sent me is the other one.
translation_verse: Maŋ gbagba ne di ma wose man adansɛɛ, te me kya ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ mɔ ne di ma wose man adansɛɛ.”

english_verse: “Where is your Father?” they asked. “You don't know me or my Father!” Jesus answered. “If you knew me, you would know my Father.”
translation_verse: Keŋte be bɔɔse e nyiaa, “N kya ŋonaŋ kyaa aleŋ?” Te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ ba gyeŋ maŋ ya te ɛ ba gyeŋ me kya ŋon mɔ ya. Nyi akpaa yeŋ gyeŋ maŋ nɛ, nafɔ ɛ ka gyeŋ me kya ŋon mɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus said this while he was still teaching in the place where the temple treasures were stored. But no one arrested him, because his time had not yet come. You Cannot Go Where I Am Going
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ Yesu na wola nombia kɛŋa Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man nɛ, na ɔ seŋɛɛ benaa na adakaase ŋan bɛɛ mo abɔɔ do ŋe man fa Wurubuarɛ nɛ. Mɔna ŋolo te tale kyaŋ e ya, nawolo nyi debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba see e nɛ te lii ta ya. Yesu kekolosi lee ɔ ka bese gyu adido wose man

english_verse: Jesus also told them, “I am going away, and you will look for me. But you cannot go where I am going, and you will die with your sins unforgiven.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Mee gyu baa. Ɛ ke kɛo maŋ, mɔna ɛ ka yeŋ ɛ nombiakumɛɛ man. Ɛ be gyae ɛ ke tale gyu botɔɔ keŋ mee gyu nɛ ya.”

english_verse: The people asked, “Does he intend to kill himself? Is that what he means by saying we cannot go where he is going?”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, “Na ɔ ne gyae waa ko ɔ wose baa yaa sena te ɔ ne yako nyi, ɛ be gyae ɛ ke tale gyu botɔɔ keŋ mee gyu ya nɛ?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “You are from below, but I am from above. You belong to this world, but I don't.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛmɛɛ lee tɛɛle kei dɔɔ kɛbo, mɔna maŋ dɔɔ ma lee adido. Ɛ yɛɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋwɔ, mɔna maŋ te yɛɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ walaŋ e ya.

english_verse: This is why I said you will die with your sins unforgiven. If you don't have faith in me for who I am, you will die, and your sins will not be forgiven.”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ te mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, ɛ ka yeŋ ɛ nombiakumɛɛ man nɛ. Nyi akpaa ɛ bɛɛ ɛ te lɛɛ maŋ di nyi maŋ yɛna ŋon ɔ kyaa pɛte be yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ ya nɛ, ɛ ka yeŋ ɛ nombiakumɛɛ man.”

english_verse: “Who are you?” they asked Jesus. Jesus answered, “I am exactly who I told you at the beginning.
translation_verse: Keŋte be bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, “Nyaŋ yɛna amɔte?” Te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Maŋ yɛna walaŋ ŋon mɔɔ taŋ wulaa yako ŋon lee kewalaŋ keŋ nɛ.

english_verse: There is a lot more I could say to condemn you. But the one who sent me is truthful, and I tell the people of this world only what I have heard from him.”
translation_verse: Me dana nombia burum ŋan kaboena nyi maa yako lee ɛ wose man na maa bu ŋon fɔɔ. Mɔna walaŋ ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ ɔ nombia doo na ke gbɛɛ, mena dɔɔ kpene keŋ me nyi lee ɔ gyaŋ na, keŋte mɛɛ yako fa balaŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ.”

english_verse: No one understood that Jesus was talking to them about the Father.
translation_verse: Mɔna balaŋ baŋ te nyii nombia ŋan man nyi Yesu ne kolosi baa lee ɔ kya Wurubuarɛ wose man ya.

english_verse: Jesus went on to say, “When you have lifted up the Son of Man, you will know who I am. You will also know that I don't do anything on my own. I say only what my Father taught me.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Nyi akpaa ɛ gyɔ maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ mataa adido nɛ, botɔɔ te ɛ kena nyi maŋ yɛna walaŋ ŋon me kpa me yɛɛ nɛ. Botɔɔ te ɛ kena nyiaa mɔɔ ne yɛɛ kolo me baam dɔɔ ya. Mɔna mɛɛ kolosi kpene keŋ me kya ŋon ne yako maŋ nɛ bo.

english_verse: The one who sent me is with me. I always do what pleases him, and he will never leave me.”
translation_verse: Te ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ ne kpuɛ na maŋ. Ɔ te tinaa ta maŋ yela maageŋ ya, nawolo nyi debaŋ kamasɛ na mɛɛ yɛɛ ɔ kegyaebii bo.”

english_verse: After Jesus said this, many of the people put their faith in him. The Truth Will Set You Free
translation_verse: Keŋte balaŋ burum baŋ bɔɔ nyii nombia ŋan Yesu ne yako nɛ lɛɛ e di. Anokoare keŋ ne gyaela ke yela ɛ ke nyiŋ ɛ wose

english_verse: Jesus told the people who had faith in him, “If you keep on obeying what I have said, you truly are my disciples.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Yesu yako Gyudatena baŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ e di nɛ nyiaa “Akpaa ɛ di kpene keŋ mɛɛ wola ŋon nɛ dɔɔ na, kenaŋ na ɛ yɛɛ me kaseela gbagbawɔ.

english_verse: You will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.”
translation_verse: Kei gyae ke yela ɛ ka gyeŋ anokoare keŋ, te anokoare keŋ gyae ke yela ɛ ke nyiŋ ɛ wose.”

english_verse: They answered, “We are Abraham's children! We have never been anyone's slaves. How can you say we will be set free?”
translation_verse: Keŋte be tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “De yɛɛ Aberaham ɔ naanasewɔ, te de ta bese ta ŋolo gbeŋi ya. Sena yaa n ne yako nyi dɛɛ lee gbeŋiri man na dee nyiŋ da wose?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: I tell you for certain that anyone who sins is a slave of sin!
translation_verse: Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Anokoare yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi nyaŋ ŋon n yɛɛ dukum nɛ, n ta bese gbeŋi fa dukum keŋ.

english_verse: And slaves don't stay in the family forever, though the Son will always remain in the family.
translation_verse: Gbeŋi bɛɛ bese dekpaŋalaŋ duuluŋ walaŋ ya, mɔna dekpaŋalaŋte bu dɔɔ ɔ yɛɛ dekpaŋalaŋ duuluŋ walaŋ e kekpaa.

english_verse: I know that you are from Abraham's family. Yet you want to kill me, because my message isn't really in your hearts.
translation_verse: Ma gyeŋ nyi ɛ yɛɛ Aberaham ɔ naanasewɔ mɔna ɛ ne gyae ɛ ka ko maŋ, nawolo nyi me nombia be doo ɛ konɔɔse man ya.

english_verse: I am telling you what my Father has shown me, just as you are doing what your father has taught you. Your Father Is the Devil
translation_verse: Nombia ŋan me kya ba wola maŋ nɛ ŋan te mee kolosi fa ŋon, te ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ ŋan ɛ be nyii lee ɛ kya gyaŋ nɛ ŋan te ɛ ne yɛɛ.”

english_verse: The people said to Jesus, “Abraham is our father!” Jesus replied, “If you were Abraham's children, you would do what Abraham did.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te be tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Aberaham yɛna de kya.” Te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Akpaa ɛ yɛɛ Aberaham biawɔ nɛ, nafɔ ɛ keyɛɛ abɔɔ ŋan Aberaham be yɛɛ nɛ.

english_verse: Instead, you want to kill me for telling you the truth that God gave me. Abraham never did anything like that.
translation_verse: Mɔna ɛ te yase sia nyi ɛ ka ko maŋ nawolo nyi ma te yako ŋon anokoare keŋ mɔɔ nyii lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ nɛ. Aberaham te yɛɛ mena nombia kɛŋa dinɔɔ ya.

english_verse: But you are doing exactly what your father does.” “Don't accuse us of having someone else as our father!” they said. “We just have one father, and he is God.”
translation_verse: Ɛ ne yɛɛ kpene keŋ ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ kya ne yɛɛ nɛ bo.” Te be lɛɛ ka aŋmaareŋ nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ waageŋ yɛna de kya, da te yɛɛ diidɔɔre bia ya.”

english_verse: Jesus answered: If God were your Father, you would love me, because I came from God and now I am here. He sent me. I did not come on my own.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Nyi akpaa ɛ kya yɛna Wurubuarɛ nɛ nafɔ ɛ ke gyae maŋ, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ ŋonaŋ gyaŋ te ma leewɔ kɔŋ kɛbo. Na maŋ gbagba me baam dɔɔ te me kɔŋawɔ ya. Wurubuarɛ ŋonaŋ gbagba kpila na maŋ.

english_verse: Why can't you understand what I am talking about? Can't you stand to hear what I am saying?
translation_verse: Weera dɔɔ te ɛ be nyii me nombia ŋan man ya? Nawolo nyi ɛ bɛɛ gyae ɛ ke nyii me nombia ya.

english_verse: Your father is the devil, and you do exactly what he wants. He has always been a murderer and a liar. There is nothing truthful about him. He speaks on his own, and everything he says is a lie. Not only is he a liar himself, but he is also the father of all lies.
translation_verse: Ɛ kya yɛna ɔbɔnsam te ɛ kya ŋonaŋ kegyaebii te ɛ ne gyae ɛ ke yɛɛ. Ɔ yɛɛ awudiini e leenaŋ gyaŋgba gyaŋgba keŋ, te ɔ bɛɛ gyae anokoare ya nawolo nyi anokoare be doo ɔ man ya. Akpaa ɔ ne di dekara nɛ ke yɛɛ ŋon gbagba dinɔɔ ke, nawolo nyi akɛrɛɛ te wulu ɔ man te ŋon yɛna akɛrɛɛ pou nyee.

english_verse: Everything I have told you is true, and you still refuse to have faith in me.
translation_verse: Mɔna maŋ dɔɔ nombia ŋan ŋe doo na ŋe gbɛɛ nɛ te mɛɛ yako ŋon, mena dɔɔ te ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ maŋ di ya.

english_verse: Can any of you accuse me of sin? If you cannot, why won't you have faith in me? After all, I am telling you the truth.
translation_verse: Ɛ man woŋti ne gyae la waa tale wola nyi nombiikum kei te me yɛɛwɔ? Te akpaa mɛɛ yako ŋon anokoare na, woŋ dɔɔ te ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ maŋ di ya?

english_verse: Anyone who belongs to God will listen to his message. But you refuse to listen, because you don't belong to God. Jesus and Abraham
translation_verse: Walaŋ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ walaŋ e nɛ, ɔ ne nyii Wurubuarɛ dei. Kpene keŋ dɔɔ te ɛ bee nyii Wurubuarɛ dei yɛna nyi, ɛ te yɛɛ ɔ balaŋwɔ ya.”

english_verse: The people told Jesus, “We were right to say that you are a Samaritan and that you have a demon in you!”
translation_verse: Keŋte Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, “De yako nyi n yɛɛ Samaria baale e te feliŋkum doo n man na, de karae?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “I don't have a demon in me. I honor my Father, and you refuse to honor me.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Feliŋkum be doo me man ya. Mɛɛ lese me kya ŋon yele bo, mɔna ɛmɛɛ bee bu maŋ ya.

english_verse: I tell you for certain that if you obey my words, you will never die.”
translation_verse: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ di me nombia dɔɔ nɛ ɔ be gyae waa yeŋ ya.”

english_verse: Then the people said, “Now we are sure that you have a demon. Abraham is dead, and so are the prophets. How can you say that no one who obeys your words will ever die?
translation_verse: Keŋte Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ yako e nyiaa, “Nɛnɛɛ da te naa gyeŋ nyi feliŋkum doo n man. Aberaham ta yem mena mɔ te Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ mɔ yekeewɔ. Mɔna nyaŋ ne yako nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ di n nombia dɔɔ na ɔ be gyae waa yeŋ ya.

english_verse: Are you greater than our father Abraham? He died, and so did the prophets. Who do you think you are?”
translation_verse: Nyaŋ kela na de naana Aberaham ŋon? Aberaham ta yem mena mɔ te Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ mɔ yekeewɔ. N ne yako baa nyi nyaŋ yɛna amɔte?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “If I honored myself, it would mean nothing. My Father is the one who honors me. You claim that he is your God,
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Akpaa ma lese maŋ gbagba yele nɛ, ma yele kalese keŋ yɛɛ yakaa. Me kya ŋon ɛmɛɛ kpa ŋon yɛna ɛ Wurubuarɛ nɛ na lesena ma yele.

english_verse: even though you don't really know him. If I said I didn't know him, I would be a liar, just like all of you. But I know him, and I do what he says.
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ ba gyeŋ e ya, mɔna maŋ dɔɔ ma gyeŋ e. Nyi akpaa me yako ŋon nyi mɔɔ gyeŋ e ya nɛ na me ta bese dekarate ŋgba ɛmɛɛ nɛ, mɔna ma gyeŋ e te mee dii ɔ nombia dɔɔ.

english_verse: Jesus answered, “I tell you for certain that even before Abraham was, I was, and I am.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa “Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, pɛ baa lola Aberaham nɛ na maŋ kyaa.”

english_verse: The people picked up stones to kill Jesus, but he hid and left the temple.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te be gate boya baa fuŋii e, mɔna Yesu sarakeewɔ lee Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man.

english_verse: As Jesus walked along, he saw a man who had been blind since birth.
translation_verse: Yesu keta ɔ ne lam nɛ ɔ naa balee ŋolo ŋon ɔ sia be yɛlɛɛ leenaŋ ɔ biatɛɛ nɛ.

english_verse: Jesus' disciples asked, “Teacher, why was this man born blind? Was it because he or his parents sinned?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ bɔɔse e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, amɔte dukum faa la te ba lola e siayɛlɛɛsate? Yɛɛ ŋon gbagba wui yaa ɔ lolala wui?”

english_verse: “No, it wasn't!” Jesus answered. “But because of his blindness, you will see God work a miracle for him.
translation_verse: Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Ɔ sia keyɛlɛɛ te yɛɛ ɔ dukum yaa ɔ lolala wui ya. Ba lola e bo mena na balaŋ kena gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ waa yɛɛ ɔ man.

english_verse: As long as it is day, we must do what the one who sent me wants me to do. When night comes, no one can work.
translation_verse: Weese nyiŋa ɔ kyaa nɛ, kaboena nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ tom, Tɛɛ gyae ke yuu keŋ ŋolo be gyae waa tale yɛɛ tom bela ya.

english_verse: While I am in the world, I am the light for the world.”
translation_verse: Me nyiŋa me kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, maŋ yɛna keŋmaŋee fa tɛɛle kei balaŋ.”

english_verse: After Jesus said this, he spit on the ground. He made some mud and smeared it on the man's eyes.
translation_verse: Ɔ keyako nombia kɛŋa taŋ nɛ, Yesu tɔɔ nɔɔloŋ lɔ tɛɛle man te ɔ buu tɛɛle te ɔ moowɔ taa balee ŋon sia ŋan dɔɔ,

english_verse: Then he said, “Go wash off the mud in Siloam Pool.” The man went and washed in Siloam, which means “One Who Is Sent.” When he had washed off the mud, he could see.
translation_verse: keŋte ɔ yako e nyiaa, “Gyu kefaafo n sia depaa keŋ bɛɛ baake ke Siloam nɛ man.” Mena dɔɔ balee ŋon gyuuwɔ kefaafo ɔ sia, ɔ ka bese kɔŋ dɛɛ na ɔ sia te gyuusu.

english_verse: The man's neighbors and the people who had seen him begging wondered if he really could be the same man.
translation_verse: Ɔ tebia na balaŋ baŋ ba gyeŋ e nyi nafɔ ɔ kyaa na ɔ na solee abɔɔ nɛ bɔɔsewɔ nyiaa, “Na balee kei ne kyaa la kyaa solee abɔɔ nɛ yee?”

english_verse: Some of them said he was the same beggar, while others said he only looked like him. But he told them, “I am that man.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te baale kpa “Ŋon ŋonaŋ” mɔna baale mɔ kpa “Koa, na ŋon ŋonaŋ ya, ɔ lee e bo.” Mena dɔɔ balee ŋon gbagba yakowɔ nyiaa, “Maŋ ŋonaŋ.”

english_verse: “Then how can you see?” they asked.
translation_verse: Te be bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Sena yaa n yɛɛwɔ te n sia gyuusuwɔ?”

english_verse: He answered, “Someone named Jesus made some mud and smeared it on my eyes. He told me to go and wash it off in Siloam Pool. When I did, I could see.”
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Balee ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Yesu nɛ, buuna tɛɛle te ɔ moowɔ mataa me sia te ɔ yako maŋ nyi maa gyu Siloam depaa keŋ man kefaafo me sia. Mena dɔɔ me gyuuwɔ, te me kabetaa faafo me sia na te ŋe gyuusuwɔ.”

english_verse: “Where is he now?” they asked. “I don't know,” he answered. The Pharisees Try To Find Out What Happened
translation_verse: Keŋte be bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Ɔ kyaa aleŋ?” Te ɔ tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyi “Mɔɔ gyeŋ ya.” Farasiitena ne kɛo balee ŋon kekyɔ keŋ man

english_verse: The day when Jesus made the mud and healed the man was a Sabbath. So the people took the man to the Pharisees.
translation_verse: Wee kenaŋ keŋ Yesu be buu tɛɛle mo kyɔ siayɛlɛɛsate ŋon nɛ yɛɛ Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee ke.

english_verse: They asked him how he was able to see, and he answered, “Jesus made some mud and smeared it on my eyes. Then after I washed it off, I could see.”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Farasiitena baŋ besewɔ bɔɔse balee ŋon bela mena keŋ be yɛɛ te ɔ sia ŋan be gyuusu. Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyi, “Ɔ buu tɛɛle bo taa me sia, te me gyuuwɔ kefaafo te ŋe gyuusuwɔ.”

english_verse: Some of the Pharisees said, “This man Jesus doesn't come from God. If he did, he would not break the law of the Sabbath.” Others asked, “How could someone who is a sinner work such a miracle?” Since the Pharisees could not agree among themselves,
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Farasiitena baŋ baale yakowɔ nyi “Balee ŋon ɔ be yɛɛ nombii kei nɛ ta lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ ya, nawolo nyi ɔ bee di da kefɛɛfowee keŋ dɔɔ ya.” Mɔna baale mɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Sena te walaŋ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛre e nɛ, waa tale yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia kɛŋa?” Botɔɔ na te be man kpasewɔ.

english_verse: they asked the man, “What do you say about this one who healed your eyes?” “He is a prophet!” the man told them.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Farasiitena baŋ besewɔ bɔɔse balee ŋon bela nyiaa, “N sia te ɔ gyuusuwɔ. Mena dɔɔ sena te n ne yako lee ɔ wose man?” Keŋte balee ŋon tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre e.”

english_verse: But the Jewish leaders would not believe that the man had once been blind. They sent for his parents
translation_verse: Kɛŋa pou wɔle nɛ Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ te sɛɛ lɛɛ di nyi ɔ sia yɛlɛɛwɔ te nɛnɛɛ ba te kyɔ e kawee ya, te ba ke baake ɔ kya na ɔ naa kɔŋ,

english_verse: and asked them, “Is this the son that you said was born blind? How can he now see?”
translation_verse: te be bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ bu yɛna kei? Ɛ ne yako baa nyi ba lola e na ɔ sia te yɛlɛɛ? Sena kɔŋala te nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ ɔ ne naa?”

english_verse: The man's parents answered, “We are certain that he is our son, and we know that he was born blind.
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ kya na ɔ naa tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Da gyeŋ nyi de bu ŋonaŋ, te da gyeŋ nyi da lola e na ɔ sia te yɛlɛɛ.

english_verse: But we don't know how he got his sight or who gave it to him. Ask him! He is old enough to speak for himself.”
translation_verse: Mɔna dɔɔ gyeŋ mena keŋ be kɔŋ te ɔ ne naa nɛ ya, te dɔɔ gyeŋ ŋon ɔ be kyɔ e ya. Ɛ bɔɔse e, ɔ ta bee keŋ waa tale tiranɔɔ fa ɔ wose.”

english_verse: The man's parents said this because they were afraid of their leaders. The leaders had already agreed that no one was to have anything to do with anyone who said Jesus was the Messiah.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ te balee ŋon ɔ kya na ɔ naa yakowɔ nyi, “Ɔ ta bee, ɛ bɔɔse e nɛ.”

english_verse: The man replied, “I don't know if he is a sinner or not. All I know is that I used to be blind, but now I can see!”
translation_verse: Keŋte balee ŋon tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Ɔ yɛɛ dukumyɛɛre o, ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ kpaakpaa e o maŋ ba gyeŋ ya. Keŋ maŋ gyeŋ yɛna nyi nafɔ na me sia te yɛlɛɛ te nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ mɛɛ naa.”

english_verse: “What did he do to you?” they asked. “How did he heal your eyes?”
translation_verse: Keŋte ba besewɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Woŋ te ɔ yɛɛ neŋ te n sia gyuusuwɔ?”

english_verse: The man answered, “I have already told you once, and you refused to listen. Why do you want me to tell you again? Do you also want to become his disciples?”
translation_verse: Te ɔ tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Me taŋ keyako ŋon, te ɛ bɛɛ gyae ɛ ke tei ya. Woŋ dɔɔ te ɛ ne gyae ɛ kenyii ke bela? Ɛmɛɛ mɔ ne gyae ɛ ka bese ɔ kaseela?”

english_verse: The leaders insulted the man and said, “You are his follower! We are followers of Moses.
translation_verse: Keŋte ba tee e te be yakowɔ nyiaa, “Nyaŋ yɛna walaŋ ŋonaŋ kaseele, mɔna daa yɛɛ Mosesi wui kaseelawɔ.

english_verse: We are sure God spoke to Moses, but we don't even know where Jesus comes from.”
translation_verse: Da gyeŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ kolosiwɔ fa Mosesi, te balee kei dɔɔ dɔɔ gyeŋ botɔɔ keŋ ɔ ba lee gba ya.”

english_verse: “How strange!” the man replied. “He healed my eyes, and yet you don't know where he comes from.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te balee ŋon yako wɔ nyiaa, “Te doo me nɔɔ ŋgba ɛ ba gyeŋ botɔɔ keŋ ɔ ba lee ya, mɔna ŋon gyuusu na me sia.

english_verse: We know that God listens only to people who love and obey him. God doesn't listen to sinners.
translation_verse: Da gyeŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ bee nyii nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra ya. Ɔ ne nyii balaŋ baŋ bee nyii e te bɛɛ yɛɛ kpene keŋ ɔ ne gyae nɛ bo.

english_verse: And this is the first time in history anyone has ever given sight to someone born blind.
translation_verse: Lee tɛɛle kei kewalaŋ nɛ, ŋolo te nyii ta nyi ŋolo te gyuusu walaŋ ŋon bɔɔ lola e na ɔ sia te yɛlɛɛ nɛ sia ya.

english_verse: Jesus could not do anything unless he came from God.”
translation_verse: Akpaa balee kei ta lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ ya nɛ, ɔ be gyae waa tale yɛɛ nombii kei ya.”

english_verse: The leaders told the man, “You have been a sinner since the day you were born! Do you think you can teach us anything?” Then they said, “You can never come back into any of our synagogues!”
translation_verse: Te be tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Nyaŋ ŋon bɔɔ lola neŋ dukum man te bɔɔ kɛɛle neŋ dukum man na, nyaŋ ne gyae n kawola daa nombia?” Keŋte be gegi e lee te be tɔɔ e nyi ɔ na kɔŋ bɔ ɔsom deni keŋ man bela ya.

english_verse: When Jesus heard what had happened, he went and found the man. Then Jesus asked, “Do you have faith in the Son of Man?”
translation_verse: Yesu kenyii nyi ba te gegi e lee bɔ ɔsom deni keŋ man nɛ, ɔ ke kɛo e te ɔ naa e te bɔɔse e nyiaa, “N ne lɛɛ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon di?”

english_verse: He replied, “Sir, if you will tell me who he is, I will put my faith in him.”
translation_verse: Te balee ŋon tiranɔɔ fa e nyi, “Me Gbeŋgyoo, amɔte ŋonaŋ? Yako maŋ na maa lɛɛ e di.”

english_verse: “You have already seen him,” Jesus answered, “and right now he is talking with you.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako e nyiaa, “N taŋ kena e. Ŋon yɛna ŋon ɔ ne kolosi fa neŋ debokeiman nɛ.”

english_verse: The man said, “Lord, I put my faith in you!” Then he worshiped Jesus.
translation_verse: Te balee ŋon yakowɔ nyiaa, “Mɛɛ lɛɛ e di me Gbeŋgyoo.” Te ɔ buŋawɔ Yesu siaman som e.

english_verse: Jesus told him, “I came to judge the people of this world. I am here to give sight to the blind and to make blind everyone who can see.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu yakowɔ nyiaa, “Me kɔŋae tɛɛle kei dɔɔ na balaŋ nombiakumɛɛ ka lee debɔɔ. Na baŋ bɔɔ ne naa ya nɛ kena, na baŋ bɛɛ naa nɛ sia keyɛlɛɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “If you were blind, you would not be guilty. But now that you claim to see, you will keep on being guilty.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ sia deŋ te yɛlɛɛ nɛ nafɔ ŋolo be gyae waa bu ŋon fɔɔ ya. Te keŋ ɛ kpa ɛ ne naa dɔɔ te ɛ sɛɛ ɛ doo ɛ dukum keyɛɛ man.”

english_verse: Jesus said: I tell you for certain only thieves and robbers climb over the fence instead of going in through the gate to the sheep pen.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yakowɔ nyiaa, “Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, walaŋ ŋon ɔ ta mo denanɔɔ keŋ man gyoo namense debɔɔlɔɔ keŋ man ya, te ɔ gyem mo yenaŋ kale gyoo na ɔ yɛɛ ŋmɛɛlate e, te ɔ na mo doŋ bo lɛo balaŋ.

english_verse: But the gatekeeper opens the gate for the shepherd, and he goes in through it. The sheep know their shepherd's voice. He calls each of them by name and leads them out.
translation_verse: Denanɔɔ dekere ŋon na toro fa e te namense baŋ ne nyii ɔ woya. Ɔ ne baake ŋon gbagba namense baŋ yele na ɔ ta mo wɔ lee.

english_verse: When he has led out all of his sheep, he walks in front of them, and they follow, because they know his voice.
translation_verse: Te ɔ mo wɔ pou lee na ɔ ne tɛɛ be siaman, te namense baŋ ne sila e nawolo nyi ba gyeŋ ɔ woya.

english_verse: The sheep will not follow strangers. They don't recognize a stranger's voice, and they run away.
translation_verse: Bɔɔ gyae baa sila walaŋ yakaate ya. Baa yeu lee ɔ gyaŋ nawolo nyi bɔɔ gyeŋ ɔ woya ya.”

english_verse: Jesus told the people this story. But they did not understand what he was talking about. Jesus Is the Good Shepherd
translation_verse: Yesu gyɔɔ dudu kei fa wɔ, mɔna be te nyii kpene keŋ ɔ ne yako nɛ asɛɛ ya.

english_verse: Jesus said: I tell you for certain that I am the gate for the sheep.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa “Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, maŋ yɛna namense debɔɔlɔɔ keŋ denanɔɔ.

english_verse: Everyone who came before me was a thief or a robber, and the sheep did not listen to any of them.
translation_verse: Baŋ pou bɔɔ taŋgbɛɛ maŋ kɔŋ kaa beo nyi Wurubuarɛ kpila na wɔ nɛ, be yɛɛ ŋmɛɛlatenawɔ na baŋ bɛɛ mo doŋ lɛo balaŋ abɔɔ nɛ, mɔna namense baŋ te nyii wɔ ya.

english_verse: I am the gate. All who come in through me will be saved. Through me they will come and go and find pasture.
translation_verse: Maŋ yɛna denanɔɔ keŋ, akpaa ŋolo de tɛɛ me man gyoo na, maa lɛɛ ɔ nyee. Waa gyoo na waa lee na waa nyiŋ weenɛɛ kpaakpaa di.

english_verse: A thief comes only to rob, kill, and destroy. I came so everyone would have life, and have it fully.
translation_verse: Ŋmɛɛlate dɔɔ ɔ ne kɔŋ bo kaa ŋmɛɛle na ɔ ta koo na ɔ te wɔlɛɛ abɔɔ. Maŋ kɔŋae na ɛ kenyiŋ nyeedoŋ, na ɛ kenyiŋ ke kekpaa.

english_verse: I am the good shepherd, and the good shepherd gives up his life for his sheep.
translation_verse: Maŋ yɛna namensekɛɛlere kpaakpaate ŋon. Namensekɛɛlere kpaakpaate ŋon na mo ɔ wose fa yeŋ ɔ namense baŋ dɔɔ.

english_verse: Hired workers are not like the shepherd. They don't own the sheep, and when they see a wolf coming, they run off and leave the sheep. Then the wolf attacks and scatters the flock.
translation_verse: Mɔna paate ŋon bɔɔ moo nyi waa kɛɛ namense baŋ dɔɔ na baa tɔ e kom nɛ, na ŋon yɛna namensekɛɛlere ŋon ɔ tee namense baŋ ya. Mena dɔɔ ɔ naa nyi gbougbouse ne kɔŋ na, ɔ ne yeu tina namense baŋ yela, na gbougbouse baŋ te kyaŋ baale na akaŋ baŋ te yaasee.

english_verse: I have other sheep that are not in this sheep pen. I must also bring them together, when they hear my voice. Then there will be one flock of sheep and one shepherd.
translation_verse: Me dana namense baale baŋ bɔɔ kpuɛ kɛwɔ man ya. Kaboena nyi maa mo baŋ mɔ kaa kpu na kɛwɔ na be popou keyɛɛ walaŋ dokoloŋ namense, debɔɔlɔɔ dokoloŋ man, na be kɛɛlere ke yɛɛ dokoloŋ na baa nyii ma dei.

english_verse: The Father loves me, because I give up my life, so I may receive it back again.
translation_verse: Kpene keŋ dɔɔ me kya ne gyae me nombia yɛna nyi, ma te desina ma wose nyi maa mo ma wose fa yeŋ ma kegyaebii man na maa bese foŋ lee yeŋ man.

english_verse: No one takes my life from me. I give it up willingly! I have the power to give it up and the power to receive it back again, just as my Father commanded me to do.
translation_verse: Ŋolo be gyae waa tale lɛo ma nyeedoŋ lee me gyaŋ ya. Mɔna maŋ gbagba ma kegyaebii man te maa mo ma wose fa yeŋ. Me dana doŋ maa mo ma wose fa yeŋ, te me dana doŋ maa bese nyiŋ nyeedoŋ bela. ‘Kei yɛna kpene keŋ me kya ŋon be yako maŋ nyi maa yɛɛ.’ ”

english_verse: The people took sides because of what Jesus had told them.
translation_verse: Lee nɔɔwoya kɛŋa dɔɔ Gyudatena baŋ besewɔ kpase ba wose man.

english_verse: Many of them said, “He has a demon in him! He is crazy! Why listen to him?”
translation_verse: Be man balaŋ burum yakowɔ nyiaa, “Ɔ dana feliŋkum mena dɔɔ ɔ nyee ne wɔlɛɛ. Woŋ dɔɔ te ɛ ne nyii e?”

english_verse: But others said, “How could anyone with a demon in him say these things? No one like this could give sight to a blind person!” Jesus Is Rejected
translation_verse: Baale mɔ kpa, “Walaŋ ŋon feliŋkum doo ɔ man be gyae waa tale kolosi nnɛ ya! Sena te feliŋkum gyae ke tale gyuusu siayɛlɛɛsate sia?” Balaŋ baŋ kebɛɛ Yesu

english_verse: One day he was walking in the part of the temple known as Solomon's Porch,
translation_verse: Na Yesu dɔŋɛɛ ɔ ne kila poto keŋ bɛɛ baake ke Solomɔn poto Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ dekpaŋalaŋ man nɛ.

english_verse: and the people gathered all around him. They said, “How long are you going to keep us guessing? If you are the Messiah, tell us plainly!”
translation_verse: Keŋte Gyudatena baale kaa yilaawɔ ɔ gyaŋ bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Sena yaa n tinaa daa yela te de bɛɛ gyeŋ da nyee na de nawɔɔ ya nɛ? Yako daa anokoare keŋ. Nyaŋ yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ?”

english_verse: Jesus answered: I have told you, and you refused to believe me. The things I do by my Father's authority show who I am.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Me taŋ keyako ŋon mɔna ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ maŋ di ya. Gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan mɔɔ yɛɛ me kya yele man nɛ, ne diina ma wose man adansɛɛ,

english_verse: But since you are not my sheep, you don't believe me.
translation_verse: mɔna ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ maŋ di ya, nawolo nyi ɛ te yɛɛ me namensewɔ ya.

english_verse: My sheep know my voice, and I know them. They follow me,
translation_verse: Me namense ne nyii me dei, te ma gyeŋ wɔ te bee sila maŋ.

english_verse: and I give them eternal life, so that they will never be lost. No one can snatch them out of my hand.
translation_verse: Mɛɛ fa wɔ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ, te bɔɔ gyae baa yo lee Wurubuarɛ siaman ya. Ŋolo be gyae waa tale lɛo wɔ lee me gyaŋ ya.

english_verse: My Father gave them to me, and he is greater than all others. No one can snatch them from his hands,
translation_verse: Me kya ŋon ɔ ba mo wɔ do me nyiŋmaa man nɛ, dana doŋ kela kolo kamasɛ te ŋolo be gyae waa tale lɛo wɔ lee me kya nyiŋmaa man ya.

english_verse: and I am one with the Father.
translation_verse: Daa na me kya yɛɛ walaŋ dokoloŋ.”

english_verse: Once again the people picked up stones in order to kill Jesus.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Gyudatena baŋ gate boya baa fuŋ e bela.

english_verse: But he said, “I have shown you many good things my Father sent me to do. Which one are you going to stone me for?”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Me kya te yeli ma te yɛɛ nombia kpaakpaa burum ɛ siaman, ŋe man woŋti dɔɔ te ɛ ne gyae ɛ ke fuŋii maŋ boya?”

english_verse: They answered, “We are not stoning you because of any good thing you did. We are stoning you because you did a terrible thing. You are just a man, and here you are claiming to be God!”
translation_verse: Te Gyudatena baŋ tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Na abɔɔ nya ayɛɛŋ kɛŋa dɔɔ te dɛɛ gyae dee fuŋii neŋ boya ya, mɔna mena keŋ n ne yako mmusuo nombia dɔɔ. Nawolo nyi n yɛɛ deniwalaŋ e, mɔna n na mo n wose maa na Wurubuarɛ.”

english_verse: So why do you accuse me of a terrible sin for saying that I am the Son of God? After all, it is the Father who prepared me for this work. He is also the one who sent me into the world.
translation_verse: weera dɔɔ te ɛ ne yako maŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ ba lese maŋ te ɔ be kpila maŋ kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ nyi mɛɛ mo ma wose maa na Wurubuarɛ keŋ mɛɛ yako nyi me yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ bu e dɔɔ?

english_verse: If I don't do as my Father does, you should not believe me.
translation_verse: Akpaa me bɛɛ yɛɛ kpene keŋ me kya ŋon kpa ma yɛɛ ya na, ɛ na lɛɛ maŋ di ya,

english_verse: But if I do what my Father does, you should believe because of that, even if you don't have faith in me. Then you will know for certain that the Father is one with me, and I am one with the Father.
translation_verse: te akpaa mɛɛ yɛɛ ŋa na, nyi ɛ be lɛɛ maŋ di koraŋ ya na ɛ lɛɛ abɔɔ ŋan mɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ di, na ɛ ka gyeŋ na ɛ kenyii asɛɛ nyiaa daa na me kya ŋon yɛɛ dokoloŋ”

english_verse: Again they wanted to arrest Jesus. But he escaped
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ be gyaewɔ baa kyaŋ e bela, te ɔ sarakeewɔ lee be man.

english_verse: and crossed the Jordan to the place where John had earlier been baptizing. While Jesus was there,
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ toŋ Gyɔɔdan boo keŋ gyu botɔɔ keŋ Gyɔn be kyaa sɔ balaŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ kaalaŋ nɛ, te ɔ ke kyaawɔ botɔɔ.

english_verse: many people came to him. They were saying, “John didn't work any miracles, but everything he said about Jesus is true.”
translation_verse: Balaŋ burum kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ te be yako dɔŋa nyiaa, “Gyɔn te yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia ya, mɔna kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɔ be yako lee balee kei wose man nɛ yɛɛ ampaŋ.”

english_verse: A lot of those people also put their faith in Jesus.
translation_verse: Keŋte balaŋ burum baŋ ba seŋɛɛ botɔɔ nɛ lɛɛ Yesu di.

english_verse: The sisters sent a message to the Lord and told him that his good friend Lazarus was sick.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Mɛɛre na Maata doo Yesu tiila nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, n gyoo ŋon loŋ bɛɛ lam ɛmɛɛ na e nsana ya nɛ na wee.”

english_verse: When Jesus heard this, he said, “His sickness won't end in death. It will bring glory to God and his Son.”
translation_verse: Yesu kenyii tiila keŋ nɛ ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Kawee kei te yɛɛ kawee keŋ gyae ka ko Lasarose ya. Mɔna ke gyae ke yela balaŋ kena Wurubuarɛ gyoorobiiri na ka ke yela baa lese maŋ Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon mɔ yele.”

english_verse: But he stayed where he was for two more days.
translation_verse: Mɔna ɔ kenyii nyi Lasarose na wee nɛ, ɔ besewɔ di weeya ala botɔɔ keŋ ɔ kyaa nɛ.

english_verse: Then he said to his disciples, “Now we will go back to Judea.”
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ ɔ yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Ɛ fa dɛɛ bese gyu Gyudia tɛɛle man bela.”

english_verse: “Teacher,” they said, “the people there want to stone you to death! Why do you want to go back?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ yako e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, weeya akalansɛɛ ŋaale yaa ŋgba Gyudatena be yele baa fuŋii neŋ boya nɛ. Woŋ dɔɔ te n ne gyae n ka bese gyu botɔɔ bela?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Aren't there twelve hours in each day? If you walk during the day, you will have light from the sun, and you won't stumble.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Wee kamasɛ dana ke nelim na ka weese. Akpaa ŋolo dɔŋɛɛ weeseteŋteŋ nɛ, ɔ bɛɛ gyɔ ɔ nae ya nawolo nyi weese kyaa.

english_verse: But if you walk during the night, you will stumble, because you don't have any light.”
translation_verse: Mɔna nyi akpaa ɔ dɔŋɛɛ ditiŋtɛɛ man na ɔ ne gyɔ ɔ nae, nawolo nyi keŋmaŋee be kyaa na waa naa ya.”

english_verse: Then he told them, “Our friend Lazarus is asleep, and I am going there to wake him up.”
translation_verse: Kei wɔle na Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “De gyoo Lasarose te doo dosoroŋ mɔna maa gyu na me kaa gyuusu e.”

english_verse: Jesus really meant that Lazarus was dead, but they thought he was talking only about sleep.
translation_verse: Na Lasarose yeŋ nombia yaa Yesu ne yako, mɔna ɔ kaseela baŋ na gyueŋ bo nyi dosoroŋ gbagba yaa Lasarose doowɔ.

english_verse: I am glad I wasn't there, because now you will have a chance to put your faith in me. Let's go to him.”
translation_verse: mɔna lee ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ dana maŋ gyoŋ nyi mɔɔ kyaa botɔɔ pɛte ɔ yeŋawɔ ya, na ɛ kenyiŋ lɛɛ maŋ di. Ɛ fa dee gyu ɔ gyaŋ.”

english_verse: Thomas, whose nickname was “Twin,” said to the other disciples, “Come on. Let's go, so we can die with him.” Jesus Brings Lazarus to Life
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɔ kaseela baŋ man ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e Tomase yaa Ata nɛ yako ɔ tebia kaseela akaŋ baŋ nyiaa, “Ɛ fa de popou ke sila da wolale ŋon gyu. Nyi yɛɛ yeŋ ke na, de popou ka yeŋ kpu na e.”

english_verse: When Jesus got to Bethany, he found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb four days.
translation_verse: Yesu ke gyu ka gyoo Bɛtani donɔɔ man nɛ, be yako e nyiaa be kewuu Lasarose weeya anaarate kenaŋ.

english_verse: and many people had come from the city to comfort Martha and Mary because their brother had died.
translation_verse: Na Gyudatena burum te kɔŋ Maata na Mɛɛre gyaŋ be kaa gyam wɔ lee be ŋmɛɛtee ŋon yeŋ man.

english_verse: When Martha heard that Jesus had arrived, she went out to meet him, but Mary stayed in the house.
translation_verse: Maata ke nyii nyi Yesu ne kɔŋ nɛ, ɔ leewɔ gyu ke gyaŋee na e. Na Mɛɛre dɔɔ ŋon kyaa dekpaŋalaŋ man.

english_verse: Martha said to Jesus, “Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died.
translation_verse: Maata ke gyu ke tuŋ Yesu nɛ ɔ yako e nyiaa, “Me Gbeŋgyoo, nyi akpaa nyeŋ kyaa kɛbo na nafɔ me ŋmɛɛtee deŋ ta yem ya!

english_verse: Jesus told her, “Your brother will live again!”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, “N ŋmɛɛtee ŋon waa bese foŋ na waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ bela.”

english_verse: Martha answered, “I know he will be raised to life on the last day, when all the dead are raised.”
translation_verse: Te Maata yako e nyiaa, “Ayi ma gyeŋ nyi wee ɔtomante keŋ woya pou baa foŋ lee yeŋ man nɛ, ŋon mɔ waa foŋ.”

english_verse: Jesus then said, “I am the one who raises the dead to life! Everyone who has faith in me will live, even if they die.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako e bela nyiaa, “Maŋ yɛna walaŋ ɔ ne gyuusu balaŋ lee yeŋ man te be nyiŋ nyeedoŋ. Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon waa lɛɛ maŋ di na, nyi ɔ yem koraŋ na waa bese nyiŋ nyeedoŋ,
