translation_verse: Maŋ walaŋ ŋon mɔɔ naa nombia kɛŋa na me sia nɛ ne diina ka wose man adansɛɛ, te ma adansɛɛ keŋ yɛɛ anokoare. Ma gyeŋ nyi anokoare yaa mɛɛ yako, te mee di adansɛɛ baa na ɛmɛɛ mɔ ke nyiŋ lɛɛ di.

english_verse: All this happened so that the Scriptures would come true, which say, “No bone of his body will be broken”
translation_verse: Kei yela Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan kɔŋawɔ ampaŋ. Nombia ŋenaŋ yɛna nyiaa, “Bɔɔ gyae baa tekeri ɔ ŋɔe dokoloŋ pɛ gba ya.”

english_verse: and “They will see the one in whose side they stuck a spear.” Jesus Is Buried (Matthew
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ daale mɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Balaŋ baa kɛɛ walaŋ ŋon bɔɔ sɔɔ e apia nɛ.” Yesu kewuu Mateo

english_verse: The tomb was nearby, and since it was the time to prepare for the Sabbath, they were in a hurry to put Jesus' body there.
translation_verse: Keŋ Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee keŋ na benaa te bee desina ba wose mɔ te yebɔɔ keŋ mɔ benaa na botɔɔ dɔɔ nɛ, ba moo woo ŋon kewuu ke man. Keŋte be biliŋi boe dinaa daale tɔ ke nɔɔ.

english_verse: Joseph from Arimathea was one of Jesus' disciples. He had kept it secret though, because he was afraid of the Jewish leaders. But now he asked Pilate to let him have Jesus' body. Pilate gave him permission, and Joseph took it down from the cross.
translation_verse: Kɛŋa pou wɔle nɛ, Gyosɛfo ŋon ɔ ba lee Arimatia donɔɔ man nɛ kaa sola gbɛɛ lee kegyia Pilato gyaŋ na ɔ kaa lese Yesu woo ŋon kewuu. Na Gyosɛfo kei mɔ yɛɛ Yesu kaseele e, mɔna ɔ weese ɔ wose bo nawolo nyi ɔ ne yee Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ. Kegyia Pilato ke fa e gbɛɛ nɛ te ɔ gyuuwɔ ka lese woo ŋon.

english_verse: The two men wrapped the body in a linen cloth, together with the spices, which was how the Jewish people buried their dead.
translation_verse: Be balaŋ bala moo akaralɛɛ na abɔɔ ofoŋ nyiŋan mo milii Yesu ŋgba mena keŋ Gyudatena ne desina woo pɛte bee wuu e nɛ.

english_verse: Thomas replied, “You are my Lord and my God!”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Tomase tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Nyaŋ yɛna me Gbeŋgyoo na me Wurubuarɛ.”

english_verse: On Sunday morning while it was still dark, Mary Magdalene went to the tomb and saw that the stone had been rolled away from the entrance.
translation_verse: Kosida nebo nebo fuu keŋ tɛɛ te ŋmaŋee ta nideli ya nɛ, Mɛɛre ŋon ɔ ba lee Magedala donɔɔ man nɛ, karatɛɛwɔ gyu botɔɔ keŋ bɔɔ wuu Yesu nɛ. Ɔ ke gyu botɔɔ nɛ, ɔ naawɔ nyi be te biliŋi boe keŋ lee yebɔɔ keŋ nɔɔman.

english_verse: The disciple who got there first then went into the tomb, and when he saw it, he believed.
translation_verse: Wɔle wɔle na te kaseele ɔ dɔɔ ŋon mɔ gyoowɔ kena abɔɔ ŋan, keŋte ŋon mɔ lɛɛwɔ di.

english_verse: Although Thomas the Twin was one of the twelve disciples, he wasn't with the others when Jesus appeared to them.
translation_verse: Wee kenaŋ keŋ Yesu be kaa lese ɔ wose wola ɔ kaseela kufu bala baŋ nɛ, na be man ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Tomase yaa Ata nɛ be kpuɛ na ɔ tebia ya.

english_verse: and saw two angels inside. They were dressed in white and were sitting where Jesus' body had been. One was at the head and the other was at the foot.
translation_verse: keŋte ɔ naa Wurubuarɛ kpilala bala na ba doona kegbase wolomɛɛ be kyaa botɔɔ keŋ bɔɔ mo Yesu woo ŋon doosi nɛ. Ŋolo kyaa yebɔɔ keŋ nyee gbɛɛ te ŋolo mɔ kyaa ke nawɔɔ gbɛɛ.

english_verse: She ran to Simon Peter and to Jesus' favorite disciple and said, “They have taken the Lord from the tomb! We don't know where they have put him.”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɔ yeuwɔ kɔŋ Simɔn Pita na kaseele ŋon Yesu ne gyae ɔ nombia nɛ gyaŋ kaa yako wɔ nyiaa, “Be ta lese de Gbeŋgyoo lee yebɔɔ keŋ man, te dɔɔ gyeŋ botɔɔ keŋ bɔɔ moo e ka see ya.”

english_verse: Peter and the other disciple started for the tomb.
translation_verse: Keŋte Pita na kaseele ŋon yeuwɔ gyu yebɔɔ keŋ tɛɛ.

english_verse: They ran side by side, until the other disciple ran faster than Peter and got there first.
translation_verse: Be kee yeu keŋ nɛ, kaseele ŋon Yesu ne gyae ɔ nombia nɛ yeuwɔ gbaa Pita kyaŋ gbɛɛ ka gyoo yebɔɔ keŋ tɛɛ.

english_verse: He bent over and saw the strips of linen cloth lying inside the tomb, but he did not go in.
translation_verse: Ɔ buŋawɔ kɛɛ yebɔɔ keŋ man na, ɔ naa akaralɛɛ keŋ kaageŋ doo la mɔna ɔ ta gyoo ke man ya.

english_verse: When Simon Peter got there, he went into the tomb and saw the strips of cloth.
translation_verse: Simɔn Pita ka lee wɔle kɔŋ na te ŋon kaa gyoowɔ yebɔɔ keŋ man. Ɔ gyoowɔ na te ɔ naa akaralɛɛ keŋ,

english_verse: He also saw the piece of cloth that had been used to cover Jesus' face. It was rolled up and in a place by itself.
translation_verse: na kanyaŋ keŋ bɔɔ mo bake ɔ nyee nɛ. Kanyaŋ keŋ be doo akaralɛɛ keŋ gyaŋ ya, mɔna akaralɛɛ keŋ te bɔɔ ke doo yenaŋ.

english_verse: Mary Magdalene stood crying outside the tomb. She was still weeping, when she stooped down
translation_verse: Mɔna Mɛɛre ŋon ɔ be sila wɔ gyu nɛ seŋɛɛ yebɔɔ keŋ tɛɛ ɔ ne wii. Ɔ ka seŋ ɔ ne wii nɛ te ɔ buŋawɔ kɛɛ yebɔɔ keŋ man,

english_verse: The angels asked Mary, “Why are you crying?” She answered, “They have taken away my Lord's body! I don't know where they have put him.”
translation_verse: Keŋte be bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Alo, weera n ne wii?” Te ɔ tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Nawolo nyi be ta lese me Gbeŋgyoo lee yebɔɔ keŋ man, te mɔɔ gyeŋ botɔɔ keŋ bɔɔ mo e ka see ya.”

english_verse: Jesus asked her, “Why are you crying? Who are you looking for?” She thought he was the gardener and said, “Sir, if you have taken his body away, please tell me, so I can go and get him.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Alo, weera n ne wii? Amɔte yaa n ne kɛo?” Na Mɛɛre gyeŋ nyi walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne kɛɛ wɔɔbii keŋ dɔɔ nɛ ŋonaŋ, mena dɔɔ ɔ yako e nyiaa, “Tobaale, akpaa nyaŋ moona woo ŋon ka weese na, wola maŋ botɔɔ keŋ n ba mo e ka see na me kaa mo e ta.”

english_verse: Jesus told her, “Don't hold on to me! I have not yet gone to the Father. But tell my disciples I am going to the one who is my Father and my God, as well as your Father and your God.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Na tina maŋ ya, nawolo nyi me te gyuu ta me kya ŋon gyaŋ ya. Mɔna bese gyu me kaseela baŋ gyaŋ na n kaa yako wɔ nyi ‘Mee gyu me kya ŋon ɔ yɛɛ ɛ kya e nɛ, na me Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ ɛ Wurubuarɛ e nɛ gyaŋ.’ ”

english_verse: and showed them his hands and his side. When the disciples saw the Lord, they became very happy.
translation_verse: Ɔ ke yako wɔ mena nɛ, ɔ moo ɔ nyiŋmaa na ɔ yenaabia gyam keŋ wola wɔ. Keŋte yɛɛ ɔ kaseela baŋ gyoŋ nideli nyi ba te naa be Gbeŋgyoo ŋon bela.

english_verse: The disciples were afraid of the Jewish leaders, and on the evening of that same Sunday they locked themselves in a room. Suddenly, Jesus appeared in the middle of the group. He greeted them
translation_verse: Kosida kenaŋ balinɔɔ nɛ, kaseela baŋ gyuuwɔ ke gyaŋee deni man te be tɔɔ disim do ba wose, nawolo nyi bee yee Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ. Debokenaŋ man te Yesu kaa bɛɛwɔ lee ba nsana te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Wosefɛɛreŋ dɛɛ kyaa na ŋon.”

english_verse: After Jesus had greeted them again, he said, “I am sending you, just as the Father has sent me.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Wosefɛɛreŋ dɛɛ kyaa na ŋon, mena keŋ me kya ŋon be kpila maŋ nɛ, mena ke yaa mɛɛ kpila ɛmɛɛ mɔ.”

english_verse: If you forgive anyone's sins, they will be forgiven. But if you don't forgive their sins, they will not be forgiven.” Jesus and Thomas
translation_verse: Akpaa ɛ mo ŋolo nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ e na, Wurubuarɛ mɔ waa mo kyɛɛ e. Te akpaa ɛ bɛɛ ɛ ta mo ŋolo nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ e ya na, Wurubuarɛ mɔ be gyae waa mo kyɛɛ e ya.” Yesu kalese ɔ wose wola Tomase

english_verse: So they told him, “We have seen the Lord!” But Thomas said, “First, I must see the nail scars in his hands and touch them with my finger. I must put my hand where the spear went into his side. I won't believe unless I do this!”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Yesu kaseela akaŋ baŋ yako Tomase nyiaa, “Da te naa de Gbeŋgyoo ŋon.” Keŋte Tomase yako wɔ nyiaa, “Akpaa me ta mo me sia naa nyeelabia gyaŋtarɛɛ ŋan, na maa mo me nyimbii do ɔ yenaabia gyam keŋ man ya nɛ, maŋ be gyae maa lɛɛ di korakora ya.”

english_verse: A week later the disciples were together again. This time, Thomas was with them. Jesus came in while the doors were still locked and stood in the middle of the group. He greeted his disciples
translation_verse: Weeya gyanaara wɔle nɛ, na ɔ kaseela baŋ te gyaŋee deni man bela. Wee kenaŋ na Tomase mɔ kpuɛ na wɔ. Ba te tɔ asima ŋan pou, karatetee dokoloŋ te Yesu kaa bɛɛwɔ lee be dɔɔ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Wosefɛɛreŋ dɛɛ kyaa na ŋon.”

english_verse: and said to Thomas, “Put your finger here and look at my hands! Put your hand into my side. Stop doubting and have faith!”
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ yako Tomase nyiaa, “Mo n nyiŋmaa do me nyiŋmaa bɔɔ man na n ke kɛɛ me nyiŋmaase, na n ka mo n nyimbii do ma yenaŋ kɛɛ. Tina aŋmaareŋ kelɛɛ yela na n ke lɛɛ di.”

english_verse: Jesus said, “Thomas, do you have faith because you have seen me? The people who have faith in me without seeing me are the ones who are really blessed!” Why John Wrote His Book
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Keŋ n be naa maŋ dɔɔ te n lɛɛwɔ di? Balaŋ baŋ ba te naa maŋ ya te bɔɔ lɛɛ di nɛ, baŋ na nyeebam.”

english_verse: Jesus worked many other miracles for his disciples, and not all of them are written in this book.
translation_verse: Yesu besewɔ yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia burum ŋan ɔ kaseela baŋ be naa na be sia, mɔna na ŋe popou te be ŋmarasewɔ do tɔne kei man ya.

english_verse: But these are written so that you will put your faith in Jesus as the Messiah and the Son of God. If you have faith in him, you will have true life.
translation_verse: Mɔna be ŋmarase kɛŋa bo na ɛ ke lɛɛ di nyi Yesu yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu na nyeelɛɛre ŋon. Na nyaŋ ŋon n lɛɛ e di na, n ke nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ ɔ yele man.

english_verse: Simon Peter, Thomas the Twin, Nathanael from Cana in Galilee, and the brothers James and John, were there, together with two other disciples.
translation_verse: Ɔ kaseela baŋ bɛɛ baake wɔ Simɔn Pita na Tomase ŋon bɛɛ baake e Ata nɛ na Natanɛle ŋon ɔ ba lee Kaanan donɔɔ keŋ ke kyaa Galelia tɛɛle man nɛ, na Sebedeo bia na ɔ kaseela bala baale gyuuwɔ ke kyaa bom.

english_verse: Simon Peter said, “I'm going fishing!” The others said, “We will go with you.” They went out in their boat. But they didn't catch a thing that night.
translation_verse: Keŋte Simɔn Pita yako wɔ nyiaa, “Mee gyu boo man me kaa kyaŋ kpebia.” Te baŋ mɔ yako e nyiaa, “Daa mɔ dee sila neŋ gyu.” Mena dɔɔ be gyuuwɔ ka gyoo degbele man te be laŋawɔ. Be lɔɔ asawu nelim kenaŋ pou mɔna ba te kyaŋ kolo ya.

english_verse: Early the next morning Jesus stood on the shore, but the disciples did not realize who he was.
translation_verse: Tɛɛ kena nebo nebo fuu nɛ, be kɛɛ na ŋolo seŋɛɛ la boo nɔɔman, na n kɛɛ nɛ Yesu ŋonaŋ. Mɔna ɔ kaseela baŋ ta gyeŋ e ya.

english_verse: So he told them, “Let your net down on the right side of your boat, and you will catch some fish.” They did, and the net was so full of fish that they could not drag it up into the boat.
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ fuŋ ya asawu keŋ lɔ degbele keŋ dunoluŋ dɔɔ na ɛ ke kyaŋ kpebia.” Mena dɔɔ be fuŋa asawu keŋ lɔ botɔɔ yenaŋ te be kyaŋa kpebia burum keŋ nyi bɔɔ ne tale baa wɔŋee asawu keŋ gba ya.

english_verse: Jesus' favorite disciple told Peter, “It's the Lord!” When Simon heard it was the Lord, he put on the clothes he had taken off while he was working. Then he jumped into the water.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Yesu kaseele ŋon ɔ ne gyae ɔ nombia nɛ yako Pita nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo Yesu ŋonaŋ.” Simɔn Pita kenyii nyi de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu ŋonaŋ nɛ te ɔ moo ɔ kegba wurikyim milii ɔ wose, nawolo nyi ɔ wulaa kpee ke. Keŋte ɔ toŋawɔ gyoo loŋ keŋ man yɛlɛɛ ɔ ne gyu Yesu gyaŋ.

english_verse: When the disciples got out of the boat, they saw some bread and a charcoal fire with fish on it.
translation_verse: Be kelii dekeŋkeŋ dɔɔ nɛ be naa boalaŋ na nyewalaŋ, na kpebia gyakaa ke dɔɔ na bodobodo mɔ doo yenaŋ.

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples, “Bring some of the fish you just caught.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ mo kpebia baŋ ɛ be kyam nɛ baale kɔŋ.”

english_verse: large fish, but still the net did not rip.
translation_verse: ). Keŋ na kpebia baŋ booroŋ kei koraŋ nɛ, asawu keŋ te tirii ya.

english_verse: Jesus said, “Come and eat!” But none of the disciples dared ask who he was. They knew he was the Lord.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ kɔŋ kaa di ɛ nebo weenɛɛ.” Mɔna ɔ kaseela baŋ ŋolo te tale gyɔ ɔ konɔɔ bɔɔse e nyi “Nyaŋ yɛna amɔte ya?” Nawolo nyi ba gyeŋ nyi be Gbeŋgyoo Yesu ŋonaŋ.

english_verse: Jesus took the bread in his hands and gave some of it to his disciples. He did the same with the fish.
translation_verse: Te Yesu kɔŋawɔ kaa mo bodobodo keŋ fa wɔ, kenaŋ wɔle te ɔ moo kpebia baŋ mɔ fa wɔ.

english_verse: This was the third time Jesus appeared to his disciples after he was raised from death. Jesus and Peter
translation_verse: Yesu kefoŋ lee yeŋ man nɛ, kei yɛna betabotoorote keŋ ɔ ba lese ɔ wose wola ɔ kaseela baŋ. Yesu kekolosi na Pita

english_verse: When Jesus and his disciples had finished eating, he asked, “Simon son of John, do you love me more than the others do?” Simon Peter answered, “Yes, Lord, you know I do!” “Then feed my lambs,” Jesus said.
translation_verse: Be kedi weenɛɛ ŋan taŋ nɛ te Yesu bɔɔse Simɔn Pita nyiaa, “Gyɔn bu Simɔn, n ne gyae maŋ kela n tebia kɛwɔ kɛɛ gyae maŋ?” Keŋte Pita tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Ayi, me Gbeŋgyoo, n gyeŋ nyi mɛɛ gyae n nombia.” Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Kɛɛ me silala baŋ dɔɔ ŋgba mena keŋ namensekɛɛlere ne kɛɛ namense dɔɔ nɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus asked a second time, “Simon son of John, do you love me?” Peter answered, “Yes, Lord, you know I love you!” “Then take care of my sheep,” Jesus told him.
translation_verse: Te Yesu besewɔ bɔɔse e bolate nyiaa, “Gyɔn bu Simɔn, n ne gyae me nombia?” Te Pita tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Ayi me Gbeŋgyoo, nyaŋ gbagba gyeŋ nyi mɛɛ gyae n nombia.” Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa “Kɛɛ me silala baŋ dɔɔ ŋgba mena keŋ namensekɛɛlere ne kɛɛ ɔ namense dɔɔ nɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus asked a third time, “Simon son of John, do you love me?” Peter was hurt because Jesus had asked him three times if he loved him. So he told Jesus, “Lord, you know everything. You know I love you.” Jesus replied, “Feed my sheep.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ bɔɔse e betabotoorote nyiaa, “Gyɔn bu Simɔn, n ne gyae me nombia nideli?” Nombia ŋenaŋ takaa Pita nideli nawolo nyi ɔ te bɔɔse e betabotooro nyi, “N ne gyae maŋ nideli?” Keŋte Pita yako e nyi, “Me Gbeŋgyoo, nyaŋ gbagba n gyeŋ kpene kamasɛ. N gyeŋ nyi mɛɛ gyae neŋ nideli.” Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Mena na kɛɛ me silala baŋ dɔɔ ŋgba mena keŋ namensekɛɛlere ne kɛɛ ɔ namense dɔɔ nɛ.”

english_verse: I tell you for certain that when you were a young man, you dressed yourself and went wherever you wanted to go. But when you are old, you will hold out your hands. Then others will wrap your belt around you and lead you where you don't want to go.”
translation_verse: Mɛɛ yako neŋ anokoare nyiaa, “Debaŋ keŋ n yɛɛ tobaale e nɛ nyaŋ gbagba ne tale mo n kegba do n wose, na n ka koro gyu botɔɔ keŋ n ne gyae. Mɔna n kaa ŋmɛɛ na ŋolo ne gyae la waa do neŋ kegba na waa kyaŋ neŋ n nyiŋmaa man mo neŋ gyu botɔɔ keŋ n bɛɛ gyae nyi n ke gyu ya.”

english_verse: Jesus said this to tell how Peter would die and bring honor to God. Then he said to Peter, “Follow me!” Jesus and His Favorite Disciple
translation_verse: Yesu yako nombia kɛŋa baa na kawola yeŋ keŋ Pita waa yeŋ na ka lese Wurubuarɛ yele. Yesu kekolosi nombia kɛŋa pou taŋ nɛ te ɔ yako Pita nyiaa, “Sila maŋ!”

english_verse: Peter turned and saw Jesus' favorite disciple following them. He was the same one who had sat next to Jesus at the meal and had asked, “Lord, who is going to betray you?”
translation_verse: Ba ke ta bee gyu nɛ, Pita gɛɛsewɔ kɛɛ te ɔ naawɔ ŋgba kaseele ŋon Yesu ne gyae ɔ nombia nideli nɛ silana wɔ wɔle wɔle. Mena kaseele kei yɛna ŋon ɔ be kyaa teelina Yesu debaŋ keŋ bee di be balinɔɔ weenɛɛ te ɔ be bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Me Gbeŋgyoo, amɔte ne gyae la waa lese neŋ fa yeŋ nɛ?”

english_verse: When Peter saw this disciple, he asked Jesus, “Lord, what about him?”
translation_verse: Pita kena kaseele ŋon nɛ ɔ bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, “Me Gbeŋgyoo, sena te baa yɛɛ balee kei mɔ?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “What is it to you, if I want him to live until I return? You must follow me.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Akpaa mɛɛ gyae nyi waa kyaa kelii debaŋ keŋ maa bese kɔŋ na, n woŋ faa la? Nyaŋ sila maŋ!”

english_verse: So the rumor spread among the other disciples that this disciple would not die. But Jesus did not say he would not die. He simply said, “What is it to you, if I want him to live until I return?”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ nombia kɛŋa yaaseewɔ Yesu kaseela baŋ man nyiaa, kaseele ŋon Yesu ne gyae ɔ nombia nɛ be gyae waa yeŋ ya. Mɔna Yesu te yako nyi ɔ be gyae waa yeŋ ya. Keŋ ɔ be yako yɛna nyi, “Akpaa mɛɛ gyae nyi waa kyaa kelii debaŋ keŋ maa bese kɔŋ na, n woŋ faa la?”

english_verse: This disciple is the one who told all of this. He wrote it, and we know he is telling the truth.
translation_verse: Mena kaseele kei diina nombia kɛŋa wose man adansɛɛ te ɔ ŋmarase ŋa see. Te da gyeŋ mɔ nyi wa adansɛɛ keŋ yɛɛ ampaŋ.

english_verse: Jesus did many other things. If they were all written in books, I don't suppose there would be room enough in the whole world for all the books.
translation_verse: Yesu yɛɛ abɔɔ burum ŋaale mɔ. Akpaa beŋ ŋmarase ŋa dokoloŋ dokoloŋ nɛ, tɛɛle kei dɔɔ pou bɔɔ gyae baa nyiŋ botɔɔ keŋ baa mo ŋa see ya.

english_verse: until he was taken up to heaven. But before he was taken up, he gave orders to the apostles he had chosen with the help of the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: mo kelii wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo e laŋ adido nɛ. Pɛ Wurubuarɛ ka mo Yesu gyu adido nɛ, Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon tɛɛwɔ ŋon Yesu dɔɔ wola ɔ kpilala baŋ ɔ ba lese nɛ tom keŋ baa yɛɛ.

english_verse: While he was still with them, he said: Don't leave Jerusalem yet. Wait here for the Father to give you the Holy Spirit, just as I told you he has promised to do.
translation_verse: Ba kekyaa man nɛ, wee daale ɔ faree ɔ kaseela baŋ nyi “Ɛ na lee Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man ya, mɔna ɛ kyaa botɔɔ daa kpene keŋ me kya be yako see nyi waa fa ŋon nɛ.” Me wulaa yako ŋon ka wose man nombia nyi,

english_verse: John baptized with water, but in a few days you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit. Jesus Is Taken to Heaven
translation_verse: loŋ te Gyɔn moowɔ sɔ ŋon Wurubuarɛ loŋ, mɔna weeya akalansɛɛ ŋaale wɔle na Wurubuarɛ waa mo ɔ feliŋ ŋon bo sɔ ŋon Wurubuarɛ loŋ. Yesu adido kegyu Maake

english_verse: While the apostles were still with Jesus, they asked him, “Lord, are you now going to give Israel its own king again?”
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ Yesu na ɔ kpilala baŋ be yilaa nɛ te be bɔɔse e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, mena dɔɔ nɛnɛɛ n ka besena gyoori keŋ fa Iseraetena?”

english_verse: But the Holy Spirit will come upon you and give you power. Then you will tell everyone about me in Jerusalem, in all Judea, in Samaria, and everywhere in the world.”
translation_verse: Mɔna debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa mo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do ɛ man nɛ, ɛ ke nyiŋ doŋ. Na ɛ ka bese ma adansɛɛtena Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man, na Gyudia na Samaria tɛɛlese pou dɔɔ mo kelii tɛɛle pou ɔto.”

english_verse: After Jesus had said this and while they were watching, he was taken up into a cloud. They could not see him,
translation_verse: Ɔ kekolosi kɛŋa pou taŋ na, te Wurubuarɛ mo e laŋ adido be popou siaman ka gyoo ŋɔma man. Mena dɔɔ ba te naa e bela ya.

english_verse: but as he went up, they kept looking up into the sky. Suddenly two men dressed in white clothes were standing there beside them.
translation_verse: Be ka seŋ botɔɔ be sɛɛ bɛɛ kɛɛ adido fee nɛ, karatetee na tewulɛɛ bala baale seŋɛɛ laa ba yenaŋ na ba doona abɔɔ wolomɛɛ.

english_verse: They said, “Why are you men from Galilee standing here and looking up into the sky? Jesus has been taken to heaven. But he will come back in the same way you have seen him go.” Someone To Take the Place of Judas
translation_verse: Te be bɔɔse Yesu kpilala baŋ nyi, “Galeliatena, weera te ɛ seŋɛɛ ɛ ne kɛɛ adido fee mena? Yesu kei, ŋon Wurubuarɛ ba mo e lee ɛ gyaŋ laŋ adido nɛ, mena ke te waa bese kɔŋ ŋgba mena keŋ ɛ be naa e ɔ ne gyu adido nɛ.” Ba lese Matiase nyi waa kyaa Gyudase nawɔɔ man

english_verse: The Mount of Olives was about a kilometer from Jerusalem. The apostles who had gone there were Peter, John, James, Andrew, Philip, Thomas, Bartholomew, Matthew, James the son of Alphaeus, Simon, known as the Eager One, and Judas the son of James. After the apostles returned to the city, they went upstairs to the room where they had been staying.
translation_verse: Ba ke kɔŋ kaa gyoo Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ keŋ man nɛ, te ba gyeŋawɔ gyu abansoro deni be dɛɛkyae keŋ man. Balaŋ baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ yɛna, Pita na Gyɔn na Anderiase na Filipo na Tomase na Batolomeo na Mateo na Alfeose bu Gyemisi na Simɔn ŋon ɔ ne gyae ɔ balaŋ nideli nɛ, na Gyemisi bu Gyudase.

english_verse: The apostles often met together and prayed with a single purpose in mind. The women and Mary the mother of Jesus would meet with them, and so would his brothers.
translation_verse: Be popou na ala baale na Yesu ɔ naa Mɛɛre, na Yesu ɔ naabia ne yɛɛ na nɔɔdokoloŋ yilaa fane debaŋ kamasɛ.

english_verse: of the Lord's followers meeting together, and Peter stood up to speak to them.
translation_verse: ). Botɔɔ te Pita korowɔ seŋ yako wɔ nyiaa,

english_verse: He said: My friends, long ago by the power of the Holy Spirit, David said something about Judas, and what he said has now happened. Judas was one of us and had worked with us, but he brought the mob to arrest Jesus.
translation_verse: Nafɔ na Gyudase kei kpuɛ de man, nawolo nyi Yesu lese na ŋon mɔ nyi waa kpu na daa na dɛɛ yɛɛ tom kei.

english_verse: Then Judas bought some land with the money he was given for doing that evil thing. He fell headfirst into the field. His body burst open, and all his insides came out.
translation_verse: Kɔba keŋ ŋon Gyudase be nyim lee ɔ nombiakumɛɛ kɛŋa keyɛɛ man nɛ, te ɔ moowɔ lɛɛ tɛɛle. Tɛɛle kenaŋ man te ɔ yalawɔ te ɔ tɔɔ bɛɛwɔ te ɔ loobia pou leewɔ.

english_verse: When the people of Jerusalem found out about this, they called the place Akeldama, which in the local language means “Field of Blood.”
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ pou be kyaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ nyii mena nombii kei, mena dɔɔ ba doo botɔɔ yele be dɛɛ dei man nyi Akɛledama, keŋ nawolo nyi Fatabo tɛɛle.

english_verse: In the book of Psalms it says, “Leave his house empty, and don't let anyone live there.” It also says, “Let someone else have his job.”
translation_verse: Gyoo Defidi yako Gyudase wose man nombia Wurubuarɛ Nɔɔneŋ tɔne keŋ man nyi, “Ɔ dekpaŋalaŋ dɛɛ doo yakaa keŋ ŋolo na kyaa ke man ya.” Ɔ besewɔ yako nyi, “Walaŋ wɔle waa kyaa ɔ nawɔɔ man na waa yɛɛ ɔ tom.”

english_verse: So we need someone else to help us tell others that Jesus has been raised from death. He must also be one of the men who was with us from the very beginning. He must have been with us from the time the Lord Jesus was baptized by John until the day he was taken to heaven.
translation_verse: “Mena dɔɔ kaboena nyi ŋolo waa kyaa ɔ nawɔɔ man kpu na daa, na dee di de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu kefoŋ lee yeŋ man keŋ adansɛɛ. Kaboena nyi walaŋ ŋonaŋ waa yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon na ɔ kpuɛ na daa debaŋ keŋ pou daa na de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu ne gyu dɛɛ kɔŋ, lee debaŋ keŋ Gyɔn be sɔ e Wurubuarɛ loŋ kelii debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo e lee de gyaŋ gyu adido nɛ.”

english_verse: Two men were suggested: One of them was Joseph Barsabbas, known as Justus, and the other was Matthias.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ba lese Gyosɛfo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Basabase yaa Gyustuse nɛ na Matiase.

english_verse: Then they all prayed, “Lord, you know what everyone is like! Show us the one you have chosen
translation_verse: Te be fanewɔ nyi, “De Gbeŋgyoo, nyaŋ gyeŋa na walaŋ kamasɛ konɔɔ man. Mena dɔɔ wola daa tewulɛɛ bala kɛwɔ man ŋon n ba lese nyi waa yɛɛ Yesu kpilale,

english_verse: to be an apostle and to serve in place of Judas, who got what he deserved.”
translation_verse: na waa kyaa Gyudase ŋon ɔ be tina ɔ tom yela te ɔ be lam gyu botɔɔ keŋ kaboena e nɛ nawɔɔ man.”

english_verse: They drew names, and Matthias was chosen to join the group of the eleven apostles.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ be lɔɔ boe, te boe keŋ yalawɔ Matiase yele dɔɔ. Kei nawolo nyi ŋon te Wurubuarɛ lesewɔ, te ba moo e kpu Yesu kpilala kufu daale baŋ man.

english_verse: On the day of Pentecost all the Lord's followers were together in one place.
translation_verse: Gyudatena wee dinaa keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi Pentekosite keŋ kelii nɛ, baŋ pou bɔɔ lɛɛ Kristo Yesu di nɛ ke yilaawɔ debodokoloŋ.

english_verse: Suddenly there was a noise from heaven like the sound of a mighty wind! It filled the house where they were meeting.
translation_verse: Karatetee dokoloŋ te be nyii nkaŋ daale lee adido ŋgba feliŋ dinaa daale ne gyɔɔ la nɛ, te ke kaa lɛɛ dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man be kyaa nɛ pou.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit took control of everyone, and they began speaking whatever languages the Spirit let them speak.
translation_verse: Te Wurubuarɛ moo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do be popou man, te be fiasɛɛ bɛɛ kolosi deise kpookpoo ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon be fa wɔ nɛ.

english_verse: Many religious Jews from every country in the world were living in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ na Gyudatena baŋ bee yee Wurubuarɛ te bɔɔ lee tɛɛle kei dɔɔ pou nɛ, kyaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ keŋ man.

english_verse: And when they heard this noise, a crowd gathered. But they were surprised, because they were hearing everything in their own languages.
translation_verse: Be kenyii mena nkaŋ kei nɛ, te balaŋ burum kaa yilaawɔ. Balaŋ baŋ pou wose yeŋa wɔ nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ nyiiwɔ ŋgba bee kolosi ɔ dɛɛ dei nɛ.

english_verse: They were excited and amazed, and said: Don't all these who are speaking come from Galilee?
translation_verse: Yɛɛ wɔ gyakoloŋ te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, balaŋ anekoloseŋ kɛwɔ pou te yɛɛ Galeliatenawɔ yee?

english_verse: Then why do we hear them speaking our very own languages?
translation_verse: Te weera de man walaŋ kamasɛ ne nyii bee kolosi ŋon gbagba ɔ dɛɛ dei?

english_verse: Some of us are from Parthia, Media, and Elam. Others are from Mesopotamia, Judea, Cappadocia, Pontus, Asia,
translation_verse: De popou lee tɛɛlese kpookpoo dɔɔ. De man baale lee Patia na Media na Elam te baale mɔ lee Mesopotamia na Gyudia na Kapadosia, te baale mɔ lee Pɔntuse na Esia.

english_verse: Crete, and Arabia. Some of us were born Jews, and others of us have chosen to be Jews. Yet we all hear them using our own languages to tell the wonderful things God has done.
translation_verse: Gyudatena na baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya te bɔɔ kyɛɛkee gyoo Gyudatena ɔsom man nɛ, man baale mɔ lee Roman donɔɔ man kɔŋ. De baale mɔ lee Kreti na Arebia, mɔna dee nyii bee kolosi nombia dinaana ŋan Wurubuarɛ be yɛɛ nɛ daa gbagba de deise man.

english_verse: Everyone was excited and confused. Some of them even kept asking each other, “What does all this mean?”
translation_verse: Gyakoloŋ gyoo wɔ te ba nyeeya man mɔ yɛɛ wɔ basaa te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, na nombia kɛŋa asɛɛ yɛna sena baa?

english_verse: Others made fun of the Lord's followers and said, “They are drunk.” Peter Speaks to the Crowd
translation_verse: Mɔna be man baale mɔ kulu wɔ nyi kei dɔɔ be dɛɛsee. Pita kekolosi lese nombia ŋan man wola balaŋ baŋ

english_verse: Peter stood with the eleven apostles and spoke in a loud and clear voice to the crowd: Friends and everyone else living in Jerusalem, listen carefully to what I have to say!
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Pita na ɔ tebia Yesu kpilala kufu daale baŋ korowɔ seŋ, te ɔ gyinaa ɔ woya kolosi fa balaŋ dikpii keŋ nyiaa, “Me tebia Gyudatena na ɛmɛɛ baŋ pou ɛ kyaa Gyerusalɛm nɛ, ɛ tei me gyaŋ nideli na maa wola ŋon kpene keŋ nombii kei nawolo.

english_verse: You are wrong to think that these people are drunk. After all, it is only nine o'clock in the morning.
translation_verse: Ne yɛɛ ŋon nyi balaŋ kɛwɔ dɛɛsee, mɔna na be dɛɛsee ya. Nawolo nyi nebo weese gba yaa, nɛnɛɛ te gyɔɔ debaŋ kadaale.

english_verse: But this is what God told the prophet Joel to say,
translation_verse: Mɔna nombii kei yɛna kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Gyowɛle be kolosi see nyi ke gyae ke kɔŋ nɛ.

english_verse: In those days I will give my Spirit to my servants, both men and women, and they will prophesy.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ man nɛ maa mo me feliŋ do ma alebia na me tewulɛɛ man na baa kolosi nombia see ma yele man.

english_verse: “I will work miracles in the sky above and wonders on the earth below. There will be blood and fire and clouds of smoke.
translation_verse: Maa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia adido, na maa lese me wɔɔle wola tɛɛle kei dɔɔ. Ɛ gyae ɛ kena fatabo na boalaŋ na nyɔɔse tuum.

english_verse: The sun will turn dark, and the moon will be as red as blood before the great and wonderful day of the Lord appears.
translation_verse: Weeseteŋteŋ gyae ka bese ditiŋtɛɛ tuum na gyaalaŋ ke yase ŋgba fatabo nɛ. Nombia kɛŋa pou gyae ke kɔŋ, pɛte wee dinaa keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo waa lese ɔ gyoorobiiri wola nɛ kelii.

english_verse: Then the Lord will save everyone who asks for his help.”
translation_verse: Te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon waa baake de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ yele na, waa lɛɛ ɔ nyee.” ’

english_verse: Now, listen to what I have to say about Jesus from Nazareth. God proved he sent Jesus to you by having him work miracles, wonders, and signs. All of you know this.
translation_verse: Pita gyina ɔ kekolosi keŋ dɔɔ nyiaa, Iseraetena, ɛ tei nombia kɛŋa nideli. Wurubuarɛ tɛɛwɔ Yesu Nasarɛte baale ŋon dɔɔ yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia lese ɔ wɔɔle wola ɛ man ŋgba mena keŋ ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ gyeŋ nɛ. Kɛŋa diina adansɛɛ nyi Wurubuarɛ kpila na e.

english_verse: God had already planned and decided that Jesus would be handed over to you. So you took him and had evil men put him to death on a cross.
translation_verse: Lee Wurubuarɛ gbagba kegyaebii na ɔ ka gyeŋ man te ɔ wulaa bakewɔ see nyi baa lese Yesu fa ŋon, te ɛ yela balaŋ kumɛɛ gyɔɔ e ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ ko.

english_verse: But God set him free from death and raised him to life. Death could not hold him in its power.
translation_verse: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ gyuusu e lee yeŋ man, te ɔ lese e lee yeŋ weeleŋ man, nawolo nyi yeŋ be tale kyaŋ e see ɔ gyaŋ kekpaakekpaa ya.

english_verse: What David said are really the words of Jesus, “I always see the Lord near me, and I will not be afraid with him at my right side.
translation_verse: Gyoo Defidi yakowɔ lee Yesu wose man nyi, ‘Me naa de Gbeŋgyoo ŋon me siaman debaŋ kamasɛ. Ɔ seŋɛɛ ma yenaŋ mena dɔɔ kolo be tale tɛɛse maŋ ya,

english_verse: Because of this, my heart will be glad, my words will be joyful, and I will live in hope.
translation_verse: mena dɔɔ me wɔe te fɛɛ maŋ, me sia mɔ ta gye. Me yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon maa yeŋ wee daale, mɔna me sia gyakaa abɔɔ ŋan Wurubuarɛ waa kaa fa man nɛ dɔɔ.

english_verse: The Lord won't leave me in the grave. I am his holy one, and he won't let my body decay.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi n be gyae n ke yela maa dɔɔ yebɔɔ man ya. N be gyae n ke yela n walaŋ ŋon ayimɛɛ be doo ɔ man ya nɛ ke doo yebɔɔ man dala ya.

english_verse: He has shown me the path to life, and he makes me glad by being near me.”
translation_verse: N ta wola maŋ nyeedoŋ gbɛɛ. Nawolo nyi n kpuɛ na maŋ dɔɔ te yɛɛ maŋ gyoŋ nideli.’

english_verse: My friends, it is right for me to speak to you about our ancestor David. He died and was buried, and his tomb is still here.
translation_verse: Me tebia, mɛɛ gyae maa yako ŋon kolo lee de naana Defidi wose man, ɔ yeŋawɔ te be wuu e, ɔ yebɔɔ sɛɛ doo de gyaŋ kɛbo kaa lii na gyɛŋ.

english_verse: But David was a prophet, and he knew that God had made a promise he would not break. He had told David someone from his own family would someday be king.
translation_verse: Na ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre e, te ɔ gyeŋ nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ be kaŋ ntam yako see lee ɔ wose man nyi, waa mo Defidi duuluŋ man ŋolo see gyoo na waa kyaa ɔ gyoorogbeli keŋ dɔɔ di gyoori ŋgba ŋon Defidi nɛ.

english_verse: David knew this would happen, and so he told us Christ would be raised to life. He said God would not leave him in the grave or let his body decay.
translation_verse: Gyoo Defidi naa kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa kaa yɛɛ, mena dɔɔ ɔ kolosiwɔ lee Kristo kefoŋ lee yeŋ man wose man nombia nyi, ‘Wurubuarɛ te tina e yela yebɔɔ man na waa dala ya.’

english_verse: All of us can tell you that God has raised Jesus to life!
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ te gyuusu Yesu kei lee yeŋ man, te de popou ne di adansɛɛ nyi nombii kei yɛɛ ampaŋ.

english_verse: Jesus was taken up to sit at the right side of God, and he was given the Holy Spirit, just as the Father had promised. Jesus is also the one who has given the Spirit to us, and this is what you are now seeing and hearing.
translation_verse: Ɔ kya Wurubuarɛ gyinaa e adido te ɔ moo e see ɔ dunoluŋ dɔɔ te ɔ moo ɔ feliŋ ŋon fa e ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be kaŋ ntam see nɛ. Mena feliŋ kei yaa Yesu moowɔ do de man gyɛŋ. Wurubuarɛ feliŋ kei tom yaa ɛ ne naa te ɛ ne nyii nɛ.

english_verse: David didn't go up to heaven. So he wasn't talking about himself when he said, “The Lord told my Lord to sit at his right side,
translation_verse: Gyoo Defidi gbagba te gyu adido ŋgba Yesu nɛ ya, mɔna ɔ talewɔ yako Yesu wose man nombia nyi, ‘De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ ŋon yako me Gbeŋgyoo nyi, “Kyaa me dunoluŋ dɔɔ,

english_verse: until he made my Lord's enemies into a footstool for him.”
translation_verse: kelii debaŋ keŋ maa mo baŋ bɛɛ kɔla neŋ nɛ do n nawɔɔ tɛɛ.” ’

english_verse: Everyone in Israel should then know for certain that God has made Jesus both Lord and Christ, even though you put him to death on a cross.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛmɛɛ Iseraetena pou ɛ gyeŋ nideli nyi, Yesu kei ŋon ɛ be gyɔ e nyeelabia ko ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ nɛ, ŋon ke te Wurubuarɛ moowɔ yɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo na da nyeelɛɛre ŋon ɔ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ.”

english_verse: When the people heard this, they were very upset. They asked Peter and the other apostles, “Friends, what should we do?”
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ kenyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, takaa wɔ nideli te be bɔɔse Pita na Yesu kpilala akaŋ baŋ nyiaa, “De tebia, sena yaa dɛɛ yɛɛ?”

english_verse: Peter said to them, “Turn to God and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins, and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Te Pita tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyi, “Walaŋ kamasɛ waa kyɛɛkee lee ɔ nombiakumɛɛ man, na baa sɔ e Wurubuarɛ loŋ lee Yesu Kristo yele man, na Wurubuarɛ ka mo ɔ nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ e, na waa mo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do ɔ man.

english_verse: This promise is for you and your children. It is for everyone our Lord God will choose, no matter where they live.”
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ nombii keŋ ɔ be yako see nyi waa mo ɔ feliŋ do ɛ man nɛ, yɛɛ ɛmɛɛ na ɛ bia na baŋ pou be kyaa lemlem, na baŋ pou de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ waa baake wɔ kɔŋ ŋon gbagba gyaŋ nɛ wui.”

english_verse: Peter told them many other things as well. Then he said, “I beg you to save yourselves from what will happen to all these evil people.”
translation_verse: Pita kolosiwɔ fa wɔ nideli te ɔ faree wɔ te ɔ sola wɔ nyi, “Ɛ lese ɛ wose lee tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ kumɛɛ kɛwɔ man, na Wurubuarɛ ke lɛɛ ɛ nyee.”

english_verse: They spent their time learning from the apostles, and they were like family to each other. They also broke bread and prayed together. Life among the Lord's Followers
translation_verse: Be kyaŋa Yesu kpilala baŋ kawola man nideli, te be popou ne yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ gyaŋee debaŋ kamasɛ. Bɛɛ yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ di weenɛɛ ŋan Yesu kpa baa di na baa mo tɔɔse e nɛ, te bɛɛ bese yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ gyaŋee fane.

english_verse: Everyone was amazed by the many miracles and wonders that the apostles worked.
translation_verse: Yesu Kpilala baŋ yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia burum, te gyakoloŋ gyoo balaŋ baŋ pou.

english_verse: All the Lord's followers often met together, and they shared everything they had.
translation_verse: Baŋ pou bɔɔ lɛɛ Yesu di nɛ, yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ ba kekyaa man, na bɛɛ kɛɛ ba abɔɔ ŋan be dana nɛ nyi yɛɛ be popou wui.

english_verse: They would sell their property and possessions and give the money to whoever was in need.
translation_verse: Te be yɔɔ ba abɔɔ ŋaale mɔ, na bɛɛ kpala kɔba keŋ fa walaŋ kamasɛ ŋgba mena keŋ ne hia e nɛ.

english_verse: Day after day they met together in the temple. They broke bread together in different homes and shared their food happily and freely,
translation_verse: Wee kamasɛ na, be dikpii keŋ ne gyaŋee be Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man. Bɛɛ yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ te bɛɛ mo konɔɔ kpaakpaa, na sia kagye di weenɛɛ bom ba akpaŋalaŋse man.

english_verse: while praising God. Everyone liked them, and each day the Lord added to their group others who were being saved.
translation_verse: Bɛɛ lese Wurubuarɛ yele, te balaŋ pou mɔ ne kɛlɛɛ wɔ. Te wee kamasɛ mɔ nɛ, de Gbeŋgyoo na mo balaŋ wɔlɛɛ baŋ ɔ ne lɛɛ ba nyeeya nɛ kaa kpu na wɔ.

english_verse: The time of prayer was about three o'clock in the afternoon, and Peter and John were going into the temple.
translation_verse: Wee daale balinɔɔ weese ŋgba debaŋ atoorote man nɛ, Pita na Gyɔn korowɔ gyu Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man nyi be kaa fane. Debaŋ kenaŋ yɛna kefane debaŋ.

english_verse: A man who had been born lame was being carried to the temple door. Each day he was placed beside this door, known as the Beautiful Gate. He sat there and begged from the people who were going in.
translation_verse: Na balee ŋolo kyaa keŋ ɔ yɛɛ ɔtakase e leenaŋ ɔ biatɛɛ. Wee kamasɛ nɛ bɛɛ mo e kaa see Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa denanɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi Denanɔɔ Kyeote nɛ man, na waa sola kɔba lee balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ gyoo deni kenaŋ man nɛ gyaŋ.

english_verse: The man saw Peter and John entering the temple, and he asked them for money.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ ɔ be naa nyi Pita na Gyɔn ne kɔŋ be kaa gyoo deni dinaa keŋ man nɛ, te ɔ sola wɔ nyi baa fa e kɔba.

english_verse: But they looked straight at him and said, “Look up at us!”
translation_verse: Keŋte be kɛɛ e yididi te Pita yako e nyiaa, “Kɛɛ daa.”

english_verse: The man stared at them and thought he was going to get something.
translation_verse: Te balee ŋon mɔ kɛɛ wɔ gyakaa loo nyi, waa nyiŋ kolo lee be gyaŋ.

english_verse: But Peter said, “I don't have any silver or gold! But I will give you what I do have. In the name of Jesus Christ from Nazareth, get up and start walking.”
translation_verse: Te Pita yako e nyi, “Nyi kɔba na, mɔɔ dana ŋaale maa fa neŋ ya, mɔna kpene keŋ me dana nɛ te maa fa neŋ. Lee Yesu Kristo Nasarɛte baale ŋon yele man nɛ, koro seŋ na n ke ta,”

english_verse: Peter then took him by the right hand and helped him up. At once the man's feet and ankles became strong,
translation_verse: Te Pita kyaŋa ɔ dunoluŋ nyiŋmaa man kyɔ e te ɔ korowɔ seŋ. Debokenaŋ man te ɔ doona na ɔ nawɔɔ pou yɛɛ e doŋ.

english_verse: and he jumped up and started walking. He went with Peter and John into the temple, walking and jumping and praising God.
translation_verse: Karatetee dokoloŋ te ɔ toŋawɔ seŋ ɔ nawɔɔ dɔɔ, te ɔ fiasɛɛ ɔ ne tɛɛ. Keŋte ɔ sila wɔ gyoo Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man ɔ dɔŋɛɛ na ɔ na toŋee na ɔ na lese Wurubuarɛ yele.

english_verse: They knew he was the beggar who had been lying beside the Beautiful Gate, and they were completely surprised. They could not imagine what had happened to the man. Peter Speaks in the Temple
translation_verse: Te be ka gyeŋ e nyi ŋon yɛna walaŋ ŋon nafɔ ɔ ne kyaa Denanɔɔ Kyeote keŋ man solee abɔɔ nɛ, ba wose yeŋa wɔ gbaa, te gyakoloŋ gyoo wɔ lee kpene keŋ bɔɔ naa ɔ man nɛ dɔɔ. Pita kekolosi Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man

english_verse: While the man kept holding on to Peter and John, the whole crowd ran to them in amazement at the place known as Solomon's Porch.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ kena balee ŋon mataa na Pita na Gyɔn botɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake nyi Solomɔn poto man nɛ, doo be popou nɔɔ te be yeuwɔ gyu be gyaŋ botɔɔ.

english_verse: Peter saw that a crowd had gathered, and he said: Friends, why are you surprised at what has happened? Why are you staring at us? Do you think we have some power of our own? Do you think we were able to make this man walk because we are so religious?
translation_verse: Pita kena balaŋ baŋ nɛ ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Iseraetena, weera dɔɔ te kei ne doo ɛ nɔɔ? Yaa weera dɔɔ te ɛ ne kɛɛ daa fee mena? Ɛ fɛɛ ɛ sia baa ɛ ne kɛɛ daa nyi mberem daa gbagba de doŋ na de Wurubuarɛ keyee dɔɔ te de yela balee kei tɛɛwɔ?

english_verse: The God that Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and our other ancestors worshiped has brought honor to his Servant Jesus. He is the one you betrayed. You turned against him when he was being tried by Pilate, even though Pilate wanted to set him free.
translation_verse: De naanaɔ Aberaham na Asiki na Gyekɔpo Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ de naanaɔ pou Wurubuarɛ e nɛ, lesena ɔ tobaale Yesu gyoorobiiri wola. Ɛmɛɛ moona e do Romantena kegyiise nyiŋmaa man, te ɛ bɛɛ e Pilato siaman nyi baa ko e, mɔna nafɔ ɔ yɛɛ wa agyueŋ koraŋ nyi waa tina e yela, nyi ɔ te naa dukum ɔ man ya.

english_verse: and you killed the one who leads people to life. But God raised him from death, and all of us can tell you what he has done.
translation_verse: Te ɛ koo Yesu ŋon ɔ ne fa balaŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa nɛ. Mɔna Wurubuarɛ gyuusu e lee yeŋ man keŋ de popou ne di ka wose man adansɛɛ.

english_verse: You see this man, and you know him. He put his faith in the name of Jesus and was made strong. Faith in Jesus made this man completely well while everyone was watching.
translation_verse: Mena keŋ dɔɔ lɛɛ Yesu di dɔɔ, te Wurubuarɛ yela balee aseŋaŋ ɛ siaman kei ŋon ɛ gyeŋ e nɛ, nyiŋa kawee kekyɔ. Ke yɛɛ ampaŋ nyi mena keŋ dɔɔ lɛɛ Yesu di dɔɔ, te balee kei nyiŋa wosedoŋ, ŋgba mena keŋ ɛ ne naa e nɛ.

english_verse: My friends, I am sure you and your leaders didn't know what you were doing.
translation_verse: Me tebia, ma gyeŋ nyi kpene keŋ ɛmɛɛ na ɛ siamantena be yɛɛ Yesu nɛ, ɛ ta gyem bo ya.

english_verse: But God had his prophets tell that his Messiah would suffer, and now he has kept this promise.
translation_verse: Lee gyaŋgba gyaŋgba keŋ, te Wurubuarɛ tɛɛwɔ wa akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra pou dɔɔ yako see nyi, nyeelɛɛre ŋon ɔ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ, waa naa diyem. Wurubuarɛ yele la nombia kɛŋa pou kɔŋawɔ mena.

english_verse: So turn to God! Give up your sins, and you will be forgiven.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛ kyɛɛkee lee ɛ gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man, na ɛ kekɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ, na waa gberaa ya ayimɛɛ pou lee ɛ wose man.

english_verse: Then the time will come when the Lord will give you fresh strength. He will send you Jesus, his chosen Messiah.
translation_verse: Na de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ kefa ŋon wosefɛɛreŋ na waa yela ŋon ɔ yɛɛ nyeelɛɛre ŋon ɔ be wula lese e see fa ŋon nɛ kekɔŋ. Ŋon yɛna Yesu.

english_verse: But Jesus must stay in heaven until God makes all things new, just as his holy prophets promised long ago.
translation_verse: Kaboena nyi waa kyaa adido kelii debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ wɔle, ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ be tɛɛ wa akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra dɔɔ yako see gyaŋgba keŋ nɛ.

english_verse: Moses said, “The Lord your God will choose one of your own people to be a prophet, just as he chose me. Listen to everything he tells you.
translation_verse: Na Mosesi mɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre e, ɔ yakowɔ see nyi, ‘Ɛ Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ waa lese dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre lee ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ balaŋ man fa ŋon, ŋgba maŋ Mosesi nɛ. Ɛ tei kpene kamasɛ keŋ waa yako ŋon nɛ.

english_verse: No one who disobeys this prophet will be one of God's people any longer.”
translation_verse: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ te tei kpene keŋ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre kei waa yako ya nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa lese e lee ɔ balaŋ man na waa yo.’

english_verse: You are really the ones God told his prophets to speak to. And you were given the promise God made to your ancestors. He said to Abraham, “All nations on earth will be blessed because of someone from your family.”
translation_verse: Nombia ŋan pou Wurubuarɛ be tɛɛ wa akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra dɔɔ yako see nɛ yɛɛ ɛ wui, te ɛmɛɛ mɔ kpuɛ kebakesee keŋ Wurubuarɛ be bake na ɛ naanaɔ nɛ man. Nawolo nyi ɔ yako Aberaham nyi, ‘N duuluŋ man te maa ta hiraa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ pou.’

english_verse: God sent his chosen Son to you first, because God wanted to bless you and make each one of you turn away from your sins.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba lese ɔ tobaale Yesu nɛ, ɔ yela ɔ taŋgbɛɛwɔ kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ nyi ɔ kaa hiraa ŋon, na waa yela ɛ man walaŋ kamasɛ kekyɛɛkee lee ɔ gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man.”

english_verse: These men were angry because the apostles were teaching the people that the dead would be raised from death, just as Jesus had been raised from death.
translation_verse: Ba gyeŋa baŋ nideli nawolo nyi, Pita na Gyɔn na wola balaŋ baŋ nyi, Yesu te fom lee yeŋ man, keŋ nawolo nyi balaŋ pou baa foŋ lee yeŋ man bela.

english_verse: It was already late in the afternoon, and they arrested Peter and John and put them in jail for the night.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ be kyaŋa Pita na Gyɔn ka do deni man, na tɛɛ te lam dɔɔ, be yela be doowɔ kelii tɛɛ kena.

english_verse: The next morning the leaders, the elders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses met in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Tɛɛ kena keŋ nɛ, Gyudatena kegyiise na be siamantena na ba mmaraa wolala baŋ gyaŋeewɔ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man.

english_verse: The high priest Annas was there, as well as Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and other members of the high priest's family.
translation_verse: Be gyaŋeewɔ na saese pou kegyia Anase na Kaifase na Gyɔn na Alɛkesanda na baala baale baŋ bɔɔ lee saese pou kegyia ŋon dekpaŋalaŋ man nɛ.

english_verse: They brought in Peter and John and made them stand in the middle while they questioned them. They asked, “By what power and in whose name have you done this?”
translation_verse: Be yela ba moo Pita na Gyɔn kaa seŋ be siaman te be bɔɔse wɔ nyi, “Woŋ wɔɔle yaa amɔte yele man yaa ɛ kyɔɔ balee ŋon?”

english_verse: Peter was filled with the Holy Spirit and told the nation's leaders and the elders:
translation_verse: Te Pita ŋon Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon doo ɔ man nideli nɛ, tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyi, “Kegyiise na siamantena,

english_verse: You are questioning us today about a kind deed in which a man who could not walk was healed.
translation_verse: akpaa gyɛŋ ɛ te baake daa kaa seŋ ɛ siaman ɛ ne bɔɔse daa lee kolo kpaakpaa keŋ dɔɔ yɛɛ, te ɔtakase ŋon be nyiŋ doŋ koro seŋ nɛ dɔɔ na,

english_verse: But there is something we must tell you and everyone else in Israel. This man is standing here completely well because of the power of Jesus Christ from Nazareth. You put Jesus to death on a cross, but God raised him to life.
translation_verse: kenaŋ na mɛɛ gyae ɛmɛɛ na Iseraetena pou ka gyeŋ nyi, Yesu Kristo ŋon ɔ ba lee Nasarɛte nɛ yele man yaa balee aseŋaŋ ɛ siaman kei nyiŋa doŋ nɛ. Ŋon te ɛ gyɔɔwɔ sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ ko, te Wurubuarɛ gyuusu e lee yeŋ man.

english_verse: He is the stone you builders thought was worthless, and now he is the most important stone of all.
translation_verse: Yesu yɛna walaŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ nombia tɔne be yako nyi, ‘Boe keŋ ɛmɛɛ deni maara baŋ be bɛɛ ke nɛ, keŋ kaa bese na deni mokɔɔ kasee boe keŋ ne yeli deni keŋ ne yɛɛ doŋ nideli.’

english_verse: Only Jesus has the power to save! His name is the only one in all the world that can save anyone.
translation_verse: Yesu waageŋ kooŋ yɛna nyeelɛɛre, ŋon waageŋ kooŋ te Wurubuarɛ lesewɔ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nyi de baake ɔ yele na waa lɛɛ da nyee.”

english_verse: The officials were amazed to see how brave Peter and John were, and they knew that these two apostles were only ordinary men and not well educated. The officials were certain these men had been with Jesus.
translation_verse: Mena keŋ Pita na Gyɔn be kolosi nideli te ba te yee gyakoloŋ ya, te kegyiise baŋ kenyii nyi be yɛɛ balaŋ yakaa nyibaŋwɔ keŋ be te gyu sukuu ya nɛ, ba wose yeŋa wɔ. Botɔɔ te be naawɔ nyi baŋ na Yesu tɛɛ la.

english_verse: But they could not deny what had happened. The man who had been healed was standing there with the apostles.
translation_verse: Mɔna ba kena balee ŋon bɔɔ kyɔ e seŋɛɛ Pita na Gyɔn gyaŋ botɔɔ nɛ, ba te tale nyiŋ nombiikum daale yako lee ba wose man ya.

english_verse: The officials commanded them to leave the council room. Then the officials said to each other,
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ be yako wɔ nyi baa lee nombia dedii deni keŋ man, botɔɔ te kegyiise baŋ moo ba nyeeya baŋ na dɔŋa.

english_verse: “What can we do with these men? Everyone in Jerusalem knows about this miracle, and we cannot say it didn't happen.
translation_verse: Bɛɛ bɔɔse dɔŋa nyi, “Woŋ yaa dɛɛ yɛɛ balaŋ kɛwɔ? Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, gyeŋ nyi baŋ yɛɛna gyakoloŋ nombii kei, te dɔɔ gyae dɛɛ tale lɛɛ ka aŋmaareŋ ya.

english_verse: But to keep this thing from spreading, we will warn them never again to speak to anyone about the name of Jesus.”
translation_verse: Mena keŋ dɔɔ ne gyae nyi baa mo nombia kɛŋa kpeŋkpeleŋ dɔɔ ya nɛ, ɛ yela dɛɛ tɔ wɔ nideli nyi be na tekaa kolosi Yesu kei nombia fa walaŋ na walaŋ ya.”

english_verse: So they called the two apostles back in and told them they must never, for any reason, teach anything about the name of Jesus.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ be baake wɔ gyoo be gyaŋ bela, te be tɔɔ wɔ nyi be na tekaa kolosi Yesu nombia, yaa baa wola balaŋ kolo lee ɔ yele man korakora bela ya.

english_verse: Peter and John answered, “Do you think God wants us to obey you or to obey him?
translation_verse: Keŋte Pita na Gyɔn tiranɔɔ nyi, “Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ kɛɛ nombia kɛŋa man nideli nyi woŋti dei la Wurubuarɛ siaman? Dee nyii ɛ wui yaa dee nyii Wurubuarɛ wui?

english_verse: We cannot keep quiet about what we have seen and heard.”
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi daa dɔɔ, dɔɔ tale tina kpene keŋ dɔɔ naa, na keŋ dɔɔ nyii nɛ kekolosi yela ya.”

english_verse: As soon as Peter and John had been set free, they went back and told the others everything the chief priests and the leaders had said to them.
translation_verse: Be ke tina Pita na Gyɔn yela nɛ, te ba besewɔ gyu be tebia gyaŋ keyako wɔ kpene kamasɛ keŋ saese kegyiise na donɔɔ kegyiise baŋ be yako wɔ nɛ.

english_verse: When the rest of the Lord's followers heard this, they prayed together and said: Master, you created heaven and earth, the sea, and everything in them.
translation_verse: Kristotena baŋ kenyii nombia ŋenaŋ nɛ, be popou moo nɔɔdokoloŋ fane fa Wurubuarɛ nyi, “De Gbeŋgyoo, nyaŋ ŋon n be yɛɛ adido na ateta, na ɛpo na kpene kamasɛ keŋ doo ŋe man.

english_verse: And by the Holy Spirit you spoke to our ancestor David. He was your servant, and you told him to say: “Why are all the Gentiles so furious? Why do people make foolish plans?
translation_verse: Nyaŋ tɛɛ la n feliŋ ŋon dɔɔ te n yela de naana Defidi ŋon ɔ yɛɛ n tobaale e nɛ yakowɔ nyi, ‘Weera dɔɔ te tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ na gyeŋee baŋse, te bɛɛ bake nombiakumɛɛ yakaa yakaa, mɔna ŋe bee dee ya nɛ?

english_verse: The kings of earth prepare for war, and the rulers join together against the Lord and his Messiah.”
translation_verse: Tɛɛle kei dɔɔ gyooneŋ ne desina ba wose fa yoo, te siamantena baŋ mɔ na koro seŋ tia de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ na nyeelɛɛre ŋon ɔ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ.’

english_verse: Here in Jerusalem, Herod and Pontius Pilate got together with the Gentiles and the people of Israel. Then they turned against your holy Servant Jesus, your chosen Messiah.
translation_verse: Ampaŋ te gyoo Herode na Pɔntiɔse Pilato na Iseraetena na baŋ mɔ ba te yɛɛ Iseraetenawɔ ya nɛ, gyaŋeewɔ donɔɔ kei man te be bakewɔ tia n tobaale Yesu ŋon ayimɛɛ be doo ɔ wose man ya, te nyaŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo e yɛɛ nyeelɛɛre na gyoo nɛ.

english_verse: They did what you in your power and wisdom had already decided would happen.
translation_verse: Be gyaŋeewɔ te be yɛɛ kpene keŋ nyaŋ Wurubuarɛ n be wula yako see nyi gyae kekɔŋ nɛ, lee n doŋ na n kegyaebii man.

english_verse: Lord, listen to their threats! We are your servants. So make us brave enough to speak your message.
translation_verse: Nɛnɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ, kɛɛ mena keŋ bɛɛ ŋmaase daa na n ka do daa n tewulɛɛ kakyeŋ na dɛɛ tale gyɔ de konɔɔ kolosi n nombia ŋan.

english_verse: Show your mighty power, as we heal people and work miracles and wonders in the name of your holy Servant Jesus.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ koro seŋ na n kekyɔ kaweese, na n ke ta de dɔɔ yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia lese n wɔɔle wola lee n tobaale Yesu ŋon ayimɛɛ be doo ɔ wose man ya nɛ yele man.”

english_verse: After they had prayed, the meeting place shook. They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and bravely spoke God's message. Sharing Possessions
translation_verse: Ba ke fane taŋ keŋ nɛ, te botɔɔ keŋ bɔɔ gyaŋee nɛ wosiwɔ te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon kaa gyoowɔ be popou man nideli te be gyɔɔ be konɔɔ kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan. Kristotena baŋ nɔɔdokoloŋ keyɛɛ

english_verse: The group of followers all felt the same way about everything. None of them claimed that their possessions were their own, and they shared everything they had with each other.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ lɛɛ Kristo Yesu di nɛ, kyaawɔ nɔɔdokoloŋ man te ba agyueŋ mɔ yɛɛ dokoloŋ. Ŋolo bɛɛ yako nyi ɔ kolo yɛɛ waageŋ wui ya, mɔna be kpɛlɛɛ ba abɔɔ pou fa dɔŋa.

english_verse: In a powerful way the apostles told everyone that the Lord Jesus was now alive. God greatly blessed his followers,
translation_verse: Yesu kpilala baŋ moo Wurubuarɛ doŋ dinaa keŋ di adansɛɛ nyi, de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu te fom lee yeŋ man, te Wurubuarɛ moo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele baŋ pou bɔɔ lɛɛ Yesu di nɛ.

english_verse: and no one went in need of anything. Everyone who owned land or houses would sell them and bring the money
translation_verse: Kolo te hia be man ŋolo ya, nawolo nyi baŋ be dana denɛɛ na tɛɛlese nɛ yɔɔ ŋa na bɛɛ mo kɔba keŋ bɔɔ nyim nɛ,

english_verse: to the apostles. Then they would give the money to anyone in need.
translation_verse: kɔna Yesu kpilala baŋ na be ta mo kpala fa wɔ. Bɛɛ fa walaŋ kamasɛ ŋgba mena keŋ ne hia e nɛ.

english_verse: Joseph was one of the followers who had sold a piece of property and brought the money to the apostles. He was a Levite from Cyprus, and the apostles called him Barnabas, which means “one who encourages others.”
translation_verse: Ŋon mɔ ka moo ɔ tɛɛle keŋ ɔ dana nɛ yɔɔ, te ɔ moo kɔba keŋ ɔ be nyim nɛ kaa fa Yesu kpilala baŋ.

english_verse: But they agreed to cheat and keep some of the money for themselves. So when Ananias took the rest of the money to the apostles,
translation_verse: Ŋon na ɔ wɛɛle yɛɛ nɔɔ te ba moo kɔba keŋ bɔɔ nyim nɛ man ŋaale weese, te baale ŋon moo akaŋ keŋ gyu kefa Yesu kpilala baŋ.

english_verse: Peter said, “Why has Satan made you keep back some of the money from the sale of the property? Why have you lied to the Holy Spirit?
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Pita bɔɔse e nyi, “Ananiase, weera dɔɔ te n yela ɔbɔnsam dii n dɔɔ mena, te ɔ yela n dii dekara fa Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon, te n moo n kɔba keŋ n be nyim lee agyapadɛɛ keŋ n be yɔɔ nɛ man ŋaale weese?

english_verse: The property was yours before you sold it, and even after you sold it, the money was still yours. What made you do such a thing? You didn't lie to people. You lied to God!”
translation_verse: Agyapadɛɛ keŋ te yɛɛ n wui pɛte n yɔɔ ke yee? N yɔɔ ke taŋ mɔna kɔba keŋ n be nyim nɛ te yɛɛ n wui yee? Nɛnɛɛ weera n bakewɔ n konɔɔ man te n yɛɛ nombiikum kei mena? Na denibalaŋ te n dii dekara fa ya, Wurubuarɛ te n dii dekara fa.”

english_verse: As soon as Ananias heard this, he dropped dead, and everyone who heard about it was frightened.
translation_verse: Ananiase ka betaa nyii nombia kɛŋa pɛ, te kara e lɔ te ɔ yeŋawɔ. Mena dɔɔ gyakoloŋ gyoo baŋ pou bɔɔ nyii nombia ŋenaŋ nɛ.

english_verse: Some young men came in and wrapped up his body. Then they took it out and buried it.
translation_verse: Keŋte tewulɛɛ kɔŋawɔ kaa mo kolo milii e, te be gate e lee gyu kewuu.

english_verse: Three hours later Sapphira came in, but she did not know what had happened to her husband.
translation_verse: Te kyare ya te ɔ wɛɛle ŋon mɔ kaa gyoowɔ botɔɔ mɔna ŋon ba gyeŋ kpene keŋ be kɔŋ ya.

english_verse: Peter asked her, “Tell me, did you sell the property for this amount?” “Yes,” she answered, “that's the amount.”
translation_verse: Te Pita bɔɔse alo ŋon nyiaa, “Yako maŋ, kɔba keŋ ɛmɛɛ na n baale Ananiase be nyim lee agyapadɛɛ keŋ keyɔɔ man pou yaa ɔ moowɔ kɔŋ nɛ?” Te ɔ tiranɔɔ nyi ayi, mena te de yɔɔ ke.

english_verse: Then Peter said, “Why did the two of you agree to test the Lord's Spirit? The men who buried Ananias are by the door, and they will carry you out!”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Pita bɔɔse e nyi, “Weera te ɛmɛɛ na n baale yɛɛ nɔɔ nyi ɛ kekɛɛse de Gbeŋgyoo feliŋ ŋon kɛɛ! Kɛɛ, balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ moo n baale kewuu nɛ, baŋ seŋɛɛ la denanɔɔ man nɛ, baa seele nyaŋ mɔ lee.”

english_verse: At once she fell at Peter's feet and died. When the young men came back in, they found Sapphira lying there dead. So they carried her out and buried her beside her husband.
translation_verse: Karatetee dokoloŋ te kara ŋon mɔ lɔ Pita nawɔɔ tɛɛ te ɔ yeŋawɔ. Tewulɛɛ baŋ kekɔŋ kaa gyoo te ba kena nyi ŋon mɔ ta yem nɛ, te ba mo e lee gyu kewuu ɔ baale yenaŋ.

english_verse: The church members were afraid, and so was everyone else who heard what had happened. Peter's Unusual Power
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ gyakoloŋ dinaa daale gyoo Kristotena dikpii keŋ pou, na baŋ pou bɔɔ nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ. Kristo kpilala baŋ kekyɔ kaweesetena

english_verse: The apostles worked many miracles and wonders among the people. All of the Lord's followers often met in the part of the temple known as Solomon's Porch.
translation_verse: Yesu kpilala baŋ yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia na nombia dinaana burum balaŋ baŋ man. Te baŋ pou bɔɔ lɛɛ Yesu Kristo di nɛ, ne gyu ke yilaa Solomɔn poto keŋ man.

english_verse: No one outside their group dared join them, even though everyone liked them very much.
translation_verse: Na balaŋ ne bu wɔ nideli, mɔna balaŋ yenaasetena baŋ yee mena keŋ baa kaa kpu na wɔ.

english_verse: Many men and women started having faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Mɔna ala na baala burum lɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu di, te Wurubuarɛ moo wɔ kaa kpu na Kristotena baŋ.

english_verse: Then sick people were brought out to the road and placed on mats. It was hoped that Peter would walk by, and his shadow would fall on them and heal them.
translation_verse: Lee gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan Yesu kpilala baŋ ne yɛɛ dɔɔ, balaŋ na mo be kaweesetena kaa doosi afoŋoŋse na mpase dɔɔ gbɛɛnaa nɔɔman, na akpaa Pita de kɔŋ ɔ ne lam na ɔ wɔŋtaa ke yala be man baale dɔɔ, na baa nyiŋ kawee kekyɔ.

english_verse: A lot of people living in the towns near Jerusalem brought those who were sick or troubled by evil spirits, and they were all healed. Trouble for the Apostles
translation_verse: Balaŋ lee donɔɔse ŋan benaa na Gyerusalɛm nɛ kɔŋ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ keŋ man, te ba moo be kaweesetena na baŋ feliŋkumɛɛ ne naase wɔ diyem nɛ kɔŋ te be kyɔɔ wɔ pou. Kristotena baŋ diyem kena

english_verse: They arrested the apostles and put them in the city jail.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ be kyaŋa Yesu kpilala baŋ te ba moo wɔ ketɔ deni.

english_verse: But that night an angel from the Lord opened the doors of the jail and led the apostles out. The angel said,
translation_verse: Mɔna ke nelim nɛ, de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ kpilale kɔŋawɔ kaa toro deni keŋ fa wɔ, te ɔ moo wɔ lee deni keŋ man, te ɔ yako wɔ nyi,

english_verse: “Go to the temple and tell the people everything about this new life.”
translation_verse: “Ɛ gyu ka seŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man na ɛ kekolosi nyeedoŋ wɔle kei wose man nombia fa balaŋ baŋ pou.”

english_verse: So they went into the temple before sunrise and started teaching. The high priest and his men called together their council, which included all of Israel's leaders. Then they ordered the apostles to be brought to them from the jail.
translation_verse: Yesu Kristo kpilala baŋ yɛɛwɔ ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon be yako nɛ, mena dɔɔ nebo nebo fuu te be gyuuwɔ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man, te be fiasɛɛ bɛɛ wola balaŋ baŋ. Wurubuarɛ Sae kegyia ŋon na ɔ kegyiise baake Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ pou, te be gyaŋeewɔ te be kpilawɔ nyi be ka mo Yesu kpilala baŋ lee deni keŋ man kɔŋ.

english_verse: The temple police who were sent to the jail did not find the apostles. They returned and said,
translation_verse: Mɔna balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ kpili nɛ gyuuwɔ kelii botɔɔ na, ba te naa Yesu kpilala baŋ deni keŋ man ya. Mena dɔɔ ba besewɔ kɔŋ kaa yako kegyiise baŋ nyi,

english_verse: “We found the jail locked tight and the guards standing at the doors. But when we opened the doors and went in, we didn't find anyone there.”
translation_verse: “De gyuu botɔɔ keŋ bɔɔ kyaŋ wɔ do nɛ na disim keŋ tɔɛ kiiŋ, na baŋ mɔ bee gyee ke nɛ seŋɛɛ be daseŋae man. Mɔna da toro disim keŋ na, da te naa Yesu kpilala baŋ deni keŋ man ya.”

english_verse: The captain of the temple police and the chief priests listened to their report, but they did not know what to think about it.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ kegyia ŋon ɔ ne kɛɛ balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ gyee Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ dɔɔ nɛ, na saese kegyiise baŋ be nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, ta seŋ wɔ nideli ya. Te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyi, “Na woŋ yɛna tewulɛɛ kɛwɔ baa?”

english_verse: Just then someone came in and said, “Now those men you put in jail are in the temple, teaching the people!”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te balee ŋolo kɔŋawɔ kaa yako wɔ nyi, “Ɛ nyii, balaŋ baŋ ɛ be kyam do deni man nɛ, seŋɛɛ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man bɛɛ wola balaŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia.”

english_verse: The captain went with some of the temple police and brought the apostles back. But they did not use force. They were afraid that the people might start throwing stones at them.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ kegyia ŋon ɔ ne kɛɛ balaŋ baŋ bee gyee Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ dɔɔ nɛ, na ɔ tewulɛɛ gyuuwɔ ka moo wɔ kɔŋ kegyiise baŋ gyaŋ, mɔna be ta mo wɔ doŋ man ya, nawolo nyi bee yee nyi balaŋ baŋ baa fuŋii wɔ boya.

english_verse: When the apostles were brought before the council, the high priest said to them,
translation_verse: Be ka mo wɔ kɔŋ nombiadiira baŋ siaman nɛ, te sae kegyia ŋon yako wɔ

english_verse: “We told you plainly not to teach in the name of Jesus. But look what you have done! You have been teaching all over Jerusalem, and you are trying to blame us for his death.”
translation_verse: nyi, “De faree ŋon nyi ɛ na wola kolo lee Yesu yele man ya, mɔna ɛ kɛɛ ya aneyɛɛŋ, ɛ ta mo ɔ nombia ŋan ɛ na wolo nɛ wulu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ pou, te ɛ ne gyae ɛ ka mo ɔ yeŋ seele daa.”

english_verse: Peter and the apostles replied: We don't obey people. We obey God.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Pita na Yesu kpilala akaŋ baŋ tiranɔɔ nyi, “Kaboena nyi dee nyii Wurubuarɛ dei kela balaŋ wui.”

english_verse: You killed Jesus by nailing him to a cross. But the God our ancestors worshiped raised him to life
translation_verse: De naanaɔ Wurubuarɛ ŋon gyuusu na Yesu ŋon ɛ be gyɔɔ e sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ ko e nɛ lee yeŋ man.

english_verse: and made him our Leader and Savior. Then God gave him a place at his right side, so that the people of Israel would turn back to him and be forgiven.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ gyina e adido te ɔ mo e see ɔ dunoluŋ dɔɔ, te ɔ moo e yɛɛ siamante na nyeelɛɛre, na waa yela Iseraetena ke nyiŋ gbɛɛ kyɛɛkee lee be nombiakumɛɛ man na baa kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ, na waa mo be nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ.

english_verse: We are here to tell you about all this, and so is the Holy Spirit, who is God's gift to everyone who obeys God.
translation_verse: Daa ne diina nombia kɛŋa adansɛɛ. Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ɔ ba mo do baŋ bee nyii ɔ dei man nɛ, mɔ ne di nombia kɛŋa adansɛɛ.

english_verse: When the council members heard this, they became so angry they wanted to kill the apostles.
translation_verse: Kegyiise baŋ bɔɔ gyaŋee nɛ nyii nombia kɛŋa na ba gyeŋa baŋ nideli, mena dɔɔ be gyaewɔ nyi baa ko Yesu kpilala baŋ.

english_verse: But one of the members was the Pharisee Gamaliel, a highly respected teacher. He ordered the apostles to be taken out of the room for a little while.
translation_verse: Mɔna be man ŋolo, ŋon ɔ doo Farasiitena dikpii keŋ man te bɛɛ baake e nyi Gamaleli, te ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ mmaraa keŋ wolale e, te ɔ wose man dana obuo donɔɔ keŋ man nɛ, korowɔ seŋ be man. Botɔɔ te ɔ yela ba lese Yesu kpilala baŋ yenaŋ.

english_verse: Then he said to the council: Men of Israel, be careful what you do with these men.
translation_verse: Te ɔ yako kegyiise baŋ nyi, “Me tebia Iseraetena, ɛ kɛɛ kpene keŋ ɛ ne gyae ɛ ke yɛɛ balaŋ kɛwɔ nɛ nideli.

english_verse: men joined him. But he was killed, and all his followers were scattered. That was the end of that.
translation_verse: ) korowɔ sila e. Mɔna be ka ko e na ɔ balaŋ baŋ pou mɔ yaaseewɔ, keŋte ɔ kawola keŋ pou mɔ kaa yɛɛwɔ yakaa.

english_verse: Later, when the people of our nation were being counted, Judas from Galilee showed up. A lot of people followed him, but he was killed, and all his followers were scattered.
translation_verse: Kei mɔ wɔle debaŋ keŋ bɛɛ kala balaŋ nɛ, te Galelia baale Gyuda korowɔ seŋ, te ŋon mɔ kaa beo balaŋ burum sila ɔ wose. Be ka ko ŋon mɔ nɛ, ɔ balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ sila e nɛ yaaseewɔ.

english_verse: So I advise you to stay away from these men. Leave them alone. If what they are planning is something of their own doing, it will fail.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ nombia kɛŋa man nɛ, mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi ɛ na yɛɛ balaŋ kɛwɔ kolo ya. Ɛ yela baa ta, nawolo nyi akpaa kpene keŋ bɛɛ wolo te bɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ, lee deniwalaŋ gyaŋ na, keŋ mɔ gyae ke yɛɛ yakaa bo mena.

english_verse: But if God is behind it, you cannot stop it anyway, unless you want to fight against God. The council members agreed with what he said,
translation_verse: Mɔna akpaa ka lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ na, ɛ be gyae ɛ ke tale tɔ wɔ gbɛɛ ya. Ɛ kena bo nyi ɛ ne yoo bo na Wurubuarɛ.” Mena dɔɔ kegyiise baŋ mɔ yɛɛwɔ ŋgba mena keŋ Gamaleli be yako wɔ nɛ.

english_verse: and they called the apostles back in. They had them beaten with a whip and warned them not to speak in the name of Jesus. Then they let them go.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ be baake Yesu kpilala baŋ kɔŋ te be yela be tuku wɔ, te be tɔɔ wɔ nyi be na kolosi kolo lee Yesu yele man bela ya. Te be tina wɔ yela.

english_verse: The apostles left the council and were happy, because God had considered them worthy to suffer for the sake of Jesus.
translation_verse: Yesu kpilala baŋ ka lee kegyiise baŋ gyaŋ nɛ na be sia ta gye, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ te kɛɛ wɔ nyi baŋ mɔ kaboena nyi baa do wɔ desɛŋ lee Yesu dɔɔ.

english_verse: Every day they spent time in the temple and in one home after another. They never stopped teaching and telling the good news that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_verse: Te wee kamasɛ na, bee gyu Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa man na balaŋ akpaŋalaŋse man. Bɛɛ wola nombia kpaakpaa ŋan nyi Yesu yɛna Kristo, nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ. Te be te tina nombia kɛŋa kawola yela ya.

english_verse: A lot of people were now becoming followers of the Lord. But some of the ones who spoke Greek started complaining about the ones who spoke Aramaic. They complained that the Greek-speaking widows were not given their share when the food supplies were handed out each day.
translation_verse: Weeya ala ŋenaŋ man na baŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ Yesu di nɛ korowɔ bee wuku te kekolosi kaa gyoo Gyudatena baŋ bɛɛ kolosi Giriki dei, na Gyudatena akaŋ baŋ man. Gyudatena baŋ bɛɛ kolosi Giriki dei nɛ, kpa Gyudatena akaŋ baŋ ne wuritii wɔ weenɛɛ kekpala man, nawolo nyi be kpa wee kamasɛ weenɛɛ ŋan bɛɛ kpɛlɛɛ nɛ, bee tuŋ baŋ wui liala baŋ ya.

english_verse: The twelve apostles called the whole group of followers together and said, “We should not give up preaching God's message in order to serve at tables.
translation_verse: Keŋte Yesu kpilala kufu bala baŋ baake baŋ pou bɔɔ lɛɛ Yesu di nɛ gyaŋee, te be yako wɔ nyiaa, “Tekaboena nyi daa dɛɛ bese tina Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan keyako yela, na dɛɛ kɔŋ kaa kɛɛ abɔɔ kekpɛlɛɛ dɔɔ bela ya.

english_verse: My friends, choose seven men who are respected and wise and filled with God's Spirit. We will put them in charge of these things.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ de tebia, ɛ kɛɛ ɛ man na ɛ ka lese baala nyetooro baŋ ba wose man dana obuo, te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon doo be man nideli te be sia be kare mɔ nɛ, na dɛɛ mo mena tom kei do be nyiŋmaa man.

english_verse: We can spend our time praying and serving God by preaching.”
translation_verse: Na daa ke nyiŋ mo de doŋ pou do kefane, na Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan keyako man.”

english_verse: This suggestion pleased everyone, and they began by choosing Stephen. He had great faith and was filled with the Holy Spirit. Then they chose Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and also Nicolaus, who worshiped with the Jewish people in Antioch.
translation_verse: Nombia naŋ Yesu kaseela baŋ be yako nɛ, seŋa balaŋ baŋ pou nideli. Te ba lese Sitifin ŋon ɔ ne lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di nideli te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ doo ɔ man nideli nɛ. Te ba besewɔ lese Filipo na Prokoro na Nikanɔ na Timɔn na Pamena na Nikolase ŋon ɔ ba lee Antiɔke te ɔ ba mo ɔ wose kpu Gyuda ɔsom keŋ man nɛ.

english_verse: These men were brought to the apostles. Then the apostles prayed and placed their hands on the men to show they had been chosen to do this work.
translation_verse: Keŋte ba moo balaŋ nyetooro kɛwɔ kaa seŋ Yesu kpilala baŋ siaman. Te ba moo be nyiŋmaase gyakaa be dɔɔ te be fanewɔ fa wɔ.

english_verse: God's message spread, and many more people in Jerusalem became followers. Even a large number of priests put their faith in the Lord. Stephen Is Arrested
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan yaaseewɔ bileŋbileŋ, te baŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ Yesu di nɛ wukuwɔ burum Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ keŋ man. Te Wurubuarɛ saese burum baale mɔ lɛɛ Yesu di. Ba kekyaŋ Sitifin

english_verse: God gave Stephen the power to work great miracles and wonders among the people.
translation_verse: Te Sitifin ŋon Wurubuarɛ ba mo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele e nideli, te ɔ doŋ doo ɔ man nɛ yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia na nombia dinaana balaŋ baŋ man.

english_verse: But some men from Cyrene and Alexandria were members of a group who called themselves “Free Men.” They started arguing with Stephen. Some others from Cilicia and Asia also argued with him.
translation_verse: Mɔna Gyudatena baale baŋ bɛɛ gyaŋee Gyudatena ɔsom deni keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi gbeŋa baŋ bɔɔ tina wɔ yela nɛ ɔsom deni man nɛ, fiasɛɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ aŋmaareŋ na Sitifin. Balaŋ benaŋ baale lee Kirene na Alɛkesanderia na Silisia na Esia tɛɛlese man.

english_verse: But they were no match for Stephen, who spoke with the great wisdom that the Spirit gave him.
translation_verse: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon faa Sitifin nyansa te ɔ kolosiwɔ nideli mena dɔɔ be te nyiŋ keŋ baa yako bela ya.

english_verse: So they talked some men into saying, “We heard Stephen say terrible things against Moses and God!”
translation_verse: Keŋ dɔɔ be gyuuwɔ ke feefu do balaŋ baale nyi baa di dekara gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ nyi, “De nyiiwɔ ɔ ne kolosi mmusuo nombia gyakaa de naana Mosesi na Wurubuarɛ dɔɔ.”

english_verse: They turned the people and their leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses against Stephen. Then they all grabbed him and dragged him in front of the council.
translation_verse: Nnɛ te be wɔlɛɛ e fa balaŋ baŋ na ɔsom kegyiise na mmaraa wolala baŋ pou, te ba korowɔ seŋ ɔ dɔɔ te ba kyaŋa e gyu donɔɔ kegyiise siaman.

english_verse: Some men agreed to tell lies about Stephen, and they said, “This man keeps on saying terrible things about this holy temple and the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ be gyae balaŋ baale kɔŋ, te be kaa dii akɛrɛɛ gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ nyi, “Debaŋ kamasɛ na balee kei ne kolosi bo tia de Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ, na Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan Mosesi ba wola daa nɛ.”

english_verse: We have heard him claim that Jesus from Nazareth will destroy this place and change the customs Moses gave us.”
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi de nyiiwɔ ŋgba ɔ ne yako nyi, “Yesu ŋon ɔ ba lee Nasarɛte nɛ, waa yɛlɛɛ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa kei lɔ, na waa kyɛɛkee abɔɔ ŋan pou Mosesi ba mo see daa nɛ.”

english_verse: Then all the council members stared at Stephen. They saw that his face looked like the face of an angel.
translation_verse: Te nombiadiira baŋ be kyaa deni keŋ man nɛ, kɛɛ Sitifin nɛ yididi te be naawɔ ŋgba ɔ sia gbɛɛ te kyɛɛkee yɛɛ ŋgba Wurubuarɛ kpilale sia gbɛɛ nɛ.

english_verse: The high priest asked Stephen, “Are they telling the truth about you?”
translation_verse: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ sae kegyia ŋon bɔɔse Sitifin nyi, “Mena dɔɔ nombia ba ayakoŋ kɛŋa pou yɛɛ ampaŋ?”

english_verse: Stephen answered: Friends, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he had moved to Haran.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Sitifin tiranɔɔ nyi, “Me tebia na me kyaɔ, ɛ tei me gyaŋ. Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ ne di gyoori nɛ lese na ɔ wose wola de naana Aberaham, debaŋ keŋ ɔ kyaa Mesopotamia tɛɛle man nɛ, kenaŋ wɔle pɛte ɔ korowɔ gyu kekyaa Haran donɔɔ man,

english_verse: God told him, “Leave your country and your relatives and go to a land that I will show you.”
translation_verse: botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ yako e nyi, ‘Koro lee n tɛɛle man na n loŋtona gyaŋ, na n ke tu gyu ke kyaa tɛɛle keŋ maa wola neŋ nɛ dɔɔ.’

english_verse: Then Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After his father died, Abraham came and settled in this land where you now live.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Aberaham korowɔ lee Kaldea tɛɛle man, te ɔ gyuuwɔ kekyaa Haran donɔɔ man. Ɔ kya yeŋ wɔle nɛ, Wurubuarɛ moo e kaa see tɛɛle ya akyaŋ ke dɔɔ kei dɔɔ.

english_verse: God didn't give him any part of it, not even a square meter. But God did promise to give it to him and his family forever, even though Abraham didn't have any children.
translation_verse: Tɛɛle naetɔɔ dokoloŋ koraŋ ɔ te fa e botɔɔ nyi dɛɛ bese ŋon gbagba ɔ wui ya. Mɔna Wurubuarɛ yakowɔ see nyi, waa mo tɛɛle kenaŋ fa ŋon na ɔ duuluŋ man balaŋ. Debaŋ kenaŋ mɔ na Aberaham be dana bu koraŋ gba ya.

english_verse: God said Abraham's descendants would live for a while in a foreign land. There they would be slaves and would be mistreated
translation_verse: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ yako e nyi, ‘N duuluŋ man balaŋ baa kyaa dei wɔle daale tɛɛle dɔɔ bese ŋɔɔla. Baa moo wɔ yɛɛ gbeŋa, na baa yɛɛ wɔ nyeemandoŋ na akpanyaŋnyaŋ kulutooneŋ sɔŋonoŋ akpeŋ anaara (

english_verse: But he also said, “I will punish the nation that makes them slaves. Then later they will come and worship me in this place.”
translation_verse: Mɔna maa wɔŋ tɛɛle kenaŋ mantena baŋ baa mo n duuluŋ man balaŋ baŋ yɛɛ gbeŋa nɛ deŋele. Kenaŋ wɔle na, n duuluŋ man balaŋ baŋ baa lee tɛɛle kenaŋ man, na be kaa som maŋ kɛbo kei.’

english_verse: God said to Abraham, “Every son in each family must be circumcised to show you have kept your agreement with me.” So when Isaac was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him. Later, Isaac circumcised his son Jacob, and Jacob circumcised his twelve sons.
translation_verse: Te Wurubuarɛ na Aberaham bakewɔ see nyi, ŋon na ɔ duuluŋ man balaŋ pou baa kara ba baala wose. Mena dɔɔ Aberaham ka lola ɔ bu Asiki weeya gyanaarate nɛ te ɔ kara ɔ baala wose. Kenaŋ wɔle Asiki mɔ kaa lola Gyekɔpo, te Gyekɔpo mɔ lola de naanaɔ kufu bala baŋ.

english_verse: These men were our ancestors. Joseph was also one of our famous ancestors. His brothers were jealous of him and sold him as a slave to be taken to Egypt. But God was with him
translation_verse: Gyosɛfo mɔ yɛɛ de naanaɔ kufu bala baŋ man ŋolo e. Keŋ ɔ kegyiise ne kɔla e dɔɔ nɛ te ba mo e yɔɔ te ɔ ka bese gbeŋi Igyipite tɛɛle man, mɔna Wurubuarɛ seŋawɔ ɔ wɔle,

english_verse: and rescued him from all his troubles. God made him so wise that the Egyptian king Pharaoh thought highly of him. The king even made Joseph governor over Egypt and put him in charge of everything he owned.
translation_verse: te ɔ lese e lee ɔ diyem pou man. Te ɔ fa e nyansa, te ɔ yela Igyipitetena gyoo Faaro gyae ɔ nombia, mena dɔɔ gyoo ŋon moo ɔ dekpaŋalaŋ na Igyipite tɛɛle keŋ pou do ŋon Gyosɛfo nyiŋmaa man nyi waa kɛɛ ŋe dɔɔ.

english_verse: Everywhere in Egypt and Canaan the grain crops failed. There was terrible suffering, and our ancestors could not find enough to eat.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ te tanam dinaa daale kaa yalawɔ Igyipite na Kaanan tɛɛlese pou dɔɔ, te ka moo kaale dinaa mɔ kaa lɔ be dɔɔ. Mena dɔɔ de naanaɔ baŋ te nyiŋ weenɛɛ baa di ya.

english_verse: But when Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ Gyekɔpo be nyii nyi weenɛɛ kyaa Igyipite nɛ, te ɔ kpila ɔ bia de naanaɔ baŋ botɔɔ.

english_verse: It was on their second trip that Joseph told his brothers who he was, and the king learned about Joseph's family.
translation_verse: Be ka bese gyu Igyipite bolate nɛ, Gyosɛfo lese ɔ wose wola ɔ kegyiise baŋ, te gyoo Faaro mɔ kaa gyeŋa Gyosɛfo ɔ loŋtona baŋ.

english_verse: His father went to Egypt and died there, just as our ancestors did.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Gyekɔpo gyuuwɔ ke kyaa Igyipite, te ŋon na ɔ bia pou yekeewɔ botɔɔ.

english_verse: Later their bodies were taken back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had bought from the sons of Hamor.
translation_verse: Ba besena ba woya baŋ kɔŋ Sekyɛm donɔɔ man, te be kaa wuu wɔ boe bɔɔ keŋ Aberaham be kala kɔba lɛɛ lee Hamɔ bia gyaŋ nɛ man.

english_verse: Finally, the time came for God to do what he had promised Abraham. By then the number of our people in Egypt had greatly increased.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombii ayakoŋ see fa Aberaham keŋ na benaa kekɔŋ ampaŋ nɛ, te de balaŋ baŋ be kyaa Igyipite nɛ wukuwɔ nideli.

english_verse: Another king was ruling Egypt, and he didn't know anything about Joseph.
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, gyoo wɔle ŋolo ŋon ɔ ba gyeŋ kolo lee Gyosɛfo wose man ya nɛ, kaa dii gyoori Igyipite tɛɛle keŋ dɔɔ.

english_verse: He tricked our ancestors and was cruel to them. He even made them leave their babies outside, so they would die.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɔ yɛɛ de naanaɔ baŋ nyeemandoŋ na akpanyaŋnyaŋ, te ɔ ŋere wɔ dekpaŋkpasare man nyi baa tirii be bia wɔlɛɛ leki na baa nyiŋ yeŋ.

english_verse: During this time Moses was born. He was a very beautiful child, and for three months his parents took care of him in their home.
translation_verse: Mena debaŋ kenaŋ man mɔ te ba lola Mosesi, mɔna ɔ te yɛɛ bu yakaate e Wurubuarɛ siaman ya. Ba weese e ɔ kya deni man te be kɛɛle e botɔɔ gyaalaŋna batooro.

english_verse: Then when they were forced to leave him outside, the king's daughter found him and raised him as her own son.
translation_verse: Lii debaŋ daale na, be tira e leki debɔɔ man, te gyoo Faaro bu alebu naa e mo ke kɛɛle ŋgba ŋon gbagba ɔ bu nɛ.

english_verse: Moses was given the best education in Egypt. He was a strong man and a powerful speaker.
translation_verse: Ba wola e Igyipitetena nyansa pou. Te ɔ kaa bese walaŋ dinaa ɔ kekolosi na ɔ tom keyɛɛ pou man.

english_verse: years in the desert.
translation_verse: ) depampaa dinaa keŋ man ya.

english_verse: One day he saw an Egyptian mistreating one of them. So he rescued the man and killed the Egyptian.
translation_verse: Ɔ ke gyu keŋ nɛ, ɔ ke naawɔ ŋgba Igyipite baale ŋolo ne naase Iserae baale ŋolo diyem na, te ɔ gyuuwɔ ke lɛɛ ɔ dɛɛ baale ŋon nɔɔ te ɔ koo Igyipite baale ŋon.

english_verse: Moses thought the rest of his people would realize God was going to use him to set them free. But they didn't understand.
translation_verse: Mosesi agyueŋ man na, ŋon gyeŋ nyi ɔ balaŋ baŋ baa nyii asɛɛ nyi Wurubuarɛ waa ta ɔ dɔɔ bo lese wɔ lee be kaale man, mɔna be te nyii asɛɛ mena ya.

english_verse: The next day Moses saw two of his own people fighting, and he tried to make them stop. He said, “Men, you are both Israelites. Why are you so cruel to each other?”
