translation_verse: Ke tɛɛ naa nɛ, ɔ ke naa Iseraetena wolɛɛwolɛɛ bala mɔ ne yoo, te ɔ gyuuwɔ ɔ kaa pataa wɔ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, ‘Me tebia ɛ kɛɛ, ɛ wolɛɛwolɛɛ. Weera dɔɔ te ɛ ne yɛɛ dɔŋa akpanyaŋnyaŋ mena?’

english_verse: But the man who had started the fight pushed Moses aside and asked, “Who made you our ruler and judge?
translation_verse: Mɔna ŋon ɔ ne yɛɛ ɔ dɔɔ akpanyaŋnyaŋ nɛ tɛɛse Mosesi lee yenaŋ te ɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, ‘Amɔte moona neŋ yɛɛ de siamante na de nombiadiiri?’

english_verse: Are you going to kill me, just as you killed that Egyptian yesterday?”
translation_verse: ‘N ne gyae baa n ka ko maŋ ŋgba mena keŋ dana n ba ko Igyipite baale ŋon nɛ?’

english_verse: When Moses heard this, he ran away to live in the country of Midian. His two sons were born there.
translation_verse: Mosesi kenyii nombii kei nɛ te ɔ korowɔ yeu gyu ke kyaa Midian tɛɛle man bese ŋɔɔle botɔɔ. Botɔɔ te ɔ yalawɔ te ɔ lola bia baala bala.

english_verse: Forty years later, an angel appeared to Moses from a burning bush in the desert near Mount Sinai.
translation_verse: Kulutooneŋ sɔŋola wɔle nɛ, Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋolo kaa lese ɔ wose wola Mosesi, boalaŋ daale keŋ ne kpaa depoŋfa daale man depampaa nsana Sinai bula keŋ gyaŋ nɛ.

english_verse: Moses was surprised by what he saw. He went closer to get a better look, and the Lord said,
translation_verse: Mosesi naa ke na, gyakoloŋ kyaŋa e. Te ɔ tuuwɔ benaa ke na waa nyiŋ naa ke nideli. Botɔɔ te ɔ nyii de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ woya nyiaa,

english_verse: “I am the God who was worshiped by your ancestors, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” Moses started shaking all over and didn't dare to look at the bush.
translation_verse: ‘Maŋ yɛna n naanaɔ Aberaham na Asiki na Gyekɔpo Wurubuarɛ ŋon.’ Te gyakoloŋ gyoo Mosesi te ɔ sereewɔ, mena dɔɔ ɔ te tale kɛɛ ke bela ya.

english_verse: The Lord said to him, “Take off your sandals, because the place where you are standing is holy.
translation_verse: Te de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ yako e nyiaa, ‘Kpee nyaŋ nkyokotase lee n naawɔ man, nawolo nyi maŋ Wurubuarɛ gbagba kyaa botɔɔ keŋ n seŋɛɛ nɛ.

english_verse: With my own eyes I have seen the suffering of my people in Egypt. I have heard their groans and have come down to rescue them. Now I am sending you back to Egypt.”
translation_verse: Ampaŋ, ma te naa me balaŋ baŋ be kyaa Igyipite nɛ kaale te me te nyii mena keŋ mɔ bɛɛ toroŋ, mena dɔɔ me te tisi lee adido kɔŋ me kaa lɛɛ wɔ. Desina n wose na maa kpila neŋ Igyipite!’

english_verse: This was the same Moses that the people rejected by saying, “Who made you our leader and judge?” God's angel had spoken to Moses from the bush. And God had even sent the angel to help Moses rescue the people and be their leader.
translation_verse: Mosesi kei nombia te be bɛɛwɔ, te be bɔɔse e nyi, ‘Amɔte yɛɛ na neŋ de siamante, na de nombiadiiri.’ Wurubuarɛ tɛɛwɔ ɔ kpilale ŋon ɔ ba lese ɔ wose wola Mosesi depoŋfa keŋ man nɛ dɔɔ, yɛɛ e siamante na nyeelɛɛre.

english_verse: In Egypt and at the Red Sea and in the desert, Moses rescued the people by working miracles and wonders for
translation_verse: Mosesi kei yɛna gyakoloŋ nombia na nombia dinaana debaŋ keŋ ɔ ba mo Iseraetena lee Igyipite ta be siaman mo wɔ kaa toŋ ɛpo yayam keŋ, gyu ke ta depampaa dinaa keŋ man kulutooneŋ sɔŋola (

english_verse: Moses is the one who told the people of Israel, “God will choose one of your people to be a prophet, just as he chose me.”
translation_verse: Mosesi kei yako na Iseraetena nyiaa, ‘Wurubuarɛ waa kpila ɔ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre ŋolo ɛ gyaŋ ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ. Waa lee bo ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ balaŋ man.’

english_verse: Moses brought our people together in the desert, and the angel spoke to him on Mount Sinai. There he was given these life-giving words to pass on to us.
translation_verse: Mosesi kei na Iseraetena baŋ ke kyaa depampaa keŋ man nɛ, Wurubuarɛ kpilale kaa kolosiwɔ na e Sinai bula keŋ dɔɔ te ɔ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nyeedoŋ nombia ŋan fa de naanaɔ, te ŋe kaa tuŋa daa mɔ.

english_verse: But our ancestors refused to obey Moses. They rejected him and wanted to go back to Egypt.
translation_verse: Mɔna de naanaɔ yɛɛ deŋele doŋ te be bɛɛ ɔ nombia ŋan, te be tɛɛse e see yenaŋ. Te baŋ be konɔɔse man na, nafɔ be gyaewɔ nyi baa bese gyu Igyipite.

english_verse: Then they made an idol in the shape of a calf. They offered sacrifices to the idol and were pleased with what they had done.
translation_verse: Weeya ŋenaŋ man te be yɛɛ nunkyue bu mo yɛɛ be bane, te be faa ke kedi te be dii be nɔɔ mo lese kpene keŋ baŋ gbagba ba mo be nyiŋmaase yɛɛ nɛ yele.

english_verse: God turned his back on his people and left them. Then they worshiped the stars in the sky, just as it says in the Book of the Prophets, “People of Israel, you didn't offer sacrifices and offerings to me during those
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ lese ɔ sia yela wɔ, te ɔ moo wɔ fa adido kyolobiise kasom, ŋgba mena keŋ bɔɔ ŋmarase do Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra tɔne keŋ man nɛ. Ba ŋolo ŋmarasewɔ nyi Wurubuarɛ kpa, ‘O Iseraetena, na maŋ te ɛ koo wonembia deesi fa kulutooneŋ sɔŋola (

english_verse: Instead, you carried the tent where the god Molech is worshiped, and you took along the star of your god Rephan. You made those idols and worshiped them. So now I will have you carried off beyond Babylonia.”
translation_verse: Ɛ bane Molɛki te ɛ yɛɛ kanyaŋ deni do ke, na ɛ bane Refan wulu keŋ ke yɛɛ ŋgba kyolobii nɛ, te ɛ moowɔ sila ɛ wose. Baŋ yɛna banɛɛ baŋ ɛ be yɛɛ nyi ɛ ka som! Mena dɔɔ maa yela baa mo ŋon gyu lemlem, naŋ Babilɔn donɔɔ keŋ wɔle.’

english_verse: The tent where our ancestors worshiped God was with them in the desert. This was the same tent that God had commanded Moses to make. And it was made like the model that Moses had seen.
translation_verse: Depampaa dinaa keŋ man nɛ, na de naanaɔ dana kanyaŋ deni dinaa daale keŋ ne di wɔ adansɛɛ nyi Wurubuarɛ kyaa be gyaŋ. Be yɛɛ kanyaŋ deni dinaa kei ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba lese wola Mosesi nyi waa yɛɛ ke nɛ.

english_verse: Later it was given to our ancestors, and they took it with them when they went with Joshua. They carried the tent along as they took over the land from those people that God had chased out for them. Our ancestors used this tent until the time of King David.
translation_verse: De naanaɔ ke lɛɛ kanyaŋ deni dinaa kei lee be kyaɔ gyaŋ nɛ, ba moo ke sila be siamante Gyosua kelii tɛɛle keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yeli bɔɔ yoo gegi ke balaŋ te ɔ be lɛo ke fa wɔ nɛ man. Kanyaŋ deni dinaa kei doowɔ botɔɔ kelii debaŋ keŋ Defidi be kaa di gyoori nɛ.

english_verse: He pleased God and asked him if he could build a house of worship for the people of Israel.
translation_verse: Defidi kei nombia gyoo Wurubuarɛ sia, te ɔ yako Wurubuarɛ nyi waa fa e gbɛɛ na waa baŋ ɔsom deni dinaa gbagba fa ɔ naana Gyekɔpo Wurubuarɛ ŋon.

english_verse: And it was finally King Solomon who built a house for God.
translation_verse: Mɔna ɔ bu Solomɔn kaa baŋa na ɔsom deni dinaa kenaŋ.

english_verse: But the Most High God doesn't live in houses made by humans. It is just as the prophet said, when he spoke for the Lord,
translation_verse: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kela kpene kamasɛ nɛ, bɛɛ kyaa deni keŋ bɔɔ mo nyiŋmaase ma ke nɛ man ya. Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre ŋolo yako mena nombii kei nyiaa,

english_verse: “Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me? In what place will I rest?
translation_verse: De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ kpa, ‘Adido te me gyooro gbeli kyaa, te tɛɛle dɔɔ yɛna me nae dɛɛsee. Woŋ deni dinɔɔ yaa ɛ ke ma fa maŋ, yaa aleŋ te maa kyaa fɛɛfo?

english_verse: Is there one prophet that your ancestors didn't mistreat? They killed the prophets who told about the coming of the One Who Obeys God. And now you have turned against him and killed him.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre ŋolo kyaawɔ keŋ ɛ naanaɔ te naase e diyem yee? Ba koo Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ bɔɔ wula yako see nyi Yesu Kristo ŋon ɔ nombia tenɛɛ nɛ waa kɔŋ nɛ, te nɛnɛɛ ɛ lese Yesu ŋonaŋ fa te ba koo e.

english_verse: Angels gave you God's Law, but you still don't obey it. Stephen Is Stoned to Death
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ yɛna baŋ ɛ be nyiŋ Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan lee ɔ kpilala gyaŋ, mɔna ɛ te di ŋe dɔɔ ya.” Be ke fuŋii Sitifin boya

english_verse: When the council members heard Stephen's speech, they were angry and furious.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ donɔɔ kegyiise baŋ be nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, be sia yɛɛwɔ ɔ dɔɔ te ba gyeŋa baŋ nideli.

english_verse: But Stephen was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked toward heaven, where he saw our glorious God and Jesus standing at his right side.
translation_verse: Mɔna Sitifin ŋon Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon doo ɔ man nideli nɛ kɛɛ adido na, te ɔ naa Wurubuarɛ gyoorobiiri keŋ, na Yesu mɔ seŋɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dunoluŋ dɔɔ.

english_verse: Then Stephen said, “I see heaven open and the Son of Man standing at the right side of God!”
translation_verse: Te Sitifin yakowɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ kɛɛ, mɛɛ naa ŋgba adido ta toro, na Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon seŋɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dunoluŋ dɔɔ.”

english_verse: The council members shouted and covered their ears. At once they all attacked Stephen
translation_verse: Ɔ keyako mena nɛ te kegyiise baŋ moo be nyiŋmaase tɔ ba deŋela, te be fae yeesa keŋkeŋ. Karatetee te be popou kaa wuuwɔ ɔ dɔɔ,

english_verse: and dragged him out of the city. Then they started throwing stones at him. The men who had brought charges against him put their coats at the feet of a young man named Saul.
translation_verse: te be wɔŋee e lee donɔɔ keŋ man ke fuŋii e boya. Baŋ bɔɔ gyu be kaa fuŋii e boya nɛ, kpee ba kegba wurikyimse ŋan fa tobaale ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Sɔɔl nɛ.

english_verse: As Stephen was being stoned to death, he called out, “Lord Jesus, please welcome me!”
translation_verse: Be kee fuŋii Sitifin boya ŋan nɛ, na ɔ ne fane nyi, “Me Gbeŋgyoo Yesu, lɛɛ ma woo.”

english_verse: Saul started making a lot of trouble for the church. He went from house to house, arresting men and women and putting them in jail. The Good News Is Preached in Samaria
translation_verse: Mɔna Sɔɔl korowɔ seŋ ɔ ne naase Kristotena baŋ diyem nideli. Ɔ na gyoo akpaŋalaŋse man ke wɔŋee Kristotena ala na baala gyu ketɔ deni. Filipo yako Wurubuarɛ nombia Samaria tɛɛle man

english_verse: The Lord's followers who had been scattered went from place to place, telling the good news.
translation_verse: Yenaŋ kamasɛ keŋ Kristotena baŋ be yaasee gyu nɛ, be kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan fa balaŋ.

english_verse: Philip went to the city of Samaria and told the people about Christ.
translation_verse: Te Filipo gyuu Samariatena donɔɔ dinaa daale man keyako Wurubuarɛ nombia nyi, Yesu Kristo yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ.

english_verse: They crowded around Philip because they were eager to hear what he was saying and to see him work miracles.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ balaŋ burum baŋ be nyii nombia ŋan Filipo be yako, te bɔɔ naa gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan ɔ be yɛɛ nɛ dɔɔ, be tei ɔ nombia ŋan nideli.

english_verse: Many people with evil spirits were healed, and the spirits went out of them with a shout. A lot of paralyzed and lame people were also healed.
translation_verse: Filipo kekolosi keŋ nɛ, balaŋ baŋ feliŋkumɛɛ doo be man nɛ fae yeesa keŋkeŋ, te feliŋkumɛɛ ŋan leewɔ be man. Te baŋ mɔ be baamense ba yekee, na ɔtakasese burum mɔ wose yɛɛ wɔ doŋ.

english_verse: Everyone in that city was very glad because of what was happening.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ yɛɛ donɔɔ kenaŋ mantena baŋ pou gyoŋ nideli.

english_verse: For some time a man named Simon had lived there and had amazed the people of Samaria. He practiced witchcraft and claimed to be somebody great.
translation_verse: Donɔɔ kenaŋ man na balee ŋolo kyaa botɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake e nyi Simɔn. Ɔ ne yɛɛ baalabiiri nombia wola Samariatena, keŋ nyi ba wose na gbirisi wɔ. Ɔ ne gyina ɔ wose adido wola ɔ wose nyi ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ dinaa ŋolo e.

english_verse: Everyone, rich and poor, crowded around him. They said, “This man is the power of God called ‘The Great Power.’ ”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ walaŋ kamasɛ, bia na kegyiise pou ne tei e te bɛɛ yako nyi, “Ŋon yɛna Wurubuarɛ doŋ keŋ bɛɛ baake nyi doŋ dinaa nɛ.”

english_verse: For a long time, Simon had used witchcraft to amaze the people, and they kept crowding around him.
translation_verse: Ɔ ta mo ɔ baalabiiri nombia kɛŋa wola wɔ yela ba wose te gbirisi wɔ kyare, mena dɔɔ bɛɛ lɛɛ e di nideli.

english_verse: But when they believed what Philip was saying about God's kingdom and about the name of Jesus Christ, they were all baptized.
translation_verse: Mɔna Filipo ke yako wɔ Wurubuarɛ gyoori na Yesu Kristo wose man nombia ŋan nɛ be lɛɛwɔ di, te be sɔɔ ala na baala pou Wurubuarɛ loŋ.

english_verse: Even Simon believed and was baptized. He stayed close to Philip, because he marveled at all the miracles and wonders.
translation_verse: Simɔn gbagba mɔ lɛɛ nombia ŋan di te be sɔɔ e Wurubuarɛ loŋ, te ɔ moo ɔ wose do na Filipo nideli. Te gyakoloŋ nombia na nombia dinaana ŋan ɔ be naa Filipo ne yɛɛ dɔɔ, ɔ wose gbirisi e.

english_verse: The apostles in Jerusalem heard that some people in Samaria had accepted God's message, and they sent Peter and John.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ Yesu kpilala baŋ be kyaa Gyerusalɛm be nyii nyi Samariatena te lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan di nɛ, be kpila Pita na Gyɔn be gyaŋ.

english_verse: When the two apostles arrived, they prayed that these people would be given the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Ba kekɔŋ kaa gyoo na, be fanewɔ fa wɔ nyi Wurubuarɛ waa mo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do be man.

english_verse: Before this, the Holy Spirit had not been given to any of them, though some of them had been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi na Wurubuarɛ ta moo ta ɔ feliŋ ŋon do baŋ Samariatena benaŋ man ŋolo man ya. Be sɔɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ bo lee de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu waageŋ yele man.

english_verse: Peter and John then placed their hands on everyone who had faith in the Lord, and they were given the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Te Pita na Gyɔn moo be nyiŋmaase gyakaa be dɔɔ te be fanewɔ fa wɔ, te Wurubuarɛ moo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do be man.

english_verse: Simon noticed that the Spirit was given only when the apostles placed their hands on the people. So he brought money
translation_verse: Simɔn naawɔ nyi Yesu kpilala baŋ ka mo be nyiŋmaase gyakaa balaŋ baŋ dɔɔ fane fa wɔ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ moo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do be man keŋte ɔ lese kɔba waa fa wɔ,

english_verse: and said to Peter and John, “Let me have this power too! Then anyone I place my hands on will also be given the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ mo mena doŋ kei fa maŋ mɔ, na walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon maa mo me nyiŋmaa gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ na, Wurubuarɛ ka mo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do ɔ man.”

english_verse: Peter said to him, “You and your money will both end up in hell if you think you can buy God's gift!
translation_verse: Te Pita yako e nyi, “Wurubuarɛ waa yela ɛmɛɛ na n kɔba pou ka gyoo boalaŋ man. Nawolo nyi n te yɛɛ nya agyueŋ nyi n ka mo kɔba lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ kolo anefaŋ.

english_verse: You don't have any part in this, and God sees that your heart isn't right.
translation_verse: N be dana dedii yaa daseŋae da tom kei man ya, nawolo nyi n konɔɔ man nombia ba tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman ya.

english_verse: Get rid of these evil thoughts and ask God to forgive you.
translation_verse: Kyɛɛkee nya agyueŋ lee n nombiikum kei man, na n kefane fa de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ na waa mo ke kyɛɛ neŋ.

english_verse: I can see that you are jealous and bound by your evil ways.”
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi ma te naa nyi n dana sibiikum, te nombiakumɛɛ te di n dɔɔ.”

english_verse: Simon said, “Please pray to the Lord, so that what you said won't happen to me.”
translation_verse: Te Simɔn yako wɔ nyi, “Mɛɛ waase ŋon bo ɛ fane fa de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ fa maŋ, na ŋan pou ɛ be yako nɛ man dokoloŋ gba be kɔŋ me dɔɔ ya.”

english_verse: After Peter and John had preached about the Lord, they returned to Jerusalem. On their way they told the good news in many villages of Samaria. Philip and an Ethiopian Official
translation_verse: Pita na Gyɔn kekolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan di adansɛɛ lee Yesu Kristo wose man taŋ nɛ, te ba besewɔ laŋ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man. Be ka bese bee gyu keŋ nɛ, be kolosi Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan Samariatena akuraase burum man. Filipo na Itiopia baale ŋolo wose man nombia

english_verse: The Lord's angel said to Philip, “Go south along the desert road that leads from Jerusalem to Gaza.”
translation_verse: De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋolo lee adido kaa yako Filipo nyiaa, “Koro desina n wose na n ka mo ata yenaŋ gbɛɛ bene keŋ ba lee Gyerusalɛm ne gyu Gasa donɔɔ man nɛ.”

english_verse: So Philip left. An important Ethiopian official happened to be going along that road in his chariot. He was the chief treasurer for Candace, the Queen of Ethiopia. The official had gone to Jerusalem to worship
translation_verse: Keŋte Filipo korowɔ desina ɔ wose moo gbɛɛ. Ɔ kee gyu keŋ na te ɔ ke gyaŋee na Itiopia baale saayɛte ŋolo. Ɔ yɛɛ Itiopia ala gyoo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Kandase nɛ wose man walaŋ kegyia ŋolo e. Ŋon ne kɛɛ na ala gyoo ŋon kɔba pou dɔɔ. Balee kei gyuu Gyerusalɛm bo ka som Wurubuarɛ,

english_verse: and was now on his way home. He was sitting in his chariot, reading the book of the prophet Isaiah.
translation_verse: te ɔ besewɔ kaa laŋ ɔ ne gyu ɔ dɛɛ. Na ɔ kyaa anebiliŋinbii daale man keŋ dɛɛkyɔŋse ne wɔŋee na ke. Ɔ kyaa ɔ ne kala Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya tɔne.

english_verse: The Spirit told Philip to catch up with the chariot.
translation_verse: Te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon yako Filipo nyi, “Tuu benaa anebiliŋinbii keŋ.”

english_verse: Philip ran up close and heard the man reading aloud from the book of Isaiah. Philip asked him, “Do you understand what you are reading?”
translation_verse: Keŋte Filipo yeuwɔ gyu botɔɔ, te ɔ ke nyiiwɔ ŋgba balee ŋon ne kala kolo lee Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya tɔne keŋ man nɛ te Filipo bɔɔse e nyi, “Mena dɔɔ n ne nyii kpene keŋ n ne kale nɛ man?”

english_verse: The official answered, “How can I understand unless someone helps me?” He then invited Philip to come up and sit beside him.
translation_verse: Te balee ŋon tiranɔɔ fa Filipo nyi, “Sena te maa tale nyii ke man, keŋ ŋolo ta lese ke man fa maŋ ya?” Te ɔ baake Filipo nyi waa gyeŋ kaa kyaa ɔ gyaŋ.

english_verse: The man was reading the passage that said, “He was led like a sheep on its way to be killed. He was silent as a lamb whose wool is being cut off, and he did not say a word.
translation_verse: Na Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan bɔɔ ŋmarase see te ɔ ne kale yɛna nyi, “Ba te kparaa e laŋ ŋgba nam ŋon bee gyu be kaa ko e nɛ. Ɔ yɛɛ ŋgba nambu ŋon bɛɛ kɛrɛɛ ɔ wona te ɔ seŋɛɛ yididi nɛ. Ɔ te wii koraŋ gba ya.

english_verse: He was treated like a nobody and did not receive a fair trial. How can he have children, if his life is snatched away?”
translation_verse: Be tisinaa e tɛɛle man, do e desɛŋ, te be sukutii ɔ nombia do. Amɔ ne gyae la waa tale yako kolo lee ɔ duuluŋ man balaŋ wose man, ŋgba bɔɔ ko e bileŋ nɛ.”

english_verse: The official said to Philip, “Tell me, was the prophet talking about himself or about someone else?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Itiopia baale kegyia ŋon bɔɔse Filipo nyiaa, “Kegyia waaseda, amɔte wose man nombia yaa Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre kei ne yako nɛ, ŋon gbagba yaa walaŋ wɔle ŋolo bo?”

english_verse: So Philip began at this place in the Scriptures and explained the good news about Jesus.
translation_verse: Te Filipo seŋawɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan bɔɔ kale nɛ dɔɔ yako e Yesu wose man nombia ŋan.

english_verse: As they were going along the road, they came to a place where there was some water. The official said, “Look! Here is some water. Why can't I be baptized?”
translation_verse: Te Filipo yako e nyi, “Akpaa n mo n konɔɔ pou lɛɛ Kristo di na, gbɛɛ doo nyi maa sɔ neŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ.” Balee ŋon mɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Mɛɛ lɛɛdi nyi Yesu Kristo yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon.”

english_verse: He ordered the chariot to stop. Then they both went down into the water, and Philip baptized him.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ balee ŋon yela anebiliŋinbii keŋ seŋawɔ, te ŋon na Filipo pou tisiwɔ ka gyoo loŋ keŋ man te Filipo sɔɔ e Wurubuarɛ loŋ.

english_verse: After they had come out of the water, the Lord's Spirit took Philip away. The official never saw him again, but he was very happy as he went on his way.
translation_verse: Be ka lee loŋ keŋ man nɛ, te de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon moo Filipo laŋ debokenaŋ man, mena dɔɔ balee ŋon te naa e bela ya. Mɔna balee ŋon besewɔ mo ɔ gbɛɛ ɔ ne gyu gyoŋ man.

english_verse: Philip later appeared in Azotus. He went from town to town, all the way to Caesarea, telling people about Jesus.
translation_verse: Filipo kɛɛwɔ na, ɔ kyaa Asotuse donɔɔ man. Ɔ tɛɛwɔ donɔɔse ŋan man kolosi Kristo nombia ŋan, kaboena ɔ gyuuwɔ ka lee Kaesaria donɔɔ man.

english_verse: Saul preached with such power that he completely confused the Jewish people in Damascus, as he tried to show them that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_verse: Te Sɔɔl besewɔ nyiŋ doŋ kpu te ɔ kolosiwɔ lese ŋe man nideli fa Gyudatena baŋ nyi, Yesu yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ. Ɔ kolosiwɔ nideli keŋ nyi Gyudatena baŋ be kyaa Damasekuse donɔɔ man nɛ, te tale gyuusu be nɔɔ bela ya.

english_verse: Now get up and go into the city, where you will be told what to do.”
translation_verse: Mɔna koro na n ke gyu donɔɔ keŋ man, na baa yako neŋ kpene keŋ kaboena nyi nya yɛɛ.”

english_verse: I will show him how much he must suffer for worshiping in my name.”
translation_verse: Maŋ ne gyae la maa wola e mena keŋ kaboena nyi waa naa diyem nideli lee ma yele dɔɔ.”

english_verse: When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the followers. But they were all afraid of him, because they did not believe he was a true follower.
translation_verse: Sɔɔl ka bese gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, ɔ gyaewɔ nyi waa mo ɔ wose kpu na Yesu silala baŋ. Mɔna be popou ne yee nawolo nyi na ba te lɛɛ ta di nyi, ŋon mɔ ta bese Yesu silale gbagba ya.

english_verse: and he has seen a vision. He saw a man named Ananias coming to him and putting his hands on him, so he could see again.”
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ ta lese wola ŋon Sɔɔl mɔ ŋgba dosɛɛ man nɛ nyi, balee ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Ananiase nɛ, te kɔŋ ɔ kaa mo ɔ nyiŋmaase gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ, na waa bese naa bela.”

english_verse: Suddenly something like fish scales fell from Saul's eyes, and he could see. He got up and was baptized.
translation_verse: Debokenaŋ man te kolo leewɔ ɔ sia ŋan man yala ŋgba kpebii bɔɔlɛɛ bia nɛ, te Sɔɔl sia ŋan gyuusuwɔ, te ɔ besewɔ ɔ ne naa bela. Ɔ korowɔ seŋ te ba ke sɔɔ e Wurubuarɛ loŋ.

english_verse: Saul kept on threatening to kill the Lord's followers. He even went to the high priest
translation_verse: Weeya ŋenaŋ pou man na Sɔɔl sɛɛ ɔ ne ŋmaase de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu silala baŋ nyi waa ko wɔ. Mena dɔɔ ɔ gyuu Wurubuarɛ sae kegyia ŋon gyaŋ,

english_verse: and asked for letters to the leaders of the synagogues in Damascus. He did this because he wanted to arrest and take to Jerusalem any man or woman who had accepted the Lord's Way.
translation_verse: ka sola e lɛɛ ɔ nyiŋmaa man tɔne keŋ gyae ke fa e doŋ na waa mo gyu Damasekuse donɔɔ man kefa Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man kegyiise baŋ. Na baa fa e gbɛɛ waa kyaŋ walaŋ kamasɛ, ala na baala baŋ pou ɔ naa nyi be silana Yesu kawola wɔle kei nɛ. Na waa nyiŋ kyaŋ wɔ kɔŋ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man kaa tɔ deni.

english_verse: When Saul had almost reached Damascus, a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around him.
translation_verse: Ɔ ke gyu ka benaa Damasekuse donɔɔ nɛ, debokenaŋ man te boalaŋ daale lee adido kaa ŋmaale polɛ kilisi e.

english_verse: He fell to the ground and heard a voice saying, “Saul! Saul! Why are you so cruel to me?”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɔ leewɔ yala te ɔ nyii woya ŋaale baake e bɔɔse nyi, “Sɔɔl, Sɔɔl, weera dɔɔ te n ne naase maŋ diyem mena?”

english_verse: “Who are you?” Saul asked. “I am Jesus,” the Lord answered. “I am the one you are so cruel to.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Sɔɔl tiranɔɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Nyaŋ amɔte ŋonaŋ me Gbeŋgyoo?” Te ŋon mɔ yako e nyiaa, “Maŋ yɛna Yesu ŋon n ne naase diyem nɛ.

english_verse: The men with Saul stood there speechless. They had heard the voice, but they had not seen anyone.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ baŋ na Sɔɔl dɔŋɛɛ nɛ, ba woya yeuwɔ lee be gyaŋ keŋ ba te tale gyuusu be nɔɔ ya. Baŋ mɔ nyii nɔɔwoya ŋan, mɔna ba te naa ŋolo ya.

english_verse: Saul got up from the ground, and when he opened his eyes, he could not see a thing. Someone then led him by the hand to Damascus,
translation_verse: Sɔɔl korowɔ seŋ te ɔ gyuusu ɔ sia mɔna ɔ te tale naa kolo ya, mena dɔɔ be kyaŋa e ɔ nyiŋmaa man, te ba moo e gyu Damasekuse donɔɔ man.

english_verse: and for three days he was blind and did not eat or drink.
translation_verse: Ɔ kyaawɔ botɔɔ weeya kpaakpaa atooro keŋ nyi ɔ bɛɛ naa ya, te ɔ bee dii ya, te ɔ bɛɛ nyɔɔ mɔ ya.

english_verse: A follower named Ananias lived in Damascus, and the Lord spoke to him in a vision. Ananias answered, “Lord, here I am.”
translation_verse: Na Yesu silale ŋolo kyaa Damasekuse botɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake e nyi Ananiase. Ɔ nyiiwɔ de Gbeŋgyoo baake e ŋgba dosɛɛ man nɛ nyi, “Ananiase.” Te ɔ kpaawɔ nyi, “Me Gbeŋgyoo maŋ yaa!”

english_verse: The Lord said to him, “Get up and go to the house of Judas on Straight Street. When you get there, you will find a man named Saul from the city of Tarsus. Saul is praying,
translation_verse: Te de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu yako e nyi, “Koro gyu Gyuda dɛɛ, ke kyaa gbɛɛnaa keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi gbɛɛnaa keŋ ka Tenɛɛ nɛ nɔɔman, na n ke bɔɔse Sɔɔl ŋon ɔ ba lee Tasuse nɛ dɔɔ. Ɔ kyaa botɔɔ ɔ ne fane.

english_verse: Ananias replied, “Lord, a lot of people have told me about the terrible things this man has done to your followers in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Ananiase tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Me Gbeŋgyoo, balaŋ burum te yako maŋ balee kei wose man nombia, na mena keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ n balaŋ baŋ be kyaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ akpanyaŋnyaŋ.

english_verse: Now the chief priests have given him the power to come here and arrest anyone who worships in your name.”
translation_verse: Ɔ lɛɛ tɔne keŋ ne fa e doŋ baa lee Gyudatena saese kegyiise gyaŋ kɔŋ Damasekuse kɛbo, na ɔ kaa kyaŋ baŋ pou bɛɛ som neŋ nɛ.”

english_verse: The Lord said to Ananias, “Go! I have chosen him to tell foreigners, kings, and the people of Israel about me.
translation_verse: Keŋte de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu tiranɔɔ fa Ananiase nyiaa, “Gyu ɔ gyaŋ, nawolo nyi maŋ lese na e nyi waa som maŋ, na waa yela baŋ pou ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya, na be gyooneŋ na Iseraetena pou ke nyii ma yele.

english_verse: Ananias left and went into the house where Saul was staying. Ananias placed his hands on him and said, “Saul, the Lord Jesus has sent me. He is the same one who appeared to you along the road. He wants you to be able to see and to be filled with the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Ananiase gyuuwɔ ka gyoo dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man. Te ɔ moo ɔ nyiŋmaase gyakaa Sɔɔl nyee dɔ fane nyiaa, “Me dɔɔ Sɔɔl, de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu ŋon ɔ ba lese ɔ wose wola neŋ gbɛɛnaa man debaŋ keŋ ne kɔŋ kɛbo nɛ, ŋon kpila na maŋ n gyaŋ nyi me kaa fane fa neŋ na n kabese naa. Na Wurubuarɛ kenyiŋ mo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do n man.”

english_verse: Then he ate and felt much better. Saul Preaches in Damascus For several days Saul stayed with the Lord's followers in Damascus.
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle te be faa e weenɛɛ. Ɔ ke di taŋ nɛ, ɔ wose besewɔ yɛɛ e doŋ. Te ɔ dii weeya akalansɛɛ ŋaale Yesu silala baŋ gyaŋ Damasekuse donɔɔ man botɔɔ. Sɔɔl kekolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia Damasekuse

english_verse: Soon he went to the synagogues and started telling people that Jesus is the Son of God.
translation_verse: Debokenaŋ man te Sɔɔl fiasɛɛ ɔ ne kolosi Yesu nombia Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man nyi, Yesu yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon.

english_verse: Everyone who heard Saul was amazed and said, “Isn't this the man who caused so much trouble for those people in Jerusalem who worship in the name of Jesus? Didn't he come here to arrest them and take them to the chief priests?”
translation_verse: Baŋ pou bɔɔ nyii e nɛ wose gbirisi wɔ, te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyi, “Na balee kei kyaa la Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man ɔ ne naase baŋ bɛɛ som Yesu nɛ diyem yee? Na keŋ dɔɔ te ɔ kɔŋawɔ kɛbo ɔ kaa kyaŋ wɔ na waa mo wɔ gyina saese kegyiise baŋ yee?”

english_verse: Later some of them made plans to kill Saul,
translation_verse: Weeya ala ŋaale wɔle nɛ, Gyudatena kegyiise gyaŋeewɔ yɛɛ nɔɔ nyi baa ko Sɔɔl.

english_verse: but he found out about it. He learned that they were guarding the gates of the city day and night in order to kill him.
translation_verse: Mɔna Sɔɔl nyii be nombiakumɛɛ ŋan bɔɔ bake ɔ nyeeman nɛ. Nelim na ka weese pou na, bɛɛ deke donɔɔ keŋ anaanɔɔse ŋan na baa nyiŋ ko e.

english_verse: Then one night his followers let him down over the city wall in a large basket. Saul in Jerusalem
translation_verse: Mɔna nelim daale nɛ, ɔ kaseela baŋ moo e do dokyoŋoloŋ man, te be tisinaa e mo nfansere man gbaa dekyaŋ keŋ bɔɔ maa kilisi donɔɔ keŋ nɛ, te ɔ yeuwɔ laŋ Gyerusalɛm. Sɔɔl kegyu Gyerusalɛm

english_verse: Then Barnabas helped him by taking him to the apostles. He explained how Saul had seen the Lord and how the Lord had spoken to him. Barnabas also said when Saul was in Damascus, he had spoken bravely in the name of Jesus.
translation_verse: Keŋte Banabase moo e gyu Kristo kpilala baŋ gyaŋ. Te ɔ yako wɔ mena keŋ Sɔɔl be naa de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Damasekuse gbɛɛnaa man, na mena keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu be kolosi na e, na mena keŋ ɔ be gyɔ ɔ konɔɔ mo Yesu yele keyako Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan Damasekuse donɔɔ man nɛ.

english_verse: He was always arguing with the Jews who spoke Greek, and so they tried to kill him.
translation_verse: Sɔɔl kolosiwɔ nideli te ŋon na Gyudatena baŋ bee kolosi Giriki dei nɛ, lɛɛ aŋmaareŋ nideli. Te be gyae gbɛɛ keŋ baa mo ko e.

english_verse: But the followers found out about this and took Saul to Caesarea. From there they sent him to the city of Tarsus.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Yesu silala baŋ ka gyeŋ mena nɛ, ba moo e gyu Kaesaria donɔɔ man te be yela ɔ laŋa Tasuse.

english_verse: The church in Judea, Galilee, and Samaria now had a time of peace and kept on worshiping the Lord. The church became stronger, as the Holy Spirit encouraged it and helped it grow. Peter Heals Aeneas
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Kristotena baŋ be kyaa Gyudia na Galelia na Samaria tɛɛlese man nɛ, wose fɛɛ wɔ mena debaŋ kenaŋ man. Be lɛɛ Yesu nombia ŋan di nideli, te be tɛɛwɔ Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii man, te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon doo wɔ kakyeŋ te be wukuwɔ kpu. Pita kekyɔ Anease na Dɔkase

english_verse: While Peter was traveling from place to place, he visited the Lord's followers who lived in the town of Lydda.
translation_verse: Kɔŋawɔ debaŋ daale nyi Pita keta ɔ ne kila tɛɛle keŋ nɛ, ɔ gyuu Lida donɔɔ man ɔ kaa kɛɛ Kristotena baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ.

english_verse: There he met a man named Aeneas, who for eight years had been sick in bed and could not move.
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ ke naa balee ŋolo na ɔ yele yɛna Anease ŋon ɔ wose pou ba yem nɛ. Ɔ doowɔ dofoŋoŋ dɔɔ kulutooneŋ gyanaara keŋ ɔ bɛɛ tale koro ya.

english_verse: Peter said to Aeneas, “Jesus Christ has healed you! Get up and make up your bed.” At once he stood up.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Pita baake e nyiaa, “Anease kɛɛ, Yesu Kristo te kyɔ neŋ n kawee. Koro seŋ na n ke bɔ n dofoŋoŋ!” Debokenaŋ man te Anease korowɔ seŋ.

english_verse: Many people in the towns of Lydda and Sharon saw Aeneas and became followers of the Lord. Peter Brings Dorcas Back to Life
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ pou be kyaa Lida na Sarɔn kena Anease dɔŋɛɛ nɛ, be lɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu nombia ŋan di te ba bese ɔ silala.

english_verse: In Joppa there was a follower named Tabitha. Her Greek name was Dorcas, which means “deer.” She was always doing good things for people and had given much to the poor.
translation_verse: Na alo ŋolo mɔ kyaa Gyopa keŋ ŋon mɔ yɛɛ Yesu silale e te ɔ yele yɛna Tabita. Giriki dei man na baa baake e nyi Dɔkase keŋ nawolo nyi ŋaa. Debaŋ kamasɛ na alo kei ne yɛɛ deeli fa balaŋ, te ɔ ne kyɔ ayematena mɔ nideli.

english_verse: But she got sick and died, and her body was washed and placed in an upstairs room.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ man nɛ, ɔ gyoo kawee te ɔ yeŋawɔ. Te be sɔɔ e teeli abansoro deni daale man.

english_verse: Joppa wasn't far from Lydda, and the followers heard that Peter was there. They sent two men to say to him, “Please come with us as quickly as you can!”
translation_verse: Lida na Gyopa donɔɔse taŋ lem na dɔŋa ya. Te kaseela baŋ be kyaa Gyopa kenyii nyi Pita kyaa Lida nɛ, be kpila tewulɛɛ bala gyu ɔ gyaŋ nyi, “Pita, waaseda, yɛɛ bileŋ na n kekɔŋ de gyaŋ.”

english_verse: At once, Peter went with them. The men took Peter upstairs into the room. Many widows were there crying. They showed him the coats and clothes that Dorcas had made while she was still alive.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Pita korowɔ sila wɔ. Be ka gyoo botɔɔ nɛ te ba moo e gyu deni adidote keŋ man. Te liala baŋ pou kaa yilaawɔ kilisi Pita bee wii, na bɛɛ mo kegbase na abɔɔ ŋan Tabita be ŋare fa wɔ pɛte ɔ ba yem nɛ wola e.

english_verse: After Peter had sent everyone out of the room, he knelt down and prayed. Then he turned to the body of Dorcas and said, “Tabitha, get up!” The woman opened her eyes, and when she saw Peter, she sat up.
translation_verse: Keŋte Pita lese be popou lee deni keŋ man, te ɔ ŋmiŋawɔ ɔ doona dɔɔ te ɔ fanewɔ. Te ɔ besenawɔ kɛɛ woo ŋon te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Tabita, koro!” Te woo ŋon gyuusu ɔ sia, te ɔ kena Pita nɛ, ɔ korowɔ kyaa.

english_verse: He took her by the hand and helped her to her feet. Peter called in the widows and the other followers and showed them that Dorcas had been raised from death.
translation_verse: Keŋte Pita kyaŋa e ɔ nyiŋmaa man kyɔ e te ɔ korowɔ seŋ. Te ɔ baake Yesu silala baŋ na liala baŋ, te ɔ moo Dɔkase do wɔ be nyiŋmaa man na ɔ delena.

english_verse: Everyone in Joppa heard what had happened, and many of them put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Nombia kɛŋa yaaseewɔ kila Gyopa keŋ pou, te balaŋ burum kaa lɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu di.

english_verse: Peter stayed on for a while in Joppa in the house of a man named Simon, who made leather.
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle na Pita dii weeya burum Gyopa donɔɔ man botɔɔ. Na ɔ kyae Simɔn ŋon ɔ ne yɛɛ wonembia tɔna wose man tom nɛ gyaŋ.

english_verse: In Caesarea there was a man named Cornelius, who was the captain of a group of soldiers called “The Italian Unit.”
translation_verse: Balee ŋolo kyaawɔ Kaesaria donɔɔ man na ɔ yele yɛna Kɔnelio. Ɔ yɛɛ Itali tɛɛle yoonɔɔtena dikpii siamante e. Na yoonɔɔtena sɔŋonoŋ doo la ɔ tɛɛ.

english_verse: Cornelius was a very religious man. He worshiped God, and so did everyone else who lived in his house. He had given a lot of money to the poor and was always praying to God.
translation_verse: Ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ ba mo ɔ wose fa Wurubuarɛ, te ŋon na ɔ dekpaŋalaŋmantena pou ne yee Wurubuarɛ nideli. Ɔ dana wɔe fa Gyudatena ayematena te ɔ ne fane fa Wurubuarɛ debaŋ kamasɛ.

english_verse: One afternoon at about three o'clock, Cornelius had a vision. He saw an angel from God coming to him and calling him by name.
translation_verse: Wee daale balinɔɔ weese debaŋ atoorote nɛ, ɔ naa kolo na ɔ sia ŋgba dosɛɛ man nɛ. Ɔ naa Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋolo ta lee adido kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ te ɔ baake e nyi, “Kɔnelio.”

english_verse: Cornelius was surprised and stared at the angel. Then he asked, “What is this all about?” The angel answered, “God has heard your prayers and knows about your gifts to the poor.
translation_verse: Te Kɔnelio kɛɛ e yididi gyakoloŋ man te ɔ bɔɔse e nyi, “Me Gbeŋgyoo weera?” Keŋte Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon yako e nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ te lɛɛ n kefane. Ɔ te naa mena keŋ n ne yɛɛ wɔe fa ayematena baŋ, te ŋe ta gyoo ɔ sia.

english_verse: Now send some men to Joppa for a man named Simon Peter.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ kpila balaŋ gyu Gyopa donɔɔ keŋ man, be kaa baake walaŋ ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Simɔn yaa Pita nɛ.

english_verse: He is staying with Simon the leather maker, who lives in a house near the sea.”
translation_verse: Ɔ kyaa Simɔn ŋon ɔ ne yɛɛ wonembia tɔna wose man tom nɛ dɛɛ. Ɔ dɛɛ kyaa ɛpo nɔɔman.”

english_verse: After saying this, the angel left. Cornelius called in two of his servants and one of his soldiers who worshiped God.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon kekɔŋ kaa kolosi fa e taŋ te ɔ be lam nɛ, Kɔnelio baake ɔ dekpaŋalaŋ man tewulɛɛ bala, na yoonɔɔtena baŋ bee gyee e nɛ man ŋolo, ŋon ɔ benaa na e te ɔ ne yee Wurubuarɛ nɛ.

english_verse: He explained everything to them and sent them off to Joppa.
translation_verse: Te ɔ yako wɔ kpene keŋ pou be kɔŋ nɛ, te ɔ kpila wɔ Gyopa donɔɔ man.

english_verse: The next day about noon these men were coming near Joppa. Peter went up on the roof of the house to pray
translation_verse: Tɛɛ kena weeseteŋteŋ keŋ be dɔŋ bee gyuu te be ka benaa Gyopa donɔɔ keŋ nɛ, debaŋ kenaŋ te Pita mɔ gyeŋawɔ laŋ abansoro dɔɔ ɔ kaa fane.

english_verse: and became very hungry. While the food was being prepared, he fell sound asleep and had a vision.
translation_verse: Tanam kyaŋa e te ɔ gyae kolo waa di, mɔna debaŋ keŋ bɛɛ saa weenɛɛ ŋan nɛ, ɔ naa kolo na ɔ sia ŋgba dosɛɛ man nɛ.

english_verse: He saw heaven open, and something came down like a huge sheet held up by its four corners.
translation_verse: Ɔ naawɔ ŋgba adido ba toro na, te ɔ naa kolo ŋgba kanyaŋ dinaa daale nɛ, na be dana ke nɔɔsebia anaara ŋan man lee adido, te be tisinaa ke kaa see tɛɛle man.

english_verse: A voice said to him, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ nyii nɔɔwoya ŋaale nyi, “Pita, koro na n ka ko di.”

english_verse: But Peter said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never eaten anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
translation_verse: Te Pita yakowɔ nyi, “Koa, dee gyu lem me Gbeŋgyoo, nawolo nyi me te dii ta kolo kum yaa kpene keŋ dɛɛ kɔle nɛ see ya.”

english_verse: The voice spoke to him again, “When God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.”
translation_verse: Te nɔɔwoya ŋan besewɔ yako e bela nyi, “Kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ be lɛɛ do nyi ke dei nɛ, na baake ke nyi kolo anekɔlaŋ bela ya.”

english_verse: This happened three times before the sheet was suddenly taken back to heaven.
translation_verse: Kei yɛɛwɔ mena betabotooro. Te debokenaŋ man ba moo kanyaŋ keŋ laŋ adido bela.

english_verse: Peter was still wondering what all this meant, when the men sent by Cornelius came and stood at the gate. They had found their way to Simon's house
translation_verse: Pita kekyaa ɔ na gyueŋ nombia ŋan ɔ be naa nɛ wose man nɛ, na Kɔnelio kpilala baŋ mɔ te bɔɔse gyu kena dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man Simɔn kyaa, te be gyuuwɔ ka seŋ ka denanɔɔ man.

english_verse: While Peter was still thinking about the vision, the Holy Spirit said to him, “Three men are here looking for you.
translation_verse: Na Pita sɛɛ ɔ na gyueŋ nombia ŋan ɔ be naa nɛ wose man, te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ yako e nyiaa, “Simɔn, nyii kɛbo, tewulɛɛ batooro baale ne gyae neŋ.

english_verse: Peter went down and said to the men, “I am the one you are looking for. Why have you come?”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Pita tisiwɔ gyu balaŋ baŋ gyaŋ te ɔ ke yako wɔ nyiaa, “Maŋ yɛna walaŋ ŋon ɛ ne kɛo nɛ. Ɛ keta gyakoloŋ?”

english_verse: They answered, “Captain Cornelius sent us. He is a good man who worships God and is liked by the Jewish people. One of God's holy angels told Cornelius to send for you, so he could hear what you have to say.”
translation_verse: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “De gbeŋgyoo yoonɔɔtena dikpii siamante ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Konelio nɛ kpila na daa n gyaŋ. Ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne yee Wurubuarɛ, te ŋon na e mɔ nsana dei, te Gyudatena pou ne bu e. Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋolo kɔŋala kaa yako e nyi, waa kpila kaa mo neŋ kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ, na waa nyii kpene keŋ n dana fa e.”

english_verse: Peter invited them to spend the night. The next morning, Peter and some of the Lord's followers in Joppa left with the men who had come from Cornelius.
translation_verse: Te Pita moo balaŋ baŋ gyu ɔ dɛɛ, te ɔ yela be doowɔ ɔ gyaŋ. Tɛɛ kena keŋ nɛ, Pita desina ɔ wose te ŋon na wɔ pou laŋawɔ. Ɔ tebia Kristotena baale mɔ leewɔ Gyopa sila wɔ gyu.

english_verse: The next day they arrived in Caesarea where Cornelius was waiting for them. He had also invited his relatives and close friends.
translation_verse: Tɛɛ kena te ba gyoo Kaesaria, na Kɔnelio ne kɛɛ be gbɛɛ mena dɔɔ ɔ yilaa ɔ loŋtona na ɔ gyoona kpaakpaa nyibaŋ.

english_verse: When Peter arrived, Cornelius greeted him. Then he knelt at Peter's feet and started worshiping him.
translation_verse: Pita ka gyoo dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man nɛ, Kɔnelio kaa gyaŋeewɔ na e te ɔ tisiwɔ buŋ ɔ nawɔɔtɛɛ obuo man.

english_verse: But Peter took hold of him and said, “Stand up! I am nothing more than a human.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Pita yako e nyi “Koro, na yɛɛ mena ya. Nawolo nyi maŋ mɔ yɛɛ deniwalaŋ e ŋgba nyaŋ nɛ.”

english_verse: As Peter entered the house, he was still talking with Cornelius. Many people were there,
translation_verse: Te ŋon na Pita moo afa keyɔɔ mo gyoo dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man, te Pita ke naa balaŋ burum baŋ pou bɔɔ yilaa botɔɔ nɛ.

english_verse: and Peter said to them, “You know that we Jews are not allowed to have anything to do with other people. But God has shown me that he doesn't think anyone is unclean or unfit.
translation_verse: Te ɔ yako wɔ nyi, “Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ gyeŋ nideli nyi nafɔ daa Gyudatena ɔsom kɛɛ wolo man na, gbɛɛ be doo keŋ nyi Gyuda baale ka mo ɔ wose do na walaŋ ŋon ɔ te yɛɛ Gyuda baale e ya, yaa waa gyu ɔ gyaŋ ya. Mɔna Wurubuarɛ ta lese wola maŋ nyi, me na kɛɛ walaŋ ŋolo nyi ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ yakaate e ya.

english_verse: I agreed to come here, but I want to know why you sent for me.”
translation_verse: Lee mena dɔɔ n ke kpila baake maŋ na, ma te bɛɛ nyi mɔɔ kɔŋ ya. Mɛɛ gyae maa nyii kpene keŋ dɔɔ n be kpili baake maŋ.”

english_verse: Cornelius answered: Four days ago at about three o'clock in the afternoon I was praying at home. Suddenly a man in bright clothes stood in front of me.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Kɔnelio tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Weeya anaara yaa ŋgba me kyaa me dɛɛ mɛɛ fane balinɔɔ weese ŋgba debaŋ atoorote man nɛ, debokenaŋ man te baale ŋolo kaa bɛɛwɔ lee me dɔɔ. Na ɔ doona abɔɔ ŋan ne ŋalakee nɛ, te ɔ kaa seŋawɔ me siaman.

english_verse: He said, “Cornelius, God has heard your prayers, and he knows about your gifts to the poor.
translation_verse: Te ɔ yako maŋ nyi, ‘Kɔnelio, Wurubuarɛ te nyii n kefane te ɔ ne tɔɔse n wɔe keŋ n dana fa ayematena nɛ, mena dɔɔ

english_verse: Now send to Joppa for Simon Peter. He is visiting in the home of Simon the leather maker, who lives near the sea.”
translation_verse: kpila gyu Gyopa donɔɔ man ke baake Simɔn Pita, ɔ kyaa Simɔn ŋon ɔ ne yɛɛ wonembia tɔna wose man tom te ɔ dekpaŋalaŋ kyaa ɛpo nɔɔman nɛ gyaŋ.’

english_verse: I sent for you at once, and you have been good enough to come. All of us are here in the presence of the Lord God, so that we can hear what he has to say.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ te me kpila balaŋ n gyaŋ debokenaŋ man. N yɛɛ ke nideli ŋgba n be kɔŋ nɛ. Nɛnɛɛ de popou te kaa gyaŋee kɛbo Wurubuarɛ siaman dɛɛ daa neŋ, na dee nyii nɔɔwoya ŋan de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ kpa nya yako daa nɛ.” Pita kekolosi

english_verse: God is pleased with everyone who worships him and does right, no matter what nation they come from.
translation_verse: Mɔna walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne yee e te ɔ ne yɛɛ abɔɔ ŋan doo na ŋe gbɛɛ na, ɔ ne lɛɛ e do. Ɔ be famenɛ na ɔtenate dalee ya.

english_verse: This is the same message that God gave to the people of Israel, when he sent Jesus Christ, the Lord of all, to offer peace to them.
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ gbagba gyeŋ nɔɔwoya kpaakpaa ŋan Wurubuarɛ ba mo fa Iseraetena nɛ. Ɔ yako wɔ nyi baa sila Kristo ŋon ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ kamasɛ Gbeŋgyoo e nɛ, na baŋ na ŋon Wurubuarɛ nsana ke de na baa nyiŋ wosefɛɛreŋ.

english_verse: You surely know what happened everywhere in Judea. It all began in Galilee after John had told everyone to be baptized.
translation_verse: Ɛ gyeŋ nombii dinaa keŋ be kɔŋ Iserae tɛɛle dɔɔ, keŋ be fiasɛɛ lee Galelia tɛɛle man debaŋ keŋ Gyɔn be kolosi lee Wurubuarɛ loŋkesɔ keŋ wose man nombia ŋan wɔle nɛ.

english_verse: God gave the Holy Spirit and power to Jesus from Nazareth. He was with Jesus, as he went around doing good and healing everyone who was under the power of the devil.
translation_verse: Ɛ gyeŋ mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo ɔ feliŋ na ɔ doŋ keŋ do Yesu Nasarɛte baale ŋon man, mena dɔɔ Yesu tɛɛwɔ yɛɛ deeli te ɔ kyɔɔ baŋ pou ɔbɔnsam be di be dɔɔ nɛ kaweese. Ɔ yɛɛ kɛŋa pou nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ kpuɛ na e.

english_verse: We all saw what Jesus did both in Israel and in the city of Jerusalem. Jesus was put to death on a cross.
translation_verse: Te dee di adansɛɛ lee kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ Gyudatena tɛɛle man na Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ. Be gyɔɔ e sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ ko.

english_verse: But three days later, God raised him to life and let him be seen.
translation_verse: Mɔna weeya atoorote keŋ man nɛ, Wurubuarɛ gyuusu e lee yeŋ man, te ɔ yela ɔ lese ɔ wose wola balaŋ.

english_verse: Not everyone saw him. He was seen only by us, who ate and drank with him after he was raised from death. We were the ones God chose to tell others about him.
translation_verse: Na balaŋ pou naa na e ya, mɔna daa baŋ Wurubuarɛ be wulaa lese nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ wa adansɛɛtena nɛ, daa daageŋ naa na e. Wurubuarɛ ke gyuusu e lee yeŋ man mɔ nɛ, daa na e diiwɔ te de nyɔɔwɔ.

english_verse: God told us to announce clearly to the people that Jesus is the one he has chosen to judge the living and the dead.
translation_verse: Te ɔ yako daa nyi dee kolosi ɔ nombia fa balaŋ na dee di adansɛɛ lee ɔ wose man nyi, ŋon yɛna walaŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ ba lese nyi, waa di balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ yekee na baŋ be kyaa na be sia nɛ pou nombia.

english_verse: Every one of the prophets has said that all who have faith in Jesus will have their sins forgiven in his name.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ pou kolosiwɔ lee ɔ wose man nyi, walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon waa lɛɛ e di na, Wurubuarɛ waa mo ɔ nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ e lee Yesu yele man.” Wurubuarɛ ta mo ɔ feliŋ do balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ man

english_verse: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit took control of everyone who was listening.
translation_verse: Pita kegyakaa ɔ ne kolosi nɛ, te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon kaa gyoo baŋ pou bee tei e nɛ man.

english_verse: Some Jewish followers of the Lord had come with Peter, and they were surprised that the Holy Spirit had been given to Gentiles.
translation_verse: Te Gyudatena baŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ Yesu di te bɔɔ sila Pita kɔŋ nɛ, wose gbirisi wɔ ŋgba Wurubuarɛ ba mo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya mɔ man nɛ.

english_verse: Now they were hearing Gentiles speaking unknown languages and praising God. Peter said,
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi be nyiiwɔ bee kolosi deise kpookpoo, na bɛɛ lese Wurubuarɛ yele. Keŋte Pita yako wɔ nyiaa,

english_verse: “These Gentiles have been given the Holy Spirit, just as we have! I am certain that no one would dare stop us from baptizing them.”
translation_verse: “Wurubuarɛ ta mo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do balaŋ kɛwɔ mɔ man, ŋgba mena keŋ keŋ ɔ ba mo do de man nɛ. Ŋolo waa tale tɔ wɔ gbɛɛ nyi be na sɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ yee?”

english_verse: Peter ordered them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ, and they asked him to stay on for a few days.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɔ yela be sɔɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ lee Yesu Kristo yele man. Te ba sola e nyi, waa kyaa be gyaŋ weeya ala.

english_verse: The apostles and the followers in Judea heard that Gentiles had accepted God's message.
translation_verse: Yesu kpilala na Kristotena baŋ be kyaa Gyudia tɛɛle keŋ dɔɔ nyiiwɔ nyi, balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ mɔ te lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan do.

english_verse: So when Peter came to Jerusalem, some of the Jewish followers started arguing with him. They wanted Gentile followers to be circumcised, and
translation_verse: Pita ke gyu ka gyoo Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, Gyudatena baŋ ba seŋɛɛ nyi Yesu silala pou baa kara be baala wose nɛ te sɛɛ e ya.

english_verse: they said, “You stayed in the homes of Gentiles, and you even ate with them!”
translation_verse: Be bɔɔse e nyi, “Weera te n gyuuwɔ balaŋ baŋ ba te kara be baala wose ya nɛ gyaŋ, te n ke diiwɔ bom na wɔ?”

english_verse: Then Peter told them exactly what had happened:
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Pita lese kpene keŋ be kɔŋ nɛ man dokoloŋ dokoloŋ fa wɔ nyiaa,

english_verse: I was in the town of Joppa and was praying when I fell sound asleep and had a vision. I saw heaven open, and something like a huge sheet held by its four corners came down to me.
translation_verse: “Na me kyaa Gyopa donɔɔ man mɛɛ fane. Keŋte me naa kolo ŋgba kanyaŋ dinaa daale nɛ na me sia ŋgba dosɛɛ man nɛ, keŋ be dana ke nɔɔbia anaara ŋan man te be tisinaa ke lee adido kaa see me siaman.

english_verse: I heard a voice saying to me, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
translation_verse: Te me nyii nɔɔwoya ŋaale nyi, ‘Pita, koro na n ka ko di.’

english_verse: But I said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never taken a bite of anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
translation_verse: Te me yakowɔ nyiaa, ‘Me Gbeŋgyoo koa, dee gyu lem, kolo kum yaa kpene keŋ dɛɛ kɔle nɛ te tinaa ta me nɔɔ see ya.’

english_verse: The voice from heaven spoke to me again, “When God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.”
translation_verse: Te nɔɔwoya ŋan besewɔ yako man lee adido bolate nyiaa, ‘Kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ be lɛɛ do nyi ke dei nɛ, na baake ke nyi kolo anekɔlaŋ ya.’

english_verse: This happened three times before it was all taken back into heaven.
translation_verse: Ke yɛɛwɔ mena betabotooro te be wɔŋa woni keŋ pou laŋ adido bela.

english_verse: Suddenly three men from Caesarea stood in front of the house where I was staying.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ na baala batooro baŋ Konelio be kpili lee Kaesaria kɔŋ me gyaŋ nɛ, te kaa sem dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man me kyaa nɛ.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit told me to go with them and not to worry. Then six of the Lord's followers went with me to the home of a man
translation_verse: Te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ yako maŋ nyi, me na lɛɛ ma wose aŋmaareŋ ya, maa sila wɔ gyu. Me tebia looro kɛwɔ sila maŋ lee Gyopa gyu Kaesaria te de popou gyuuwɔ ka gyoo Konelio dɛɛ.

english_verse: who told us that an angel had appeared to him. The angel had ordered him to send to Joppa for someone named Simon Peter.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔ yako daa mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ kpilale be kaa bɛɛ lee ɔ dɔɔ ɔ dekpaŋalaŋ man, te ɔ yako e nyi kpila gyu Gyopa ke baake balee ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Simɔn Pita nɛ.

english_verse: Then Peter would tell him how he and everyone in his house could be saved.
translation_verse: Waa kɔŋ kaa yako ŋon nombia kpaakpaa ŋan gyae ke yela ɛmɛɛ na n dekpaŋalaŋmantena pou ke nyiŋ nyeekelɛɛ.

english_verse: After I started speaking, the Holy Spirit was given to them, just as the Spirit had been given to us at the beginning.
translation_verse: Me ke gyina me nɔɔ mee kolosi pɛ, te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon kɔŋawɔ kaa gyoo be man, ŋgba mena keŋ ba gyoo de man kewalaŋ keŋ nɛ.

english_verse: I remembered that the Lord had said, “John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te me tɔɔse de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu nɔɔwoya ŋan ɔ be yako nyi, ‘Loŋ te Gyɔn moowɔ sɔ balaŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ, mɔna Wurubuarɛ waa mo ɔ feliŋ ŋon bo sɔ ŋon Wurubuarɛ loŋ.’

english_verse: God gave those Gentiles the same gift that he gave us when we put our faith in the Lord Jesus Christ. So how could I have gone against God?
translation_verse: Mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo ɔ feliŋ do de man debaŋ keŋ man dɔɔ lɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo di nɛ, mena ke yaa ɔ moowɔ do baŋ mɔ man. Kenaŋ na, maŋ yɛna amɔte ŋon maa tale tɔ Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛ.”

english_verse: When they heard Peter say this, they stopped arguing and started praising God. They said, “God has now let Gentiles turn to him, and he has given life to them!” The Church in Antioch
translation_verse: Be ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ be tinaa nombia ŋan kewɔŋee yela te ba lese Wurubuarɛ yele nyi, “Nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ, Wurubuarɛ te fa balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ mɔ gbɛɛ nyi, baa kyɛɛkee lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man na baa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ.” Kristotena dikpii keŋ kyaa Antiɔke nɛ

english_verse: Some of the Lord's followers had been scattered because of the terrible trouble that started when Stephen was killed. They went as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch, but they told the message only to the Jews.
translation_verse: Be ka ko Sitifin wɔle nɛ, baŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ Kristo di nɛ yaaseewɔ lee mena keŋ bɛɛ naase wɔ diyem dɔɔ. Baale gyuuwɔ naŋ Tunisia na Saiporɔse tɛɛle man, na Antiɔke donɔɔ man bɛɛ yako Yesu nombia ŋan fa Gyudatena baageŋ.

english_verse: Some of the followers from Cyprus and Cyrene went to Antioch and started telling Gentiles the good news about the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Mɔna baŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ Kristo di nɛ man baale baŋ bɔɔ lee Saiporɔse na Kirene nɛ, gyuu Antiɔke ke yako de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu wose man nombia kpaakpaa ŋan fa balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ.

english_verse: The Lord's power was with them, and many people turned to the Lord and put their faith in him.
translation_verse: De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ seŋawɔ be wɔle te balaŋ burum lɛɛ nombia ŋan di te be kyɛɛkeewɔ lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man, te be kɔŋawɔ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu gyaŋ.

english_verse: News of what was happening reached the church in Jerusalem. Then they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ Kristotena baŋ be kyaa Gyerusalɛm be nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, be kpila Banabase gyu Antiɔke.

english_verse: When Barnabas got there and saw how God had blessed them with undeserved grace, he was very glad. So he begged them to remain faithful to the Lord with all their hearts.
translation_verse: Ɔ ke gyu ka gyoo botɔɔ te ɔ kena mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele wɔ nɛ, yɛɛ e gyoŋ te ɔ doo be popou kakyeŋ nyi baa mo be konɔɔ pou sila de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu.

english_verse: Barnabas was a good man of great faith, and he was filled with the Holy Spirit. Many more people turned to the Lord.
translation_verse: Banabase yɛɛ walaŋ kpaakpaate ŋon ɔ be lɛɛ Kristo nombia ŋan di, te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ doo ɔ man nideli. De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ moo balaŋ burum kaa kpu na Kristotena dikpii keŋ.

english_verse: Barnabas went to Tarsus to look for Saul.
translation_verse: Te Banabase gyuu Tasuse ɔ kaa kɛo Sɔɔl.

english_verse: He found Saul and brought him to Antioch, where they met with the church for a whole year and taught many of its people. There in Antioch the Lord's followers were first called Christians.
translation_verse: Ɔ kena e nɛ ɔ moo e kɔŋ Antiɔke. Kulutoo dibiliŋ pou na Banabase na Sɔɔl ne kyaa gyaŋee Yesu kaseela dikpii dinaa keŋ na bɛɛ wola wɔ. Antiɔke botɔɔ te be taŋgbɛɛwɔ baake Yesu silala baŋ nyi Kristotena.

english_verse: During this time some prophets from Jerusalem came to Antioch.
translation_verse: Weeya ŋenaŋ man te Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baale lee Gyerusalɛm gyu Antiɔke.

english_verse: One of them was Agabus. Then with the help of the Spirit, he told that there would be a terrible famine everywhere in the world. And it happened when Claudius was Emperor.
translation_verse: Keŋte be man ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e Agabuse nɛ korowɔ seŋ te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ tɛɛwɔ ɔ dɔɔ yako nombia see nyiaa, tanam dinaa daale gyae kekɔŋ tɛɛle keŋ pou dɔɔ.

english_verse: So they appointed Barnabas and Saul to take their gifts to the church leaders in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Te ampaŋ be yɛɛwɔ mena. Ba moo abɔɔ ŋan bɔɔ faa nɛ do Banabase na Sɔɔl nyiŋmaa man, te ba moowɔ gyina kegyiise baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ.

english_verse: At that time King Herod caused terrible suffering for some members of the church.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ man te gyoo Herode fiasɛɛ ɔ ne naase Kristotena baŋ baale diyem.

english_verse: He ordered soldiers to cut off the head of James, the brother of John.
translation_verse: Ɔ yela ba moo sila ko Gyɔn ɔ kegyia Gyemisi.

english_verse: He put Peter in jail and ordered four squads of soldiers to guard him. Herod planned to put him on trial in public after the festival.
translation_verse: Ɔ ke kyaŋ Pita do deni man wɔle nɛ, ɔ moo e do yoonɔɔtena akpeŋ anaara nyiŋmaa man nyi baa gyee e. Yoonɔɔtena banaara doo la dekpeŋ kamasɛ man. Herode yɛɛ ke nyi akpaa be di ba nyeekelɛɛwee dinaa keŋ taŋ nɛ, na waa di ɔ nombia balaŋ nsana.

english_verse: While Peter was being kept in jail, the church never stopped praying to God for him. Peter Is Rescued
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɔ yela Pita doowɔ deni man botɔɔ, mɔna Kristotena dikpii keŋ ne fane nideli fa Wurubuarɛ ɔ dɔɔ. Wurubuarɛ kpilale kalese Pita lee deni man

english_verse: The night before Peter was to be put on trial, he was asleep and bound by two chains. A soldier was guarding him on each side, and two other soldiers were guarding the entrance to the jail.
translation_verse: Nelim keŋ pɛ tɛɛ kena, na gyoo Herode ka lese Pita lee deni man na baa di ɔ nombia nɛ, na ɔ doo yoonɔɔtena bala nsana keŋ bɔɔ do e agbaraagbaraase ala. Debaŋ kenaŋ na yoonɔɔtena baŋ bee gyee denanɔɔ keŋ mɔ seŋɛɛ be daseŋae man.

english_verse: Suddenly an angel from the Lord appeared, and light flashed around in the cell. The angel poked Peter in the side and woke him up. Then he said, “Quick! Get up!” The chains fell off his hands,
translation_verse: Karatetee dokoloŋ na te de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋolo bɛɛwɔ lee botɔɔ te deni keŋ man ŋmaŋeewɔ. Te ɔ kyaŋa Pita ɔ degbaŋgbaŋ dɔɔ wosi e te ɔ gyuusu e yako e nyi, “Koro bileŋ.” Debokenaŋ man te agbaraagbaraase ŋan leewɔ ɔ nyiŋmaase man yala.

english_verse: and the angel said, “Get dressed and put on your sandals.” Peter did what he was told. Then the angel said, “Now put on your coat and follow me.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon yako e nyiaa, “Mo nya abɔɔ do na n ka do nyaŋ nkyokotase.” Te Pita mɔ yɛɛwɔ mena te kpilale ŋon yako e bela nyi, mo n kegba wurikyim keŋ do na n ke sila maŋ.

english_verse: They went past the two groups of soldiers, and when they came to the iron gate to the city, it opened by itself. They went out and were going along the street, when all at once the angel disappeared.
translation_verse: Be tɛɛwɔ gbaa balaŋ gyaŋgba nyibaŋ baŋ bee gyee botɔɔ nɛ, na baŋ be gyakaa be dɔɔ nɛ, kaboena be ke lii nyeele denanɔɔ keŋ na mo lee donɔɔ keŋ man nɛ gyaŋ. Be kelii botɔɔ na, te nyeele denanɔɔ keŋ torowɔ fa wɔ ka wose dɔɔ, te ba leewɔ laŋ. Ba ke ta bee gyuu na, te kpilale ŋon ŋaalaawɔ yela Pita karatetee dokoloŋ.

english_verse: Peter now realized what had happened, and he said, “I am certain that the Lord sent his angel to rescue me from Herod and from everything the Jewish leaders planned to do to me.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Pita sia ŋmaŋee e te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Nɛnɛɛ, me ta gyem nyi de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ kpila na ɔ kpilale nyi ɔ kaa lese maŋ lee gyoo Herode nyiŋmaa man, na ŋan pou nafɔ Gyudatena be bake see nyi baa yɛɛ maŋ nɛ.”

english_verse: Then Peter went to the house of Mary the mother of John whose other name was Mark. Many of the Lord's followers had come together there and were praying.
translation_verse: Pita kena mena nombia kɛŋa nɛ, te ɔ gyuu Gyɔn Maake ɔ naa Mɛɛre dɛɛ. Debaŋ kenaŋ na balaŋ burum te yilaa botɔɔ bɛɛ fane.

english_verse: Peter knocked on the gate, and a servant named Rhoda came to answer.
translation_verse: Pita ke gyɔ be disim keŋ te alebu ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Roda nɛ, gyuuwɔ ke kɛɛ nyi amɔte ne gyɔɔ laa.

english_verse: When she heard Peter's voice, she was too excited to open the gate. She ran back into the house and said Peter was standing there.
translation_verse: Ɔ ke gyu kenyii woya ŋan te ɔ ba gyem nyi Pita woya ŋenaŋ nɛ, yɛɛ e gyoŋ nideli te ɔ woleewɔ nyi waa toro e denanɔɔ keŋ. Mena dɔɔ ɔ yeuwɔ keyako wɔ nyiaa, “Pita te kɔŋ ɔ seŋɛɛ denanɔɔ man!”

english_verse: “You are crazy!” everyone told her. But she kept saying it was Peter. Then they said, “It must be his angel.”
translation_verse: Te be bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Yaa n na gyoo diteŋ bo?” Mɔna ɔ yase ɔ sia yako wɔ nyi yɛɛ ampaŋ. Keŋte be yakowɔ nyi gyae ke yɛɛ ɔ woo bo.

english_verse: But Peter kept on knocking, until finally they opened the gate. They saw him and were completely amazed.
translation_verse: Na Pita gyakaa bo ɔ ne gyɔ denanɔɔ keŋ. Nɛnɛɛ be ka toro denanɔɔ keŋ na Pita seŋɛɛ laa, mena dɔɔ ba wose gbirisi wɔ.

english_verse: Peter motioned for them to keep quiet. Then he told how the Lord had led him out of jail. He also said, “Tell James and the others what has happened.” After that, he left and went somewhere else.
translation_verse: Te Pita gyina ɔ nyiŋmaa adido mo yako wɔ nyi baa wu be nɔɔ. Te ɔ yako wɔ mena keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ ba lese e lee deni keŋ man. Te ɔ yako wɔ nyi, “Ɛ yako Gyemisi na Kristotena akaŋ baŋ mena nombia kɛŋa.” Te ɔ korowɔ laŋ yenaŋ wɔle.

english_verse: The next morning the soldiers who had been on guard were terribly worried and wondered what had happened to Peter.
translation_verse: Tɛɛ kena keŋ nɛ, te aŋmaareŋ dinaa daale gyoowɔ yoonɔɔtena baŋ nsana. Be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyi, “Aleŋ yaa Pita moowɔ baa?”

english_verse: Herod ordered his own soldiers to search for him, but they could not find him. Then he questioned the guards and had them put to death. After this, Herod left Judea to stay in Caesarea for a while. Herod Dies
translation_verse: Gyoo Herode yela be kɛo Pita nideli, mɔna ba te naa e ya. Mena dɔɔ ɔ baake yoonɔɔtena baŋ bɔɔ gyee e nɛ, nyii be nɔɔman nombia, te ɔ yela ba koo wɔ pou. Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, gyoo Herode leewɔ Gyudia tɛɛle man gyu kekyaa Kaesaria donɔɔ man kyomii. Gyoo Herode yeŋ

english_verse: Herod and the people of Tyre and Sidon were very angry with each other. But their country got its food supply from the region that he ruled. So a group of them went to see Blastus, who was one of Herod's high officials. They convinced Blastus that they wanted to make peace between their cities and Herod,
translation_verse: Gyoo Herode gyeŋa baŋ Taetena na Sidɔntena dɔɔ. Mena dɔɔ balaŋ benaŋ yɛɛ nɔɔ te be gyuuwɔ kena e. Be taŋgbɛɛwɔ mo nombia ŋenaŋ do Bilasituse nɔɔman nyi waa doo e teree fa wɔ, nawolo nyi donɔɔse ala ŋenaŋ pou ne nyiŋ be weenɛɛ bo lee gyoo ŋonaŋ tɛɛle man. Bilasituse yɛna gyoo ŋon dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ pou kegyia.

english_verse: and a day was set for them to meet with him. Herod came dressed in his royal robes. He sat down on his throne and made a speech.
translation_verse: Wee keŋ bɔɔ see kelii nɛ, te gyoo Herode moo ɔ gyooro abɔɔ do, te ɔ gyuuwɔ ka gyeŋ gyakaa ɔ gyooro gbeli keŋ dɔɔ te ɔ kolosiwɔ fa balaŋ baŋ pou.

english_verse: The people shouted, “You speak more like a god than a man!”
translation_verse: Te balaŋ baŋ pou fae yeesa yako nyi, “Kei dɔɔ te yɛɛ deniwalaŋ ne kolosi la ya, yɛɛ bane ŋolo e.”

english_verse: At once an angel from the Lord struck him down because he took the honor that belonged to God. Later, Herod was eaten by worms and died.
translation_verse: Debokenaŋ man te de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ kpilale kɔŋawɔ kaa gyɔ e do kawee, nawolo nyi ɔ ta lese Wurubuarɛ yele ya. Te gyoo ŋon wose pou gyoo feelɛɛ te ɔ yeŋawɔ.

english_verse: And after Barnabas and Saul had done the work they were sent to do, they went back to Jerusalem with John, whose other name was Mark.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ Banabase na Sɔɔl be taŋ ba tom keŋ nɛ, ba besewɔ lee Gyerusalɛm gyu Antiɔke. Be kee gyu keŋ nɛ, te ba moo Gyɔn Maake sila ba wose.

english_verse: While they were worshiping the Lord and going without eating, the Holy Spirit told them, “Appoint Barnabas and Saul to do the work for which I have chosen them.”
translation_verse: Wee daale be kɛɛ som Wurubuarɛ nɛ, be bake be nɔɔ bɛɛ fane te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ lese Banabase na Sɔɔl fa maŋ, na baa yɛɛ tom keŋ dɔɔ mɔɔ baake wɔ nɛ.”

english_verse: Everyone prayed and went without eating for a while longer. Next, they placed their hands on Barnabas and Saul to show that they had been appointed to do this work. Then everyone sent them on their way. Barnabas and Saul in Cyprus
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ba kebake be nɔɔ fane taŋ nɛ, ba moo be nyiŋmaase gyakaa be dɔɔ fane fa wɔ te be lɔɔ wɔ gbɛɛ.

english_verse: After Barnabas and Saul had been sent by the Holy Spirit, they went to Seleucia. From there they sailed to the island of Cyprus.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ke kpila Banabase na Sɔɔl nɛ, be gyuu Selusia. Botɔɔ te ba moo degbele dinaa mo loŋ dɔɔ gyu Saiporɔse tɛɛle keŋ loŋ be kilisi ke nɛ man.

english_verse: They arrived at Salamis and began to preach God's message in the synagogues. They also had John as a helper.
translation_verse: Be kelii Salamise nɛ be yako Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man. Na Gyɔn Maake mɔ kpuɛ na wɔ ɔ ne kyɔ wɔ.

english_verse: Barnabas and Saul went all the way to the city of Paphos on the other end of the island, where they met a Jewish man named Bar-Jesus. He practiced witchcraft and was a false prophet.
translation_verse: Be kila tɛɛle kenaŋ dɔɔ pou gbaa ke gyu ke lii Pafose donɔɔ man. Botɔɔ te ba ke naa ŋmeeseŋte ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Baa Yesu nɛ. Ɔ yɛɛ Gyuda baale e te ɔ moo ɔ wose yɛɛ ŋgba Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre nɛ.

english_verse: He also worked for Sergius Paulus, who was very smart and was the governor of the island. Sergius Paulus wanted to hear God's message, and he sent for Barnabas and Saul.
translation_verse: Na ɔ yɛɛ gyoo ŋon ɔ seŋɛɛ gyoo dinaa nawɔɔ man tɛɛle kenaŋ man nɛ ɔ dɔɔ e. Bɛɛ baake e nyi Segiuse Pɔluse. Na ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ gyeŋ nombia nideli. Ɔ baake Banabase na Sɔɔl ɔ gyaŋ, nawolo nyi na ɔ ne gyae waa nyii Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan.

english_verse: But Bar-Jesus, whose other name was Elymas, was against them. He even tried to keep the governor from having faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Mɔna ŋmeeseŋte ŋon ɔ yele Giriki dei man yɛna nyi Elimase nɛ, korowɔ seŋ tia Banabase na Sɔɔl. Ɔ gyaewɔ nyi waa lese gyoo ŋon agyueŋ lee Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan man, na ɔ be nyiŋ lɛɛ ŋa di ya.

english_verse: Then Saul, better known as Paul, was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked straight at Elymas
translation_verse: Keŋte Sɔɔl ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Pɔɔl te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ doo ɔ man nideli nɛ, kɛɛ ŋmeeseŋte ŋon diiŋ te ɔ yako e nyiaa,

english_verse: and said, “You son of the devil! You are a liar, a crook, and an enemy of everything that is right. When will you stop speaking against the true ways of the Lord?
translation_verse: “Nyaŋ ɔbɔnsam bu! N ne kɔla kpene kamasɛ keŋ dei, nyaŋ yɛna dekara na adao kumɛɛ pou nyee. Woromɔ te n ke tina de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan doo na ŋe gbɛɛ nɛ ketulii yela?

english_verse: The Lord is going to punish you by making you completely blind for a while.” Suddenly the man's eyes were covered by a dark mist, and he went around trying to get someone to lead him by the hand.
translation_verse: De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ waa lese ɔ doŋ wola neŋ. N sia gyae keyɛlɛɛ, te n be gyae n kena weese ya, kelii debaŋ daale.” Debokenaŋ man te ɔ sia yɛlɛɛwɔ, na ɔ dɔŋ ɔ ne lalaa ɔ ne gyae walaŋ ŋon waa kyaŋ e ɔ nyiŋmaa man wola e gbɛɛ.

english_verse: When the governor saw what had happened, he was amazed at this teaching about the Lord. So he put his faith in the Lord. Paul and Barnabas in Antioch of Pisidia
translation_verse: Gyoo kemaasa ŋon kena kpene keŋ be kɔŋ balee kei dɔɔ nɛ ɔ lɛɛ Yesu di, nawolo nyi ɔ nombia ŋan bɔɔ wolo nɛ yela ɔ wose gbirisi e. Pɔɔl na Banabase gyuu Antiɔke, donɔɔ keŋ ke kyaa Pisidia tɛɛle man nɛ

english_verse: Paul and the others left Paphos and sailed to Perga in Pamphylia. But John left them and went back to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl na ɔ tebia moowɔ loŋ dɔɔ lee Pafose kelii Pɛga keŋ kyaa Pamfilia tɛɛle man nɛ. Botɔɔ te Gyɔn Maake tinaa wɔ yela te ɔ besewɔ laŋ Gyerusalɛm.

english_verse: The rest of them went on from Perga to Antioch in Pisidia. Then on the Sabbath they went to the synagogue and sat down.
translation_verse: Be ka lee Pɛga nɛ, be gyuuwɔ kelii Antiɔke keŋ kyaa Pisidia tɛɛle man nɛ, te Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee keŋ kelii nɛ, be gyuu Gyudatena ɔsom deni man kekyaa.

english_verse: After the reading of the Law and the Prophets, the leaders sent someone over to tell Paul and Barnabas, “Friends, if you have anything to say that will help the people, please say it.”
translation_verse: Gyudatena baŋ kekala lee Mosesi mmaraa tɔne na akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra tɔna ŋan man taŋ nɛ, bɔ ɔsom deni man kegyiise baŋ yako Pɔɔl na ɔ tebia nyiaa, “De tebia, akpaa ɛ dana kolo ɛ ka mo do balaŋ baŋ kakyeŋ na, ɛ kolosi fa wɔ.”

english_verse: Paul got up. He motioned with his hand and said: People of Israel, and everyone else who worships God, listen!
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Pɔɔl korowɔ seŋ te ɔ gyinaa ɔ nyiŋmaa adido nyi baa yela kekolosi. Te ɔ fiasɛɛ ɔ ne kolosi nyiaa, “Iseraetena na ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ te yɛɛ Iseraetenawɔ ya, te ɛ ne yee Wurubuarɛ nɛ ɛ tei maŋ!

english_verse: The God of Israel chose our ancestors, and he let our people prosper while they were living in Egypt. Then with his mighty power he led them out,
translation_verse: Iseraetena Wurubuarɛ lese na de naanaɔ, te ɔ yela be nyiŋa ba wose debaŋ keŋ be kyaa Igyipite tɛɛle man nɛ. Wurubuarɛ moo ɔ doŋ dinaa keŋ lese wɔ lee tɛɛle kenaŋ man.

english_verse: Make sure what the prophets have said doesn't happen to you. They said,
translation_verse: Lee mena dɔɔ ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose man nideli, na nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra be ŋmarase see nɛ, be kɔŋ ɛ dɔɔ ya. Be ŋmarasewɔ nyiaa,

english_verse: He destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan and gave their land to our people.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ yela ɔ balaŋ dii debiise nyetooro dɔɔ Kaanan tɛɛle man, te ɔ lɛo be tɛɛlese fa wɔ kekpaakekpaa.

english_verse: years. Then God gave our people judges until the time of the prophet Samuel,
translation_verse: ). Kei wɔle nɛ, Wurubuarɛ faa wɔ nombiadiira kelii ɔ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Samuɛle dɔɔ.

english_verse: Later, God removed Saul and let David rule in his place. God said about him, “David the son of Jesse is the kind of person who pleases me most! He does everything I want him to do.”
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ ka lese Sɔɔl lee gyoori man nɛ, ɔ moo Defidi see gyoo. Wurubuarɛ dii adansɛɛ lee ɔ wose man nyiaa, “Me te nyiŋ Gyese bu Defidi ŋon mɛɛ gyae e lee me konɔɔ man. Ŋon ne gyae la waa yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ keŋ mɛɛ gyae.

english_verse: God promised that someone from David's family would come to save the people of Israel, and this one is Jesus.
translation_verse: Defidi kei duuluŋ man te Wurubuarɛ lese nyeelɛɛre Yesu, te ɔ kɔŋawɔ Iseraetena gyaŋ ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be yako see nɛ.

english_verse: But before Jesus came, John was telling everyone in Israel to turn back to God and be baptized.
translation_verse: Pɛte Yesu ke kɔŋ na, Gyɔn taŋ kekolosi fa Iseraetena pou nyi, baa kyɛɛkee lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man, na baa kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ na baa sɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ.

english_verse: Then, when John's work was almost done, he said, “Who do you people think I am? Do you think I am the Promised One? He will come later, and I am not good enough to untie his sandals.”
translation_verse: Gyɔn kɛɛ gyae waa taŋ ɔ tom nɛ ɔ bɔɔse balaŋ baŋ nyiaa, ‘Ɛmɛɛ gyeŋ nyi maŋ yɛna amɔte? Na maŋ yɛna walaŋ ŋon ɛ ne daa nɛ ya. Ɛ kɛɛ, ŋon ɛ ne daa nɛ dɔŋ me wɔle ɔ ne kɔŋ. Te maŋ Gyɔn be kaboena nyi maa buŋ booli ɔ nkyokota ŋmeeseŋ gba ya.’

english_verse: Now listen, you descendants of Abraham! Pay attention, all of you Gentiles who are here to worship God! Listen to this message about how to be saved, because it is for everyone.
translation_verse: Me tebia Aberaham duuluŋ man bia, na ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ te yɛɛ Iseraetenawɔ ya te ɛ ne yee Wurubuarɛ nɛ, daa te Wurubuarɛ yela ba moo nyeedoŋ nombia kɛŋa kɔna.

english_verse: The people of Jerusalem and their leaders didn't realize who Jesus was. And they didn't understand the words of the prophets they read each Sabbath. So they condemned Jesus just as the prophets had said.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi Gyerusalɛmtena, na be siamantena baŋ ta gyem nyi ŋon yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon ya. Be te nyii nɔɔwoya ŋan akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ be ŋmarase see, te bɛɛ kale ba kefɛɛfowee kamasɛ man nɛ ya. Mɔna be ke bu e fɔɔ keŋ nɛ, yela akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra nɔɔwoya ŋan bɔɔ ŋmarase see lee ɔ wose man nɛ kɔŋawɔ mena.

english_verse: They did exactly what the Scriptures said they would. Even though they couldn't find any reason to put Jesus to death, they still asked Pilate to have him killed. After Jesus had been put to death, he was taken down from the cross and placed in a tomb.
translation_verse: Ba ke yɛɛ ŋan pou bɔɔ ŋmarase lee ɔ wose man taŋ nɛ, ba lese e lee ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ dɔɔ ke wuu boe bɔɔ daale man.

english_verse: But God raised him from death!
translation_verse: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ gyuusu e lee yeŋ man.

english_verse: Then for many days Jesus appeared to his followers who had gone with him from Galilee to Jerusalem. Now they are telling our people about him.
translation_verse: Te ɔ lese ɔ wose wola balaŋ baŋ ŋon na wɔ ne kyaa ta lee Galelia gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ weeya burum man. Balaŋ baŋ ɔ ba lese ɔ wose wola wɔ nɛ, ne diina ɔ wose man adansɛɛ fa de balaŋ Iseraetena gyɛŋ nɛ.

english_verse: God raised Jesus from death and will never let his body decay. It is just as God said, “I will make to you the same holy promises that I made to David.”
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ besewɔ yako ɔ tɔne keŋ yenaŋ daale mena keŋ waa gyuusu Yesu lee yeŋ man na ɔ be doo yebɔɔ man dala ya nɛ nyi, ‘Maa mo me nyiŋmaa gyakaa ɛ dɔɔ ampaŋ ampaŋ, ŋgba mena keŋ mɔɔ yako see fa Defidi nɛ.’

english_verse: And in another psalm it says, “God will never let the body of his Holy One decay.”
translation_verse: Defidi nɔɔneŋ tɔne keŋ man yenaŋ daale na, ɔ besewɔ yako nyi, ‘N be gyae n ke yela n tobaale ŋon ayimɛɛ be doo ɔ wose man ya nɛ ke dala ya.’

english_verse: When David was alive, he obeyed God. Then after he died, he was buried in the family grave, and his body decayed.
translation_verse: Defidi ke kyaa na ɔ sia, te ɔ ka som Wurubuarɛ taŋ ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ ne gyae nɛ, ɔ moo ɔ kpaa teeli te ba moo e ke wuu ɔ naanaɔ gyaŋ te ɔ dalawɔ.

english_verse: But God raised Jesus from death, and his body did not decay.
translation_verse: Mɔna walaŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ be gyuusu e lee yeŋ man nɛ, ŋon te dale ya.

english_verse: My friends, the message is that Jesus can forgive your sins! The Law of Moses could not set you free from all your sins.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ me tebia, mɛɛ gyae ɛ ka gyeŋ nideli nyi Yesu adaworoma dɔɔ te dɛɛ yako ŋon mena keŋ walaŋ waa yɛɛ, na Wurubuarɛ ka mo ɔ dukum kyɛɛ e.

english_verse: “Look, you people who make fun of God! Be amazed and disappear. I will do something today that you won't believe, even if someone tells you about it!”
translation_verse: ‘Ɛ kɛɛ, ɛ mɛɛ baŋ ɛ ne kulu Wurubuarɛ nɛ, gyakoloŋ gyae ka gyoo ŋon, na ɛ ka yeŋ futaa ɛ wose. Nawolo nyi maa yɛɛ tom daale ɛ weeya kɛŋa man, keŋ nyi ɛ be gyae ɛ ke lɛɛdi ya. Akpaa ŋolo de yako ŋon koraŋ na, ɛ be gyae ɛ ke lɛɛdi ya.’ ”

english_verse: As Paul and Barnabas were leaving the synagogue, the people begged them to say more about these same things on the next Sabbath.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ debaŋ keŋ Pɔɔl na Banabase ba lee bɔ ɔsom deni keŋ man bee gyu keŋ nɛ, balaŋ baŋ waase wɔ nyi baa bese kɔŋ ba kefɛɛfowee anekɔŋaŋ, na be kaa yako wɔ mena nombia kɛŋa bela kpu.

english_verse: After the service, many Jews and a lot of Gentiles who worshiped God went with them. Paul and Barnabas begged them all to remain faithful to God, who had treated them with undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ ke yaasee taŋ keŋ nɛ, Gyudatena burum na balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya te bee yee Wurubuarɛ te bɔɔ mo ba wose do Gyudatena ɔsom keŋ man nɛ, sila Pɔɔl na Banabase. Te Pɔɔl na Banabase kolosiwɔ do wɔ kakyeŋ nyi, wee kamasɛ na baa tɔɔse Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ keŋ ɔ ba mo boele wɔ nɛ nideli na baa kyaa fa e.

english_verse: The next Sabbath almost everyone in town came to hear the message about the Lord.
translation_verse: Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee ka bese lii nɛ, donɔɔ keŋ pou kaa yilaawɔ nyi baa nyii de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ nombia.

english_verse: When the Jewish people saw the crowds, they were very jealous. They insulted Paul and spoke against everything he said.
translation_verse: Gyudatena kena balaŋ baŋ yaanɔɔle nɛ be sia yɛɛwɔ be dɔɔ, mena dɔɔ be lɛɛ Pɔɔl nombia ŋan ɔ ne yako nɛ aŋmaareŋ te ba tee e.

english_verse: But Paul and Barnabas bravely said: We had to tell God's message to you before we told it to anyone else. But you rejected the message! This proves that you don't deserve eternal life. Now we are going to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Te Pɔɔl na Banabase gyɔɔ be konɔɔ yako wɔ nyi, “Nafɔ kaboena nyi dɛɛ taŋgbɛɛ kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan bo fa ŋon pɛ, mɔna keŋ ɛ be bɛɛ ŋa te ɛ be kɛɛ ɛ wose nyi ɛ be kaboena nyi ɛ ke nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ dɔɔ ya nɛ, dɛɛ lee ɛ gyaŋ dee gyu baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ gyaŋ.

english_verse: The Lord has given us this command, “I have placed you here as a light for the Gentiles. You are to take the saving power of God to people everywhere on earth.”
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ be yako daa yɛna nyi, ‘Me ta mo neŋ yɛɛ keŋmaŋee fa balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ, na n ka wola wɔ gbɛɛ keŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ baa mo na baa nyiŋ nyeekelɛɛ nɛ.’

english_verse: This message made the Gentiles glad, and they praised what they had heard about the Lord. Everyone who had been chosen for eternal life then put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Mɔna balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, yɛɛ wɔ gyoŋ nideli te ba lese Wurubuarɛ yele lee ɔ nyeedoŋ nombia ŋan dɔɔ. Te baŋ Wurubuarɛ ba lese wɔ nyi baa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ nɛ, lɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu di.”

english_verse: The message about the Lord spread all over this region.
translation_verse: Te de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu nombia ŋan yaaseewɔ tɛɛle keŋ dɔɔ pou.

english_verse: But the Jewish leaders went to some of the important men in the town and to some respected women who were religious. They turned them against Paul and Barnabas and started making trouble for them. They even chased them out of this part of the country.
translation_verse: Mɔna Gyudatena baŋ feefuwɔ do kegyiise baŋ na ala baŋ bee yee Wurubuarɛ te ba wose dana obuo donɔɔ kenaŋ man nɛ, te be naase Pɔɔl na Banabase diyem, te be gegi wɔ lee tɛɛle keŋ man.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas shook the dust from that place off their feet and went on to the city of Iconium.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Kristo kpilala baŋ kpiisi be nawɔɔ tɛɛ aŋasa see wɔ na kawola nyi ba te bɛɛ wɔ, te ba lese be nae botɔɔ te be laŋa Ikonium.

english_verse: But the Lord's followers in Antioch were very happy and were filled with the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Mɔna Yesu silala baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ dɔɔ, yɛɛ wɔ gyoŋ nideli na Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon mɔ doo be man nideli.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas spoke in the synagogue in Iconium, just as they had done at Antioch, and many Jews and Gentiles put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl na Banabase ka gyoo Ikonium donɔɔ keŋ man nɛ, be gyuu Gyudatena ɔsom deni keŋ man ŋgba mena keŋ bɛɛ taŋ yɛɛ nɛ. Be kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan nideli, te Gyudatena na baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ burum lɛɛ Yesu di.

english_verse: But the Jews who did not have faith in him made the other Gentiles angry and turned them against the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Mɔna Gyudatena baŋ bɔɔ bɛɛ nyi bɔɔ lɛɛ Yesu di ya nɛ, feefuwɔ do baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ te ba korowɔ seŋ tia Pɔɔl na Banabase.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas stayed there for a while, having faith in the Lord and bravely speaking his message. The Lord gave them the power to work miracles and wonders, and he showed that their message about his gift of undeserved grace was true.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl na Banabase kyaawɔ botɔɔ weeya akalansɛɛ ŋaale, te be gyɔɔ be konɔɔ kolosi de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu nombia ŋan. Ŋon mɔ seŋawɔ be wɔle te ɔ moo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele wɔ, te be yɛɛ nombia dinaana na gyakoloŋ nombia botɔɔ wola nyi ɔ nombia ŋan bee kolosi nɛ doo na ŋe gbɛɛ.
