english_verse: The people of Iconium did not know what to think. Some of them believed the Jewish group, and others believed the apostles.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ donɔɔ keŋ man balaŋ kpasewɔ akpeŋ ala. Dekpeŋ daale seŋawɔ Gyudatena baŋ wɔle, te daale mɔ seŋawɔ Kristo kpilala baŋ wɔle.

english_verse: Finally, some Gentiles and Jews, together with their leaders, decided to make trouble for Paul and Barnabas and to stone them to death.
translation_verse: Keŋte Gyudatena na baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ, man baale na be siamantena yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ nyi, baa naase Pɔɔl na Banabase diyem na baa fuŋii wɔ boya.

english_verse: But when the two apostles found out what was happening, they escaped to the region of Lycaonia. They preached the good news there in the towns of Lystra and Derbe and in the nearby countryside. Paul and Barnabas in Lystra
translation_verse: Pɔɔl na Banabase ke nyii mena nombia kɛŋa nɛ, be yeuwɔ lee donɔɔ kenaŋ man gyu Listra na Dɛbi donɔɔse ŋan kyaa Likonia tɛɛle man nɛ, na donɔɔse ŋan benaa na botɔɔ nɛ pou kekolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia kpaakpaa ŋan. Ɔtakase ŋolo kawee kekyɔ

english_verse: In Lystra there was a man who had been born with paralyzed feet and had never been able to walk.
translation_verse: Be ke gyu kelii Listra donɔɔ keŋ man nɛ, na ɔtakase ŋolo kyaa botɔɔ keŋ nyi leenaŋ bɔɔ lola e na, ɔ te tale ta koro ta ya.

english_verse: The man was listening to Paul speak, when Paul saw that he had faith in Jesus and could be healed. So he looked straight at the man
translation_verse: Ɔ ke kyaa botɔɔ ɔ ne tei Pɔɔl kekolosi nɛ, Pɔɔl kɛɛ e yididi te ɔ naawɔ nyi ɔ kelɛɛdi gyae ke tale yela waa nyiŋ kawee kekyɔ,

english_verse: When the crowd saw what Paul had done, they yelled out in the language of Lycaonia, “The gods have turned into humans and have come down to us!”
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ kena kpene keŋ Pɔɔl be yɛɛ nɛ, be fae yeesa be dɛɛ Likonia dei man nyi, “Bɛɛneŋ besena na ba wose yɛɛ denibalaŋ kaa seŋ de gyaŋ yaa?”

english_verse: The people then gave Barnabas the name Zeus, and they gave Paul the name Hermes, because he did the talking.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ba moo be bɛɛneŋ yela do Banabase na Pɔɔl. Ba doo Banabase nyi Sewuse, te ba doo Pɔɔl mɔ nyi Hemise nawolo nyi ŋon yɛna be kolosire.

english_verse: The temple of Zeus was near the entrance to the city. Its priest and the crowds wanted to offer a sacrifice to Barnabas and Paul. So the priest brought some bulls and flowers to the city gates.
translation_verse: Na be bane ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Sewuse nɛ deni seŋɛɛ donɔɔ keŋ nɔɔman. Te bane Sewuse sae ŋon moo nunkyuese na kyeŋkyeŋkɔɔse kaa seŋ donɔɔ keŋ nɔɔman, nawolo nyi ŋon na balaŋ dikpii keŋ ne gyae baa fa Pɔɔl na Banabase kedi ŋgba bɛɛneŋ nɛ.

english_verse: When the two apostles found out about this, they tore their clothes in horror and ran to the crowd, shouting:
translation_verse: Mɔna Banabase na Pɔɔl ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, be kyaŋa ba kegbase tirii na kawola nyi nombia ŋan te takaa wɔ, te ba moo yeesa yeu karatetee dokoloŋ kagyoo balaŋ dikpii burum baŋ nsana nyi,

english_verse: In times past, God let each nation go its own way.
translation_verse: Gyaŋgba keŋ na, ɔ yela balaŋ yɛɛ kpene keŋ baŋ gbagba ne gyae.

english_verse: But he showed he was there by the good things he did. God sends rain from heaven and makes your crops grow. He gives food to you and makes your hearts glad.
translation_verse: Mɔna ɔ na lese ɔ wose wola nyi ɔ kyaa ampaŋ, lee deeli keŋ ɔ ne yɛɛ nɛ dɔɔ, te ɔ ne fa ŋon ɔga na ɛ wɔɔse man weenɛɛ debaŋ kpaakpaate man. Ɔ ne fa ŋon weenɛɛ te ɔ ne yeli ɛ sia na gye.”

english_verse: Even after Paul and Barnabas had said all this, they could hardly keep the people from offering a sacrifice to them.
translation_verse: Kristo kpilala baŋ ke yako kɛŋa koraŋ na, yɛɛ wɔ doŋ pɛte be talewɔ tɔ balaŋ baŋ gbɛɛ nyi be na fa wɔ kedi ya.

english_verse: Some Jewish leaders from Antioch and Iconium came and turned the crowds against Paul. They hit him with stones and dragged him out of the city, thinking he was dead.
translation_verse: Gyudatena baale lee Antiɔke keŋ kyaa Pisidia tɛɛle man nɛ, na Ikonium kɔŋ botɔɔ kaa wɔlɛɛ balaŋ baŋ agyueŋ te ba seŋawɔ be wɔle. Keŋte be fuŋii Pɔɔl boya, te be tuu e na tɛɛle lee donɔɔ keŋ man kelɔ donɔɔ nɔɔman. Na baŋ gyeŋ nyi ɔ ta yem.

english_verse: But when the Lord's followers gathered around Paul, he stood up and went back into the city. The next day he and Barnabas went to Derbe. Paul and Barnabas Return to Antioch in Syria
translation_verse: Mɔna Yesu silala baŋ kekɔŋ kaa yilaa ɔ gyaŋ nɛ, ɔ korowɔ bese gyu donɔɔ keŋ man. Ke tɛɛ kena na te ŋon na Banabase laŋa Dɛbi donɔɔ man. Pɔɔl na Banabase besewɔ gyu Antiɔke keŋ kyaa Siria tɛɛle man nɛ

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas preached the good news in Derbe and won some people to the Lord. Then they went back to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch in Pisidia.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl na Banabase kekolosi Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan Dɛbi donɔɔ man nɛ, balaŋ burum kɔŋawɔ kaa lɛɛ Yesu di te ba bese ɔ silala, Keŋte ba besewɔ gyu Listra na Ikonium na Antiɔke keŋ kyaa Pisidia nɛ.

english_verse: They encouraged the followers and begged them to remain faithful. They told them, “We have to suffer a lot before we can get into God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Be kolosiwɔ fa Kristotena baŋ te ba doo wɔ kakyeŋ nyi Kristo nombia ŋan bɔɔ lɛɛ di nɛ, baa kyaŋ ŋa keŋkeŋ. Ba besewɔ do wɔ kakyeŋ nyi, “Kaboena nyi dɛɛ naa diyem nideli pɛna dɛɛ tale gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man.”

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas chose some leaders for each of the churches. Then they went without eating and prayed that the Lord would take good care of these leaders who had trusted in the Lord.
translation_verse: Kristotena dikpii kamasɛ man na, Pɔɔl na Banabase na lese siamantena fa wɔ. Ba lese wɔ taŋ na, bɛɛ bake be nɔɔ fane fa wɔ, na be ta mo be popou do de Gbeŋgyoo Kristo ŋon bɔɔ lɛɛ e di nɛ nyiŋmaa man.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas went on through Pisidia to Pamphylia,
translation_verse: Ba ke ta kila Pisidia tɛɛle dɔɔ taŋ nɛ, be gyuu Pamfilia tɛɛle man.

english_verse: where they preached in the town of Perga. Then they went down to Attalia
translation_verse: Be kolosi Kristo nombia ŋan Pɛga donɔɔ man. Be ka lee botɔɔ na, te be gyuuwɔ Atalia donɔɔ man.

english_verse: and sailed to Antioch in Syria. It was there that they had been placed in God's care for the work they had now completed.
translation_verse: Be ka lee botɔɔ mɔ nɛ, ba moowɔ loŋ dɔɔ bese kɔŋ Antiɔke. Antiɔke botɔɔ te Kristotena baŋ fanewɔ mo wɔ do Wurubuarɛ nyiŋmaa man nyi, waa mo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele wɔ na baa gyu ke yɛɛ tom keŋ bɔɔ yɛɛ taŋ nɛ.

english_verse: After arriving in Antioch, they called the church together. They told the people what God had helped them do and how he had made it possible for the Gentiles to believe.
translation_verse: Ba ke kɔŋ kaa gyoo botɔɔ nɛ, be yilaa Kristotena dikpii keŋ pou te be yako wɔ tom keŋ pou Wurubuarɛ be tɛɛ be dɔɔ yɛɛ, na mena keŋ ɔ ba toro gbɛɛ fa balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya, te baŋ mɔ be lɛɛ Kristo nombia ŋan di nɛ.

english_verse: Then they stayed there with the followers for a long time.
translation_verse: Lee kenaŋ wɔle na, be kyaawɔ be tebia Kristotena baŋ gyaŋ weeya burum.

english_verse: Some people came from Judea and started teaching the Lord's followers that they could not be saved, unless they were circumcised as Moses had taught.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baale leewɔ Gyudia tɛɛle man kɔŋ Antiɔke donɔɔ man bɛɛ wola Kristotena baŋ nyi, “Nyi akpaa ɛ te kara ɛ baala wose ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan ɔ be yela Mosesi be ŋmarase see nɛ nawolo ya na, Wurubuarɛ be gyae waa tale lɛɛ ɛ nyee ya.”

english_verse: This caused trouble, and Paul and Barnabas argued with them about this teaching. So it was decided to send Paul and Barnabas and a few others to Jerusalem to discuss this problem with the apostles and the church leaders. The Church Leaders Meet in Jerusalem
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ aŋmaareŋ dinaa daale gyoo balaŋ benaŋ na Pɔɔl na Banabase nsana. Keŋte Kristotena baŋ lese Pɔɔl na Banabase na Antiɔke Kristotena baŋ baale te be kpila wɔ Gyerusalɛm na be kaa naa Kristo kpilala baŋ na ɔsom kegyiise baŋ lee nombii kei dɔɔ.

english_verse: The men who were sent by the church went through Phoenicia and Samaria, telling how the Gentiles had turned to God. This news made the Lord's followers very happy.
translation_verse: Kristotena baŋ ke lɔ wɔ gbɛɛ taŋ nɛ, ba moowɔ Fonisia na Samaria tɛɛlese man. Te be yako be tebia Kristotena baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ, mena keŋ balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ ke lɛɛ Yesu nombia ŋan di. Kristotena baŋ nyii nombia kɛŋa na, yɛɛ wɔ gyoŋ nideli.

english_verse: When the men arrived in Jerusalem, they were welcomed by the church, including the apostles and the leaders. They told them everything God had helped them do.
translation_verse: Be ke gyu ka gyoo Gyerusalɛm nɛ, Kristo kpilala baŋ na ɔsom kegyiise na Kristotena dikpii keŋ pou lɛɛ wɔ nideli, te baŋ mɔ yako wɔ kpene kamasɛ keŋ Wurubuarɛ be tɛɛ be dɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ.

english_verse: But some Pharisees had become followers of the Lord. They stood up and said, “Gentiles who have faith in the Lord must be circumcised and told to obey the Law of Moses.”
translation_verse: Te balaŋ baŋ nafɔ be doo Farasiitena dikpii keŋ man te nɛnɛɛ bɔɔ lɛɛ Yesu di nɛ, man baale korowɔ seŋ te be yako wɔ nyi “Kaboena nyi baa yela baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ, ke kara be baala wose na baa di Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan ɔ be yeli Mosesi be ŋmarase see nɛ dɔɔ.”

english_verse: The apostles and church leaders met to discuss this problem about Gentiles.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Kristo kpilala baŋ, na ɔsom kegyiise baŋ gyaŋeewɔ na baa gyoo nombia ŋenaŋ man.

english_verse: They had talked it over for a long time, when Peter got up and said: My friends, you know that God decided long ago to let me be the one from your group to preach the good news to the Gentiles. God did this so they would hear and obey him.
translation_verse: Ba ke kyɔɔle dɔŋa gyu siaman nɛ, Pita korowɔ seŋ te ɔ yako wɔ nyi, “Me tebia, ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ gyeŋ nyi leenaŋ kewalaŋ keŋ, te Wurubuarɛ lese maŋ nyi maa kolosi Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan fa balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ, na baa nyiŋ nyii na baa lɛɛ Yesu di.

english_verse: He knows what is in everyone's heart. And he showed he had chosen the Gentiles, when he gave them the Holy Spirit, just as he had given his Spirit to us.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ gyeŋ walaŋ kamasɛ konɔɔ man nombia nɛ, lesewɔ wola nyi ɔ te lɛɛ wɔ do lee ɔ feliŋ ŋon ɔ ba mo do be man, ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ ba mo do daa mɔ man nɛ dɔɔ.

english_verse: God treated them in the same way that he treated us. They put their faith in him, and he made their hearts pure.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ te kɛɛ wɔ nyi be yɛɛ balaŋ kpoo lee de man ya, mɔna ɔ moo be nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ lee keŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ Kristo di dɔɔ.

english_verse: Now why are you trying to make God angry by placing a heavy burden on these followers? This burden was too heavy for us or our ancestors.
translation_verse: Nɛnɛɛ weera te ɛ ne gyae ɛ ke kɛɛse Wurubuarɛ kɛɛ, keŋ ɛ ne gyae ɛ ka mo kasolɔ keŋ daa na de naanaɔ te tale seele ya nɛ, seele baŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ Yesu di nɛ?

english_verse: But we believe that we will be saved by the gift of undeserved grace from our Lord Jesus Christ, just as the Gentiles are.
translation_verse: Ɛ na yeli ke kɔŋ mena ya! Dɛɛ lɛɛ di nyi de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu bɛrɛɛ keŋ dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ lɛɛ da nyee, ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be lɛɛ baŋ mɔ nɛ.”

english_verse: Everyone kept quiet and listened as Barnabas and Paul told how God had given them the power to work a lot of miracles and wonders for the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ gyaŋee nɛ, kyaawɔ yididi debaŋ keŋ bee tei Banabase na Pɔɔl ne yako wɔ mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ be tɛɛ be dɔɔ yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia na nombia dinaana balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya man nɛ.

english_verse: After they had finished speaking, James said: My friends, listen to me!
translation_verse: Ba kekolosi taŋ nɛ, te Gyemisi mɔ gyinaa ɔ nɔɔ nyi, “Ɛ tei me gyaŋ, me tebia.

english_verse: Simon Peter has told how God first came to the Gentiles and made some of them his own people.
translation_verse: Debokeiman te Simɔn Pita yako daa mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ be taŋgbɛɛ yɛɛ wola daa nyi ɔ ne gyae balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ, te ɔ lese be man baale mo yɛɛ ɔ wui.

english_verse: “I, the Lord, will return and rebuild David's fallen house. I will build it from its ruins and set it up again.
translation_verse: ‘Kei wɔle na, maa bese kɔŋ na maa bese ma Defidi dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ be yɛlɛɛ nɛ, na maa desina ka abɔɔ ŋan be wɔlɛɛ nɛ, na ke ka seŋ nideli.

english_verse: Then other nations will turn to me and be my chosen ones. I, the Lord, say this.
translation_verse: Denibalaŋ akaŋ baŋ na balaŋ baŋ pou ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya te mɔɔ lese wɔ yɛɛ me wui nɛ, baa kɛo maŋ be Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ ŋon.

english_verse: And so, my friends, I don't think we should place burdens on the Gentiles who are turning to God.
translation_verse: Gyemisi besewɔ gyina ɔ nɔɔwoya ŋan dɔɔ nyi, “Ma agyueŋ man na, ɛ na yeli dɛɛ mo nombia ŋaale ŋere balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya, te bɛɛ mo ba wose fa Wurubuarɛ nɛ ya.

english_verse: We should simply write and tell them not to eat anything that has been offered to idols. They should be told not to eat the meat of any animal that has been strangled or that still has blood in it. They must also not commit any terrible sexual sins.
translation_verse: Mɔna ɛ yela dɛɛ ŋmarase wɔ tɔne nyi, be na di bane dɛɛ weenɛɛ ya, na baa lese ba wose lee kafoŋ nombia man. Be na di wonembu ŋon ba te kara ɔ nyee ya te ɔ ba yem nɛ na wonembu fatabo ya.

english_verse: We must remember that the Law of Moses has been preached in city after city for many years, and every Sabbath it is read in our synagogues. A Letter to Gentiles Who Had Faith in the Lord
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi be wula bɛɛ kala Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan ɔ ba mo fa Mosesi nɛ Gyudatena ɔsom deni man kefɛɛfowee kamasɛ, te bee kolosi ɔ nombia ŋenaŋ donɔɔ kamasɛ mɔ man.” Tɔne keŋ bɔɔ ŋmarase yelee Kristotena baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ

english_verse: The apostles, the leaders, and all the church members decided to send some men to Antioch along with Paul and Barnabas. They chose Silas and Judas Barsabbas, who were two leaders of the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Keŋte Kristo kpilala baŋ na ɔsom kegyiise baŋ na Kristotena dikpii keŋ mantena akaŋ pou yɛɛ nɔɔ nyi baa lese be man balaŋ baale na baa kpu na Pɔɔl na Banabase, na baa kpila wɔ Antiɔke. Mena dɔɔ ba lese Gyuda ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Basabase nɛ, na Silase baŋ ba wose man dana obuo be man nɛ.

english_verse: They wrote a letter that said: We apostles and leaders send friendly greetings to all of you Gentiles who are followers of the Lord in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia.
translation_verse: Nɔɔwoya ŋan doo tɔne keŋ man yɛna nyi. “Daa Kristo kpilala na ɔsom kegyiise na ɛ tebia baŋ be kyaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, ne ŋmarasena tɔne kei dee yelee ɛmɛɛ Kristotena baŋ ɛ te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya, te ɛ kyaa Antiɔke na Siria na Silisia tɛɛlese man nɛ. Dɛɛ do ŋon nɔɔ.

english_verse: We have heard that some people from here have terribly upset you by what they said. But we did not send them!
translation_verse: De te nyii nyi balaŋ baale ta lee de man kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ, kaa mo nɔɔwoya ŋaale bɛɛ ŋere ŋon te bɛɛ do ŋon kaale, keŋ nyi na daa faa na wɔ gbɛɛ ya.

english_verse: So we met together and decided to choose some men and to send them to you along with our good friends Barnabas and Paul.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ de gyaŋeewɔ kolosi see nyi, dɛɛ lese balaŋ baale kpu na de gyoona kpaakpaa Banabase na Pɔɔl, na dee kpila wɔ ɛ gyaŋ.

english_verse: These men have risked their lives for our Lord Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: Be yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ lee de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo yele man dɔɔ, te ba moo ba wose fa yeŋ.

english_verse: We are also sending Judas and Silas, who will tell you in person the same things that we are writing.
translation_verse: Keŋ dɔɔ te dee kpila Gyudase na Silase mɔ, na baa mo be nɔɔ yako ŋon nombia ŋan dɔɔ ŋmarase do tɔne keŋ man nɛ.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit has shown us that we should not place any extra burden on you.
translation_verse: Daa na Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon te sɛɛ nyi dɔɔ gyae dɛɛ mo nombia ŋaale ŋere ŋon ya. Mɔna ŋan dɛɛ gyae ɛ ke yɛɛ yɛna nyi,

english_verse: But you should not eat anything offered to idols. You should not eat any meat that still has the blood in it or any meat of any animal that has been strangled. You must also not commit any terrible sexual sins. If you follow these instructions, you will do well. We send our best wishes.
translation_verse: ɛ na di bane dɛɛ weenɛɛ, yaa wonembu fatabo yaa wonembu ŋon ba te kara ɔ nyee te ɔ ba yem nɛ ya. Nɛnɛɛ mɔ ɛ lese ɛ wose lee kafoŋ nombia man. Akpaa ɛ lese ɛ wose lee kɛŋa pou man na, kpene kamasɛ gyae ke de fa ŋon. De nɔɔwoya ɔto yaa. Ɛ kyaa nideli.”

english_verse: The four men left Jerusalem and went to Antioch. Then they called the church members together and gave them the letter.
translation_verse: Be lɔɔ balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ kpili nɛ gbɛɛ, te be laŋa Antiɔke. Be ke gyu ka gyoo botɔɔ nɛ, be yilaa Kristotena dikpii keŋ pou te ba moo tɔne keŋ fa wɔ.

english_verse: When the letter was read, everyone was pleased and greatly encouraged.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ ke kala tɔne keŋ nɛ, yɛɛ wɔ gyoŋ lee mena keŋ bɔɔ do wɔ kakyeŋ nɛ dɔɔ.

english_verse: Judas and Silas were prophets, and they spoke a long time, encouraging and helping the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Gyudase na Silase baŋ mɔ be yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔrawɔ nɛ kolosiwɔ nideli do wɔ kakyeŋ na doŋ.

english_verse: The men from Jerusalem stayed on in Antioch for a while. And when they left to return to the ones who had sent them, the followers wished them well.
translation_verse: Be ke di weeya akalansɛɛ ŋaale nɛ, Kristotena baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ lɔɔ wɔ gbɛɛ wosefɛɛreŋ man te ba besewɔ laŋ balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ kpila wɔ nɛ gyaŋ.

english_verse: But Paul and Barnabas stayed on in Antioch, where they and many others taught and preached about the Lord. Paul and Barnabas Go Their Separate Ways
translation_verse: Pɔɔl na Banabase kyaawɔ Antiɔke kyare kyomii, te baŋ na balaŋ burum baale kolosi de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan fa balaŋ te ba wola wɔ mɔ. Pɔɔl na Banabase ketira dɔŋa

english_verse: Sometime later Paul said to Barnabas, “Let's go back and visit the Lord's followers in the cities where we preached his message. Then we will know how they are doing.”
translation_verse: Debaŋ daale wɔle nɛ, Pɔɔl yako Banabase nyi, “Yela dɛɛ bese gyu donɔɔ kamasɛ keŋ man dɔɔ gyu kekolosi de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu nombia ŋan nɛ, na de kaa kɛɛ balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ Kristo nombia ŋan di nɛ kekyaabii man.”

english_verse: Barnabas wanted to take along John, whose other name was Mark.
translation_verse: Nafɔ Banabase gyaewɔ nyi waa mo Gyɔn Maake kpu na ba wose.

english_verse: But Paul did not want to, because Mark had left them in Pamphylia and had stopped working with them.
translation_verse: Mɔna Pɔɔl kɛɛ ke nyi tekaboena nyi waa sila wɔ ya. Nawolo nyi ba ke kyaa Pamfilia nɛ, ɔ te yɛɛ tom na wɔ kelii ɔto ya, mɔna ɔ tinaa wɔ yela botɔɔ.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas argued, then each of them went his own way. Barnabas took Mark and sailed to Cyprus,
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ kewɔŋee dinaa daale gyoo ba nsana te be tira dɔŋa. Banabase moo Maake te ba moowɔ loŋ dɔɔ laŋ Saiporɔse.

english_verse: but Paul took Silas and left after the followers had placed them in God's care.
translation_verse: Te Pɔɔl mɔ moo Silase, te Kristotena baŋ fanewɔ nyi de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ waa mo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele wɔ te be lɔɔ wɔ gbɛɛ.

english_verse: They traveled through Syria and Cilicia, encouraging the churches.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl tɛɛwɔ Siria na Silisia tɛɛlese man do Kristotena baŋ kakyeŋ.

english_verse: Paul and Silas went back to Derbe and Lystra, where there was a follower named Timothy. His mother was also a follower. She was Jewish, and his father was Greek.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl gyuu Dɛbi donɔɔ man, te ɔ gyuu Listra donɔɔ man mɔ. Botɔɔ te ɔ ke naa Kristo baale ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Timoti nɛ. Ɔ naa yɛɛ Gyuda alo ŋon ɔ be lɛɛ Yesu di, mɔna ɔ kya yɛɛ Giriki baale e.

english_verse: The Lord's followers in Lystra and Iconium said good things about Timothy,
translation_verse: Kristotena baŋ pou be kyaa Listra na Ikonium donɔɔse man nɛ dii adansɛɛ kpaakpaate lee ɔ wose man.

english_verse: and Paul wanted him to go with them. But Paul first had him circumcised, because all the Jewish people around there knew that Timothy's father was Greek.
translation_verse: Na Pɔɔl ne gyae waa mo Timoti sila ɔ wose dɔɔ, ɔ yela be kara ɔ baala wose, nawolo nyi Gyudatena baŋ pou be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ gyeŋ nyi Timoti ɔ kya yɛɛ Giriki baale e.

english_verse: As Paul and the others went from city to city, they told the followers what the apostles and leaders in Jerusalem had decided, and they urged them to follow these instructions.
translation_verse: Ba ke ta bɛɛ lam donɔɔse ŋan man nɛ, be yako wɔ nombia ŋan Kristo kpilala na kegyiise baŋ be kyaa Gyerusalɛm be yako see nyi baa di ŋe dɔɔ nɛ.

english_verse: The churches became stronger in their faith, and each day more people put their faith in the Lord. Paul's Vision in Troas
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Kristotena baŋ seŋawɔ keŋkeŋ ba kelɛɛdi keŋ man nideli, na wee kamasɛ na balaŋ ne kaa kpuu na wɔ. Ba ke baake Pɔɔl gyu Masedonia tɛɛle man

english_verse: Paul and his friends went through Phrygia and Galatia, but the Holy Spirit would not let them preach in Asia.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl na ɔ tebia tɛɛwɔ ka mo na Firigia na Galatia tɛɛlese ŋan man, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon te fa wɔ gbɛɛ nyi baa gyu kekolosi nombia ŋan Esia tɛɛle man ya.

english_verse: After they arrived in Mysia, they tried to go into Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus would not let them.
translation_verse: Be ke gyu kelii Misia tɛɛle wulu man nɛ, be yɛɛ kakyeŋ nyi baa gbaa gyoo Bitinia tɛɛle man, mɔna Yesu feliŋ ŋon te fa wɔ gbɛɛ ya.

english_verse: So they went on through Mysia until they came to Troas.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ be gbaa Misia tɛɛle man tisi laŋ Torase donɔɔ man.

english_verse: During the night, Paul had a vision of someone from Macedonia who was standing there and begging him, “Come over to Macedonia and help us!”
translation_verse: Ke nelim na, te Pɔɔl naa kolo na ɔ sia ŋgba dosɛɛ man nɛ nyi, ɔ te naa balee ŋolo ta lee Masedonia tɛɛle man kɔŋ ɔ na sola e nyi, “Gbaa kɔŋ de gyaŋ Masedonia na n kaa kyɔ daa.”

english_verse: After Paul had seen the vision, we began looking for a way to go to Macedonia. We were sure that God had called us to preach the good news there. Lydia Becomes a Follower of the Lord
translation_verse: Pɔɔl kena ke pɛ, te da korowɔ desina da wose bileŋ na dee gyu Masedonia nawolo nyi, de lɛɛwɔ di nyi Wurubuarɛ baake na daa nyi dee gyu ke yako Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan fa balaŋ botɔɔ mɔ. Lidia kelɛɛ Yesu di

english_verse: We sailed straight from Troas to Samothrace, and the next day we arrived in Neapolis.
translation_verse: Da moo degbele dinaa lee Torase teeŋ gyu Samoterase. Tɛɛ naawɔ na te de gyuu Neyapolise donɔɔ man.

english_verse: From there we went to Philippi, which is a Roman colony in the first district of Macedonia. We spent several days in Philippi.
translation_verse: De ka lee botɔɔ na, te de gyuuwɔ Filipi donɔɔ man. Filipi donɔɔ kenaŋ yɛɛ Masedonia tɛɛle yenaŋ daale donɔɔ dinaa ke. Na Romantena kyaa donɔɔ kenaŋ man burum, te de kyaawɔ botɔɔ weeya akalansɛɛ ŋaale.

english_verse: Then on the Sabbath we went outside the city gate to a place by the river, where we thought there would be a Jewish meeting place for prayer. We sat down and talked with the women who came.
translation_verse: Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee kelii nɛ, te da leewɔ donɔɔ keŋ man gyu boo daale nɔɔman. Na da gyeŋ nyi dɛɛ naa Gyudatena dɛɛgyaŋee fane botɔɔ. De lii botɔɔ na te de tisiwɔ kyaa. Na ala baale te yilaa botɔɔ, te de fiasɛɛ dee kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia fa wɔ.

english_verse: One of them was Lydia, who was from the city of Thyatira and sold expensive purple cloth. She was a worshiper of the Lord God, and he made her willing to accept what Paul was saying.
translation_verse: Ala baŋ bɔɔ tei de nombia nɛ, man ŋolo yele yɛna Lidia. Ɔ lee Tiatira donɔɔ keŋ man te ɔ ne yɔɔ agoo kanyaŋse ŋaale, na ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon mɔ ɔ na som Wurubuarɛ nideli. Mena dɔɔ de Gbeŋgyoo toro wa agyueŋ, te ɔ lɛɛ nombia ŋan Pɔɔl ne yako nɛ do nideli.

english_verse: Then after she and her family were baptized, she kept on begging us, “If you think I really do have faith in the Lord, come stay in my home.” Finally, we accepted her invitation. Paul and Silas Are Put in Jail
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ be sɔɔ ŋon na ɔ dekpaŋalaŋmantena pou Wurubuarɛ loŋ. Te ɔ yako wɔ nyi, “Akpaa ɛ te lɛɛ maŋ do nyi ampaŋ ma te lɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu di na, kenaŋ na mɛɛ sola ŋon nyi ɛ kɔŋ kaa kyaa me dɛɛ.” Te ɔ waase daa nideli mo daa gyu ɔ dɛɛ. Ba kekyaŋ Pɔɔl na Silase tɔ deni

english_verse: One day on our way to the place of prayer, we were met by a slave girl. She had a spirit in her that gave her the power to tell the future. By doing this she made a lot of money for her owners.
translation_verse: Wee daale nɛ da korowɔ dee gyu botɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ fane nɛ, te de gyaŋee na alebubii ŋolo na ɔ yɛɛ balaŋ baale alebu e. Feliŋkum daale doo ɔ man, keŋ ne yeli ɔ ne tale gbala. Lee ɔ kegbala dɔɔ ɔ gbeŋgyoona baŋ nyiŋa kɔba nideli.

english_verse: The girl followed Paul and the rest of us, and she kept yelling, “These men are servants of the Most High God! They are telling you how to be saved.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te alebu kei sila daa na Pɔɔl na ɔ ne fae yeesa nyi, “Balaŋ kɛwɔ yɛna Wurubuarɛ adidote ŋon tewulɛɛ. Mena keŋ ɛ ke yɛɛ na Wurubuarɛ ke lɛɛ ɛ nyee nɛ yaa bɛɛ wola ŋon nɛ.”

english_verse: This went on for several days. Finally, Paul got so upset that he turned and said to the spirit, “In the name of Jesus Christ, I order you to leave this girl alone!” At once the evil spirit left her.
translation_verse: Ɔ yɛɛwɔ mena weeya burum te yɛɛ Pɔɔl ahiŋ te ɔ gɛɛsewɔ kɛɛ e, te ɔ faree e yako nyiaa, “Lee Yesu Kristo yele man, mɛɛ yako nyaŋ feliŋkum keŋ doo ɔ man nyi, lee ɔ man!” Debokenaŋ man te feliŋkum keŋ leewɔ ɔ man laŋ.

english_verse: When the girl's owners realized they had lost all chances for making more money, they grabbed Paul and Silas and dragged them into court.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ alebu ŋon gbeŋgyoona be naa nyi be kɔba kenyiŋ gbɛɛ te tɔɔ nɛ, te ba ke kyaŋa Pɔɔl na Silase te be wɔŋee wɔ gyu kegyiise dɛɛgyaŋee man balaŋ nsana.

english_verse: They are telling us to do things we Romans are not allowed to do.”
translation_verse: Bɛɛ wola abɔɔ ŋan na seŋ tia da mmaraase. Daa yɛɛ Romantenawɔ, dɔɔ gyae dɛɛ tale di ŋe dɔɔ ya.”

english_verse: The crowd joined in the attack on Paul and Silas. Then the officials tore the clothes off the two men and ordered them to be beaten with a whip.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ balaŋ dikpii keŋ pou kaa kyɔɔ wɔ te ba seŋawɔ tia wɔ. Kegyiise baŋ yela ba kpee Pɔɔl na Silase kegbase te be yela dambiise yee wɔ nideli.

english_verse: After they had been badly beaten, they were put in jail, and the jailer was told to guard them carefully.
translation_verse: Be ke tuku wɔ nideli taŋ nɛ ba moo wɔ do deni man, te be faree deni keŋ dɔɔ kɛɛre ŋon nyi waa tɔ deni keŋ nideli na bɔɔ nyiŋ lee ya.

english_verse: The jailer did as he was told. He put them deep inside the jail and chained their feet to heavy blocks of wood.
translation_verse: Deni keŋ dɔɔ kɛɛre ŋon kenyii nombia kɛŋa dɔɔ, ɔ moo wɔ do deni tɔɔmante keŋ man te ɔ gyɔɔ wɔ ɔpaŋ be nawɔɔ man.

english_verse: About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing praises to God, while the other prisoners listened.
translation_verse: Kelii tɛɛ nsana nɛ, Pɔɔl na Silase korowɔ bɛɛ fane na bee kpee nɔɔneŋ lese Wurubuarɛ yele. Na balaŋ akaŋ baŋ mɔ be doo deni man nɛ ne tei wɔ.

english_verse: Suddenly a strong earthquake shook the jail to its foundations. The doors opened, and the chains fell from all the prisoners.
translation_verse: Debokenaŋ man te tɛɛle wosiwɔ keŋkeŋ, te deni keŋ mokɔɔ wosiwɔ. Denɛɛ ŋan anaanɔɔse pou torowɔ, te be nawɔɔ leewɔ ɔpaŋ keŋ man.

english_verse: When the jailer woke up and saw that the doors were open, he thought that the prisoners had escaped. He pulled out his sword and was about to kill himself.
translation_verse: Deni keŋ dɔɔ kɛɛre ŋon kefoŋ nɛ, ɔ naawɔ nyi denɛɛ ŋan ta toro te ɔ wɔŋa ɔ sila lese nyi waa ko ɔ wose. Nawolo nyi ŋon gyeŋ nyi balaŋ baŋ pou be doo deni keŋ man nɛ ta lee yeu.

english_verse: But Paul shouted, “Don't harm yourself! No one has escaped.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Pɔɔl fae yeesa keŋkeŋ yako e nyi, “Na gooli n wose ya, de popou kyaa kɛbo!”

english_verse: The jailer asked for a torch and went into the jail. He was shaking all over as he knelt down in front of Paul and Silas.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ deni keŋ dɔɔ kɛɛre ŋon yela ba moo kanea kɔna e, te ɔ yeuwɔ gyoo deni keŋ man te ɔ ke ŋmiŋawɔ Pɔɔl na Silase nawɔɔ tɛɛ. Na ɔ ne seree kpakpakpakpa.

english_verse: After he had led them out of the jail, he asked, “What must I do to be saved?”
translation_verse: Te ɔ moo wɔ lee te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyi, “Ɛ waase da, woŋ te maa yɛɛ na Wurubuarɛ ke lɛɛ ma nyee?”

english_verse: They replied, “Have faith in the Lord Jesus and you will be saved! This is also true for everyone who lives in your home.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te be tiranɔɔ yako e nyi, “Lɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu di, na Wurubuarɛ ke lɛɛ ɛmɛɛ na n dekpaŋalaŋmantena pou nyee.”

english_verse: Then Paul and Silas told him and everyone else in his house about the Lord.
translation_verse: Te be kolosi de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan fa deni keŋ dɔɔ kɛɛre ŋon na ɔ dekpaŋalaŋmantena pou.

english_verse: While it was still night, the jailer took them to a place where he could wash their cuts and bruises. Then he and everyone in his home were baptized.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te deni keŋ dɔɔ kɛɛre ŋon moo Pɔɔl na Silase gyu ka nyekee be gyamɛɛ, te baŋ mɔ sɔɔ ŋon na ɔ dekpaŋalaŋmantena pou Wurubuarɛ loŋ.

english_verse: They were very glad they had put their faith in God. After this, the jailer took Paul and Silas to his home and gave them something to eat.
translation_verse: Ɔ moo wɔ gyu ɔ dɛɛ ke fa wɔ weenɛɛ. Yɛɛ ŋon na ɔ dekpaŋalaŋmantena pou gyoŋ, nawolo nyi ba te kaa lɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ di.

english_verse: The next morning the officials sent some police with orders for the jailer to let Paul and Silas go.
translation_verse: Tɛɛ kena te Romantena kegyiise baŋ kpila kotɔtɔɔtena kegyiise nyi, baa gyu keyako deni keŋ dɔɔ kɛɛre ŋon nyi, “Waa lese Pɔɔl na Silase na baa ta.”

english_verse: The jailer told Paul, “The officials have ordered me to set you free. Now you can leave in peace.”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ deni keŋ dɔɔ kɛɛre ŋon yako Pɔɔl nyi, “Kegyiise baŋ te kpila balaŋ kɔŋ nyi maa tina ŋon yela na ɛ ke ta, mena dɔɔ ɛ ke tale ta wosefɛɛreŋ man.”

english_verse: But Paul told the police, “We are Roman citizens, and the Roman officials had us beaten in public without giving us a trial. They threw us into jail. Now do they think they can secretly send us away? No, they cannot! They will have to come here themselves and let us out.”
translation_verse: Mɔna Pɔɔl yako kotɔtɔɔtena kegyiise baŋ nyi, “Be te di de nombia ya te be tuku daa balaŋ nsana te be kyaŋa daa ke tɔ deni, keŋ de yɛɛ Romantena gbagbawɔ. Te nɛnɛɛ bɛɛ gyae baa yo tina daa yela nyi dɛɛ ta. Koa, dɔɔ gyu ya, kegyiise baŋ gbagba baa kɔŋ na be kaa lese daa.”

english_verse: When the police told the officials that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens, the officials were afraid.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ kotɔtɔɔtena kegyiise baŋ besewɔ gyu ke yako nombia kɛŋa fa Romantena kegyiise baŋ. Te be kenyii nyi Pɔɔl na Silase yɛɛ Romantenawɔ nɛ, gyakoloŋ gyoo wɔ.

english_verse: So they came and apologized. They led them out of the jail and asked them to please leave town.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ be kɔŋawɔ be gyaŋ kaa doo wɔ teree, te ba lese wɔ lee deni keŋ man, te be waase wɔ nyi baa lee be donɔɔ keŋ man.

english_verse: But Paul and Silas went straight to the home of Lydia, where they saw the Lord's followers and encouraged them. Then they left.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl na Silase ka lee deni keŋ man nɛ, be gyuu alo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Lidia nɛ dɛɛ. Te ba ke naa Kristotena baŋ botɔɔ te be kolosiwɔ fa wɔ do wɔ kakyeŋ te be laŋawɔ.

english_verse: After Paul and his friends had traveled through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they went on to Thessalonica. A synagogue was in that city.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl na Silase moowɔ Anfipolise na Apolonia donɔɔse man te be gyuu Tesalonika donɔɔ man, na Gyudatena ɔsom deni kyaa botɔɔ.

english_verse: So as usual, Paul went there to worship, and on three Sabbaths he spoke to the people. He used the Scriptures
translation_verse: Pɔɔl gyuu Gyudatena ɔsom deni keŋ man, ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ ne taŋ yɛɛ nɛ. Pɔɔl na balaŋ benaŋ moo Gyudatena kefɛɛfoweeya atooro sɔɔŋ gyoo Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan man.

english_verse: to show them that the Messiah had to suffer, but that he would rise from death. Paul also told them that Jesus is the Messiah he was preaching about.
translation_verse: Na ɔ na lese nombia ŋan man wola wɔ nyi, “Kaboena nyi Kristo ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ waa naa diyem na waa foŋ lee yeŋ man. Te ɔ besewɔ yako nyi, Yesu ŋon nombia mɛɛ yako ŋon nɛ, ŋon yɛna Kristo nyeelɛɛre ŋon.”

english_verse: Some of them believed what Paul had said, and they became followers with Paul and Silas. Some Gentiles and many important women also believed the message.
translation_verse: Te Gyudatena baale agyueŋ kyɛɛkeewɔ te be lɛɛ Yesu di, te be sila Pɔɔl na Silase. Girikitena baŋ bee yee Wurubuarɛ na ala burum baale, baŋ ba wose man dana obuo nɛ mɔ lɛɛ nombia ŋan do.

english_verse: The Jewish leaders were jealous and got some troublemakers who hung around the marketplace to start a riot in the city. They wanted to drag Paul and Silas out to the mob, and so they went straight to Jason's home.
translation_verse: Mɔna Gyudatena baŋ ba te lɛɛ Yesu di ya nɛ, man baale sia yɛɛwɔ Pɔɔl na Silase dɔɔ, te ba korowɔ yilaa donɔɔ keŋ man aboŋtena, te be wosi donɔɔ keŋ ampaŋ ampaŋ. Mena dɔɔ be gyuu balee ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Gyasɔn nɛ dɛɛ bɛɛ kɛo Pɔɔl na Silase baa lese wɔ fa balaŋ dikpii keŋ.

english_verse: But when they did not find them there, they dragged out Jason and some of the Lord's followers. They took them to the city authorities and shouted, “Paul and Silas have been upsetting things everywhere. Now they have come here,
translation_verse: Keŋ ba te naa wɔ dɔɔ ya nɛ, be wɔŋee Gyasɔn na Kristotena baale gyu donɔɔ keŋ kegyiise gyaŋ na yeesa nyi, “Balaŋ kɛwɔ yɛɛ tɛɛleyɛlɛɛlawɔ. Ba de laŋae kamasɛ na bee loo nombia do, te nɛnɛɛ ba te kaa lii de gyaŋ kɛbo,

english_verse: and Jason has welcomed them into his home. All of them break the laws of the Roman Emperor by claiming that someone named Jesus is king.”
translation_verse: te Gyasɔn dɔɔ ŋon lɛɛ wɔ see ɔ dɛɛ. Bɛɛ wɔlɛɛ gyoo dinaa Kaesa mmaraase ŋan, te bɛɛ yako nyi gyoo ŋolo kyaa bela ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Yesu.”

english_verse: The officials and the people were upset when they heard this.
translation_verse: Donɔɔ keŋ mantena na kegyiise baŋ kenyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, ba nyonowɔ nideli.

english_verse: So they made Jason and the other followers pay bail before letting them go. People in Berea Welcome the Message
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ be yela Gyasɔn na balaŋ akaŋ baŋ tɔɔ kɔba daale mo seŋ be wɔle nyi, bɔɔ tekaa yɛɛ mena bela ya te be tina wɔ yela. Pɔɔl na Silase kegyu Beria donɔɔ man

english_verse: That same night the Lord's followers sent Paul and Silas on to Berea, and after they arrived, they went to the synagogue.
translation_verse: Tɛɛ ke yu pɛ te Kristotena baŋ moo Pɔɔl na Silase lee donɔɔ keŋ man, te be gyuu Beria donɔɔ man. Be ke gyu ka gyoo botɔɔ nɛ, be gyuuwɔ Gyudatena ɔsom deni keŋ man.

english_verse: The people in Berea were much nicer than those in Thessalonica, and they gladly accepted the message. Day after day they studied the Scriptures to see if these things were true.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ be kyaa Beria nɛ, gyeŋ nombia kela Tesalonikatena, nawolo nyi ba moo be konɔɔse pou lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan. Te bɛɛ gyoo Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan man wee kamasɛ, na bɛɛ kɛɛ nyi nombia ŋan Pɔɔl na wola wɔ nɛ yɛɛ ampaŋ?

english_verse: Many of them put their faith in the Lord, including some important Greek women and several men.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Gyudatena balaŋ burum lɛɛ Kristo nombia ŋan di. Te Girikitena ala baŋ ba wose man dana obuo na Girikitena baala burum mɔ lɛɛ Kristo nombia ŋan di.

english_verse: When the Jewish leaders in Thessalonica heard that Paul had been preaching God's message in Berea, they went there and caused trouble by turning the crowds against Paul.
translation_verse: Gyudatena baŋ be kyaa Tesalonika kenyii nyi Pɔɔl ne yako Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan Beria mɔ nɛ, be kɔŋawɔ kaa wosi botɔɔ wɔlɛɛ Pɔɔl na ɔ tebia fa wɔ.

english_verse: At once the followers sent Paul down to the coast, but Silas and Timothy stayed in Berea.
translation_verse: Debokenaŋ man te Kristotena baŋ lɔɔ Pɔɔl gbɛɛ te ɔ laŋa ɛpo nɔɔman. Mɔna Silase na Timoti dɔɔ baŋ dɔɔwɔ Beria botɔɔ.

english_verse: Some men went with Paul as far as Athens, and then returned with instructions for Silas and Timothy to join him as soon as possible. Paul in Athens
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ moo Pɔɔl ka tee nɛ, moo e kelii na Atɛnse donɔɔ man. Be ka bese bɛɛ kɔŋ Beria nɛ, Pɔɔl doo wɔ tiila nyi baa yako Silase na Timoti nyi be kaa tuŋ e bileŋ. Pɔɔl kyaa Atɛnse donɔɔ man

english_verse: While Paul was waiting in Athens, he was upset to see all the idols in the city.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl kɛɛ gyeki Silase na Timoti Atɛnse donɔɔ man nɛ, ɔ naawɔ nyi bɛɛneŋ te wulu donɔɔ keŋ man mena dɔɔ takaa e nideli.

english_verse: He went to the synagogue to speak to the Jews and to anyone who worshiped with them. Day after day he also spoke to everyone he met in the market.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɔ gyuu Gyudatena ɔsom deni keŋ man, na ŋon na Gyudatena na balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya te bee yee Wurubuarɛ nɛ, na gyoo Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan man nideli. Mena te wee kamasɛ mɔ, ɔ ne kolosi fa balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ kɔŋ botɔɔ keŋ balaŋ ne gyaŋee nɛ.

english_verse: Some of them were Epicureans and some were Stoics, and they started arguing with him. People were asking, “What is this know-it-all trying to say?” Some even said, “Paul must be preaching about foreign gods! That's what he means when he talks about Jesus and about people rising from death.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te wolala baale baŋ bɛɛ baake wɔ nyi Epikuritena na Setɔyikitena nɛ, na ŋon Pɔɔl wɔŋee nombia ŋaale. Pɔɔl kekolosi lee Yesu kabese foŋ lee yeŋ man nombia nɛ, balaŋ baale bɔɔsewɔ nyi “Na woŋ yaa nkpee kei ne yako baa? Te baale mɔ kpa, mberem ɔ ne kolosi bɛɛneŋ wɔlɛɛ baale nombia bo.”

english_verse: They brought Paul before a council called the Areopagus, and said, “Tell us what your new teaching is all about.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ba moo Pɔɔl gyu be dɛɛgyaŋee keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi Areopaguse nɛ, kegyiise siaman. Te be bɔɔse Pɔɔl nyi, “Nyaŋ ke tale lese n kawola wɔle kei man wola daa?

english_verse: We have heard you say some strange things, and we want to know what you mean.”
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi n nombia ŋan n na wolo nɛ, ta bese nombia wɔlɛɛ da deŋela man. Mena dɔɔ dɛɛ gyae nyi n ka lese ŋe man wola daa.”

english_verse: More than anything else the people of Athens and the foreigners living there loved to hear and to talk about anything new.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ be kyaa Atɛnse donɔɔ keŋ man na ŋɔɔla baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ, bɛɛ yɛɛ kolo kpu na nombia wɔlɛɛ kenyii na ŋa keyako ya.

english_verse: So Paul stood up in front of the council and said: People of Athens, I see that you are very religious.
translation_verse: Keŋte Pɔɔl korowɔ seŋ kegyiise baŋ be kyaa Areopaguse dɛɛgyaŋee keŋ siaman te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Atɛnsetena, ma te naa nyi gbɛɛ kamasɛ man na, ɛ dana yɔ ɔsom keŋ man nideli.

english_verse: As I was going through your city and looking at the things you worship, I found an altar with the words, “To an Unknown God.” You worship this God, but you don't really know him. So I want to tell you about him.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi ma keta mee kila donɔɔ keŋ na, me naa abɔɔ ŋan ɛ na som nɛ, te me naawɔ nyi ɛ te ŋmarase gyakaa ɛ kedeesa boe daale dɔɔ nyi, ‘Wurubuarɛ ŋon dɔɔ gyeŋ e ya nɛ kedeesa boe.’ Mena Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɛ ba gyeŋ e ya te ɛ na som e nɛ, ɔ nombia yaa mɛɛ gyae maa yako ŋon nɛ.

english_verse: This God made the world and everything in it. He is Lord of heaven and earth, and he doesn't live in temples built by human hands.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ be yɛɛ tɛɛle kei na kpene kamasɛ keŋ doo ke man nɛ, ŋon yɛna adido na ateta Gbeŋgyoo. Ɔ bɛɛ kyaa denɛɛ ŋan denibalaŋ be bam te bɛɛ som ŋe man nɛ man ya.

english_verse: From one person God made all nations who live on earth, and he decided when and where every nation would be.
translation_verse: Walaŋ dokoloŋ dɔɔ, te ɔ tɛɛwɔ yela debiise balaŋ pou kɔŋawɔ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ. Ŋon see na debaŋ, na botɔɔ keŋ walaŋ kamasɛ waa kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ.

english_verse: God has done all this, so that we will look for him and reach out and find him. He isn't far from any of us,
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ kɛŋa pou see na balaŋ kenyiŋ kɛo e na baa naa e. Mɔna ɔ be dana lem lee de man ŋolo gyaŋ ya.

english_verse: and he gives us the power to live, to move, and to be who we are. “We are his children,” just as some of your poets have said.
translation_verse: Ŋgba mena keŋ ŋolo be yako see nyi, ŋon dɔɔ te de dana nyeedoŋ te dɛɛ tale wosi da wose te de kyaa nɛ. Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ nɔɔneŋ kpeere ŋolo yako nombii kei nyi, daa mɔ de yɛɛ ɔ biawɔ.

english_verse: Since we are God's children, we must not think that he is like an idol made out of gold or silver or stone. He isn't like anything that humans have thought up and made.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ keŋ de yɛɛ ɔ biawɔ dɔɔ, de na kɛɛ nyi Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ ŋgba kɔba yayam, yaa nyeele wolom, yaa boe keŋ balaŋ ba mo ba agyueŋ na ba adao mo ke yɛɛ bane see nɛ ya.

english_verse: In the past, God forgave all this because people did not know what they were doing. But now he says that everyone everywhere must turn to him.
translation_verse: Gyaŋgba keŋ na Wurubuarɛ lese ɔ sia lee nombia ŋan balaŋ ba gyeŋ ya te bɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ dɔɔ. Mɔna nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ ɔ ne faree balaŋ pou yenaŋ kamasɛ nyi, baa kyɛɛkee lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man na baa kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ.

english_verse: He has set a day when he will judge the world's people with fairness. And he has chosen the man Jesus to do the judging for him. God has given proof of this to all of us by raising Jesus from death.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi ɔ ta lese wee daale see, keŋ waa ta walaŋ ŋon ɔ ba lese see nɛ dɔɔ, na waa di walaŋ kamasɛ nombia gbɛɛ kpaakpaate man. Mena keŋ balaŋ kenyiŋ gyeŋ nyi mena nombia kɛŋa yɛɛ ampaŋ dɔɔ, te ɔ gyuusu walaŋ ŋonaŋ lee yeŋ man.”

english_verse: As soon as the people heard Paul say a man had been raised from death, some of them started laughing. Others said, “We will hear you talk about this some other time.”
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ kenyii mena keŋ Pɔɔl ne kolosi lee yeŋ wɔle kefoŋ wose man nombia nɛ, be man baale kulu e te baale mɔ yako e nyi, “Dɛɛ gyae n ka bese yako daa mena nombia kɛŋa bela.”

english_verse: When Paul left the council meeting,
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Pɔɔl korowɔ lee balaŋ baŋ degyaŋee keŋ man.

english_verse: some of the men put their faith in the Lord and went with Paul. One of them was a council member named Dionysius. A woman named Damaris and several others also put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ baale seŋawɔ Pɔɔl wɔle te be lɛɛ Yesu di. Be man baale yɛna Dinisuse ŋon ɔ yɛɛ botɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ gyaŋee nɛ kegyia ŋolo e, na alo ŋon bɛɛ baake e Damarise nɛ, na be man balaŋ burum baale.

english_verse: Paul left Athens and went to Corinth,
translation_verse: Kei wɔle nɛ, Pɔɔl korowɔ lee Atɛnse donɔɔ keŋ man laŋ Korinto.

english_verse: where he met Aquila, a Jewish man from Pontus. Not long before this, Aquila had come from Italy with his wife Priscilla, because Emperor Claudius had ordered the Jewish people to leave Rome. Paul went to see Aquila and Priscilla
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ɔ ke naa Gyuda baale ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Akuila nɛ. Ɔ yɛɛ Pɔntuse baale e, te ŋon na ɔ wɛɛle Pirisila ka lee Itali kɔŋ Korinto donɔɔ man te kyare ta ya. Nawolo nyi gyoo dinaa Klaudiuse ta see mmaraa nyi, Gyudatena pou baa lee Roman donɔɔ keŋ man. Pɔɔl gyuuwɔ Akuila na ɔ wɛɛle gyaŋ Korinto botɔɔ.

english_verse: and found out they were tent makers. Paul was also a tent maker, so he stayed with them, and they worked together.
translation_verse: Te keŋ ŋon na wɔ pou tom yɛɛ dokoloŋ dɔɔ, ɔ kyaawɔ be gyaŋ te be yɛɛ tom bom. Ba tom keŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ yɛna nyi, kanyaŋse te bɛɛ mo yɛɛ denɛɛ yɔɔ.

english_verse: Every Sabbath, Paul went to the synagogue. He spoke to Jews and Gentiles and tried to win them over.
translation_verse: Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee kamasɛ nɛ, Pɔɔl ne gyu Gyudatena ɔsom deni man, na ŋon na wɔ ta gyoo Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan man nideli, na Gyudatena na Girikitena kenyiŋ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan di.

english_verse: But after Silas and Timothy came from Macedonia, he spent all his time preaching to the Jews about Jesus the Messiah.
translation_verse: Silase na Timoti ka lee Masedonia kɔŋ na, Pɔɔl te yɛɛ kolo kpu na Kristo nombia ŋan keyako ya. Ɔ na lese ŋe man wola Gyudatena baŋ nideli nyi Yesu yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ.

english_verse: Finally, they turned against him and insulted him. So he shook the dust from his clothes and told them, “Whatever happens to you will be your own fault! I am not to blame. From now on I am going to preach to the Gentiles.”
translation_verse: Mɔna be bɛɛ Pɔɔl nombia ŋan te ba tee e. Mena dɔɔ ŋon mɔ kpiisi ɔ kegba ayimɛɛ woli wɔ keŋ nawolo nyi ɔ ta lese ɔ nyiŋmaase lee be nombia man te ɔ yako wɔ nyi, “Akpaa ɛ yoo Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛman na, ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ nombia ŋenaŋ, te yɛɛ me nombia bela ya. Nawolo nyi keŋ kaboena nyi maa yɛɛ yaa me yɛɛwɔ nɛ. Lee gyɛŋ ne gyu dɔɔ, maa gyu kekolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia bo fa baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ.”

english_verse: Paul then moved into the house of a man named Titius Justus, who worshiped God and lived next door to the synagogue.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɔ leewɔ be gyaŋ gyu kekyaa Tituse Gyustuse ŋon ɔ te yɛɛ Gyuda baale e ya te ɔ na som Wurubuarɛ nɛ dekpaŋalaŋ man. Ɔ dekpaŋalaŋ kenaŋ benaa na Gyudatena ɔsom deni keŋ.

english_verse: Crispus was the leader of the synagogue. He and everyone in his family put their faith in the Lord. Many others in Corinth also heard the message, and all the people who had faith in the Lord were baptized.
translation_verse: Bɔ ɔsom deni man kegyia ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Kirispuse nɛ na ɔ dekpaŋalaŋmantena pou lɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan di. Korintotena burum mɔ nyii nombia ŋan te be lɛɛ ŋa di, te be sɔɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ.

english_verse: One night, Paul had a vision, and in it the Lord said, “Don't be afraid to keep on preaching. Don't stop!
translation_verse: Wee daale nelim nɛ, de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ kolosiwɔ fa Pɔɔl ŋgba dosɛɛ man nɛ nyi, “Na yee ya, mɔna kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan debaŋ kamasɛ. Na tina ŋa keyako yela ya.

english_verse: I am with you, and you won't be harmed. Many people in this city belong to me.”
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi me kpuɛ na neŋ. Ŋolo be gyae waa tale yɛɛ neŋ kolo ya, nawolo nyi balaŋ burum kyaa donɔɔ kei man keŋ be yɛɛ me wui.”

english_verse: Paul stayed on in Corinth for a year and a half, teaching God's message to the people.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Pɔɔl kyaawɔ botɔɔ kulutoo dokoloŋ na yaale na ɔ na wola wɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan.

english_verse: While Gallio was governor of Achaia, some of the Jewish leaders got together and grabbed Paul. They brought him into court
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ Galio ba seŋ Romantena gyoo dinaa nawɔɔ man dii gyoori Girikitena dɔɔ nɛ, te Gyudatena yɛɛ nɔɔ te be kyaŋa Pɔɔl mo e gyu nombia dedii man.

english_verse: and said, “This man is trying to make our people worship God in a way that is against our Law!”
translation_verse: Te be yakowɔ nyi “Balee kei na wola balaŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom wɔle daale keŋ ne tia da mmaraase.”

english_verse: Even before Paul could speak, Gallio said, “If you were charging this man with a crime or some other wrong, I would have to listen to you.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl ka mo waa kolosi pɛ, te Galio yako Gyudatena baŋ nyi, “Nyi akpaa deŋ yɛɛ nyi nombiikum daale yaa balee kei yɛɛwɔ na, nafɔ maa nyiŋ konɔɔ fa ŋon.

english_verse: But since this concerns only words, names, and your own law, you will have to take care of it yourselves. I refuse to judge such matters.”
translation_verse: Mɔna kei dɔɔ, ke yɛɛ nɔɔwoya na yela, na ɛmɛɛ gbagba ya mmaraase wose man te ɛ ne wɔŋee dɔɔ, ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ gyu kedi ɛ nombia. Nombia kɛŋa dinɔɔ dɔɔ, maŋ be gyae maa tale di ŋa ya.”

english_verse: Then he sent them out of the court.
translation_verse: Te ɔ yela be gegi wɔ lee nombia dedii man botɔɔ.

english_verse: The crowd grabbed Sosthenes, the Jewish leader, and beat him up in front of the court. But none of this mattered to Gallio. Paul Returns to Antioch in Syria
translation_verse: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ pou korowɔ kyaŋ Sositene ŋon ɔ yɛɛ ɔsom deni keŋ kegyia e nɛ, te be tuku e nombia dedii man botɔɔ, mɔna Galio te taŋ bua wɔ ya. Pɔɔl kabese gyu Antiɔke donɔɔ man

english_verse: After Paul had stayed for a while with the Lord's followers in Corinth, he told them goodbye and sailed on to Syria with Aquila and Priscilla. But before he left, he had his head shaved at Cenchreae because he had made a promise to God.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl kyaawɔ Kristotena baŋ be kyaa Korinto nɛ gyaŋ kelii debaŋ daale, te ɔ korowɔ lee botɔɔ mo loŋ dɔɔ gyu Siria tɛɛle man. Ɔ ne gyu na te ɔ moo Pirisila na Akuila kpu na ɔ wose. Ɔ ke gyu ka gyoo Kenkiria donɔɔ man nɛ, ɔ gbee ɔ nyee lee ntam keŋ ɔ be kam fa Wurubuarɛ nɛ dɔɔ.

english_verse: The three of them arrived in Ephesus, where Paul left Priscilla and Aquila. He then went into the synagogue to talk with the people there.
translation_verse: Be ke gyu ka gyoo Efeso donɔɔ man nɛ, ɔ tinaa Pirisila na Akuila yela, te waageŋ gyuu Gyudatena ɔsom deni man, te ŋon na wɔ gyoo Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan man.

english_verse: They asked him to stay longer, but he refused.
translation_verse: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ yako e nyi nafɔ waa kyaa be gyaŋ kyomii te ɔ bɛɛwɔ.

english_verse: Paul sailed to Caesarea, where he greeted the church. Then he went on to Antioch.
translation_verse: Ɔ kelii Kaesaria donɔɔ man nɛ, ɔ leewɔ botɔɔ gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man ka do Kristotena baŋ nɔɔ, te ɔ laŋa Antiɔke.

english_verse: After staying there for a while, he left and visited several places in Galatia and Phrygia. He helped the followers there to become stronger in their faith. Apollos in Ephesus
translation_verse: Ɔ dii weeya akalansɛɛ botɔɔ, kenaŋ wɔle nɛ ɔ tɛɛwɔ Galatia na Firigia tɛɛlese man do Kristotena baŋ kakyeŋ.

english_verse: A Jewish man named Apollos came to Ephesus. Apollos had been born in the city of Alexandria. He was a very good speaker and knew a lot about the Scriptures.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ man na, Gyuda baale ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Apolo, ŋon bɔɔ lola e Alɛkesanda donɔɔ man nɛ, te kɔŋ ɔ kyaa Efeso donɔɔ man. Ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ gyeŋ kekolosi nideli, te ɔ gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan mɔ nideli.

english_verse: He also knew much about the Lord's Way, and he spoke about it with great excitement. What he taught about Jesus was right, but all he knew was John's message about baptism.
translation_verse: Na be taŋ wola e de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu nombia ŋan nideli. Ɔ moo ɔ konɔɔ pou yase sia kolosi te ɔ wola de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu wose man nombia ŋan man nideli, mɔna Gyɔn kesɔ balaŋ Wurubuarɛ loŋ keŋ kaageŋ wose man nombia te ɔ gyeŋ.

english_verse: Apollos started speaking bravely in the synagogue. But when Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they took him to their home and helped him understand God's Way even better.
translation_verse: Ɔ gyoo Gyudatena ɔsom deni man te ɔ gyɔɔ ɔ konɔɔ kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan. Pirisila na Akuila kenyii ɔ nombia ŋan ɔ na wolo nɛ, be kaa moo e gyu be dɛɛ te be desinawɔ lese Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan man wola e nideli.

english_verse: Apollos decided to travel through Achaia. So the Lord's followers wrote letters, encouraging the followers there to welcome him. After Apollos arrived in Achaia, he was a great help to everyone who had put their faith in the Lord Jesus because of God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ Apolo be yɛɛ wa agyueŋ nyi ɔ ne gyu Akaya Girikitena tɛɛle man nɛ, Kristotena baŋ be kyaa Efeso nɛ doo e kakyeŋ, te be faa e be nyiŋmaa man tɔne nyi waa mo gyina Kristotena baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ, nyi baa lɛɛ e nideli. Ɔ ka gyoo botɔɔ nɛ, ɔ kyɔɔ baŋ lee Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ keŋ dɔɔ, bɔɔ lɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu di nɛ nideli.

english_verse: He got into fierce arguments with the Jewish people, and in public he used the Scriptures to prove that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_verse: Be gyaŋee balaŋ man bɛɛ lɛɛ aŋmaareŋ lee Wurubuarɛ nombia man na ɔ ne di Gyudatena baŋ dɔɔ, nawolo nyi ɔ na mo Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan wola wɔ nyi Yesu yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ.

english_verse: While Apollos was in Corinth, Paul traveled across the hill country to Ephesus, where he met some of the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ Apolo kyaa Korinto nɛ, Pɔɔl besewɔ kila menɛɛ kelii Efeso donɔɔ man. Botɔɔ te ɔ ke naa Yesu silala baale,

english_verse: He asked them, “When you put your faith in Jesus, were you given the Holy Spirit?” “No!” they answered. “We have never even heard of the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyi, “Debaŋ keŋ ɛ be lɛɛ Yesu di na, Wurubuarɛ moo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do ɛ man?” Te be tiranɔɔ fa e nyi, “De te nyii ta gba nyi Wurubuarɛ feliŋ daale kyaa ya.”

english_verse: “Then why were you baptized?” Paul asked. They answered, “Because of what John taught.”
translation_verse: Te Pɔɔl bɔɔse wɔ bela nyi, “Kenaŋ na Wurubuarɛ loŋ woŋti yaa be sɔɔ ŋon?” Te be tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋkesɔ te be sɔɔ daa.”

english_verse: Paul replied, “John baptized people so they would turn to God. But he also told them someone else was coming, and they should put their faith in him. Jesus is the one that John was talking about.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Pɔɔl yako wɔ nyi, “Baŋ bɔɔ kyɛɛkee lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man nɛ, baŋ te Gyɔn sɔɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ. Keŋte ɔ yako Iseraetena nyi, baa lɛɛ ŋon waa kɔŋ ŋon Gyɔn wɔle nɛ di. Ŋon yɛna Yesu.”

english_verse: After the people heard Paul say this, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Be ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, be yela be sɔɔ wɔ Wurubuarɛ loŋ lee de gbeŋgyoo Yesu yele man.

english_verse: Then Paul placed his hands on them. The Holy Spirit was given to them, and they spoke unknown languages and prophesied.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl moo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa be dɔɔ, te Wurubuarɛ moo ɔ feliŋ do be man. Botɔɔ te be kolosi deise wɔlɛɛ, te Wurubuarɛ tɛɛwɔ be dɔɔ kolosi nombia.

english_verse: There were about twelve men in this group.
translation_verse: Na be yɛɛ ŋgba baala kufu bala (

english_verse: but some of them were stubborn and refused to believe. In front of everyone they said terrible things about God's Way. Paul left and took the followers with him to the lecture hall of Tyrannus. He spoke there every day
translation_verse: Mɔna be man baale yɛɛ konɔɔyuŋ te be bɛɛwɔ bɔɔ lɛɛ Yesu nombia ŋan di ya, te be yako nombiakumɛɛ lee de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu nyeedoŋ gbɛɛ keŋ ɔ ba wolo nɛ wose man balaŋ nsana. Mena dɔɔ Pɔɔl moo silala baŋ kpu na ɔ wose, te ba korowɔ tina balaŋ baŋ yela. Wee kamasɛ na bɛɛ gyaŋee Tiranuse deni dinaa keŋ ɔ na wola abɔɔ nɛ man gyoo Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan man.

english_verse: for two years, until every Jew and Gentile in Asia had heard the Lord's message. The Sons of Sceva
translation_verse: Ɔ yɛɛ kei menɛɛ kulutooneŋ ala mena dɔɔ walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa Esia tɛɛle keŋ man nɛ, na baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ pou nyii de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu nombia ŋan. Siifa bia

english_verse: God gave Paul the power to work great miracles.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ tɛɛwɔ Pɔɔl dɔɔ yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia na nombia dinaana.

english_verse: People even took handkerchiefs and aprons that had touched Paul's body, and they carried them to everyone who was sick. All of the sick people were healed, and the evil spirits went out.
translation_verse: Keŋ nyi balaŋ moo adukuse na kanyaŋsebia ke tina Pɔɔl, na be ta mo ke tina be kaweesetena na ba wose te yɛɛ wɔ doŋ, te baŋ mɔ feliŋkumɛɛ doo be man na, ŋɛɛ lee be man.

english_verse: Some Jewish men started going around trying to force out evil spirits by using the name of the Lord Jesus. They said to the spirits, “Come out in the name of that same Jesus that Paul preaches about!”
translation_verse: Na Gyudatena baale mɔ dɔŋɛɛ bee kili gegi feliŋkumɛɛ lee balaŋ wose man. Be gyɔɔ be konɔɔ mo de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu yele nyi baa gegi feliŋkumɛɛ. Bɛɛ gyae baa gegi feliŋkumɛɛ lee balaŋ wose man na bɛɛ yako nyi, “Lee Yesu ŋon Pɔɔl ne yako ɔ nombia nɛ yele man, mɛɛ faree ŋon nyi ‘Ɛ lee ɔ man.’ ”

english_verse: Seven sons of a high priest named Sceva were doing this,
translation_verse: Na Gyudatena saese kegyia ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Siifa nɛ bia baala nyetooro baale ne yɛɛ na mena nombia kɛŋa.

english_verse: when an evil spirit said to them, “I know Jesus! And I have heard about Paul. But who are you?”
translation_verse: Be kɛɛ yɛɛ mena nɛ, wee daale feliŋkum daale bɔɔse wɔ nyi, “Ma gyeŋ Yesu te ma gyeŋ Pɔɔl mɔ, te ɛmɛɛ yɛna bomɔ?”

english_verse: Then the man with the evil spirit jumped on them and beat them up. They ran out of the house, naked and bruised.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ balee ŋon feliŋkum keŋ doo ɔ man nɛ yeuwɔ gyoo wɔ ketuku. Ɔ tuku wɔ tirii ba kegbase, te ba moo fatabose yekii lee dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man diteŋteli.

english_verse: When the Jews and Gentiles in Ephesus heard about this, they were so frightened that they praised the name of the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Te Gyudatena na baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya te be kyaa Efeso donɔɔ man nɛ, nyii nombia kɛŋa na, gyakoloŋ gyoo wɔ. Mena dɔɔ ba moo obuo fa de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu, te be ta mo ɔ yele do nsendee ya.

english_verse: Many who were followers now started telling everyone about the evil things they had been doing.
translation_verse: Te baŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ Yesu di nɛ, be man balaŋ burum leewɔ balaŋ nsana tɔlɛɛ be nɔɔwoya lese be nombiakumɛɛ ŋan bɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ debɔɔ.

english_verse: So the Lord's message spread and became even more powerful. The Riot in Ephesus
translation_verse: Lee mena dɔɔ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu nombia ŋan yaaseewɔ gyu lem te balaŋ lɛɛ ŋa do nideli. Efeso donɔɔ kesiŋii

english_verse: After all this had happened, Paul decided to visit Macedonia and Achaia on his way to Jerusalem. Paul had said, “From there I will go on to Rome.”
translation_verse: Nombia kɛŋa pou wɔle nɛ, Pɔɔl yɛɛ wa agyueŋ nyi waa mo na Masedonia na Giriki tɛɛlese man, na waa gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man. Botɔɔ te ɔ yakowɔ nyi, “Akpaa me gyu kelii botɔɔ na, kaboena nyi maa gyu Roman donɔɔ man mɔ.”

english_verse: So he sent his two helpers, Timothy and Erastus, to Macedonia. But he stayed on in Asia for a while.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɔ kpila balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ kyɔ e nɛ man balaŋ bala, baŋ yɛna Timoti na Erasituse nyi baa gyu Masedonia, na ŋon Pɔɔl kekyaa Esia tɛɛle man kyomii.

english_verse: At that time there was serious trouble because of the Lord's Way.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ man te aŋmaareŋ dinaa daale gyoo Efesotena man lee de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu nyeedoŋ gbɛɛ keŋ ɔ silala baŋ na wolo nɛ dɔɔ.

english_verse: A silversmith named Demetrius had a business that made silver models of the temple of the goddess Artemis. Those who worked for him earned a lot of money.
translation_verse: Na nyeele wolom luure ŋolo kyaa ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Demetiriyu. Ɔ na mo nyeele wolom luu be bane Atemise denɛɛbia yɔɔ. Mena dɔɔ balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ mena tom kei nɛ nyiŋa kɔba nideli.

english_verse: Demetrius brought together everyone who was in the same business and said: Friends, you know we make a good living at this.
translation_verse: Keŋte Demetiriyu ŋon baake ɔ tomyɛɛra, na baŋ pou bɛɛ yɛɛ mena tom kenaŋ dinɔɔ nɛ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Me tebia ŋgba ɛ gyeŋ nyi dee nyiŋ kɔba lee da tom kei man.

english_verse: But you have surely seen and heard how this man Paul is upsetting a lot of people, not only in Ephesus, but almost everywhere in Asia. He claims that the gods we humans make are not really gods at all.
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ ne naa te ɛ ne nyii mena keŋ Pɔɔl kei ne wɔlɛɛ balaŋ nyeeya, te ɔ na wola balaŋ nyi kpene keŋ bɛɛ mo nyiŋmaase yɛɛ nɛ, te yɛɛ kpene keŋ kaboena nyi baa som ya. Na Efeso kɛbo kaageŋ te ɔ ne wɔlɛɛ balaŋ nyeeya ya, mɔna ɔ ta mo ɔ nombia ŋenaŋ kila Esia tɛɛle kei pou.

english_verse: Everyone will start saying terrible things about our business. They will stop respecting the temple of the goddess Artemis, who is worshiped in Asia and all over the world. Our great goddess will be forgotten!
translation_verse: Te yɛɛ nyi da tom kei kaageŋ ne gyae la ke wɔlɛɛ ya, mɔna de bane alo Atemise deni dinaa keŋ Esia na tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ pou ne kaa som nɛ, yele gyae ke yala te de bane Atemise ŋon mɔ be gyae waa tekaa nyiŋ obuo bela ya.”

english_verse: When the workers heard this, they got angry and started shouting, “Great is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!”
translation_verse: Balaŋ dikpii keŋ ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, ba gyeŋa baŋ te ba gyoo yeesa kefae nyi, “Daa Efesotena bane Atemise ŋon yɛɛ bane dinaa e!”

english_verse: Soon the whole city was in a riot, and some men grabbed Gaius and Aristarchus, who had come from Macedonia with Paul. Then everyone in the crowd rushed to the place where the town meetings were held.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ donɔɔ keŋ siŋiiwɔ, te ba ke kyaŋa Gayose na Arisitakuse. Baŋ yɛna Masedonia tewulɛɛ bala baŋ be kpuɛ na Pɔɔl nɛ, keŋte be yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ wɔŋee wɔ gyu be dɛɛgyaŋee keŋ man.

english_verse: Paul wanted to go out and speak to the people, but the Lord's followers would not let him.
translation_verse: Nafɔ Pɔɔl gyaewɔ nyi waa gyu botɔɔ, mɔna kaseela baŋ te fa e gbɛɛ ya.

english_verse: A few of the local officials were friendly to Paul, and they sent someone to warn him not to go.
translation_verse: Te Esiatena kegyiise baŋ be yɛɛ Pɔɔl ɔ gyoonawɔ nɛ, kpilawɔ ka sola Pɔɔl nyi ɔ na fa waa gyu ba degyaŋee keŋ man ya.

english_verse: Some of the people in the meeting were shouting one thing, and others were shouting something else. Everyone was completely confused, and most of them did not even know why they were there.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ na donɔɔ keŋ te siŋii na bɛɛ fae yeesa. Baale kpa kei, baale mɔ ne yako ŋaale kpoo. Be man dikpii dinaa daale mɔ dɔɔ, baŋ ba gyeŋ kpene keŋ be kɔŋ dɔɔ te bɔɔ kaa gyaŋee botɔɔ ya.

english_verse: Several of the Jewish leaders pushed a man named Alexander to the front of the crowd and started telling him what to say. He motioned with his hand and tried to explain what was going on.
translation_verse: Te Gyudatena baŋ moo nombia gyakaa Alɛkesanda dɔɔ. Be tɛɛse e lee siaman kɔŋ balaŋ nsana, mena dɔɔ balaŋ baŋ baale gyueŋwɔ nyi Alɛkesanda gyae na nombia ŋan kɔŋ, keŋte Alɛkesanda gyinaa ɔ nyiŋmaa adido nyi waa kolosi lese ɔ nɔɔse.

english_verse: But when the crowd saw that he was Jewish, they all shouted for two hours, “Great is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!”
translation_verse: Mɔna balaŋ baŋ ka gyeŋ nyi ɔ yɛɛ Gyuda baale e nɛ, ba seŋawɔ nombii dokoloŋ dɔɔ bɛɛ fae yeesa ŋgba debaŋse ala nɛ nyi, “Daa Efesotena bane Atemise yɛɛ bane dinaa e!”

english_verse: Finally, a town official made the crowd be quiet. Then he said: People of Ephesus, who in the world doesn't know that our city is the center for worshiping the great goddess Artemis? Who doesn't know that her image which fell from heaven is right here?
translation_verse: Keŋte donɔɔ keŋ tɔne ŋmarasere ŋon yela balaŋ baŋ pou tinaa kekolosi yela te ɔ yako wɔ nyi, “Efesotena, amɔte kyaa la tɛɛle kei dɔɔ keŋ nyi ɔ ba gyeŋ nyi Efeso donɔɔ yɛna de bane dinaa Atemise deni na ɔ boe keŋ kɛɛ wola ɔ dinɔɔ keŋ ba lee adido kaa yala nɛ nyee ya?

english_verse: No one can deny this, and so you should calm down and not do anything foolish.
translation_verse: Ŋolo be kyaa keŋ nyi waa tale lɛɛ mena nombia kɛŋa aŋmaareŋ ya. Mena dɔɔ ɛ nyiŋ konɔɔ, ɛ na barase yɛɛ kolo ya.

english_verse: You have brought men in here who have not robbed temples or spoken against our goddess.
translation_verse: Balaŋ kɛwɔ baŋ ɛ ba mo wɔ kɔŋ nɛ, te ŋmɛɛle kolo lee de bane deni man ya. Te ba te yako nombiikum mɔ tia de bane kei ya.

english_verse: If Demetrius and his workers have a case against these men, we have courts and judges. Let them take their complaints there.
translation_verse: Akpaa Demetiriyu na ɔ tebia na ŋolo dana nombia na, nombia kedi weeya kyaa te kegyiise baŋ mɔ kyaa. Baa tale mo dɔŋa gyu botɔɔ.

english_verse: But if you want to do more than that, the matter will have to be brought before the city council.
translation_verse: Mɔna nyi akpaa ɛ dana nombia wɔlɛɛ ŋaale na, kaboena nyi donɔɔsetena gbagba baa di ŋa ŋgba mena keŋ da mmaraa keŋ na wolo nɛ.

english_verse: We could easily be accused of starting a riot today. There is no excuse for it! We cannot even give a reason for this uproar.
translation_verse: Akpaa da te kɛɛ nideli ya na, dee nyiŋ nombia lee gyɛŋ basabasa kei dɔɔ. Te akpaa de nyiŋ nombia mɔ na, dɔɔ gyae dɛɛ tale tiranɔɔ lese da wose lee ŋe man ya, nawolo nyi basabasa kei be taŋ nyee na nawɔɔ ya.”

english_verse: After saying this, he told the people to leave.
translation_verse: Ɔ kekolosi taŋ nɛ, te ɔ yela balaŋ baŋ pou yaaseewɔ.

english_verse: When the riot was over, Paul sent for the followers and encouraged them. He then told them goodbye and left for Macedonia.
translation_verse: Basabasa keŋ wɔle nɛ, Pɔɔl baake Kristotena baŋ pou yilaa te ɔ kolosiwɔ fa wɔ do wɔ kakyeŋ te ɔ kalaa wɔ, te ɔ lee botɔɔ laŋ Masedonia.

english_verse: As he traveled from place to place, he encouraged the followers with many messages. Finally, he went to Greece
translation_verse: Ɔ tɛɛwɔ kila botɔɔ yenaŋ pou, na ɔ ne kolosi do balaŋ baŋ pou kakyeŋ te ɔ leewɔ botɔɔ kɔŋ Giriki tɛɛle man.

english_verse: and stayed there for three months. Paul was about to sail to Syria. But some of the Jewish leaders plotted against him, so he decided to return by way of Macedonia.
translation_verse: Ɔ kyaawɔ botɔɔ gyaalaŋna batooro. Ɔ kɛɛ desina ɔ wose waa gyu Siria tɛɛle man na te ɔ naawɔ nyi Gyudatena ne bake ɔ nyeeman. Mena dɔɔ ɔ yɛɛ wa agyueŋ nyi waa bese ka mo na Masedonia tɛɛle man.

english_verse: With him were Sopater, son of Pyrrhus from Berea, and Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica. Gaius from Derbe was also with him, and so were Timothy and the two Asians, Tychicus and Trophimus.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ sila e gyu nɛ yɛna Piruse ŋon ɔ ba lee Beria nɛ bu Sopata, na Arisitakuse na Sekunduse baŋ bɔɔ lee Tesalonika nɛ, na Gayose ŋon ɔ ba lee Dɛbi nɛ, na Timoti na Tikikuse na Tirofimuse baŋ bɔɔ lee Esia tɛɛle man nɛ.

english_verse: They went on ahead to Troas and waited for us there.
translation_verse: Mena balaŋ kɛwɔ kyaŋa gbɛɛ te ba ke kyaawɔ Torase donɔɔ man daa daa.

english_verse: After the Festival of Thin Bread, we sailed from Philippi. Five days later we met them in Troas and stayed there for a week. Paul's Last Visit to Troas
translation_verse: Gyudatena bodobodo keŋ tee be doo ke man ya kedi wee dinaa keŋ wɔle nɛ, da moo degbele dinaa lee Filipi. Weeya anoŋ wɔle nɛ te de ka tuŋa baŋ bɔɔ kyaŋ gbɛɛ laŋ nɛ Torase donɔɔ man te de dii weeya nyetooro botɔɔ. Pɔɔl kegyuusu Yutikuse lee yeŋ man

english_verse: On the first day of the week we met to break bread together. Paul spoke to the people until midnight because he was leaving the next morning.
translation_verse: Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee keŋ wɔle nɛ de popou kaa gyaŋeewɔ dee di weenɛɛ ŋan de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu ba wola daa nyi dee di na dɛɛ mo tɔɔse e nɛ. Lee keŋ tɛɛ de naa na Pɔɔl waa bese dɔɔ, ɔ kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan menɛɛ kelii tɛɛ nsana.

english_verse: In the upstairs room where we were meeting, there were a lot of lamps.
translation_verse: Na kanease burum doo abansoro deni keŋ man dɔɔ gyaŋee nɛ.

english_verse: A young man by the name of Eutychus was sitting on a window sill. While Paul was speaking, the young man got very sleepy. Finally, he went to sleep and fell three floors all the way down to the ground. When they picked him up, he was dead.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl kɛɛ kolosi keŋ nɛ, na tobaale ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Yutikuse baŋɛɛ abansoro deni kebaŋ gyakaa atoorote keŋ nfansere dɔɔ ɔ ne deŋeli. Lee Pɔɔl kekolosi kyare dɔɔ dosoroŋ moo tobaale ŋon, te tira e lee botɔɔ kaa lɔ tɛɛle man. Be gyuuwɔ ke gate e na ɔ ta yem.

english_verse: Paul went down and bent over Eutychus. He took him in his arms and said, “Don't worry! He's alive.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Pɔɔl tisiwɔ gyu ke buŋ tobaale ŋon dɔɔ te ɔ pukaa e te ɔ gyuusu e lee yeŋ man. Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyi, “Ɛ na yeli ɛ wɔe ke lɔ ŋon ya, ɔ delena.”

english_verse: Then the followers took the young man home alive and were very happy. The Voyage from Troas to Miletus
translation_verse: Te balaŋ baŋ moo tobaale ŋon ɔ ba yem te Pɔɔl be gyuusu e nɛ, gyu ɔ dekpaŋalaŋ man te be popou wɔe fɛɛ wɔ. Pɔɔl kekalaa Efeso kegyiise baŋ

english_verse: Paul decided to travel by land to Assos. The rest of us went on ahead by ship, and we were to take him aboard there.
translation_verse: Keŋte da gyoowɔ degbele dinaa keŋ man gyu Asose donɔɔ man, na de kaa mo Pɔɔl kpu na da wose. Nawolo nyi ɔ yako daa nyi waa kyaŋ gbɛɛ na ɔ naawɔ, na de kaa mo e botɔɔ.

english_verse: When he met us in Assos, he came aboard, and we sailed on to Mitylene.
translation_verse: Ɔ ke kɔŋ kaa tuŋ daa Asose donɔɔ man keŋ nɛ, da moo e kpu na da wose gyu Mitilene donɔɔ keŋ man.

english_verse: The next day we came to a place near Chios, and the following day we reached Samos. The day after that we sailed to Miletus.
translation_verse: Tɛɛ kena keŋ nɛ te da moo degbele dinaa keŋ bela kelii Kiyose tɛɛlebii keŋ loŋ be kilisi ke nɛ yenaŋ. Weeya alate keŋ na te de gbaawɔ gyu Samose tɛɛlebii keŋ loŋ be kilisi ke nɛ man, te ka weeya atoorote na, te da gyoo Miletuse donɔɔ man.

english_verse: Paul had decided to sail on past Ephesus, because he did not want to spend too much time in Asia. He was in a hurry and wanted to be in Jerusalem in time for Pentecost. Paul Says Goodbye to the Church Leaders of Ephesus
translation_verse: Pɔɔl yɛɛ wa agyueŋ nyi, ɔ be gyae waa seŋ Efeso donɔɔ man ya, na ɔ be nyiŋ wɔlɛɛ debaŋ Esia tɛɛle man ya. Nawolo nyi ɔ ne gyae bileŋ nyi akpaa ɔ tale na, waa gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man ke tuŋ Gyudatena wee dinaa keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi Pentekosite nɛ.

english_verse: From Miletus, Paul sent a message for the church leaders at Ephesus to come and meet with him.
translation_verse: Ɔ ke kyaa Miletuse na, te ɔ kpilawɔ ka mo Efeso Kristotena ɔsom kegyiise baŋ.

english_verse: When they got there, he said: You know everything I did during the time I was with you when I first came to Asia.
translation_verse: Ba ke kɔŋ kaa gyoo nɛ, ɔ yako wɔ nyi “Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ gyeŋ nyi, leenaŋ wee gyaŋgbate keŋ mɔɔ kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ Esia tɛɛle man nɛ, daa na ŋon kyaawɔ nideli weeya ŋenaŋ pou man.

english_verse: Some of the Jews plotted against me and caused me a lot of sorrow and trouble. But I served the Lord and was humble.
translation_verse: Ɛ gyeŋ mena keŋ mɔɔ yɔkɔse ma wose te mɔɔ mo siasaraŋ yɛɛ tom keŋ nideli ŋgba de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ tobaale nɛ. Te ɛ naa mena keŋ Gyudatena ba koro seŋ me dɔɔ te bɔɔ do maŋ sibiiyam nɛ.

english_verse: When I preached in public or taught in your homes, I didn't hold back from telling anything that would help you.
translation_verse: Ɛ gyeŋ nyi debaŋ keŋ mee kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan fa ŋon balaŋ nsana na ya akpaŋalaŋse man nɛ, me ta mo kolo weese ŋon ya. Ma lese abɔɔ ŋan gyae ke kyɔ ŋon nɛ pou wola ŋon.

english_verse: I told Jews and Gentiles to turn to God and have faith in our Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Gyudatena na Girikitena pou te me fareewɔ nyi, baa kyɛɛkee lee be nombiakumɛɛ man na baa kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ na baa lɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu di.

english_verse: I don't know what will happen to me in Jerusalem, but I must obey God's Spirit and go there.
translation_verse: Te nɛnɛɛ, mɛɛ gyae maa yɛɛ kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ne gyae dɔɔ mee gyu Gyerusalɛm, mɔna mɔɔ gyeŋ kpene keŋ gyae ke kɔŋ me dɔɔ botɔɔ ya.

english_verse: In every city I visit, I am told by the Holy Spirit that I will be put in jail and will be in trouble in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Nombii dokoloŋ keŋ ma gyeŋ yɛna nyi, Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ne faree maŋ donɔɔ kamasɛ man nyi, baa naase maŋ diyem na baa kyaŋ maŋ tɔ deni.

english_verse: But I don't care what happens to me, as long as I finish the work the Lord Jesus gave me to do. And this work is to tell the good news about God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Mɔna mɔɔ ne kɛɛ ma nyeedoŋ nyi ke yɛɛ kolo ke fa maŋ ya. Mɛɛ gyae maa yɛɛ tom keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu be fa maŋ nyi maa yɛɛ nɛ ke si kɔ ɔto. Tom kenaŋ yɛna nyi, maa yako ɔ nombia kpaakpaa ŋan fa balaŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ waa mo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele wɔ.”

english_verse: I have gone from place to place, preaching to you about God's kingdom, but now I know that none of you will ever see me again.
translation_verse: Nɛnɛɛ ma gyeŋ nyi ɛmɛɛ baŋ pou mɔɔ kili ɛ dɔɔ yako ŋon Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ wose man nombia ŋan nɛ, ɛ man ŋolo be gyae waa tekaa naa maŋ bela ya.

english_verse: I tell you today I am no longer responsible for any of you!
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ gyɛŋ wee kei mɛɛ yako ŋon baa nyi, akpaa ɛ man ŋolo da yoo lee Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛ man na, me nɔɔ be doo ya.

english_verse: I have told you everything God wants you to know.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi ma te yako ŋon Wurubuarɛ nyeeman nombia ŋan pou.

english_verse: Look after yourselves and everyone the Holy Spirit has placed in your care. Be like shepherds to God's church. It is the flock he bought with the blood of his own Son.
translation_verse: Ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose dɔɔ nideli, na ɛ ke kɛɛ balaŋ baŋ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ba mo wɔ do ɛ nyiŋmaa man nɛ mɔ dɔɔ nideli. Ɛ kɛɛ Kristotena dikpii keŋ dɔɔ nideli ŋgba mena keŋ wonembu kɛɛlere ne kɛɛ ɔ wonembia dɔɔ nɛ. Baŋ yɛna baŋ Wurubuarɛ be yela ɔ bu ba yem lɛɛ ba nyeeya nɛ.

english_verse: I know that after I am gone, others will come like fierce wolves to attack you.
translation_verse: Ma gyeŋ nyi ma ke laŋ wɔle na, balaŋ kumɛɛ baa kaa gyoo ɛ man ŋgba mena keŋ gbougbouse na gyoo wonembia man nɛ, te bɔɔ gyae baa tina ŋon yela ya.

english_verse: Some of your own people will tell lies to win over the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Te debaŋ daale gyae kelii nɛ, baale baa lee ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ dikpii keŋ man na baa beo ɛ tebia Kristotena baŋ baale sila ba wose.

english_verse: Be on your guard! Remember how day and night for three years I kept warning you with tears in my eyes.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛ doo ɛ wose dɔɔ, na ɛ ke tɔɔse nyi kulutooneŋ atooro sɔɔŋ te me faree ŋon na siasaraŋ nelim na ka weese pou.

english_verse: I now place you in God's care. Remember the message about his gift of undeserved grace! This message can help you and give you what belongs to you as God's people.
translation_verse: Te nɛnɛɛ mɛɛ mo ŋon mɛɛ do Wurubuarɛ nyiŋmaa man. Nombia ŋan na wola Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ keŋ ɔ dana fa deniwalaŋ nɛ, ne gyae la ke kyɔ ŋon na ɛ ke tale seŋ nombia ŋan ɛ be lɛɛ di nɛ man nideli. Wurubuarɛ waa mo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa ɛmɛɛ na ɔ balaŋ akaŋ baŋ ɔ ba lese nɛ pou dɔɔ.

english_verse: I have never wanted anyone's money or clothes.
translation_verse: Me sia te yɛɛ ŋolo kɔba yayam yaa ɔ kɔba, yaa wa abɔɔ dɔɔ ya.

english_verse: You know how I have worked with my own hands to make a living for myself and my friends.
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ gyeŋ nyi ma nyiŋmaase kɛŋa te ma moowɔ yɛɛ tom te me nyiŋa abɔɔ ŋan pou gyae ke hia daa na me tebia nɛ.

english_verse: By everything I did, I showed how you should work to help everyone who is weak. Remember that our Lord Jesus said, “More blessings come from giving than from receiving.”
translation_verse: Ma wola ŋon kpene kamasɛ man nyi, kaboena nyi walaŋ kamasɛ waa yɛɛ tom nideli mena, na waa nyiŋ kolo kyɔ baŋ bɔɔ dana doŋ ya nɛ. Na ɛ ke tɔɔse de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu kawola keŋ nyi, “Kolo kefa dana gyoŋ na wosefɛɛreŋ kela kolo kenyiŋ lee ŋolo gyaŋ.”

english_verse: After Paul had finished speaking, he knelt down with all of them and prayed.
translation_verse: Ɔ kekolosi kɛŋa taŋ nɛ, be popou tisiwɔ buŋ ba doona dɔɔ te be fanewɔ.

english_verse: Everyone cried and hugged and kissed him.
translation_verse: Keŋte be man walaŋ kamasɛ moo kewii pukaa e te be kalaa e.

english_verse: They were especially sad because Paul had told them, “You will never see me again.” Then they went with him to the ship.
translation_verse: Keŋ ɔ be yako wɔ nyi bɔɔ gyae baa tekaa naa e bela ya nɛ, keŋ yɛna kpene keŋ be takaa wɔ nideli. Ba moo e ka tee, te ɔ gyoo degbele dinaa keŋ man laŋ.

english_verse: After saying goodbye, we sailed straight to Cos. The next day we reached Rhodes and from there sailed on to Patara.
translation_verse: Da ke kalaa dɔŋa taŋ keŋ nɛ, da moowɔ loŋ keŋ dɔɔ teeŋ te da toŋawɔ gyu Kosi donɔɔ man. Tɛɛ naawɔ na te de ka gyoo Rodesi donɔɔ man, te da leewɔ botɔɔ gyu Patara donɔɔ man.

english_verse: We found a ship going to Phoenicia, so we got on board and sailed off.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te de naa degbele dinaa daale na kee gyu Fonisia tɛɛle man te da gyoo ke man laŋ.

english_verse: We came within sight of Cyprus and then sailed south of it on to the port of Tyre in Syria, where the ship was going to unload its cargo.
translation_verse: De sia ke gyɔ Saiporɔse tɛɛlebii keŋ loŋ be kilisi ke nɛ, te de tinaa ke yela te de kpasewɔ mo ka ata yenaŋ gyu ka seŋ Tae boo nɔɔman Siria tɛɛle keŋ dɔɔ. Botɔɔ te ba lese kasolɔse ŋan doo degbele dinaa keŋ man nɛ see.

english_verse: But when the week was over, we started on our way again. All the men, together with their wives and children, walked with us from the town to the seashore. We knelt on the beach and prayed.
translation_verse: Mɔna debaŋ keŋ kelii nyi dɛɛ koro lee be gyaŋ nɛ, te da korowɔ mo de gbɛɛ bela. Keŋte baŋ na be wɛɛla na be bia pou gyuuwɔ ka tee daa donɔɔ keŋ nɔɔman. Te de popou buŋawɔ da doona dɔɔ ɛpo keŋ nɔɔman te de fanewɔ.

english_verse: Then after saying goodbye to each other, we got into the ship, and they went back home.
translation_verse: De kalaa dɔŋa te de ka gyoo degbele dinaa keŋ man te baŋ mɔɔ besewɔ laŋ be dɛɛ.

english_verse: We sailed from Tyre to Ptolemais, where we greeted the followers and stayed with them for a day.
translation_verse: Da besewɔ mo de gbɛɛ keŋ dɔɔ lee Tae menɛɛ kelii Pitolemase te da doo Kristotena baŋ be kya botɔɔ nɛ nɔɔ te de kyaawɔ be gyaŋ wee dokoloŋ.

english_verse: The next day we went to Caesarea and stayed with Philip, the preacher. He was one of the seven men who helped the apostles,
translation_verse: Tɛɛ kena na te da korowɔ ka gyoo Kaesaria donɔɔ man, te de ka soo Filipo ŋon ɔ ne tɛɛ kolosi Kristo nombia nɛ. Ŋon Filipo mɔ kpuɛ balaŋ nyetooro baŋ bɔɔ kyaa lese Gyerusalɛm nyi baa kɛɛ weenɛɛ kekpɛlɛɛ dɔɔ nɛ man.

english_verse: We had been in Caesarea for several days, when the prophet Agabus came to us from Judea.
translation_verse: De ke di weeya akalansɛɛ ŋaale botɔɔ nɛ, te Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Agabuse nɛ, lee Gyudia tɛɛle man kɔŋ.

english_verse: He took Paul's belt, and with it he tied up his own hands and feet. Then he told us, “The Holy Spirit says that some of the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem will tie up the man who owns this belt. They will also hand him over to the Gentiles.”
