translation_verse: Ɔ kɔŋa de gyaŋ na, te ɔ moo Pɔɔl abɔsɔɔ mo baake ŋon gbagba ɔ nawɔɔ na ɔ nyiŋmaase te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Nnɛ yaa Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ne yako. Nnɛ te Gyudatena baŋ be kyaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, baa bake ŋon ɔ tee abɔsɔɔ kei nɛ, na baa lese e fa balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ.”

english_verse: After Agabus said this, we and the followers living there begged Paul not to go to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ dɔɔ nyii mena nombia kɛŋa nɛ, te daa na balaŋ akaŋ baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ waase Pɔɔl nyi ɔ na gyu Gyerusalɛm ya.

english_verse: But Paul answered, “Why are you crying and breaking my heart? I am not only willing to be put in jail for the Lord Jesus, but I am even willing to die for him in Jerusalem!”
translation_verse: Keŋte Pɔɔl tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyi, “Weera ɛ ne wii te ɛ ne yeli dekɔkɔrɔkɔɔ ne kyaŋ maŋ mena? Na me desina ma wose bo fa kebake kaageŋ Gyerusalɛm ya, mɔna ma te desina ma wose fa yeŋ mɔ lee de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu yele man.”

english_verse: Since we could not get Paul to change his mind, we gave up and prayed, “Lord, please make us willing to do what you want.”
translation_verse: Da te tale kyɛɛkee wa agyueŋ ya, mena dɔɔ de tina e yela te de yakowɔ nyi, “Wurubuarɛ gbagba waa yɛɛ ɔ kegyaebii.”

english_verse: Then we got ready to go to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Da ke kyaa botɔɔ kyomii nɛ, da moo da wose te de laŋa Gyerusalɛm.

english_verse: Some of the followers from Caesarea went with us and took us to stay in the home of Mnason. He was from Cyprus and had been a follower from the beginning. Paul Visits James
translation_verse: Kristotena baŋ bɔɔ lee Kaesaria nɛ man baale sila daa, te ba moo daa gyu Masɔne ŋon ɔ ba lee Saiporɔse donɔɔ man nɛ dɛɛ. Masɔne kei yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ be wulaa lɛɛ Kristo di leenaŋ kewalaŋ keŋ. Pɔɔl kagyoo Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man

english_verse: When we arrived in Jerusalem, the Lord's followers gladly welcomed us.
translation_verse: De ka gyoo Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, Kristotena baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ lɛɛ daa nideli.

english_verse: Paul went with us to see James the next day, and all the church leaders were present.
translation_verse: Ke tɛɛ naa keŋ nɛ, daa na Pɔɔl gyuuwɔ ka do Gyemisi nɔɔ. De ke gyu keŋ na ɔsom kegyiise baŋ pou kyaa botɔɔ.

english_verse: Paul greeted them and told how God had used him to help the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl doo wɔ nɔɔ te ɔ yako wɔ kpene kamasɛ keŋ Wurubuarɛ be tɛɛ ɔ dɔɔ yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya man nɛ.

english_verse: Everyone who heard this praised God and said to Paul: My friend, you can see how many tens of thousands of our people have become followers! And all of them are eager to obey the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Be ke nyii nombia ŋan taŋ nɛ, be popou lese Wurubuarɛ yele. Te be yako e nyi, “Kegyia Pɔɔl, ŋgba n naa mena keŋ Gyudatena burum be lɛɛ Kristo nombia ŋan di, mɔna be sɛɛ be dana Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan ɔ be yeli Mosesi be ŋmarase see nɛ man nideli.

english_verse: But they have been told that you are teaching those who live among the Gentiles to disobey this Law. They claim that you are telling them not to circumcise their sons or to follow our customs.
translation_verse: Be te nyii n nombia nyi, n na wola Gyudatena baŋ pou be kyaa balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ tɛɛle dɔɔ nyi, baa tina Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan ɔ be yeli Mosesi be ŋmarase see nɛ dɔɔ kedi yela. Be na kara ba bia baala wose ya, te be na sila de naanaɔ beneŋ nombia ŋan bela ya.

english_verse: What should we do now that our people have heard you are here?
translation_verse: Kei na sena yaa dɛɛ yɛɛ baa, de yɛɛ senaa baa nyii nyi n te kɔŋ kɛbo.

english_verse: Please do what we ask, because four of our men have made special promises to God.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ yɛɛ kpene keŋ dɛɛ yako neŋ nɛ. Balaŋ banaara baale kyaa de gyaŋ kɛbo keŋ bɔɔ kaŋ ntam.

english_verse: Join with them and prepare yourself for the ceremony that goes with the promises. Pay the cost for their heads to be shaved. Then everyone will learn that the reports about you are not true. They will know you do obey the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Gyu ke kpu na balaŋ banaara kɛwɔ na ɛ ka gberaa ɛ wose, na n ke tɔ kom keŋ baa gyoo nɛ, na baa nyiŋ tale tisi ba nyeeya. N yɛɛ mena na, walaŋ kamasɛ waa naa nyi nombia ŋan pou bɔɔ yako lee n wose man nɛ te yɛɛ ampaŋ ya. Mɔna baa naa nyi nyaŋ gbagba mɔ n ne di Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan ɔ be yeli Mosesi be ŋmarase see nɛ dɔɔ nideli.

english_verse: Some while ago we told the Gentile followers what we think they should do. We instructed them not to eat anything offered to idols. They were told not to eat any meat with blood still in it or the meat of an animal that has been strangled. They were also told not to commit any terrible sexual sins.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya te bɔɔ lɛɛ Yesu di nɛ dɔɔ, da te ŋmarase tɔne yelee wɔ te de yako wɔ kpene keŋ dɔɔ bake see nɛ nyi be na di bane dɛɛ weenɛɛ ya. Be na di fatabo ya te be na di wonembu ŋon ba te kara ɔ nyee pɛte ɔ ba yem nɛ ya. Nɛnɛɛ baa lese ba wose lee kafoŋ nombia man.”

english_verse: The next day Paul took the four men with him and got himself ready at the same time they did. Then he went into the temple and told when the final ceremony would take place and when an offering would be made for each of them. Paul Is Arrested
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ tɛɛ kena keŋ na, te Pɔɔl moo balaŋ banaara baŋ, te be gyuuwɔ kegberaa ba wose. Te ɔ gyuu Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man ka see wee keŋ ba wose kegberaa keŋ gyae ke taŋ, na wee keŋ be man walaŋ kamasɛ waa fa Wurubuarɛ kedi. Ba kekyaŋ Pɔɔl

english_verse: When the period of seven days for the ceremony was almost over, some of the Jewish people from Asia saw Paul in the temple. They got a large crowd together and started attacking him.
translation_verse: Weeya nyetooro ŋan kɛɛ benaa keŋ nɛ, Gyudatena baale baŋ bɔɔ lee Esia tɛɛle man nɛ naa Pɔɔl Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man. Te be feefuwɔ do balaŋ dikpii keŋ bɔɔ gyaŋee botɔɔ nɛ te be kyaŋa e,

english_verse: They were shouting, “Friends, help us! This man goes around everywhere, saying bad things about our nation and about the Law of Moses and about this temple. He has even brought shame to this holy temple by bringing in Gentiles.”
translation_verse: te be fae yeesa nyi, “Iseraetena ɛ kaa kyɔ daa, balee kei yɛna walaŋ ŋon ɔ dɔŋ yenaŋ kamasɛ ɔ na wola balaŋ pou nombia tia de balaŋ na Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan ɔ be yeli Mosesi be ŋmarase see fa daa nɛ, na Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ. Lee kenaŋ wɔle koraŋ na, ɔ ta mo balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man mo ayimɛɛ kaa buraa ke.”

english_verse: Some of them thought that Paul had brought Trophimus from Ephesus into the temple, because they had seen them together in the city.
translation_verse: Keŋ dɔɔ bɔɔ yako mena yɛna nyi, na be wulaa naa Efeso baale ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Tirofimuse nɛ, na Pɔɔl dɔŋɛɛ donɔɔ keŋ man, mena dɔɔ na baŋ gyeŋ nyi Pɔɔl ta mo e gyu Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man.

english_verse: The whole city was in an uproar, and the people turned into a mob. They grabbed Paul and dragged him out of the temple. Then suddenly the doors were shut.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te donɔɔ keŋ pou siŋiiwɔ te balaŋ yeuwɔ lee yenaŋ kamasɛ kɔŋ kaa kyaŋ Pɔɔl te be wɔŋee e lee Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man. Karatetee dokoloŋ te be kyaŋa ka asima ŋan pou tɔ.

english_verse: The people were about to kill Paul when the Roman army commander heard that all Jerusalem was starting to riot.
translation_verse: Be kɛɛ gyae baa ko e nɛ, te tiila ke tuŋa Romantena yoonɔɔtena siamante ŋon nyi, Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ keŋ pou te siŋii.

english_verse: So he quickly took some soldiers and officers and ran to where the crowd had gathered. As soon as the mob saw the commander and soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.
translation_verse: Debokenaŋ man te ɔ moo yoonɔɔtena kegyiise na yoonɔɔtena baale, te be yeuwɔ gyu balaŋ dikpii keŋ gyaŋ. Balaŋ baŋ kena siamante ŋon na yoonɔɔtena baŋ nɛ, te be tinaa Pɔɔl ketuku yela.

english_verse: The army commander went over and arrested him and had him bound with two chains. Then he tried to find out who Paul was and what he had done.
translation_verse: Be siamante ŋon gyuu Pɔɔl gyaŋ te ɔ ke kyaŋa e te ɔ yakowɔ nyi baa mo agbaraagbaraase ala bake e. Keŋte ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyi, “Amɔte yɛna balee kei, te woŋ yaa ɔ yɛɛwɔ?”

english_verse: Part of the crowd shouted one thing, and part of them shouted something else. But they were making so much noise that the commander could not find out a thing. Then he ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ baale ne yako nombii dokoloŋ na baale mɔ ne yako ŋaale kpoo. Botɔɔ kee wosi dɔɔ, yoonɔɔtena siamante ŋon ta gyeŋ nombia ŋan nyee na ŋe nawɔɔ ya, mena dɔɔ ɔ yako ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ nyi baa mo e gyu ba dekyae man.

english_verse: As they reached the steps, the crowd became so wild that the soldiers had to lift Paul up and carry him.
translation_verse: Be ka mo e kelii botɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ mo gyeŋ gyoo deni keŋ man nɛ, balaŋ baŋ sia keyɛɛ dɔɔ yoonɔɔtena baŋ gate Pɔɔl seele adido.

english_verse: The crowd followed and kept shouting, “Kill him! Kill him!” Paul Speaks to the Crowd
translation_verse: Na balaŋ baŋ be silana wɔ nɛ, ne fae yeesa nyii, “Ɛ ko e.”

english_verse: When Paul was about to be taken into the fortress, he asked the commander, “Can I say something to you?” “How do you know Greek?” the commander asked.
translation_verse: Be kɛɛ gyae baa mo Pɔɔl gyoo yoonɔɔtena baŋ dekyae keŋ man nɛ, ɔ yako yoonɔɔtena siamante ŋon nyi, “Mɛɛ gyae maa yako neŋ nombii daale.” Te ɔ bɔɔse Pɔɔl nyi, “N ne nyii Giriki dei?”

english_verse: “Aren't you that Egyptian who started a riot not long ago and led
translation_verse: “Na nyaŋ yɛna Igyipite baale ŋon n be kyaa koro seŋ tia kegyiise te n ba mo awudiina kakpoŋse banaara (

english_verse: “No!” Paul replied. “I am a Jew from Tarsus, an important city in Cilicia. Please let me speak to the crowd.”
translation_verse: Te Pɔɔl tiranɔɔ fa e nyi, “Koa, me yɛɛ Gyuda baale e te ba lola maŋ bo Silisia tɛɛle man. Ma lee Tasuse donɔɔ keŋ ke dana yele nɛ man. Waaseda, fa maŋ gbɛɛ na maa kolosi fa balaŋ baŋ.”

english_verse: The commander told him he could speak, so Paul stood on the steps and motioned to the people. When they were quiet, he spoke to them in Aramaic:
translation_verse: Te yoonɔɔtena siamante ŋon faa e gbɛɛ nyi waa kolosi. Mena dɔɔ Pɔɔl seŋawɔ botɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ mo gyeŋ gyoo deni keŋ man nɛ, te ɔ gyinaa ɔ nyiŋmaa adido nyi baa wu be nɔɔ na baa tei e. Balaŋ baŋ ke wu be nɔɔse nɛ, te Pɔɔl kolosiwɔ Hiburu dei man fa wɔ.

english_verse: “My friends and leaders of our nation, listen as I explain what happened!”
translation_verse: Keŋte Pɔɔl kolosiwɔ lese ɔ nɔɔse nyiaa, “Me tebia na me kyaɔ, ɛ tei maŋ.”

english_verse: When the crowd heard Paul speak to them in Aramaic, they became even quieter. Then Paul said:
translation_verse: Be kenyii ɔ kekolosi na wɔ Hiburu dei man nɛ, be wuu be nɔɔse te botɔɔ yɛɛwɔ diiŋ. Te Pɔɔl yakowɔ nyi,

english_verse: I am a Jew, born in the city of Tarsus in Cilicia. But I grew up here in Jerusalem where I was a student of Gamaliel and was taught to follow every single law of our ancestors. In fact, I was just as eager to obey God as any of you are today.
translation_verse: “Me yɛɛ Gyuda baale e te ba lola maŋ bo Tasuse Silisia tɛɛle dɔɔ mɔna be kɛɛle maŋ bo Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man kɛbo, Na me yɛɛ Gamaleli tobaale e. Ɔ kɛɛle maŋ nideli de naanaɔ ɔsom amanberɛ gbɛɛ man te me yase me sia mo ma wose lɔ Wurubuarɛ dɔɔ ŋgba mena keŋ ɛ man walaŋ kamasɛ ne yɛɛ gyɛŋ nɛ.

english_verse: I made trouble for everyone who followed the Lord's Way, and I even had some of them killed. I had others arrested and put in jail. I didn't care if they were men or women.
translation_verse: Me naase baŋ be dɔŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu nyeedoŋ gbɛɛ kei dɔɔ nɛ diyem nideli keŋ nyi be baale koraŋ yekeewɔ, te me kyaŋa be man ala na baala pou ketɔ deni.

english_verse: The high priest and all the council members can tell you this is true. They even gave me letters to the Jewish leaders in Damascus, so that I could arrest people there and bring them to Jerusalem to be punished.
translation_verse: Sae kegyia na nombiadiira kegyiise baa di adansɛɛ nyi kpene keŋ mɛɛ yako nɛ yɛɛ anokoare. Nawolo nyi me lɛɛ tɔna koraŋ lee be gyaŋ, ŋan bɔɔ ŋmarase fa be tebia Gyudatena baŋ be kyaa Damasekuse nɛ, te me gyuuwɔ me kaa kyaŋ Kristotena baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ, na maa do wɔ akyakaase kɔŋ Gyerusalɛm na baa wɔŋ ba deŋela. Pɔɔl kekolosi lee ɔ kekyɛɛkee wose man

english_verse: One day about noon I was getting close to Damascus, when a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around me.
translation_verse: Keŋte Pɔɔl yakowɔ nyi, ma keta gyu ka benaa Damasekuse donɔɔ keŋ na weese ta seŋ dɛɛ. Karatetee dokoloŋ te keŋmaŋee dinaa daale lee adido kaa ŋmaale polɛ kilisi maŋ.

english_verse: I fell to the ground and heard a voice asking, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me?”
translation_verse: Kara maŋ lɔ tɛɛle man te me nyii nɔɔwoya ŋaale ne bɔɔse maŋ nyi, ‘Sɔɔl! Sɔɔl! Weera te n ne naase man diyem?’

english_verse: “Who are you?” I answered. The Lord replied, “I am Jesus from Nazareth! I am the one you are so cruel to.”
translation_verse: Te me bɔɔse e nyi, ‘Nyaŋ amɔte ŋonaŋ me Gbeŋgyoo?’ Te ɔ tiranɔɔ nyi ‘Maŋ Yesu Nasarɛte baale ŋon n ne naase diyem nɛ.’

english_verse: Those who were traveling with me saw the light, but did not hear the voice.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ daa na wɔ dɔŋɛɛ nɛ naa keŋmaŋee keŋ, mɔna be te nyii nɔɔwoya ŋan be kolosi na maŋ nɛ man ya.

english_verse: I asked, “Lord, what do you want me to do?” Then he told me, “Get up and go to Damascus. When you get there, you will be told what to do.”
translation_verse: Me bɔɔsewɔ nyi, ‘Woŋ te maa yɛɛ me Gbeŋgyoo?’ Te de Gbeŋgyoo yako maŋ nyi, ‘Koro gyu Damasekuse. Botɔɔ te baa yako neŋ kpene kamasɛ keŋ Wurubuarɛ ne gyae nyi n ke yɛɛ.’

english_verse: The light had been so bright that I couldn't see. And the others had to lead me by the hand to Damascus.
translation_verse: Keŋmaŋee dinaa keŋ yela me sia yɛlɛɛwɔ, mena dɔɔ balaŋ baŋ daa na wɔ dɔŋ nɛ, kyaŋa na maŋ me nyiŋmaa man gyu ka gyoo Damasekuse donɔɔ man.

english_verse: In that city there was a man named Ananias, who faithfully obeyed the Law of Moses and was well liked by all the Jewish people living there.
translation_verse: Balee ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Ananiase nɛ kɔŋawɔ kaa kɛɛ maŋ. Ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne yee Wurubuarɛ, te ɔ ne di Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ nideli, te Gyudatena baŋ pou be kyaa Damasekuse nɛ ne bu e nideli.

english_verse: He came to me and said, “Saul, my friend, you can now see again!” At once I could see.
translation_verse: Ɔ kɔŋawɔ kaa seŋ me gyaŋ te ɔ yakowɔ nyi, ‘Me dɔɔ Sɔɔl, gyuusu n sia na n kena.’ Debokenaŋ man te me sia gyuusuwɔ te me naa e.

english_verse: Then Ananias told me, “The God that our ancestors worshiped has chosen you to know what he wants done. He has chosen you to see the One Who Obeys God and to hear his voice.
translation_verse: Te Ananiase yako maŋ nyi, ‘De naanaɔ Wurubuarɛ lese na neŋ nyi nya gyeŋ kpene keŋ ɔ ne gyae nyi nya yɛɛ, na n kena ɔ walaŋ ŋon ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ, na n ke nyii ɔ nɔɔwoya.

english_verse: of the Lord who said to me, “Hurry and leave Jerusalem! The people won't listen to what you say about me.”
translation_verse: Keman te me naa de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu te ɔ yako maŋ nyi, ‘Yɛɛ bileŋ na n ka lee Gyerusalɛm debokeiman! Nawolo nyi, balaŋ kɛwɔ be gyae baa lɛɛ nya adansɛɛ keŋ n ne dii lee ma wose maŋ nɛ di ya.’

english_verse: I replied, “Lord, they know that in many of our synagogues I arrested and beat people who had faith in you.
translation_verse: Keŋte me tiranɔɔ nyi, ‘Me Gbeŋgyoo, balaŋ kɛwɔ gyeŋ nyi me gyuu Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man donɔɔ kamasɛ man, ke kyaŋee balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ lɛɛ neŋ di nɛ, te me tuku wɔ.

english_verse: Stephen was killed because he spoke for you, and I stood there and cheered them on. I even guarded the clothes of the men who murdered him.”
translation_verse: Te debaŋ keŋ bɛɛ ko Sitifin ŋon ɔ be di n wose man adansɛɛ nideli nɛ, na ma seŋɛɛ botɔɔ mɛɛ do wɔ kakyeŋ, te maŋ koraŋ kɛɛ na ba kegbase dɔɔ.’

english_verse: But the Lord told me to go, and he promised to send me far away to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Te de Gbeŋgyoo yako maŋ nyi, ‘Ta! Maa kpila neŋ lemlem, balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ gyaŋ.’ ”

english_verse: The crowd listened until Paul said this. Then they started shouting, “Get rid of this man! He doesn't deserve to live.”
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ tei e kelii debaŋ keŋ ɔ be kolosi nombia kɛŋa pou taŋ nɛ. Botɔɔ te be fae yeesa nyi, “Ɛ ko e! Ɛ ko e! Ɔ tekaboena nyi waa kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ ya.”

english_verse: They kept shouting. They waved their clothes around and threw dust into the air. Paul and the Roman Army Commander
translation_verse: Na bɛɛ fae yeesa, te ba kpee ba kegbase na bee fiili ŋa adido na be fuŋii aŋasa gyu adido.

english_verse: The Roman commander ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress and beaten with a whip. He did this to find out why the people were screaming at Paul.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ yoonɔɔtena siamante ŋon faree ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ nyi, baa mo Pɔɔl gyu yoonɔɔtena dekyae keŋ man na baa kebii e dambiise na baa bɔɔse e kpene keŋ dɔɔ balaŋ ne fae yeesa ɔ dɔɔ mena.

english_verse: While the soldiers were tying Paul up to be beaten, he asked the officer standing there, “Is it legal to beat a Roman citizen before he has been tried in court?”
translation_verse: Ba ke bake e baa tuku e nɛ te Pɔɔl bɔɔse yoonɔɔtena kegyia ŋon ɔ seŋɛɛ botɔɔ nɛ nyi, “Gbɛɛ doo nyi ya tuku Roman bu dalolasa ŋon ɛ te di ɔ nombia na ɛ ke bu e fɔɔ yee?”

english_verse: When the officer heard this, he went to the commander and said, “What are you doing? This man is a Roman citizen!”
translation_verse: Yoonɔɔtena kegyia ŋon ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, ɔ korowɔ gyu yoonɔɔtena siamante ŋon gyaŋ ke yako e nyi, “Woŋ yaa ɛ ne gyae ɛ ke yɛɛ nɛ? Balee kei yɛɛ Roman bu dalolasa e o!”

english_verse: The commander went to Paul and asked, “Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?” “Yes,” Paul answered.
translation_verse: Yoonɔɔtena siamante ŋon gyuu Pɔɔl gyaŋ ke bɔɔse e nyi, “Yako maŋ, n yɛɛ Roman bu dalolasa e?” Keŋte Pɔɔl tiranɔɔ nyi, “Ayi me yɛɛ Roman bu dalolasa e.”

english_verse: The commander then said, “I paid a lot of money to become a Roman citizen.” But Paul replied, “I was born a Roman citizen.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te yoonɔɔtena siamante ŋon yakowɔ nyi, “Maŋ tɔɔ kom dinaa pɛte ma bese Roman baale.” Te Pɔɔl mɔ yako e nyi, “Maŋ dɔɔ me yɛɛ Roman bu dalolasa e.”

english_verse: The men who were about to beat and question Paul quickly backed off. And the commander himself was frightened when he realized that he had put a Roman citizen in chains. Paul Is Tried by the Council
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ baŋ nafɔ bɔɔ yeli baa bɔɔse e nombia nɛ, tuuwɔ lee ɔ gyaŋ bileŋ. Debaŋ keŋ yoonɔɔtena siamante ŋon gbagba be naa nyi Pɔɔl yɛɛ Roman bu dalolasa e te bɔɔ do e akyakaase nɛ, gyakoloŋ gyoo e. Be kamo Pɔɔl gyu donɔɔ kegyiise siaman

english_verse: The next day the commander wanted to know the real reason why the Jewish leaders had brought charges against Paul. So he had Paul's chains removed, and he ordered the chief priests and the whole council to meet. Then he had Paul led in and made him stand in front of them.
translation_verse: Yoonɔɔtena siamante ŋon gyaewɔ waa gyeŋ kpene keŋ kitii dɔɔ Gyudatena ne kara Pɔɔl nɔɔse nɛ nideli, mena dɔɔ tɛɛ kena nɛ ɔ yela ba lese Pɔɔl akyakaase ŋan, te ɔ yela Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na donɔɔ kegyiise pou gyaŋeewɔ, te ɔ moo Pɔɔl kaa seŋ be siaman.

english_verse: Paul looked straight at the council members and said, “My friends, to this day I have served God with a clear conscience!”
translation_verse: Pɔɔl kɛɛ kegyiise baŋ bɔɔ gyaŋee nɛ sia gbɛɛ te ɔ yakowɔ nyi, “Me tebia Iseraetena, me konɔɔ bee bu maŋ fɔɔ gbɛɛ kamasɛ man ya, nawolo nyi me kyaawɔ nideli Wurubuarɛ siaman kaa lii naŋ gyɛŋ.”

english_verse: Then Ananias the high priest ordered the men standing beside Paul to hit him on the mouth.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ sae kegyia Ananiase faewɔ do baŋ ba seŋɛɛ benaa na Pɔɔl nɛ nyi, baa gyɔ e ɔ nɔɔ gbɛɛ.

english_verse: Paul turned to the high priest and said, “You whitewashed wall! God will hit you. You sit there to judge me by the Law of Moses. But at the same time you order men to break the Law by hitting me.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Pɔɔl mɔ yako e nyi, “Wurubuarɛ waa gyɔ nyaŋ mɔ. Kɛɛ ɔ ke kyaa botɔɔ ŋgba deni dekyaŋ keŋ bɔɔ gyilaa ke wɔle wolom nɛ. N kyaa botɔɔ n na mo mmaraa di me nombia, mɔna nyaŋ gbagba mɔ n ta fom Wurubuarɛ mmaraa, ŋgba n be fae do wɔ nyi baa gyɔ maŋ nɛ.”

english_verse: The men standing beside Paul asked, “Don't you know you are insulting God's high priest?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te balaŋ baŋ ba seŋɛɛ benaa na Pɔɔl nɛ yakowɔ nyi, “Wurubuarɛ sae kegyia yaa n na tee nɛ!”

english_verse: Paul replied, “Oh! I didn't know he was the high priest. The Scriptures do tell us not to speak evil about a leader of our people.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Pɔɔl tiranɔɔ nyi, “O me tebia, me ta gyem nyi ŋon yɛna Wurubuarɛ sae kegyia ŋon ya. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako nyi, ‘Ɛ na kolosi walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne di balaŋ dɔɔ nɛ wose man nombiikum ya.’ ”

english_verse: When Paul saw that some of the council members were Sadducees and others were Pharisees, he shouted, “My friends, I am a Pharisee and the son of a Pharisee. I am on trial simply because I believe that the dead will be raised to life.”
translation_verse: Te Pɔɔl kena nyi balaŋ baŋ baale yɛɛ Sadukiitenawɔ te baale mɔ yɛɛ Farasiitenawɔ nɛ ɔ fae yeesa nyi, “Me tebia, me yɛɛ Farasii baale e, te me kya mɔ yɛɛ Farasii baale e. Keŋ dɔɔ bee di me nombia yɛna nyi, me sia gyakaa nyi baŋ bɔɔ yem nɛ baa foŋ lee yeŋ man.”

english_verse: As soon as Paul said this, the Pharisees and the Sadducees got into a big argument, and the council members started taking sides.
translation_verse: Ɔ ke yako mena nɛ, kale gyoo Sadukiitena na Farasiitena baŋ man te dikpii keŋ kpasewɔ akpeŋ ala.

english_verse: The Sadducees do not believe in angels or spirits or that the dead will rise to life. But the Pharisees believe in all of these,
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi Sadukiitena ne yako nyi yeŋ wɔle kefoŋ be kyaa bela ya, yaa Wurubuarɛ kpilala na feliŋkumɛɛ be kyaa ya, mɔna Farasiitena ne lɛɛ kɛŋa pou di nyi ŋe kyaa.

english_verse: and so there was a lot of shouting. Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees. Finally, they became angry and said, “We don't find anything wrong with this man. Maybe a spirit or an angel really did speak to him.”
translation_verse: Kale dinaa daale gyoo wɔ, keŋte Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ be yɛɛ Farasiitenawɔ nɛ, man baale korowɔ seŋ keŋkeŋ nyi, “Da te naa dukum keŋ balee kei be yɛɛ ya! Mberem Wurubuarɛ feliŋ yaa Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋolo kolosila na e.”

english_verse: The argument became fierce, and the commander was afraid that Paul would be pulled apart. So he ordered the soldiers to go in and rescue Paul. Then they took him back into the fortress.
translation_verse: Kale keŋ nɔɔ ke yɛɛ doŋ nɛ, yoonɔɔtena siamante ŋon yeewɔ nyi mberem baa tirii Pɔɔl kpetekpetekpete. Mena dɔɔ ɔ faewɔ do ɔ yoonɔɔtena baŋ nyi baa lɛo Pɔɔl doŋ man lee balaŋ baŋ gyaŋ, na baa mo e gyu ba dekyae man.

english_verse: That night the Lord stood beside Paul and said, “Don't worry! Just as you have told others about me in Jerusalem, you must also tell about me in Rome.” A Plot To Kill Paul
translation_verse: Wee kenaŋ nelim nɛ, de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu kɔŋawɔ kaa seŋ Pɔɔl gyaŋ yako e nyi, “Nyiŋ konɔɔ! Na yee ya! Mena keŋ n be di ma wose man adansɛɛ Gyerusalɛm kɛbo nɛ, mena mɔ te kaboena nyi nya di ma wose man adansɛɛ Roman donɔɔ man.” Ba kebake Pɔɔl nyee man apam

english_verse: Then some of them went to the chief priests and the nation's leaders and said, “We have promised God that we would not eat a thing until we have killed Paul.
translation_verse: Te be gyuu Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na donɔɔ kegyiise baŋ gyaŋ ke yako wɔ nyi, “Da te kaŋ ntam nyi, de nɔɔ be gyae ke daa kolo ya, kelii debaŋ keŋ dɛɛ ko Pɔɔl.

english_verse: You and everyone in the council must go to the commander and pretend that you want to find out more about the charges against Paul. Ask for him to be brought before your court. Meanwhile, we will be waiting to kill him before he gets there.”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛmɛɛ na nombiadiira baŋ ɛ kpila gyu Romantena yoonɔɔtena siamante ŋon gyaŋ ka sola e na waa mo Pɔɔl kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ. Ɛ gyu ka beo e nyi ɛ ne gyae ɛ ka gyoo Pɔɔl nombia ŋan man nideli, mɔna nyi ɔ fa gbɛɛ nyi baa mo e kɔŋ na, da te desina da wose nyi dɛɛ koe e gbɛɛnaa man.”

english_verse: When Paul's nephew heard about the plot, he went to the fortress and told Paul about it.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl wa agbelebii nyii nombii kei na, te ɔ korowɔ gyu yoonɔɔtena baŋ dekyae man ke bɔ Pɔɔl amandɛɛ.

english_verse: So Paul said to one of the army officers, “Take this young man to the commander. He has something to tell him.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Pɔɔl baake yoonɔɔtena baŋ man kegyia ŋolo te ɔ yako e nyi, “Mo tobaalebii kei gyu yoonɔɔtena siamante ŋon gyaŋ, nawolo nyi ɔ dana nombii daale waa yako e.”

english_verse: The officer took him to the commander and said, “The prisoner named Paul asked me to bring this young man to you, because he has something to tell you.”
translation_verse: Te ɔ moo tobaalebii ŋon gyu yoonɔɔtena siamante ŋon gyaŋ te yoonɔɔtena kegyia ŋon yako e nyi, “Pɔɔl ŋon ɔ doo deni man nɛ kpila la baake maŋ te ɔ yako maŋ nyi maa mo tobaalebii kei kɔŋ n gyaŋ, nawolo nyi ɔ dana nombii daale waa yako neŋ.”

english_verse: The commander took the young man aside and asked him in private, “What do you want to tell me?”
translation_verse: Yoonɔɔtena siamante ŋon kyaŋa bu ŋon nyiŋmaa man te ɔ mo e lee yenaŋ bɔɔse e nyi, “Woŋ te n dana n ke yako maŋ?”

english_verse: He answered, “Some men are planning to ask you to bring Paul down to the Jewish council tomorrow. They will claim they want to find out more about him.
translation_verse: Keŋte bu ŋon tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Gyudatena kegyiise te yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ nyi baa sola neŋ na n ke yela Pɔɔl ke gyu kena wɔ kyoo. Baa beo neŋ nyi bɛɛ gyae baa gyoo ɔ nombia ŋan man nideli.

english_verse: The commander sent the young man away after saying to him, “Don't let anyone know you told me this.” Paul Is Sent to Felix the Governor
translation_verse: Te yoonɔɔtena siamante ŋon lɔɔ tobaalebii ŋon gbɛɛ te ɔ faree e nyi, “Na yeli ŋolo ke nyii nyi, n te kaa yako maŋ mena nombia kɛŋa ya.” Be kalese Pɔɔl gyu Kaesaria donɔɔ man

english_verse: The commander called in two of his officers and told them, “By nine o'clock tonight have
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ yoonɔɔtena siamante ŋon baake ɔ yoonɔɔtena kegyiise baŋ man balaŋ bala te ɔ yako wɔ nyi, “Ɛ gyae yoonɔɔtena sɔŋonoŋ akpeŋ ala (

english_verse: Get a horse ready for Paul and make sure he gets safely through to Felix the governor.”
translation_verse: Ɛ gyae dɛɛkyɔŋ fa Pɔɔl na ɛ ka mo e ka do gyoo Felisi ŋon ɔ ne kɛɛ botɔɔ tɛɛle dɔɔ nɛ nyiŋmaa man wosefɛɛreŋ man.”

english_verse: The commander wrote a letter that said:
translation_verse: Te yoonɔɔtena siamante ŋon ŋmarase tɔne fa wɔ nyi.

english_verse: Greetings from Claudius Lysias to the Honorable Governor Felix:
translation_verse: “Maŋ Klaudiuse Lisiase ne ŋmarasena tɔne kei mee yelee nyaŋ gyoo kpaakpaa Felisi. Mɛɛ do neŋ nɔɔ.

english_verse: Some Jews grabbed this man and were about to kill him. But when I found out that he was a Roman citizen, I took some soldiers and rescued him.
translation_verse: Gyudatena kyaŋana balee kei nyi baa ko e, te ma moo me yoonɔɔtena te de gyuuwɔ ke lɛɛ e, nawolo nyi me naawɔ nyi ɔ yɛɛ Roman bu dalolasa e.

english_verse: I wanted to find out what they had against him. So I brought him before their council
translation_verse: Me gyaewɔ maa gyeŋ kpene keŋ dɔɔ bɔɔ mo nombiakumɛɛ gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ, mena dɔɔ ma moo e gyu baŋ gbagba be kegyiise siaman.

english_verse: and learned that the charges concern only their religious laws. This man isn't guilty of anything for which he should die or even be put in jail.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te me naawɔ nyi, nombia ŋan bɔɔ mo gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ nɛ, yɛɛ ba mmaraase nombia ŋa, mɔna ma te naa kpene keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ keŋ kaboena nyi baa ko e yaa baa kyaŋ e tɔ deni ya.

english_verse: The soldiers obeyed the commander's orders, and that same night they took Paul to the city of Antipatris.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ yoonɔɔtena baŋ yɛɛwɔ ŋgba mena keŋ be kegyia ŋon be faree wɔ nyi baa yɛɛ nɛ. Ba moo Pɔɔl nelim kenaŋ menɛɛ te be keliiwɔ Antipatirise donɔɔ man.

english_verse: The next day the foot soldiers returned to the fortress and let the soldiers on horseback take him the rest of the way.
translation_verse: Tɛɛ kena nɛ, yoonɔɔtena baŋ be dɔŋ na be nawɔɔ nɛ, besewɔ gyu be dalee man, te baŋ bɛɛ kyaa dɛɛkyɔŋse nɛ moo e laŋ.

english_verse: When they came to Caesarea, they gave the letter to the governor and handed Paul over to him.
translation_verse: Be ka gyoo Kaesaria donɔɔ man nɛ, ba moo tɔne keŋ fa gyoo Felisi te ba moo Pɔɔl do ɔ nyiŋmaa man.

english_verse: The governor read the letter. Then he asked Paul and found out that he was from Cilicia.
translation_verse: Gyoo ŋon kala tɔne keŋ te ɔ bɔɔse Pɔɔl nyi, tɛɛle kei yenaŋ woŋti man yaa n leewɔ. Gyoo ŋon ke nyii nyi ɔ lee Silisia tɛɛle man nɛ,

english_verse: The governor said, “I will listen to your case as soon as the people come to bring their charges against you.” After saying this, he gave orders for Paul to be kept as a prisoner in Herod's palace.
translation_verse: ɔ yakowɔ nyi, “Debaŋ keŋ balaŋ baŋ ɛmɛɛ na wɔ be nyiŋ nombia de kɔŋ nɛ, na maa nyii n nɔɔwoya.” Te ɔ yakowɔ nyi baa mo Pɔɔl gyu gyoo Herode dekpaŋalaŋ dinaa keŋ man na baa gyee e.

english_verse: Five days later Ananias the high priest, together with some of their leaders and a lawyer named Tertullus, went to the governor to present their case against Paul.
translation_verse: Weeya anoŋ wɔle nɛ, Wurubuarɛ sae kegyia Ananiase na donɔɔ kegyiise baale na Lɔya ŋon bɛɛ baake e Tɛtulɔse nɛ gyuu Kaesaria ke tɔlɛɛ be nɔɔwoya fa gyoo Felisi lee Pɔɔl wose man.

english_verse: So Paul was called in, and Tertullus stated the case against him: Honorable Felix, you have brought our people a long period of peace, and because of your concern our nation is much better off.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te be baake Pɔɔl kɔŋ. Keŋte Tɛtulɔse tɔlɛɛ ɔ nɔɔwoya lee Pɔɔl wose man nyi, Gyoo kpaakpaa, de te nyiŋ wosefɛɛreŋ kyare n gyoori kedi kei man. Nawolo nyi nyansa keŋ n ba mo di gyoori nɛ, te desina abɔɔ burum de tɛɛle kei dɔɔ.

english_verse: I don't want to bother you, but please be patient with us and listen to me for just a few minutes.
translation_verse: Mɔna me bɛɛ gyae maa wɔlɛɛ n debaŋ bela ya, mena dɔɔ mɛɛ sola neŋ bo, nyiŋ konɔɔ na n ke tei daa kyomii.

english_verse: This man has been found to be a real pest and troublemaker for our people all over the world. He is also a leader of a group called Nazarenes.
translation_verse: “Da gyeŋ nyi balee kei yɛɛ tɛɛle yɛlɛɛre e. Ɔ te loo nombia do Gyudatena baŋ be kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ pou nɛ, te ɔ yɛɛ Nasarɛtena ɔsom siamante mɔ e.

english_verse: When he tried to disgrace the temple, we arrested him. If you question him, you will find out for yourself that our charges are true.
translation_verse: Akpaa n bɔɔse e ɔ nɔɔwoya na, nyaŋ gbagba n kena nyi ŋan pou dɔɔ yako lee ɔ wose man nɛ, doo na ke gbɛɛ.”

english_verse: The Jewish crowd spoke up and agreed with what Tertullus had said. Paul Defends Himself
translation_verse: Te Gyudatena mɔ moo be nɔɔ do nyi mena nombia ŋan dɔɔ yako lee ɔ wose man nɛ, doo na ŋe gbɛɛ. Pɔɔl kekolosi lese ɔ nɔɔse

english_verse: The governor motioned for Paul to speak, and he began: I know that you have judged the people of our nation for many years, and I am glad to defend myself in your court.
translation_verse: Gyoo Felisi ke fa Pɔɔl gbɛɛ nyi waa kolosi nɛ, Pɔɔl tɔlɛɛ ɔ nɔɔwoya nyi, “Ma gyeŋ nyi n ne di nombia tɛɛle kei dɔɔ te yɛɛ gyɛŋ ya. Mena dɔɔ dana maŋ gyoŋ ŋgba mɛɛ kolosi maa lese me nɔɔse fa neŋ nɛ.

english_verse: It was no more than twelve days ago that I went to worship in Jerusalem. You can find this out easily enough.
translation_verse: Nyaŋ gbagba n gyoo ke man na, n kena nyi weeya kufu ala yaa ŋgba mɔɔ gyu Gyerusalɛm me kaa som nɛ.

english_verse: Never once did the Jews find me arguing with anyone in the temple. I didn't cause trouble in the synagogues or in the city itself.
translation_verse: Te balaŋ baŋ daa na wɔ be nyim nɛ, te naa daa na ŋolo ke lɛɛ aŋmaareŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man ya. Ba te naa maŋ nyi maa loo nombia do balaŋ Gyudatena ɔsom deni man, yaa yenaŋ daale ya.

english_verse: There is no way that they can prove these charges they are now bringing against me.
translation_verse: Te bɔɔ gyae baa tale gyɔ be konɔɔ na baa seŋ kolo dɔɔ wola nyi nombia ŋan bɛɛ mo gyakaa me dɔɔ nɛ yɛɛ ampaŋ ya.

english_verse: I admit that their leaders think the Lord's Way which I follow is based on wrong beliefs. But I still worship the same God that my ancestors worshiped. And I believe everything written in the Law of Moses and in the Prophets.
translation_verse: Mɔna ma te sɛɛ nyi, mɛɛ som de naanaɔ Wurubuarɛ lee de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu gbɛɛ keŋ baŋ ne yako nyi ke yɛɛ ɔsom kum ke nɛ man. Te mɛɛ lɛɛ kpene kamasɛ keŋ bɔɔ ŋmarase do Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan ɔ be yeli Mosesi be ŋmarase see nɛ, na Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra tɔna man nɛ di.

english_verse: I am just as sure as these people are that God will raise from death everyone, good or evil.
translation_verse: Me loo gyakaa Wurubuarɛ nombii dokoloŋ kei dɔɔ nyi, yeŋ wɔle na, balaŋ kpaakpaa na balaŋ kumɛɛ pou baa foŋ lee yeŋ man ŋgba mena keŋ baŋ mɔ loo gyakaa nɛ.

english_verse: And because I am sure, I try my best to have a clear conscience in whatever I do for God or for people.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ yɛɛ kakyeŋ wee kamasɛ na maa yɛɛ kpene keŋ me konɔɔ be gyae ke bu maŋ fɔɔ Wurubuarɛ na balaŋ siaman ya.

english_verse: After being away for several years, I returned here to bring gifts for the poor people of my nation and to offer sacrifices.
translation_verse: Me ka lee Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man kulutooneŋ burum wɔle nɛ, ma besewɔ kɔŋ botɔɔ te ma moo kɔba na abɔɔ kɔna me tebia Gyudatena baŋ na baa mo fa ayematena, te me faa Wurubuarɛ mɔ kedi.

english_verse: This is what I was doing when I was found going through a ceremony in the temple. I wasn't with a crowd, and there was no uproar.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kei man te be naa maŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man na ma te gberaa ma wose taŋ, na balaŋ dikpii mɔ be kpuɛ na maŋ ya, te gidigidi mɔ te kɔŋ botɔɔ ya.

english_verse: Some Jews from Asia were there at that time, and if they have anything to say against me, they should be here now.
translation_verse: Mɔna Gyudatena baale baŋ bɔɔ lee Esia tɛɛle dɔɔ nɛ kɔŋawɔ botɔɔ. Baŋ te nafɔ kaboena nyi baa kɔŋ n siaman kɛbo, na nombii keŋ doo na dɛɛ gyoo ke man.

english_verse: Or ask the ones who are here. They can tell you that they didn't find me guilty of anything when I was tried by their own council.
translation_verse: Te n gyae mɔ na, yela baŋ ba seŋɛɛ kɛbo nɛ ke yako dukum keŋ bɔɔ naa lee ma wose man debaŋ keŋ bɔɔ kyaŋ maŋ gyu donɔɔ kegyiise baŋ gyaŋ nɛ.

english_verse: The only charge they can bring against me is what I shouted out in court, when I said, “I am on trial today because I believe that the dead will be raised to life!”
translation_verse: Nombii dokoloŋ keŋ mɔɔ gyɔ me konɔɔ yako be siaman yɛna nyi, baŋ bɔɔ yem nɛ baa bese foŋ lee yeŋ man bela. Mena nombii kei dɔɔ te bee di me nombia gyɛŋ nɛ.”

english_verse: Felix knew a lot about the Lord's Way. But he brought the trial to an end and said, “I will make my decision after Lysias the commander arrives.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Felisi ŋon ɔ wulaa ɔ gyeŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu nyeedoŋ gbɛɛ keŋ wose man nombia nideli nɛ, tuu nombia kedi wee keŋ see wee wɔle. Keŋte ɔ yakowɔ nyi, “Wee keŋ yoonɔɔtena kegyia Lisiase ŋon de kɔŋ na, maa di n nombia kɛŋa.”

english_verse: He then ordered the army officer to keep Paul under guard, but not to lock him up or to stop his friends from helping him. Paul Is Kept under Guard
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ yako yoonɔɔtena kegyia ŋon bɔɔ mo Pɔɔl do ɔ nyiŋmaa man nɛ nyi waa kɛɛ ɔ dɔɔ nideli, be naa seŋ ɔ dɔɔ beiŋ ya. Mɔna waa fa ɔ tebia gbɛɛ baa fa e kpene kamasɛ keŋ ne hia e.

english_verse: Several days later Felix and his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, went to the place where Paul was kept under guard. They sent for Paul and listened while he spoke to them about having faith in Christ Jesus.
translation_verse: Ke di weeya ala ŋaale nɛ, Felisi na ɔ wɛɛle Durusila ŋon ɔ yɛɛ Gyuda alo e nɛ kɔŋawɔ botɔɔ. Keŋte Felisi yela Pɔɔl kɔŋawɔ kaa kolosi fa wɔ, te Pɔɔl kolosiwɔ lee Kristo Yesu kelɛɛdi wose man.

english_verse: But Felix was frightened when Paul started talking to them about doing right, about self-control, and about the coming judgment. So he said to Paul, “That's enough for now. You may go. But when I have time I will send for you.”
translation_verse: Pɔɔl ke gyakaa ɔ ne kolosi lee abɔɔ ŋan tenɛɛ na deeli keyɛɛ, na wose dɔɔ kedi na mena keŋ Yesu waa di balaŋ pou nombia wee daale nɛ, gyakoloŋ gyoo Felisi te ɔ yakowɔ nyi, “Tekaboe mena ta pɛ, me nyiŋ ma wose na maa baake neŋ bela.”

english_verse: After this, Felix often sent for Paul and talked with him, because he hoped that Paul would offer him a bribe.
translation_verse: Na ɔ sia gyakaa nyi Pɔɔl waa fa e kɔba na waa tina e yela mena dɔɔ ɔ kyaa ɔ kyaa na, ɔ te baake Pɔɔl kolosi na e.

english_verse: Two years later Porcius Festus became governor in place of Felix. But since Felix wanted to do the Jewish leaders a favor, he kept Paul in jail.
translation_verse: Kulutooneŋse ala wɔle nɛ, Pokiuse Fesituse kaa dii gyoori gyoo Felisi nawɔɔ man. Mɔna keŋ Felisi ne gyae waa yɛɛ Gyudatena deeli dɔɔ, ɔ ta lese Pɔɔl lee deni man ya.

english_verse: Three days after Festus had become governor, he went from Caesarea to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Fesituse ke kɔŋ tɛɛle keŋ dɔɔ ɔ ne di gyoori nɛ, ka weeya atooro wɔle na ɔ leewɔ Kaesaria gyu Gyerusalɛm.

english_verse: There the chief priests and some Jewish leaders told him about their charges against Paul. They also asked Festus
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na Gyudatena ɔsom kegyiise moo Pɔɔl nombia ŋan baŋ kpa ɔ be yɛɛ nɛ kaa see ɔ siaman.

english_verse: if he would be willing to bring Paul to Jerusalem. They begged him to do this because they were planning to attack and kill Paul on the way.
translation_verse: Ba sola Fesituse nyi waa wii wɔ na waa yela baa lese Pɔɔl kɔŋ Gyerusalɛm nawolo nyi, na ba te bake ba nyeeman nyi de kɔŋ mena na baa yo gbɛɛnaa man na baa deke e ko.

english_verse: But Festus told them, “Paul will be kept in Caesarea, and I am soon going there myself.
translation_verse: Keŋte Fesituse tiranɔɔ nyi, “Pɔɔl waa doo deni man Kaesaria botɔɔ, nawolo nyi maŋ gbagba maa gyu botɔɔ weeya ala kɛŋa man.

english_verse: If he has done anything wrong, let your leaders go with me and bring charges against him there.”
translation_verse: Akpaa nyi ɔ te yɛɛ nombiikum daale na ɛ yela ɛ kegyiise baale ke sila maŋ gyu Kaesaria na be kaa wola dukum keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ.”

english_verse: Festus stayed in Jerusalem for eight or ten more days before going to Caesarea. Then the next day he took his place as judge and had Paul brought into court.
translation_verse: Ɔ ke kyaa be gyaŋ weeya gyanaara yaa kufu nɛ, ɔ korowɔ gyu Kaesaria. Ke tɛɛ kena nɛ, ɔ baake nombia ŋan te ɔ yela ba moo Pɔɔl kɔŋ be siaman.

english_verse: As soon as Paul came in, the leaders from Jerusalem crowded around him and said he was guilty of many serious crimes. But they could not prove anything.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl ka betaa bɛɛ lee pɛ, te Gyudatena baŋ bɔɔ lee Gyerusalɛm nɛ kaa seŋawɔ kilisi e te be fiasɛɛ bɛɛ mo nombiakumɛɛ dinaana gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ, mɔna be te nyiŋ nombii keŋ baa seŋ ke dɔɔ wola nyi ŋe yɛɛ ampaŋ ya.

english_verse: Then Paul spoke in his own defense, “I have not broken the Law of my people. And I have not done anything against either the temple or the Emperor.”
translation_verse: Pɔɔl kolosiwɔ lese ɔ nɔɔse nyi, “Ma te yɛɛ nombiikum kamasɛ tia Gyudatena mmaraase yaa Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ ya. Ma te yɛɛ nombiikum tia Romantena gyoo dinaa mɔ ya.”

english_verse: Festus wanted to please the leaders. So he asked Paul, “Are you willing to go to Jerusalem and be tried by me on these charges?”
translation_verse: Fesituse gyaewɔ waa yɛɛ Gyudatena deeli mena dɔɔ, ɔ bɔɔse Pɔɔl nyi, “N ne gyae n ke sila maŋ gyu Gyerusalɛm na me kaa di n nombia ŋan botɔɔ?”

english_verse: Paul replied, “I am on trial in the Emperor's court, and that's where I should be tried. You know very well I have not done anything to harm the Jewish nation.
translation_verse: Keŋte Pɔɔl tiranɔɔ nyi, “Gyoo dinaa Kaesa nombia dedii dekpaŋalaŋ man yaa ma seŋɛɛ nɛ. Mena dɔɔ kɛbo te kaboena nyi baa di me nombia. Nyaŋ gbagba n gyeŋ nideli nyi ma te yɛɛ Gyudatena dukum kamasɛ ya.

english_verse: If I had done something deserving death, I would not ask to escape the death penalty. But I am not guilty of any of these crimes, and no one has the right to hand me over to these people. I now ask to be tried by the Emperor himself.”
translation_verse: Akpaa ma te yɛɛ dukum keŋ kaboena nyi baa ko man na, baa tale ko maŋ, mɔɔ ne bɛɛ ya. Mɔna akpaa nyi nombia ŋan bɛɛ mo gyakaa me dɔɔ yɛɛ akɛrɛɛ ŋa na, ŋolo be gyae waa tale mo maŋ do be nyiŋmaa man nyi baa ko man ya. Akpaa nnɛ te doo na mɛɛ sola neŋ bo, mo maŋ gyu Roman gyoo dinaa ŋon gyaŋ.”

english_verse: After Festus had talked this over with members of his council, he told Paul, “You have asked to be tried by the Emperor, and to the Emperor you will go!” Paul Speaks to Agrippa and Bernice
translation_verse: Gyoo Fesituse na ɔ kegyiise baŋ ka lee yenaŋ kolosi na dɔŋa taŋ nɛ ɔ kaa tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Gyoo dinaa Kaesaria gyaŋ te n ne gyae baa di n nombia, mena dɔɔ ɔ gyaŋ te n ke gyu.” Fesituse kekolosi na gyoo Agiripa

english_verse: A few days later King Agrippa and Bernice came to Caesarea to visit Festus.
translation_verse: Ke di weeya ala nɛ, gyoo Agiripa na ɔ deesi Bɛɛnise kɔŋawɔ Kaesaria kaa do Fesituse nɔɔ.

english_verse: They had been there for several days, when Festus told the king about the charges against Paul. He said: Felix left a man here in jail,
translation_verse: Be ke di weeya ala ŋaale botɔɔ nɛ, te Fesituse moo Pɔɔl nombia ŋan see gyoo Agiripa siaman e nyiaa, “Tobaale ŋolo kyaa kɛbo kei ŋon gyoo Felisi be yeli ɔ be dɔɔ deni man,

english_verse: and when I went to Jerusalem, the chief priests and the Jewish leaders came and asked me to find him guilty.
translation_verse: te debaŋ keŋ mɔɔ gyu Gyerusalɛm nɛ, Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na Gyudatena ɔsom kegyiise moo nombia gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ te be yako maŋ nyi maa bu e fɔɔ.

english_verse: I told them it isn't the Roman custom to hand a man over to people who are bringing charges against him. He must first have the chance to meet them face to face and to defend himself against their charges.
translation_verse: Mɔna me yako wɔ nyi, daa Romantena kɛɛ yɛɛ yɛna nyi, akpaa balaŋ bala de nyiŋ nombia te da te gyaŋee ta wɔ na baa lese be nɔɔse ya na, de bee bu fɔɔ ya.

english_verse: So when they came here with me, I wasted no time. On the very next day I took my place on the judge's bench and ordered him to be brought in.
translation_verse: Te daa na wɔ ke kɔŋ kɛbo nɛ, ma te kyaa nombia ŋan dɔɔ kyare ya, mɔna tɛɛ kena nɛ, me baake nombia ŋan te me yela ba lese balee ŋon kɔŋ.

english_verse: But when the men stood up to make their charges against him, they did not accuse him of any of the crimes that I thought they would.
translation_verse: Te balaŋ baŋ ŋon na wɔ be nyim nɛ korowɔ tɔlɛɛ be nɔɔwoya. Nombiakumɛɛ ŋan maŋ ba gyueŋ nyi nafɔ baa yako lee ɔ wose man nɛ, na ŋan gba te be yakowɔ ya.

english_verse: Instead, they argued with him about some of their beliefs and about a dead man named Jesus, who Paul said was alive.
translation_verse: Mɔna baŋ na Pɔɔl lɛɛ dɔŋa aŋmaareŋ bo lee be Wurubuarɛ ɔsom wose man, na balee ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Yesu ŋon ɔ ba yem te Pɔɔl ne gyɔ ɔ konɔɔ yako nyi ɔ delena nɛ wose man.

english_verse: Since I did not know how to find out the truth about all this, I asked Paul if he would be willing to go to Jerusalem and be put on trial there.
translation_verse: Na mɔɔ gyeŋ mena keŋ maa gyoo nombia kɛŋa dinɔɔ man ya, mena dɔɔ me bɔɔse Pɔɔl nyi waa sɛɛ gyu Gyerusalɛm na be kaa di ɔ nombia botɔɔ?

english_verse: But Paul asked to be kept in jail until the Emperor could decide his case. So I ordered him to be kept here until I could send him to the Emperor.
translation_verse: Mɔna Pɔɔl solawɔ nyi baa yela waa doo be nyiŋmaa man na gyoo dinaa Kaesa kedi ɔ nombia. Mena dɔɔ me yela be gyee e kelii debaŋ keŋ maa kaa mo e gyina gyoo dinaa ŋon.”

english_verse: Then Agrippa said to Festus, “I would also like to hear what this man has to say.” Festus answered, “You can hear him tomorrow.”
translation_verse: Keŋte gyoo Agiripa yako Fesituse nyi, “Maŋ gbagba ne gyae nyi maa nyii balee kei nɔɔwoya.” Te Fesituse tiranɔɔ fa e nyi, “Nyaŋ ke nyii ɔ nɔɔwoya kyoo.”

english_verse: The next day Agrippa and Bernice made a big show as they came into the meeting room. High ranking army officers and leading citizens of the town were also there. Festus then ordered Paul to be brought in
translation_verse: Ke tɛɛ kena nɛ, gyoo Agiripa na Bɛɛnise desinaa ba wose te yoonɔɔtena kegyiise na donɔɔ kegyiise sila wɔ kaa gyoo deni keŋ man bɛɛ gyaŋee nɛ. Be kee gyu na nɔɔneŋ na suya silana wɔ. Te Fesituse yela ba moo Pɔɔl kɔŋ.

english_verse: and said: King Agrippa and other guests, look at this man! Every Jew from Jerusalem and Caesarea has come to me, demanding for him to be put to death.
translation_verse: Te Fesituse yakowɔ nyi, “Gyoo Agiripa na baŋ pou e be yilaa kɛbo, ɛ ne naa balee kei. Gyudatena baŋ be kyaa Gyerusalɛm na Kaesaria kɛbo kaa wiiwɔ fa maŋ nyi kaboena nyi baa ko e.

english_verse: I have not found him guilty of any crime deserving death. But because he has asked to be judged by the Emperor, I have decided to send him to Rome.
translation_verse: Maŋ te naa dukum keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ keŋ kaboena nyi baa koe ya. Mɔna ɔ ka sola nyi Roman gyoo dinaa waa di ɔ nombia nɛ, mena dɔɔ te me yɛɛ ma gyueŋ nyi maa mo e yelee e.

english_verse: I have to write some facts about this man to the Emperor. So I have brought him before all of you, but especially before you, King Agrippa. After we have talked about his case, I will then have something to write.
translation_verse: Mɔna mɔɔ taŋ kolo kitii maa ŋmarase yelee gyoo dinaa lee ɔ wose man ya. Mena dɔɔ te ma moo e kɔŋ ɛ popou siaman tuturu na, nyaŋ gyoo Agiripa gyaŋ nyi n gyoo ɔ nombia ŋan man na, maa nyiŋ kolo ŋmarase lee ɔ wose man yelee gyoo dinaa ŋon.

english_verse: It makes no sense to send a prisoner to the Emperor without stating the charges against him.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi me naawɔ ma agyueŋ man nyi, tekaboena nyi maa mo walaŋ ŋon ɔ doo deni man nɛ yelee gyoo dinaa keŋ nyi, mɔɔ tale wola nombiikum keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ ya.”

english_verse: King Agrippa, I am glad for this chance to defend myself before you today on all these charges my own people have brought against me.
translation_verse: “Gyoo Agiripa, ma te yɛɛ nyeebam ŋgba gyɛŋ wee kei ma seŋɛɛ n siaman mɛɛ lese me nɔɔse lee nombia ŋan pou Gyudatena ba moo gyakaa me dɔɔ nɛ.

english_verse: You know a lot about our religious customs and the beliefs that divide us. So I ask you to listen patiently to me.
translation_verse: Gbagbaagba na te yɛɛ maŋ gyoŋ nideli nawolo nyi n gyeŋ Gyudatena mmaraase na kewɔŋee keŋ doo ŋe man nɛ pou. Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ sola neŋ nyi, nya nyiŋ konɔɔ na n ke tei man.

english_verse: All the Jews have known me since I was a child. They know what kind of life I have lived in my own country and in Jerusalem. And if they were willing, they could tell you I was a Pharisee, a member of a group that is stricter than any other.
translation_verse: Ba gyeŋ maŋ te yɛɛ gyɛŋ ya, te akpaa nyi baa sɛɛ yako nombii na ke gbɛɛ na, baa tale di adansɛɛ nyi me kyaŋa dɔ ɔsom mmaraase ŋan nyiŋmaase ala ŋgba Farasii baale nɛ.

english_verse: Day and night our twelve tribes have earnestly served God, waiting for his promised blessings. King Agrippa, because of this hope, some of our leaders have brought charges against me.
translation_verse: Mena nombii kei dɔɔ te daa Gyudatena akpaŋalaŋ kufu ala ŋan pou sia gyakaa nyi Wurubuarɛ waa yɛɛ fa daa. Mena dɔɔ te dɛɛ som Wurubuarɛ na sibiiyam debaŋ kamasɛ. Gyoo kpaakpaa, me sia gyakaa mena nombii kei dɔɔ nyi Wurubuarɛ waa yɛɛ fa daa, keŋ dɔɔ te Gyudatena moo nombia gyakaa me dɔɔ.

english_verse: Why should any of you doubt that God raises the dead to life?
translation_verse: Weera te dana doŋ fa ŋon nyi ya lɛɛ di nyi Wurubuarɛ waa gyuusu baŋ bɔɔ yem nɛ?

english_verse: I did this first in Jerusalem, and with the authority of the chief priests I put many of God's people in jail. I even voted for them to be killed.
translation_verse: Mena mɔ te me yɛɛwɔ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man. Saese kegyiise faa maŋ gbɛɛ te me kyaŋa Kristotena burum tɔ deni, te akpaa bɛɛ gyae baa ko wɔ mɔ na, mɛɛ seŋ be wɔle nyi ke gbɛɛ kenaŋ, baa ko wɔ.

english_verse: I often had them punished in our synagogues, and I tried to make them give up their faith. In fact, I was so angry with them, that I went looking for them in foreign cities.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kamasɛ na me dɔŋɛɛ Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man mɛɛ naase Kristotena diyem, na baa nyiŋ tina ba kelɛɛdi keŋ yela. Me baŋ ka koro be dɔɔ nideli nɛ, me sila wɔ gyu tɛɛlese wɔlɛɛ dɔɔ ke naase wɔ diyem nideli. Pɔɔl keyako ɔ kekyɛɛkee wose man nombia Kpilala

english_verse: King Agrippa, one day I was on my way to Damascus with the authority and permission of the chief priests.
translation_verse: Lee kei dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise faa maŋ be nyiŋmaa man tɔne keŋ ne fa maŋ doŋ, te ma moo ma nyee baŋ Damasekuse gbɛɛ.

english_verse: About noon I saw a light brighter than the sun. It flashed from heaven on me and on everyone traveling with me.
translation_verse: Gyoo kpaakpaa, weeseteŋteŋ me ka koro mee gyu keŋ nɛ, karatetee dokoloŋ te keŋmaŋee daale keŋ ne naa kela weese nɛ, leewɔ adido kaa ŋmaale kilisi daa na me tebia baŋ de dɔŋɛɛ nɛ.

english_verse: We all fell to the ground. Then I heard a voice say to me in Aramaic, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me? It's foolish to fight against me!”
translation_verse: De popou leewɔ yala tɛɛle man te me nyii nɔɔwoya ŋaale yako maŋ Hiburu dei man nyi, ‘Sɔɔl, Sɔɔl, weera te n ne naase man diyem? N ne naase n wose diyem bo ŋgba mena keŋ bɛɛ wɔŋee wonembu te ŋon mɔ ne wɔŋee ɔ wose lee wɔle naase ɔ wose diyem nɛ.’

english_verse: “Who are you?” I asked. Then the Lord answered, “I am Jesus! I am the one you are so cruel to.
translation_verse: Te me bɔɔsewɔ nyi, ‘Nyaŋ amɔte ŋonaŋ me Gbeŋgyoo?’ Te de Gbeŋgyoo tiranɔɔ nyi, ‘Maŋ yɛna Yesu ŋon n ne naase diyem nɛ.

english_verse: Now stand up. I have appeared to you, because I have chosen you to be my servant. You are to tell others what you have learned about me and what I will show you later.”
translation_verse: Mɔna koro seŋ ma lese ma wose baa wola neŋ, na maa mo neŋ yɛɛ me tobaale na n ke di adansɛɛ lee kpene keŋ n be naa lee ma wose man na ŋan maa kaa wola neŋ nɛ.

english_verse: The Lord also said, “I will protect you from the Jews and from the Gentiles that I am sending you to.
translation_verse: Maa lɛɛ neŋ lee nyaŋ gbagba n balaŋ, na baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya te mee kpila neŋ be gyaŋ nɛ nyiŋmaa man.

english_verse: I want you to open their eyes, so they will turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God. Then their sins will be forgiven, and by faith in me they will become part of God's holy people.”
translation_verse: Mee kpila neŋ baa na n kaa gyuusu be sia, na baa tale lee ditiŋtɛɛ man kɔŋ keŋmaŋee man, na baa tale lee ɔbɔnsam nyiŋmaa man kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ. Na baa lɛɛ maŋ di na Wurubuarɛ ka mo be nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ na baa nyiŋ dekyae kpu na baŋ Wurubuarɛ ba lese see fa ɔ wose nɛ.’ Pɔɔl kekolosi lee ɔ tom keyɛɛ wose man

english_verse: First I preached to the people in Damascus, and then I went to Jerusalem and all over Judea. Finally, I went to the Gentiles and said, “Stop sinning and turn to God! Then prove what you have done by the way you live.”
translation_verse: Me taŋgbɛɛwɔ yako Wurubuarɛ nombia Damasekuse te me gyuu Gyerusalɛm na Gyudia tɛɛle dɔɔ pou. Kenaŋ wɔle te me kaa kolosi ŋa fa baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ. Me yako wɔ nyi baa kyɛɛkee lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man na baa kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ, na baa yɛɛ abɔɔ ŋan gyae kawola nyi ba te kyɛɛkee lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ nɛ.

english_verse: That is why some men grabbed me in the temple and tried to kill me.
translation_verse: Kei dɔɔ te Gyudatena kyaŋa maŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man te bɛɛ gyae gbɛɛ baa ko maŋ nɛ.

english_verse: But all this time God has helped me, and I have preached both to the rich and to the poor. I have told them only what the prophets and Moses said would happen.
translation_verse: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ ta seŋ me wɔle kaa lii gyɛŋ, mena dɔɔ te me talewɔ seŋ kɛbo keŋkeŋ mɛɛ di nombia kɛŋa adansɛɛ fa bia na kegyiise pou nyi, kpene keŋ mɛɛ yako nɛ na keŋ Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra na Mosesi be yako see nyi gyae ke kɔŋ nɛ, yɛɛ dokoloŋ.

english_verse: I told them how the Messiah would suffer and be the first to be raised from death, so he could bring light to his own people and to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Nombii keŋ bɔɔ yako see yɛna nyi, nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ, waa naa diyem te ŋon ne gyaela waa taŋgbɛɛ foŋ lee yeŋ man, na waa wola ɔ balaŋ Gyudatena na baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ pou nyi, keŋmaŋee keŋ man baa ta na baa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ nɛ te kɔŋ.”

english_verse: Before Paul finished defending himself, Festus shouted, “Paul, you're crazy! Too much learning has driven you out of your mind.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te gyoo Fesituse gyɔɔ Pɔɔl nɔɔ dɔɔ fae e nyi, “Pɔɔl, n nyee ne wɔlɛɛ do? N tɔne kagyeŋ beiŋ na doo na neŋ diteŋ nɛ.”

english_verse: But Paul replied, “Honorable Festus, I am not crazy. What I am saying is true, and it makes sense.
translation_verse: Keŋte Pɔɔl tiranɔɔ nyi, “Gyoo kpaakpaa! Ma nyee bɛɛ wɔlɛɛ ya, me sia ala kaeŋ. Nombia ŋan mee kolosi nɛ yɛɛ nombia kpaakpaa ŋa, te ŋe doo na ŋe gbɛɛ mɔ.

english_verse: None of these things happened off in a corner somewhere. I am sure that King Agrippa knows what I am talking about. That's why I can speak so plainly to him.”
translation_verse: Gyoo Agiripa, n gyeŋ nombia kɛŋa pou man mena dɔɔ maa tale gyɔ me konɔɔ kolosi na neŋ lee me konɔɔ man. Mɛɛ lɛɛ di nyi n gyeŋ nombia kɛŋa man keŋ dokoloŋ koraŋ te lam n dɔɔ ya, nawolo nyi ba te yɛɛ ŋa weese ya.

english_verse: Then Paul said to Agrippa, “Do you believe what the prophets said? I know you do.”
translation_verse: Gyoo Agiripa, n ne lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ nɔɔwoya di? Ma gyeŋ nyi n ne lɛɛ ŋa di.”

english_verse: Agrippa asked Paul, “In such a short time do you think you can talk me into being a Christian?”
translation_verse: Keŋte gyoo Agiripa mɔ bɔɔse Pɔɔl nyi, “N gyeŋ nyi nyaŋ ke tale yela maa bese Kristobaale debokeiman?”

english_verse: Paul answered, “Whether it takes a short time or a long time, I wish you and everyone else who hears me today would become just like me! Except, of course, for these chains.”
translation_verse: Te Pɔɔl tiranɔɔ nyi, “Gyɛŋ bo o kyoo bo o, maŋ ne fane bo nyi Wurubuarɛ waa yela ɛmɛɛ na balaŋ akaŋ baŋ pou ɛ be tei maŋ gyɛŋ wee kei nɛ, ɛ ka bese Kristotena ŋgba maŋ Pɔɔl nɛ. Mɔna be na do ŋon akyakaase bo ya!”

english_verse: Then King Agrippa, Governor Festus, Bernice, and everyone who was with them got up.
translation_verse: Keŋte gyoo Agiripa na Fesituse ŋon ɔ seŋɛɛ Romantena gyoo dinaa nawɔɔ man nɛ, na Bɛɛnise na balaŋ akaŋ baŋ korowɔ lee deni keŋ man.

english_verse: But before they left, they said, “This man isn't guilty of anything. He doesn't deserve to die or to be put in jail.”
translation_verse: Be ka koro nɛ be kolosiwɔ na dɔŋa nyiaa, “Balee kei te yɛɛ dukum keŋ kaboena nyi baa ko e yaa baa do e deni ya.”

english_verse: Agrippa told Festus, “Paul could have been set free, if he had not asked to be tried by the Roman Emperor.”
translation_verse: Keŋte gyoo Agiripa yako Fesituse nyi, “Nyi balee kei deŋ te yako nyi dɛɛ mo ɔ nombia gyina gyoo dinaa Kaesa ya na, nafɔ dɛɛ tale tina e yela.”

english_verse: When it was time for us to sail to Rome, Captain Julius from the Emperor's special troops was put in charge of Paul and the other prisoners.
translation_verse: Keŋte be bakewɔ see nyi dɛɛ mo degbele dinaa na dee gyu Romantena gyoo dinaa ŋon gyaŋ Itali tɛɛle man. Te ba moo Pɔɔl na balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ kyam tɔ deni nɛ baale do yoonɔɔtena kegyia ŋon bɛɛ baake e Gyuliɔse nɛ nyiŋmaa man. Gyuliɔse yɛɛ Roman gyoo dinaa ŋon yoonɔɔtena kegyia ŋolo e.

english_verse: We went aboard a ship from Adramyttium that was about to sail to some ports along the coast of Asia. Aristarchus from Thessalonica in Macedonia sailed on the ship with us.
translation_verse: Te da gyoo degbele dinaa keŋ kɔɔ lee Adaramitium donɔɔ man kee gyu Esia yenaŋ donɔɔse ŋan ŋe kya boo nɔɔman nɛ, te de kuluwɔ laŋ. Na Arisitakuse ŋon ɔ ba lee Tesalonika donɔɔ keŋ ke kyaa Masedonia tɛɛle man nɛ doo de man.

english_verse: The next day we came to shore at Sidon. Captain Julius was very kind to Paul. He even let him visit his friends, so they could give him whatever he needed.
translation_verse: Tɛɛ kena keŋ nɛ, de gyuuwɔ ka gyoo Sidɔn donɔɔ man, botɔɔ te Gyuliɔse naa Pɔɔl waraŋase te ɔ faa e gbɛɛ te ɔ gyuu ɔ tebia gyaŋ na baa fa e kpene keŋ ne hia e.

english_verse: When we left Sidon, the winds were blowing against us, and we sailed close to the island of Cyprus to be safe from the wind.
translation_verse: De ka koro lee botɔɔ dee gyu keŋ nɛ, na feliŋ ne gyɔɔ kɔŋ de gyaŋ mena dɔɔ da moowɔ na Saiporɔse tɛɛlebii keŋ loŋ be kilisi ke nɛ yenaŋ, nawolo nyi na feliŋ bɛɛ gyɔɔ kɔŋ botɔɔ ya.

english_verse: Then we sailed south of Cilicia and Pamphylia until we came to the port of Myra in Lycia.
translation_verse: Da moowɔ ɛpo dɔɔ ke laŋ Silisia na Pamfilia tɛɛlese yenaŋ te de ka gyoo Mira donɔɔ keŋ ke kyaa Lisiase tɛɛle dɔɔ nɛ.

english_verse: There the army captain found a ship from Alexandria that was going to Italy. So he ordered us to board that ship.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te yoonɔɔtena kegyia ŋon naa degbele dinaa daale na ke ta lee Alɛkesanda kee gyu Itali te ɔ lese daa do ke maŋ.

english_verse: We sailed along slowly for several days and had a hard time reaching Cnidus. The wind would not let us go any farther in that direction, so we sailed past Cape Salmone, where the island of Crete would protect us from the wind.
translation_verse: Degbele keŋ moo daa dɛɛdɛɛ te de naa diyem mɔ nideli weeya burum pɛte de ka benaa donɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake ke Kiniduse nɛ. Feliŋ keŋ te fa daa gbɛɛ dɛɛ mo da nyee baŋ botɔɔ keŋ nafɔ dee gyu nɛ ya, mena dɔɔ de gyuuwɔ ka mo na Kreti tɛɛle keŋ loŋ be kilisi ke nɛ, ata ata kelii Salimone donɔɔ yenaŋ dɔɔ, nawolo nyi na feliŋ keŋ bɛɛ gyɔɔ kɔŋ botɔɔ yenaŋ ya.

english_verse: We went slowly along the coast and finally reached a place called Fair Havens, not far from the town of Lasea.
translation_verse: Da moowɔ na ɛpo nɔɔ nɔɔ, te de kaawɔ mɔ nideli pɛte de kaa lii botɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake nyi Agbelese daseŋae kpaakpaa, keŋ benaa na donɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake nyi Lasea nɛ.

english_verse: By now we had already lost a lot of time, and sailing was no longer safe. In fact, even the Great Day of Forgiveness was past.
translation_verse: De wɔlɛɛ debaŋ boo keŋ dɔɔ ampaŋ ampaŋ, te wee keŋ Gyudatena ne fa Wurubuarɛ kedi na waa mo ba dukum kyɛɛ wɔ nɛ kaa laŋawɔ. Mena debaŋ kenaŋ na feliŋ ne yeli loŋ dɔɔ keta ne yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nideli. Mena dɔɔ Pɔɔl kolosiwɔ fa wɔ nyi,

english_verse: Then Paul spoke to the crew of the ship, “Men, listen to me! If we sail now, our ship and its cargo will be badly damaged, and many lives will be lost.”
translation_verse: “Me tebia, ma te naa nyi dɛɛ naa diyem nideli na dee di gyerebi de gbɛɛ kei man. Na degbele dinaa kei na kasolɔse ŋan ŋaageŋ ne gyae la ke wɔlɛɛ ya, mɔna balaŋ mɔ baa yekee. Mena dɔɔ ɛ yela dɛɛ kyaa kɛbo.”

english_verse: But Julius listened to the captain of the ship and its owner, rather than to Paul.
translation_verse: Mɔna yoonɔɔtena kegyia ŋon bɛɛ Pɔɔl nɔɔwoya bo, te ɔ moo degbele dinaa keŋ gbeŋgyoo na saare ŋon wui.

english_verse: The harbor at Fair Havens wasn't a good place to spend the winter. Because of this, almost everyone agreed that we should at least try to sail along the coast of Crete as far as Phoenix. It had a harbor that opened toward the southwest and northwest, and we could spend the winter there. The Storm at Sea
translation_verse: Keŋte be kpa agbelese dɛɛseŋae kenaŋ be dei na kekyaa kyeŋgbelelee debaŋ kenaŋ man ya. Mena dɔɔ be man dekpeŋ dinaa gyaewɔ nyi baa kyaŋ ba wose na be kaa lii donɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi Fonisi nɛ, na baa kyaa botɔɔ kyeŋgbelelee debaŋ kenaŋ man. Fonisi yɛɛ agbelese dɛɛseŋae keŋ ke kyaa Kreti tɛɛle man te ke sia tee bo weese dɛɛlee na weese dɛɛyalae nsana. Feliŋkum kegyɔ ɛpo keŋ dɔɔ

english_verse: When a gentle wind from the south started blowing, the men thought it was a good time to do what they had planned. So they pulled up the anchor, and we sailed along the coast of Crete.
translation_verse: Feliŋbii daale kɛɛ gyɔɔ lee ata yenaŋ nɛ, balaŋ baŋ gyueŋwɔ nyi baa nyiŋ gbɛɛ ta ba kegyaebii, mena dɔɔ be wɔŋee nyeele keŋ ne kyaŋ degbele dinaa seŋ debodokoloŋ dekeŋkeŋ dɔɔ nɛ, kaa do degbele dinaa keŋ man te be kuluwɔ ta na Kreti ɛpo nɔɔ nɔɔ.

english_verse: The wind struck the ship, and we could not sail against it. So we let the wind carry the ship.
translation_verse: Feliŋ keŋ gyɔɔwɔ gyaŋee na degbele dinaa keŋ mena dɔɔ ka te tale gyu ke siaman bela ya. Mena dɔɔ de yela feliŋ keŋ wɔŋee daa gyu botɔɔ keŋ kɛɛ gyae.

english_verse: We went along the island of Cauda on the side that was protected from the wind. We had a hard time holding the lifeboat in place,
translation_verse: Te de gyuuwɔ ka mo na Kauda tɛɛlebii keŋ loŋ be kilisi ke nɛ yenaŋ, na feliŋ bɛɛ gyɔɔ botɔɔ nideli ya, mɔna de kaawɔ pɛte de talewɔ lese degbele bii keŋ bɛɛ mo lɛɛ balaŋ nyeeya nɛ lee degbele dinaa keŋ man.

english_verse: but finally we got it where it belonged. Then the sailors wrapped ropes around the ship to hold it together. They lowered the sail and let the ship drift along, because they were afraid it might hit the sandbanks in the gulf of Syrtis.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ wɔŋee degbelebii keŋ do degbele dinaa keŋ man te ba moo ŋmeeseŋ bake degbele dinaa keŋ na feliŋ be nyiŋ gyɔ ke tekerii ya. Be yeewɔ nyi degbele dinaa keŋ kaa ta dɔɔ Libia boo nɔɔ aŋasa man ya, mena dɔɔ be tisinaa degbele dinaa keŋ kanyaŋse te be tinaa degbele keŋ yela, te feliŋ moo ke ke dɔŋ dɛɛ dɛɛ.

english_verse: The storm was so fierce that the next day they threw some of the ship's cargo overboard.
translation_verse: Feliŋkum keŋ moo daa kyeŋgbeŋ kyeŋgbeŋ boo keŋ dɔɔ, mena dɔɔ tɛɛ kena nɛ te be fiasɛɛ bɛɛ lese kasolɔse lee degbele dinaa keŋ man do loŋ man.

english_verse: Then on the third day, with their bare hands they threw overboard some of the ship's gear.
translation_verse: Ke tɛɛ kena nɛ, ba lese degbele keŋ gbagba abɔɔ ŋan ŋaale fuŋii leki do ɛpo man.

english_verse: For several days we could not see either the sun or the stars. A strong wind kept blowing, and we finally gave up all hope of being saved.
translation_verse: Dii weeya burum na de bɛɛ tekaa naa weese yaa kyolobiise ya, na feliŋ keŋ mɔ gyakaa ne gyɔɔ akpanyaŋnyaŋ man, mena dɔɔ de ta teka gyakaa loo nyi dee nyiŋ nyeedoŋ bela ya.

english_verse: Since none of us had eaten anything for a long time, Paul stood up and told the men: You should have listened to me! If you had stayed on in Crete, you would not have had this damage and loss.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ kyaawɔ weeya burum keŋ be bee di weenɛɛ ya, keŋte Pɔɔl korowɔ seŋ be siaman yako wɔ nyi, “Me tebia, nyi yeŋ nyii kpene keŋ mɔɔ yako ŋon nyi ɛ na kulu degbele dinaa keŋ lee Kreti ya nɛ, nafɔ deŋ te naa diyem kei na dee di gyerebi kei mɔ ya.

english_verse: But now I beg you to cheer up, because you will be safe. Only the ship will be lost.
translation_verse: Nɛnɛɛ mɛɛ sola ŋon bo nyi ɛ nyiŋ konɔɔ, ɛ man ŋolo be gyae waa yeŋ ya mɔna degbele dinaa keŋ kaageŋ ne gyae la ke wɔlɛɛ.

english_verse: I belong to God, and I worship him. Last night he sent an angel
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi dana nelim me Wurubuarɛ ŋon me yɛɛ ɔ wui te mɛɛ som e nɛ, kpila ɔ kpilale kɔŋ me gyaŋ,

english_verse: to tell me, “Paul, don't be afraid! You will stand trial before the Emperor. And because of you, God will save the lives of everyone on the ship.”
translation_verse: kaa yako maŋ nyi, ‘Pɔɔl na yee ya, kaboena nyi nya gyu ka seŋ gyoo dinaa Kaesa siaman, na baa di n nombia ŋan. Lee nyaŋ dɔɔ, Wurubuarɛ waa gyee baŋ pou be doo degbele dinaa keŋ man nɛ.’

english_verse: But we will first be shipwrecked on some island.
translation_verse: Mɔna dɛɛ kaa lee tɛɛlebii daale keŋ loŋ be kilisi nɛ dɔɔ.” Degbele dinaa keŋ kewɔlɛɛ

english_verse: They cut the anchors loose and let them sink into the sea. At the same time they untied the ropes that were holding the rudders. Next, they raised the sail at the front of the ship and let the wind carry the ship toward the beach.
translation_verse: Te be kɛrɛɛ ŋmeeseŋ ŋan bɔɔ mo kpaŋ na nyeela ŋan ne kyaŋ degbele dinaa seŋ debodokoloŋ nɛ do loŋ man, te be booli ŋmeeseŋ ŋan bɔɔ mo bake na woni keŋ ne kii degbele dinaa nɛ. Te be gyinaa degbele dinaa keŋ kanyaŋ adido degbele keŋ siaman te feliŋ gyɔɔwɔ, te tuu degbele dinaa keŋ sia tee dekeŋkeŋ dɔɔ gbɛɛ.

english_verse: The sailors wanted to escape from the ship. So they lowered the lifeboat into the water, pretending that they were letting down some anchors from the front of the ship.
translation_verse: Baŋ bɛɛ saa degbele dinaa keŋ nɛ yela baa yeu tina daa yela, mena dɔɔ ba lese degbelebii keŋ ke gyakaa degbele dinaa keŋ dɔɔ nɛ gyakaa loŋ dɔɔ. Te ba beowɔ yɛɛ ŋgba bee gyu be kaa fuŋ nyeela ŋan ne kyaŋ degbele dinaa seŋ debodokoloŋ nɛ do loŋ man degbele keŋ siaman.

english_verse: The sailors were afraid that we might hit some rocks, and they let down four anchors from the back of the ship. Then they prayed for daylight.
translation_verse: Be yeewɔ nyi degbele dinaa keŋ gyae ka too na boe, mena dɔɔ ba lese nyeela anaara ŋan ne kyaŋ agbelese seŋ debodokoloŋ nɛ do loŋ man degbele keŋ wɔle. Te be fanewɔ nyi tɛɛ dɛɛ naa bileŋ.

english_verse: But Paul said to Captain Julius and the soldiers, “If the sailors don't stay on the ship, you won't have any chance to save your lives.”
translation_verse: Te Pɔɔl yako yoonɔɔtena kegyia ŋon na yoonɔɔtena baŋ nyi, “Akpaa balaŋ kɛwɔ te kyaa degbele dinaa kei man ya na, ɛ be gyae ɛ ke nyiŋ nyeedoŋ ya.”

english_verse: The soldiers then cut the ropes that held the lifeboat and let it fall into the sea.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ yoonɔɔtena baŋ tirii ŋmeeseŋ ŋan bɔɔ mo kpaŋ na degbelebii keŋ nɛ te loŋ wɔŋee ke laŋ.

english_verse: I beg you to eat something. Your lives depend on it. Do this and not one of you will be hurt.”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ waase ŋon bo nyi yaa di na ɛ ke nyiŋ nyiŋ nyeedoŋ, nawolo nyi kolo na kolo be kyaa keŋ gyae ke yɛɛ ɛ man ŋolo ya.”

english_verse: After Paul had said this, he took a piece of bread and gave thanks to God. Then in front of everyone, he broke the bread and ate some.
translation_verse: Ɔ kekolosi nɔɔwoya kɛŋa taŋ nɛ, ɔ moo bodobodo te ɔ fanewɔ fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ be popou siaman, te ɔ kara ke te ɔ diiwɔ.

english_verse: They all felt encouraged, and each of them ate something.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te be popou wɔe fɛɛ wɔ te ba moo weenɛɛ do be nɔɔman.

english_verse: and after everyone had eaten, they threw the cargo of wheat into the sea to make the ship lighter. The Shipwreck
translation_verse: Be popou kedi baa nɛ, ba lese weenɛɛ ŋan doo degbele keŋ man nɛ fuŋ do ɛpo man, na debgele dinaa keŋ ke nyiŋ koro yuŋ.

english_verse: Morning came, and the ship's crew saw a coast they did not recognize. But they did see a cove with a beach. So they decided to try to run the ship aground on the beach.
translation_verse: Tɛɛ kɛɛ naa keŋ nɛ, be naawɔ ŋgba ɛpo keŋ yenaŋ daale te sɔɔ gyoo tɛɛle daale man, na aŋasa doo botɔɔ mɔna be ta gyeŋ tɛɛle dokoloŋ kenaŋ ya. Mena dɔɔ be yɛɛ ba gyueŋ nyi akpaa baa tale na, baa besena degbele dinaa keŋ gyu ka seŋ botɔɔ.

english_verse: But it ran aground on a sandbank. The front of the ship stuck firmly in the sand, and the rear was being smashed by the force of the waves.
translation_verse: Mɔna degbele dinaa keŋ siagbɛɛ gyuuwɔ ka gyoo aŋasa man te ke dɔɔwɔ debodokoloŋ, te ka ateta keŋ ŋerewɔ gyoo aŋasa ŋan man te ke dɔɔwɔ keŋ ke bee wosi gba ya. Te feliŋ dinaa keŋ ne gyɔɔ nɛ yela ɛpo loŋ keŋ gyɔɔ degbele dinaa keŋ wɔle gbɛɛ aguŋ kpetekpetekpete.

english_verse: The soldiers decided to kill the prisoners to keep them from swimming away and escaping.
translation_verse: Yoonɔɔtena baŋ yɛɛ ba agyueŋ nyi baa ko balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ kyam do deni nɛ pou, na be man ŋolo be nyiŋ yɛlɛɛ toŋ yeu ya.

english_verse: But Captain Julius wanted to save Paul's life, and he did not let the soldiers do what they had planned. Instead, he ordered everyone who could swim to jump into the water and head for shore.
translation_verse: Mɔna yoonɔɔtena kegyia ŋon gyaewɔ waa lɛɛ Pɔɔl nyee mena dɔɔ ɔ tɔɔ wɔ nyi be na yɛɛ ŋgba mena keŋ bɔɔ bake see nɛ ya. Mena dɔɔ ɔ yako balaŋ baŋ pou nyi baŋ baa tale yɛlɛɛ na, baa taŋgbɛɛ toŋ gyoo loŋ man na baa yɛlɛɛ toŋ gyu dekeŋkeŋ dɔɔ.

english_verse: Then he told the others to hold on to planks of wood or parts of the ship. At last, everyone safely reached shore.
translation_verse: Te baŋ bɔɔ kaa nɛ baa gɔtaa degbele dinaa keŋ be yɛlɛɛ nɛ daase pampasaŋse ŋan be kyakaree nɛ, na baa toŋ gyu dekeŋkeŋ dɔɔ. Nnɛ te de popou yɛɛwɔ toŋ gyu dekeŋkeŋ dɔɔ keŋ ŋolo te dɔɔ ya.

english_verse: The local people were very friendly, and they welcomed us by building a fire, because it was rainy and cold.
translation_verse: Donɔɔsetena benaŋ kɛɛ de dɔɔ nideli. Na ɔga te fiasɛɛ ɔ ne ŋɛɛ, mena dɔɔ ba doo boalaŋ fa daa nyi dee woli nawolo nyi, na waare kyaa.

english_verse: After Paul had gathered some wood and had put it on the fire, the heat caused a snake to crawl out, and it bit him on the hand.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl ke kuu daase ŋaale waa do boalaŋ keŋ man nɛ, te boalaŋ ogyefeliŋ keŋ yela dom keŋkeŋteŋte ŋolo leewɔ daase ŋan man kaa milii ɔ nyiŋmaa.

english_verse: When the local people saw the snake hanging from Paul's hand, they said to each other, “This man must be a murderer! He didn't drown in the sea, but the goddess of justice will kill him anyway.”
translation_verse: Donɔɔsetena baŋ kena mena nɛ be yako dɔŋa nyi, “Balee kei waa yɛɛ awudiini e. Nawolo nyi, nyi ɔ yɛɛ nyeebam ŋgba ɔ te dɔɔ ɛpo man koraŋ ya nɛ, ɔ nombiakumɛɛ be gyae ke yela waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ ya.”

english_verse: Paul shook the snake off into the fire and wasn't harmed.
translation_verse: Mɔna Pɔɔl ŋmanyaŋ dom ŋon do boalaŋ man, keŋ kolo te yɛɛ e ya.

english_verse: The people kept thinking that Paul would either swell up or suddenly drop dead. They watched him for a long time, and when nothing happened to him, they changed their minds and said, “This man is a god.”
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ gyueŋwɔ nyi waa bokee, yaa gyae ke kara e lɔ karatetee dokoloŋ na waa yeŋ. Mɔna ba ke daa gyu siaman te ba kena nyi kolo te yɛɛ e ya nɛ, be kyɛɛkee ba agyueŋ nyi balee kei waa yɛɛ bane ŋolo e.

english_verse: The governor of the island was named Publius, and he owned some of the land around there. Publius was very friendly and welcomed us into his home for three days.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ gyoo yele yɛna Pubiliyuse. Na ɔ tɛɛle benaa na botɔɔ keŋ de kyaa nɛ. Ɔ kɛɛ de dɔɔ nideli te de kyaawɔ ɔ dɛɛ weeya kpaakpaa atooro.

english_verse: His father was in bed, sick with fever and stomach trouble, and Paul went to visit him. Paul healed the man by praying and placing his hands on him.
translation_verse: Gyoo Pubiliyuse ŋonaŋ ɔ kya kaa wee kpaŋalaŋ, na ɔ na kyeree. Keŋte Pɔɔl gyuuwɔ ka mo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ fane fa Wurubuarɛ fa e, te ɔ kyɔɔ e kawee.

english_verse: After this happened, everyone on the island brought their sick people to Paul, and they were all healed.
translation_verse: Ke kɔŋ mena nɛ, kaweesetena akaŋ baŋ pou be kyaa Maleta tɛɛle keŋ dɔɔ nɛ kɔŋawɔ, te Pɔɔl kyɔɔ wɔ be kaweese pou.

english_verse: The people were very respectful to us, and when we sailed, they gave us everything we needed. From Malta to Rome
translation_verse: Balaŋ benaŋ moo obuo dinaa fa daa, te be fa daa abɔɔ burum, te de ka koro lee botɔɔ dee gyuu nɛ, be fa daa abɔɔ ŋan gyae ke hia daa gbɛɛnaa man nɛ. Pɔɔl ka lee Maleta gyu Roman donɔɔ man

english_verse: Three months later we sailed in a ship that had been docked at Malta for the winter. The ship was from Alexandria in Egypt and was known as “The Twin Gods.”
translation_verse: De dii gyaalaŋna batooro tɛɛlebii keŋ dɔɔ pɛte da leewɔ botɔɔ. Keŋte da moo degbele dinaa keŋ kɔɔ lee Alɛkesanda donɔɔ man kaa seŋ botɔɔ dugbutu tɛɛ nɛ. Ba te yɛɛ banɛɛ atawaase gyakaa degbele dinaa keŋ dɔɔ, te da gyoo ke laŋ.

english_verse: We arrived in Syracuse and stayed for three days.
translation_verse: De gyuuwɔ ka gyoo Sirakuse donɔɔ man, te de kyaawɔ botɔɔ weeya atooro.

english_verse: From there we sailed to Rhegium. The next day a south wind began to blow, and two days later we arrived in Puteoli.
translation_verse: Da leewɔ botɔɔ ka gyoo Regium donɔɔ man. Tɛɛ kena keŋ nɛ, feliŋ daale lee ata yenaŋ kaa gyɔ, mena dɔɔ ka weeya alate man te de gyuuwɔ ka gyoo Puteyoli donɔɔ man.

english_verse: There we found some of the Lord's followers, who begged us to stay with them. A week later we left for the city of Rome.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te de naa Kristotena baale te ba sola daa nyi dee di weeya gyanaara be gyaŋ. Nnɛ te de tɛɛwɔ menɛɛ ka gyoo Roman donɔɔ man.

english_verse: Some of the followers in Rome heard about us and came to meet us at the Market of Appius and at the Three Inns. When Paul saw them, he thanked God and was encouraged. Paul in Rome
translation_verse: Kristotena baŋ be kyaa Roman kenyii da nkaŋ nɛ, be man baale kɔŋawɔ kaa gyaŋee na daa naŋ Apiuse donɔɔ man, te baale mɔ kaa gyaŋee na daa ŋɔɔla dɛɛsoe denɛɛ atooro ŋan man. Pɔɔl kena wɔ nɛ, doo e kakyeŋ te ɔ faa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ.

english_verse: We arrived in Rome, and Paul was allowed to live in a house by himself with a soldier to guard him.
translation_verse: De ka gyoo Roman donɔɔ man nɛ, yoonɔɔtena kegyia ŋon faa Pɔɔl gbɛɛ nyi waa gyu ke kyaa botɔɔ keŋ dei fa e nɛ, te ba lese yoonɔɔte ŋolo nyi waa gyee e. Pɔɔl kekolosi Roman donɔɔ man

english_verse: Three days after we got there, Paul called together some of the Jewish leaders and said: My friends, I have never done anything to hurt our people, and I have never gone against the customs of our ancestors. But in Jerusalem I was handed over as a prisoner to the Romans.
translation_verse: Weeya atooro wɔle nɛ, Pɔɔl baake Gyudatena kegyiise baŋ be kyaa donɔɔ kenaŋ man nɛ gyaŋee. Ba ke gyaŋee keŋ nɛ te ɔ yako wɔ nyi, “Me tebia Iseraetena, ma te yɛɛ kolo kum tia de tebia yaa tia amanberɛse ŋan de naanaɔ ba wola daa koraŋ ya, mɔna be kyaŋa maŋ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man te ba moo maŋ do Romantena nyiŋmaa man.

english_verse: They looked into the charges against me and wanted to release me. They found that I had not done anything deserving death.
translation_verse: Ba korowɔ gyoo me nombia man na, be yela baa tina maŋ yela, nawolo nyi ba te naa nombiikum keŋ mɔɔ yɛɛ keŋ kaboena nyi baa ko man ya.

english_verse: The Jewish leaders disagreed, so I asked to be tried by the Emperor. But I don't have anything to say against my own nation.
translation_verse: Mɔna keŋ Gyudatena be bɛɛ dɔɔ, yela ma solawɔ nyi maa mo me nombia kɔŋ gyoo dinaa gyaŋ, mɔna maŋ dɔɔ ma gyeŋ nyi daa na me tebia betaŋ daale ya.

english_verse: I am bound by these chains because of what we people of Israel hope for. This is why I have called you here to talk about this hope of ours.
translation_verse: Kei dɔɔ te me baake ŋon na maa nyiŋ lese nombia ŋan man wola ŋon. Walaŋ ŋon Iseraetena sia gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ dɔɔ te me doo akyakaase kɛŋa man nɛ.”

english_verse: The leaders replied, “No one from Judea has written us a letter about you. And not one of them has come here to report on you or to say anything against you.
translation_verse: Keŋte kegyiise baŋ tiranɔɔ fa e nyi, “Da te lɛɛ n wose man tɔne lee Gyudia tɛɛle man ya. Te de walaŋ ŋolo mɔ ta lee botɔɔ kɔŋ kaa yako daa kolo kum lee n wose man ya.

english_verse: But we would like to hear what you have to say. We understand that people everywhere are against this new group.”
translation_verse: Mɔna dɛɛ gyae dɛɛ gyeŋ nya agyueŋ nawolo nyi da gyeŋ nyi yenaŋ kamasɛ na balaŋ ne kolosi tia dikpii keŋ man n doo nɛ.”

english_verse: They agreed on a time to meet with Paul, and many of them came to his house. From early morning until late in the afternoon, Paul talked to them about God's kingdom. He used the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets to try to win them over to Jesus.
translation_verse: Te balaŋ baŋ na Pɔɔl see wee keŋ baa gyaŋee. Wee kenaŋ kelii nɛ, be man balaŋ burum kɔŋawɔ botɔɔ keŋ Pɔɔl kyaa nɛ. Lee nebo kelii balinɔɔ nɛ, ɔ kolosi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ wose man nombia. Ɔ moo Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan ɔ be yeli Mosesi be ŋmarase see nɛ, na Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra nɔɔwoya aŋmaraseŋ see kolosi de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu wose man nombia fa wɔ na baa nyiŋ lɛɛ e di.

english_verse: Some of the leaders agreed with what Paul said, but others did not.
translation_verse: Be man baale lɛɛ ɔ nombia ŋan di mɔna baale dɔɔ ba te lɛɛ ŋa di ya.

english_verse: Since they could not agree among themselves, they started leaving. But Paul said, “The Holy Spirit said the right thing when he sent Isaiah the prophet
translation_verse: Mɔna, pɛna Pɔɔl nɔɔ ke si na, nombii dokoloŋ te ɔ yakowɔ te aŋmaareŋ gyoo wɔ, te be popou korowɔ yaasee. Nombii keŋ ɔ be yako wɔ yɛna nyi, “Nombii keŋ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ be tɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya dɔɔ yako fa ɛ naanaɔ nɛ, doo na ke gbɛɛ. Ɔ yako wɔ nyi,

english_verse: to tell our ancestors, ‘Go to these people and tell them: You will listen and listen, but never understand. You will look and look, but never see.
translation_verse: ‘Gyu ke yako balaŋ kɛwɔ nyi, Ɛ ke tei, na ɛ ke tei, mɔna ɛ be gyae ɛ ke nyii nombia ŋenaŋ man ya. Ɛ ke kɛo, na ɛ ke kɛo, mɔna ɛ be gyae ɛ kena kolo ya.

english_verse: All of you have stubborn hearts. Your ears are stopped up, and your eyes are covered. You cannot see or hear or understand. If you could, you would turn to me, and I would heal you.’ ”
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi balaŋ kɛwɔ agyueŋ ta yem. Ba te tɔ ba deŋela te be wuu be sia, na mena ya na nafɔ be sia gyae kena, na ba deŋela ke nyii, na baa nyii nombia ŋan man, na baa bese kɔŋ me gyaŋ, na maa kyɔ wɔ be kaweese.’ ”

english_verse: Paul said, “You may be sure that God wants to save the Gentiles! And they will listen.”
translation_verse: Pɔɔl ke yako nombia kɛŋa nɛ, te baageŋ baageŋ moo aŋmaareŋ koro yaasee.

english_verse: For two years Paul stayed in a rented house and welcomed everyone who came to see him.
translation_verse: Kulutooneŋ ala sɔɔŋ na Pɔɔl kyaa botɔɔ keŋ ɔ be hare deni ɔ kyaa nɛ, na ɔ ne lɛɛ baŋ pou bɛɛ kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ nɛ nideli.

english_verse: He bravely preached about God's kingdom and taught about the Lord Jesus Christ, and no one tried to stop him.
translation_verse: Ɔ gyɔɔ ɔ konɔɔ kolosi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ na de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu wose man nombia ŋan fa balaŋ keŋ ŋolo te tɔ e gbɛɛ ya.

english_verse: From Paul, a servant of Christ Jesus. God chose me to be an apostle, and he appointed me to preach the good news
