translation_verse: Tɔne kei lee maŋ Pɔɔl ŋon me yɛɛ Yesu Kristo tobaale e nɛ gyaŋ. Wurubuarɛ baake na maŋ nyi me kaa yɛɛ ɔ kpilale, te ɔ lese maŋ see nyi maa yako ɔ nombia kpaakpaa ŋan.

english_verse: that he promised long ago by what his prophets said in the holy Scriptures.
translation_verse: Gyaŋgba gyaŋgba keŋ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ tɛɛwɔ wa akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra dɔɔ yako ɔ nombia kɛŋa see ɔ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan man.

english_verse: This good news is about his Son, our Lord Jesus Christ! As a human, he was from the family of David. But the Holy Spirit proved that Jesus is the powerful Son of God, because he was raised from death.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon doŋ keŋ ɔ ba mo gyuusu Yesu lee yeŋ man nɛ, keŋ lese la wola nideli nyi ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ bu e keŋ ayimɛɛ be doo ɔ man ya.

english_verse: Jesus was kind to me and chose me to be an apostle, so that people of all nations would obey and have faith.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ tɛe ŋon Kristo ŋonaŋ dɔɔ mo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele daa nyi de kaa bese ɔ kpilala. Na ke yela tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ pou ke lɛɛ ŋon Wurubuarɛ di, na baa nyii ɔ dei na baa lese ɔ yele.

english_verse: This letter is to all of you in Rome. God loves you and has chosen you to be his very own people. I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! A Prayer of Thanks
translation_verse: Lee mena dɔɔ te mɛɛ ŋmarase tɔne kei mee yelee ɛmɛɛ Romantena baŋ pou Wurubuarɛ ne gyae ŋon te ɔ be baake ŋon nyi ɛ kaa bese ɔ balaŋ nɛ. De kya Wurubuarɛ na de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo baa mo be bɛrɛɛ boele ŋon, na baa fa ŋon wosefɛɛreŋ.

english_verse: First, I thank God in the name of Jesus Christ for all of you. I do this because people everywhere in the world are talking about your faith.
translation_verse: Pɛ maa kolosi nombii daale nɛ, me dɔŋ Yesu Kristo dɔɔ mɛɛ fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ lee ɛ popou dɔɔ. Nawolo nyi balaŋ ne nyii mena keŋ ɛ be lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan di tɛɛle kei dɔɔ pou.

english_verse: God has seen how I never stop praying for you, while I serve him with all my heart and tell the good news about his Son.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ ŋon mɛɛ mo me konɔɔ pou som e te mɛɛ yako ɔ bu nyeekelɛɛ nombia ŋan nɛ, ŋon yɛna ma adansɛɛ nyi mɛɛ tɔɔse ŋon nideli debaŋ kamasɛ ma kefane man.

english_verse: In all my prayers, I ask God to make it possible for me to visit you.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kamasɛ keŋ mɛɛ fane na, mɛɛ sola Wurubuarɛ nyi waa toro gbɛɛ fa maŋ lee ŋon gbagba kegyaebii man na maa nyiŋ kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ.

english_verse: I want to see you and share with you the same blessings that God's Spirit has given me. Then you will grow stronger in your faith.
translation_verse: Ɛ nombia ne gyɔ maŋ loo, mena dɔɔ mɛɛ gyae nyi maa kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ na maa yela ɛ ke nyiŋ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ abɔɔ anefaŋ ŋan, na ŋe ka do ŋon doŋ ɛ kelɛɛdi man.

english_verse: What I am saying is that we can encourage each other by the faith that is ours.
translation_verse: Kei nawolo baa nyi akpaa me kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ na, daa na ŋon pou kelɛɛdi gyae ka do dɔŋa kakyeŋ.

english_verse: My friends, I want you to know that I have often planned to come for a visit. But something has always kept me from doing it. I want to win followers to Christ in Rome, as I have done in many other places.
translation_verse: Me tebia mɛɛ gyae ɛ ka gyeŋ nyi nafɔ me gyaewɔ agbaatee agbaatee nyi maa kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ, na Wurubuarɛ ke ta me dɔɔ yela balaŋ ke lɛɛ Yesu di nideli ɛ man, ŋgba mena keŋ ɛ tebia baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ be lɛɛ e di debiise akaŋ ŋan man nɛ, mɔna gbɛɛ ta toro fa maŋ debaŋ kenaŋ man ya.

english_verse: It doesn't matter if people are civilized and educated, or if they are uncivilized and uneducated. I must tell the good news to everyone. That's why I am eager to visit all of you in Rome. The Power of the Good News
translation_verse: Lee kei dɔɔ te me sia yɛɛwɔ nyi maa kolosi Kristo nombia ŋan fa ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ kyaa Roman donɔɔ man mɔ nɛ.

english_verse: I am proud of the good news! It is God's powerful way of saving all people who have faith, whether they are Jews or Gentiles.
translation_verse: Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan kekolosi be dana maŋ desɛŋ ya. Nawolo nyi ŋan yɛna Wurubuarɛ doŋ keŋ ɔ na mo lɛɛ walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne lɛɛ ŋa di nɛ nyee. Gyudatena taŋgbɛɛ la nyii nombia kɛŋa, pɛte balaŋ akaŋ baŋ mɔ kaa nyii ŋa.

english_verse: From heaven God shows how angry he is with all the wicked and evil things that sinful people do to crush the truth.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ ta lese wola nyi ɔ ta gyeŋ baŋ balaŋ kumɛɛ na balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ ne nyii ɔ dei ya nɛ dɔɔ. Nawolo nyi be nombiakumɛɛ bee yeli Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan ŋe doo na ŋe gbɛɛ nɛ ka lee debɔɔ ya.

english_verse: They know everything that can be known about God, because God has shown it all to them.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ ta gyeŋ baŋ nawolo nyi gbɛɛ keŋ man balaŋ baa mo gyeŋ e nɛ te yɛɛ nombia aweesesa ya. Ŋon Wurubuarɛ gbagba lese na ŋa wola wɔ.

english_verse: They know about God, but they don't honor him or even thank him. Their thoughts are useless, and their stupid minds are in the dark.
translation_verse: Mena balaŋ kɛwɔ gyeŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ kyaa mɔna bɔɔ na mo obuo keŋ kaboena e nɛ fa e ya, te bɔɔ na lese ɔ yele mɔ ya. Nyansa nombii kamasɛ be doo ba agyueŋ man ya, te ditiŋtɛɛ wolowolo doo la be konɔɔse man.

english_verse: They claim to be wise, but they are fools.
translation_verse: Bɛɛ yɛɛ ŋgba be sia te kare nɛ, mɔna be yɛɛ ayiŋtenawɔ.

english_verse: They don't worship the glorious and eternal God. Instead, they worship idols that are made to look like humans who cannot live forever, and like birds, animals, and reptiles.
translation_verse: Be te tina Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa kekpaakekpaa nɛ yela, gyu bɛɛ som banɛɛ ŋan bɔɔ maa yaa bɔɔ yae ŋa ŋgba balaŋ yaa gyebuse yaa wonembia nɛ.

english_verse: So God let these people go their own way. They did what they wanted to do, and their filthy thoughts made them do shameful things with their bodies.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ mɔ ta besena ɔ wɔle tee wɔ, te bɛɛ yɛɛ nombia nkaraŋkaraŋ ŋan ba lee baŋ gbagba be konɔɔse man nɛ. Be dana kewɔlɛɛ nombia bee fulu ba wose.

english_verse: God let them follow their own evil desires. Women no longer wanted to have sex in a natural way, and they did things with each other that were not natural.
translation_verse: Keŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ mena dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ ta besena ɔ wɔle tee wɔ te ba gyoo desɛŋ nombia keyɛɛ. Be man ala baŋ gba koraŋ te tina baala gyaŋ kedoo ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba wolo nɛ yela gyu bee doo na dɔŋa.

english_verse: Men behaved in the same way. They stopped wanting to have sex with women and had strong desires for sex with other men. They did shameful things with each other, and what has happened to them is punishment for their foolish deeds.
translation_verse: Mena mɔ te baala mɔ bɛɛ ala gyaŋ kedoo ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba wolo nɛ yela gyu bɛɛ yeŋ dɔŋa yeŋ, te ba gyoo desɛŋ nombia nkaraŋkaraŋ keyɛɛ na dɔŋa. Mena dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ waa wɔŋ ba deŋela ŋgba mena keŋ kaboena nɛ.

english_verse: Since these people refused even to think about God, he let their useless minds rule over them. That's why they do all sorts of indecent things.
translation_verse: Keŋ bɔɔ bɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan kagyeŋ dɔɔ, ŋon mɔ ta besena ɔ wɔle tee wɔ. Mena dɔɔ nombia nkaraŋkaraŋ te bɛɛ gyueŋ te bɛɛ yɛɛ nombia ŋan tekaboena nyi nafɔ balaŋ baa yɛɛ ya nɛ.

english_verse: They are evil, wicked, and greedy, as well as mean in every possible way. They want what others have, and they murder, argue, cheat, and are hard to get along with. They gossip,
translation_verse: Nombiakumɛɛ te wulu be man. Akpanyaŋnyaŋ na nombiakumɛɛ na sibii na balaŋ kekɔla na sia keyɛɛ balaŋ abɔɔ dɔɔ, na balaŋ kako na kale nombia na akɛrɛɛ kedi na nombia ka mo gyakaa balaŋ dɔɔ te wulu be man. Mena mɔ te bɛɛ yako balaŋ wose man nombia.

english_verse: say cruel things about others, and hate God. They are proud, conceited, and boastful, always thinking up new ways to do evil. These people don't respect their parents.
translation_verse: Bɛɛ mo nombiakumɛɛ gyakaa dɔŋa dɔɔ, te bɛɛ kɔla Wurubuarɛ. Be dana deboŋ te bɔɔ ne bu balaŋ ya, te bɛɛ wola ba wose mɔ. Bɛɛ gyae gbɛɛneŋ wɔlɛɛ ŋan baa mo yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛ debaŋ kamasɛ, te bɔɔ ne bu ba lolala mɔ ya.

english_verse: They know God has said that anyone who acts this way deserves to die. But they keep on doing evil things, and they even encourage others to do them.
translation_verse: Keŋ na keŋ ba gyeŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ mmaraa nawolo nyi walaŋ ŋon ɔ doo mena dinɔɔ kei man na ɔtenate be gyae waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa ya koraŋ nɛ, be gyakaa bɛɛ yɛɛ mena nombiakumɛɛ kɛŋa kpu. Kenaŋ wɔle koraŋ nɛ, bɛɛ bese do balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ mena nombiakumɛɛ kɛŋa mɔ nɛ kakyeŋ.

english_verse: Some of you accuse others of doing wrong. But there is no excuse for what you do. When you judge others, you condemn yourselves, because you are guilty of doing the very same things.
translation_verse: Nyaŋ walaŋ ŋon n ne bu n dɔɔ fɔɔ nɛ, n be dana daale n ke yako ya nawolo nyi nyaŋ mɔ n ne yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛ ŋan ɔtenate ne yɛɛ nɛ. Mena dɔɔ nyi akpaa n bu ŋolo fɔɔ na, nyaŋ gbagba n wose yaa n buu fɔɔ nɛ.

english_verse: We know that God is right to judge everyone who behaves in this way.
translation_verse: Da gyeŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ de di mena balaŋ kɛwɔ nombia te ɔ bu wɔ fɔɔ na, na ke doo na ke gbɛɛ.

english_verse: You surely don't think much of God's wonderful goodness or of his patience and willingness to put up with you. Don't you know that the reason God is good to you is because he wants you to turn to him?
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ ta lese ɔ wɔe na ɔ konɔɔ keŋ ɔ dana nɛ wola neŋ kekpaa, mɔna n ta mo ŋa yɛɛ kolo ya. Yaa n ba gyeŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ lese ɔ wɔe baa wola neŋ na ke yela n ke kyɛɛkee nya agyueŋ lee n gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man, na n ke kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ yee?

english_verse: But you are stubborn and refuse to turn to God. So you are making things even worse for yourselves on that day when he will show how angry he is and will judge the world with fairness.
translation_verse: Mɔna n dana konɔɔyuŋ na deŋele doŋ. Mena dɔɔ n deŋele kewɔŋ keŋ ne wuku kpu, te ke doo kɛɛ daa neŋ kelii wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa mo baŋ di balaŋ nombia nɛ. Mena wee kenaŋ te waa wɔŋ n deŋele. Wee kenaŋ te balaŋ baa naa mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ ne di ɔ nombia na ke gbɛɛ nɛ.

english_verse: God will reward each of us for what we have done.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ waa tɔ walaŋ kamasɛ kom ŋgba mena keŋ ɔtenate be yɛɛ nɛ.

english_verse: He will give eternal life to everyone who has patiently done what is good in the hope of receiving glory, honor, and life that lasts forever.
translation_verse: Baale ta mo be loo gyakaa nyi baa nyiŋ gyoorobiiri na yele na nyeedoŋ keŋ ka dokoso be kyaa ya nɛ. Mena dɔɔ bee nyiŋ konɔɔ te bɛɛ yɛɛ nombia kpaakpaa weeya pou man. Baŋ te Wurubuarɛ waa fa nyeedoŋ kekpaakekpaa keŋ.

english_verse: But he will show how angry and furious he can be with every selfish person who rejects the truth and wants to do evil.
translation_verse: Mɔna balaŋ baŋ lee sibii dɔɔ te bɔɔ bɛɛ anokoare keŋ gyu ke sila nombiakumɛɛ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ baŋ kum na ɔ deŋele kewɔŋ keŋ gyae ke kɔŋ be dɔɔ na baa naa diyem nideli.

english_verse: All who are wicked will be punished with trouble and suffering. It doesn't matter if they are Jews or Gentiles.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ waa yela kaale na kayeyɛɛ ke kɔŋ balaŋ baŋ pou bɛɛ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛ nɛ dɔɔ. Ke gyae ke fiasɛɛ bo lee Gyudatena dɔɔ, pɛna ke kɔŋ balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ mɔ dɔɔ.

english_verse: when God appoints Jesus Christ to judge everyone's secret thoughts, just as my message says. The Jews and the Law
translation_verse: Mena yaa Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan me dɔŋɛɛ mee kolosi nɛ na wolo. Nombia ŋenaŋ na wolo baa nyiaa, wee daale Wurubuarɛ waa ta Yesu Kristo dɔɔ di balaŋ nombia ŋan bɛɛ yɛɛ weese nɛ na waa bu wɔ fɔɔ.

english_verse: Some of you call yourselves Jews. You trust in the Law and take pride in God.
translation_verse: Nɛnɛɛ nyaŋ kpa n yɛɛ Gyuda baale e te n na mo n wose lɔ Mosesi mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ, mena dɔɔ n ne gyina n wose adido nyi n gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ nideli.

english_verse: By reading the Scriptures you learn how God wants you to behave, and you discover what is right.
translation_verse: Nyaŋ kpa n gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii nawolo nyi Mosesi mmaraase ŋan na wola na neŋ nombia ŋan ŋe doo na ŋe gbɛɛ nɛ.

english_verse: You are sure that you are a guide for the blind and a light for all who are in the dark.
translation_verse: Nyaŋ kpa nyaŋ dɔŋɛɛ la siayɛlɛɛsatena siaman, te n kpa nyaŋ yɛna kanea fa balaŋ baŋ be doo ditiŋtɛɛ man nɛ.

english_verse: But how can you teach others when you refuse to learn? You preach it is wrong to steal. But do you steal?
translation_verse: Nyaŋ ŋon n kpa n na wola balaŋ nombia nɛ, weera dɔɔ te n be tale wola n wose ya? N ne yako balaŋ nyi be na ŋmɛɛle ya mɔna nyaŋ gbagba ne ŋmɛɛle.

english_verse: You say people should be faithful in marriage. But are you faithful? You hate idols, yet you rob their temples.
translation_verse: N ne yako balaŋ nyi be na lee be wɛɛla yaa be baala wɔle ya, mɔna nyaŋ gbagba ne yɛɛ mena. Nyaŋ kpa balaŋ na som bɛɛneŋ ya, mɔna nyaŋ dɔŋ bɛɛneŋ denɛɛ man n ne ŋmɛɛle.

english_verse: You take pride in the Law, but you disobey the Law and bring shame to God.
translation_verse: N na wola n wose lee Mosesi mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ, mɔna n bee di ŋe dɔɔ ya. Lee mena dɔɔ n na do Wurubuarɛ desɛŋ.

english_verse: It is just as the Scriptures tell us, “You have made foreigners say insulting things about God.”
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako nyiaa, lee ɛmɛɛ Gyudatena nombia ayɛɛsa dɔɔ balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ ne kolosi mmusuo nombia tia Wurubuarɛ.

english_verse: Being circumcised is worthwhile, if you obey the Law. But if you don't obey the Law, you are no better off than people who are not circumcised.
translation_verse: Akpaa n ne di Mosesi mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ nɛ, kenaŋ na n baala wose kekara keŋ dana tɔnɔɔ. Mɔna akpaa n bee di Mosesi mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ ya nɛ, kenaŋ na n baala wose kekara keŋ yɛɛ yakaa. Kenaŋ na yɛɛ yaa ŋgba n te kara n baala wose gba ya nɛ.

english_verse: In fact, if they obey the Law, they are as good as anyone who is circumcised.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa ŋolo te kara ɔ baala wose ya te ɔ ne di Mosesi mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ siaman na yɛɛ yaa ŋgba ɔtenate kara ɔ baala wose baa nɛ.

english_verse: So everyone who obeys the Law, but has never been circumcised, will condemn you. Even though you are circumcised and have the Law, you still don't obey its teachings.
translation_verse: Nyaŋ Gyuda baale, n gyeŋ mmaraase ŋan nawolo nyi be ŋmarase ŋa bo see neŋ te be kara n baala wose mɔ, mɔna n bee di ŋe dɔɔ ya. Mena dɔɔ walaŋ ŋon ɔ te kara ɔ baala wose ya te ɔ ne di mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ nɛ, waa bu neŋ fɔɔ.

english_verse: To be a real Jew you must obey the Law. True circumcision is something that happens deep in your heart, not something done to your body. And besides, you should want praise from God and not from humans.
translation_verse: Akpaa nyi ŋolo yɛɛ Gyuda baale gbagba e na, ɔ konɔɔ man na wola la. Te baala wose kekara gbagba mɔ yɛɛ konɔɔ man nombia ŋa. Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ne kyɛɛkee na ɔtenate konɔɔ. Ka te yɛɛ nyi mmaraase ŋan bɔɔ ŋmarase see nɛ dɔɔ kedi ya. Mena walaŋ ŋonaŋ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ na lese na ɔ yele. Te yɛɛ denibalaŋ ya.

english_verse: It is good in a lot of ways! First of all, God's messages were spoken to the Jews.
translation_verse: Ke yɛɛ ampaŋ nyi n yɛɛ Gyuda baale e na, ke dana tɔnɔɔ lee gbɛɛ kamasɛ man. Gyaŋgbate yɛna nyi, Wurubuarɛ moo ɔ nombia ŋan bo do Gyudatena nyiŋmaa man.

english_verse: It is true that some of them did not believe the message. But does this mean that God cannot be trusted, just because they did not have faith?
translation_verse: Ke yɛɛ ampaŋ nyi Gyudatena baŋ man baale te lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan di ya. Mɔna ba deŋele doŋ keŋ bɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ, gyae ke tale tɔ Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛ nyi ɔ na yɛɛ kpene keŋ ɔ be wulaa yako see nyi waa yɛɛ nɛ yee?

english_verse: If our evil deeds show how right God is, then what can we say? Is it wrong for God to become angry and punish us? What a foolish thing to ask.
translation_verse: Deniwalaŋ waa tale bɔɔse nyiaa, nyi de nombiakumɛɛ de yeli balaŋ de naa nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia doo na ke gbɛɛ na, kenaŋ na akpaa de yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛ te ɔ wɔŋ da deŋele na, dɛɛ tale yako nyi ɔ ta ŋere daa do?

english_verse: Since your lies bring great honor to God by showing how truthful he is, you may ask why God still says you are a sinner.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa ma dekara keŋ mee dii nɛ da lese Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan ŋe doo na ke gbɛɛ nɛ wola nideli te da lese ɔ yele na, woŋ dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ sɛɛ ɔ ne bu maŋ fɔɔ nyi me yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛre e?

english_verse: You might as well say, “Let's do something evil, so that something good will come of it!” Some people even claim that we are saying this. But God is fair and will judge them as well. No One Is Good
translation_verse: Mena na woŋ dɔɔ te dɔɔ ne yako nyi, “Ɛ yela de kaa yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛ na Wurubuarɛ nombia kpaakpaa ke nyiŋ lee debɔɔ ya.” Balaŋ baale taŋ kara me nɔɔ nyi ma te yako mena nombii kei see. Balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ kara me nɔɔ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa wɔŋ ba deŋele ŋgba mena keŋ kaboena nɛ. Walaŋ kamasɛ gbɛɛ ba tenɛɛ ya

english_verse: What does all this mean? Does it mean that we Jews are better off than the Gentiles? No, it doesn't! Jews, as well as Gentiles, are ruled by sin, just as I have said.
translation_verse: Nɛnɛɛ sena yaa dɛɛ gyueŋ baa lee nombia kɛŋa man? Daa Gyudatena na gyueŋ baa nyi de dei kela balaŋ akaŋ baŋ yaa? Koa be doo mena ya. Nawolo nyi de wulaa yako ŋon nyi Gyudatena na baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ pou daseŋae yɛɛ dokoloŋ. Nombiakumɛɛ te wulu be popou man.

english_verse: They have all turned away and are worthless. There isn't one person who does right.
translation_verse: Balaŋ pou te wɔŋ ba wose lee Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛneŋ man. Be popou te wɔlɛɛ. Ŋolo na ŋolo be kyaa keŋ ɔ ne yɛɛ nombia kpaakpaa ya”

english_verse: Their words are like an open pit, and their tongues are good only for telling lies. Each word is as deadly as the fangs of a snake,
translation_verse: “Bɛɛ gyae nombia nkaraŋkaraŋ keyako. Bɛɛ mo be nɔɔ beo balaŋ.” “Be nɔɔwoya na koo ŋgba yeŋ nɛ.”

english_verse: and they say nothing but bitter curses.
translation_verse: “Bɛɛ mo nɔɔwoya ŋaŋ nyiŋan falaa balaŋ.”

english_verse: These people quickly become violent.
translation_verse: “Balaŋ kako be dana wɔ baŋ ya.

english_verse: They don't know how to live in peace.
translation_verse: Mena balaŋ kɛwɔ bɛɛ yɛɛ kpene keŋ gyae ka mo wosefɛɛreŋ kɔŋ ya.”

english_verse: They don't even fear God.”
translation_verse: “Te Wurubuarɛ keyee be doo be man mɔ ya.”

english_verse: We know that everything in the Law was written for those who are under its power. The Law says these things to stop anyone from making excuses and to let God show that the whole world is guilty.
translation_verse: Da gyeŋ nyi kpene kamasɛ keŋ Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan na wolo nɛ, doo bo fa balaŋ baŋ be doo mmaraase ŋan tɛɛ nɛ. Mena dɔɔ ŋolo be kyaa nyi waa nyiŋ gbɛɛ tira nombii daale nɔɔ yako nyi ɔ te yɛɛ ta dukum keŋ kaboena na nyi Wurubuarɛ waa wɔŋ ɔ deŋele ya. Kei nawolo baa nyi Wurubuarɛ waa di walaŋ kamasɛ nombia wee daale.

english_verse: God doesn't accept people simply because they obey the Law. No, indeed! All the Law does is to point out our sin. God's Way of Accepting People
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ kedi be gyae ke tale yela ŋolo gbɛɛneŋ ka tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman ya. Mɔna mmaraase ŋenaŋ ne yele la te dɛɛ gyem nyi de yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛrawɔ. Kelɛɛdi ne yele la walaŋ gbɛɛneŋ na tenee

english_verse: Now we see how God does make us acceptable to him. The Law and the Prophets tell how we become acceptable, and it isn't by obeying the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Te nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ ta wola daa gbɛɛ keŋ dɛɛ mo na de nombia ka tenee ɔ siaman, keŋ te yɛɛ nyi mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ kedi ya. Mosesi mmaraase ŋan na Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan wulaa yako mena gbɛɛnaa kenaŋ nombia.

english_verse: God sent Christ to be our sacrifice. Christ offered his life's blood, so by faith in him we could come to God. And God did this to show that in the past he was right to be patient and forgive sinners. This also shows that God is right when he accepts people who have faith in Jesus.
translation_verse: Mɔna nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ bɛɛ lese ɔ sia lee deniwalaŋ dukum keyɛɛ dɔɔ ya. Kei na wola la nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia ayɛɛsa doo na ke gbɛɛ, te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ Yesu di na waa yela ɔtenate mɔ nombia ka tenee ŋon Wurubuarɛ siaman.

english_verse: What is left for us to brag about? Not a thing! Is it because we obeyed some law? No! It is because of faith.
translation_verse: Nɛnɛɛ woŋ dɔɔ te dɛɛ gyɔ de konɔɔ? Kolo be kyaa ya. Nawolo nyi na mmaraase ŋan kesila ne yele la te walaŋ gbɛɛneŋ na tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman ya, mɔna lee Yesu Kristo kelɛɛdi dɔɔ te walaŋ gbɛɛneŋ na tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman.

english_verse: We see that people are acceptable to God because they have faith, and not because they obey the Law.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ de nombii keŋ dɔɔ da seŋɛɛ yɛna nyi, na mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ kedi dɔɔ te walaŋ gbɛɛneŋ na tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman ya. Mɔna lee Yesu Kristo kelɛɛdi dɔɔ, te walaŋ gbɛɛneŋ na tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman.

english_verse: Does God belong only to the Jews? Isn't he also the God of the Gentiles? Yes, he is!
translation_verse: Ɛ yela maa bɔɔse ŋon nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ Gyudatena baageŋ Wurubuarɛ e? Koa, ɔ yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ mɔ Wurubuarɛ e.

english_verse: Do we destroy the Law by our faith? Not at all! We make it even more powerful.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ dee sila kelɛɛdi keŋ gbɛɛ, na dee tina mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ kedi yela yaa? Koa, da kelɛɛdi ne gyae la ke yela na dɛɛ tale di mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ nideli.

english_verse: If he became acceptable to God because of what he did, then he would have something to brag about. But he would never be able to brag about it to God.
translation_verse: Akpaa nyi Wurubuarɛ lɛɛ Aberaham bo do nyi ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ ŋon Wurubuarɛ siaman lee tom keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ nɛ dɔɔ na, kenaŋ na ŋon Aberaham waa tale gyɔ ɔ konɔɔ, mɔna na Wurubuarɛ siaman ya.

english_verse: In the Scriptures David talks about the blessings that come to people who are acceptable to God, even though they don't do anything to deserve these blessings. David says,
translation_verse: Gyoo Defidi wulaa yako nombia kɛŋa Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see man wola nyiaa, akpaa Wurubuarɛ te kɛɛ ŋolo nombia ayɛɛsa ya te ɔ lɛɛ e do nyi ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ ŋon Wurubuarɛ siaman na, kenaŋ na walaŋ ŋonaŋ ŋon na nyeebam. Gyoo Defidi nombia ŋenaŋ yɛna nyiaa,

english_verse: “What a blessing when God forgives our sins and our evil deeds. What a blessing when the Lord erases our sins from his book.”
translation_verse: Walaŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ bɛɛ mo ɔ nombiakumɛɛ do ɔ konɔɔ man ya nɛ, ŋon na nyeebam.”

english_verse: Are these blessings meant for circumcised people or for those who are not circumcised? Well, the Scriptures say that God accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith in him.
translation_verse: Mena nyeebam kei nɛ, na ke yɛɛ fa balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ kara be baala wose nɛ baageŋ yaa baŋ ba te kare ya nɛ mɔ kpuɛ? Koa, ke yɛɛ be popou wui. De wulaa dɛɛ yako nyiaa, lee Aberaham kelɛɛdi dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ lɛɛ e do nyi ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ ŋon Wurubuarɛ siaman.

english_verse: Abraham let himself be circumcised to show he had been accepted because of his faith even before he was circumcised. This makes Abraham the father of all who are acceptable to God because of their faith, even though they are not circumcised.
translation_verse: Aberaham baala wose kekara yɛɛ kpene keŋ ne di adansɛɛ nyi lee ɔ kelɛɛdi dɔɔ te debaŋ keŋ ɔ te kara ta ɔ baala wose ya nɛ, Wurubuarɛ lɛɛ e do nyi ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ ŋon Wurubuarɛ siaman. Mena dɔɔ balaŋ baŋ pou ba te kara ta be baala wose ya te bɔɔ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di nɛ, ɔ te yeli be gbɛɛneŋ ta tenɛɛ ŋon Wurubuarɛ siaman. Kei te yeli Aberaham te kaa bese baŋ mɔ be naana.

english_verse: God promised Abraham and his descendants that he would give them the world. This promise wasn't made because Abraham had obeyed a law, but because his faith in God made him acceptable.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ yakowɔ see nyiaa, Aberaham na ɔ duuluŋ man balaŋ ne gyae la baa di tɛɛle kei dɔɔ. Te yɛɛ nyi keŋ Aberaham be di Mosesi mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ, dɔkaa te Wurubuarɛ yako nombia kɛŋa ya, mɔna keŋ Aberaham be lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di te Wurubuarɛ be lɛɛ e do nyi ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ ŋon Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ dɔɔ te ɔ yako nombia ŋenaŋ.

english_verse: If Abraham and his descendants were given this promise because they had obeyed a law, then faith would mean nothing, and the promise would be worthless.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa balaŋ baŋ bee di mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ nɛ dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ yako keyako see kei nɛ, kenaŋ na Wurubuarɛ kelɛɛdi keŋ yɛɛ yakaa te ɔ keyako see keŋ mɔ be dana tɔnɔɔ ya.

english_verse: God becomes angry when his Law is broken. But where there isn't a law, it cannot be broken.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi mmaraase na moona Wurubuarɛ baŋ kɔŋ. Te botɔɔ keŋ mɔ mmaraase be kyaa ya na, dukum mɔ keyɛɛ be kyaa ya.

english_verse: Everything depends on having faith in God, so that God's promise is assured by his gift of undeserved grace. This promise isn't only for Abraham's descendants who have the Law. It is for all who are Abraham's descendants because they have faith, just as he did. Abraham is the ancestor of us all.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ lee kelɛɛdi dɔɔ te balaŋ baa tale nyiŋ Wurubuarɛ abɔɔ ŋan ɔ be yako see nɛ. Na abɔɔ kɛŋa ka bese kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ na mo boele balaŋ yakaa. Kei na wolo baa nyi Aberaham duuluŋ man balaŋ pou baa nyiŋ mena abɔɔ ŋenaŋ. Te yɛɛ nyi baŋ bee di mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ nɛ baageŋ ne gyae la baa nyiŋ ŋa ya, mɔna baŋ pou bɔɔ lɛɛ di ŋgba mena keŋ Aberaham be lɛɛ di nɛ mɔ baa nyiŋ mena abɔɔ ŋenaŋ. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ siaman na, Aberaham yɛna de popou de naana.

english_verse: Abraham's faith never became weak, not even when he was nearly
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yako Aberaham mena nombii kei nɛ, na ɔ te di kulutooneŋ sɔŋonoŋ (

english_verse: But Abraham never doubted or questioned God's promise. His faith made him strong, and he gave all the credit to God.
translation_verse: Mena mɔ te ɔ te lɛɛ aŋmaareŋ nyi nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ be yako see nɛ be gyae ke kɔŋ mena ya, mɔna ɔ yɛɛ kakyeŋ seŋ ɔ kelɛɛdi keŋ man keŋkeŋ te ɔ lese Wurubuarɛ yele.

english_verse: just as we read in the Scriptures. But these words were not written only for Abraham.
translation_verse: Ba ke ŋmarase nyi “Ɔ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di dɔɔ te ɔ gbɛɛneŋ teneewɔ Wurubuarɛ siaman” nɛ, na Aberaham waageŋ dɔɔ te be ŋmarase mena nombii kenaŋ ya.

english_verse: They were written for us, since we will also be accepted because of our faith in God, who raised our Lord Jesus to life.
translation_verse: Mɔna daa baŋ mɔ dɛɛ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ be gyuusu de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo lee yeŋ man nɛ di, na waa yela de gbɛɛneŋ ka tenee ɔ siaman nɛ mɔ dɔɔ te be ŋmarase mena nombii kenaŋ.

english_verse: God gave Jesus to die for our sins, and he raised him to life, so that we would be made acceptable to God.
translation_verse: Lee de nombiakumɛɛ dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ lese Yesu Kristo nyi ɔ kaa yeŋ fa daa. Mena mɔ te ɔ gyuusu e lee yeŋ man, na ke yela de gbɛɛneŋ ka tenee ŋon Wurubuarɛ siaman.

english_verse: By faith we have been made acceptable to God. And now, thanks to our Lord Jesus Christ, we have peace with God.
translation_verse: Nɛnɛɛ lee keŋ dɔɔ lɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu di dɔɔ, Wurubuarɛ te lɛɛ daa do nyi de gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ ɔ siaman. Te lee de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo ŋonaŋ dɔɔ, gyoŋ te kaa doo daa na Wurubuarɛ nsana.

english_verse: Christ has also introduced us to God's gift of undeserved grace on which we now take our stand. So we are happy, as we look forward to sharing in the glory of God.
translation_verse: Lee de Kristo kelɛɛdi dɔɔ te de nyiŋa Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ keŋ ɔ ba mo boele daa te de dɔŋ ke man nɛ. Mena dɔɔ dana daa gyoŋ, nawolo nyi de loo gyakaa nyi dɛɛ kaa di gyoori kpu na e.

english_verse: But that's not all! We gladly suffer, because we know that suffering helps us to endure.
translation_verse: Na keŋ kaageŋ ya. Kenaŋ wɔle na dana daa gyoŋ bela lee da diyem kena man, nawolo nyi diyem kena ne yele la walaŋ ne tale kyaŋ ɔ wose seŋ keŋkeŋ.

english_verse: that will never disappoint us. All of this happens because God has given us the Holy Spirit, who fills our hearts with his love.
translation_verse: Mena loo kegyakaa kei mɔ be gyae ka do daa desɛŋ ya, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ te tɛɛ ɔ feliŋ ŋon ɔ ba mo do de man nɛ dɔɔ mo ɔ balaŋ kegyae do de man.

english_verse: No one is really willing to die for an honest person, though someone might be willing to die for a truly good person.
translation_verse: Gyae ke yɛɛ doŋ nideli pɛte ŋolo ke tale lɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ yeŋ yeŋ. Mɔna nyi akpaa ŋolo ne yɛɛ deeli fa balaŋ na, mberem na ŋolo waa sɛɛ lɛɛ ɔ yeŋ yeŋ.

english_verse: But God showed how much he loved us by having Christ die for us, even though we were sinful.
translation_verse: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ ta lese wola nyi ɔ ne gyae daa nideli, nawolo nyi debaŋ keŋ de sɛɛ de yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛrawɔ nɛ, ɔ yela Kristo kaa yeŋawɔ fa daa.

english_verse: But there is more! Now that God has accepted us because Christ sacrificed his life's blood, we will also be kept safe from God's anger.
translation_verse: Lee Kristo fatabo keŋ ɔ ba mo feefo de nombiakumɛɛ dɔɔ, te Wurubuarɛ lɛɛ daa do nyi de gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ ɔ siaman. Mena mɔ te lee Kristo fatabo kenaŋ dɔɔ, Wurubuarɛ waa lɛɛ da nyee wee keŋ waa mo baŋ di tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ pou nombia nɛ.

english_verse: Even when we were God's enemies, he made peace with us, because his Son died for us. Yet something even greater than friendship is ours. Now that we are at peace with God, we will be saved by the life of his Son.
translation_verse: Nafɔ na de yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ dɛɛ kɔla Wurubuarɛ, mɔna lee ɔ bu yeŋ keŋ dɔɔ ɔ ta mo daa yɛɛ ɔ gyoona baŋ ɔ ne gyae. Nyi ɔ bu Yesu yeŋ keŋ te yeli de ta bese ɔ gyoona na, kenaŋ na Yesu nyeedoŋ keŋ ɔ be nyim nɛ be gyae ke yela waa lɛɛ da nyee kekpaa yee?

english_verse: And in addition to everything else, we are happy because God sent our Lord Jesus Christ to make peace with us. Adam and Christ
translation_verse: Te yɛɛ nyi ɔ lɛɛ da nyee bo kaageŋ ya, mɔna lee Yesu Kristo dɔɔ dee di gyoŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom keŋ man. Nawolo nyi lee ŋon Yesu Kristo dɔɔ gyoŋ te kaa doo daa na Wurubuarɛ nsana. Be ka mo Adam maa na Kristo

english_verse: Adam sinned, and that sin brought death into the world. Now everyone has sinned, and so everyone must die.
translation_verse: Lee walaŋ dokoloŋ dɔɔ te nombiakumɛɛ kɔŋawɔ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ. Ŋon yɛna de naana Adam. Nombiakumɛɛ keyɛɛ mɔ moona yeŋ kɔŋ. Mena dɔɔ te yeŋ nyiŋa doŋ balaŋ dɔɔ, nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ te yɛɛ dukum.

english_verse: Sin was in the world before the Law came. But no record of sin was kept, because there was no Law.
translation_verse: Pɛte Wurubuarɛ ka mo mmaraase ŋan fa Mosesi na, nombiakumɛɛ wulaa kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ. Mɔna botɔɔ keŋ mmaraa be kyaa ya nɛ, bɔɔ ne kala dukum keyɛɛ do kolo man ya.

english_verse: Yet death still had power over all who lived from the time of Adam to the time of Moses. This happened, though not everyone disobeyed a direct command from God, as Adam did. In some ways Adam is like Christ who came later.
translation_verse: Leenaŋ de naana Adam debaŋ kaa lii de naana Mosesi debaŋ man nɛ, yeŋ ne dii na doŋ balaŋ pou dɔɔ. Balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ deŋele doŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia ayakoŋ see ŋan dɔɔ ŋgba Adam koraŋ ya nɛ, yeŋ dii doŋ be dɔɔ. Adam seŋɛɛ fa walaŋ ŋon waa kɔŋ nɛ. Walaŋ ŋonaŋ yɛna Yesu Kristo.

english_verse: But the gift of God's undeserved grace was very different from Adam's sin. That one sin brought death to many others. Yet in an even greater way, Jesus Christ alone brought God's gift of undeserved grace to many people.
translation_verse: Ke yɛɛ anokoare ke nyi mena balaŋ bala kɛwɔ yɛɛ kpookpoo. Nawolo nyi kpene keŋ be tɛɛ Adam dɔɔ kɔŋ, na kpene keŋ be tɛɛ Kristo dɔɔ kɔŋ nɛ te yɛɛ dokoloŋ ya. Lee Adam dukum keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ nɛ dɔɔ te balaŋ burum nyiŋa yeŋ. Te lee Yesu Kristo bɛrɛɛ dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ lese ɔ bɛrɛɛ wola balaŋ burum, kela balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ yem lee Adam dukum dɔɔ nɛ.

english_verse: There is a lot of difference between Adam's sin and God's gift. That one sin led to punishment. But God's gift made it possible for us to be acceptable to him, even though we have sinned many times.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ keŋ ɔ ba mo boele daa nɛ, na kpene keŋ Adam dukum keŋ ba mo kɔŋ nɛ te yɛɛ dokoloŋ ya. Adam dukum keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ nɛ yele la te balaŋ pou dii fɔɔ Wurubuarɛ siaman. Mɔna balaŋ nombiakumɛɛ burum kɛŋa wɔle na, Wurubuarɛ ta mo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele daa te ɔ moo da akumɛɛ kyɛɛ daa te ɔ yela de kaa bese balaŋ baŋ be gbɛɛneŋ tenee ɔ siaman.

english_verse: Everyone was going to be punished because Adam sinned. But because of the good thing that Christ has done, God accepts us and gives us the gift of life.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ŋgba mena keŋ lee walaŋ dokoloŋ nombiakumɛɛ keyɛɛ dɔɔ te balaŋ pou be di fɔɔ Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ, mena ke mɔ te lee walaŋ dokoloŋ nombia kpaakpaa keyɛɛ dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ waa lɛɛ balaŋ burum do nyi be gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ ɔ siaman na waa fa wɔ nyeedoŋ.

english_verse: Adam disobeyed God and caused many others to be sinners. But Jesus obeyed him and will make many people acceptable to God.
translation_verse: Mena keŋ lee walaŋ dokoloŋ deŋele doŋ dɔɔ te balaŋ pou ba bese nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra nɛ, mena ke mɔ te lee walaŋ dokoloŋ obuo dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ waa lɛɛ balaŋ burum do nyi be gbɛɛneŋ tenee ɔ siaman.

english_verse: The Law came, so that the full power of sin could be seen. Yet where sin was powerful, God's gift of undeserved grace was even more powerful.
translation_verse: Mosesi mmaraase ŋan kɔŋae na ke yela balaŋ kena be nombiakumɛɛ ŋan bɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ nideli. Mɔna debaŋ keŋ nombiakumɛɛ na boo na, Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ keŋ mɔ na boo kpu.

english_verse: Sin ruled by means of death. But God's gift of grace now rules, and God has accepted us because of Jesus Christ our Lord. This means that we will have eternal life.
translation_verse: Nafɔ na nombiakumɛɛ ne diina doŋ balaŋ dɔɔ, te ne kpase baŋ na Wurubuarɛ nsana. Mɔna nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ ta mo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele daa yela de ta bese balaŋ baŋ be gbɛɛneŋ tenee ŋon Wurubuarɛ siaman, na ke yela dɛɛ ta de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu dɔɔ nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa.

english_verse: What should we say? Should we keep on sinning, so that God's gift of undeserved grace will show up even better?
translation_verse: Akpaa nnɛ te nombia kɛŋa doo na, na sena yaa dɛɛ yako baa? Kaboena nyi dɛɛ dɔɔ da dukum keyɛɛ man na Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ keŋ ka bo yaa?

english_verse: No, we should not! If we are dead to sin, how can we go on sinning?
translation_verse: Korakora, be kɔŋ mena ya. Nawolo nyi da te wɔŋ da wose lee dukum keyɛɛ man. Mena dɔɔ tekaboena nyi dɛɛ bese gyu ke kyaa da dukum keyɛɛ man bela ya.

english_verse: Don't you know that all who share in Christ Jesus by being baptized also share in his death?
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ bɔɔ sɔ daa Wurubuarɛ loŋ te daa na Yesu Kristo be yɛɛ dokoloŋ nɛ, na ɛ ba gyeŋ nyi da te yɛɛ dokoloŋ baa lee ɔ yeŋ keŋ man yee?

english_verse: When we were baptized, we died and were buried with Christ. We were baptized, so we would live a new life, as Christ was raised to life by the glory of God the Father.
translation_verse: Ba ke sɔ daa Wurubuarɛ loŋ keŋ nɛ, na yɛɛ yaa ŋgba daa na Yesu Kristo yeŋa la te be wuu daa na e nɛ, nawolo nyi daa na e te yɛɛ dokoloŋ. Mena dɔɔ ŋgba mena keŋ de kya Wurubuarɛ ba mo ɔ gyoorobiiri doŋ keŋ gyuusu Yesu Kristo lee woya man fa e nyeedoŋ nɛ, mena te kaboena nyi daa mɔ dɛɛ kyaa dinɔɔ wɔle man.

english_verse: We know that the persons we used to be were nailed to the cross with Jesus. This was done, so our sinful bodies would no longer be the slaves of sin.
translation_verse: Da gyeŋ nyi debaŋ keŋ bɔɔ gyɔ e mataa ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ dɔɔ te ɔ ba yem nɛ, na ŋon Yesu na de dinɔɔ bene keŋ yaa be gyɔɔwɔ ko nɛ. Kei te yeli doŋ keŋ nombiakumɛɛ dana de dɔɔ nɛ nɔɔ ta yem, keŋ de ta tekaa yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛ gbeŋawɔ bela ya.

english_verse: We know sin doesn't have power over dead people.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi akpaa ŋolo da yem na, ɔ te tira ɔ wose lee nombiakumɛɛ keyɛɛ man.

english_verse: As surely as we died with Christ, we believe we will also live with him.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa daa na Kristo ta yem na, kenaŋ na dɛɛ lɛɛ di nyi daa na e mɔ dɛɛ kyaa nyeedoŋ man.

english_verse: We know that death no longer has any power over Christ. He died and was raised to life, never again to die.
translation_verse: Da gyeŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ gyuusuna Yesu Kristo lee yeŋ man, te ɔ be gyae waa yeŋ bela ya. Nawolo nyi yeŋ ba tekaa dana doŋ ɔ dɔɔ bela ya.

english_verse: When Christ died, he died for sin once and for all. But now he is alive, and he lives only for God.
translation_verse: Yeŋ keŋ ɔ ba yem nɛ, ɔ yeŋae karatetee dokoloŋ na waa ko nombiakumɛɛ nɔɔ. Mena dɔɔ nɛnɛɛ keŋ ɔ kyaa nyeedoŋ man nɛ, ɔ kyae fa Wurubuarɛ.

english_verse: In the same way, you must think of yourselves as dead to the power of sin. But Christ Jesus has given life to you, and you live for God.
translation_verse: Mena ke mɔ te kaboena nyi yaa kɛɛ ɛ wose nyi ɛmɛɛ mɔ yeŋae na ɛ ka ko nombiakumɛɛ nɔɔ, te nɛnɛɛ ɛ kyae fa Wurubuarɛ. Nawolo nyi ɛmɛɛ na Kristo Yesu te yɛɛ dokoloŋ.

english_verse: Don't let sin rule your body. After all, your body is bound to die, so don't obey its desires
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛ na tekaa yela nombiakumɛɛ kedi doŋ ɛ wosenaane keŋ gyae ka yeŋ nɛ dɔɔ, na ɛ ke sila abɔɔ kumɛɛ ŋan ɛmɛɛ gbagba ne gyae tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ ya.

english_verse: or let any part of it become a slave of evil. Give yourselves to God, as people who have been raised from death to life. Make every part of your body a slave that pleases God.
translation_verse: Ɛ na mo ɛ wosenaane yenaŋ daale fa nombiakumɛɛ keyɛɛ ya. Mɔna ɛ mo ɛ wose fa Wurubuarɛ ŋgba balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ lese wɔ lee yeŋ man kɔŋ nyeedoŋ man nɛ. Ɛ mo ɛ wose pou fa Wurubuarɛ na waa mo ŋon yɛɛ kpene keŋ dei te ke doo na ke gbɛɛ nɛ.

english_verse: Don't let sin keep ruling your lives. You are ruled by God's undeserved grace and not by the Law. Slaves Who Do What Pleases God
translation_verse: Tekaboena nyi nombiakumɛɛ dee di doŋ ɛ dɔɔ bela ya, nawolo nyi ɛ ba tekaa ɛ doo Mosesi mmaraase ŋan tɛɛ ya, mɔna nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ ɛ dɔŋ bo Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ keŋ man.

english_verse: What does all this mean? Does it mean we are free to sin, because we are ruled by God's gift of undeserved grace and not by the Law? Certainly not!
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ nɛnɛɛ keŋ de dɔŋ Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ keŋ man, te dɔɔ tekaa doo mmaraase ŋan tɛɛ ya nɛ, na kaboena nyi dɛɛ gyakaa nombiakumɛɛ keyɛɛ dɔɔ bela yaa? Korakora, be doo mena ya.

english_verse: You used to be slaves of sin. But I thank God that with all your heart you followed the example set forth in the teaching you received.
translation_verse: Mɔna dɛɛ do Wurubuarɛ nɔɔ nawolo nyi debaŋ daale na, nafɔ ɛ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛ gbeŋawɔ. Te nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ ɛ ta mo ɛ konɔɔ pou ɛ silana Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛneŋ ŋan bɔɔ wola ŋon nɛ.

english_verse: I am using these everyday examples, because in some ways you are still weak. You used to let the different parts of your body be slaves of your evil thoughts. But now you must make every part of your body serve God, so that you will belong completely to him.
translation_verse: Ɛ nombia man kenyii dana doŋ kyomii, mena dɔɔ te ma moo nombii kei mɛɛ maa na deniwalaŋ kekyaabii man nɛ. Gyaŋgba keŋ na ɛ moo ɛ wose pou besena gbeŋa fa nombia nkaraŋkaraŋ na nombiakumɛɛ keyɛɛ. Mena mɔ te kaboena nyi nɛnɛɛ yaa mo ɛ wose bese Wurubuarɛ gbeŋa, na ɛ ke tale yɛɛ nombia ŋan ŋe doo na ŋe gbɛɛ na ɛ ka bese balaŋ baŋ ayimɛɛ be doo ba wose man ya kelii ɔto.

english_verse: When you were slaves of sin, you didn't have to please God.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ ɛ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛ gbeŋawɔ nɛ, na ɛ be famenɛ na abɔɔ ŋan ŋa tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ keyɛɛ ya.

english_verse: But what good did you receive from the things you did? All you have to show for them is your shame, and they lead to death.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ ɛ be yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛ ŋan gyɛŋ kei ŋe dana ŋon desɛŋ nɛ, woŋ tɔnɔɔ te ɛ nyiŋawɔ lee ŋe man? Mena nombia ŋenaŋ tɔnɔɔ yɛna yeŋ.

english_verse: Now you have been set free from sin, and you are God's slaves. This will make you holy and will lead you to eternal life.
translation_verse: Mɔna nɛnɛɛ Wurubuarɛ te yeli ɛ ta lese ɛ wose lee nombiakumɛɛ keyɛɛ man te ɛ bese ɔ gbeŋa. Kpene keŋ ɛ ke nyiŋ lee ke man yɛna nyi, ɛ ke kyaa dinɔɔ kpaakpaate keŋ ayimɛɛ be doo ke man ya nɛ man na ɛ ke nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa.

english_verse: Sin pays off with death. But God's gift is eternal life given by Jesus Christ our Lord.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi kpene keŋ n ke nyiŋ lee nombiakumɛɛ keyɛɛ man yɛna yeŋ, mɔna walaŋ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo di na, kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa fa yɛna nyeedoŋ kekpaakekpaa keŋ.

english_verse: My friends, you surely understand enough about law to know that laws only have power over people who are alive.
translation_verse: Me tebia, ɛ popou ɛ gyeŋ Mosesi mmaraase ŋan. Te ɛ gyeŋ mɔ nideli nyi, akpaa walaŋ kyaa na ɔ sia na te mmaraase ŋan ne di doŋ ɔ dɔɔ.

english_verse: That is how it is with you, my friends. You are now part of the body of Christ and are dead to the power of the Law. You are free to belong to Christ, who was raised to life so we could serve God.
translation_verse: Me tebia, mena yaa doo fa ɛmɛɛ mɔ nɛ. Debaŋ keŋ ɛ be lɛɛ Kristo di nɛ, na yɛɛ yaa ŋgba ɛmɛɛ na e yeŋa la nɛ. Keŋ ɛmɛɛ na Kristo be yɛɛ dokoloŋ ɔ yeŋ man dɔɔ, mmaraase ŋan ba tekaa dana doŋ ɛ dɔɔ bela ya. Mena dɔɔ ɛ yɛɛ walaŋ wɔle ŋon Wurubuarɛ be gyuusu e lee yeŋ man nɛ wui, na ɛ ke nyiŋ yɛɛ nombia kpaakpaa fa Wurubuarɛ.

english_verse: When we thought only of ourselves, the Law made us have sinful desires. It made every part of our bodies into slaves who are doomed to die.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi debaŋ keŋ de doo de dinɔɔ bene keŋ man nɛ, na mmaraase ŋan yela da moo da wose do abɔɔ kumɛɛ ŋan daa gbagba ne gyae nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ man nideli. Nafɔ mena abɔɔ kumɛɛ kɛŋa deŋ moo yeŋ bo kɔna daa, na daa na Wurubuarɛ nsana ke tira kekpaakekpaa.

english_verse: But the Law no longer rules over us. We are like dead people, and it cannot have any power over us. Now we can serve God in a new way by obeying his Spirit, and not in the old way by obeying the written Law. The Battle with Sin
translation_verse: Mɔna nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ te yeli mmaraase ŋan nafɔ ŋɛɛ bake daa nɛ, ba tekaa dana doŋ de dɔɔ bela ya. Te keŋ daa na Yesu Kristo be yɛɛ dokoloŋ ɔ yeŋ man dɔɔ, de ta tekaa yɛɛ mmaraase ŋan nafɔ ŋɛɛ bake daa nɛ gbeŋawɔ bela ya. Mena dɔɔ de te nyiŋ da wose dɛɛ som Wurubuarɛ lee gbɛɛ wɔle keŋ ɔ feliŋ ŋon ba wola daa nɛ man. Ka te yɛɛ gbɛɛ bene keŋ Mosesi mmaraase ŋan ba wola daa nɛ ya. Mmaraase na lese na dukum wola

english_verse: Does this mean that the Law is sinful? Certainly not! But if it had not been for the Law, I would not have known what sin is really like. For example, I would not have known what it means to want something that belongs to someone else, unless the Law had told me not to do this.
translation_verse: Nɛnɛɛ sena yaa dɛɛ yako baa? Dɛɛ yako baa nyi mmaraase ŋan yɛɛ kolo kum ke? Korakora na mena ya. Mmaraase ŋan yele la te ma gyeŋa dukum. Nafɔ akpaa mmaraase ŋenaŋ deŋ te yako nyi, “Na yeli n sia keyɛɛ ŋolo kolo ya nɛ,” nafɔ mɔɔ gyeŋ nyi sia keyɛɛ ŋolo kolo dɔɔ yɛɛ dukum ke ya.

english_verse: It was sin that used this command as a way of making me have all kinds of desires. But without the Law, sin is dead.
translation_verse: Mɔna mena mmaraa kei yele la te sibii keyɛɛ kum kamasɛ nyiŋa gbɛɛ kaa gyoo me maŋ. Nyi mmaraase deŋ be kyaa ya nɛ, nafɔ dukum be dana yele ya.

english_verse: and I died. The very command that was supposed to bring life to me, instead brought death.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ me naawɔ nyi mmaraase ŋan nafɔ ŋe gyae ke fa maŋ nyeedoŋ nɛ, ŋa moo yeŋ bo kɔna maŋ.

english_verse: Sin used this command to trick me, and because of it I died.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi lee mmaraase ŋenaŋ dɔɔ, te dukum nyiŋa gbɛɛ beo maŋ yela me nyiŋa yeŋ.

english_verse: We know that the Law is spiritual. But I am merely a human, and I have been sold as a slave to sin.
translation_verse: Da gyeŋ nyi mmaraase ŋan dei nawolo nyi ŋe tɛe Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon dɔɔ kɔŋ, mɔna maŋ dɔɔ me yɛɛ deniwalaŋ yakaate e, te nombiakumɛɛ te wulu ma nyee. Mena dɔɔ me ta bese gbeŋi fa dukum.

english_verse: In fact, I don't understand why I act the way I do. I don't do what I know is right. I do the things I hate.
translation_verse: Maŋ gbagba bee nyii nombia ŋan mɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ man ya. Nawolo nyi nombia ŋan mɛɛ gyae nyi maa yɛɛ nɛ mɔɔ ne yɛɛ ŋa ya, te ŋan mɔɔ ne gyae nyi maa yɛɛ ya nɛ ŋan te mɛɛ yɛɛ.

english_verse: Although I don't do what I know is right, I agree that the Law is good.
translation_verse: Te akpaa nyi abɔɔ ŋan mɔɔ ne gyae nyi maa yɛɛ ya nɛ te mɛɛ yɛɛ na, kenaŋ na mɛɛ lɛɛ ke do nyi mmaraase ŋan dei.

english_verse: So I am not the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
translation_verse: Kedoo kei nɛ, te yɛɛ maŋ gbagba ne yɛɛ na nombia kɛŋa ya. Dukum doŋ keŋ ne di me dɔɔ nɛ, keŋ ne yele la te mɛɛ yɛɛ mena nombia ŋenaŋ.

english_verse: I know that my selfish desires won't let me do anything that is good. Even when I want to do right, I cannot.
translation_verse: Ma gyeŋ nyi debaŋ keŋ me doo me dukum dinɔɔ kei man nɛ, kolo kpaakpaa kamasɛ be doo me man ya. Nawolo nyi mɛɛ gyae nyi maa yɛɛ nombia kpaakpaa, mɔna mɔɔ ne tale yɛɛ ŋa ya.

english_verse: And so, if I don't do what I know is right, I am no longer the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
translation_verse: Akpaa nyi nombia ŋan mɔɔ ne gyae nyi maa yɛɛ ya nɛ te me gyakaa mɛɛ yɛɛ na, kenaŋ na te yɛɛ nyi maŋ gbagba ne yɛɛ na ŋa ya. Mɔna dukum doŋ keŋ ne di me dɔɔ nɛ, ne yele la te mɛɛ yɛɛ mena nombia ŋenaŋ.

english_verse: The Law has shown me that something in me keeps me from doing what I know is right.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ma te naa nyi dukum doŋ keŋ ne diina me dɔɔ. Nawolo nyi akpaa mɛɛ gyae nyi maa yɛɛ kolo kpaakpaa na, ka te ka lee kolo kum dɔɔ.

english_verse: With my whole heart I agree with the Law of God.
translation_verse: Maŋ gbagba me konɔɔ man na, nafɔ mɛɛ gyae nideli nyi maa di Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ.

english_verse: But in every part of me I discover something fighting against my mind, and it makes me a prisoner of sin that controls everything I do.
translation_verse: Mɔna mɛɛ naa nyi mmaraa wɔle daale keŋ kɔɔ ne kyaa na Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan ya nɛ ne yoo na dɔŋa me man. Mena mmaraa kum kei te yeli nombiakumɛɛ ta gyoo me man di ma nyee pou.

english_verse: What a miserable person I am. Who will rescue me from this body that is doomed to die?
translation_verse: Me nombia dana waraŋase nideli. Amɔte ne gyae la waa lese maŋ lee mena dinɔɔ kum keŋ doo me man, te ke dana maŋ kee gyu yeŋ man nɛ man?

english_verse: The Holy Spirit will give you life that comes from Christ Jesus and will set you free from sin and death.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi lee Yesu Kristo dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ta lese daa lee dukum na yeŋ man te ɔ faa daa nyeedoŋ wɔle.

english_verse: He did this, so that we would do what the Law commands by obeying the Spirit instead of our own desires.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ ke mena na ke yela daa baŋ dɔɔ tekaa doo dinɔɔ kum keŋ man ya te dee sila Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon nɛ, ke nyiŋ tale yɛɛ nombia ŋan ŋa tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman ŋgba mena keŋ mmaraase ŋan na wolo nɛ.

english_verse: If our minds are ruled by our desires, we will die. But if our minds are ruled by the Spirit, we will have life and peace.
translation_verse: Walaŋ ŋon ɔ mo ɔ loo gyakaa abɔɔ kumɛɛ ŋan ŋon gbagba ne gyae nɛ dɔɔ nɛ, na mo yeŋ bo kɔna e. Te walaŋ ŋon ɔ mo ɔ loo gyakaa Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon dɔɔ nɛ, na mo nyeedoŋ na wosefɛɛreŋ bo kɔna e.

english_verse: You are no longer ruled by your desires, but by God's Spirit, who lives in you. People who don't have the Spirit of Christ in them don't belong to him.
translation_verse: Mɔna ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ nyi akpaa Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon doo ɛ man nɛ, na ɛ ba tekaa doo ɛ dinɔɔ bene keŋ man ya. Ɛ silana kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ne gyae nyi yaa yɛɛ nɛ bo. Akpaa Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon be doo ŋolo man ya nɛ, na ɔtenate te yɛɛ Kristo walaŋ e ya.

english_verse: But Christ lives in you. So you are alive because God has accepted you, even though your bodies must die because of your sins.
translation_verse: Te akpaa Kristo doo ɛ man na, keŋ na keŋ ɛ ka yeŋ lee nombiakumɛɛ keyɛɛ dɔɔ koraŋ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon waa yela ɛ ke nyiŋ nyeedoŋ bela. Nawolo nyi lee Kristo dɔɔ ɛ gbɛɛneŋ ta tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman.

english_verse: Yet God raised Jesus to life! God's Spirit now lives in you, and he will raise you to life by his Spirit.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ be gyuusu Yesu lee yeŋ man nɛ feliŋ doo ɛ man na, kenaŋ na waa ta ɔ feliŋ ŋonaŋ dɔɔ yela ɛ wosenaane kei keŋ gyae ka yeŋ nɛ ke nyiŋ nyeedoŋ wɔle bela.

english_verse: Only those people who are led by God's Spirit are his children.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ bee yeli Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon na wola wɔ gbɛɛ nɛ, baŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ bia gbagba.

english_verse: His Spirit lets us know that together with Christ we will be given what God has promised. We will also share in the glory of Christ, because we have suffered with him. A Wonderful Future for God's People
translation_verse: Nɛnɛɛ akpaa de yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ bia gbagbawɔ na, kenaŋ na dee kpu na Kristo nyiŋ abɔɔ ŋan Wurubuarɛ be desina see fa ɔ balaŋ nɛ. Te akpaa daa mɔ de naa diyem ŋgba mena keŋ Kristo be naa diyem nɛ na, kenaŋ na Wurubuarɛ waa yela dee kpu na Kristo di gyoori ɔ gyoorobiiri keŋ man.

english_verse: I am sure what we are suffering now cannot compare with the glory that will be shown to us.
translation_verse: Akpaa ma to me sia kɛɛ gyoorobiiri keŋ ke doo ke daa na daa nɛ, na kaale keŋ dɛɛ kaa debaŋ kei nɛ te yɛɛ kolo ke ya.

english_verse: In fact, all creation is eagerly waiting for God to show who his children are.
translation_verse: Tɛɛle kei na ka abɔɔ ŋan pou Wurubuarɛ be yɛɛ nɛ, sia te yɛɛ ŋɛɛ daa wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa lese ɔ bia gbagba baŋ wola nɛ.

english_verse: We know that all creation is still groaning and is in pain, like a woman about to give birth.
translation_verse: Da gyeŋ nyi kaa lii na gyeŋ wee kei nɛ, tɛɛle kei na abɔɔ ŋan pou doo ke man nɛ na keketee ŋgba alo ŋon ɔ na doŋee nɛ.

english_verse: The Spirit makes us sure about what we will be in the future. But now we groan silently, while we wait for God to show that we are his children. This means that our bodies will also be set free.
translation_verse: Na ŋan ŋaageŋ na keketee la ya. Mɔna daa baŋ dɔɔ nyiŋ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ kpene gyaŋgbate keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo fa daa nɛ, daa mɔ na keketee de konɔɔse man dɛɛ daa Wurubuarɛ ke lɛɛ da nyee lee de dinɔɔ kum kei man na waa yela dɛɛ bese ɔ bia.

english_verse: However, we hope for something we have not yet seen, and we patiently wait for it.
translation_verse: Mɔna akpaa da mo de loo gyakaa kpene keŋ dɔɔ dana ke ya nɛ dɔɔ na, kenaŋ na dee nyiŋ konɔɔ kyaa daa ke.

english_verse: In certain ways we are weak, but the Spirit is here to help us. For example, when we don't know what to pray for, the Spirit prays for us in ways that cannot be put into words.
translation_verse: Mena mɔ te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ne kyɔ daa de kaale man wee na wee lee da kefane man. Nawolo nyi dɔɔ gyeŋ mena keŋ dɛɛ fane na ke kaboena Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii ya. Mɔna ŋon Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ɔ doo de man nɛ, na seŋala de nawɔɔ man mo kakeketee dinaa keŋ ke taŋ keyako ya nɛ kolosi seŋ de wɔle.

english_verse: All of our thoughts are known to God. He can understand what is in the mind of the Spirit, as the Spirit prays for God's people.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ gyeŋ kpene keŋ doo walaŋ kamasɛ konɔɔ man, te ɔ gyeŋ kpene kamasɛ keŋ mɔ doo ɔ feliŋ ŋon agyueŋ man. Nawolo nyi ɔ feliŋ ŋonaŋ seŋɛɛ la de nawɔɔ man ɔ ne fane fa daa Kristotena lee Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii man. Gyoorobiiri keŋ dee nyiŋ wee daale nɛ

english_verse: and he has always known who his chosen ones would be. He had decided to let them become like his own Son, so his Son would be the first of many children.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi balaŋ baŋ Wurubuarɛ wulaa ɔ gyeŋ nɛ, ɔ wulaa lese wɔ see na waa yela be dinɔɔ ke yɛɛ ŋgba ɔ bu ŋon wui nɛ. Na ɔ bu ŋon ke yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ bia man pou bu kegyia.

english_verse: What can we say about all this? If God is on our side, can anyone be against us?
translation_verse: Kɛŋa pou wɔle na, sena te dɛɛ yako bela? Nyi akpaa Wurubuarɛ seŋɛɛ de wɔle na, amɔte ne gyae la waa tale koro seŋ tia daa?

english_verse: It is exactly as the Scriptures say, “For you we face death all day long. We are like sheep on their way to be butchered.”
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see yakowɔ yenaŋ daale nyiaa, “Lee nyaŋ dɔɔ wee kamasɛ na bɛɛ gyae daa baa ko. Bɛɛ yɛɛ daa ŋgba namense baŋ bɛɛ wɔŋee wɔ be kaa ko nɛ.”

english_verse: In everything we have won more than a victory because of Christ who loves us.
translation_verse: Abɔɔ kɛŋa pou man na dee yoo lee, lee Yesu Kristo ŋon ɔ ne gyae daa nɛ dɔɔ.

english_verse: I am a follower of Christ, and the Holy Spirit is a witness to my conscience. So I tell the truth and I am not lying when I say
translation_verse: Nombii na anokoare yaa mee kolosi fa ŋon nɛ. Ka te yɛɛ dekara ya nawolo nyi me yɛɛ Kristo kpilale e. Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ɔ doo me man nɛ ne di maŋ adansɛɛ nyi me konɔɔ man nombii kei yɛɛ anokoare.

english_verse: I would gladly be placed under God's curse and be separated from Christ for the good of my own people.
translation_verse: Nyi ke yɛɛ kpene keŋ ke gyae ke de nyi Wurubuarɛ waa falaa maŋ na daa na Kristo nsana ke tira, na me tebia Iseraetena kenyiŋ nyiŋ nyeedoŋ na nafɔ me gyae.

english_verse: They are the descendants of Israel, and they are also God's chosen people. God showed them his glory. He made agreements with them and gave them his Law. The temple is theirs and so are the promises that God made to them.
translation_verse: Iseraetena yɛna balaŋ baŋ Wurubuarɛ ba lese wɔ te ɔ ba mo wɔ yɛɛ ɔ bia. Wurubuarɛ lese ɔ gyoorobiiri wola wɔ te ɔ yakowɔ see nyi waa hiraa wɔ. Ɔ moo ɔ mmaraase ŋan fa wɔ, te ɔ wola wɔ gbɛɛ kpaakpaate keŋ baa mo som e. Kɛŋa wɔle na Wurubuarɛ besewɔ yako see nyi, waa mo abɔɔ kpaakpaa burum ŋaale mɔ kpu wɔ.

english_verse: They have those famous ancestors, who were also the ancestors of the Christ. I pray that God, who rules over all, will be praised forever! Amen.
translation_verse: Iseraetena kɛwɔ man te Wurubuarɛ balaŋ tuturu baŋ bɔɔ kyaa kaalaŋ nɛ leewɔ. Kalola man na, Yesu Kristo mɔ lee Iseraetena duuluŋ man te ŋon yɛna kpene kamasɛ nyee. Wurubuarɛ te kaboena nyi baa lese ɔ yele kekpaakekpaa Amɛn.

english_verse: It cannot be said that God broke his promise. After all, not all of the people of Israel are the true people of God.
translation_verse: Kei bɛɛ wolo nyi abɔɔ ŋan Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa yɛɛ fa Iseraetena nɛ, ɔ te yɛɛ kina ya. Nawolo nyi na balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ lee Iserae nɛ yɛna Wurubuarɛ balaŋ gbagba ya.

english_verse: In fact, when God made the promise to Abraham, he meant only Abraham's descendants by his son Isaac. God was talking only about Isaac when he promised
translation_verse: Kei nawolo baa nyiaa, na bia baŋ pou Aberaham ba lolo nɛ yɛna Wurubuarɛ bia gbagba ya. Mɔna baŋ Wurubuarɛ be yako see pɛte Aberaham ba lola wɔ nɛ, baŋ yɛna Aberaham duuluŋ man balaŋ gbagba.

english_verse: Sarah, “At this time next year I will return, and you will already have a son.”
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ be yako see fa Aberaham yɛna nyiaa, “Ŋaale kala debaŋ kei me kaa bese kɔŋ na, n wɛɛle Saara ta lola bu baale.”

english_verse: Don't forget what happened to the twin sons of Isaac and Rebekah.
translation_verse: Na nombii kei kaageŋ ya. Mɔna Rebeka lola bia baala bala, te be popou be kya yɛna de naana Asiki.

english_verse: Even before they were born or had done anything good or bad, the Lord told Rebekah that her older son would serve the younger one. The Lord said this to show he makes his own choices and it wasn't because of anything either of them had done.
translation_verse: Pɛte Rebeka ka lola bia kɛwɔ na be kaa bee gyeŋ kolo kpaakpaa na kolo kum nɛ, na Wurubuarɛ wulaa yako ba wose man nombia see nyiaa, “Kegyia ŋon waa kaa som kemaasa ŋon.” Kpene keŋ dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ be yɛɛ mena yɛna nyi, ɔ ne gyae balaŋ ka gyeŋ nyi te yɛɛ nyi ŋolo tom keŋ ɔ ne yɛɛ nɛ dɔɔ te ŋon Wurubuarɛ na lese ɔtenate mo e yɛɛ ɔ wui ya. Mɔna Wurubuarɛ na lese walaŋ bo lee ŋon gbagba kegyaebii man.

english_verse: That's why the Scriptures say that the Lord liked Jacob more than Esau.
translation_verse: Mena ke te be yakowɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan man nyiaa, “Gyekɔpo te ma lesewɔ, te me tinaa Esawu yela.”

english_verse: In the Scriptures the Lord says to the king of Egypt, “I let you become king, so that I could show you my power and be praised by all people on earth.”
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ yakowɔ ɔ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan man lee Igyipite gyoo Faaro wose man nyiaa, “Kpene keŋ dɔɔ te mɔɔ see neŋ gyoori yɛna nyi, mɛɛ gyae nyi maa ta n dɔɔ lese me doŋ wola na tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ pou kenyiŋ nyii ma yele.”

english_verse: Everything depends on what God decides to do, and he can either have pity on people or make them stubborn. God's Anger and Mercy
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ da te naa nyi akpaa Wurubuarɛ ne gyae nyi waa wii balaŋ waraŋase na ɔ ne wii be waraŋase, te akpaa ɔ ne gyae nyi balaŋ ke yɛɛ konɔɔyuŋ na ɔ ne yeli bɛɛ yɛɛ konɔɔyuŋ.

english_verse: Someone may ask, “How can God blame us, if he makes us behave in the way he wants us to?”
translation_verse: Ɛ man ŋolo waa tale bɔɔse nyiaa, “Nyi akpaa mena te doo na, weera dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ ne bu balaŋ fɔɔ lee be nombiakumɛɛ keyɛɛ dɔɔ? Te amɔte ne gyae la waa tale kyɛɛkee Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii?”

english_verse: But, my friend, I ask, “Who do you think you are to question God? Does the clay have the right to ask the potter why he shaped it the way he did?
translation_verse: Mɛɛ bɔɔse ŋon baa nyiaa, deniwalaŋ yɛna amɔte keŋ waa tale ŋon na Wurubuarɛ ke wɔŋee nombia? Osuŋ gyae ke tale bɔɔse walaŋ ŋon ɔ be ma ke nɛ nyi, “Weera dɔɔ te n maa maŋ nnɛ?”

english_verse: Doesn't a potter have the right to make a fancy bowl and a plain bowl out of the same lump of clay?”
translation_verse: Kei be tale kɔŋ mena ya, nawolo nyi walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne ma osuŋse nɛ waa tale mo tɛɛle degbaŋ dokoloŋ ma osuŋ kpaakpaate. Mena mɔ te waa tale mo tɛɛle degbaŋ dokoloŋ kenaŋ ma osuŋ yakaate.

english_verse: God wanted to show his anger and reveal his power against everyone who deserved to be destroyed. But instead, he patiently put up with them.
translation_verse: Mena ke te Wurubuarɛ mɔ doo. Nafɔ ɔ ne gyae nyi waa lese ɔ baŋ wola na balaŋ kena nyi ɔ dana doŋ. Mɔna ɔ ne nyiŋ konɔɔ nideli fa baŋ ɔ ba gyeŋ baŋ be dɔɔ nɛ. Nafɔ kaboena nyi waa wɔlɛɛ mena balaŋ benaŋ kekpaakekpaa.

english_verse: He did this by showing how glorious he is when he has pity on the people he has chosen to share in his glory.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ nyiŋa konɔɔ fa daa baŋ ɔ ne gyae nyi waa nyiŋ konɔɔ fa nɛ, nawolo nyi ɔ ne gyae nyi waa lese ɔ gyoorobiiri dinaa keŋ wola balaŋ baŋ ɔ be wulaa lese wɔ see nyi baa kaa di gyoori kpu na e nɛ.

english_verse: just as the Lord says in the book of Hosea, “Although they are not my people, I will make them my people. I will treat with love those nations that have never been loved.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ yako mena nombia kɛŋa ɔ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Hosea tɔne man nyiaa, “Balaŋ baŋ nafɔ ba te yɛɛ me balaŋwɔ ya nɛ, maa baake wɔ nyi me balaŋ. Te balaŋ baŋ nafɔ mɔɔ ne gyae wɔ ya nɛ, baa kaa yɛɛ me balaŋ baŋ mɛɛ gyae.”

english_verse: “Once they were told, ‘You are not my people.’ But in that very place they will be called children of the living God.”
translation_verse: Mena mɔ te, “Botɔɔ keŋ nafɔ bɛɛ yako wɔ nyi ba te yɛɛ me balaŋwɔ ya nɛ, botɔɔtena te baa kaa baake nyi Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa kekpaakekpaa nɛ bia.”

english_verse: And this is what the prophet Isaiah said about the people of Israel, “The people of Israel are as many as the grains of sand along the beach. But only a few who are left will be saved.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya mɔ gyɔɔ dekpeŋkpeŋ yako lee Iseraetena wose man nyiaa, “Doo nyi Iseraetena boe ŋgba ɛpo nɔɔman aŋasa nɛ, mɔna be man balaŋ kyomii baale te Wurubuarɛ waa lɛɛ ba nyee.

english_verse: The Lord will be quick and sure to do on earth what he has warned he will do.”
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi be gyae ke kyare ya, de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ waa di tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ pou nombia karatetee dokoloŋ.”

english_verse: Isaiah also said, “If the Lord All-Powerful had not spared some of our descendants, we would have been destroyed like the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah.” Israel and the Good News
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya mɔ yako mena nombii kei kaalaŋ nyiaa, “Nafɔ Wurubuarɛ doŋte ŋon deŋ te kaase de duuluŋ man balaŋ kyomii baale see ya nɛ, nafɔ de popou deŋ munuwɔ ŋgba mena keŋ Sodom na Gomora donɔɔse na ŋe man balaŋ pou be munu nɛ.”

english_verse: What does all of this mean? It means that the Gentiles were not trying to be acceptable to God, but they found that he would accept them if they had faith.
translation_verse: Lee mena dɔɔ kpene keŋ mɛɛ gyae maa yako yɛna nyi balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ, nafɔ ba te yase be sia nyi baa yela be gbɛɛneŋ ka tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman ya. Mɔna lee ba kelɛɛdi dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ lɛɛ wɔ do yela be gbɛɛneŋ teneewɔ ɔ siaman.

english_verse: It also means that the people of Israel were not acceptable to God. And why not? It was because they were trying to be acceptable by obeying the Law instead of by having faith in God. The people of Israel fell over the stone that makes people stumble,
translation_verse: Weera dɔɔ te be yɛɛwɔ kina? Nawolo nyi ba te kɛɛ ke nyi Wurubuarɛ kelɛɛdi ne gyae la ke yela be gbɛɛneŋ ka tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman ya. Baŋ gyeŋ nyi be nombia ayɛɛsa ne gyae la ke yela be gbɛɛneŋ ka tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman. Lee kei dɔɔ ba te yɛɛ ŋgba balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ gyoo na boe dinaa nɛ.

english_verse: just as God says in the Scriptures, “Look! I am placing in Zion a stone to make people stumble and fall. But those who have faith in that one will never be disappointed.”
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see yako boe kei nombia nyiaa, “Kɛɛ, me ta mo boe dinaa daale see Siyɔn donɔɔ man. Balaŋ baa gyoo na boe kei kpaŋte, na baa gyoo na ke yala. Mɔna walaŋ ŋon ɔ mo ɔ wose lɔ walaŋ ŋon boe kei seŋɛɛ fa e nɛ dɔɔ na, desɛŋ be gyae ke di e ya.”

english_verse: Dear friends, my greatest wish and my prayer to God is for the people of Israel to be saved.
translation_verse: Me tebia, kpene keŋ mɛɛ gyae me konɔɔ man te mɛɛ fane ke dɔɔ yɛna nyi, Wurubuarɛ waa lɛɛ me tebia Iseraetena pou nyee.

english_verse: But Christ makes the Law no longer necessary for those who become acceptable to God by faith. Anyone Can Be Saved
translation_verse: Kristo yɛna mmaraase ŋan pou nyee, te ɔ kekɔŋ dɔɔ mmaraase ŋan kesila be dana tɔnɔɔ bela ya. Lee kei dɔɔ walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ e di na, ɔtenate gbɛɛneŋ gyae ka tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman.

english_verse: Moses said a person could become acceptable to God by obeying the Law. He did this when he wrote, “If you want to live, you must do all the Law commands.”
translation_verse: De naana Mosesi ŋmarasewɔ lee mena keŋ deniwalaŋ waa sila mmaraase ŋan na ɔ gbɛɛneŋ ka tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ nyiaa, “Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne di mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ na ɔ gbɛɛneŋ ka tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ, akpaa ɔ kyaŋ ɔ wose di ŋe dɔɔ nideli keŋ ɔ te yale ŋe man dokoloŋ dɔɔ ya nɛ, kenaŋ na waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ.”

english_verse: Neither will they ask, “Who will go down into the world of the dead to raise him to life?”
translation_verse: Yaa ŋolo mɔ ke bɔɔse nyi, “Amɔte ne gyae la waa tisi gyu tɛɛle ateta” na ɔ kaa gyuusu Kristo lee woya man kɔŋ ya.

english_verse: All who are acceptable because of their faith simply say, “The message is as near as your mouth or your heart.” And this is the same message we preach about faith.
translation_verse: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ nombia ne yako lee balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ Yesu di, te lee ba kelɛɛdi dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ be lɛɛ wɔ do nyi be gbɛɛneŋ tenee ŋon Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ wose man nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan benaa na ŋon. Ŋe doo ɛ nɔɔse man, te ŋe doo ɛ konɔɔse man mɔ.” Nombia ŋenaŋ yɛna kelɛɛdi nombia ŋan dɛɛ yako nɛ.

english_verse: So you will be saved, if you honestly say, “Jesus is Lord,” and if you believe with all your heart that God raised him from death.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ nyi akpaa n gyuusu n nɔɔ yako balaŋ man nyi, “Yesu yɛna de Gbeŋgyoo” te n lɛɛ di n konɔɔ man nyi Wurubuarɛ gyuusu e lee yeŋ man na, Wurubuarɛ waa lɛɛ n nyee.

english_verse: no matter if that person is a Jew or a Gentile. There is only one Lord, and he is generous to everyone who asks for his help.
translation_verse: N yɛɛ Gyuda baale e o, n te yɛɛ Gyuda baale e ya o, de popou yɛɛ dokoloŋ Wurubuarɛ siaman. Nawolo nyi Kristo ŋonaŋ yɛna de popou de Gbeŋgyoo. Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ wii baake e na, waa mo wa abɔɔ kpaakpaa ŋan boele e.

english_verse: And how can anyone tell them without being sent by the Lord? The Scriptures say it is a beautiful sight to see even the feet of someone coming to preach the good news.
translation_verse: Te akpaa Wurubuarɛ te kpila balaŋ nyi baa gyu ke yako ɔ wose man nombia ŋenaŋ ya na, sena te baa yɛɛ na baa tale gyu ke yako ŋa? Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako nyiaa, “Akpaa balaŋ ne kɔŋ be kaa kolosi nyeedoŋ nombia ŋan na, ba keta na mo nombia kpaakpaa bo kɔŋ.”

english_verse: Yet not everyone has believed the message. For example, the prophet Isaiah asked, “Lord, has anyone believed what we said?”
translation_verse: Mɔna na baŋ pou bɔɔ nyii nombia kpaakpaa ŋan nɛ lɛɛ na ŋa di ya. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya yakowɔ nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, amɔte lɛɛ na de nombia ŋan dɔɔ yako nɛ di?”

english_verse: But am I saying that the people of Israel did not hear? No, I am not! The Scriptures say, “The message was told everywhere on earth. It was announced all over the world.”
translation_verse: “Mɔna ɛ yela maa bɔɔse ŋon nyiaa, mena balaŋ kɛwɔ te nyii Kristo nombia ŋan baa yee?” Kenyii dɔɔ be te nyii, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako nyiaa, “Nombia ŋan te kili gyu tɛɛle kei dɔɔ pou. Te deniwalaŋ kamasɛ mɔ te nyii nombia ŋenaŋ.”

english_verse: Did the people of Israel understand or not? Moses answered this question when he told that the Lord had said, “I will make Israel jealous of people who are a nation of nobodies. I will make them angry with people who don't understand a thing.”
translation_verse: Ɛ yela maa bɔɔse ŋon bela nyiaa, mena dɔɔ ɛ ne yako baa nyi Iseraetena baŋ bɔɔ nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, be te nyii ŋe man yee? Kenyii dɔɔ be nyiiwɔ. Mosesi yɛna walaŋ gyaŋgbate ŋon ɔ be tira mena nombia kɛŋa nɔɔ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ kpa, “Maa mo balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ do ɛmɛɛ Gyudatena sibiiyam. Maa mo balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ gyeŋ daale lee ma wose man ya nɛ do ŋon baŋ.”

english_verse: Isaiah was fearless enough to tell that the Lord had said, “I was found by people who were not looking for me. I appeared to the ones who were not asking about me.”
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya mɔ gyɔɔ ɔ konɔɔ yako nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ kpa, “Balaŋ baŋ ba te yase be sia kɛo maŋ ya nɛ, baŋ naa na maŋ. Te balaŋ baŋ mɔ bɔɔ ne bɔɔse me dɔɔ ya nɛ, baŋ te maa lese ma wose wola.”

english_verse: And Isaiah said about the people of Israel, “All day long the Lord has reached out to people who are stubborn and refuse to obey.”
translation_verse: Mɔna Iseraetena baŋ dɔɔ Yesaya yakowɔ nyi Wurubuarɛ kpa, “Wee na wee na mɛɛ kɛɛ gbɛɛ nyi baa kaa lɛɛ maŋ di na maa lɛɛ ba nyee, mɔna ba te yɛɛ deŋele doŋ koro seŋ tia maŋ.”

english_verse: Am I saying that God has turned his back on his people? Certainly not! I am one of the people of Israel, and I myself am a descendant of Abraham from the tribe of Benjamin.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa nnɛ te nombia kɛŋa doo na, ɛ yela maa bɔɔse ŋon bela nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ bɛɛ ŋon gbagba ɔ balaŋ baŋ baa yaa?” Korakora, ɔ te bɛɛ wɔ ya. Nawolo nyi maŋ Pɔɔl gbagba yɛɛ Iserae baale e. Ma lee Aberaham duuluŋ man, te ma lee Bɛngyamɛn dekpaŋalaŋ man.

english_verse: God did not turn his back on his chosen people. Don't you remember reading in the Scriptures how Elijah complained to God about the people of Israel?
translation_verse: Te yɛɛ nyi Wurubuarɛ bɛɛ ɔ balaŋ baŋ ɔ wulaa ɔ gyeŋ wɔ, te ɔ be wulaa lese wɔ see lee tɛɛle kei kewalaŋ nɛ bo ya. Ɛ tɔɔse mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan be yako lee Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Elia wose man.

english_verse: He said, “Lord, they killed your prophets and destroyed your altars. I am the only one left, and now they want to kill me.”
translation_verse: Elia moo Iseraetena nombiakumɛɛ kaa see Wurubuarɛ siaman te ɔ yako e nyiaa, “Me Gbeŋgyoo, Iseraetena ta koe nya akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ pou te be yɛlɛɛ n kedeesa boya ŋan pou. Maŋ maageŋ kooŋ kaa la, te bɛɛ kɛo maŋ gba koraŋ baa ko.”

english_verse: This means that only a chosen few of the people of Israel found what all of them were searching for. And the rest of them were stubborn,
translation_verse: Nɛnɛɛ sena yaa dɛɛ yako bela? Kpene keŋ Iseraetena be yase be sia kɛo nɛ, be te nyiŋ ke ya. Mɔna balaŋ akalansɛɛ baŋ Wurubuarɛ ba lese wɔ see nɛ, baŋ nyiŋa na ke. Balaŋ akaŋ baŋ dɔɔ, baŋ yɛɛ deŋele doŋ bo.

english_verse: It is the same way now. God treated the people of Israel with undeserved grace, and so a few of them are still his followers.
translation_verse: Mena mɔ te gyɛŋ wee kei nɛ, Wurubuarɛ te tɛɛ ɔ bɛrɛɛ keŋ dɔɔ lese balaŋ akalansɛɛ baale see fa ɔ wose.

english_verse: This happened because of God's undeserved kindness and not because of anything they have done. It could not have happened except for God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Akpaa Wurubuarɛ tɛe ɔ bɛrɛɛ keŋ dɔɔ lese balaŋ na, kenaŋ na te yɛɛ nyi ɔ tɛe ba tom keŋ bɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ dɔɔ lese wɔ ya. Nyi ŋon tɛe ba tom keŋ bɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ dɔɔ lese wɔ nɛ, kenaŋ na ɔ bɛrɛɛ keŋ ta tekaa yɛɛ bɛrɛɛ gbagba ya.

english_verse: Then David said, “Turn their meals into bait for a trap, so that they will stumble and be given what they deserve.
translation_verse: Gyoo Defidi mɔ sola Wurubuarɛ nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ yela be dedii ka bese ba deyalae. Yela baa naa diyem tɔ ba dukum kom.

english_verse: Blindfold their eyes! Don't let them see. Bend their backs beneath a burden that will never be lifted.” Gentiles Will Be Saved
translation_verse: Yela be sia ke yɛlɛɛ na baa ta kpaŋte, na baa kyaa kaale man debaŋ kamasɛ.”

english_verse: Do I mean that the people of Israel fell, never to get up again? Certainly not! Their failure made it possible for the Gentiles to be saved, and this will make the people of Israel jealous.
translation_verse: Ɛ yela maa bɔɔse ŋon bela nyiaa, ke yɛɛ ampaŋ nyi Iseraetena baŋ kpaŋtewɔ yala keŋ be ta bese tale koro yee? Koa, na mena ya. Mɔna kelɛɛdi keŋ ba te lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di ya nɛ, keŋ toro na gbɛɛ fa balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Iseraetenawɔ ya nɛ, na Wurubuarɛ ke lɛɛ ba nyee na ke nyiŋ do Iseraetena benaŋ sibiiyam.

english_verse: I am now speaking to you Gentiles, and as long as I am an apostle to you, I will take pride in my work.
translation_verse: Nɛnɛɛ ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ ɛ tei nombii kei. Me yɛɛ Kristo kpilale e te ŋon kpila na maŋ ɛ gyaŋ nyi me kaa kolosi ɔ nombia ŋan fa ŋon. Mena dɔɔ mɔɔ na mo ma tom kei do nsendee ya.

english_verse: I hope in this way to make some of my own people jealous enough to be saved.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi me loo gyakaa nyi me kyɔ ŋon na, gyae ka do me tebia Gyudatena baŋ sibiiyam na ke yela Wurubuarɛ ke lɛɛ ba nyee.

english_verse: When Israel rejected God, the rest of the people in the world were able to turn to him. So when God makes friends with Israel, it will be like bringing the dead back to life.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba besena ɔ wɔle tee Iseraetena nɛ, ke yela balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Iseraetenawɔ ya nɛ kaa bese Wurubuarɛ ɔ gyoona. Nyi akpaa mena te doo nɛ, kenaŋ na debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa kaa lɛɛ Iseraetena baŋ do na, gyae ke yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Iseraetenawɔ ya nɛ gyoŋ ŋgba be foŋae lee yeŋ man nɛ.

english_verse: If part of a batch of dough is made holy by being offered to God, then all of the dough is holy. If the roots of a tree are holy, the rest of the tree is holy too.
translation_verse: Akpaa n dana kolo te n lese ke man ŋaale deesi fa Wurubuarɛ na, kenaŋ na nawolo baa nyi kpene kenaŋ pou yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ wui. Mena mɔ te akpaa daŋ lila yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ wui na, kenaŋ na nawolo baa nyi daŋ kenaŋ pou yɛɛ ɔ wui.

english_verse: You Gentiles are like branches of a wild olive tree made to be part of a cultivated olive tree. You have taken the place of some branches that were cut away from it. And because of this, you enjoy the blessings that come from being part of that cultivated tree.
translation_verse: Gyudatena yɛɛ ŋgba oliifi daŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ gbagba be duu nɛ. Te balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ, baŋ yɛɛ yaa ŋgba ŋale man oliifi daŋ nɛ. Mena dɔɔ yɛɛ yaa ŋgba Wurubuarɛ te kara oliifi daŋ keŋ ɔ be duu nɛ tombia ŋaale leki, te ɔ ke kara ŋale man oliifi daŋ keŋ tombia ka towaa na ke nɛ. Kei nawolo baa nyi ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ, ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ ke nyiŋ doŋ nawolo nyi ɛ ke nyiŋ loŋ lee oliifi daŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ gbagba be duu nɛ man.

english_verse: But don't think you are better than the branches that were cut away. Just remember you are not supporting the roots of this tree. Its roots are supporting you.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛ na gyɔ ɛ konɔɔ nyi ɛ kela tombia ŋan bɔɔ kare leki te bɔɔ mo ŋon towaa nɛ ya. Nawolo nyi na daŋ tombia danaana ke ka seŋɛɛ ya, mɔna ke lila danaana ke ka seŋɛɛ.

english_verse: Maybe you think those branches were cut away, so you could be put in their place.
translation_verse: Mberem na ɛ ke yako nyi, “Be keretii tombia ŋan bo leki na daa baŋ da te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ, ke nyiŋ gbɛɛ kaa towaa botɔɔ ne dee nyiŋ nyeedoŋ”

english_verse: That's true enough. But they were cut away because they did not have faith, and you are where you are because you do have faith. So don't be proud, but be afraid.
translation_verse: Kei yɛɛ ampaŋ. Keŋ ba te lɛɛ di dɔɔ ya te Wurubuarɛ keretii wɔ leki, te keŋ ɛmɛɛ be kaa lɛɛ di dɔɔ te ɛ kaa seŋawɔ be nawɔɔ man. Mena dɔɔ ɛ na wola ɛ wose ya, mɔna ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose dɔɔ nideli.

english_verse: Now you see both how kind and how hard God can be. He was hard on those who fell, but he was kind to you. And he will keep on being kind to you, if you keep on trusting in his kindness. Otherwise, you will be cut away too.
translation_verse: N kɛɛ nombii kei na, dɛɛ tale naa nyi Wurubuarɛ dana wɔe nideli te ɔ nombia mɔ dana doŋ nideli. Ɔ nombia dana doŋ fa baŋ bɔɔ ne yɛɛ ɔ kegyaebii ya nɛ, mɔna ɛmɛɛ balaŋ baŋ ɛ yɛɛ kakyeŋ te ɛ dɔŋ ɔ gbɛɛneŋ man keŋ ɛ te tina yela ya na, waa lese ɔ wɔe wola ŋon. Na mena ya na waa keretii ɛmɛɛ mɔ leki.

english_verse: If those other branches will start having faith, they will be made a part of that tree again. God has the power to put them back.
translation_verse: Te akpaa Iseraetena baŋ bɔɔ tina ba kelɛɛdi yela nɛ da bese kɔŋ kaa lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di na, kenaŋ na waa besena wɔ kaa see be daseŋae bene keŋ man. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ waa tale mo wɔ kaa towaa na oliifi daŋ keŋ bela.

english_verse: After all, it wasn't natural for branches to be cut from a wild olive tree and to be made part of a cultivated olive tree. So it is much more likely that God will join the natural branches back to the cultivated olive tree. The People of Israel Will Be Brought Back
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ te yɛɛ Iseraetenawɔ ya nɛ, ɛ yɛɛ ŋgba ŋale man oliifi daŋ tombia ŋan bɔɔ kare mo kaa towaa na oliifi daŋ gbagba keŋ nɛ. N kɛɛ na nafɔ bɔɔ ne kara daŋ daale tombii kaa towaa na daale ya. Mɔna Iseraetena baŋ dɔɔ, baŋ yɛna oliifi daŋ gbagba keŋ bɔɔ kara ke tombia leki nɛ. Mena dɔɔ be gyae ke yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ doŋ nyi waa bese mo tombia ŋenaŋ kaa towaa na ke naa keŋ ya. Wurubuarɛ waa lɛɛ Iseraetena pou nyee

english_verse: My friends, I don't want you Gentiles to be too proud of yourselves. So I will explain the mystery of what has happened to the people of Israel. Some of them have become stubborn, and they will stay like that until the complete number of you Gentiles has come in.
translation_verse: Me tebia, nombii daale weesɛɛ keŋ mɛɛ gyae ɛ ke nyii ke man. Akpaa ɛ nyii ke man na, be gyae ke yela ɛ ke yɛɛ ya agyueŋ nyi ɛ gyeŋ nombia gbaa ya. Nombii kenaŋ yɛna nyi Iseraetena dana deŋele doŋ, mɔna te yɛɛ nyi baa yɛɛ deŋele doŋ kenaŋ kekpaakekpaa ya. Baa yɛɛ deŋele doŋ kenaŋ bo kelii debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa kaa lɛɛ balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Iseraetenawɔ ya nɛ yaanɔɔle keŋ ɔ ne gyae nɛ nyee pɛ.

english_verse: In this way all of Israel will be saved, as the Scriptures say, “From Zion someone will come to rescue us. Then Jacob's descendants will stop being evil.
translation_verse: Dekɔŋ mena na Wurubuarɛ waa lɛɛ Iseraetena pou nyee ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako nɛ nyiaa, “Siyɔn donɔɔ man te nyeelɛɛre ŋon waa lee kɔŋ, na ɔ kaa lese nombia nkaraŋkaraŋ pou lee Iseraetena man.

english_verse: The people of Israel are treated as God's enemies, so the good news can come to you Gentiles. But they are still the chosen ones, and God loves them because of their famous ancestors.
translation_verse: Keŋ Iseraetena te lɛɛ nyeedoŋ nombia ŋan di dɔɔ ya, te be kaa bese balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ kɔla Wurubuarɛ. Mɔna kei kaa yɛɛ tɔnɔɔ fa ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ te yɛɛ Iseraetenawɔ ya nɛ. Te keŋ be yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ Wurubuarɛ be wulaa lese wɔ see dɔɔ, mena dɔɔ ɔ ne gyae be nombia lee be naanaɔ dɔɔ.

english_verse: God doesn't take back the gifts he has given or disown the people he has chosen.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi akpaa Wurubuarɛ da lese ŋolo te ɔ mo kolo boele e na, ɔ bɛɛ kyɛɛkee wa agyueŋ bela ya.

english_verse: At one time you Gentiles rejected God. But now Israel has rejected God, and you have been shown mercy.
translation_verse: Debaŋ daale kaa laŋawɔ na ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ te yɛɛ Iseraetenawɔ ya nɛ ɛ yɛɛ deŋele doŋ Wurubuarɛ dɔɔ, mɔna nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ lee Iseraetena deŋele doŋ dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ te kaa wii ɛ waraŋase.

english_verse: And because of the mercy shown to you, they will also be shown mercy.
translation_verse: Mena ke te nɛnɛɛ baŋ mɔ dana deŋele doŋ, na ke yela Wurubuarɛ ke wii be waraŋase ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be wii ɛ waraŋase nɛ.

english_verse: All people have disobeyed God, and that's why he treats them as prisoners. But he does this, so that he can have mercy on all of them.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ te yeli balaŋ pou te yɛɛ deŋele doŋ, na waa nyiŋ wii be popou waraŋase.

english_verse: “Has anyone known the thoughts of the Lord or given him advice?
translation_verse: Amɔte gyeŋa na de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ nyeeman nombia, te amɔte ne gyae la waa tale kolosi fa e?

english_verse: Has anyone loaned something to the Lord that must be repaid?”
translation_verse: Amɔte faa na Wurubuarɛ kolo see, keŋ kaboena nyi ŋon mɔ waa tɔ ɔtenate kom?

english_verse: Everything comes from the Lord. All things were made because of him and will return to him. Praise the Lord forever! Amen.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi kpene kamasɛ yɛɛ ɔ wui. Lee ɔ doŋ man dɔɔ te kpene kamasɛ kyaa, te ŋe popou mɔ kyae fa e. Ŋon kaboela nyi dɛɛ lese ɔ yele kekpaakekpaa. Amɛn.

english_verse: Don't be like the people of this world, but let God change the way you think. Then you will know how to do everything that is good and pleasing to him.
translation_verse: Ɛ na tekaa mo ɛ wose do tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ abɔɔ ayɛɛsa man bela ya. Mɔna ɛ yela Wurubuarɛ ke kyɛɛkee ɛ konɔɔ man nombia na ɛ dinɔɔ pou, na ɛ ka bese balaŋ wɔlɛɛ ɛ kekyaabii man. Kei gyae ke yela ɛ ka gyeŋ kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ ne gyae, te ke dei te kɛɛ gyoo ɔ sia nɛ nideli nɛ.
