translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ nombia kpaakpaa ŋan dɔɔ te mɛɛ yɛɛ kɛŋa pou, na maŋ mɔ kenyiŋ abɔɔ kpaakpaa ŋan gyae ka lee ŋe man kɔŋ nɛ.

english_verse: I keep my body under control and make it my slave, so I won't lose out after telling the good news to others.
translation_verse: Mɔna maŋ dɔɔ mɛɛ naase ma wose diyem, te mɛɛ yɛɛ ke ŋgba me gbeŋi nɛ. Na wee daale dekɔŋ nyi ma kekolosi Kristo nombia ŋan fa balaŋ taŋ wɔle nɛ, na bɔɔ lese maŋ see yenaŋ keŋ mɔɔ nyiŋ yele ya.

english_verse: Friends, I want to remind you that all our ancestors walked under the cloud and went through the sea.
translation_verse: Me tebia, mɛɛ gyae ɛ ke tɔɔse kpene keŋ be tuŋ de naanaɔ baŋ bɔɔ sila Mosesi nɛ. Wurubuarɛ moo ɔ debuŋta wuu wɔ, te ɔ yela ba gyoowɔ ɛpo yayam keŋ man lee.

english_verse: All of them also ate the same spiritual food
translation_verse: Be popou dii weenɛɛ ŋan ba lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ kaa yala nɛ,

english_verse: and drank the same spiritual drink, which flowed from the spiritual rock that followed them. That rock was Christ.
translation_verse: te be popou nyɔɔ loŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yeli kɔɔ lee boe keŋ man nɛ. Mena boe kenaŋ yɛna Kristo gbagba, ŋon na ɔ kpuɛ na wɔ ba keta man nɛ.

english_verse: But most of them did not please God. So they died, and their bodies were scattered all over the desert.
translation_verse: Mɔna be man balaŋ burum nombia ta gyoo Wurubuarɛ sia ya, mena dɔɔ ba yekeewɔ leki depampaa dinaa keŋ man.

english_verse: What happened to them is a warning to keep us from wanting to do the same evil things.
translation_verse: Be nombia kɛŋa pou yɛɛ kpene keŋ dɛɛ kɛɛ na dɛɛ kasee, na dɔɔ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛ ŋgba baŋ nɛ ya.

english_verse: They worshiped idols, just as the Scriptures say, “The people sat down to eat and drink. Then they got up to dance around.” So don't worship idols.
translation_verse: Ɛ na som bɛɛneŋ ŋgba mena keŋ be man baale be yɛɛ nɛ ya. Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see yakowɔ nyiaa, “Balaŋ baŋ tisiwɔ kyakee na bee dii na bɛɛ nyɔɔ, te ba korowɔ bɛɛ yɛɛ nombia nkaraŋkaraŋ.”

english_verse: Some of those people did shameful things, and in a single day about
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛ na yɛɛ kafoŋ ŋgba mena keŋ bɔɔ yɛɛ kafoŋ nombia, te be man balaŋ kakpoŋse saobatooro (

english_verse: Some of you say, “We can do whatever we want to!” But I tell you not everything may be good or helpful.
translation_verse: “Gbɛɛ doo nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ” mɔna na ŋe popou danaana tɔnɔɔ ya. “Gbɛɛ doo nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ” mɔna na ŋe popou ne gyae la ke yela dɛɛ bee ya.

english_verse: died. Don't do shameful things as they did.
translation_verse: ) ba yem wee dokoloŋ nɛ ya.

english_verse: And don't try to test Christ, as some of them did and were later bitten by poisonous snakes.
translation_verse: Tekaboena nyi dɛɛ teese Wurubuarɛ nɔɔman, ŋgba mena keŋ be man baale be yɛɛ te ɔ be yeli doma be kaa koe wɔ nɛ ya.

english_verse: Don't even grumble, as some of them did and were killed by the destroying angel.
translation_verse: Tekaboena nyi dɛɛ toroŋ ŋgba mena keŋ be man baale ba toroŋ, te Wurubuarɛ be yeli ɔ kpilale ŋon ɔ na koe balaŋ nɛ be kaa koe wɔ nɛ ya.

english_verse: These things happened to them as a warning to us. All this was written in the Scriptures to teach us who live in these last days.
translation_verse: Mena nombia kɛŋa pou kɔŋae be dɔɔ na balaŋ ka mo kasee kolo. Te be ŋmarase ŋa bo see na baa mo faree daa, nawolo nyi de kyaa debaŋ keŋ tɛɛle kei ne gyu kɔ ɔto nɛ man.

english_verse: Even if you think you can stand up to temptation, be careful not to fall.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ nyaŋ walaŋ ŋon ne yɛɛ neŋ nyi n seŋɛɛ keŋkeŋ nɛ, kɛɛ n wose nideli keŋ nyi n ba lee yala ya.

english_verse: You are tempted in the same way that everyone else is tempted. But God can be trusted not to let you be tempted too much, and he will show you how to escape from your temptations.
translation_verse: Kekɛɛsekɛɛ daale te kɔŋa ta ɛ dɔɔ, keŋ nyi ke dinɔɔ te kɔŋa ta ŋolo dɔɔ see ya. Mɔna Wurubuarɛ ne di ɔ nombia dɔɔ, mena dɔɔ ɔ be gyae waa yela kekɛɛsekɛɛ keŋ kela ŋon nɛ kekɔŋ ɛ dɔɔ ya. Te akpaa nyi kekɛɛsekɛɛ de kɔŋ ɛ dɔɔ na, waa wola ŋon gbɛɛ keŋ ɛ ka mo seŋ keŋkeŋ na ɛ ke tale di ke dɔɔ.

english_verse: My friends, you must keep away from idols.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ me gyoona kpaakpaa, ɛ lese ɛ wose lee bɛɛneŋ ɔsom man.

english_verse: I am speaking to you as people who have enough sense to know what I am talking about.
translation_verse: Mee kolosi baa fa ɛmɛɛ nyansatena. Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ gyoo me nombia ŋan man na ɛ ke kɛɛ kpene keŋ mɛɛ yako nɛ.

english_verse: By sharing in the same loaf of bread, we become one body, even though there are many of us.
translation_verse: Bodobodo dokoloŋ te dɛɛ mo kpala fa dɔŋa, mena dɔɔ daa balaŋ burum baŋ pou ta bese walaŋ dokoloŋ. Nawolo nyi bodobodo dokoloŋ kenaŋ te de popou diiwɔ.

english_verse: Aren't the people of Israel sharing in the worship when they gather around the altar and eat the sacrifices offered there?
translation_verse: Ɛ mo kɛɛ Iseraetena. Balaŋ baŋ bee di weenɛɛ ŋan bɔɔ mo deesi fa Wurubuarɛ nɛ, baŋ na Wurubuarɛ na bese na walaŋ dokoloŋ.

english_verse: Am I saying that either the idols or the food sacrificed to them is anything at all?
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ sena yaa me nombia kɛŋa na wolo baa? Bane yɛɛ kolo ke, yaa weenɛɛ ŋan bɛɛ mo deesi fa bane nɛ yɛna kolo?

english_verse: No, I am not! This food is really sacrificed to demons and not to God. I don't want you to have anything to do with demons.
translation_verse: Koa, kpene keŋ mɛɛ wolo yɛna nyiaa, weenɛɛ ŋan bane somra na mo deesi fa banɛɛ nɛ, te yɛɛ nyi Wurubuarɛ te bɛɛ mo deesi fa ya. Feliŋkumɛɛ te bee deesi fa, mena dɔɔ mɔɔ ne gyae nyi ɛmɛɛ na feliŋkumɛɛ ke yɛɛ dokoloŋ ya.

english_verse: You cannot drink from the cup of demons and still drink from the Lord's cup. You cannot eat at the table of demons and still eat at the Lord's table.
translation_verse: Ɛ be gyae ɛ ke tale mo de Gbeŋgyoo Kristo leenɔɔ nyɔɔ, na ɛ ka bese mo feliŋkumɛɛ mɔ leenɔɔ nyɔɔ ya. Te be gyae ke de nyi ɛ ke di de Gbeŋgyoo Kristo nyefale man, na ɛ ka bese ke di feliŋkumɛɛ mɔ nyefale man ya.

english_verse: We would make the Lord jealous if we did this. And we are not stronger than the Lord. Always Honor God
translation_verse: Dɛɛ gyae baa nyi de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ ka gyeŋ baŋ? Yaa dɛɛ kɛɛ da wose baa nyi de dana doŋ kela e?

english_verse: We should think about others and not about ourselves.
translation_verse: Ŋolo na gyae ŋon gbagba wui deeli ya, mɔna waa gyae ɔ tebia wui deeli bo.

english_verse: However, when you buy meat in the market, go ahead and eat it. Keep your conscience clear by not asking where the meat came from.
translation_verse: Naane kamasɛ keŋ bɛɛ yɔɔ kebu man nɛ ɛ ke tale lɛɛ di, mɔna na bɔɔse naane keŋ dalee na ke yela ŋolo konɔɔ man nombia ke yɔkɔse e ya.

english_verse: The Scriptures say, “The earth and everything in it belong to the Lord.”
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako nyiaa “Tɛɛle kei na kpene kamasɛ keŋ doo ke man nɛ pou, yɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ wui.”

english_verse: If an unbeliever invites you to dinner, and you want to go, then go. Eat whatever you are served. Don't cause a problem for someone's conscience by asking where the food came from.
translation_verse: Akpaa walaŋ ŋon ɔ te yɛɛ Kristo baale e ya de baake ŋon nyi ɛ kaa di, te ke yɛɛ keŋ ɛ ke di na, ɛ gyu ke di mɔna ɛ na bɔɔse weenɛɛ ŋan dalee na ŋolo konɔɔ man nombia ke yɔkɔse e ya.

english_verse: But if you are told it has been sacrificed to idols, don't cause a problem by eating it. I don't mean a problem for yourself, but for the one who told you. Why should my freedom be limited by someone else's conscience?
translation_verse: Na ɛmɛɛ gbagba konɔɔ man nombia dɔɔ ya, mɔna walaŋ ŋon ɔ be yako ŋon nɛ konɔɔ man nombia dɔɔ. Nawolo nyi ŋolo konɔɔ man nombia be gyae ke tale tɔ maŋ gbɛɛ, nyi me na yɛɛ kpene keŋ me dana gbɛɛ nyi maa yɛɛ nɛ ya.

english_verse: If I give thanks for what I eat, why should anyone accuse me of doing wrong?
translation_verse: Mɔna akpaa me fane fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ te me di weenɛɛ kɛŋa na, woŋ dɔɔ te ŋolo waa bu maŋ fɔɔ lee weenɛɛ kɛŋa kedi dɔɔ?

english_verse: When you eat or drink or do anything else, always do it to honor God.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɛ ne dii yaa ɛ ne nyɔɔ, yaa kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɛ ne yɛɛ na, ɛ yɛɛ ŋe popou mo lese Wurubuarɛ yele.

english_verse: Don't cause problems for Jews or Greeks or anyone else who belongs to God's church.
translation_verse: Ɛ na yeli ɛ nombia ayɛɛsa ke tɔ Gyuda baale, yaa Giriki baale yaa Kristo walaŋ kamasɛ gbɛɛ ya.

english_verse: You must follow my example, as I follow the example of Christ. Rules for Worship
translation_verse: Ɛ kasee me dinɔɔ ŋgba mena keŋ maŋ mɔ ne kasee Kristo dinɔɔ nɛ.

english_verse: I am proud of you, because you always remember me and obey the teachings I gave you.
translation_verse: Mɛɛ kɛlɛɛ ŋon ŋgba mena keŋ ɛ ne tɔɔse maŋ ɛ nombia pou man, te ɛ dana me nombia ŋan mɔɔ wola ŋon man nideli nɛ.

english_verse: Now I want you to know that Christ is the head of all men, and a man is the head of a woman. But God is the head of Christ.
translation_verse: Mɔna mɛɛ gyae ɛ ka gyeŋ nyi baale kamasɛ nyee yɛna Kristo, te alo mɔ nyee yɛna ɔ baale te Kristo mɔ nyee yɛna Wurubuarɛ.

english_verse: This means that any man who prays or prophesies with something on his head brings shame to his head.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ baale kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne fane yaa ɔ ne kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia balaŋ man, te ɔ mo kolo wu ɔ nyee na ɔ ta do ɔ nyee desɛŋ.

english_verse: But any woman who prays or prophesies without something on her head brings shame to her head. In fact, she may as well shave her head.
translation_verse: Mena mɔ te alo kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne fane yaa ɔ ne kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia balaŋ man, te ɔ ta mo kolo wu ɔ nyee ya na ɔ ta do ɔ nyee desɛŋ. Na yɛɛ yaa ŋgba ɔ gbee ɔ nyee baa nɛ.

english_verse: A woman should wear something on her head. It is a disgrace for a woman to shave her head or cut her hair. But if she refuses to wear something on her head, let her cut off her hair.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa alo bɛɛ gyae nyi waa mo kolo wu ɔ nyee ya na, kenaŋ na kaboena nyi waa tisi ɔ nyee. Te akpaa desɛŋ dana alo ŋon nyi waa tisi ɔ nyee yaa ɔ be tale gbee ɔ nyee ya nɛ, kenaŋ na kaboena nyi waa mo kolo wu ɔ nyee.

english_verse: Men were created to be like God and to bring honor to God. This means a man should not wear anything on his head. Women were created to bring honor to men.
translation_verse: Tekaboena nyi baale waa mo kolo wu ɔ nyee ya, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ baale bo ŋon gbagba dinɔɔ man na waa mo lese ŋon Wurubuarɛ gyoorobiiri wola. Mɔna alo dɔɔ ɔ na lese baale gyoorobiiri bo wola.

english_verse: It was the woman who was made from a man, and not the man who was made from a woman.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi na baale te Wurubuarɛ lesewɔ lee alo man ya, mɔna alo te ɔ lesewɔ lee baale man.

english_verse: He wasn't created for her. She was created for him.
translation_verse: Na alo dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ baale ya, mɔna baale dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ alo.

english_verse: And so, because of this, and also because of the angels, a woman ought to wear something on her head, as a sign of her authority.
translation_verse: Nombia kɛŋa na Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ mɔ dɔɔ nɛ, kaboena nyi alo waa mo kolo wu ɔ nyee na ka wola nyi ɔ doe ɔ baale tɛɛ.

english_verse: It is true that the first woman came from a man, but all other men have been given birth by women. Yet God is the one who created everything.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi ŋgba mena keŋ alo ba lee baale man nɛ, mena mɔ te alo na lolana baale. Te kpene kamasɛ mɔ na lee bo Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ.

english_verse: Ask yourselves if it is proper for a woman to pray without something on her head.
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ gyoo nombia kɛŋa man kɛɛ. Dei nyi alo waa fane fa Wurubuarɛ keŋ nyi ɔ na mo kolo wu ɔ nyee yee?

english_verse: But long hair is a beautiful way for a woman to cover her head.
translation_verse: Mɔna akpaa alo dana nyeenyɔɔse burum na, ne yeli ɔ ne yɛɛ kyeo. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ faa e nyeenyɔɔse dɔɔloŋ bo nyi ŋe dee wu ɔ nyee.

english_verse: This is how things are done in all of God's churches, and this is why none of you should argue about what I have said. Rules for the Lord's Supper
translation_verse: Akpaa ŋolo ne gyae waa lɛɛ nombia kɛŋa aŋmaareŋ na, daa na Kristotena akaŋ baŋ pou nombia ayɛɛsa bɛɛ wolo nyi dɛɛ lɛɛ nombia kɛŋa aŋmaareŋ ya. De Gbeŋgyoo weenɛɛ ŋan ɔ kpa dee di na dɛɛ mo tɔɔse e nɛ

english_verse: Your worship services do you more harm than good. I am certainly not going to praise you for this.
translation_verse: Nɛnɛɛ lee nombia ŋan mɛɛ gyae maa kolosi fa ŋon debokeiman nɛ dɔɔ, mɔɔ ne kɛlɛɛ ŋon ya. Nawolo nyi ɛ gyaŋee ɛ ka som na, bɛɛ mo kolo kpaakpaa kɔŋ ya. Na bese wɔlɛɛ abɔɔ bo kpu.

english_verse: I am told you can't get along with each other when you worship, and I am sure that some of what I have heard is true.
translation_verse: Nombii gyaŋgbate yɛna nyi, mee nyii nyi ɛmɛɛ Kristotena dikpii keŋ de gyaŋee na ɛ ne kpase kpookpoo, te mɛɛ lɛɛ nombia kɛŋa ŋaale di.

english_verse: You are bound to argue with each other, but it is easy to see which of you have God's approval.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi kaboena nyi kekpase kpookpoo kei dɛɛ kɔŋ ɛ man, na ke nyiŋ lese baŋ be gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ wola.

english_verse: You even start eating before everyone gets to the meeting, and some of you go hungry, while others get drunk.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi ɛ gyaŋee ɛ ke di nɛ, walaŋ kamasɛ ne di ɔ wui weenɛɛ bo keŋ ɛ bɛɛ kyaa daa dɔŋa ya. Tanam na ko baale, te baale mɔ ne dii baa dɛɛse.

english_verse: The Lord meant that when you eat this bread and drink from this cup, you tell about his death until he comes.
translation_verse: Kei nawolo nyi debaŋ kamasɛ keŋ ɛ ke di bodobodo kei na ɛ ke nyɔɔ lee leenɔɔ kei man na, ɛ ne di de Gbeŋgyoo yeŋ keŋ adansɛɛ baa kelii wee keŋ waa bese kɔŋ.

english_verse: If you fail to understand that you are the body of the Lord, you will condemn yourselves by the way you eat and drink.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ dii te ɔ nyɔɔ, te ɔ te lɛɛ do nyi de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu wosenaane na ɔ fatabo kenaŋ ya nɛ, na ɔtenate falaa ɔ wose baa.

english_verse: After the meal, Jesus took a cup of wine in his hands and said, “This is my blood, and with it God makes his new agreement with you. Drink this and remember me.”
translation_verse: Mena mɔ te be ke di weenɛɛ ŋan taŋ nɛ, ɔ moo leenɔɔ na soloŋ keŋ te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Kei yɛna me fatabo keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo yɛɛ nɔɔkebakesee wɔle. Ɛ kyaa yɛɛ kei debaŋ kamasɛ keŋ ɛ ke nyɔɔ ke nɛ, na ɛ ka mo tɔɔse maŋ.”

english_verse: I have already told you what the Lord Jesus did on the night he was betrayed. And it came from the Lord himself. He took some bread in his hands.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi kpene keŋ mɔɔ kasee lee de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu gyaŋ nɛ, keŋ yaa maŋ mɔ moowɔ mɛɛ wola ŋon nɛ. Nelim keŋ baa lese de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu fa nɛ ɔ moo bodobodo,

english_verse: Then after he had given thanks, he broke it and said, “This is my body, which is given for you. Eat this and remember me.”
translation_verse: te ɔ kefane fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ taŋ nɛ ɔ kɛrɛɛ ke te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Kei yɛna ma wosenaane keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo fa ŋon nɛ. Ɛ kyaa yɛɛ kei na ɛ ka mo tɔɔse maŋ.”

english_verse: But if you eat the bread and drink the wine in a way that isn't worthy of the Lord, you sin against his body and blood.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ta mo gbɛɛ kpaakpaa di bodobodo kei, na waa nyɔɔ lee de Gbeŋgyoo leenɔɔ kei man ya nɛ, na ɔtenate te yɛɛ dukum baa. Nawolo nyi ɔ te bu de Gbeŋgyoo wosenaane na ɔ fatabo keŋ ya.

english_verse: This is why many of you are sick and weak and why a lot of others have died.
translation_verse: Lee mena dɔɔ te ɛ man balaŋ burum baale wose yɔkɔsewɔ, te baale mɔ gyoo kaweese te baale mɔ yekeewɔ nɛ.

english_verse: But when the Lord judges and punishes us, he does it to keep us from being condemned with the rest of the world.
translation_verse: Akpaa de Gbeŋgyoo de bu daa fɔɔ na ɔ ne wɔŋ da deŋele bo, na bɔɔ nyiŋ wɔlɛɛ daa kpu na tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ akaŋ baŋ ya.

english_verse: My dear friends, you should wait until everyone gets there before you start eating.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ me tebia, akpaa ɛ kɔŋ kaa gyaŋee ɛ ke di na ɛ kyaa daa dɔŋa.

english_verse: If you really are hungry, you can eat at home. Then you won't condemn yourselves when you meet together. After I arrive, I will instruct you about the other matters.
translation_verse: Mɔna akpaa nyi tanam na ko ŋolo na waa di lee ɔ dɛɛ pɛna waa kɔŋ, na ɛ kɔŋ kaa gyaŋee na bɔɔ nyiŋ bu ŋolo fɔɔ ya. Nyi akpaa me kɔŋ na, maa kaa wola ŋon nombia akaŋ ŋan man kpu.

english_verse: I want you to remember that before you became followers of the Lord, you were led in all the wrong ways by idols that cannot even talk.
translation_verse: Ɛ gyeŋ nyi debaŋ keŋ ɛ doo wiase man keŋ ɛ te lɛɛ ta Wurubuarɛ di ya nɛ, ɛ yela ba beo ŋon gyu bɛɛneŋ gyaŋ. Bɛɛneŋ kɛwɔ mɔ bɛɛ fa nyeedoŋ ya.

english_verse: Now I want you to know that if you are led by God's Spirit, you will say that Jesus is Lord, and you will never curse Jesus.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ gyae ɛ ka gyeŋ nyi walaŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ feliŋ doo ɔ man nɛ, ɔ be gyae waa tale yako mmusuo nombia gyakaa Yesu dɔɔ ya. Te ŋolo mɔ be kyaa waa tale yako nyi Yesu yɛna ɔ Gbeŋgyoo, keŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ feliŋ be doo ɔtenate man ya.

english_verse: There are different kinds of spiritual gifts, but they all come from the same Spirit.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon abɔɔ anefaŋ kyaa kpookpoo, mɔna ŋe popou lee Wurubuarɛ feliŋ dokoloŋ ŋonaŋ gyaŋ.

english_verse: There are different ways to serve the same Lord,
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ ɔsom tomse kpookpoo kyaa, mɔna de Gbeŋgyoo dokoloŋ ŋonaŋ yaa dɛɛ yɛɛ fa.

english_verse: and we can each do different things. Yet the same God works in all of us and helps us in everything we do.
translation_verse: Tomse kpookpoo mɔ kyaa, mɔna Wurubuarɛ dokoloŋ ŋonaŋ ne faa na ɔ balaŋ doŋ te bɛɛ mo yɛɛ ŋe popou.

english_verse: Some of us can speak with wisdom, while others can speak with knowledge, but these gifts come from the same Spirit.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋonaŋ ne fa ŋolo nyansa keŋ waa mo kolosi nombia, te ɔ ne fa ŋolo mɔ nombia kagyeŋ.

english_verse: To others the Spirit has given great faith or the power to heal the sick
translation_verse: Mena Wurubuarɛ feliŋ dokoloŋ ŋonaŋ ne yele la ŋolo Wurubuarɛ kelɛɛdi ne yɛɛ doŋ, te ɔ ne fa ŋolo mɔ doŋ keŋ waa mo kyɔ kaweese.

english_verse: or the power to work mighty miracles. Some of us are prophets, and some of us recognize when God's Spirit is present. Others can speak different kinds of languages, and still others can tell what these languages mean.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋonaŋ ne faa na ŋolo doŋ keŋ waa mo yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia, te ɔ ne fa ŋolo mɔ doŋ keŋ waa mo kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia. Ŋon ne yele la ŋolo ne tale gyeŋ nyi nombii daale lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ yaa ke ta lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ ya. Ŋon ne faa na ŋolo deise kpookpoo kekolosi, te ŋon ke ne faa na ŋolo mɔ deise kpookpoo man kalese.

english_verse: But it is the Spirit who does all this and decides which gifts to give to each of us. One Body with Many Parts
translation_verse: Mena abɔɔ kɛŋa pou yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ dokoloŋ ŋonaŋ tom ke, te ɔ ne fa walaŋ kamasɛ lee ŋon feliŋ ŋonaŋ gbagba kegyaebii man.

english_verse: Some of us are Jews, and others are Gentiles. Some of us are slaves, and others are free. But God's Spirit baptized each of us and made us part of the body of Christ. Now we each drink from that same Spirit.
translation_verse: Daa Kristotena pou na Gyudatena na Girikitena na gbeŋa na baŋ be kyaa ba wose dɔɔ nɛ pou, Wurubuarɛ feliŋ dokoloŋ ŋonaŋ te ba moowɔ sɔ de popou Wurubuarɛ loŋ. Ŋon moona daa bom te de popou bese Kristo wosenaane dokoloŋ keŋ. Keŋte de nyiŋa feliŋ dokoloŋ ŋonaŋ, te ɔ ne yɛɛ tom de popou man.

english_verse: Our bodies don't have just one part. They have many parts.
translation_verse: Na yenaŋ dokoloŋ ne yɛɛ na deniwalaŋ wose keŋ pou ya, mɔna yenaase kpookpoo ŋan pou bom la yɛɛ wosenaane dokoloŋ.

english_verse: Suppose a foot says, “I'm not a hand, and so I'm not part of the body.” Wouldn't the foot still belong to the body?
translation_verse: Akpaa nae de yako nyi, “Ma te yɛɛ nyiŋmaa ya mena dɔɔ mɔɔ kpuɛ wosenaane keŋ man ya na,” kei gyae ke tale lese nae keŋ lee wosenaane kenaŋ man?

english_verse: Or suppose an ear says, “I'm not an eye, and so I'm not part of the body.” Wouldn't the ear still belong to the body?
translation_verse: Te akpaa deŋele de yako nyi, “Na maŋ yɛna sibii ya mena dɔɔ mɔɔ kpuɛ wosenaane keŋ man ya na,” keŋ nawolo baa nyi kɔɔ kpuɛ wosenaane keŋ man yee? Kei gyae ke tale lese deŋele kenaŋ lee wosenaane man?

english_verse: If our bodies were only an eye, we couldn't hear a thing. And if they were only an ear, we couldn't smell a thing.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa wosenaane keŋ pou deŋ yɛɛ sibii ke na, sena te wosenaane keŋ gyae ke yɛɛ na ke nyii kolo? Te akpaa wosenaane keŋ pou deŋ yɛɛ deŋele ke na, sena te wosenaane keŋ gyae ke yɛɛ na ke wɔŋ nyii?

english_verse: But God has put all parts of our body together in the way that he decided is best.
translation_verse: Mɔ n kɛɛ na, Wurubuarɛ moo wosenaane kpookpoo ŋan pou bo bom yɛɛ wosenaane dokoloŋ, lee ŋon gbagba kegyaebii man.

english_verse: A body isn't really a body, unless there is more than one part.
translation_verse: Nyi wosenaane yenaase kpookpoo ŋan pou deŋ yɛɛ wosenaane yenaŋ dokoloŋ na, nafɔ ke gyae ke yɛɛ gyakoloŋ.

english_verse: It takes many parts to make a single body.
translation_verse: Mɔna ŋgba mena keŋ ke doo nɛ, wosenaane yenaase ŋan pou boe mɔna wosenaane keŋ pou yɛɛ dokoloŋ.

english_verse: That's why the eyes cannot say they don't need the hands. That's also why the head cannot say it doesn't need the feet.
translation_verse: Sibii be gyae ke tale yako nyiŋmaa nyi, “N be dana tɔnɔɔ fa maŋ ya,” te nyee mɔ be gyae ke tale yako nae nyi, “N be dana tɔnɔɔ fa maŋ ya.”

english_verse: In fact, we cannot get along without the parts of the body that seem to be the weakest.
translation_verse: Mɔna wosenaane yenaase ŋan dɛɛ kɛɛ nyi ŋe taŋ doŋ ya nɛ, nyi ŋɔɔ doo wosenaane man ya nɛ nafɔ wosenaane be gyae ke tale yɛɛ kolo ya.

english_verse: but we don't have to be modest about other parts. God put our bodies together in such a way that even the parts that seem the least important are valuable.
translation_verse: Da wosenaane yenaase ŋan dɛɛ kɛɛ nyi ŋe dana kyeo nɛ, be doo nyi dɛɛ kɛɛ ŋe dɔɔ mena ya. Mɔna Wurubuarɛ ta mo wosenaane yenaase ŋan pou towaa, te ɔ moo obuo fa ŋan nafɔ ŋɔɔ taŋ obuo ya nɛ.

english_verse: He did this to make all parts of the body work together smoothly, with each part caring about the others.
translation_verse: Ɔ yɛɛ ke mena na kekpase be nyiŋ doo wosenaane yenaase kpookpoo ŋan man ya, mɔna wosenaane yenaŋ kamasɛ dɛɛ kɛɛ ke dɔɔ dɔɔ nideli.

english_verse: If one part of our body hurts, we hurt all over. If one part of our body is honored, the whole body will be happy.
translation_verse: Akpaa de kɔŋ nyi wosenaane yenaŋ daale ne naa diyem na, akaŋ ŋan pou mɔ ne naa diyem kpu na ke. Mɔna akpaa de kɔŋ nyi wosenaane yenaŋ daale de nyiŋ yele na, ne yɛɛ yenaase akaŋ ŋan mɔ pou gyoŋ.

english_verse: Together you are the body of Christ. Each one of you is part of his body.
translation_verse: Nɛnɛɛ ɛ popou bom la yɛɛ Kristo wosenaane keŋ, te ɛ man walaŋ kamasɛ yɛɛ ɔ yenaŋ daale ke.

english_verse: Not everyone is an apostle. Not everyone is a prophet. Not everyone is a teacher. Not everyone can work miracles.
translation_verse: N kɛɛ na, na be popou yɛna Kristo kpilala ya. Na be popou yɛna Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra ya. Na be popou yɛna wolala ya. Na be popou ne yɛɛ na gyakoloŋ nombia ya.

english_verse: Not everyone can heal the sick. Not everyone can speak different kinds of languages. Not everyone can tell what these languages mean.
translation_verse: Na be popou ne kyɔɔna kaweese ya. Na be popou ne kolosina deise kpookpoo ya. Na be popou mɔ na lesena deise kpookpoo man ya.

english_verse: I want you to desire the best gifts. So I will show you a much better way.
translation_verse: Mɔna ɛ yela ɛ sia keyase Wurubuarɛ abɔɔ anefaŋ dinaana ŋan ŋa kela kɛŋa nɛ dɔɔ. Nɛnɛɛ maa wola ŋon nombia kpaakpaa ŋan ŋa kela kɛŋa nɛ pou.

english_verse: What if I could speak all languages of humans and even of angels? If I did not love others, I would be nothing more than a noisy gong or a clanging cymbal.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa mɛɛ tale kolosi deise wɔlɛɛ kpookpoo yaa Wurubuarɛ kpilala dei, te balaŋ kegyae be doo me man ya na, kenaŋ na ma kekolosi pou yɛɛ ŋgba dowuro yaa akpaakpaase ŋan ŋɛɛ yɛɛ yeesa yakaa bo nɛ.

english_verse: What if I could prophesy and understand all mysteries and all knowledge? And what if I had faith that moved mountains? I would be nothing, unless I loved others.
translation_verse: Akpaa me yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre e, te maa tale naa Wurubuarɛ nombia aweeseŋ ŋan na maa yako, te ma gyeŋ nombia pou te me dana kelɛɛdi kpaakpaate keŋ maa tale mo kulii bulase, te balaŋ kegyae be doo me man ya na me yɛɛ yakaa.

english_verse: What if I gave away all that I owned and let myself be burned alive? I would gain nothing, unless I loved others.
translation_verse: Mena mɔ te akpaa ma mo abɔɔ ŋan pou me dana nɛ fa ayematena, te ma mo ma wosenaane fa nyi baa too, te balaŋ kegyae be doo me man ya na ŋan pou mɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ yɛɛ yakaa.

english_verse: Love never fails! Everyone who prophesies will stop, and unknown languages will no longer be spoken. All that we know will be forgotten.
translation_verse: Nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra ne kolosi see, na deise kpookpoo kekolosi na nombia kagyeŋ pou gyae ke gyu ɔto. Mɔna balaŋ kegyae dɔɔ kɔɔ taŋ ɔto ya.

english_verse: But what is perfect will someday appear, and what isn't perfect will then disappear.
translation_verse: Mɔna akpaa nyi kpene keŋ ke yɛɛ debiliŋ nɛ de kɔŋ na, ŋan ŋe yɛɛ yaale yaale nɛ gyae ke ŋaalaa.

english_verse: For now there are faith, hope, and love. But of these three, the greatest is love.
translation_verse: Abɔɔ kɛŋa pou man nɛ nombia atooro kyaa la keŋ ŋɔɔ taŋ ɔto ya. Ŋan yɛna kelɛɛdi na lookegyakaa na kegyae. Mɔna ŋe popou man na, kegyae yɛna ŋa nyee.

english_verse: Love should be your guide. Be eager to have the gifts that come from the Holy Spirit, especially the gift of prophecy.
translation_verse: Ɛ yɛɛ kakyeŋ na ɛ ke gyae dɔŋa, na ɛ ke yase ɛ sia gyae Wurubuarɛ feliŋ abɔɔ anefaŋ ŋan gbɛɛ. Tuturu na, ɛ yase ɛ sia gyae keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa ta ɛ dɔɔ kolosi nɛ gbɛɛ.

english_verse: If you speak languages that others don't know, God will understand what you are saying, though no one else will know what you mean. You will be talking about mysteries that only the Spirit understands.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne kolosi deise wɔlɛɛ kpookpoo nɛ, ɔ bɛɛ kolosi fa denibalaŋ ya mɔna ɔ ne kolosi bo fa Wurubuarɛ. N kɛɛ na ŋolo bee nyii ɔ nombia ŋan man ya, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ne tɛɛ la ɔ dɔɔ kolosi nombia aweeseŋ ŋenaŋ.

english_verse: But when you prophesy, you will be understood, and others will be helped. They will be encouraged and made to feel better.
translation_verse: Mɔna walaŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ ne tɛɛ ɔ dɔɔ kolosi nɛ, ɔ nombia ŋan na do balaŋ doŋ na kakyeŋ te ne yeli bɛɛ tale kyaŋ ba wose.

english_verse: I am glad for you to speak unknown languages, although I prefer that you would prophesy. In fact, prophesying does much more good than speaking unknown languages, unless someone can help the church by explaining what you mean.
translation_verse: Nafɔ ma kegyae yɛna nyi ɛ popou yaa kolosi deise wɔlɛɛ kpookpoo, mɔna keŋ mɛɛ gyae kela yɛna nyi, Wurubuarɛ ke ta ɛ dɔɔ kolosi. Nawolo nyi walaŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ ne tɛɛ ɔ dɔɔ kolosi na, ɔ kela walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne kolosi deise kpookpoo. Gyisɛ ŋolo kyaa botɔɔ keŋ ɔ ne tale lese deise wɔlɛɛ kpookpoo ŋan man, na kekyɔ Kristotena dikpii keŋ pou, na baa bee bɔ ɔsom keŋ man.

english_verse: My friends, what good would it do, if I came and spoke unknown languages to you and didn't explain what I meant? How would I help you, unless I told you what God had shown me or gave you some knowledge or prophecy or teaching?
translation_verse: Me tebia, nyi akpaa me kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ te me kolosi deise wɔlɛɛ kpookpoo na, woŋ tɔnɔɔ yaa ɛ ke nyiŋ lee ŋe man? Mɔna akpaa me kaa yako ŋon nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ ba lese wola man, yaa nombia kagyeŋ wose man nombia, yaa nombia ŋan Wurubuarɛ ba mo fa maŋ nyi me kaa yako ŋon, yaa me kaa wola ŋon nombia na, na kɛŋa be dana tɔnɔɔ fa ŋon yee?

english_verse: If all musical instruments sounded alike, how would you know the difference between a flute and a harp?
translation_verse: Mena mɔ te abɔɔ ŋan nyeedoŋ be doo ŋe man ya ŋgba kefereese na kaberɛse nɛ, akpaa be ta wola ŋa nideli ya na amɔte ne gyae la waa nyii ŋe man?

english_verse: If a bugle call isn't clear, how would you know to get ready for battle?
translation_verse: Te akpaa yoonɔɔte ŋon ta wola ɔ kaberɛ nideli ya na, sena te ŋolo waa nyii ke man na waa koro desina ɔ wose fa yoo?

english_verse: This is how it is when you speak unknown languages. If no one can understand what you are talking about, you will only be talking to the wind.
translation_verse: Mena te doo fa ɛmɛɛ mɔ. Akpaa ŋolo de kolosi dei wɔle kpoo daale, te ŋolo te nyii ke man ya na sena te ŋolo waa yɛɛ na waa tale nyii nombia ŋan ɔ ne yako nɛ man? Dekɔŋ mena na, ɔ ne kolosi bo do feliŋ man yakaa.

english_verse: But if I don't understand the language that someone is using, we will be like foreigners to each other.
translation_verse: Akpaa nyi mɔɔ ne nyii dei keŋ ŋolo ne kolosi ya na me yɛɛ ŋɔɔle e ɔ siaman, te mena mɔ te ŋon mɔ yɛɛ ŋɔɔle e me siaman.

english_verse: When we speak languages that others don't know, we should pray for the power to explain what we mean.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ akpaa nyi ŋolo ne kolosi dei wɔle kpoo daale na, kaboena nyi waa fane na waa nyiŋ tale lese ke man.

english_verse: For example, if I use an unknown language in my prayers, my spirit prays but my mind is useless.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi akpaa me fane dei kpoo man na, feliŋ ŋon ɔ doo me man nɛ ne fane la, mɔna ma agyueŋ be doo botɔɔ ya.

english_verse: Then what should I do? There are times when I should pray with my spirit, and times when I should pray with my mind. Sometimes I should sing with my spirit, and at other times I should sing with my mind.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ sena yaa maa yɛɛ? Maa mo feliŋ ŋon ɔ doo me man nɛ fane, na maa mo ma agyueŋ mɔ fane. Maa mo feliŋ ŋon ɔ doo me man nɛ kpee nɔɔneŋ, na maa mo ma agyueŋ mɔ kpee nɔɔneŋ.

english_verse: Suppose some strangers are in your worship service, when you are praising God with your spirit. If they don't understand you, how will they know to say, “Amen”?
translation_verse: Nyi ɛ gyaŋee ɛ ne fane te n mo dei daale kpoo n na lese Wurubuarɛ yele, te walaŋ yakaa ŋolo kyaa ɛ man keŋ ɔ bee nyii n dei keŋ man ya na, sena te waa tale sɛɛ neŋ nyi, “Dɛɛkɔŋ Mena,” keŋ nyi ɔ te nyii kpene keŋ n be yako nɛ man ya?

english_verse: You may be worshiping God in a wonderful way, but no one else will be helped.
translation_verse: Mberem na n fanewɔ nideli lese Wurubuarɛ yele, mɔna ke taŋ tɔnɔɔ fa n balate ŋon ya.

english_verse: I thank God that I speak unknown languages more than any of you.
translation_verse: Mɛɛ fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ ŋgba mɛɛ tale kolosi deise kpookpoo kela ɛ man walaŋ kamasɛ nɛ.

english_verse: But words that make sense can help the church. This is why in church I would rather speak five words that make sense than to speak
translation_verse: Mɔna akpaa me kpuɛ Kristotena dikpii keŋ man na, mɛɛ gyae nyi maa kolosi nyansa nombia kyomii bo mo wola balaŋ nombia, kela keŋ nyi maa kolosi nombia burum deise kpookpoo man keŋ bɔɔ gyae baa nyii ŋe man ya.

english_verse: In the Scriptures the Lord says, “I will use strangers who speak unknown languages to talk to my people. They will speak to them in foreign languages, but still my people won't listen to me.”
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ kpa, “Maa ta balaŋ baŋ bee kolosi deise kpookpoo nɛ dɔɔ kolosi fa me balaŋ. Maa ta ŋɔɔla dɔɔ kolosi fa me balaŋ. Mɔna kɛŋa wɔle koraŋ na, bɔɔ gyae baa nyii me dei ya.”

english_verse: Suppose everyone in your worship service started speaking unknown languages, and some outsiders or some unbelievers come in. Won't they think you are crazy?
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ akpaa Kristotena dikpii keŋ de gyaŋee te walaŋ kamasɛ ne kolosi deise wɔlɛɛ kpookpoo, te akpaa balaŋ wɔlɛɛ yaa baŋ ba te yɛɛ Kristotenawɔ ya nɛ de kɔŋ botɔɔ na, bɔɔ gyae baa yako nyi ba nyeeya ne wɔlɛɛ baa yee?

english_verse: But suppose all of you are prophesying when those unbelievers and outsiders come in. They will realize that they are sinners, and they will want to change their ways because of what you are saying.
translation_verse: Mɔna nyi akpaa Wurubuarɛ de tɛɛ ɛ popou dɔɔ ɔ ne kolosi, te walaŋ yakaate yaa ŋon ɔ te yɛɛ Kristo walaŋ e ya nɛ de kaa gyoo ɛ man na, nombia ŋan ɔ ne nyii nɛ gyae ka lese ɔ dukum keyɛɛ debɔɔ, na ke yela waa gyeŋ nyi ɔ yɛɛ dukumyɛɛre e.

english_verse: They will tell what is hidden in their hearts. Then they will kneel down and say to God, “We are certain that you are with these people.” Worship Must Be Orderly
translation_verse: Nombia ŋenaŋ gyae ka lese ɔ konɔɔ man nombia ŋan pou weesɛɛ nɛ debɔɔ, na waa buŋ som Wurubuarɛ na waa yako nyiaa, “Ampaŋ Wurubuarɛ kpuɛ ɛ man.” Kristotena kegyaŋee mmaraase

english_verse: My friends, when you meet to worship, you must do everything for the good of everyone there. That's how it should be when someone sings or teaches or tells what God has said or speaks an unknown language or explains what the language means.
translation_verse: Me tebia, nombia ŋan mɛɛ yako nɛ mɛɛ wolo baa nyi akpaa ɛ kɔŋ kaa gyaŋee nɛ, mberem na ŋolo dana nɔɔneŋ keŋ waa kpee na ŋolo mɔ dana kolo waa wola, yaa ŋolo mɔ dana nombii keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba lese wola e. Mberem na ŋolo mɔ ne gyae waa mo deise wɔlɛɛ kpookpoo kolosi, yaa ŋolo mɔ kyaa keŋ waa lese dei wɔle kei man nɛ, kaboena nyi yaa yɛɛ kɛŋa pou na ɛ ka mo do Kristotena baŋ kakyeŋ.

english_verse: No more than two or three of you should speak unknown languages during the meeting. You must take turns, and someone should always be there to explain what you mean.
translation_verse: Akpaa ɛ man baale baa kolosi deise wɔlɛɛ kpookpoo na, baa yɛɛ balaŋ bala te ba boo paa na baa yɛɛ balaŋ batooro. Mɔna kaboena nyi baa kolosi dokoloŋ dokoloŋ, na ŋolo mɔ ka lese ŋe man.

english_verse: If no one can explain, you must keep silent in church and speak only to yourself and to God.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa ŋolo be kyaa keŋ waa lese nombia ŋan man ya nɛ, kenaŋ na ŋon ɔ ne kolosi ŋa nɛ waa kyaŋ ɔ nɔɔ wu Kristotena dikpii keŋ man, na waa kolosi fa Wurubuarɛ na ŋon gbagba.

english_verse: Two or three persons may prophesy, and everyone else must listen carefully.
translation_verse: Ɛ yela Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra bala yaa batooro kekolosi, na balaŋ akaŋ baŋ ka gyoo be nombia ŋan bee kolosi nɛ man nideli.

english_verse: If someone sitting there receives a message from God, the speaker must stop and let the other person speak.
translation_verse: Te akpaa ŋolo kyaa te Wurubuarɛ da lese nombia ŋaale wola e nɛ, ɛ yela walaŋ ŋon ɔ be taŋgbɛɛ ɔ ne kolosi nɛ ke tina kekolosi yela.

english_verse: Let only one person speak at a time, then all of you will learn something and be encouraged.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi ɛ yɛɛ mena na Wurubuarɛ waa tale ta ɛ dɔɔ dokoloŋ dokoloŋ kolosi, na ke yela ɛ popou ke kasee kolo lee ke man na ka do ŋon kakyeŋ.

english_verse: A prophet should be willing to stop and let someone else speak.
translation_verse: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon Wurubuarɛ ne tɛɛ ɔ dɔɔ kolosi nɛ, kaboena nyi waa tale kyaŋ ɔ wose lee ɔ kekolosi keŋ man.

english_verse: God wants everything to be done peacefully and in order. When God's people meet in church,
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ te yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne yɛɛ abɔɔ basabasa ya, mɔna ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ ne yɛɛ wa abɔɔ na ŋe gbɛɛ.

english_verse: If there is something they want to know, they can ask their husbands when they get home. It is disgraceful for women to speak in church.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa bɛɛ gyae baa nyii nombii daale man nideli na, kaboena nyi baa bɔɔse be baala deni man. Nawolo nyi ke yɛɛ desɛŋ ke nyi alo ke yɛɛ nɔɔdoŋ Kristotena dɛɛgyaŋee man.

english_verse: God's message did not start with you people, and you are not the only ones it has reached.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛ na wolo baa nyi ɛ gyaŋ te Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan leewɔ kɔŋ, yaa ɛmɛɛ yaageŋ nyii na ŋa?

english_verse: If you think of yourself as a prophet or a spiritual person, you will know I am writing only what the Lord has commanded.
translation_verse: Akpaa ŋolo ne baake ɔ wose nyi ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre e, yaa ɔ ne kɛɛ ɔ wose nyi ɔ dana Wurubuarɛ feliŋ abɔɔ anefaŋ ŋan na, kaboena waa gyeŋ nyi me nombia ŋan mee kolosi nɛ pou lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ.

english_verse: So don't pay attention to anyone who ignores what I am writing.
translation_verse: Te akpaa nyi ŋolo te lɛɛ me nombia kɛŋa di ya nɛ, ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ na mo e yɛɛ kolo ya.

english_verse: My friends, be eager to prophesy and don't stop anyone from speaking languages that others don't know.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ me tebia, ɛ yase ɛ sia na Wurubuarɛ ke ta ɛ dɔɔ kolosi, mɔ ɛ na tɔ baŋ bee kolosi deise wɔlɛɛ kpookpoo nɛ gbɛɛ ya.

english_verse: My friends, I want you to remember the message I preached and that you believed and trusted.
translation_verse: Me tebia, nɛnɛɛ mɛɛ gyae maa tɔɔse ŋon nyeedoŋ nombia ŋan mɔɔ yako ŋon te ɛ be lɛɛ di te ɛ seŋɛɛ ŋe man keŋkeŋ nɛ.

english_verse: You will be saved by this message, if you hold firmly to it. But if you don't, your faith was all for nothing.
translation_verse: Akpaa ɛ kyaŋ nombia ŋan mɔɔ yako ŋon nɛ man keŋkeŋ na, Wurubuarɛ waa ta ŋe dɔɔ lɛɛ ɛ nyee. Mɔna akpaa ɛ te kyaŋ ŋa keŋkeŋ ya na ɛ kelɛɛdi keŋ yɛɛ yakaa.

english_verse: I told you the most important part of the message exactly as it was told to me. This part is: Christ died for our sins, as the Scriptures say.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi kpene keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu ba wola maŋ te ke dana tɔnɔɔ kela kpene kamasɛ nɛ, keŋ yaa ma moowɔ mɛɛ wola ŋon nɛ. Nombia ŋenaŋ yɛna nyi, De nombiakumɛɛ dɔɔ te Kristo kaa yeŋawɔ, ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako nɛ.

english_verse: He was buried, and three days later he was raised to life, as the Scriptures say.
translation_verse: Be wuu e te weeya atoorote keŋ na Wurubuarɛ gyuusu e lee yeŋ man, ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako nɛ.

english_verse: After this, he appeared to more than five hundred other followers. Most of them are still alive, but some have died.
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ ɔ besewɔ lese ɔ wose wola ɔ kaseela baŋ be yaanɔɔle kela balaŋ sɔŋonoŋ akpeŋ anoŋ (

english_verse: He also appeared to James, then to all of the apostles.
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ lese ɔ wose wola Gyemisi, te ɔ besewɔ lese ɔ wose wola kpilala baŋ pou.

english_verse: Finally, he appeared to me, even though I am like someone who was born at the wrong time.
translation_verse: Wɔle wɔle nɛ, ɔ kaa lese ɔ wose wola maŋ Pɔɔl ŋon nafɔ ŋolo te gyakaa loo nyi maa kaa bese Kristo walaŋ ya nɛ.

english_verse: I am the least important of all the apostles. In fact, I caused so much trouble for God's church that I don't even deserve to be called an apostle.
translation_verse: Maŋ yɛna Kristo kpilala baŋ pou man kemaasa, te ma tekaboena gba nyi nafɔ baa baake maŋ nyi Kristo kpilale ya. Nawolo nyi me naase Kristotena baŋ diyem nideli.

english_verse: But God treated me with undeserved grace! He made me what I am, and his grace wasn't wasted. I worked much harder than any of the other apostles, although it was really God's grace at work and not me.
translation_verse: Mɔna lee Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ dɔɔ te gyɛŋ me kaa bese walaŋ nɛ. Ɔ bɛrɛɛ keŋ ɔ ba mo boele maŋ nɛ te yɛɛ yakaa ya, nawolo nyi me yɛɛ tom kela Kristo kpilala akaŋ baŋ pou. Mɔna na maŋ gbagba doŋ te ma moowɔ yɛɛ ya, Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ keŋ ɔ ba mo boele maŋ nɛ yɛɛ la.

english_verse: But it doesn't matter if I preached or if they preached. All of you believed the message just the same. God's People Will Be Raised to Life
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Kristo nombia kɛŋa lee me gyaŋ o ŋa lee be gyaŋ bo o, ŋan te de popou ne kolosi te ŋan mɔ te ɛ lɛɛwɔ di. Kefoŋ lee yeŋ man

english_verse: If we preach that Christ was raised from death, how can some of you say the dead will not be raised to life?
translation_verse: Mɔna akpaa dee kolosi nyeekelɛɛ nombia ŋan nyi Kristo yeŋawɔ te Wurubuarɛ gyuusu e lee yeŋ man na, woŋ dɔɔ te ɛmɛɛ baale ne yako nyi kefoŋ lee yeŋ man be kyaa ya?

english_verse: If they won't be raised to life, Christ himself wasn't raised to life.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa kefoŋ lee yeŋ man be kyaa ya na, kenaŋ na nawolo baa nyi Wurubuarɛ te gyuusu Kristo lee yeŋ man ya.

english_verse: And if Christ wasn't raised to life, our message is worthless, and so is your faith.
translation_verse: Te akpaa Wurubuarɛ te gyuusu Kristo lee yeŋ man ya na, kenaŋ na Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan dee kolosi na ɛ kelɛɛdi keŋ mɔ yɛɛ yakaa.

english_verse: If the dead won't be raised to life, we have told lies about God by saying he raised Christ to life, when he really did not.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa be doo mena ya na kenaŋ na daa Kristo kpilala baŋ te di dekara gyakaa Wurubuarɛ dɔɔ, nawolo nyi dɛɛ yako nyi Wurubuarɛ gyuusu Kristo lee yeŋ man. Nyi akpaa woya be gyae baa foŋ lee yeŋ man ya na, kenaŋ na Wurubuarɛ te gyuusu Kristo lee yeŋ man ya.

english_verse: So if the dead won't be raised to life, Christ wasn't raised to life.
translation_verse: Mɔ akpaa kefoŋ lee yeŋ man be kyaa ya na, kenaŋ na Wurubuarɛ te gyuusu Kristo lee yeŋ man ya.

english_verse: Unless Christ was raised to life, your faith is useless, and you are still living in your sins.
translation_verse: Te akpaa nyi Wurubuarɛ te gyuusu Kristo lee yeŋ man ya nɛ, kenaŋ na ɛ kelɛɛdi keŋ yɛɛ yakaa te ɛ sɛɛ ɛ doo ɛ nombiakumɛɛ man.

english_verse: And those people who died after putting their faith in him are completely lost.
translation_verse: Kenaŋ na Kristotena baŋ mɔ bɔɔ yekee nɛ ta yekee leki.

english_verse: If our hope in Christ is good only for this life, we are worse off than anyone else.
translation_verse: Akpaa nyi tɛɛle kei dɔɔ kaageŋ kekyaa dɔɔ te de loo gyakaa Kristo dɔɔ na, kenaŋ na de nombia dana waraŋase kela walaŋ kamasɛ.

english_verse: But Christ has been raised to life! And he makes us certain that others will also be raised to life.
translation_verse: Mɔna anokoare keŋ doo ke man yɛna nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ gyuusu Kristo lee yeŋ man. Ŋon Kristo yɛna woya baŋ pou Wurubuarɛ waa kaa gyuusu wɔ nɛ be gyaŋgbate.

english_verse: Just as we will die because of Adam, we will be raised to life because of Christ.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi mena keŋ yeŋ be tɛɛ walaŋ dokoloŋ dɔɔ kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, mena mɔ te kefoŋ lee yeŋ man mɔ tɛɛwɔ walaŋ dokoloŋ dɔɔ kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ.

english_verse: But we must each wait our turn. Christ was the first to be raised to life, and his people will be raised to life when he returns.
translation_verse: Mɔna walaŋ kamasɛ dana ɔ debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa gyuusu e lee yeŋ man. Kristo taŋgbɛɛ la foŋ lee yeŋ man, te wee keŋ waa bese kɔŋ na balaŋ baŋ be yɛɛ ɔ wui nɛ mɔ baa foŋ lee yeŋ man.

english_verse: Then after Christ has destroyed all powers and forces, the end will come, and he will give the kingdom to God the Father.
translation_verse: Kei wɔle na kpene kamasɛ gyae ke kɔŋ kɔ ɔto. Debaŋ kenaŋ te Kristo waa di doŋse pou na gyooneŋ na ŋe gyoorobiiri pou dɔɔ, na waa mo gyoori keŋ do ɔ kya Wurubuarɛ nyiŋmaa man.

english_verse: Christ will rule until he puts all his enemies under his power,
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi kaboena nyi Kristo waa di gyoori, kelii debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa mo ɔ kɔlala pou do ɔ nawɔɔ tɛɛ.

english_verse: and the last enemy he destroys will be death.
translation_verse: Te kɔlare ɔtomante ŋon Wurubuarɛ waa wɔlɛɛ e nɛ yɛna yeŋ.

english_verse: After everything is under the power of God's Son, he will put himself under the power of God, who put everything under his Son's power. Then God will mean everything to everyone.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ da mo abɔɔ kɛŋa pou do ɔ bu ŋon tɛɛ taŋ nɛ, ɔ bu ŋon mɔ waa mo ɔ wose do ɔ kya Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ ba mo kpene kamasɛ do ɔ tɛɛ nɛ tɛɛ. Na balaŋ pou kena nyi Wurubuarɛ yɛna kpene kamasɛ nyee.

english_verse: and face death every day? The pride that I have in you because of Christ Jesus our Lord is what makes me say this.
translation_verse: Me tebia, mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi yeŋ nombia ne tuŋ maŋ wee kamasɛ. Lee mena keŋ daa na ŋon na Kristo yɛɛ dokoloŋ dɔɔ, mɛɛ tale mo ŋon kyɔ poaa. Mena dɔɔ te mee kolosi nombii kei.

english_verse: Don't fool yourselves. Bad friends will destroy you.
translation_verse: Ɛ na yeli ŋolo ka beo ŋon ya, “Nawolo nyi dɔɔre kum ne wɔlɛɛ dinɔɔ kpaakpaate.”

english_verse: Be sensible and stop sinning. You should be embarrassed that some people still don't know about God. What Our Bodies Will Be Like
translation_verse: Ɛ bese gyoo ɛ wose man nideli na ɛ ke tina nombiakumɛɛ keyɛɛ yela. Nawolo nyi dana desɛŋ ŋgba ɛ man baale sɛɛ ɛ ba gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia korakora ya nɛ. Kefoŋ lee yeŋ man na ka wosenaane wɔle

english_verse: Some of you have asked, “How will the dead be raised to life? What kind of bodies will they have?”
translation_verse: Mberem na ŋolo waa bɔɔse nyiaa, “Sena te woya baa tale foŋ lee yeŋ man bela, te wosenaane woŋti yaa baa nyiŋ?”

english_verse: Don't be foolish. A seed must die before it can sprout from the ground.
translation_verse: Diyiŋ nombia kebɔɔse yaa kei. Nawolo nyi kpene kamasɛ keŋ n duu tɛɛle man nɛ, kɛɛ daale pɛte kolo wɔle ne tuu lee ke man.

english_verse: Wheat seeds and all other seeds look different from the sprouts that come up.
translation_verse: Akpaa n duu kolo bii nɛ, keŋ n ba mo duu na keŋ nyaŋ ke kɔ nɛ te yɛɛ kyɛɛkyɛɛ ya.

english_verse: This is because God gives everything the kind of body he wants it to have.
translation_verse: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ ne faa na ke ke dinɔɔ, ŋgba mena keŋ ŋon gbagba ne gyae nɛ. Kpene kamasɛ keŋ n duu na, Wurubuarɛ ne yeli kɛɛ bee nyiŋ ke dinɔɔ.

english_verse: People, animals, birds, and fish are each made of flesh, but none of them are alike.
translation_verse: Wosenaane pou te yɛɛ dokoloŋ ya. Denibalaŋ wui kpoo te wonembia wui kpoo te gyebuse wui kpoo te kpebia mɔ wui kpoo.

english_verse: Everything in the heavens has a body, and so does everything on earth. But each one is very different from all the others.
translation_verse: Mena mɔ te abɔɔ ŋan pou Wurubuarɛ be yɛɛ nɛ, ŋan ŋe kyaa adido nɛ ŋa kyeo kpoo te ŋan ŋe kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ mɔ nɛ kyeo kpoo.

english_verse: The sun isn't like the moon, the moon isn't like the stars, and each star is different.
translation_verse: Weese kɛɛ ŋalakee kpoo te gyaalaŋ mɔ wui keŋalakee kpoo te kyolobiise mɔ kɛɛ ŋalakee kpoo. Kyolobii kamasɛ mɔ dana mena keŋ ɔ ne ŋalakee.

english_verse: That's how it will be when our bodies are raised to life. These bodies will die, but the bodies that are raised will live forever.
translation_verse: Mena ke te gyae ke yɛɛ debaŋ keŋ balaŋ baa foŋ lee yeŋ man nɛ. Akpaa be wuu walaŋ na waa dala, mɔna Wurubuarɛ de gyuusu e na waa fa e wosenaane wɔle keŋ be gyae ke dala ya.

english_verse: As surely as there are physical bodies, there are spiritual bodies. And our physical bodies will be changed into spiritual bodies.
translation_verse: Deniwalaŋ wosenaane keŋ bɛɛ mo wuu nɛ, ke yɛɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ wosenaane ke. Mɔna akpaa Wurubuarɛ de gyuusu ke na ke ta bese Wurubuarɛ man wosenaane. Tɛɛle kei dɔɔ wosenaane kyaa, mena dɔɔ kaboena nyi Wurubuarɛ man wosenaane mɔ dɛɛ kyaa.

english_verse: The first man was named Adam, and the Scriptures tell us that he was a living person. But Jesus, who may be called the last Adam, is a life-giving spirit.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see yakowɔ nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ moo ɔ feliŋ do Adam gyaŋgbate ŋon man te ɔ nyiŋa nyeedoŋ. Mɔna Yesu Kristo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Adam balate ŋon nɛ, ŋon yɛna Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ɔ ne fa nyeedoŋ kekpaa.”

english_verse: We see that the one with a spiritual body did not come first. He came after the one who had a physical body.
translation_verse: Na ŋon ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ e nɛ taŋgbɛɛ la kɔŋ ya, mɔna ŋon ɔ yɛɛ deniwalaŋ e nɛ taŋgbɛɛ la kɔŋ. Kenaŋ wɔle na pɛte ŋon ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ e nɛ kɔŋawɔ.

english_verse: The first man was made from the dust of the earth, but the second man came from heaven.
translation_verse: Adam gyaŋgbate ŋon lee tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nawolo nyi tɛɛle te Wurubuarɛ moowɔ yɛɛ e, mɔna Adam balate ŋon dɔɔ ŋon lee Wurubuarɛ man adido.

english_verse: Everyone on earth has a body like the body of the one who was made from the dust of the earth. And everyone in heaven has a body like the body of the one who came from heaven.
translation_verse: Mena keŋ Adam ŋon ɔ ba lee tɛɛle kei dɔɔ doo nɛ, mena mɔ te tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ doo. Te mena keŋ Adam ŋon ɔ ba lee Wurubuarɛ man doo nɛ, mena mɔ te Wurubuarɛ man balaŋ doo.

english_verse: Just as we are like the one who was made out of earth, we will be like the one who came from heaven.
translation_verse: Mena keŋ walaŋ ŋon bɔɔ mo tɛɛle ma e dinɔɔ doo de man nɛ, mena mɔ te walaŋ ŋon ɔ ba lee adido nɛ dinɔɔ gyae ke doo de man.

english_verse: My friends, I want you to know that our bodies of flesh and blood will decay. This means they cannot share in God's kingdom, which lasts forever.
translation_verse: Me tebia, mɛɛ gyae ɛ ka gyeŋ nyi kpene keŋ ke yɛɛ wosenaane na fatabo ke nɛ, be gyae ke tale gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man ya. Mena mɔ te wosenaane kei keŋ gyae ke dala nɛ, be gyae ke kyaa kekpaakekpaa ya.

english_verse: It will happen suddenly, quicker than the blink of an eye. At the sound of the last trumpet the dead will be raised. We will all be changed, so we will never die again.
translation_verse: Ba wola kaberɛ ɔtomante keŋ na Wurubuarɛ waa kyɛɛkee de popou, te baŋ bɔɔ yekee nɛ baa foŋ lee yeŋ man keŋ bɔɔ gyae baa yeŋ bela ya.

english_verse: The bodies we now have are weak and can die. But they will be changed into bodies that are eternal. Then the Scriptures will come true, “Death has lost the battle!
translation_verse: Akpaa wosenaane keŋ kɛɛ dale nɛ da bese wosenaane keŋ ke bɛɛ dale ya, te wosenaane keŋ kɛɛ yem nɛ da bese wosenaane keŋ ke bɛɛ yem ya nɛ, kenaŋ na Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan bɔɔ ŋmarase see nɛ te kɔŋ mena. Nombia ŋenaŋ yɛna nyiaa, “Da te yoo di yeŋ dɔɔ. Yeŋ be taŋ doŋ de dɔɔ bela ya.”

english_verse: Sin is what gives death its sting, and the Law is the power behind sin.
translation_verse: Yeŋ doŋ keŋ ɔ dana ɔ na do balaŋ kayeyɛɛ nɛ yɛna dukum, te mmaraa mɔ ne yele la dukum ne nyiŋ doŋ.

english_verse: But thank God for letting our Lord Jesus Christ give us the victory!
translation_verse: Mɔna dɛɛ do Wurubuarɛ nɔɔ, nawolo nyi ŋon yele la te de tɛɛwɔ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo dɔɔ di kpene kamasɛ dɔɔ.

english_verse: When you collect money for God's people, I want you to do exactly what I told the churches in Galatia to do.
translation_verse: Nɛnɛɛ toto keŋ ɛ ka do na baa mo kyɔ Wurubuarɛ balaŋ baŋ be kyaa Gyudia tɛɛle man nɛ, ɛ yɛɛ ŋgba mena keŋ mɔɔ wola Kristotena akpiise ŋan kya Galatia tɛɛle man nɛ.

english_verse: That is, each Sunday each of you must put aside part of what you have earned. If you do this, you won't have to take up a collection when I come.
translation_verse: Kosida kamasɛ keŋ ɛ gyaŋee nɛ, kɔba keŋ walaŋ kamasɛ be yɛɛ tom Kosida kenaŋ man te ɔ be nyim nɛ, waa lese ke man ɔ doŋ ketalebii see yenaŋ. Na kɔbase ŋan pou kegyaŋee pɛna maa kɔŋ.

english_verse: Choose some followers to take the money to Jerusalem. I will send them on with the money and with letters which show that you approve of them.
translation_verse: Na akpaa me kaa gyoo ɛ gyaŋ na, ɛ ka mo ya abɔɔ ŋan do balaŋ baŋ ɛmɛɛ gbagba ba lese wɔ nɛ nyiŋmaa man, na maa ŋmarase me nyiŋmaa man tɔne kpu na ya abɔɔ ŋan fa wɔ, na baa mo gyina Kristotena baŋ be kyaa Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ.

english_verse: If you think I should go along, they can go with me. Paul's Travel Plans
translation_verse: Te akpaa de kɔŋ nyi maŋ gbagba maa gyu na, maa kpu na wɔ na de popou ke gyu.

english_verse: and visit with you for a while. I may even stay all winter, so that you can help me on my way to wherever I will be going next.
translation_verse: Me kɔŋ na, mberem maa di weeya ala ŋaale ɛ gyaŋ, yaa maa kyaa kelii waare tɛɛ na ɛ ke nyiŋ kyɔ maŋ me gbɛɛneŋ ŋan maa gyu nɛ man.

english_verse: If the Lord lets me, I would rather come later for a longer visit than to stop off now for only a short visit.
translation_verse: Me gbɛɛ kei man dɔɔ, mɔɔ ne gyae nyi maa kpase kɔŋ kaa do ŋon nɔɔ yakaa na maa bese ya, mɔna akpaa Wurubuarɛ de sɛɛ na, maa kaa di weeya akalansɛɛ ŋaale ɛ gyaŋ.

english_verse: I will stay in Ephesus until Pentecost,
translation_verse: Mɔna maa kyaa Efeso donɔɔ man kɛbo kelii Pentekosite wee keŋ.

english_verse: because there is a wonderful opportunity for me to do some work here. But there are also many people who are against me.
translation_verse: Doo nyi balaŋ burum ta koro seŋ tia maŋ, mɔna ma te naa nyi Wurubuarɛ ta toro gbɛɛ dinaa daale fa maŋ, nyi maa yɛɛ tom keŋ ke gyae ke yɛɛ tɔnɔɔ nɛ kɛbo.

english_verse: When Timothy arrives, give him a friendly welcome. He is doing the Lord's work, just as I am.
translation_verse: Akpaa Timoti de kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ na ɛ lɛɛ e nideli na ɔ wɔe ke fɛɛ e, nawolo nyi ŋon mɔ ne yɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu tom bo ŋgba maŋ nɛ.

english_verse: Don't let anyone mistreat him. I am looking for him to return to me together with the other followers. So when he leaves, send him off with your blessings.
translation_verse: Ɛ man ŋolo na ŋolo na bɛɛ nyi ɔ be lɛɛ e ya, mɔna ɛ lɔ e gbɛɛ wosefɛɛreŋ man na waa nyiŋ bese kɔŋ me gyaŋ. Mɛɛ kɛɛ ɔ gbɛɛ nyi de tebia Kristotena baale baa kpu na e na baa kɔŋ.

english_verse: I have tried hard to get our friend Apollos to visit you with the other followers. He doesn't want to come just now, but he will come when he can. Personal Concerns and Greetings
translation_verse: De gyoo Apolo dɔɔ ma doo e kakyeŋ nideli nyi waa kpu na de tebia Kristotena akaŋ baŋ na baa kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ, mɔna ɔ te sɛɛ nyi waa kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ debokeiman ya. Akpaa Wurubuarɛ de fa e gbɛɛ na, waa kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ.

english_verse: Show love in everything you do.
translation_verse: Ɛ yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ dɔŋa kegyae man.

english_verse: I was glad to see Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus. Having them here was like having you.
translation_verse: Setefanase na Fɔtunatuse na Akayakuse ke kɔŋ me gyaŋ nɛ yɛɛ maŋ gyoŋ nideli. Nawolo nyi baŋ kɔŋala kaa seŋ ɛ nawɔɔ man.

english_verse: They made me feel much better, just as they made you feel better. You should appreciate people like them.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi ba te yeli ma nyee man nombia te yɛɛ maŋ dokoloŋ, ŋgba mena keŋ ɛmɛɛ mɔ wui be yɛɛ ŋon dokoloŋ nɛ. Kaboena nyi dɛɛ lese mena balaŋ kɛwɔ dinɔɔ yele. Nɔɔkado ɔtomante

english_verse: Greetings from the churches in Asia. Aquila and Priscilla, together with the church that meets in their house, send greetings in the name of the Lord.
translation_verse: Kristotena akpiise ŋan kyaa Esia tɛɛle man nɛ na do ŋon nɔɔ. Akuila na Pirisila na Kristotena dikpii keŋ ne gyaŋee be dɛɛ nɛ, na do ŋon nɔɔ nideli lee de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu yele man.

english_verse: All the Lord's followers send their greetings. Give each other a warm greeting.
translation_verse: De tebia Kristotena baŋ pou be kyaa kɛbo nɛ na do ŋon nɔɔ. Ɛ do dɔŋa nɔɔ gyoŋ man.

english_verse: I am signing this letter myself: PAUL.
translation_verse: Maŋ Pɔɔl gbagba moona me nyiŋmaa ŋmarase nɔɔkado tɔne kei.

english_verse: I pray that God will put a curse on everyone who doesn't love the Lord. And may the Lord come soon.
translation_verse: Akpaa nyi ŋolo bɛɛ gyae de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu nombia ya na, Wurubuarɛ waa falaa e. Maranata .

english_verse: I pray the Lord Jesus will be kind to you.
translation_verse: De Gbeŋgyoo Yesu waa mo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele ŋon, na waa seŋ ɛ wɔle.

english_verse: I love everyone who belongs to Christ Jesus.
translation_verse: Mɛɛ gyae ɛ popou, te mɛɛ do ŋon nɔɔ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo yele man. Amɛn.

english_verse: From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Jesus Christ, and from Timothy, who is also a follower. To God's church in Corinth and to all of God's people in Achaia.
translation_verse: Tɔne kei lee maŋ Pɔɔl ŋon me yɛɛ Yesu Kristo kpilale e lee Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii man nɛ, na de dɔɔ Timoti gyaŋ. Dɛɛ ŋmarase ke dee yelee ɛmɛɛ Kristotena dikpii keŋ ɛ kyaa Korinto donɔɔ man, na Wurubuarɛ balaŋ baŋ ɔ be baake te ɔ be desina wɔ see fa ɔ wose te be kyaa Akaya tɛɛle dɔɔ pou nɛ.

english_verse: I pray that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! Paul Gives Thanks
translation_verse: De kya Wurubuarɛ na de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo baa mo be bɛrɛɛ boele ŋon, na baa fa ŋon wosefɛɛreŋ. Pɔɔl kalese Wurubuarɛ yele

english_verse: Praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! The Father is a merciful God, who always gives us comfort.
translation_verse: Ɛ yela dɛɛ do Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo ɔ kya e nɛ nɔɔ. Ŋon yɛna Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ ne wii balaŋ waraŋase te ɔ ne yako do wɔ.

english_verse: He comforts us when we are in trouble, so that we can share this same comfort with others in trouble.
translation_verse: Ŋon ne yakola do daa de kayeyɛɛ man, na ke yela daa mɔ ke tale mo keyakodo keŋ ɔ be yako do daa nɛ yako do de tebia baŋ bɛɛ yeyɛɛ nɛ.

english_verse: We share in the terrible sufferings of Christ, but also in the wonderful comfort he gives.
translation_verse: De kɛɛ yeyɛɛ ŋgba mena keŋ Kristo mɔ ba yeyɛɛ nɛ, mena mɔ te Wurubuarɛ waa ta Kristo dɔɔ yako do daa ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be yako do Kristo nɛ.

english_verse: We suffer in the hope that you will be comforted and saved. And because we are comforted, you will also be comforted, as you patiently endure suffering like ours.
translation_verse: Nyi daa Kristo kpilala ne naa diyem na, ɛ nyeekelɛɛ na ɛ keyakodo dɔɔ te dɛɛ naa diyem. Mena keŋ dee nyiŋ keyakodo nɛ, ne yeli ɛmɛɛ mɔ ne nyiŋ keyakodo. Kei ne yeli ɛ ne tale nyiŋ konɔɔ naa diyem ŋgba mena keŋ daa mɔ ne naa nɛ.

english_verse: My friends, I want you to know what a hard time we had in Asia. Our sufferings were so horrible and so unbearable that death seemed certain.
translation_verse: Me tebia, dɔɔ ne gyae ɛ ke kyaa ditiŋtɛɛ man lee diyem keŋ dɔɔ naa Esia tɛɛle man nɛ ya. Balaŋ yela da wose kyaŋa daa nideli, keŋ nyi da te gyakaa loo gba nyi dee nyiŋ nyeedoŋ ya.

english_verse: In fact, we felt sure we were going to die. But this made us stop trusting in ourselves and start trusting God, who raises the dead to life.
translation_verse: De konɔɔse man na, yɛɛ daa ŋgba ba te yako daa nyi baa ko daa nɛ. Kei yela de ta mo da wose lɔ daa gbagba de doŋ dɔɔ ya, mɔna da moo da wose bo lɔ Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ ne gyuusu balaŋ lee yeŋ man nɛ dɔɔ.

english_verse: God saved us from the threat of death, and we are sure that he will do it again and again.
translation_verse: Ŋon lesena daa lee yeŋ nombia kɛŋa man, te ɔ gyakaa ɔ na lese daa kaa lii gyɛŋ. De loo gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ nyi waa lese daa debaŋ kamasɛ.

english_verse: Please help us by praying for us. Then many people will give thanks for the blessings we receive in answer to all these prayers. Paul's Change of Plans
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi ɛ ne fane fa daa dɔɔ. Dekɔŋ mena na, Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ keŋ gyae ke kɔŋ de dɔɔ lee balaŋ burum kefane dɔɔ nɛ, gyae ke yela balaŋ burum ke fane fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ. Pɔɔl te kyɛɛkee wa agyueŋ lee kpene keŋ ɔ ne gyae waa yɛɛ nɛ man

english_verse: I am not writing anything you cannot read and understand. I hope you will understand it completely,
translation_verse: Nombia ŋan pou dɛɛ ŋmarase yelee ŋon nɛ, yɛɛ nombia ŋan ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ ke tale kala na ɛ ke nyii ŋe man.

english_verse: I was so sure of your pride in us that I had planned to visit you first of all. In this way you would have the blessing of two visits from me—
translation_verse: Me loo gyakaa nyi ɛ ke nyii nombia kɛŋa man nideli, mena dɔɔ te nafɔ me yɛɛ ma agyueŋ nyi maa taŋgbɛɛ kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ, na ɛ ke nyiŋ di gyoŋ bola bola.

english_verse: Do you think I couldn't make up my mind about what to do? Or do I seem like someone who says “Yes” or “No” simply to please others?
translation_verse: Ma ke kyɛɛkee ma agyueŋ kei na, ke yɛɛ biatɛɛ agyueŋ ke? Yaa mɛɛ yɛɛ me nombia baa ŋgba tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ nɛ? Yaa nawolo baa nyi me dana nɔɔse ala ala?

english_verse: God can be trusted, and so can I, when I say our answer to you has always been “Yes” and never “No.”
translation_verse: Mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ anokoarete e keŋ ɔ bɛɛ lee ɔ nombii wɔle ya nɛ, mena mɔ yaa daa mɔ de nombia ŋan dɛɛ yako nɛ be yɛɛ nɔɔse ala ala ya.

english_verse: This is because Jesus Christ the Son of God is always “Yes” and never “No.” And he is the one Silas, Timothy, and I told you about.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ bu Yesu Kristo ŋon daa na Silase na Timoti be kolosi ɔ nombia fa ŋon nɛ, ɔ te yɛɛ nɔɔse ala ala walaŋ e ya, keŋ waa “Sɛɛ” na waa bese “Bɛɛ” ya. Mɔna ŋon dɔɔ, ɔ sɛɛ na ɔ te sɛɛ.

english_verse: Christ says “Yes” to all God's promises. This is why we have Christ to say “Amen” for us to the glory of God.
translation_verse: Lee Kristo dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan ɔ be yako see nɛ pou kɔŋawɔ mena. Lee ŋon mɔ dɔɔ te de fane taŋ na, dɛɛ yako nyi “Amɛn” mo lese Wurubuarɛ yele.

english_verse: And so God makes it possible for you and us to stand firmly together with Christ. God is also the one who chose us
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ gbagba ne yɛɛ la te daa na ŋon pou na sem keŋkeŋ Kristo kelɛɛdi keŋ man. Ŋon lesena daa te ɔ kpila daa nyi dee gyu ke yɛɛ ɔ kegyaebii.

english_verse: God is my witness that I stayed away from Corinth, just to keep from being hard on you.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ yɛna ma adansɛɛ nyi ma te gyae nyi maa do ŋon kaale ya, mena dɔɔ te me kyɛɛkee ma agyueŋ nyi mɔɔ bese kɔŋ ɛmɛɛ Korintotena gyaŋ ya.

english_verse: We are not bosses who tell you what to believe. We are working with you to make you glad, because your faith is strong.
translation_verse: Dɔɔ ne gyae dɛɛ ŋere ŋon nyi yaa lɛɛ Kristo nombia ŋan dɛɛ yako ŋon nɛ di ya. Nawolo nyi ɛ wulaa ɛ seŋɛɛ ɛ kelɛɛdi keŋ man keŋkeŋ. Mɔna keŋ dɛɛ gyae nyi ke yɛɛ ŋon gyoŋ dɔɔ te daa na ŋon ne yɛɛ tom bom nɛ.

english_verse: I have decided not to make my next visit with you so painful.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ma te yɛɛ ma agyueŋ nyi, mɔɔ gyae maa bese kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ na maa yela nombia ke takaa ŋon ya.

english_verse: If I make you feel bad, who would be left to cheer me up, except the people I had made to feel bad?
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi akpaa me kɔŋ te me yeli nombia de takaa ŋon na, amɔte ne gyae la waa yela me sia kagye. Na ɛmɛɛ baŋ mɔɔ yeli nombia be takaa ŋon nɛ yee?

english_verse: The reason I want to be happy is to make you happy. I wrote as I did because I didn't want to visit you and be made to feel bad, when you should make me feel happy.
translation_verse: Keŋ dɔɔ te mɔɔ ŋmarase ŋon tɔne kenaŋ yɛna nyiaa, mɔɔ ne gyae nyi me kɔŋ na kolo ke takaa ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ ke yela me sia kagye nɛ ya. Nawolo nyi mɛɛ lɛɛ ɛ popou di nideli nyi akpaa me sia da gye na, ɛmɛɛ mɔ sia gyae ka gye.

english_verse: At the time I wrote, I was suffering terribly. My eyes were full of tears, and my heart was broken. But I didn't want to make you feel bad. I only wanted to let you know how much I cared for you. Forgiveness
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ mɛɛ ŋmarase tɔne kei nɛ, na me tɔɔ ne kyoŋii maŋ. Nombia atakaasa na siasaraŋ te ma moowɔ ŋmarase ŋon tɔne kenaŋ. Na me ŋmarase ŋon tɔne kenaŋ bo na ɛ wose ka yeŋ ŋon ya, mɔna mɛɛ gyae nyi ɛ ka gyeŋ mena keŋ mɛɛ gyae ŋon nɛ nideli. Nombiakumɛɛ kamo kyɛɛ

english_verse: I don't want to be hard on you. But if one of you has made someone feel bad, I am not really the one who has been made to feel bad. Some of you are the ones.
translation_verse: Walaŋ ŋon ɔ be yɛɛ nombiikum te ɔ be yeli nombia be takaa ŋon nɛ, na maŋ maageŋ te ɔ yɛɛ ke tia ya, mɔna gbɛɛ daale man na ɛ popou yaa ɔ yɛɛ ke tia. Kei te yɛɛ nyi mɛɛ wɔŋee nombia ŋan man bo ya.

english_verse: Most of you have already pointed out the wrong that person did, and this is punishment enough for what was done.
translation_verse: Mena keŋ ɛ man balaŋ burum be wɔŋ ɔ deŋele nɛ, keŋ kaageŋ tekaboe.

english_verse: When people sin, you should forgive and comfort them, so they won't give up in despair.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ kaboena nyi yaa mo ɔ nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ e na ɛ kekolosi do e, na mena ya na gyae ke yela waa mutii yeŋ.

english_verse: You should make them sure of your love for them.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ waase ŋon nyiaa, ɛ lese ɛ kegyae keŋ wola e nideli.

english_verse: I also wrote because I wanted to test you and find out if you would follow my instructions.
translation_verse: Kpene keŋ dɔɔ te mɔɔ ŋmarase ŋon tɔne keŋ yɛna nyiaa, mɛɛ gyae maa kɛɛ nyi ɛ ka seŋ keŋkeŋ kekɛɛsekɛɛ man, te ɛ ne nyii me dei debaŋ kamasɛ?

english_verse: I will forgive anyone you forgive. Yes, for your sake and with Christ as my witness, I have forgiven whatever needed to be forgiven.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa ɛ mo ŋolo nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ e na, maŋ mɔ maa mo kyɛɛ e. Mɔna nyi akpaa nombii daale doo keŋ kaboena nyi maa mo kyɛɛ e koraŋ gba na, lee ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ te maa mo kyɛɛ e lee Kristo yele man.

english_verse: I have done this to keep Satan from getting the better of us. We all know what goes on in his mind.
translation_verse: Kei gyae ke yela na ɔbɔnsam be nyiŋ tale di de dɔɔ ya. Nawolo nyi da gyeŋ wa adaose ŋan pou.

english_verse: When I went to Troas to preach the good news about Christ, I found that the Lord had already prepared the way.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ mɔɔ gyu ka gyoo Torase donɔɔ man me kaa kolosi de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan nɛ, me naawɔ nyi de Gbeŋgyoo ta toro gbɛɛ fa maŋ nyi maa kolosi ɔ nombia ŋan botɔɔ.

english_verse: But I was worried when I didn't find my friend Titus there. So I left the other followers and went on to Macedonia.
translation_verse: Mɔna me wɔe te fɛɛ maŋ ya, nawolo nyi ma te naa me dɔɔ Tituse botɔɔ ya. Mena dɔɔ me kalaa Torasetena baŋ te me laŋa Masedonia tɛɛle man.

english_verse: I am grateful that God always makes it possible for Christ to lead us to victory. God also helps us spread the knowledge about Christ everywhere, and this knowledge is like the smell of perfume.
translation_verse: Mɔna mɛɛ do Wurubuarɛ nɔɔ nawolo nyi Kristo te di ɔ kɔlala pou dɔɔ. Mena mɔ te lee Kristo dɔɔ, daa mɔ ne di de kɔlala baŋ dɔɔ debaŋ kamasɛ. Kristo te tɛɛ de dɔɔ yela ɔ nombia ŋan man kagyeŋ te yaasee gyu yenaŋ kamasɛ ŋgba kolo ofoŋte nɛ.

english_verse: In fact, God thinks of us as a perfume that brings Christ to everyone. For people who are being saved, this perfume has a sweet smell and leads them to a better life. But for people who are lost, it has a bad smell and leads them to a horrible death. No one really has what it takes to do this work.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ baa yeŋ leki nɛ gyaŋ na, ofoŋ kei yɛɛ ŋgba kolo wɔŋte keŋ gyae kako wɔ nɛ. Mɔna balaŋ baŋ Wurubuarɛ ne lɛɛ ba nyee nɛ dɔɔ, ofoŋ kei yɛɛ ŋgba kpene keŋ ne fa wɔ nyeedoŋ nɛ. Te amɔte kaboela na Kristo tom kei keyɛɛ?

english_verse: A lot of people try to get rich from preaching God's message. But we are God's sincere messengers, and by the power of Christ we speak our message with God as our witness.
translation_verse: Daa be doo ŋgba balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ mo Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan di kebu nɛ ya. Mɔna daa dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ siaman na, de yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ ba lese te ɔ be kpila daa, te dɛɛ tɛɛ Kristo dɔɔ mo de konɔɔ pou yako ɔ nombia ŋan anokoare man nɛ.

english_verse: Are we once again bragging about ourselves? Do we need letters to you or from you to tell others about us? Some people do need letters telling about them.
translation_verse: Nombia da ayakoŋ kɛŋa nɛ, nawolo nyi da bese yaa dɛɛ kɛlɛɛ da wose baa yaa? Yaa kaboena nyi dɛɛ tela tɔne keŋ gyae ka lese daa wola nɛ kɔna ŋon? Yaa dɛɛ lɛɛ tɔne keŋ gyae ka lese daa wola nɛ lee ɛ gyaŋ, ŋgba mena keŋ baale ne yɛɛ nɛ?

english_verse: You are like a letter written by Christ and delivered by us. But you are not written with pen and ink or on tablets made of stone. You are written in our hearts by the Spirit of the living God.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛmɛɛ na wola la nyi, ɛmɛɛ yɛna Kristo tɔne keŋ be tɛɛ de dɔɔ kɔŋ nɛ. Kei te yɛɛ tɔne keŋ bɔɔ mo nyiŋmaa yakaa ŋmarase ya. Mɔna Wurubuarɛ feliŋ te ba moowɔ ŋmarase ke. Ka te yɛɛ ŋgba mena keŋ gyaŋgbatena ne ŋmarase gyakaa boya paŋsapaŋsa dɔɔ nɛ ya, mɔna be ŋmarase ŋa bo do deniwalaŋ konɔɔ man.

english_verse: We are sure about all this. Christ makes us sure in the very presence of God.
translation_verse: Dɛɛ yako mena nombia kɛŋa pou nawolo nyi, dɛɛ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di lee Kristo yele man.

english_verse: We don't have the right to claim that we have done anything on our own. God gives us what it takes to do all we do.
translation_verse: Daa gbagba de doŋ man na, nafɔ da tekaboena tom kei keyɛɛ ya. Nawolo nyi daa gbagba bɛɛ tale yɛɛ kolo de baam dɔɔ ya. De doŋ pou lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ.

english_verse: He makes us worthy to be the servants of his new agreement that comes from the Holy Spirit and not from a written Law. After all, the Law brings death, but the Spirit brings life.
translation_verse: Ŋon faa na daa doŋ te de kaa bese Wurubuarɛ nɔɔkebakesee wɔle keŋ somra. Nɔɔkebakesee wɔle kei doŋ ta lee mmaraase ŋan bɔɔ ŋmarase see nɛ man ya, mɔna doŋ kenaŋ lee Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon gyaŋ. Nawolo nyi mmaraase ŋan bɔɔ ŋmarase see nɛ ne kɔna yeŋ. Mɔna Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon dɔɔ, ŋon ne fa nyeedoŋ bo.

english_verse: The Law of Moses brought only the promise of death, even though it was carved on stones and given in a wonderful way. Still the Law made Moses' face shine so brightly the people of Israel could not look at it, even though it was a fading glory.
translation_verse: Mosesi mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ, be ŋmarase ŋa bo gyakaa boya patalapatala dɔɔ. Te debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo mmaraase ŋan fa ŋon Mosesi nɛ, ŋe kɔŋae Wurubuarɛ gyoorobiiri man te ke yela Mosesi siagbɛɛ ŋalakeewɔ keŋ Iseraetena te tale kɛɛ ɔ siagbɛɛ ya. Mɔna mena keŋalakee kenaŋ dɔɔ, na ke nɔɔ na yem. Mena dɔɔ akpaa nyi Mosesi mmaraase ŋan na mo yeŋ kɔŋ nɛ, kɔŋae na Wurubuarɛ gyoorobiiri keŋ na,

english_verse: So won't the agreement the Spirit brings to us be even more wonderful?
translation_verse: kenaŋ na Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ɔ ne fa nyeedoŋ nɛ, wui gyoorobiiri be gyae ka kela kei yee?

english_verse: If something that brings the death sentence is glorious, won't something that makes us acceptable to God be even more glorious?
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa ɔsom keŋ ne bu balaŋ fɔɔ nɛ dana gyoorobiiri mena na, kenaŋ na ɔ som keŋ ne yele balaŋ gbɛɛneŋ na tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ, wui gyoorobiiri be gyae ka kela kenaŋ yee?

english_verse: In fact, the new agreement is so wonderful that the Law is no longer glorious at all.
translation_verse: Gyoorobiiri keŋ ke kyaa debaŋ kei nɛ dɔɔ, te yeli gyoorobiiri keŋ nafɔ ke kyaa gyaŋgba keŋ nɛ te ŋaalaa.

english_verse: The Law was given with a glory that faded away. But the glory of the new agreement is much greater, because it will never fade away.
translation_verse: Akpaa nyi keŋ ka te kyaa kekpaa ya nɛ kɔŋawɔ na gyoorobiiri na, kenaŋ na keŋ gyae ke kyaa kekpaakekpaa nɛ wui gyoorobiiri gyae ka bo kela ke.

english_verse: This wonderful hope makes us feel like speaking freely.
translation_verse: Keŋ de dana mena loo kegyakaa kei dɔɔ, te dɛɛ tale gyɔ de konɔɔ kolosi.

english_verse: We are not like Moses. His face was shining, but he covered it to keep the people of Israel from seeing the brightness fade away.
translation_verse: Daa be doo ŋgba Mosesi ŋon ɔ ba mo kolo wu ɔ siagbɛɛ, na Iseraetena be nyiŋ naa ɔ siagbɛɛ debaŋ keŋ keŋalakee keŋ nɔɔ na yem nɛ ya.

english_verse: The Lord and the Spirit are one and the same, and the Lord's Spirit sets us free.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi de Gbeŋgyoo Kristo na Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon yɛɛ dokoloŋ. Te botɔɔ keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo feliŋ ŋon kyaa na, walaŋ ne nyiŋ ɔ wose lee mmaraase ŋan tɛɛ.

english_verse: God has been kind enough to trust us with this work. This is why we never give up.
translation_verse: Lee mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ be wii de waraŋase, te ɔ ba mo tom kei do daa de nyiŋmaa man dɔɔ nɛ, dɔɔ ne yee ya.

english_verse: We don't do shameful things that must be kept secret. And we don't try to fool anyone or twist God's message around. God is our witness that we speak only the truth, so others will be sure we can be trusted.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ te da lese da wose lee desɛŋ nombia man, te dɔɔ ne yɛɛ kolo mɔ weese ya. Dɔɔ na mo adao gbɛɛneŋ man mo kolo kyakale na Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan ya. Mɔna dɛɛ lese Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan ŋe yɛɛ anokoare nɛ man nideli, na walaŋ kamasɛ kagyeŋ nyi kpene keŋ dɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ, te yɛɛ dekara Wurubuarɛ siaman ya.

english_verse: If there is anything hidden about our message, it is hidden only to someone who is lost.
translation_verse: Te akpaa Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan yɛɛ nombia aweeseŋ ŋa na, kenaŋ na ŋe yɛɛ nombia aweeseŋ ŋa bo fa balaŋ baŋ baa yo nɛ baageŋ.

english_verse: The god who rules this world has blinded the minds of unbelievers. They cannot see the light, which is the good news about our glorious Christ, who shows what God is like.
translation_verse: Mena balaŋ kɛwɔ bɛɛ lɛɛ nombia kpaakpaa ŋan di ya, nawolo nyi ɔbɔnsam ŋon ɔ yɛɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ gyoo e nɛ, ta mo wɔ do ditiŋtɛɛ man. Mena dɔɔ bɔɔ ne naa keŋmaŋee keŋ doo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan na lese ɔ gyoorobiiri wola nɛ ya. Te bɔɔ ne naa, na baa gyeŋ nyi Kristo yɛna Wurubuarɛ dinɔɔ gbagba ŋon ya.

english_verse: The Scriptures say, “God commanded light to shine in the dark.” Now God is shining in our hearts to let you know that his glory is seen in Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ be yako nyi, “Keŋmaŋee dɛɛ lee ditiŋtɛɛ man ŋmaŋee nɛ,” ŋon ke yela la te keŋmaŋee ŋmaŋeewɔ de konɔɔse man, na ke yela dɛɛ gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ gyoorobiiri keŋ doo Kristo man nɛ.

english_verse: We often suffer, but we are never crushed. Even when we don't know what to do, we never give up.
translation_verse: Dɛɛ naa diyem lee gbɛɛ kamasɛ man, mɔna da seŋɛɛ keŋkeŋ. Debaŋ ŋaale man na da agyueŋ bee doo debodokoloŋ ya, mɔna da wose bee gbirisi daa ya.

english_verse: In times of trouble, God is with us, and when we are knocked down, we get up again.
translation_verse: Bɛɛ naase daa diyem, mɔna Wurubuarɛ bɛɛ lese ɔ sia yela daa ya. Bɛɛ gyɔ daa lɔ yeŋ yeŋ, mɔna dɔɔ na yem ya.

english_verse: We face death every day because of Jesus. Our bodies show what his death was like, so his life can also be seen in us.
translation_verse: Daa baŋ de kyaa na de sia nɛ, wee na wee na lee Yesu dɔɔ bɛɛ lese daa fa yeŋ. Na ke yela walaŋ kamasɛ kena nyi, Yesu nyeedoŋ keŋ doo daa baŋ da wosenaane kei gyae ka yeŋ nɛ mɔ man.

english_verse: This means that death is working in us, but life is working in you.
translation_verse: Lee mena dɔɔ daa ne gyaŋee na yeŋ nombia wee kamasɛ, na ɛmɛɛ kenyiŋ nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa.

english_verse: In the Scriptures it says, “I spoke because I had faith.” We have this same kind of faith. So we speak
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see yenaŋ daale ne yako nyiaa, “Me lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nombia di dɔɔ, te me kolosi ɔ nombia.” Kelɛɛdi dokoloŋ kenaŋ te daa mɔ lɛɛwɔ di, mena dɔɔ te dee kolosi ɔ nombia nɛ.

english_verse: because we know that God raised the Lord Jesus to life. And just as God raised Jesus, he will also raise us to life. Then he will bring us into his presence together with you.
translation_verse: Da gyeŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ be gyuusu de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu lee yeŋ man nɛ, waa ta Yesu dɔɔ gyuusu daa na waa mo ŋon kpu na daa kaa seŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ siaman.

english_verse: All of this has been done for you, so more and more people will know how kind God is and will praise and honor him. Faith in the Lord
translation_verse: Ɛ deeli dɔɔ te nombia kɛŋa pou kɔŋawɔ mena, na ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo ɔ bɛrɛɛ boele balaŋ burum te ɔ ne lɛɛ ba nyee nɛ, ke yela baa fane fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ nideli na baa lese ɔ yele.

english_verse: We never give up. Our bodies are gradually dying, but we ourselves are being made stronger each day.
translation_verse: Lee mena dɔɔ da wose bɛɛ yɔkɔse daa ya. Nawolo nyi keŋ na da wosenaane kɛɛ yɔkɔse kei koraŋ nɛ, de dinɔɔ wɔle keŋ dɔɔ kee nyiŋ doŋ wee kamasɛ.

english_verse: Our bodies are like tents that we live in here on earth. But when these tents are destroyed, we know that God will give each of us a place to live. These homes will not be buildings someone has made, but they are in heaven and will last forever.
translation_verse: Da gyeŋ nyi tɛɛle kei dɔɔ de kyaa nɛ, dɔɔ gyae dɛɛ kyare ya na de ta yem. Mɔna akpaa da yem na, Wurubuarɛ waa fa daa wosenaane wɔle adido, keŋ ka te yɛɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ wosenaane ke ya. Wurubuarɛ gbagba yɛɛ na ke, te ke gyae ke kyaa kekpaakekpaa.

english_verse: While we are here on earth, we sigh because we want to live in that heavenly home.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kei na dɛɛ keketee da wose man, nawolo nyi dɛɛ gyae nideli nyi Wurubuarɛ waa faa daa mena wosenaane wɔle kei.

english_verse: We want to put it on like clothes and not be naked.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa Wurubuarɛ da mo ke do daa na, dɔɔ gyae dɛɛ ta diteŋteli ya.

english_verse: These tents we now live in are like a heavy burden, and we groan. But we don't do this just because we want to leave these bodies that will die. It is because we want to change them for bodies that will never die.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ de sɛɛ de doo wosenaane kei man nɛ, dɛɛ yeyɛɛ ŋgba kasolɔ daale na ŋere na daa nɛ. Na dɛɛ yako baa nyi yeŋ yaa dɛɛ gyae debokeiman ya. Mɔna nafɔ da kegyaebii yɛna nyi Wurubuarɛ waa mo wosenaane wɔle keŋ kyaa adido nɛ do daa. Na wosenaane wɔle keŋ kaa kyaa wosenaane bene keŋ na yem nɛ nawɔɔ man.

english_verse: God is the one who makes all this possible. He has given us his Spirit to make us certain he will do it.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ gbagba desina na daa fa mena kekyɛɛkee kenaŋ, te ɔ moo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do de man. Ŋon te ɔ moowɔ see ateta, mo kyaŋ abɔɔ ŋan pou waa kaa yɛɛ fa daa nɛ nae.

english_verse: We should be cheerful, because we would rather leave these bodies and be at home with the Lord.
translation_verse: Lee mena dɔɔ de dana lookegyakaa nideli. Nafɔ da kegyaebii yɛna nyi, dɛɛ lee wosenaane kei man na de kaa kyaa de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu gyaŋ.

english_verse: But whether we are at home with the Lord or away from him, we still try our best to please him.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ nyi de kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ bo o, yaa da yeŋae o, kpene keŋ dɔɔ dɛɛ yase de sia yɛna nyi, dɛɛ yɛɛ kpene keŋ gyae ka gyoo de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu sia.

english_verse: We know what it means to respect the Lord, and we encourage everyone to turn to him. God himself knows what we are like, and I hope you also know what kind of people we are.
translation_verse: Da gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ keyee kedoobii man dɔɔ, te dɛɛ do balaŋ kakyeŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom man. Wurubuarɛ gyeŋ de dinɔɔ, te dɛɛ lɛɛdi nyi ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ gyeŋ de dinɔɔ lee ɛ konɔɔse man.

english_verse: We are not trying once more to brag about ourselves. But we want you to be proud of us, when you are with those who are not sincere and brag about what others think of them.
translation_verse: Te yɛɛ nyi da bese yaa dɛɛ kɛlɛɛ da wose bo fa ŋon ya. Mɔna dɛɛ tɔɔse ŋon nombia ŋan gyae ke yela ɛ ke tale gyina ɛ wose adido lee daa dɔɔ nɛ bo. Dekɔŋ mena na, ɛ ke tale nyiŋ nombii yako balaŋ baŋ bee gyina ba wose adido lee abɔɔ ŋan bɛɛ mo be sia naa ŋa nɛ dɔɔ, kela ŋan ŋe doo deniwalaŋ konɔɔ man nɛ.

english_verse: If we seem out of our minds, it is between God and us. But if we are in our right minds, it is for your good.
