translation_verse: Akpaa nombia kɛŋa dɔɔ te ɛ yako nyi da nyeeya ne wɔlɛɛ na, ŋe wɔlɛe fa Wurubuarɛ. Te akpaa da nyeeya doo mɔ na, kenaŋ na ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ te da nyeeya doo.

english_verse: We are ruled by Christ's love for us. We are certain that if one person died for everyone else, then all of us have died.
translation_verse: Kristo kegyae keŋ te di da nyee, nawolo nyi da gyeŋ nyi ŋon lɛɛ na balaŋ pou yeŋ yeŋ. Mena dɔɔ yɛɛ yaa ŋgba balaŋ pou yeŋa la nɛ.

english_verse: And Christ did die for all of us. He died so we would no longer live for ourselves, but for the one who died and was raised to life for us.
translation_verse: Kristo yeŋa la fa balaŋ pou, na ke yela baŋ be kyaa na be sia nɛ be nyiŋ kyaa fa ba wose ya. Mɔna baa kyaa fa Yesu Kristo ŋon ɔ ba yem fa wɔ, te Wurubuarɛ be gyuusu e nɛ.

english_verse: Anyone who belongs to Christ is a new person. The past is forgotten, and everything is new.
translation_verse: Lee mena dɔɔ akpaa nyi ŋolo de lɛɛ Kristo di na, ɔ ta bese walaŋ wɔle. Ɔ dinɔɔ bene keŋ te lam, te nɛnɛɛ ɔ te nyiŋ dinɔɔ wɔle.

english_verse: God has done it all! He sent Christ to make peace between himself and us, and he has given us the work of making peace between himself and others.
translation_verse: Nombia kɛŋa pou lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ. Ŋon tɛɛ la Yesu Kristo dɔɔ desina daa na ŋon Wurubuarɛ nsana, te ɔ faa daa tom nyi dɛɛ yako ɔ nombia kɛŋa, na dɛɛ mo desina ŋon na balaŋ nsana.

english_verse: What we mean is that God was in Christ, offering peace and forgiveness to the people of this world. And he has given us the work of sharing his message about peace.
translation_verse: Nombii keŋ dɛɛ yako ŋon yɛna nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ tɛɛ la Kristo dɔɔ desina ŋon na balaŋ pou nsana, te ɔ moo be nombiakumɛɛ pou kyɛɛ wɔ. Te ɔ moo ŋon na balaŋ nsana kedesina tom kei do daa de nyiŋmaa man.

english_verse: We were sent to speak for Christ, and God is begging you to listen to our message. We speak for Christ and sincerely ask you to make peace with God.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ daa yɛna Kristo kpilala baŋ da seŋɛɛ ɔ nawɔɔ man, keŋ Wurubuarɛ ne tɛɛ de dɔɔ kolosi sola ŋon. Da seŋɛɛ Kristo nawɔɔ man dɛɛ sola ŋon nyi, ɛ yela ɛmɛɛ na Wurubuarɛ nsana keyɛɛ gyoŋ.

english_verse: Christ never sinned! But God treated him as a sinner, so Christ could make us acceptable to God.
translation_verse: Kristo te yɛɛ nombiikum kamasɛ ya. Mɔna Wurubuarɛ yela ɔ kaa moo de nombiakumɛɛ pou seele. Te ɔ yeŋawɔ ŋgba nombiakumɛɛyɛɛre nɛ, na ke yela de nombia ka tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman lee ŋon Kristo dɔɔ.

english_verse: We work together with God, and we beg you to make good use of God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: De yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ daa na Wurubuarɛ ne yɛɛ na tom. Lee mena dɔɔ dɛɛ waase ŋon bo nyiaa, ɛ na yeli Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ keŋ ɔ ba mo boele ŋon nɛ ke yɛɛ yakaa ya.

english_verse: In the Scriptures God says, “When the time came, I listened to you, and when you needed help, I came to save you.” That time has come. This is the day for you to be saved.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see yakowɔ yenaŋ daale nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ kpa debaŋ keŋ kaboena nyi maa wii ŋon nɛ, me nyii ɛ ke fane. Debaŋ keŋ mɔ kaboena nyi maa lɛɛ ɛ nyee nɛ, me lɛɛ ŋon.” Te maŋ Pɔɔl ne yako ŋon baa ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, nɛnɛɛ yɛna debaŋ keŋ kaboena nyi Wurubuarɛ waa wii ŋon. Gyɛŋ wee kei yɛna nyeekelɛɛwee keŋ. Diyem keŋ Pɔɔl be naa

english_verse: We don't want anyone to find fault with our work, and so we try hard not to cause problems.
translation_verse: De bɛɛ gyae nyi ŋolo ke wɔlɛɛ da yele lee tom keŋ dɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ man ya. Mena dɔɔ dɛɛ yɛɛ kakyeŋ nyi dɔɔ yɛɛ kolo, na ŋolo kenyiŋ gbɛɛ mo ayimɛɛ wuu daa ya.

english_verse: But in everything and in every way we show we truly are God's servants. We have always been patient, though we have had a lot of trouble, suffering, and hard times.
translation_verse: Lee mena dɔɔ de nombia ayɛɛsa pou man na, dɛɛ lese wola ŋon nyi de yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ tewulɛɛwɔ. Nawolo nyi de nyiŋa konɔɔ seŋ keŋkeŋ de kayeyɛɛ, na debaŋ keŋ da wose be kyaŋ daa nɛ, na de kaale burum man.

english_verse: We have been beaten, put in jail, and hurt in riots. We have worked hard and have gone without sleep or food.
translation_verse: Be tuku daa nideli wɔŋee daa balaŋ man, te be kyaŋa daa tɔ deni. De kyaŋa da wose yɛɛ tom nideli keŋ nyi dɔɔ ne nyiŋ dosoroŋ gba dee doo ya, te de tɛɛwɔ mɔ na tanam.

english_verse: But we have kept ourselves pure and have been understanding, patient, and kind. The Holy Spirit has been with us, and our love has been real.
translation_verse: Dɛɛ lese wola nyi de yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ tewulɛɛwɔ, mo lee da kekyaabii keŋ ayimɛɛ be doo ke man ya nɛ man. Da gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan, te dee nyiŋ konɔɔ te dɛɛ yɛɛ deeli. Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon doo de man, te dɛɛ gyae balaŋ anokoare man.

english_verse: We have spoken the truth, and God's power has worked in us. In all our struggles we have said and done only what is right.
translation_verse: Nombii na anokoare te dee kolosi, te dɛɛ yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ mɔ bo lee Wurubuarɛ doŋ man. De nombia ŋan tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ, te de dana demena na dunoluŋ ŋgba yoo abɔɔ nɛ. Ŋan te dɛɛ mo yoo lɛɛ da wose.

english_verse: Whether we were honored or dishonored or praised or cursed, we always told the truth about ourselves. But some people said we did not.
translation_verse: Baale na lese da yele, te baale mɔ na do daa desɛŋ. Baale na tee daa, mɔna baale mɔ ne kɛlɛɛ daa. Baale ne kɛɛ daa nyi de yɛɛ akɛrɛɛtenawɔ, mɔna de nombia doo na ke gbɛɛ.

english_verse: We are unknown to others, but well known to you. We seem to be dying, and yet we are still alive. We have been punished, but never killed,
translation_verse: Baale gyaŋ na, da te yɛɛ balaŋwɔ ya, mɔna da yele ta lee nideli. Kayeyɛɛ dɔɔ akpaa n naa daa na n kpa dɛɛ yem baa, mɔna de dana nyeedoŋ. Bɛɛ naase daa diyem nideli, mɔna de bɛɛ yem ya.

english_verse: and we are always happy, even in times of suffering. Although we are poor, we have made many people rich. And though we own nothing, everything is ours.
translation_verse: De dɔŋ waraŋase man, mɔna Kristo dɔɔ dee di gyoŋ debaŋ kamasɛ. De dɔŋ na diyem, mɔna dee yeli balaŋ burum ne nyiŋ abɔɔ kpaakpaa lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ. De dɔŋ ŋgba balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ dana kolo ya nɛ, mɔna Kristo dɔɔ kolo na kolo bee hia daa ya.

english_verse: Friends in Corinth, we are telling the truth when we say there is room in our hearts for you.
translation_verse: Me tebia Korintotena, da te kolosi nombii na anokoare keŋ fa ŋon, keŋ de ta mo kolo weese ŋon ya. Te dɛɛ gyae ŋon nideli lee de konɔɔ man.

english_verse: We are not holding back on our love for you, but you are holding back on your love for us.
translation_verse: De bɛɛ wɔŋ da wose lee ɛ man ya, mɔna ɛmɛɛ ne wɔŋ ɛ wose lee de man.

english_verse: I speak to you as I would speak to my own children. Please make room in your hearts for us. The Temple of the Living God
translation_verse: Mee kolosi baa fa ŋon ŋgba mena keŋ maa kolosi fa me bia nɛ. Mena dɔɔ ɛ na wɔŋ ɛ wose lee de man na daa mɔ ke wɔŋ da wose lee ɛ man ya. Ɛmɛɛ na balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Kristotenawɔ ya nɛ, na yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ ya

english_verse: Stay away from people who are not followers of the Lord! Can someone who is good get along with someone who is evil? Are light and darkness the same?
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ na balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Kristotenawɔ ya nɛ na yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ ya. Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ kɛɛ nyi, walaŋ ŋon ɔ nombia tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman na walaŋkum baa tale yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ? Yaa ditiŋtɛɛ na keŋmaŋee gyae ke tale kyaa bom?

english_verse: Is Christ a friend of Satan? Can people who follow the Lord have anything in common with those who don't?
translation_verse: Loŋtoro woŋti doola Kristo na ɔbɔnsam nsana? Yaa Kristo walaŋ na walaŋ ŋon ɔ te yɛɛ Kristo walaŋ e ya nɛ baa tale yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ.

english_verse: Do idols belong in the temple of God? We are the temple of the living God, as God himself says, “I will live with these people and walk among them. I will be their God, and they will be my people.”
translation_verse: Loŋtoro woŋti doola Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni na bane nsana? Daa yɛna Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa kekpaakekpaa nɛ ɔsom deni keŋ. Wurubuarɛ gbagba yakowɔ ɔ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan man nyiaa, “Maa kyaa be man, na maa kpu na wɔ, na maa yɛɛ be Wurubuarɛ, na baŋ mɔ ke yɛɛ me balaŋ.

english_verse: The Lord also says, “Leave them and stay away! Don't touch anything that isn't clean. Then I will welcome you
translation_verse: Ɛ lese ɛ wose lee be man, na ɛ ke tira ɛ wose na wɔ. Ɛ na tina kpene keŋ ka wose dana ayimɛɛ nɛ ya. Ɛ yɛɛ mena na, maa lɛɛ ŋon do.

english_verse: and be your Father. You will be my sons and my daughters, as surely as I am God, the All-Powerful.”
translation_verse: Maa yɛɛ ɛ kya, na ɛmɛɛ mɔ ke yɛɛ me bia. Nnɛ yaa de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ doŋte ŋon ne yako.”

english_verse: My friends, God has made us these promises. So we should stay away from everything that keeps our bodies and spirits from being clean. We should honor God and try to be completely like him. The Church Makes Paul Happy
translation_verse: Me gyoona kpaakpaa, Wurubuarɛ te wulaa yako see nyi waa yɛɛ mena abɔɔ kɛŋa pou fa daa. Mena dɔɔ ɛ yela dɛɛ lese ayimɛɛ kamasɛ ŋan ne wɔlɛɛ da wosenaane na da woo nɛ leki. Ɛ yela Wurubuarɛ keyee ke doo de man, na dee desina da wose nideli see fa e, na dɛɛ mo obuo fa e lee da kekyaabii man. Pɔɔl sia kagye

english_verse: Make a place for us in your hearts! We haven't mistreated or hurt anyone. We haven't cheated anyone.
translation_verse: Ɛ booli ɛ wose na daa, na ɛ ka mo ɛ konɔɔ pou fa daa. Da te yɛɛ ŋolo nombiikum daale ya. Da te wɔlɛɛ ŋolo nyee ya, te de ta ŋere ŋolo mɔ do ya.

english_verse: I am not saying this to be hard on you. But, as I have said before, you will always be in our thoughts, whether we live or die.
translation_verse: Mɔɔ ne yako mena nombia kɛŋa na maa mo bu ŋon fɔɔ ya. Me wulaa yako ŋon kaa laŋ nyiaa, dɛɛ gyae ɛ nombia nideli lee de konɔɔ man. Mena dɔɔ nyi nyeedoŋ man o, yeŋ man o, daa na ŋon yaa.

english_verse: I trust you completely. I am always proud of you, and I am greatly encouraged. In all my trouble I am still very happy.
translation_verse: Mɛɛ lɛɛ ŋon di nideli te lee ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ mee gyina ma wose adido tɛɛle kei dɔɔ. Keŋ na da diyem keŋ dɛɛ naa kei koraŋ nɛ, mena dinɔɔ kei na do maŋ kakyeŋ nideli te gyoŋ keŋ doo me man mɔ nɛ be taŋ ɔto ya.

english_verse: After we came to Macedonia, we didn't have any chance to rest. We were faced with all kinds of problems. We were troubled by enemies and troubled by fears.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ dɔɔ kɔŋ Masedonia tɛɛle man nɛ, de te nyiŋ da wose gba koraŋ dɛɛ kyaa fɛɛfo ya. Balaŋ naase daa diyem lee gbɛɛ kamasɛ man. Baale korowɔ seŋ tia daa, yela gyakoloŋ gyoo daa.

english_verse: But God cheers up people in need, and this is what he did when he sent Titus to us.
translation_verse: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ ne yako do balaŋ baŋ dekɔkɔrɔkɔɔ be kyaŋ wɔ nɛ, ŋon tɛɛ la Tituse ŋon ɔ ba lee ɛ gyaŋ nɛ dɔɔ kɔŋ kaa yako do daa mɔ.

english_verse: Of course, we were glad to see Titus, but what really made us glad is the way you cheered him up. He told how sorry you were and how concerned you were about me. And this made me even happier.
translation_verse: Na Tituse kekɔŋ kaageŋ keŋ doo na daa kakyeŋ ya, mɔna kakyeŋ keŋ ɛ ba do e nɛ mɔ kpuɛ. Nawolo nyi ɔ yako daa mena keŋ me nombia ne gyɔ ŋon loo, na mena keŋ ɛ ne wii maŋ, na mena keŋ ɛ be desina ɛ wose nyi ɛ ke yoo seŋ me wɔle nɛ. Mena dɔɔ te yeli me sia ta gye nideli.

english_verse: I don't feel bad anymore, even though my letter hurt your feelings. I did feel bad at first, but I don't now. I know that the letter hurt you for a while.
translation_verse: Me nyiiwɔ nyi tɔne keŋ mɔɔ ŋmarase yelee ŋon nɛ te fɛɛ ɛ wɔe ya. Mɔna te takaa maŋ ŋgba mɔɔ ŋmarase ke nɛ ya. Me naawɔ nyi tɔne kenaŋ man nombia takaa ŋon berɛ, mena dɔɔ takaa maŋ mɔ. Mɔna ma gyeŋ nyi ka be gyae ke takaa ŋon kekpaakekpaa ya.

english_verse: Now I am happy, but not because I hurt your feelings. It is because God used your hurt feelings to make you turn back to him, and none of you were harmed by us.
translation_verse: Lee mena dɔɔ nɛnɛɛ dana maŋ gyoŋ. Te yɛɛ nyi keŋ tɔne keŋ man nombia be takaa ŋon dɔɔ ya, mɔna keŋ ka be yele ɛ be kyɛɛkee lee ɛ gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man dɔɔ. Nawolo nyi ka ke takaa ŋon keŋ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii man ke. Mena dɔɔ da gyeŋ nyi da te yɛɛ ŋon kolo kum ya.

english_verse: When God makes you feel sorry enough to turn to him and be saved, you don't have anything to feel bad about. But when this world makes you feel sorry, it can cause your death.
translation_verse: Kolo de takaa ŋolo lee Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii man na, ne yele ɔ ne kyɛɛkee lee ɔ gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man na ɔ kaa nyiŋ nyeekelɛɛ. Ke bɛɛ takaa ɔtenate wɔle wɔle bela ya. Mɔna nyi kolo de takaa ŋolo keŋ ka te yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii man ke ya na, kɛɛ mo ɔtenate gyu yeŋ man.

english_verse: When I wrote you, it wasn't to accuse the one who was wrong or to take up for the one who was hurt. I wrote, so God would show you how much you do care for us.
translation_verse: Mena tɔne keŋ mɔɔ ŋmarase yelee ŋon nɛ, na walaŋ ŋon ɔ be yɛɛ dukum, yaa ŋon bɔɔ yɛɛ dukum tia e nɛ baageŋ dɔɔ te me ŋmarase ke ya. Mɔna me ŋmarase ke bo na Wurubuarɛ ke yela ɛ ka gyeŋ lee ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ konɔɔ man nyi, de nombia yɛɛ ɛ nombia ŋa Wurubuarɛ siaman.

english_verse: And we were greatly encouraged. Although we were encouraged, we felt even better when we saw how happy Titus was, because you had shown he had nothing to worry about.
translation_verse: Mena abɔɔ kɛŋa pou na do daa kakyeŋ. Kei wɔle na, debaŋ keŋ dɔɔ naa nyi Tituse sia ta gye lee mena keŋ ɛ popou be kyɔ e, te wa agyueŋ be yɛɛ e dokoloŋ nɛ, yɛɛ daa mɔ gyoŋ nideli.

english_verse: We had told him how much we thought of you, and you did not disappoint us. Just as we have always told you the truth, so everything we told him about you has also proved to be true.
translation_verse: Me kɛlɛɛ ŋon fa Tituse, te ɛ te yeli desɛŋ ke kyaŋ maŋ ya. Mena keŋ kpene kamasɛ keŋ dɔɔ yako ŋon yɛɛ ampaŋ nɛ, mena mɔ te da ke kɛlɛɛ ŋon fa Tituse keŋ te kaa yɛɛ ampaŋ.

english_verse: Titus loves all of you very much, especially when he remembers how you obeyed him and how you trembled with fear when you welcomed him.
translation_verse: Akpaa ɔ kɛɛ mena keŋ ɛ be bu e, na mena keŋ ɛ ba mo obuo lɛɛ e nɛ, ne yeli ɔ ne gyae ŋon nideli kpu.

english_verse: It makes me really glad to know I can depend on you.
translation_verse: Dana maŋ gyoŋ nideli, nawolo nyi ma gyeŋ nyi maa tale mo ma wose lɔ ɛ dɔɔ nideli.

english_verse: My friends, we want you to know that the churches in Macedonia have shown others God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: De tebia, dɛɛ gyae ɛ ka gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ keŋ ɔ ba mo boele Kristotena baŋ be kyaa Masedonia tɛɛle dɔɔ nɛ.

english_verse: Although they were going through hard times and were very poor, they were glad to give generously.
translation_verse: Be naa diyem nideli. Mɔna gyoŋ dinaa keŋ doo be man dɔɔ nɛ, keŋ na ba diyem koraŋ na bɛɛ yɛɛ wɔe nideli lee balaŋ abɔɔ kefa man.

english_verse: They even asked and begged us to let them have the joy of giving their money for God's people.
translation_verse: Keŋ na ba diyem koraŋ na, ba sola daa nideli nyi dɛɛ fa wɔ gbɛɛ na baa kyɔ Kristotena baŋ be kyaa Gyudia tɛɛle man nɛ.

english_verse: And they did more than we had hoped. They gave themselves first to the Lord and then to us, just as God wanted them to do.
translation_verse: Be yɛɛ kpene keŋ da te gyakaa loo gba nyi baa yɛɛ ya nɛ. Be taŋgbɛɛwɔ mo ba wose fa de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu, kenaŋ wɔle na te ba moo ba wose fa daa mɔ Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii man.

english_verse: Titus was the one who got you started doing this good thing, so we begged him to help you finish what you had begun.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ da sola Tituse nyi, mena keŋ ɔ be taŋ fi kɔba kelɛɛ asɛɛ ɔ na mo kyɔ ayematena nɛ, waa yɛɛ kakyeŋ na waa kyɔ ŋon na ɛ ke yɛɛ ke kelii ɔto.

english_verse: You do everything better than anyone else. You have stronger faith. You speak better and know more. You are eager to give, and you love us better. Now you must give more generously than anyone else.
translation_verse: Ma gyeŋ nyi ɛ ne tale yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ nideli, lee ɛ kelɛɛdi na ɛ kekolosi na ɛ nombia kagyeŋ, na ɛ kakyeŋ kado, na ɛ balaŋ kegyae pou man. Mena dɔɔ ɛ yɛɛ kakyeŋ na ɛ ke yɛɛ ɛ kɔba kefa tom keŋ mɔ nideli.

english_verse: I am not ordering you to do this. I am simply testing how real your love is by comparing it with the concern that others have shown.
translation_verse: Te yɛɛ nyi mɛɛ ŋere ŋon bo ya. Mɔna mɛɛ gyae maa mo Masedoniatena kakyeŋ keŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ lee balaŋ abɔɔ kefa man nɛ, mo maa na ŋon na maa naa mena keŋ ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ balaŋ kegyae doo.

english_verse: You know our Lord Jesus Christ treated us with undeserved grace by giving up all his riches, so you could become rich.
translation_verse: Ɛ gyeŋ mena keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo bɛrɛɛ keŋ doo. Ɛ gyeŋ nyi ɔ yɛɛ gyoorobu e, mɔna lee ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ te ɔ kaa bese diyemte, na ɔ diyem keŋ ke yela ɛmɛɛ ka bese gyoorobia.

english_verse: I think you should finish what you started. If you give according to what you have, you will prove you are as eager to give as you were to think about giving.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ tom keŋ ɛ be fiasɛɛ ɛ ne yɛɛ nɛ, ɛ gyeŋ yɛɛ ke kelii ɔto. Kegyae keŋ ɛ ne gyae nyi ɛ ke fa nɛ, ɛ mo mena kegyae kenaŋ fa kelii ɔto lee ɛ ketalebii man.

english_verse: I am not trying to make life easier for others by making life harder for you. But it is only fair
translation_verse: Na da kegyaebii yɛna nyi baale wose dɛɛ fɛɛ wɔ, na ɛmɛɛ wose kekyaŋ ŋon ya. Mɔna kpene keŋ dɛɛ gyae yɛna nyi, ɛ popou wose dɛɛ fɛɛ ŋon kyɛɛkyɛɛ.

english_verse: for you to share with them when you have so much, and they have so little. Later, when they have more than enough, and you are in need, they can share with you. Then everyone will have a fair share,
translation_verse: Debaŋ kei dɔɔ ɛmɛɛ dana, mena dɔɔ kaboena nyi yaa kyɔ baŋ ba wose be kyaŋ wɔ nɛ. Na wee daale ɛmɛɛ mɔ wose de kyaŋ ŋon na, baŋ mɔ ke nyiŋ kyɔ ŋon. Dekɔŋ mena na, ɛ popou wose gyae ke fɛɛ ŋon kyɛɛkyɛɛ.

english_verse: just as the Scriptures say, “Those who gathered too much had nothing left. Those who gathered only a little had all they needed.” Titus and His Friends
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see yakowɔ lee weenɛɛ ŋan Wurubuarɛ be fa Iseraetena lee adido nɛ wose man nyiaa, “Walaŋ ŋon ɔ be yilaa ŋa burum na, ɔ nɔɔnɔɔle te ɔ nyiŋawɔ. Te ŋon mɔ ɔ be yilaa kyomii nɛ, ŋon mɔ nyiŋa ɔ nɔɔnɔɔle bo.” Pɔɔl kekpila Tituse Korinto donɔɔ man

english_verse: I am grateful that God made Titus care as much about you as we do.
translation_verse: Mɛɛ fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be yeli Tituse ne gyae ɛ nombia, ŋgba mena keŋ maŋ mɔ ne gyae ɛ nombia nɛ.

english_verse: When we begged Titus to visit you, he said he would. He wanted to because he cared so much for you.
translation_verse: Na keŋ dɔɔ waase Tituse dɔɔ te ɔ sɛɛwɔ nyi waa kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ ya. Mɔna ɛ nombia wulaa gyɔɔ e loo, te ɔ wulaa yɛɛ wa agyueŋ nyi waa kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ.

english_verse: With Titus we are also sending one of the Lord's followers who is well known in every church for spreading the good news.
translation_verse: Dee kpila de gyoo Kristobaale ŋolo nyi waa kpu na Tituse na baa kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ. De gyoo ŋonaŋ kɛɛ tɛɛ kolosi Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan dɔɔ, te yeli ɔ te nyiŋ yele Kristotena pou man.

english_verse: The churches chose this follower to travel with us while we carry this gift that will bring praise to the Lord and show how much we hope to help.
translation_verse: Agyakaŋ yɛna nyi, Kristotena baŋ pou lese na e nyi waa kpu na daa na dɛɛ ta lɛɛ kɔbase ŋan dɛɛ lɛɛ nɛ. Mena tom kei de dɔŋɛɛ dɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ, na lese Wurubuarɛ yele te kɛɛ lese de kakyeŋ keŋ daa mɔ na doo na dɛɛ mo kyɔ ŋon nɛ wola.

english_verse: We are also sending someone else with Titus and the other follower. We approve of this man. In fact, he has already shown us many times that he wants to help. And now he wants to help even more than ever, because he trusts you so much.
translation_verse: Dee kpila de gyoo Kristobaale ŋolo ŋon daa na e ne yɛɛ tom nɛ, nyi waa kpu na wɔ na baa kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ. De ta gyoo ɔ man naa nyi, gbɛɛ kamasɛ man na ɔ dana kakyeŋ. Ɔ sia te yɛɛ nyi waa kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ na ɔ kaa kyɔ ŋon, nawolo nyi ɔ te lɛɛ ŋon di nideli.

english_verse: Titus is my partner, who works with me to serve you. The other two followers are sent by the churches, and they bring honor to Christ.
translation_verse: Tewulɛɛ kɛwɔ man na, Tituse yɛna walaŋ ŋon daa na e ne yɛɛ tom dokoloŋ som ŋon. Tewulɛɛ akaŋ baŋ dɔɔ, Kristotena baŋ lese na wɔ nyi be kaa seŋ be nawɔɔ man, na baa lese Kristo yele.

english_verse: Treat them in such a way that the churches will see your love and will know why we bragged about you.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛ lese ɛ balaŋ kegyae keŋ wola wɔ, na ke yela baa gyeŋ kpene keŋ dɔɔ dee gyina da wose adido lee ɛ dɔɔ nɛ, na Kristotena baŋ man balaŋ pou ke nyiŋ naa.

english_verse: I don't need to write you about the money you plan to give for God's people.
translation_verse: Lee toto keŋ ɛ na doo nyi baa mo kyɔ ɛ tebia Kristotena nɛ dɔɔ, nafɔ tekaboena nyi maa ŋmarase ŋon kolo lee ka wose man bela ya.

english_verse: I know how eager you are to give. And I have proudly told the Lord's followers in Macedonia that you people in Achaia have been ready for a whole year. Now your desire to give has made them want to give.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi ma gyeŋ mena keŋ ɛ ne yɛɛ kakyeŋ nyi ɛ ke kyɔ ɛ tebia lee ɛ konɔɔ man. Mena dɔɔ ne yeli mɛɛ kɛlɛɛ ŋon fa Masedoniatena. Mɛɛ yako wɔ nyi leenaŋ dewii te ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ kyaa Akaya tɛɛle man nɛ, desina ɛ wose fa mena toto kei kado. Kakyeŋ keŋ ɛ dana ɛ na do mena toto kei nɛ ta do balaŋ burum mɔ kakyeŋ.

english_verse: This is why I am sending Titus and the two others to you. I want you to be ready, just as I promised. This will prove we were not wrong to brag about you.
translation_verse: Mee kpila tewulɛɛ kɛwɔ ɛ gyaŋ na de kɛɛ kɛlɛɛ ŋon keŋ nɛ be yɛɛ yakaa ya. Nawolo nyi debaŋ kamasɛ na, mɛɛ kɛlɛɛ ŋon fa Kristotena baŋ be kyaa Masedonia nɛ nyi ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ ɛ taŋ desina ɛ wose see.

english_verse: Some followers from Macedonia may come with me, and I want them to find that you have the money ready. If you don't, I would be embarrassed for trusting you to do this. But you would be embarrassed even more.
translation_verse: Akpaa nyi ɛ te desina ɛ wose ya te Masedoniatena baale de sila maŋ kɔŋ na, baa kaa naa nyi me kɛɛ kɛlɛɛ ŋon keŋ nɛ yɛɛ yakaa. Dekɔŋ mena nɛ na daa daageŋ ne gyae la dee di desɛŋ ya, mɔna ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ sia gyae ka yeŋ.

english_verse: So I have decided to ask Titus and the others to spend some time with you before I arrive. This way they can arrange to collect the money you have promised. Then you will have the chance to give because you want to, and not because you feel forced to.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ me kɛɛ ke nyi, kaboena nyi maa kpila tewulɛɛ kɛwɔ na baa kyaŋ gbɛɛ kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ na ɛ ke nyiŋ yilaa abɔɔ ŋan ɛ be yako see nyi ɛ ke fa nɛ see. Kenaŋ na akpaa me kɔŋ na be gyae ke yɛɛ nyi mɛɛ ŋere ŋon bo ya, mɔna ɛmɛɛ gbagba faa la lee ɛ konɔɔ man.

english_verse: Each of you must make up your own mind about how much to give. But don't feel sorry that you must give and don't feel you are forced to give. God loves people who love to give.
translation_verse: Mena keŋ walaŋ kamasɛ be yako see nyi waa fa lee ɔ konɔɔ man nɛ, waa fa mena. Te yɛɛ nyi bɛɛ ŋere e bo ya. Nawolo nyi walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne booli ɔ sia fa kolo lee ɔ konɔɔ man na, ŋon te Wurubuarɛ ne gyae.

english_verse: God can bless you with everything you need, and you will always have more than enough to do all kinds of good things for others.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ waa tale mo ɔ bɛrɛɛ keŋ boele ŋon, na waa yela ɛ ke nyiŋ kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɛ ne gyae debaŋ kamasɛ. Waa yela ɛ ke nyiŋ gbaa, na ɛ ka mo ŋaale yɛɛ deeli.

english_verse: The Scriptures say, “God freely gives his gifts to the poor, and always does right.”
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see yakowɔ yenaŋ daale nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ te gyuusu ɔ nyiŋmaa ɔ ne fa ayematena abɔɔ. Ɔ bɛrɛɛ keŋ ɔ dana nɛ be gyae ke taŋ ya.”

english_verse: God gives seed to farmers and provides everyone with food. He will increase what you have, so you can give even more to those in need.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ ne faa na wɔɔfarale abɔɔ duuluŋ, te ŋon ke ne faa na daa weenɛɛ. Mena dɔɔ waa fa ŋon abɔɔ ŋan ɛ ne gyae nɛ pou na waa yela ŋe ke wuku. Na ɛ kenyiŋ yɛɛ wɔe nideli fa ayematena.

english_verse: You will be blessed in every way, and you will be able to keep on being generous. Then many people will thank God when we deliver your gift.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ waa yela ɛ ke nyiŋ ɛ wose nideli kpene kamasɛ man, na ɛ ke tale yɛɛ wɔe debaŋ kamasɛ. Te mena keŋ ɛ ne tɛɛ de dɔɔ fa balaŋ abɔɔ nɛ, gyae ke yela balaŋ ke fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ.

english_verse: What you are doing is much more than a service that supplies God's people with what they need. It is something that will make many others thank God.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi mena wɔe ya aneyɛɛŋ kei nɛ, te yeli Kristotena burum te nyiŋ kpene keŋ bɛɛ gyae, te bɛɛ lese Wurubuarɛ yele nideli.

english_verse: The way in which you have proved yourselves by this service will bring honor and praise to God. You believed the message about Christ, and you obeyed it by sharing generously with God's people and with everyone else.
translation_verse: Ɛ ta lese ɛ dinɔɔ wola lee ɛ deeli keyɛɛ keŋ man. Ɛ deeli keyɛɛ keŋ gyae kawola nyi na Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan ŋaageŋ te ɛ lɛɛwɔ di ya, mɔna balaŋ baa naa nyi ɛ ne di Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan dɔɔ. Lee mena dɔɔ balaŋ baa lese Wurubuarɛ yele.

english_verse: Now they are praying for you and want to see you, because God used you to bless them so very much.
translation_verse: Baa fane fa ŋon nideli lee be konɔɔ man. Nawolo nyi bɛɛ naa Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ dinaa keŋ doo ɛ man nɛ.

english_verse: Thank God for his gift that is too wonderful for words!
translation_verse: Ɛ yela dɛɛ do Wurubuarɛ nɔɔ, nawolo nyi ɔ bɛrɛɛ keŋ ɔ ba mo boele denibalaŋ nɛ ka dokoso be kyaa ya.

english_verse: Do you think I am a coward when I am with you and brave when I am far away? Well, I ask you to listen, because Christ himself was humble and gentle.
translation_verse: Maŋ Pɔɔl gbagba ne waase na ŋon nombii kei dɔɔ. Ɛ man baale ne yako nyi akpaa me kyaa ɛ gyaŋ na mɔɔ dana nɔɔdoŋ ya. Mɔna akpaa ma lee ɛ gyaŋ na, me nɔɔdoŋ kela kpene kamasɛ. Mɛɛ mo de Gbeŋgyoo Kristo konɔɔ kenyiŋ na ɔ wose keyɔkɔse mɛɛ sola ŋon nyiaa,

english_verse: Some people have said we act like the people of this world. So when I arrive, I expect I will have to be firm and forceful in what I say to them. Please don't make me treat you that way.
translation_verse: akpaa me kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ na ɛ na yeli maa mo sibiiyam kolosi na ŋon, ŋgba mena keŋ maa kolosi na baŋ bɛɛ yako nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ da abɔɔ bo ŋgba tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ nɛ ya.

english_verse: We live in this world, but we don't act like its people
translation_verse: Doo nyi de yɛɛ denibalaŋwɔ, mɔna na tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ adao gbɛɛneŋ te dɛɛ mo yoo ya.

english_verse: or fight our battles with the weapons of this world. Instead, we use God's power that can destroy fortresses. We destroy arguments
translation_verse: De yoo abɔɔ ŋan dɛɛ mo yoo nɛ, te yɛɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ yoo abɔɔ ya. Mɔna ŋe yɛɛ yoo abɔɔ ŋan ŋe doŋ ba lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ. Mena yoo abɔɔ ŋenaŋ, ne tale yɔkɔse ɔbɔnsam kpene kamasɛ keŋ kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ.

english_verse: You judge by appearances. If any of you think you are the only ones who belong to Christ, then think again. We belong to Christ as much as you do.
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ ɛ ne kɛɛ abɔɔ bo adido adido. Akpaa ŋolo de gyɔ ɔ konɔɔ nyi ɔ yɛɛ Kristo walaŋ e na, kaboena nyi mena walaŋ ŋonaŋ waa tɔɔse nyi daa mɔ de yɛɛ Kristo balaŋwɔ ŋgba ŋon nɛ.

english_verse: Maybe I brag a little too much about the authority that the Lord gave me to help you and not to hurt you. Yet I am not embarrassed to brag.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa ma mo doŋ keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ be fa maŋ nɛ gyina ma wose adido na, be gyae ke yɛɛ maŋ desɛŋ ya. Nawolo nyi ɔ moo ɔ doŋ kenaŋ bo fa daa, na ke yela ɛ kelɛɛdi keŋ ka bee. Mɔna te yɛɛ nyi ka ke wɔlɛɛ ŋon bo ya.

english_verse: And I am not trying to scare you with my letters.
translation_verse: Mɔɔ ne gyae ke yɛɛ ŋon ŋgba, mɛɛ mo me tɔna keŋmarase ŋan bo ŋmaase ŋon nɛ ya.

english_verse: Some of you are saying, “Paul's letters are harsh and powerful. But in person, he is a weakling and has nothing worth saying.”
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi balaŋ baale ne yako nyiaa, me tɔna ŋan mɛɛ ŋmarase nɛ dana doŋ, te ŋe man nombia mɔ dana yuŋ. Mɔna akpaa maŋ gbagba kyaa ɛ gyaŋ na, me nɔɔ bɛɛ yɛɛ doŋ ya te ma kekolosi mɔ yɛɛ yakaa.

english_verse: Those people had better understand that when I am with you, I will do exactly what I say in my letters.
translation_verse: Kaboena nyi mena balaŋ benaŋ baa gyeŋ nyiaa, mena keŋ de kyaa lem te dɛɛ ŋmarase tɔna yelee ŋon nɛ, akpaa de kyaa ɛ gyaŋ koraŋ na mena ke te dɛɛ yɛɛ.

english_verse: We won't dare compare ourselves with those who think so much of themselves. But they are foolish to compare themselves with themselves.
translation_verse: Kegbagba na, dɔɔ gyae dɛɛ taŋ mo da wose maa na balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ kɛlɛɛ ba wose nɛ ya. Ɛ kɛɛ be diyiŋ nombia. Bɛɛ mo ba wose maa na dɔŋa, te bɛɛ mo ba wose kɛɛse na dɔŋa.

english_verse: We won't brag about something we don't have a right to brag about. We will only brag about the work God has sent us to do, and you are part of that work.
translation_verse: Daa dɔɔ, dɔɔ gyae dɛɛ gyina da wose adido gbaa mena keŋ kaboena nɛ ya. Kpene keŋ dɔɔ dee gyina da wose yɛna tom keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo fa daa nyi dɛɛ yɛɛ, te dɔɔ yɛɛ ke kaa lii ɛmɛɛ mɔ gyaŋ nɛ.

english_verse: We don't brag about what others have done, as if we had done those things ourselves. But I hope as you become stronger in your faith, we will be able to reach many more of the people around you. That has always been our goal.
translation_verse: Ka te yɛɛ keŋ nyi dɛɛ mo tom keŋ baale be yɛɛ nɛ, gyina da wose adido gbaa mena keŋ kaboena nɛ ya. Mɔna de loo gyakaa bo nyi ɛ ka bee ɛ kelɛɛdi man, na tomse ŋan dɛɛ yɛɛ ɛ man nɛ ke gyu siaman nideli.

english_verse: Then we will be able to preach the good news in other lands where we cannot take credit for work someone else has already done.
translation_verse: Na dee nyiŋ tale gbaa ŋon gyu yenaase wɔlɛɛ mɔ ke yako Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan. Nawolo nyi dɔɔ ne gyae dɛɛ mo tom keŋ ŋolo be yɛɛ nɛ lɛɛ yele ya.

english_verse: But now I fear that you will be tricked, just as Eve was tricked by that lying snake. I am afraid that you might stop thinking about Christ in an honest and sincere way.
translation_verse: Mɔna me wɔe ne lɔ maŋ nyi, ŋolo waa beo ŋon na ɛ ka lese ya agyueŋ lee Kristo ɔsom keŋ dɔɔ, ŋgba mena keŋ dom ŋon ba mo adao gbɛɛneŋ man beo Efa gyaŋgba gyaŋgba keŋ nɛ.

english_verse: We told you about Jesus, and you received the Holy Spirit and accepted our message. But you let some people tell you about another Jesus. Now you are ready to receive another spirit and accept a different message.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi akpaa ŋolo de kɔŋ kaa kolosi Yesu wɔle ŋolo wose man nombia fa ŋon, lee Yesu ŋon daa be kolosi ɔ wose man nombia fa ŋon nɛ wɔle na, ɛ ne nyiŋ konɔɔ lɛɛ ɔtenate nombia do. Te ɛ bɛɛ gataa na feliŋ wɔlɛɛ mɔ kelɛɛdi kpu na Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ɛ be wulaa nyiŋ, na kawola wɔlɛɛ na nyeedoŋ nombia ŋan ɛ be wulaa nyii nɛ ya.

english_verse: I think I am as good as any of those super apostles.
translation_verse: Mɔɔ ne lɛɛ di mɔ nyi mena balaŋ baŋ be kpa baŋ yɛna Kristo kpilala gbagba nɛ, be man ŋolo kela maŋ ya.

english_verse: I may not speak as well as they do, but I know as much. And this has already been made perfectly clear to you.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa mɔɔ gyeŋ kekolosi ya nɛ, keŋ bɛɛ wolo nyi mɔɔ gyeŋ nombia bo ya. De ta lese mena nombia kɛŋa pou man wola ŋon lee gbɛɛ kamasɛ man.

english_verse: Was it wrong for me to lower myself and honor you by preaching God's message free of charge?
translation_verse: Mena keŋ mɔɔ yɔkɔse ma wose kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan fa ŋon lese ŋon yele, te ma te lɛɛ ŋon kolo ya nɛ, me yɛɛ dukum baa?

english_verse: Even when I was in need, I still didn't bother you. In fact, some of the Lord's followers from Macedonia brought me what I needed. I have not been a burden to you in the past, and I will never be a burden.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi debaŋ keŋ me kyaa ɛ gyaŋ nɛ, ma wose kyaŋa maŋ na, me ta mo kaale kenaŋ lɔ ɛ dɔɔ ya. Mɔna de tebia Kristotena baŋ bɔɔ lee Masedonia nɛ, kaa faa maŋ abɔɔ ŋan pou mɛɛ gyae nɛ. Mena dɔɔ me kyaŋa ma wose, te me gyakaa mɛɛ kyaŋ ma wose nyi, mɔɔ bese kasolɔ ɛ dɔɔ lee gbɛɛ kamasɛ maŋ ya.

english_verse: As surely as I speak the truth about Christ, no one in Achaia can stop me from bragging about this.
translation_verse: Nombii na anokoare yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon lee Kristo man nyiaa, ŋolo be kyaa Akaya tɛɛle kei dɔɔ na waa tale tɔ maŋ gbɛɛ nyi me na gyina ma wose adido lee nombii kei dɔɔ ya.

english_verse: And it isn't because I don't love you. God himself knows how much I do love you.
translation_verse: Woŋ dɔɔ te mɛɛ yako mena nombia kɛŋa? Nawolo baa nyi mɔɔ ne gyae ŋon yee? Wurubuarɛ gyeŋ nyi mɛɛ gyae ŋon.

english_verse: I plan to go on doing just what I have always done. Then those people won't be able to brag about doing the same things we are doing.
translation_verse: Mena keŋ mɔɔ wulaa bɛɛ nyi mɔɔ lɛɛ kolo lee ɛ gyaŋ ya nɛ, mena yaa me sɛɛ ma seŋɛɛ. Maa yɛɛ mena na maa nyiŋ mo tɔ baŋ bee gyina ba wose adido nɛ gbɛɛ. Na bɔɔ nyiŋ gbɛɛ wola ba wose nyi daa na wɔ ne yɛɛ na tom dokoloŋ ya.

english_verse: And it is no wonder. Even Satan tries to make himself look like an angel of light.
translation_verse: Be yɛɛ mena na be dana gyakoloŋ ya, nawolo nyi ɔbɔnsam gbagba koraŋ moo ɔ dinɔɔ gbagba weese, te ɔ moo ɔ wose yɛɛ ŋgba Wurubuarɛ kpilale kpaakpaate ŋolo nɛ.

english_verse: I don't want any of you to think I am a fool. But if you do, then let me be a fool and brag a little.
translation_verse: Mɛɛ yako ŋon baa bela nyiaa, ŋolo na yɛɛ wa agyueŋ nyi me yɛɛ diyiŋte e ya. Mɔna akpaa ɛ yɛɛ ya agyueŋ mena na, kenaŋ na ɛ lɛɛ maŋ ŋgba diyiŋte nɛ, na maa nyiŋ tale wola ma wose kyomii.

english_verse: When I do all this bragging, I do it as a fool and not for the Lord.
translation_verse: Nombii na anokoare na, mena wose kawola kei te yɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo kegyaebii man yaa mee kolosi ya. Mɔna mee kolosi baa ŋgba diyiŋte nɛ.

english_verse: Yet if others want to brag about what they have done, so will I.
translation_verse: Mena keŋ balaŋ burum na wola ba wose ŋgba mena keŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ ne yɛɛ nɛ dɔɔ, maŋ mɔ maa wola ma wose mena.

english_verse: And since you are so smart, you will gladly put up with a fool.
translation_verse: Ɛ kpa ɛ yɛɛ siakaralabiawɔ, mɔna ɛ ne nyiŋ konɔɔ kyaa tei ayiŋtena.

english_verse: In fact, you let people make slaves of you and cheat you and steal from you. Why, you even let them strut around and slap you in the face.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa ŋolo da besena ŋon yɛɛ ɔ kegbetibii, yaa ɔ na ŋere ŋon do, yaa ɔ ne kɔ ɛ nyeeya man di, yaa ɔ ne di gyoori ɛ dɔɔ, yaa ɔ bɛɛ kala ŋon do balaŋ man koraŋ ya na, ɛ ne nyiŋ konɔɔ lɛɛ ɔtenate do.

english_verse: I am ashamed to say we are too weak to behave in such a way. If they can brag, so can I, but it is a foolish thing to do.
translation_verse: Ɛ wui dei ŋgba de ta gyem na dɛɛ gyɔ de konɔɔ mo mena nombia kɛŋa yɛɛ ŋon ya nɛ. Mɔna kpene kamasɛ keŋ man ŋolo de gyɔ ɔ konɔɔ ɔ na wola ɔ wose na, maŋ mɔ maa tale gyɔ me konɔɔ wola ma wose kpene kenaŋ dɔɔ mena. Mee kolosi baa ŋgba diyiŋte nɛ.

english_verse: Are they servants of Christ? I am a fool to talk this way, but I serve him better than they do. I have worked harder and have been put in jail more times. I have been beaten with whips more and have been in danger of death more often.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa be kpa be yɛɛ Kristo tewulɛɛwɔ na, maŋ yɛɛ Kristo tobaale kpaakpaate e kela wɔ. Nawolo nyi me yɛɛ tom na doŋ kela wɔ, te me naa diyem nideli mɔ kela wɔ. Be kyaŋa maŋ tɔ deni agbaatee agbaatee, te ba ŋmeree maŋ dambiise nideli, te be naase maŋ diyem yeŋ yeŋ wee na wee. Ɛ ke yako nyi mee kolosi baa ŋgba walaŋ ŋon ɔ nyee be doo ya nɛ, mɔna nombii na anokoare yaa mɛɛ yako.

english_verse: Five times my own people gave me
translation_verse: Gyudatena ŋmeree maŋ dambiise saonakufukadaale (

english_verse: lashes with a whip.
translation_verse: ) betabonoŋ.

english_verse: Three times the Romans beat me with a big stick, and once my enemies stoned me. I have been shipwrecked three times, and I even had to spend a night and a day in the sea.
translation_verse: Ba moo nyeela dambiise tuku maŋ betabotooro. Wee daale na, be fuŋii maŋ boya yeŋ yeŋ. Me kyaawɔ agbele dinaana man betabotooro keŋ ŋɛɛ buŋ maŋ, te ɛpo yela kedi maŋ. Te me kyaawɔ loŋ dɔɔ wee dibiliŋ nelim na ka weese.

english_verse: During my many travels, I have been in danger from rivers, robbers, my own people, and foreigners. My life has been in danger in cities, in deserts, at sea, and with people who only pretended to be the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Me tɛɛ gbɛɛneŋ burum, te me naa diyem yeŋ yeŋ lee booneŋ katoŋ, na balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ ŋere balaŋ lɛo ba abɔɔ dekpaŋkpasare man nɛ nyiŋmaa man. Me dɛɛ Gyudatena, na baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ, naase maŋ diyem yeŋ yeŋ. Me naa diyem yeŋ yeŋ donɔɔ dinaana man, na ŋalese man na ɛpo dɔɔ, te me naa diyem balaŋ baŋ bee dii akɛrɛɛ nyi be yɛɛ Kristotenawɔ nɛ nyiŋmaa man.

english_verse: I have worked and struggled and spent many sleepless nights. I have gone hungry and thirsty and often had nothing to eat. I have been cold from not having enough clothes to keep me warm.
translation_verse: Me yɛɛ tom nideli keŋ te yɛɛ nsendee ya, te me naa diyem mɔ nideli. Me tɛɛwɔ weeya burum keŋ mɔɔ ne nyiŋ dosoroŋ gba maa doo ya, te me tɛɛwɔ na tanam na loŋboaneŋ. Me tɛɛwɔ weeya ŋaale man keŋ mɔɔ ne nyiŋ kolo na kolo gba tina me nɔɔ korakora ya. Me tɛɛwɔ na waare keŋ me te nyiŋ kolo kpaakpaate wuu ma wose ya.

english_verse: Besides everything else, each day I am burdened down, worrying about all the churches.
translation_verse: Nombia kɛŋa pou wɔle na, Kristotena akpiise ŋan pou nombia ne takaa na maŋ wee kamasɛ.

english_verse: When others are weak, I am weak too. When others are tricked into sin, I get angry.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi, nyi ŋolo kelɛɛdi de yɔkɔse na, maŋ mɔ wose te yɔkɔse maŋ. Nyi ŋolo mɔ de yɛɛ dukum na, me tɔɔ loobia te kyoŋii maŋ fa ɔtenate.

english_verse: If I have to brag, I will brag about how weak I am.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa kaboena nyi maa wola ma wose na, maa wola ma wose bo lee abɔɔ ŋan na lese ma keyɔkɔse wola nɛ bo dɔɔ.

english_verse: God, the Father of our Lord Jesus, knows I am not lying. And God is to be praised forever!
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Wurubuarɛ na ɔ kya e, te ɔ kaboena nyi dɛɛ lese ɔ yele kekpaakekpaa nɛ, ɔ gyeŋ nyi na dekara yaa mee dii ya.

english_verse: The governor of Damascus at the time of King Aretas had the city gates guarded, so he could capture me.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ me kyaa Damasekuse donɔɔ man nɛ, walaŋ ŋon ɔ seŋɛɛ gyoo Aretase nawɔɔ man ɔ ne kɛɛ tɛɛle daale dɔɔ nɛ lese balaŋ nyiaa, baa deke Damasekuse donɔɔ keŋ anaanɔɔse ŋan pou na baa nyiŋ kyaŋ maŋ.

english_verse: But I escaped by being let down in a basket through a window in the city wall.
translation_verse: Mɔna ba moo maŋ do dokyoŋoloŋ man, te be tisinaa maŋ ta na nfansere te me nyiŋa gbɛɛ yeu lee tɛɛle dɔɔ kɛɛre ŋon nyiŋmaa man.

english_verse: I have to brag. There is nothing to be gained by it, but I must brag about the visions and other things that the Lord has shown me.
translation_verse: Kaboena nyi maa wola ma wose, mɔna ma gyeŋ nyi ma wose kawola kei be dana tɔnɔɔ ya. Ɛ yela maa yako ŋon nombia ŋan de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo ba lese wola maŋ, na ŋan mɔɔ naa ŋa na me sia fonɛɛ ŋgba dosɛɛ man nɛ.

english_verse: I am planning to visit you for the third time. But I still won't make a burden of myself. What I really want is you, and not what you have. Children are not supposed to save up for their parents, but parents are supposed to take care of their children.
translation_verse: Ma te desina ma wose nyi maa bese kaa ta ɛ dɔɔ betabotoorote. Mɔɔ ne gyae nyi me kaa mo ma wose lɔ ɛ dɔɔ ya, nawolo nyi na ya abɔɔ ŋan ɛ dana nɛ te mɛɛ gyae ya, mɔna ɛmɛɛ gbagba te mɛɛ gyae. Kegbagba na, na bia ne gyae na abɔɔ see ba lolala ya, mɔna lolala ne gyae na abɔɔ see be bia.

english_verse: But he was taken up into paradise, where he heard things too wonderful to tell.
translation_verse: Nombia ŋan walaŋ ŋonaŋ be nyii botɔɔ nɛ ŋe taŋ keyako ya, te walaŋ mɔ be tale mo ɔ nɔɔ yako ŋa gba ya.

english_verse: Yet even if I did brag, I would not be foolish. I would simply be speaking the truth. But I will try not to say too much. That way, none of you will think more highly of me than you should because of what you have seen me do and say.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa ma wola ma wose na te yɛɛ diyiŋ nombia ŋa ya, nawolo nyi nombia ŋan mɔɔ yako ŋon nɛ yɛɛ ampaŋ. Mɔna mɔɔ gyae maa wola ma wose ya. Nawolo nyi mɔɔ ne gyae nyi ŋolo ka mo maŋ maa kela mena keŋ kaboena nɛ, lee abɔɔ ŋan mɛɛ yɛɛ yaa nombia ŋan mee kolosi fa ŋon nɛ dɔɔ ya.

english_verse: Three times I begged the Lord to make this suffering go away.
translation_verse: Me waase de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu betabotooro nyi, waa lese mena woseyooreŋ kenaŋ lee ma wose man.

english_verse: But he replied, “My gift of undeserved grace is all you need. My power is strongest when you are weak.” So if Christ keeps giving me his power, I will gladly brag about how weak I am.
translation_verse: Mɔna de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu bɛe te ɔ yako maŋ nyiaa, “Yela me bɛrɛɛ keŋ mɔɔ mo boele neŋ nɛ ke kaboena neŋ. Nawolo nyi n wose keyɔkɔse man te nyaŋ kena me doŋ.” Mena dɔɔ dana maŋ gyoŋ nyi mɛɛ wola ma wose bo lee ma wose keyɔkɔse man, na Kristo doŋ keŋ kenyiŋ doo me man.

english_verse: Yes, I am glad to be weak or insulted or mistreated or to have troubles and sufferings, if it is for Christ. Because when I am weak, I am strong. Paul's Concern for the Lord's Followers at Corinth
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ lee Kristo dɔɔ nɛ, wose keyɔkɔse man bo o tuubia man bo o, diyem kena man yaa diyem kenaase man yaa kaale man bo koraŋ na, ma wose ne fɛɛ maŋ. Nawolo nyi ma wose keyɔkɔse man te mɛɛ naa Kristo doŋ. Pɔɔl kayeyɛɛ lee Korintotena dɔɔ

english_verse: I have been making a fool of myself. But you forced me to do it, when you should have been speaking up for me. I may be nothing at all, but I am as good as those super apostles.
translation_verse: Me ta bese diyiŋte, mɔna ɛmɛɛ yele la te ma bese diyiŋte. Ɛmɛɛ te kaboena nyi nafɔ yaa kɛlɛɛ maŋ. Nawolo nyi ma te yɛɛ kolo e ma wose dɔɔ koraŋ ya na, mɔɔ doo balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ baake ba wose nyi be yɛɛ Kristo kpilalawɔ nɛ tɛɛ lee gbɛɛ kamasɛ man ya.

english_verse: So I will gladly give all I have and all I am. Will you love me less for loving you too much?
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ dana maŋ gyoŋ nyi maa mo kpene kamasɛ keŋ me dana nɛ, kpu na me dibiliŋ wɔlɛɛ ɛ dɔɔ. Nyi akpaa mɛɛ gyae ɛ nombia nideli na, ɛmɛɛ be gyae maŋ mena yee?

english_verse: You agree that I wasn't a burden to you. Maybe that's because I was trying to catch you off guard and trick you.
translation_verse: Ma gyeŋ nyi ɛ ke sɛɛ nyiaa, ayi me ta mo kasolɔ kaa ŋere ŋon ya. Mɔna ɛ man baale ne yako nyiaa me dana nyansa, mena dɔɔ te ma moo adao gbɛɛneŋ nyiŋ ŋon.

english_verse: Were you cheated by any of those I sent to you?
translation_verse: Ma moo balaŋ baŋ mɔɔ kpili ɛ gyaŋ nɛ dɔɔ di ɛ nyeeya man?

english_verse: I urged Titus to visit you, and I sent another follower with him. But Titus didn't cheat you, and we felt and behaved the same way he did.
translation_verse: Me waase Tituse nyi waa kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ te me kpila Kristobaale ŋolo mɔ kpu na e. Tituse kaa lɛɛ ɛ nyeeman kolo bo di yaa? Na agyueŋ dokoloŋ te daa na e dana yee? Yaa na gbɛɛ dokoloŋ dɔɔ te daa na e dɔŋ yee?

english_verse: Have you been thinking all along that we have been defending ourselves to you? Actually, we have been speaking to God as followers of Christ. But, my friends, we did it all for your good.
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ gyeŋ nyi da seŋɛɛ baa dee tira de nombia kɛŋa nɔɔ na dee di deŋele? Koa te yɛɛ mena ya. Wurubuarɛ ne naa daa nyi Kristo kegyaebii man yaa dee kolosi nɛ. Me tebia kpaakpaa, lee ɛ deeli dɔɔ te de kolosi nombia kɛŋa pou mena nɛ.

english_verse: I am afraid when I come, we won't be pleased with each other. I fear that some of you may be arguing or jealous or angry or selfish or gossiping or insulting each other. I even fear that you may be proud and acting like a mob.
translation_verse: Me wɔe ne lɔ maŋ nyi akpaa me kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ na, mɔɔ gyae maa naa ŋon nyi ɛ kyaa ŋgba mena keŋ mɛɛ gyae nɛ ya, te ɛmɛɛ mɔ be gyae ɛ kena me dinɔɔ ŋgba mena keŋ ɛ ne gyae nyi ɛ kena ke nɛ ya. Me wɔe ne lɔ maŋ nyi mberem na kale nombia, na sibii na baŋ na yoo nombia na balaŋ kewɔlɛɛ, na wose kawola na nombia nkaraŋkaraŋ keyɛɛ gyae ke doo ɛ man.

english_verse: I am afraid God will make me ashamed when I visit you again. I will feel like crying because many of you have never given up your old sins. You are still doing things that are immoral, indecent, and shameful.
translation_verse: Me wɔe ne lɔ maŋ nyi akpaa me kɔŋ bela na, Wurubuarɛ waa yɔkɔse maŋ ɛ siaman na me tɔɔ ke kyoŋii maŋ lee ɛ man baale nombiakumɛɛ ŋan bɛɛ yɛɛ, te bɔɔ ne gyae baa kyɛɛkee lee ŋe man ya nɛ dɔɔ. Me wɔe ne lɔ maŋ nyi be sɛɛ bɛɛ yɛɛ kafoŋ na nombia nkaraŋkaraŋ, te bɔɔ ne gyae baa kyɛɛkee lee be nombiakumɛɛ ŋenaŋ man kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ ya.

english_verse: I am on my way to visit you for the third time. And as the Scriptures say, “Any charges must be proved true by at least two or three witnesses.”
translation_verse: Kei yɛna betabotoorote ŋgba mɛɛ kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ nɛ. Mɛɛ gyae ɛ ke tɔɔse nombii keŋ doo Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see yenaŋ daale nɛ nyiaa, “Pɛna ɛ ke bu ŋolo fɔɔ na, kaboena nyi yaa nyii lee balaŋ bala, yaa balaŋ batooro nɔɔman nyi nombii keŋ yɛɛ ampaŋ.”

english_verse: During my second visit I warned you that I would punish you and anyone else who doesn't stop sinning. I am far away from you now, but I give you the same warning.
translation_verse: Ma kekɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ bolate keŋ nɛ, me wulaa faree balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛ kaalaŋ nɛ, na balaŋ akaŋ baŋ pou. Debaŋ kei keŋ mɔɔ kyaa ɛ gyaŋ ya nɛ, mena yaa me gyakaa mɛɛ faree ŋon nyiaa, akpaa ma bese kɔŋ na, mɔɔ gyae maa mo baŋ be sɛɛ bɛɛ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛ nɛ wose kyɛɛ wɔ ya.

english_verse: This should prove to you that I am speaking for Christ. When he corrects you, he won't be weak. He will be powerful!
translation_verse: Ɛmɛɛ kpa ɛ ne gyae ɛ kena nyi Kristo ne tɛɛ la me dɔɔ kolosi nɛ, ɛ kena ke. Ɛ kena nyi Kristo ba woɛ keŋ ɔ be gyae waa wɔŋ ɛ deŋele ya. Ɔ dana doŋ ɛ dɔɔ.

english_verse: Although he was weak when he was nailed to the cross, he now lives by the power of God. We are weak, just as Christ was. But you will see that we will live by the power of God, just as Christ does.
translation_verse: Ke yɛɛ ampaŋ nyi be gyɔɔ e mataa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ, ŋgba walaŋ ŋon ɔ be taŋ doŋ ya nɛ. Mɔna debokeiman dɔɔ, ɔ te fom lee yeŋ man te ɔ kyaa Wurubuarɛ doŋ man. Mena mɔ te daa mɔ be taŋ doŋ ya. Mɔna keŋ de doo Kristo man dɔɔ, Wurubuarɛ doŋ doo de man te de dana dɛɛ som ŋon.

english_verse: Test yourselves and find out if you really are true to your faith. If you pass the test, you will discover that Christ is living in you. But if Christ isn't living in you, you have failed.
translation_verse: Ɛ gyoo ɛ wose man nideli na ɛ ke kɛɛ nyi ɛ ne lɛɛ Yesu di nideli? Yaa ɛ ba gyeŋ nyi ɛmɛɛ na Yesu Kristo yɛɛ dokoloŋ yee? Akpaa ɛ ba gyeŋ mena ya na, kenaŋ na ɛ te yale ɛ kelɛɛdi man.

english_verse: I hope you will discover we have not failed.
translation_verse: Mɛɛ lɛɛ di nyi ɛ ka gyeŋ nyi, daa dɔɔ da te yala ta da kelɛɛdi man ya.

english_verse: We pray you will stop doing evil things. We don't pray like this to make ourselves look good, but to get you to do right, even if we are failures.
translation_verse: Dɛɛ fane fa Wurubuarɛ nyiaa, ɔ na yeli ɛ ke yɛɛ kolo kum ya. Te yɛɛ keŋ nyi balaŋ ke kɛlɛɛ daa nyi da te yɛɛ daale dɔɔ ya, mɔna akpaa dekɔŋ nyi da te yale da kelɛɛdi man koraŋ na, dɛɛ gyae bo nyi ɛ ke yɛɛ nombia ŋan tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ.

english_verse: All we can do is to follow the truth and not fight against it.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi dɔɔ gyae dɛɛ tale lee nombii na anokoare keŋ wɔle ya. Ke man te da seŋɛɛ debaŋ kamasɛ.

english_verse: Even though we are weak, we are glad that you are strong, and we pray you will do even better.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa daa de yɔkɔse te ɛmɛɛ seŋɛɛ keŋkeŋ ɛ kelɛɛdi man na, dana daa gyoŋ. Nawolo nyi dɛɛ fane bo nyi yaa tale seŋ keŋkeŋ ɛ kelɛɛdi man Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii man.

english_verse: I am writing these things to you before I arrive. This way I won't have to be hard on you when I use the authority the Lord has given me. I was given this authority, so I could help you and not destroy you.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ te de kyaa lem na lem te mɛɛ ŋmarase tɔne kei mee yelee ŋon nɛ. Kenaŋ na akpaa me kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ na, mɔɔ gyae maa mo doŋ keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu be fa maŋ nɛ kolosi na ŋon akpanyaŋnyaŋ man ya. Nawolo nyi de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ faa maŋ doŋ kei bo, nyi maa mo kyɔ ŋon na ɛ ka bee ɛ kelɛɛdi man. Ɔ te fa maŋ ke bo nyi maa mo wɔlɛɛ ŋon ya.

english_verse: Goodbye, my friends. Do better and pay attention to what I have said. Try to get along and live peacefully with each other. Now I pray that God, who gives love and peace, will be with you.
translation_verse: Me tebia, me nɔɔwoya ɔto yɛna nyiaa, ɛ kyaa nideli. Ɛ yase ɛ sia na ɛ ke kyaa nideli kelii ɔto, na ɛ ke tei nombia ŋan mɔɔ yako ŋon nɛ. Ɛ yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ na ɛ ke kyaa wosefɛɛreŋ man. Na Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ ne gyae balaŋ te ɔ yɛɛ wosefɛɛreŋ Gbeŋgyoo e nɛ ke kyaa na ŋon.

english_verse: Give each other a warm greeting. All God's people send their greetings.
translation_verse: Ɛ mo Kristo balaŋ kegyae keŋ do dɔŋa nɔɔ lee ɛ konɔɔ man.

english_verse: From the apostle Paul and from all the Lord's followers with me. I was chosen to be an apostle by Jesus Christ and by God the Father, who raised him from death. No mere human chose or appointed me to this work. To the churches in Galatia:
translation_verse: Mɛɛ ŋmarase ke mee yelee ɛmɛɛ Kristotena akpii akpii ŋan pou ɛ kyaa Galatia tɛɛle man nɛ. Daa na me tebia Kristotena baŋ pou be kyaa me gyaŋ kɛbo nɛ na do ŋon nɔɔ.

english_verse: I pray that God the Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
translation_verse: De kya Wurubuarɛ na de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu Kristo baa mo be bɛrɛɛ boele ŋon, na baa fa ŋon wosefɛɛreŋ.

english_verse: Christ obeyed God our Father and gave himself as a sacrifice for our sins to rescue us from this evil world.
translation_verse: De nombiakumɛɛ dɔɔ te Yesu Kristo moo ɔ wose fa yeŋ, na waa lese daa lee gyɛŋ duuluŋ kei nombiakumɛɛ man. Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ de kya e nɛ, kegyaebii man te nombia kɛŋa pou kɔŋawɔ mena.

english_verse: God will be given glory forever and ever. Amen. The Only True Message
translation_verse: Ŋon Wurubuarɛ te kaboena nyi dɛɛ lese ɔ yele kekpaakekpaa. Amɛn. Nombii wɔle daale be kyaa Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan wɔle ya

english_verse: when there is really only one true message. But some people are causing you trouble and want to make you turn away from the good news about Christ.
translation_verse: N kɛɛ gbagba na, mena kawola kenaŋ ta lee Kristo gyaŋ korakora ya. Mɔna balaŋ baale ne wɔlɛɛ na ya agyueŋ. Bɛɛ gyae bo nyi baa tulii Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan.

english_verse: I pray that God will punish anyone who preaches anything different from our message to you! It doesn't matter if that person is one of us or an angel from heaven.
translation_verse: Mɔna akpaa nyi daa gbagba, yaa Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋolo koraŋ da lee adido kɔŋ kaa kolosi nombia wɔlɛɛ ŋaale kpoo fa ŋon lee Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan daa be wulaa kolosi fa ŋon nɛ wɔle na, ɔtenate dɛɛ laako. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ waa wɔlɛɛ e kekpaakekpaa.

english_verse: I have said it before, and I will say it again. I hope God will punish anyone who preaches anything different from what you have already believed.
translation_verse: De te wulaa yako ŋon mena nombia kɛŋa. Mɔna ma bese yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon bela nyiaa, akpaa ŋolo de kɔŋ kaa yako ŋon nombia wɔlɛɛ ŋaale bela lee Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan daa be yako ŋon nɛ wɔle na, Wurubuarɛ waa wɔlɛɛ ɔtenate kekpaakekpaa.

english_verse: I am not trying to please people. I want to please God. Do you think I am trying to please people? If I were doing that, I would not be a servant of Christ. How Paul Became an Apostle
translation_verse: Me kɛɛ yako ŋon nombia kɛŋa na, nawolo baa nyi mɛɛ gyae nyi denibalaŋ ke kɛlɛɛ maŋ yaa? Koa, na mena ya. Yaa mɛɛ gyae baa nyi maa yɛɛ denibalaŋ kegyaebii? Nyi akpaa me sɛɛ mɛɛ gyae bo nyi denibalaŋ ke kɛlɛɛ maŋ na, kenaŋ na nafɔ ma te yɛɛ Kristo tobaale e ya. Mena keŋ Pɔɔl ba bese Kristo kpilale

english_verse: My friends, I want you to know that no one made up the message I preach.
translation_verse: Me tebia mɛɛ gyae ɛ ka gyeŋ nyiaa, Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan mɔɔ kolosi fa ŋon nɛ te yɛɛ nombia ŋan denibalaŋ be kyaa bake see ya.

english_verse: It wasn't given or taught to me by some mere human. My message came directly from Jesus Christ when he appeared to me.
translation_verse: Na deniwalaŋ ŋolo moona mena nombia kɛŋa do maŋ me nyiŋmaa man ya, te na deniwalaŋ ŋolo mɔ wola na maŋ mena nombia kɛŋa ya. Mɔna Yesu Kristo lese na mena nombia kɛŋa wola maŋ.

english_verse: I was a much better Jew than anyone else my own age, and I obeyed every law our ancestors had given us.
translation_verse: Me yase me sia kasee Gyudatena ɔsom mmaraase ŋan nideli gyu siaman, kela me tebia Gyudatena baŋ daa na wɔ yɛɛ akoso nɛ pou. Ma moo ma nyee pou do de naanaɔ beneŋ nombia ŋan keyɛɛ man.

english_verse: I didn't say a word, not even to the men in Jerusalem who were apostles before I was. Instead, I went at once to Arabia, and afterwards I returned to Damascus.
translation_verse: Mena ke mɔ te me te gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nyi me kaa naa Kristo kpilala gyaŋgba nyibaŋ baŋ nyi baa fa maŋ agyueŋ lee nombia kɛŋa man ya. Mɔna ma korowɔ karatetee dokoloŋ gyu Arebia tɛɛle man, pɛte ma besewɔ kɔŋ Damasekuse donɔɔ man.

english_verse: Three years later I went to visit Peter in Jerusalem and stayed with him for
translation_verse: Kulutooneŋse atooro wɔle nɛ, me gyuu Pita gyaŋ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man, te me kyaawɔ ɔ gyaŋ weeya kufu anoŋ.

english_verse: The only other apostle I saw was James, the Lord's brother.
translation_verse: Me ke gyu botɔɔ nɛ, ma te naa Kristo kpilala baŋ man ŋolo ya gyisɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu ɔ naabu Gyemisi waageŋ.

english_verse: And in the presence of God I swear I am telling the truth.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ gbagba gyeŋ nyi nombia ŋan mɛɛ ŋmarase nɛ yɛɛ ampaŋ.

english_verse: Later, I went to the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle na te me gyuu Siria na Silisia tɛɛlese dɔɔ.

english_verse: But no one who belonged to Christ's churches in Judea had ever seen me in person.
translation_verse: Mena debaŋ kenaŋ na, Kristotena akpii akpii ŋan pou be kyaa Gyudia tɛɛle dɔɔ nɛ ba gyeŋ maŋ gbagba ya.

english_verse: They had only heard that the one who had been cruel to them was now preaching the message that he had once tried to destroy.
translation_verse: Kpene keŋ bee nyii yɛna nyiaa, “Balee ŋon gyaŋgba ɔ ne naase daa diyem nɛ, nɛnɛɛ ŋon dɔŋɛɛ la ɔ ne kolosi Kristo kelɛɛdi keŋ nafɔ ɔ be gyae gbɛɛ nyi waa wɔlɛɛ ke nɛ wose man nombia nɛ.”

english_verse: Fourteen years later I went to Jerusalem with Barnabas. I also took along Titus.
translation_verse: Kulutooneŋ kufu anaara wɔle nɛ, ma besewɔ gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man. Me ke gyu kei na, daa na Banabase na Tituse gyuu la.

english_verse: But I went there because God had told me to go, and I explained the good news I had been preaching to the Gentiles. Then I met privately with the ones who seemed to be the most important leaders. I wanted to make sure my work in the past and my future work would not be for nothing.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ lese la wola maŋ nyi maa gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man botɔɔ mena dɔɔ te me gyuuwɔ. De ke gyu botɔɔ na, dɛɛ kyaa gyaŋee na Kristotena kegyiise baŋ baageŋ bo. De gyaŋee na, mɛɛ lese Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan me dɔŋ mee kolosi fa balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ man wola wɔ, na daa na wɔ ke yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ. Nawolo nyi ma te gyae nyi tom keŋ mɔɔ yɛɛ kaalaŋ, na keŋ me gyakaa mɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ ke yɛɛ yakaa ya.

english_verse: We went there because of those who pretended to be followers and had sneaked in among us as spies. They had come to take away the freedom Christ Jesus had given us, and they were trying to make us their slaves.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baale beo la mo ba wose nyi be yɛɛ Kristotenawɔ te be kaa kpuuwɔ de man. Baŋ gyae la nyi nafɔ baa kara e ɔ baala wose. Mena balaŋ benaŋ wɛɛlee la kaa gyoo de man, te bɛɛ gyae nyi baa wɔlɛɛ da wosekenyiŋ keŋ dɔɔ nyim lee de Gbeŋgyoo Kristo man nɛ. Bɛɛ gyae nyi dɛɛ bese gyu ke doo Gyudatena mmaraase ŋan tɛɛ na dee di ŋe dɔɔ, na baa mo daa besena ba mmaraase ŋan gbeŋa.

english_verse: But we wanted you to have the true message. This is why we didn't give in to them, not even for a second.
translation_verse: Mɔna da te fa wɔ gbɛɛ korakora ya. Nawolo nyi dɛɛ gyae nyi ɛ ke kyaŋ Kristo anokoare nombia ŋan dɔɔ wola ŋon nɛ man nideli.

english_verse: They realized God had sent me with the good news for Gentiles, and he had sent Peter with the same message for Jews.
translation_verse: Mɔna mena balaŋ kɛwɔ naawɔ nyi Wurubuarɛ lese na maŋ nyi maa kolosi ɔ nombia kpaakpaa ŋan fa balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ, ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ ba lese Pita nyi waa kolosi nombia kpaakpaa ŋenaŋ fa Gyudatena nɛ.

english_verse: James, Peter, and John realized that God had given me the message about his gift of undeserved grace. And these men are supposed to be the backbone of the church. They even gave Barnabas and me a friendly handshake. This was to show that we would work with Gentiles and that they would work with Jews.
translation_verse: Gyemisi na Pita na Gyɔn baŋ balaŋ ne kɛɛ wɔ nyi be yɛɛ kegyiisewɔ Kristo ɔsom keŋ man kena nyi Wurubuarɛ ta mo ɔ bɛrɛɛ keŋ boele daa nɛ, be gbaa daa do daa nɔɔ na ke yela walaŋ kamasɛ kena nyi daa na wɔ pou yɛɛ dokoloŋ tom keŋ dɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ man. Mena dɔɔ daa na wɔ yakowɔ sɛɛ dɔŋa nyiaa, daa na Banabase dee kolosi Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan fa balaŋ baŋ ba te Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ, na baŋ mɔ kekolosi ŋa fa Gyudatena.

english_verse: They only asked us to remember the poor, and this was something I had always been eager to do. Paul Corrects Peter at Antioch
translation_verse: Nombii dokoloŋ keŋ bɔɔ yako daa nyi dɛɛ kyaŋ ke keŋkeŋ yɛna nyi, de na wolee ayematena dɔɔ ya. Na mena nombii kei taŋ doo la me konɔɔ man. Pɔɔl kakoro seŋ tia Pita

english_verse: When Peter came to Antioch, I told him face to face that he was wrong.
translation_verse: Pita ke kɔŋ de gyaŋ Antiɔke donɔɔ man nɛ, ma korowɔ seŋ tia e de tebia Kristotena baŋ siaman. Nawolo nyi kpene keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ nɛ be dei ya.

english_verse: But when I saw they were not really obeying the truth that is in the good news, I corrected Peter in front of everyone and said: Peter, you are a Jew, but you live like a Gentile. So how can you force Gentiles to live like Jews?
translation_verse: Ma kena nyi be nombia ayɛɛsa bɛɛ kyaa na Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan ya nɛ, ma korowɔ seŋ tia Pita balaŋ baŋ pou siaman nyiaa, “Pita, n yɛɛ Gyuda baale e, mɔna n nombia ayɛɛsa bɛɛ wolo nyi n yɛɛ Gyuda baale e ya. Woŋ dɔɔ te nɛnɛɛ n na ŋere balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ nyi baa di Gyudatena mmaraase dɔɔ?”

english_verse: But if I tear down something and then build it again, I prove that I was wrong at first.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa ma bese lee me wɔle gyu ke di mmaraase ŋan mɔɔ wulaa bɛɛ ŋa nɛ dɔɔ na, kenaŋ na nawolo baa nyi me yɛɛ dukumyɛɛre e.

english_verse: It was the Law itself that killed me and freed me from its power, so I could live for God. I have been nailed to the cross with Christ.
translation_verse: Gyaŋgba keŋ na nafɔ me doo Mosesi mmaraa keŋ tɛɛ, mɔna nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ Yesu ta lese maŋ lee ke tɛɛ na maa nyiŋ kyaa fa Wurubuarɛ.

english_verse: I don't turn my back on God's gift of undeserved grace. If we can be acceptable to God by obeying the Law, it was useless for Christ to die.
translation_verse: Mɔɔ ne bɛɛ Wurubuarɛ bɛrɛɛ keŋ ɔ ba mo boele maŋ nɛ ya. Nawolo nyi nafɔ akpaa Wurubuarɛ ne lɛɛ balaŋ bo do nyi be gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ ɔ siaman mo lee mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ kedi dɔɔ na, kenaŋ na Kristo yeŋ keŋ ɔ ba yem nɛ be dana tɔnɔɔ ya.

english_verse: I want to know only one thing. How were you given God's Spirit? Was it by obeying the Law of Moses or by hearing about Christ and having faith in him?
translation_verse: Ɛ tira nombii kei nɔɔ fa maŋ. Mmaraase ŋan kedi yele la te ɛ nyiŋa Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon yaa, nyeekelɛɛ nombia ŋan dɔɔ kolosi fa ŋon te ɛ be lɛɛ ŋa di nɛ dɔɔ?

english_verse: How can you be so stupid? Do you think that by yourself you can complete what God's Spirit started in you?
translation_verse: Sena yaa ɛ gyimwɔ mena? Ɛ fiasɛɛ nideli ɛ ne sila Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon gbɛɛneŋ. Nɛnɛɛ weera dɔɔ te ɛ ne gyae ɛ ka mo denibalaŋ gbɛɛneŋ tɔɔle ke?

english_verse: Have you gone through all of this for nothing? Is it all really for nothing?
translation_verse: Kenaŋ na nawolo baa nyi ɛ diyem keŋ ɛ be naa lee ɛ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom man nɛ yɛɛ yakaa baa? Koa, mɔɔ ne lɛɛ di nyi ke yɛɛ yakaa ya.

english_verse: God gives you his Spirit and works miracles in you. But does he do this because you obey the Law of Moses or because you have heard about Christ and have faith in him?
translation_verse: Mosesi mmaraase ŋan ɛ be di ŋe dɔɔ nɛ dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ moo ɔ feliŋ ŋon do ɛ man te ɔ yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia ɛ man? Yaa keŋ ɛ be nyii nyeekelɛɛ nombia ŋan te ɛ be lɛɛ ŋa di nɛ dɔɔ?

english_verse: And so, you should understand that everyone who has faith is a child of Abraham.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ kaboena nyi yaa gyeŋ nyiaa, balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di ŋgba mena keŋ Aberaham be lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di nɛ, baŋ yɛna Aberaham duuluŋ man balaŋ gbagba.

english_verse: Long ago the Scriptures said God would accept the Gentiles because of their faith. This is why God told Abraham the good news that all nations would be blessed because of him.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan wulaa yakowɔ see lee gyaŋgba gyaŋgba keŋ pɛte nombia ŋenaŋ kɔŋawɔ mena nyiaa, akpaa balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ de lɛɛ ŋon Wurubuarɛ di na, ŋon mɔ waa lɛɛ wɔ do nyi be gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ ɔ siaman. Wurubuarɛ wulaa yakowɔ see fa Aberaham nyiaa, “Lee nyaŋ dɔɔ te maa hiraa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ pou.”

english_verse: This means everyone who has faith will share in the blessings given to Abraham because of his faith.
translation_verse: Lee mena dɔɔ balaŋ baŋ pou baa lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di ŋgba mena keŋ Aberaham be lɛɛ e di nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa hiraa wɔ ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be hiraa Aberaham nɛ.

english_verse: And because of what Jesus Christ has done, the blessing promised to Abraham was taken to the Gentiles. This happened so that by faith we would be given the promised Holy Spirit. The Law and the Promise
translation_verse: Kristo yeŋa la tɔ de nombiakumɛɛ pou kom na Wurubuarɛ ke ta ɔ dɔɔ hiraa balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ, ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be hiraa Aberaham nɛ. Na lee da ke lɛɛ Yesu di dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ ke fa daa ɔ feliŋ ŋon ɔ be yako see nyi waa fa daa nɛ. Mosesi mmaraase ŋan na Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan ɔ be yako see fa Aberaham nɛ

english_verse: My friends, I will use an everyday example to explain what I mean. Once someone agrees to something, no one else can change or cancel the agreement.
translation_verse: Me tebia, ɛ yela maa mo nombia ŋan ɛ wulaa ɛ gyeŋ nɛ mo lese nombia kɛŋa man wola ŋon. Nyi balaŋ bala de yako nombii see na, ŋolo be kyaa keŋ waa tale wɔlɛɛ ke yaa waa kyɛɛkee ke ya. Mena yaa Wurubuarɛ nombia ayakoŋ see kɛŋa mɔ doo.

english_verse: That is how it is with the promises God made to Abraham and his descendant. The promises were not made to many descendants, but only to one, and that one is Christ.
translation_verse: De naana Aberaham na ɔ duuluŋ man walaŋ te Wurubuarɛ yako mena nombia kɛŋa see fa. Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan te yako nyi, “Aberaham na ɔ duuluŋ man balaŋ” na kawola nyi ba boe ya. Mɔna ɔ yako e nyi, “Aberaham na ɔ duuluŋ man walaŋ” na kawola nyi walaŋ dokoloŋ kooŋ nombia te Wurubuarɛ ne yako. Walaŋ ŋonaŋ yɛna Yesu Kristo.

english_verse: What I am saying is that the Law cannot change or cancel God's promise made
translation_verse: Nombii keŋ mɛɛ yako nɛ ka asɛɛ yɛna nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ wulaa yakowɔ see fa Aberaham kulutooneŋ sɔŋonoŋse akpeŋ anaara na sao na kufu (

english_verse: If we have to obey the Law in order to receive God's blessings, those blessings don't really come to us because of God's promise. But God was kind to Abraham and made him a promise.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ be gyae waa mo ɔ nhira keŋ fa ŋon nyi keŋ ɛ ne di Mosesi mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ nɛ dɔɔ ya, mɔna lee keŋ ɔ be wulaa kaŋ ntam yako see nyi waa mo fa ŋon dɔɔ. Mena ke mɔ te Wurubuarɛ moo ɔ nhira keŋ fa Aberaham yakaa, nawolo nyi ɔ wulaa yakowɔ see nyi waa mo ke fa e.

english_verse: What is the use of the Law? It was given later to show that we sin. But it was only supposed to last until the coming of that descendant who was given the promise. In fact, angels gave the Law to Moses, and he gave it to the people.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa mena na, woŋ dɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ faa mmaraase ŋan? Kpene keŋ dɔɔ ɔ ba mo ŋa kpu na keyako see keŋ fa wɔ yɛna nyiaa, ɔ ne gyae nyi balaŋ ka gyeŋ kpene keŋ yɛɛ dukum ke. Wurubuarɛ faa mmaraase ŋan bo na ŋa ke doo kelii debaŋ keŋ Aberaham duuluŋ man walaŋ ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ ke kɔŋ. Wurubuarɛ tɛe ɔ kpilala dɔɔ mo ɔ mmaraase ŋan fa Mosesi, te ŋon mɔ moowɔ fa balaŋ baŋ. Nawolo nyi ŋon doo la Wurubuarɛ na balaŋ baŋ nsana.

english_verse: In fact, the Law was to be our teacher until Christ came. Then we could have faith and be acceptable to God.
translation_verse: Kei nawolo baa nyi, na Mosesi mmaraase ŋan ne diina de dɔɔ pɛte Kristo kɔŋawɔ. Kristo ke kɔŋ wɔle na, lee ŋon Kristo kelɛɛdi man te walaŋ gbɛɛneŋ na tenee Wurubuarɛ siaman.

english_verse: But once a person has learned to have faith, there is no more need to have the Law as a teacher.
translation_verse: Nɛnɛɛ keŋ dɔɔ lɛɛ Kristo di dɔɔ, mmaraase ŋan ba tekaa dana doŋ de dɔɔ bela ya.

english_verse: All of you are God's children because of your faith in Christ Jesus.
translation_verse: Lee mena keŋ ɛ be lɛɛ Yesu Kristo di dɔɔ, te ɛ popou bese Wurubuarɛ bia gbagba nɛ.

english_verse: And when you were baptized, it was as though you had put on Christ in the same way you put on new clothes.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi ɛmɛɛ baŋ pou bɔɔ sɔ ŋon Wurubuarɛ loŋ Kristo man nɛ, te yeli ɛmɛɛ na Kristo ta bese walaŋ dokoloŋ. Lee mena dɔɔ Kristo dinɔɔ keŋ te kaa doo ɛ man.

english_verse: Faith in Christ Jesus is what makes each of you equal with each other, whether you are a Jew or a Greek, a slave or a free person, a man or a woman.
translation_verse: Lee mena dɔɔ n yɛɛ Gyuda baale o, n te yɛɛ Gyuda baale ya o, n yɛɛ gbeŋi o yaa n yɛɛ gyooro bu e, n yɛɛ alo e yaa n yɛɛ baale o nɛ, keŋ be famenɛ bela ya. Nawolo nyi ɛ popou te yɛɛ dokoloŋ Kristo man.

english_verse: So if you belong to Christ, you are now part of Abraham's family, and you will be given what God has promised.
translation_verse: Nyi akpaa ɛ yɛɛ Kristo balaŋwɔ na, kenaŋ na ɛ yɛɛ Aberaham duuluŋ man balaŋwɔ. Mena ke mɔ te Wurubuarɛ waa hiraa ŋon, ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be yako see nyi waa hiraa Aberaham nɛ.

english_verse: But when the time was right, God sent his Son, and a woman gave birth to him. His Son obeyed the Law,
translation_verse: Mɔna debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba see nɛ kelii na, ɔ kpila ŋon gbagba bu kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ te ɔ yela alo lola e. Mena debaŋ kenaŋ na, balaŋ doo bo Gyudatena mmaraase ŋan tɛɛ.

english_verse: Now that we are his children, God has sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts. And his Spirit tells us that God is our Father.
translation_verse: Nɛnɛɛ keŋ dɔɔ bese Wurubuarɛ bia dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ te yeli ɔ bu ŋon feliŋ ŋon te kaa kyaa de konɔɔse man. Feliŋ ŋonaŋ ne yele la te dɛɛ tale baake Wurubuarɛ nyi “De kya” nɛ.

english_verse: You are no longer slaves. You are God's children, and you will be given what he has promised. Paul's Concern for the Galatians
translation_verse: Lee mena dɔɔ ɛ te yɛɛ gbeŋawɔ bela ya, mɔna nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ ɛ ta bese Wurubuarɛ bia. Te lee mena keŋ ɛ ba bese Wurubuarɛ bia dɔɔ, ɛ ke nyiŋ abɔɔ ŋan pou waa mo fa ɔ bia nɛ. Pɔɔl kayeyɛɛ Galatiatena dɔɔ

english_verse: when I first preached to you? No you didn't, even though you knew I had come there because I was sick.
translation_verse: Ɛ tɔɔse nyi ma kekɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ gyaŋgbate keŋ nɛ, kawee yele la te me nyiŋa gbɛɛ kolosi Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan fa ŋon.

english_verse: My illness must have caused you some trouble, but you didn't hate me or turn me away because of it. You welcomed me as though I were one of God's angels or even Christ Jesus himself.
translation_verse: Doo nyi na me kawee kenaŋ yɛɛ kateesa ke fa ŋon, mɔna ɛ te ŋmanyaŋ maŋ ya te ɛ te bɛɛ maŋ mɔ ya. Ɛ lɛɛ maŋ ŋgba Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋolo lee la adido kɔŋ nɛ, yaa ŋgba maŋ yɛna Yesu Kristo ŋon gbagba nɛ.

english_verse: Where is that good feeling now? I am sure if it had been possible, you would have taken out your own eyes and given them to me.
translation_verse: Mena debaŋ kenaŋ na me naawɔ nyi gyoŋ daale doo ɛ man keŋ nyi nafɔ yeŋ nyiŋa gbɛɛ koraŋ na, yeŋ kulii ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ siabia do maŋ. Nɛnɛɛ mena gyoŋ kenaŋ laŋa aleŋ?

english_verse: Am I now your enemy, just because I told you the truth?
translation_verse: Keŋ mɔɔ yako ŋon anokoare keŋ dɔɔ na, me ta bese ɛ kɔlare?

english_verse: Those people may be paying you a lot of attention, but it isn't for your good. They only want to keep you away from me, so you will pay them a lot of attention.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ beo yɛɛ ba wose nyi be yɛɛ Kristotenawɔ nɛ, bɛɛ gyae bo nyi baa beo ŋon na ɛ kaa di Gyudatena mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ, mɔna ŋa be gyae ke yɛɛ tɔnɔɔ kamasɛ fa ŋon ya. Bɛɛ gyae bo nyi baa tira daa na ŋon nsana, na ɛ ke nyiŋ mo ɛ loo pou gyakaa be dɔɔ.

english_verse: It is always good to give your attention to something worthwhile, even when I am not with you.
translation_verse: Akpaa ɛ yase ɛ sia te ɛ ne yɛɛ kolo kpaakpaate na ke dei. Mɔna kaboena nyi yaa yɛɛ ke debaŋ kamasɛ. Te yɛɛ nyi me kyaa ɛ gyaŋ kaageŋ te ɛ ke yɛɛ ke ya.

english_verse: I wish I were with you now. Then I would not have to talk this way. You really have me puzzled. Hagar and Sarah
translation_verse: Ma kegyaebii yɛna nyi nafɔ na me kyaa ɛ gyaŋ, na maa kyɛɛkee ma kekolosi na ke kyaa na ɛ kegyaebii. Nawolo nyi ɛ nombia ayɛɛsa dana maŋ gyakoloŋ. Nɔɔse ala ŋan Wurubuarɛ be bake see nɛ

english_verse: Some of you would like to be under the rule of the Law of Moses. But do you know what the Law says?
translation_verse: Ɛ yela maa bɔɔse ŋon nombii kei. Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ ne gyae nyi ɛ ke doo Mosesi mmaraase ŋan tɛɛ nɛ, gbagbaagba na ɛ gyeŋ kpene keŋ mmaraase ŋan ne yako?

english_verse: In the Scriptures we learn that Abraham had two sons. The mother of one of them was a slave, while the mother of the other one had always been free.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako nyiaa, Aberaham lola bia baala bala. Be man ŋolo ɔ naa yɛɛ gbeŋi e, te ŋolo mɔ ɔ naa yɛɛ gyoorobu e.

english_verse: The son of the slave woman was born in the usual way. But the son of the free woman was born because of God's promise.
translation_verse: Aberaham bu ŋon gbeŋi ŋon ba lolo fa e nɛ, ba lola e bo ŋgba mena keŋ alo kamasɛ ne taŋ lolo nɛ. Mɔna ŋon gyooro bu ŋon ba lolo fa e nɛ dɔɔ, Wurubuarɛ wulaa yakowɔ see nyi baa lola e pɛte be kaa lola e.

english_verse: All of this has another meaning as well. Each of the two women stands for one of the agreements God made with his people. Hagar, the slave woman, stands for the agreement that was made at Mount Sinai. Everyone born into her family is a slave.
translation_verse: Mena nombia kɛŋa yɛɛ dudu ke keŋ na lese nombia ŋaale wola. Ala bala kɛwɔ seŋɛɛ fa nɔɔkebakesee ala ŋan Wurubuarɛ be bake see fa ɔ balaŋ nɛ. Nɔɔkebakesee keŋ ba lee Sinai bula dɔɔ nɛ, keŋ seŋɛɛ la fa alo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Haga nɛ. Ɔ bia yɛna balaŋ baŋ Mosesi mmaraase ŋan ba besena wɔ yɛɛ gbeŋa nɛ.

english_verse: Hagar also stands for Mount Sinai in Arabia and for the present city of Jerusalem. She and her children are slaves.
translation_verse: Haga seŋɛɛ fa Sinai bula keŋ ke kyaa Arebia tɛɛle man nɛ. Ɔ yɛɛ ŋgba gyɛŋ wee kei Gyerusalɛm nɛ. Mosesi mmaraase ŋan ta besena ke man balaŋ baŋ pou yɛɛ gbeŋa.

english_verse: The Scriptures say about her, “You have never had children, but now you can be glad. You have never given birth, but now you can shout. Once you had no children, but now you will have more children than a woman who has been married for a long time.”
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako nyiaa, “Nyaŋ ɔlamanbo ŋon n ta lola ta see ya nɛ, yela ke yɛɛ neŋ gyoŋ. Nyaŋ ŋon n ta doŋee ta see ya nɛ, yela ke yɛɛ neŋ gyoŋ na n ke lɔ akelelee. Doo nyi n baale ta lese ɔ sia lee n dɔɔ, mɔna maa yela waa bese kɔŋ n gyaŋ na n bia ke wuku kela ŋon ɔ kyaa yale tɛɛ nɛ.”

english_verse: But the child who was born in the natural way made trouble for the child who was born because of the Spirit. The same thing is happening today.
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ man na, bu ŋon bɔɔ lola e ŋgba mena keŋ bɛɛ lola walaŋ kamasɛ nɛ, naase bu ŋon bɔɔ tɛɛ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon doŋ man lola e nɛ diyem. Mena ke yaa sɛɛ doo kaa lii gyɛŋ wee kei nɛ.

english_verse: The Scriptures say, “Get rid of the slave woman and her son! He won't be given anything. The son of the free woman will receive everything.”
translation_verse: Mɔna sena te Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ne yako? Nombia ŋan yakowɔ nyiaa, “Gegi gbeŋi ŋon na ɔ bu, nawolo nyi tekaboena nyi gbeŋi bu ŋon waa di ɔ kya abɔɔ kpu na gyooro bu ŋon ya.”

english_verse: My friends, we are children of the free woman and not of the slave.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ me tebia, da te yɛɛ gbeŋi ŋon biawɔ ya. De yɛɛ gyooro bu ŋon biawɔ.

english_verse: Christ has set us free! This means we are really free. Now hold on to your freedom and don't ever become slaves of the Law again.
translation_verse: Kristo te lɛɛ da nyeeya yela de te nyiŋ da wose bese gyoorobia. Mena dɔɔ kaboena nyi yaa seŋ ɛ kelɛɛdi keŋ man keŋkeŋ. Ɛ na fa ŋolo gbɛɛ na waa besena ŋon yɛɛ ɔ gbeŋa bela ya.

english_verse: But the Spirit makes us sure God will accept us because of our faith in Christ.
translation_verse: Daa dɔɔ, Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon doŋ man dɔɔ de loo gyakaa nyi Wurubuarɛ waa lɛɛ daa do nyi de gbɛɛneŋ tenee ɔ siaman, nawolo nyi dɛɛ lɛɛ Kristo di.

english_verse: If you are a follower of Christ Jesus, it makes no difference whether you are circumcised or not. All that matters is your faith that makes you love others.
translation_verse: Yesu Kristo kelɛɛdi be famenɛ nyi ŋolo kara ɔ baala wose yaa ɔ te kare ya. Mɔna kpene keŋ ne hia yɛna nyi dɛɛ lɛɛ Yesu Kristo di, na dɛɛ gyae Wurubuarɛ na de tebia.

english_verse: You were doing so well until someone made you turn from the truth.
translation_verse: Kewalaŋ keŋ na, nafɔ ɛ ne yɛɛ nombia kpaakpaa lee ɛ kelɛɛdi keŋ man. Nɛnɛɛ amɔte beo na ŋon te ɛ tinaa anokoare keŋ kesila yela?

english_verse: And that person was certainly not sent by the one who chose you.
translation_verse: Ma gyeŋ nyi mena kabeo kei ta lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ ya. Nawolo nyi ŋon baake na ŋon nyi ɛ kaa bese ɔ bia.

english_verse: but you belong to the Lord. This makes me certain you will do what I say, instead of what someone else tells you to do. Whoever is causing trouble for you will be punished.
translation_verse: Mɔna mɛɛ lɛɛ de Gbeŋgyoo Yesu di nyiaa, ɛ be gyae ɛ ka lee me wɔle ke lɛɛ nombii wɔle daale di bela ya. Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon waa yela ɛ ka lee me wɔle ke lɛɛ nombii wɔle daale di na, ɔtenate yɛɛ woŋ e o, ɔ te yɛɛ woŋ e ya o nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa wɔŋ ɔ deŋele.

english_verse: I wish everyone who is upsetting you would not only get circumcised, but would cut off much more!
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ be dana baala wose kekara nombia bɛɛ naase ŋon diyem nɛ, nafɔ maŋ kegyaebii yɛna nyi baa gyeŋ gyu ka lese ba wose saayɛ gba.

english_verse: My friends, you were chosen to be free. So don't use your freedom as an excuse to do anything you want. Use it as an opportunity to serve each other with love.
translation_verse: Me tebia, ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ baake ŋon bo nyi ɛ kaa bese gyooro bia. Mɔna ɛ na yeli ɛ gyoorobiiri keŋ ke fa ŋon gbɛɛ na ɛ ke yela ɛ lookum ke di ɛ dɔɔ ya. Ɛ mo balaŋ kegyae gyae dɔŋa na ɛ ke kyɔ dɔŋa.

english_verse: All the Law says can be summed up in the command to love others as much as you love yourself.
translation_verse: Mosesi mmaraase ŋan pou seŋɛɛ bo nombii dokoloŋ dɔɔ. Keŋ yɛna nyiaa, “Gyae n dɔɔ ŋgba nyaŋ gbagba n wose nɛ.”

english_verse: But if you keep attacking each other like wild animals, you had better watch out or you will destroy yourselves. God's Spirit and Our Own Desires
translation_verse: Mɔna nyi akpaa ɛ sɛɛ ɛ ne yoo na dɔŋa ŋgba wonembia kumɛɛ na, kenaŋ na ɛ kɛɛ ɛ wose nideli. Na mena ya na, ɛ ka koe dɔŋa munu.

english_verse: The Spirit and your desires are enemies of each other. They are always fighting each other and keeping you from doing what you feel you should.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyiaa, abɔɔ ŋan deniwalaŋ kegyaebii keŋ ne gyae waa yɛɛ nɛ, na ŋan te Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ne gyae nyi waa yɛɛ ya. Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon na deniwalaŋ kegyaebii keŋ bɛɛ gyoo ya. Lee mena dɔɔ ɛmɛɛ be gyae ɛ ke tale yɛɛ ɛ kegyaebii ya.

english_verse: But if you obey the Spirit, the Law of Moses has no control over you.
translation_verse: Mɔna akpaa ɛ yɛɛ kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon ne gyae nyi yaa yɛɛ na, kenaŋ na ɛ ba tekaa doo Gyudatena mmaraase ŋan tɛɛ bela ya.

english_verse: They worship idols, practice witchcraft, hate others, and are hard to get along with. People become jealous, angry, and selfish. They not only argue and cause trouble, but they are
translation_verse: Ŋaale mɔ yɛna bane ɔsom, na yolo nombia, na balaŋ kekɔla, na kale kegyae, na daaworo nombia, na baŋ kagyeŋ, na kedikum, na aŋmaareŋ nombia, na dɔŋa wɔle kalee.

english_verse: envious. They get drunk, carry on at wild parties, and do other evil things as well. I told you before, and I am telling you again: No one who does these things will share in the blessings of God's kingdom.
translation_verse: Ŋaale mɔ yɛna sibii, na soloŋ kenyɔɔ dɛɛse, na nɔɔkedi keŋ ayoi kumɛɛ doo ke man, na nombia nkaraŋkaraŋ keyɛɛ, na nombiakumɛɛ ŋan pou ŋe yɛɛ ŋgba kɛŋa dinɔɔ nɛ. Mɛɛ faree ŋon baa bela ŋgba mena keŋ mɔɔ wulaa faree ŋon kaalaŋ nɛ nyiaa, walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne yɛɛ mena abɔɔ kɛŋa na, ɔ be gyae waa nyiŋ gbɛɛ gyu Wurubuarɛ gyoorobiiri keŋ man ya.

english_verse: gentle, and self-controlled. There is no law against behaving in any of these ways.
translation_verse: na wose keyɔkɔse, na wose dɔɔ kedi. Mmaraa kamasɛ be kyaa keŋ ne tɔ mena abɔɔ kɔŋa keyɛɛ ya.

english_verse: And because we belong to Christ Jesus, we have killed our selfish feelings and desires.
translation_verse: Baŋ pou be yɛɛ Yesu Kristo balaŋwɔ nɛ, be te tina be nombiakumɛɛ na lookum keŋ pou doo be man nɛ yela.

english_verse: But don't be conceited or make others jealous by claiming to be better than they are.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛ na yeli dɛɛ gate da wose adido, yaa dɛɛ do dɔŋa baŋ yaa de sia keyɛɛ dɔŋa abɔɔ ya.

english_verse: My friends, you are spiritual. So if someone is trapped in sin, you should gently lead that person back to the right path. But watch out, and don't be tempted yourself.
