english_verse: elders knelt down before the one sitting on the throne. And as they worshiped the one who lives forever, they placed their crowns in front of the throne and said,
translation_verse: ) mɔ kyakɛɛ gbelɛɛ ŋenaŋ dɔɔ. Kegyiise benaŋ doona abɔɔ wolomɛɛ pareparepare, te be wuuna gyooro kotɔtɔɔse ŋan bɔɔ mo kɔba yayam yɛɛ ŋa nɛ.

english_verse: “Our Lord and God, you are worthy to receive glory, honor, and power. You created all things, and by your decision they are and were created.”
translation_verse: “De Gbeŋgyoo, nyaŋ yɛna de Wurubuarɛ. N kaboena nyi baa lese n yele na baa mo obuo fa neŋ. N dana doŋ, nawolo nyi nyaŋ yɛɛna kpene kamasɛ. N yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ bo n kegyaebii man, te n kegyaebii man te ŋe popou mɔ kyaa nɛ.”

english_verse: In the right hand of the one sitting on the throne I saw a scroll that had writing on the inside and on the outside. And it was sealed in seven places.
translation_verse: Kɛŋa wɔle nɛ, me naa tɔne debɔɔsa daale doo walaŋ ŋon ɔ kyaa gyooro gbeli keŋ dɔɔ nɛ dunoluŋ nyiŋmaa man. Ba te ŋmarase do tɔne keŋ siaman na ke wɔle pou, te ba moo kolo anemaataŋ ŋgba keeni nɛ tɔ ke nɔɔ betabonyetooro.

english_verse: I saw a mighty angel ask with a loud voice, “Who is worthy to open the scroll and break its seals?”
translation_verse: Keŋte me naa Wurubuarɛ kpilale doŋte ŋolo fae yeesa keŋkeŋ bɔɔse nyiaa, “Amɔte kaboela nyi waa wolee keenise ŋan, na waa toro tɔne keŋ?”

english_verse: No one in heaven or on earth or under the earth was able to open the scroll or look inside it.
translation_verse: Mɔna, na ŋolo be kyaa adido yaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ, yaa tɛɛle kei ateta keŋ waa tale toro tɔne keŋ na waa kala ke ya.

english_verse: Then one of the elders said to me, “Stop crying and look! The one who is called both the ‘Lion from the Tribe of Judah’ and ‘King David's Great Descendant’ has won the victory. He will open the scroll and its seven seals.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te kegyiise baŋ man ŋolo yako maŋ nyiaa, “Na wii ya. Kɛɛ, walaŋ ŋon bɛɛ baake nyi Dinaate ŋon ɔ ba lee Gyuda dekpaŋalaŋ man na gyoo Defidi duuluŋ man te ɔ yɛɛ walaŋ dinaa e nɛ te yoo di ɔbɔnsam dɔɔ. Mena dɔɔ ŋon ne gyae la waa tale wolee keenise nyetooro ŋan, na waa toro tɔne keŋ.”

english_verse: Then I looked and saw a Lamb standing in the center of the throne surrounded by the four living creatures and the elders. The Lamb looked as if it had once been killed. It had seven horns and seven eyes, which are the seven spirits of God, sent out to all the earth.
translation_verse: Keŋte me naa Nambu ŋon ɔ yɛɛ ŋgba ba koo e bo nɛ seŋɛɛ gyooro gbeli keŋ na wonise banaara baŋ, na kegyiise sao banaara baŋ nsana nsana. Ɔ dana woo nyetooro na sia nyetooro. Sia nyetooro ŋan yɛna Wurubuarɛ feliŋse nyetooro ŋan ɔ be kpili kɔŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ yenaŋ kamasɛ nɛ.

english_verse: The Lamb went over and took the scroll from the right hand of the one who sat on the throne.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Nambu ŋon kɔŋawɔ kaa lɛɛ tɔne keŋ lee walaŋ ŋon ɔ kyaa gyooro gbeli keŋ dɔɔ nɛ nyiŋmaa man.

english_verse: After he had taken it, the four living creatures and the
translation_verse: Ɔ ke lɛɛ tɔne keŋ nɛ, wonise banaara (

english_verse: elders knelt down before him. Each of them had a harp and a gold bowl full of incense, which are the prayers of God's people.
translation_verse: ) baŋ kaa buŋawɔ som Nambu ŋonaŋ. Na be man walaŋ kamasɛ dana adolɛɛ na kɔba yayam nyefale, na oliifi te wulu ŋe man. Oliifi keŋ seŋɛɛ baa fa Kristotena kefane keŋ bɛɛ fane fa Wurubuarɛ nɛ.

english_verse: Then they sang a new song, “You are worthy to receive the scroll and open its seals, because you were killed. And with your own blood you bought for God people from every tribe, language, nation, and race.
translation_verse: Keŋte be kpee nɔɔneŋ wɔle fa Nambu ŋon nyiaa, “Nyaŋ kaboela nyi nya lɛɛ tɔne keŋ na n ke wolee keenise ŋan. Nawolo nyi ba koo neŋ, te n moo n fatabo lɛɛ balaŋ fa Wurubuarɛ. N lɛɛ balaŋ lee akpaŋalaŋ kamasɛ man, na deise kamasɛ na tɛɛle kei dɔɔ duuluŋ kpookpoo man, na tɛɛlese kamasɛ man fa Wurubuarɛ.

english_verse: As I looked, I heard the voices of a large number of angels around the throne and the voices of the living creatures and of the elders. There were millions and millions of them,
translation_verse: Me ka bese gɛɛse kɛɛ nɛ, te me nyii Wurubuarɛ kpilala burum baale woya. Ba boe keŋ nyi ŋolo na ŋolo be tale kala wɔ ya. Na be te kilisi gyooro gbeli keŋ na wonise banaara baŋ na kegyiise sao banaara baŋ pou.

english_verse: and they were saying in a loud voice, “The Lamb who was killed is worthy to receive power, riches, wisdom, strength, honor, glory, and praise.”
translation_verse: Na bee kpee nɔɔneŋ keŋkeŋ nyiaa, “Nambu ŋon bɔɔ ko e nɛ, ŋon kaboela nyi dɛɛ lese ɔ yele na dɛɛ mo obuo fa nideli. Ŋon te doŋ na nyansa na wosekenyiŋ pou kaboena.”

english_verse: Then I heard all beings in heaven and on the earth and under the earth and in the sea offer praise. Together, all of them were saying, “Praise, honor, glory, and strength forever and ever to the one who sits on the throne and to the Lamb!”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te me nyii kpene kamasɛ keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yɛɛ te ŋe kyaa adido, na ŋan ŋe kyaa tɛɛle dɔɔ na ŋan ŋe doo tɛɛle ateta, na ŋan ŋe doo ɛpo man nɛ pou kpee nɔɔneŋ yako nyiaa, “Walaŋ ŋon ɔ kyaa gyooro gbeli keŋ dɔɔ, na Nambu ŋon te kaboena nyi dɛɛ lese ba yele na dɛɛ mo obuo na gyoorobiiri fa wɔ kekpaakekpaa.”

english_verse: At the same time I saw the Lamb open the first of the seven seals, I heard one of the four living creatures shout with a voice like thunder. It said, “Come out!”
translation_verse: Me ka seŋ mɛɛ kɛɛ nɛ, te Nambu ŋon wolee keenise nyetooro ŋan bɔɔ mo mataa tɔne keŋ tɔ ke nɔɔ nɛ man gyaŋgbate keŋ. Botɔɔ te me nyii wonise banaara baŋ man ŋolo fae yeesa keŋkeŋ ŋgba ɔga fae la nɛ baake nyiaa, “Kɔŋ!”

english_verse: Then I saw a white horse. Its rider carried a bow and was given a crown. He had already won some victories, and he went out to win more.
translation_verse: Karatetee dokoloŋ me kɛɛ na, dɛɛkyɔŋ wolom pareparepare ŋolo seŋɛɛ laa. Na walaŋ ŋon ɔ kyaa ɔ dɔɔ nɛ dana baŋdaŋ, te ba moo gyooro kotɔtɔɔ fa e. Keŋte ɔ kyaawɔ dɛɛkyɔŋ ŋon dɔɔ lee laŋ ŋgba walaŋ ŋon ɔ ne yoo di debaŋ kamasɛ nɛ, te ɔ laŋawɔ ɔ kaa yoo di.

english_verse: When the Lamb opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature say, “Come out!”
translation_verse: Nambu ŋon ke wolee keeni alate keŋ nɛ, te me nyii wonise banaara baŋ man balate ŋon woya baakewɔ nyiaa, “Kɔŋ!”

english_verse: Then another horse came out. It was fiery red. And its rider was given the power to take away all peace from the earth, so people would slaughter one another. He was also given a big sword.
translation_verse: Karatetee dokoloŋ te me naa dɛɛkyɔŋ balate ŋolo mɔ leewɔ kɔŋ. Kei ŋon yɛɛ dɛɛkyɔŋ yayam e. Te be faa walaŋ ŋon ɔ kyaa ɔ dɔɔ nɛ doŋ nyi waa lese wosefɛɛreŋ lee tɛɛle kei dɔɔ, na balaŋ ke nyiŋ yoo koe dɔŋa. Keŋte ba moo yoosila dinaa daale do e ɔ nyiŋmaa man.

english_verse: When the Lamb opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature say, “Come out!” Then I saw a black horse, and its rider had a balance scale in one hand.
translation_verse: Nambu ŋon ke wolee keeni atoorote keŋ nɛ, me nyii wonise banaara baŋ man batoorote ŋon woya baakewɔ nyiaa, “Kɔŋ!” Me kɛɛ na dɛɛkyɔŋ bibiini kirikirikiri ŋolo seŋɛɛ laa me siaman. Na walaŋ ŋon ɔ kyaa ɔ dɔɔ nɛ dana kɔta keŋ bɛɛ mo keri abɔɔ nɛ ɔ nyiŋmaa man.

english_verse: I heard what sounded like a voice from somewhere among the four living creatures. It said, “A liter of wheat will cost you a whole day's wages! Three liters of barley will cost you a day's wages too. But don't ruin the olive oil or the wine.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te me nyii kolo ŋgba walaŋ kekolosi nɛ wonise banaara baŋ man nyiaa, “Baa yɔɔ kefaa biliŋ kuruwaatɔɔ lɛɛ walaŋ wee dokoloŋ tom keyɛɛ kɔba. Mena mɔ te baa yɔɔ wambela biliŋ kuruwaa tɔɔse atooro lɛɛ walaŋ wee dokoloŋ tom keyɛɛ kɔba. Mɔna nuŋ na soloŋ dɔɔ, ŋolo na tina ŋa ya!”

english_verse: When the Lamb opened the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living creature say, “Come out!”
translation_verse: Nambu ŋon ke wolee keeni anaarate keŋ nɛ, te me nyii wonise banaara baŋ man banaarate ŋon woya baakewɔ nyiaa, “Kɔŋ!”

english_verse: Then I saw a pale green horse. Its rider was named Death, and Death's Kingdom followed close behind. They were given power over one fourth of the earth, and they could kill its people with swords, famines, diseases, and wild animals.
translation_verse: Me gɛɛse kɛɛ na, dɛɛkyɔŋ kpalaa kɔŋkɔsɔŋ ŋolo seŋɛɛ laa botɔɔ. Walaŋ ŋon ɔ kyaa ɔ dɔɔ nɛ te bɛɛ baake nyi Yeŋ. Na woyadɛɛ mɔ silana e wɔle wɔle. Ba te fa wɔ doŋ nyi baa mo yoosila na tanam, na kaweese na wonembia kumɛɛ baŋ be kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, koe balaŋ baŋ be kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ be man kekpase anaara man dekpeŋ dokoloŋ.

english_verse: When the Lamb opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar the souls of everyone who had been killed for speaking God's message and telling about their faith.
translation_verse: Nambu ŋon ke wolee keeni anoŋte keŋ nɛ, te me naa balaŋ baale woya na be doo Wurubuarɛ kedeesa boe keŋ ateta. Mena woya kɛwɔ yɛna balaŋ baŋ lee Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan keyako dɔɔ te bɔɔ koe wɔ nɛ. Mena balaŋ benaŋ seŋawɔ keŋkeŋ di Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan wose man adansɛɛ, keŋ be ta lee be wɔle ya.

english_verse: Then each of those who had been killed was given a white robe and told to rest for a little while. They had to wait until the complete number of the Lord's other servants and followers would be killed.
translation_verse: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ faa woya baŋ man walaŋ kamasɛ kegba wolom wurikyim. Te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ nyiŋ konɔɔ kyaa kyomii. Nawolo nyi kaboena nyi ɛ tebia Kristotena akaŋ baŋ kaboena nyi baa koe wɔ lee Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan keyako dɔɔ, ŋgba mena keŋ bɔɔ koe ŋon nɛ be yaanɔɔle keŋ kelii pɛ.”

english_verse: When I saw the Lamb open the sixth seal, I looked and saw a great earthquake. The sun turned as dark as sackcloth, and the moon became red as blood.
translation_verse: Nambu ŋon ke wolee keeni loorote keŋ nɛ, karatetee dokoloŋ te tɛɛle wosiwɔ keŋkeŋ, te weese kyɛɛkeewɔ biri tuum ŋgba kanyaŋ bibiini nɛ. Te gyaalaŋ mɔ kyɛɛkeewɔ yase gooŋ ŋgba fatabo nɛ.

english_verse: The stars in the sky fell to earth, just like figs shaken loose by a windstorm.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te kyolobiise kuliiwɔ lee adido yala tɛɛle man, ŋgba mena keŋ feliŋ dinaa de gyɔɔ te daasebia adendelɛɛ ne tirii yala nɛ.

english_verse: Then the sky was rolled up like a scroll, and all mountains and islands were moved from their places.
translation_verse: Keŋte adido mɔ bɔɔwɔ ŋgba mena keŋ bɛɛ bɔɔ dofoŋoŋ nɛ te ke ŋaalaawɔ. Te bulase na tɛɛlesebia ŋan loŋ be kilisi ŋa gyaŋee nɛ, pou ŋaalaawɔ lee botɔɔ keŋ ŋa seŋɛɛ nɛ.

english_verse: The kings of the earth, its famous people, and its military leaders hid in caves or behind rocks on the mountains. They hid there together with the rich and the powerful and with all the slaves and free people.
translation_verse: Te tɛɛle kei dɔɔ gyooneŋ na yoonɔɔtena kegyiise, na balaŋ baŋ be dana kɔba na tɛɛle kei dɔɔ kegyiise na gbeŋi kamasɛ, na gyoorobia pou yekiiwɔ ka yo boya bɔɔneŋ man na kpanaŋse ŋan ŋe gyakaa bulase dɔɔ nɛ ateta.

english_verse: Then they shouted to the mountains and the rocks, “Fall on us! Hide us from the one who sits on the throne and from the anger of the Lamb.
translation_verse: Keŋte be fae yeesa baake bulase na kpanaŋse ŋan nyiaa, “Ɛ biliŋi kaa wuu de dɔɔ. Na walaŋ ŋon ɔ kyaa gyooro gbeli keŋ dɔɔ nɛ be nyiŋ naa daa ya, na Nambu ŋon mɔ be nyiŋ mo baŋ wɔŋ da deŋele ya.

english_verse: That terrible day has come! God and the Lamb will show their anger, and who can face it?”
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi wee dinaa keŋ baa mo baŋ di balaŋ nombia nɛ te lii. Te amɔte ne gyae la waa tale seŋ be siaman?”

english_verse: After this I saw four angels. Each one was standing on one of the earth's four corners. The angels held back the four winds, so that no wind would blow on the earth or on the sea or on any tree. These angels had also been given the power to harm the earth and the sea. Then I saw another angel come up from where the sun rises in the east, and he was ready to put the mark of the living God on people. He shouted to the four angels,
translation_verse: Keŋte ma besewɔ naa Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋolo, na ɔ ta lee weese dɛɛlee yenaŋ. Ɔ dana kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa kekpaakekpaa nɛ na mo do ɔ balaŋ nso nɛ ɔ nyiŋmaa man. Botɔɔ te ɔ lɔɔ nɔɔ yako Wurubuarɛ kpilala banaara baŋ Wurubuarɛ be fa wɔ doŋ nyi baa mo kayeyɛɛ kɔŋ tɛɛle na ɛpo dɔɔ nɛ nyiaa,

english_verse: “Don't harm the earth or the sea or any tree! Wait until I have marked the foreheads of the servants of our God.”
translation_verse: “Ɛ na wɔlɛɛ tɛɛle yaa ɛpo yaa daase ŋan ya, kelii debaŋ keŋ dɛɛ mo kpene keŋ de Wurubuarɛ na mo do ɔ balaŋ nso nɛ, fee baŋ pou bɛɛ som e nɛ tirinɔɔse dɔɔ pɛ.”

english_verse: Then I heard how many people had been marked on the forehead. There were
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ba wola maŋ balaŋ baŋ Wurubuarɛ ba do wɔ nso nɛ be yaanɔɔle. Be yaanɔɔle yɛɛ balaŋ kakpoŋse kufu ala akpeŋ kufu ala (

english_verse: and said, “Amen! Praise, glory, wisdom, thanks, honor, power, and strength belong to our God forever and ever! Amen!”
translation_verse: te be sɛɛwɔ nyiaa, “Amɛn. De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ danaana doŋ na nyansa na gyoorobiiri. Mena dɔɔ ɛ yela dɛɛ do e nɔɔ lese ɔ yele kekpaakekpaa. Amɛn.”

english_verse: After this, I saw a large crowd with more people than could be counted. They were from every race, tribe, nation, and language, and they stood before the throne and before the Lamb. They wore white robes and held palm branches in their hands,
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle me kɛɛ na, balaŋ burum baale seŋɛɛ laa keŋ ŋolo na ŋolo be tale kala wɔ ya. Mena balaŋ benaŋ lee tɛɛlese na debiise na deise na akpaŋalaŋse na balaŋ kpookpoo kamasɛ man. Mena balaŋ benaŋ pou seŋɛɛ gyooro gbeli keŋ na Nambu ŋon siaman. Ba doona kegbase wolomɛɛ wurikyim, te walaŋ kamasɛ mɔ dana ɔkɛɛ faareŋ ɔ nyiŋmaa man.

english_verse: as they shouted, “Our God, who sits upon the throne, has the power to save his people, and so does the Lamb.”
translation_verse: Na bɛɛ fae yeesa keŋkeŋ yako nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa gyooro gbeli keŋ dɔɔ nɛ, na Nambu ŋon ne lɛɛ na da nyee.”

english_verse: The angels who stood around the throne knelt in front of it with their faces to the ground. The elders and the four living creatures knelt there with them. Then they all worshiped God
translation_verse: Debaŋ kenaŋ na, Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ pou seŋɛɛ kilisi gyooro gbeli keŋ, na kegyiise sao banaara baŋ na wonise banaara baŋ. Be popou tisiwɔ buŋii mo be sia buŋ tɛɛle man gyooro gbeli keŋ siaman, te ba som Wurubuarɛ

english_verse: One of the elders asked me, “Do you know who these people are that are dressed in white robes? Do you know where they come from?”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te kegyiise sao banaara baŋ man ŋolo bɔɔse maŋ nyiaa, “Balaŋ bomɔ yɛna baŋ ba doona kegbase wolomɛɛ wurikyim kɛŋa te aleŋ yaa ba leewɔ?”

english_verse: “Sir,” I answered, “you must know.” Then he told me: “These are the ones who have gone through the great suffering. They have washed their robes in the blood of the Lamb and have made them white.
translation_verse: Keŋte me tiranɔɔ fa e nyi “Kegyia, nyaŋ gyeŋa la.” Te ɔ yako maŋ nyiaa, “Balaŋ kɛwɔ yɛna balaŋ baŋ Wurubuarɛ ba lese wɔ lee kayeyɛɛ dinaa keŋ be kɔŋ be dɔɔ nɛ man. Baŋ yɛna balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ faafo ba kegbase Nambu ŋon fatabo keŋ man te ŋɔɔ lee wolose pareparepare nɛ.

english_verse: And so they stand before the throne of God and worship him in his temple day and night. The one who sits on the throne will spread his tent over them.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ, te be yela be kaa seŋawɔ Wurubuarɛ gyooro gbeli keŋ siaman nɛ. Bɛɛ som Wurubuarɛ ɔ deni dinaa keŋ man nelim na ka weese. Walaŋ ŋon ɔ kyaa gyooro gbeli keŋ dɔɔ nɛ, waa fɛlɛɛ ɔ kanyaŋ wuu wɔ na waa gyee wɔ.

english_verse: They will never hunger or thirst again, and they won't be troubled by the sun or any scorching heat.
translation_verse: Tanam be gyae ka ko wɔ bela ya, te loŋboaneŋ mɔ be gyae ka ko wɔ bela ya. Weese be gyae waa nyakee wɔ ya, te duwulawula mɔ be gyae ke nyiŋ wɔ ya.

english_verse: “The Lamb in the center of the throne will be their shepherd. He will lead them to streams of life-giving water, and God will wipe all tears from their eyes.”
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi Nambu ŋon ɔ seŋɛɛ Wurubuarɛ gyooro gbeli keŋ nsana nsana nɛ, ŋon ne gyae la waa kɛɛ be dɔɔ ŋgba mena keŋ namensekɛɛlere ne kɛɛ ɔ namense dɔɔ nɛ. Ŋon ne gyae la waa ta be siaman mo wɔ gyu nyeedoŋ loŋ keŋ gyaŋ. Te Wurubuarɛ gbagba ne gyae la waa feefo ba siasaraŋ pou lee be siaman.”

english_verse: When the Lamb opened the seventh seal, there was silence in heaven for about half an hour.
translation_verse: Nambu ŋon ke wolee keeni nyetoorote keŋ bɔɔ mo tɔ tɔne keŋ nɔɔ nɛ, adido botɔɔ pou yɛɛwɔ yididi kelii ŋgba debaŋ yaale nɛ.

english_verse: I noticed that the seven angels who stood before God were each given a trumpet.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te me naa Wurubuarɛ kpilala nyetooro baŋ bɛɛ sem Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ. Te ba moo kaberɛse nyetooro fa wɔ.

english_verse: Another angel, who had a gold container for incense, came and stood at the altar. This one was given a lot of incense to offer with the prayers of God's people on the gold altar in front of the throne.
translation_verse: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋolo kɔŋawɔ kaa seŋ kedeesa boe keŋ gyaŋ. Na ɔ dana kɔba yayam nyefale ɔ nyiŋmaa man. Te ba moo oliifi burum ŋaale fa e nyi waa mo kpu na Wurubuarɛ balaŋ baŋ kefane keŋ bɛɛ fane nɛ, na waa mo gyakaa kedeesa boe keŋ bɔɔ mo kɔba yayam yɛɛ ke, te ke doo gyooro gbeli keŋ siaman nɛ dɔɔ.

english_verse: Then the smoke of the incense, together with the prayers of God's people, went up to God from the hand of the angel.
translation_verse: Keŋte oliifi keŋ nyɔɔse ŋan na Wurubuarɛ balaŋ baŋ kefane keŋ leewɔ Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon nyiŋmaa man gyu Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ.

english_verse: After this, the angel filled the incense container with fire from the altar and threw it on the earth. Thunder roared, lightning flashed, and the earth shook. The Trumpets
translation_verse: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon lese boalaŋ lee kedeesa boe keŋ dɔɔ do kɔba yayam nyefale keŋ man wulu. Te ɔ fuŋa ke na boalaŋ keŋ lee adido botɔɔ ke lɔ tɛɛle man. Karatetee dokoloŋ te ɔga ŋmaalewɔ te ɔ faewɔ na yeesa, te tɛɛle mɔ wosiwɔ. Wurubuarɛ kpilala kawola be kaberɛse

english_verse: The seven angels now got ready to blow their trumpets.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ kpilala nyetooro baŋ be dana kaberɛse ŋan nɛ, desina ba wose na baa wola be kaberɛse ŋenaŋ.

english_verse: When the first angel blew his trumpet, hail and fire mixed with blood were thrown down on the earth. A third of the earth, a third of the trees, and a third of all green plants were burned.
translation_verse: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ kpilale gyaŋgbate ŋon wola ɔ kaberɛ keŋ. Ɔ kawola ke pɛ te be fuŋa akpeleŋkpeleŋbia na boalaŋ keŋ fatabo kyakalɛɛ ke man nɛ lee adido kaa lɔ tɛɛle dɔɔ. Kekɔŋ mena nɛ, boalaŋ kpaa tɛɛle kei kekpase atooro man dekpeŋ dokoloŋ, na daase pou kekpase atooro man dekpeŋ dokoloŋ na ŋalese nalaŋ nyiŋan pou.

english_verse: When the second angel blew his trumpet, something like a great fiery mountain was thrown into the sea. A third of the sea turned to blood,
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle te Wurubuarɛ kpilale balate ŋon mɔ wola ɔ kaberɛ keŋ. Ɔ kawola ke nɛ, te be fuŋa kolo ŋgba bula dinaa daale ne kpaa la nɛ kaa lɔ ɛpo man. Karatetee dokoloŋ na ɛpo kekpase atooro man dekpeŋ dokoloŋ ta bese fatabo gooŋ.

english_verse: a third of the living creatures in the sea died, and a third of the ships were destroyed.
translation_verse: Ke man wonembia pou mɔ kekpase atooro man dekpeŋ dokoloŋ yekeewɔ. Te agbelese dinaana ŋan ŋe dɔŋ ɛpo dɔɔ nɛ, ŋe man kekpase atooro man dekpeŋ dokoloŋ wɔlɛɛwɔ pou.

english_verse: When the third angel blew his trumpet, a great star fell from heaven. It was burning like a torch, and it fell on a third of the rivers and on a third of the springs of water.
translation_verse: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ kpilale batoorote ŋon mɔ wola ɔ kaberɛ. Ɔ kawola ke nɛ, te kyolobii dinaa ŋon ɔ ne kpaa ŋgba boalaŋ na daakpee nɛ, leewɔ adido kaa yala booneŋ na loŋse ŋan ne puu lee tɛɛle man nɛ kekpase atooro man dekpeŋ dokoloŋ man wɔlɛɛ ŋa pou.

english_verse: The name of the star was Bitter, and a third of the water turned bitter. Many people died because the water was so bitter.
translation_verse: Kyolobii ŋonaŋ yele yɛna Ŋaŋte. Mena dɔɔ loŋse ŋan pou kekpase atooro man dekpeŋ dokoloŋ yɛɛ ŋaŋ, te balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ nyɔɔ loŋ kenaŋ nɛ, be man burum yekeewɔ lee ke ŋaŋ keŋ dɔɔ.

english_verse: When the fourth angel blew his trumpet, a third of the sun, a third of the moon, and a third of the stars were struck. They each lost a third of their light. So during a third of the day there was no light, and a third of the night was also without light.
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle te Wurubuarɛ kpilale banaarate ŋon mɔ wola ɔ kaberɛ. Ɔ kawola ke nɛ, te weese kekpase atooro man dekpeŋ, na gyaalaŋ kekpase atooro man dekpeŋ, na kyolobiise kekpase atooro man dekpeŋ pou gyoo ditiŋtɛɛ tuum. Wee dibiliŋ mɔ kekpase atooro man dekpeŋ dokoloŋ te nyiŋ keŋmaŋee korakora ya. Mena mɔ te be kpala nelim man akpeŋ atooro na, ke man dekpeŋ dokoloŋ mɔ te nyiŋ keŋmaŋee ya.

english_verse: Then I looked and saw a lone eagle flying across the sky. It was shouting, “Trouble, trouble, trouble to everyone who lives on earth! The other three angels are now going to blow their trumpets.”
translation_verse: Me ka seŋ mɛɛ kɛɛ nɛ, te me naa otoloku ŋolo na ɔ ne furufu adido adido. Keŋte me nyiiwɔ ɔ ne yako keŋkeŋ nyiaa, “Balaŋ baŋ baa kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ kelii debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ kpilala batooro akaŋ baŋ baa wola be kaberɛse nɛ, baa laako bola bola!”

english_verse: When the fifth angel blew his trumpet, I saw a star fall from the sky to earth. It was given the key to the tunnel that leads down to the deep pit.
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle te Wurubuarɛ kpilale banoŋte ŋon mɔ wola ɔ kaberɛ keŋ. Ɔ kawola ke nɛ, te me naa kyolobii ŋolo kuluwɔ lee adido kaa yala tɛɛle man. Botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ moo safowaa keŋ bɛɛ mo toro disim keŋ tɔɛ bɔɔ kyilimkyilim keŋ ke taŋ ɔto ya nɛ fa kyolobii ŋon.

english_verse: As it opened the tunnel, smoke poured out like the smoke of a great furnace. The sun and the air turned dark because of the smoke.
translation_verse: Ɔ ka toro bɔɔ keŋ nɔɔ nɛ, nyɔɔse leewɔ ke man ŋgba mena keŋ nyɔɔse na lee boalaŋ degyapaa man nɛ. Te nyɔɔse keŋ yela weese na ŋɔma pou biriwɔ tuum.

english_verse: Locusts came out of the smoke and covered the earth. They were given the same power that scorpions have.
translation_verse: Keŋte akpataana leewɔ nyɔɔse ŋan man kaa yala tɛɛle dɔɔ. Te be faa wɔ doŋ keŋ baa mo tɔ balaŋ ŋgba kanase nɛ.

english_verse: The locusts were told not to harm the grass on the earth or any plant or any tree. They were to punish only those people who did not have God's mark on their foreheads.
translation_verse: Be yako wɔ nyi be na wɔlɛɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ ŋalese na daase na abɔɔ ŋan ne tuu tɛɛle dɔɔ nɛ ya. Mɔna baa naase balaŋ baŋ Wurubuarɛ ta do wɔ nso be tirinɔɔse dɔɔ ya nɛ diyem.

english_verse: The locusts were allowed to make them suffer for five months, but not to kill them. The suffering they caused was like the sting of a scorpion.
translation_verse: Ba te fa wɔ doŋ nyi baa ko balaŋ benaŋ ya. Mɔna be faa wɔ doŋ bo nyi baa mo naase wɔ diyem gyaalaŋna banoŋ. Te diyem keŋ bɔɔ naa nɛ, wee wɔ ŋgba kana tɔɔ na walaŋ nɛ.

english_verse: In those days people will want to die, but they will not be able to. They will hope for death, but it will escape from them.
translation_verse: Mena weeya ŋenaŋ man nɛ, balaŋ baa gyae yeŋ nyi baa yeŋ mɔ bɔɔ gyae baa yeŋ ya. Baa gyae nyi yeŋ de kaa mo wɔ, mɔna yeŋ be gyae waa benaa wɔ ya.

english_verse: These locusts looked like horses ready for battle. On their heads they wore something like gold crowns, and they had human faces.
translation_verse: Mena akpataana benaŋ yɛɛ ŋgba dɛɛkyɔŋse baŋ bɔɔ desina wɔ baa mo gyu yoo nɛ. Abɔɔ ŋaale gyakaa ba nyeeya dɔɔ ŋgba kotɔtɔɔse ŋan bɔɔ mo kɔba yayam yɛɛ ŋa nɛ, te be siagbɛɛ mɔ yɛɛ ŋgba denibalaŋ nɛ.

english_verse: Their hair was like a woman's long hair, and their teeth were like those of a lion.
translation_verse: Ba wose wona yɛɛ ŋgba ala nyeenyɔɔse nɛ, te be kela mɔ yɛɛ ŋgba dinaate kela nɛ.

english_verse: On their chests they wore armor made of iron. Their wings roared like an army of horse-drawn chariots rushing into battle.
translation_verse: Na abɔɔ ŋaale mataa be konɔɔse dɔɔ, ŋgba nyeela pampasaŋ ŋan yoonɔɔtena na mo gyee be konɔɔ nɛ. Te be beŋkelɛɛ mɔ ne yɛɛ yeesa ŋgba dɛɛkyɔŋse burum ne tuu na be kɔtase bee gyu yoo nɛ.

english_verse: Their tails were like a scorpion's tail with a stinger that had the power to hurt someone for five months.
translation_verse: Na be dana gyuŋse ŋan ne tɔ balaŋ ŋgba kanase nɛ. Te doŋ keŋ baa mo naase balaŋ diyem gyaalaŋna banoŋ nɛ, doo bo be gyuŋse ŋenaŋ man.

english_verse: The first horrible thing has now happened! But wait. Two more horrible things will happen soon.
translation_verse: Kayeyɛɛ gyaŋgbate keŋ kaalaŋa laa nɛ. Ala mɔ dɔŋ gbɛɛ ne kɔŋ.

english_verse: Then the sixth angel blew his trumpet. I heard a voice speak from the four corners of the gold altar that stands in the presence of God.
translation_verse: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ kpilale loorote ŋon mɔ wola ɔ kaberɛ. Ɔ kawola ke nɛ, te me nyii woya ŋaale leewɔ Wurubuarɛ kedeesa boe keŋ bɔɔ mo kɔba yayam yɛɛ ke te ka seŋɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ, ka yenaase anaara ŋan man.

english_verse: The voice spoke to this angel and said, “Release the four angels who are tied up beside the great Euphrates River.”
translation_verse: Woya ŋenaŋ yako Wurubuarɛ kpilale loorote ŋon ɔ dana kaberɛ keŋ nɛ nyiaa, “Tina ɔbɔnsam kpilala banaara baŋ bɔɔ bake wɔ do boo dinaa Efurate man nɛ yela.”

english_verse: The four angels had been prepared for this very hour and day and month and year. Now they were set free to kill a third of all people.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ be tinaa wɔ yela. Na Wurubuarɛ te desina wɔ see fa mena debaŋ kei, na mena wee kei na mena gyaalaŋ kei na mena kulutoo kei, nyi baa koe tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ kekpase atooro man dekpeŋ dokoloŋ.

english_verse: One third of all people were killed by the three terrible troubles caused by the fire, the smoke, and the sulfur.
translation_verse: Boalaŋ na nyɔɔse na kpatakpela ŋan ba lee be nɔɔse man nɛ, ŋan yɛna kayeyɛɛ nombia ŋan ba ko tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ kekpase atooro man dekpeŋ dokoloŋ nɛ.

english_verse: The horses had powerful mouths, and their tails were like poisonous snakes that bite and hurt.
translation_verse: Dɛɛkyɔŋse baŋ doŋ keŋ bɛɛ mo koe balaŋ nɛ doe be nɔɔse na be gyuŋse man. Be gyuŋse yɛɛ ŋgba doma nɛ, te ŋe dana nyeeya ŋan bɛɛ mo gooli balaŋ.

english_verse: The people who lived through these terrible troubles did not turn away from the idols they had made, and they did not stop worshiping demons. They kept on worshiping idols that were made of gold, silver, bronze, stone, and wood. Not one of these idols could see, hear, or walk.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ kaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ keŋ be ta yem lee mena kayeyɛɛ nombia kɛŋa man ya nɛ, te tina be nombiakumɛɛ keyɛɛ yela ya. Ba te kyɛɛkee ba agyueŋ na baa tina abɔɔ ŋan baŋ gbagba ba mo be nyiŋmaase yɛɛ ŋa nɛ kasom yela ya. Be te tina feliŋkumɛɛ na bɛɛneŋ ŋan baŋ gbagba ba mo kɔba yayam yaa kɔba wolom yaa nyeela yaa boya yaa daase yɛɛ ŋa nɛ kasom yela ya. Mɔna mena bɛɛneŋ kɛwɔ bɛɛ naa ya, te ŋe bee nyii dei ya te ŋe bɛɛ tɛɛ mɔ ya.

english_verse: I saw another powerful angel come down from heaven. This one was covered with a cloud, and a rainbow was over his head. His face was like the sun, his legs were like columns of fire,
translation_verse: Kei wɔle nɛ, ma besewɔ naa Wurubuarɛ kpilale doŋte ŋolo bela na ɔ ta lee adido ɔ ne kɔŋ. Na debuŋta te kilisi ɔ wose gyaŋee ŋgba kegba te ɔ doona nɛ, te kolo mɔ te kilisi ɔ nyee gyaŋee ŋgba okyimaale nɛ. Ɔ siagbɛɛ ne ŋalakee ŋgba weese nɛ, te ɔ nawɔɔ mɔ yɛɛ ŋgba daakpeese na boalaŋ seŋɛɛ la ne kpaa nɛ.

english_verse: and with his hand he held a little scroll that had been unrolled. He stood there with his right foot on the sea and his left foot on the land.
translation_verse: Na ɔ dana tɔnebii daale keŋ bɔɔ gyuusu ke nɛ ɔ nyiŋmaa man. Keŋte ɔ moo ɔ dunoluŋ nae gyakaa ɛpo dɔɔ, na ɔ demena nae mɔ seŋɛɛ tɛɛle dɔɔ.

english_verse: Then he shouted with a voice that sounded like a growling lion. Thunder roared seven times.
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ fae yeesa keŋkeŋ ŋgba dinaate muusu la nɛ. Ɔ ke fae yeesa keŋ nɛ, te ɔga faewɔ betabonyetooro.

english_verse: After the thunder stopped, I was about to write what it had said. But a voice from heaven shouted, “Keep it secret! Don't write these things.”
translation_verse: Ɔga ke fae betabonyetooro keŋ nɛ, nafɔ me gyaewɔ nyi maa ŋmarase kpene keŋ mɔɔ nyii lee ɔga kefae keŋ man nɛ see. Mɔna me nyii woya ŋaale lee adido keŋ ne yako maŋ nyiaa, “Kyaŋ nombia ŋan n be nyii nɛ do n nyee man. Na ŋmarase ŋa see ya.”

english_verse: The angel I had seen standing on the sea and the land then held his right hand up toward heaven.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon mɔɔ naa ɔ seŋɛɛ keŋ ɔ nae daale gyakaa ɛpo dɔɔ, te ɔ nae daale mɔ seŋɛɛ tɛɛle dɔɔ nɛ gyinaa ɔ dunoluŋ nyiŋmaa adido.

english_verse: God told his secret plans to his servants the prophets, and it will all happen by the time the seventh angel sounds his trumpet.”
translation_verse: Mɔna debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ kpilale nyetoorote ŋon waa wola ɔ kaberɛ keŋ nɛ, debaŋ kenaŋ te nombia aweeseŋ ŋan Wurubuarɛ be yako fa ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ be yɛɛ wa akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔrawɔ nɛ, gyae ke kɔŋ mena.”

english_verse: Once again the voice from heaven spoke to me. It said, “Go and take the open scroll from the hand of the angel standing on the sea and the land.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te woya ŋan mɔɔ nyii lee adido kaalaŋ nɛ besewɔ kolosi fa maŋ bela nyiaa, “Ta gyu ke lɛɛ tɔnebii keŋ bɔɔ gyuusu ke nɛ lee Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon ɔ seŋɛɛ te ɔ nae daale gyakaa ɛpo dɔɔ, te ɔ nae daale mɔ seŋɛɛ tɛɛle man nɛ nyiŋmaa man.”

english_verse: When I went over to ask the angel for the little scroll, the angel said, “Take the scroll and eat it! Your stomach will turn sour, but the taste in your mouth will be sweet as honey.”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ me gyuuwɔ Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon gyaŋ nyi waa mo tɔnebii keŋ fa maŋ. Keŋte ɔ yako maŋ nyiaa, “Lɛɛ ke na n ke tao ke. N ne tao ke nɛ, ke gyae ke yɛɛ neŋ gyoŋ n nɔɔman ŋgba teebɔɔ nɛ. Mɔna akpaa n lila ke na, ke gyae ke yɛɛ ŋaŋ n tɔɔman.”

english_verse: I took the little scroll from the hand of the angel and ate it. The taste was sweet as honey, but my stomach turned sour.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te me lɛɛ tɔnebii keŋ lee Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon nyiŋmaa man te ma moo ke tao. Me kɛɛ tao ke nɛ, na ke dana gyoŋ ŋgba teebɔɔ nɛ me nɔɔman. Mɔna ma ke tao ke mo lila nɛ, me tɔɔman pou yɛɛ maŋ ŋaŋ bo.

english_verse: Then some voices said, “Keep on telling what will happen to the people of many nations, races, and languages, and also to kings.”
translation_verse: Ma ke tao ke taŋ nɛ te me nyii woya ŋaale yako maŋ nyiaa, “Kaboena nyi nya bese gyɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan ɔ be yako neŋ nɛ dekpeŋkpeŋ fa balaŋ burum, na tɛɛlese na deise na gyooneŋ ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba lese wola neŋ nɛ.”

english_verse: But don't measure the courtyard outside the temple building. Leave it out. It has been given to those people who don't know God, and they will trample all over the holy city for
translation_verse: Mɔna na maa tɛɛle keŋ ke doo Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ yenaase nɛ ya, nawolo nyi be ta mo botɔɔ fa balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ. Baŋ ne gyae la baa di doŋ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ keŋ dɔɔ gyaalaŋna sɔŋola na ala.

english_verse: These two witnesses are the two olive trees and the two lampstands that stand in the presence of the Lord who rules the earth.
translation_verse: Adansɛɛtena bala baŋ yɛna oliifi daase ala na kanease daase ala ŋan seŋɛɛ Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ tɛɛle kei pou Gbeŋgyoo e nɛ siaman nɛ.

english_verse: They have the power to lock up the sky and to keep rain from falling while they are prophesying. And whenever they want to, they can turn water to blood and cause all kinds of terrible troubles on earth.
translation_verse: Mena Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra bala kɛwɔ dana doŋ, keŋ baa tale tɔ gbɛɛ nyi debaŋ kamasɛ keŋ baa kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia na ɔga na ŋɛɛ ya. Be dana doŋ keŋ baa tale yela loŋse pou ka bese fatabo, te baa tale yela kayeyɛɛ kamasɛ mɔ ke yala tɛɛle kei dɔɔ debaŋ kamasɛ keŋ bɛɛ gyae.

english_verse: Their bodies will be left lying in the streets of the same great city where their Lord was nailed to a cross. And this city is spiritually like the city of Sodom or the country of Egypt.
translation_verse: Te baa tina ba woya yela donɔɔ dinaa keŋ abɔntere man. Mena donɔɔ kei man te be gyɔɔ be Gbeŋgyoo sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ ko. Balaŋ ta do mena donɔɔ kenaŋ yele nyi Sodom yaa Igyipite.

english_verse: For three and a half days the people of every nation, tribe, language, and race will stare at the bodies of these two witnesses and refuse to let them be buried.
translation_verse: Balaŋ baa lee donɔɔse kpookpoo, na deise kpookpoo na tɛɛlese kpookpoo man kɔŋ kaa kɛɛ woya benaŋ weeya atooro na yaale. Mɔna balaŋ baa bɛɛ nyi be na wuu wɔ ya.

english_verse: Everyone on earth will celebrate and be happy. They will give gifts to each other, because of what happened to the two prophets who caused them so much trouble.
translation_verse: Ba yeŋ gyae ke yɛɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ gyoŋ. Baa di wee kenaŋ ŋgba ba wee dinaa daale nɛ, na baa kyɛɛ dɔŋa abɔɔ. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra bala kɛwɔ naase balaŋ diyem nideli tɛɛle kei dɔɔ.

english_verse: But three and a half days later, God will breathe life into their bodies. They will stand up, and everyone who sees them will be terrified.
translation_verse: Mɔna weeya atooro na yaale kei wɔle nɛ, Wurubuarɛ yela ɔ weesɛɛ kaa gyoo be man te be foŋawɔ koro kyaa. Kei yela gyakoloŋ gyoo balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ naa wɔ nɛ nideli.

english_verse: The witnesses then heard a loud voice from heaven, saying, “Come up here.” And while their enemies were watching, they were taken up to heaven in a cloud.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ kpilala bala baŋ nyii woya ŋaale kolosiwɔ na wɔ keŋkeŋ lee adido nyiaa, “Ɛ gyeŋ kɔŋ adido kɛbo.” Keŋte ba moowɔ ŋɔma man gyeŋ gyu adido, na be kɔlala baŋ seŋɛɛ bɛɛ kɛɛ wɔ.

english_verse: At that same moment there was a terrible earthquake that destroyed a tenth of the city. Seven thousand people were killed, and the rest were frightened and praised the God who rules in heaven.
translation_verse: Mena debaŋ kenaŋ man nɛ, tɛɛle wosiwɔ nideli keŋ nyi donɔɔ dinaa keŋ kekpase kufu man dekpeŋ dokoloŋ yɛlɛɛwɔ. Tɛɛle kewosi kenaŋ mɔ koo balaŋ kakpoŋse nyetooro (

english_verse: The second horrible thing has now happened! And the third one will be here soon. The Seventh Trumpet
translation_verse: Kayeyɛɛ nombia ŋan alate kaalaŋa laa, te atoorote mɔ ne kɔŋ. Be kawola kaberɛ nyetoorote keŋ

english_verse: At the sound of the seventh trumpet, loud voices were heard in heaven. They said, “Now the kingdom of this world belongs to our Lord and to his Chosen One! And he will rule forever and ever!”
translation_verse: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ kpilale nyetoorote ŋon wola ɔ kaberɛ. Ɔ kawola ke nɛ, te me nyii woya ŋaale lee adido keŋkeŋ nyiaa, “Nɛnɛɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ gyoori ta bese de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ na ɔ bu nyeelɛɛre ŋon wui, te de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ ne gyae la waa di gyoori kekpaakekpaa.”

english_verse: When the nations got angry, you became angry too! Now the time has come for the dead to be judged. It is time for you to reward your servants the prophets and all your people who honor your name, no matter who they are. It is time to destroy everyone who has destroyed the earth.”
translation_verse: Balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ gyeŋ neŋ ya nɛ ta gyeŋ baŋ n dɔɔ, mɔna nɛnɛɛ te kaboena nyi nya lese n baŋ wola. Debaŋ te lii keŋ nyaŋ ke di balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ yekee nɛ nombia, na n ke tɔ n tewulɛɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra na n balaŋ baŋ pou bɛɛ som neŋ nɛ kom. N ke tɔ balaŋ baŋ bee yee neŋ nɛ, na balaŋ baŋ be dana yele, na balaŋ yakaa nyibaŋ pou kom. Te debaŋ te lii keŋ nyaŋ ke wɔlɛɛ balaŋ baŋ pou bɛɛ wɔlɛɛ tɛɛle kei nɛ.”

english_verse: The door to God's temple in heaven was then opened, and the sacred chest could be seen inside the temple. I saw lightning and heard roars of thunder. The earth trembled and huge hailstones fell to the ground.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te be kyaŋa Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ ke kyaa adido nɛ toro, te me naa Nɔɔkebakesee Adakaa keŋ. Keŋte ɔga ŋmaalewɔ te ɔ faewɔ, te tɛɛle wosiwɔ te akpeleŋkpeleŋbia dinaana yalawɔ.

english_verse: She was about to give birth, and she was crying because of the great pain.
translation_verse: Na alo ŋonaŋ dana fulu keŋ ɔ na doŋee, te weeleŋ keŋ dɔɔ ɔ ne fae yeesa.

english_verse: Something else appeared in the sky. It was a huge red dragon with seven heads and ten horns, and a crown on each of its seven heads.
translation_verse: Keŋte ma besewɔ naa gyakoloŋ nombia ŋaale lee adido bela. Me naa wonembukum yayam dekpatakpere ŋolo na ɔ yɛɛ ŋgba laŋaŋ nɛ, na ɔ dana nyeeya nyetooro na woo kufu. Na gyooro kotɔtɔɔse nyetooro gyakaa ɔ nyeeya ŋan dɔɔ.

english_verse: With its tail, it dragged a third of the stars from the sky and threw them down to the earth. Then the dragon turned toward the woman, because it wanted to eat her child as soon as it was born.
translation_verse: Akpaa n kpala kyolobiise man akpeŋ atooro na, wonembu ŋon moo ɔ gyuŋ gberaa ŋe man dekpeŋ dokoloŋ lɔ tɛɛle man. Na wonembu ŋonaŋ seŋɛɛ alo ŋon ɔ na doŋee nɛ siaman, nyi akpaa ɔ lola ɔ bu ŋon na waa mo e lila debokenaŋ man.

english_verse: The woman gave birth to a son, who would rule all nations with an iron rod. The boy was snatched away. He was taken to God and placed on his throne.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te alo ŋon lola bu baale ŋon waa di gyoori tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ pou dɔɔ, keŋ ɔ be gyae waa wii be waraŋase ya nɛ. Ɔ ka lola bu ŋon pɛ, te be kaa too e lɛo lee ɔ naa ŋon nyiŋmaa man mo e gyu kefa Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa ɔ gyooro gbeli dɔɔ nɛ.

english_verse: The woman ran into the desert to a place that God had prepared for her. There she would be taken care of for
translation_verse: Keŋte alo ŋon yeuwɔ ka gyoo kefula man, botɔɔ keŋ Wurubuarɛ be desina see e nyi baa kɛɛ ɔ dɔɔ gyaalaŋna sɔŋola na ala.

english_verse: A war broke out in heaven. Michael and his angels were fighting against the dragon and its angels.
translation_verse: Keŋte yoo yalawɔ adido botɔɔ. Wurubuarɛ kpilale kegyia Maekɛle na ɔ tewulɛɛ yoowɔ na wonembukum dekpatakpere ŋon na ɔ tewulɛɛ.

english_verse: But the dragon lost the battle. It and its angels were forced out of their places in heaven
translation_verse: Mɔna wonembukum ŋon na ɔ tewulɛɛ te yɛɛ doŋ ya, mena dɔɔ be te nyiŋ daseŋae adido botɔɔ ya.

english_verse: and were thrown down to the earth. Yes, that old snake and his angels were thrown out of heaven! That snake, who fools everyone on earth, is known as the devil and Satan.
translation_verse: Keŋte be gegi wonembukum ŋon lee adido botɔɔ. Ŋon yɛna beneŋ beneŋ dom ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi yele wɔlɛɛre yaa ɔbɔnsam, ŋon ɔ na beo balaŋ pou tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ. Te be fuŋa ŋon na ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ pou lee adido kaalɔ tɛɛle man.

english_verse: Then I heard a voice from heaven shout, “Our God has shown his saving power, and his kingdom has come! God's own Chosen One has shown his authority. Satan accused our people in the presence of God day and night. Now he has been thrown out!
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te me nyii woya ŋaale adido ne yako keŋkeŋ nyiaa, “Nɛnɛɛ debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa lɛɛ ɔ balaŋ nyee nɛ te lii. Wurubuarɛ ta lese ɔ doŋ na ɔ gyoori debɔɔ, te Kristo nyeelɛɛre ŋon mɔ ta lese wola nyi ɔ dana doŋ tɛɛle kei balaŋ pou dɔɔ. Nawolo nyi be te fuŋ ɔbɔnsam ŋon ɔ na mo nombiakumɛɛ gyakaa Kristotena dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ siaman nelim na ka weese nɛ ke lɔ tɛɛle man.

english_verse: “Our people defeated Satan because of the blood of the Lamb and the message of God. They were willing to give up their lives.
translation_verse: Nambu ŋon ɔ ba mo ɔ wose fa kedeesa nɛ fatabo, na anokoare nombia ŋan bɔɔ kolosi nɛ, ŋan te ba moowɔ di ɔbɔnsam dɔɔ. Be te wii baŋ gbagba nyeedoŋ ya, mɔna ba moo ba wose fa yeŋ lee ba kelɛɛdi dɔɔ.

english_verse: “The heavens should rejoice, together with everyone who lives there. But pity the earth and the sea, because the devil was thrown down to the earth. He knows his time is short, and he is very angry.”
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ ɛmɛɛ baŋ pou ɛ kyaa Wurubuarɛ man nɛ, ɛ yela ke yɛɛ ŋon gyoŋ. Mɔ ɛmɛɛ baŋ pou ɛ kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ na ɛpo man nɛ ɛ laako, nawolo nyi ɔbɔnsam ta mo baŋ tisi lee Wurubuarɛ man kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ. Ɔ ta mo baŋ tisi kɔŋ, nawolo nyi ɔ gyeŋ nyi ɔ weeya ŋan be kaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ ta boo ya!”

english_verse: When the dragon realized it had been thrown down to the earth, it tried to make trouble for the woman who had given birth to a son.
translation_verse: Debaŋ keŋ wonembukum ŋon be naa nyi be te fuŋ e lee Wurubuarɛ man kaa lɔ tɛɛle man nɛ, te ɔ korowɔ ɔ ne gegi alo ŋon ɔ ba lola bu baale ŋon nɛ.

english_verse: But the woman was given two wings like those of a huge eagle, so she could fly into the desert. There she would escape from the snake and be taken care of for a time, two times, and half a time.
translation_verse: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ faa alo ŋon otoloku dinaa ŋolo beŋkelɛɛ ala, na waa nyiŋ tale mo furufu yela wonembukum ŋon gyu kefula man, botɔɔ keŋ bɔɔ desina see e nyi baa kɛɛ ɔ dɔɔ kulutooneŋ atooro na yale nɛ, na wonembukum ŋon be nyiŋ gbɛɛ ke yɛɛ e kolo botɔɔ ya.

english_verse: The snake then spewed out water like a river to sweep the woman away.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te wonembukum ŋon puu loŋ lee ɔ nɔɔman ŋgba boo kakpela nɛ, na ke gyɔ alo ŋon laŋ.

english_verse: But the earth helped her and swallowed the water that had come from the dragon's mouth.
translation_verse: Mɔna tɛɛle kyɔɔ alo ŋon. Tɛɛle bɛɛwɔ te ke nyɔɔ loŋ keŋ pou wonembukum ŋon be puu lee ɔ nɔɔman nɛ.

english_verse: This made the dragon terribly angry with the woman. So it started a war against the rest of her children. They are the people who obey God and are faithful to what Jesus did and taught.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ wonembukum ŋon gyeŋa baŋ alo ŋon dɔɔ, te ɔ laŋawɔ yela e nyi ɔ kaa yoo na ɔ bia akaŋ baŋ. Alo ŋonaŋ bia benaŋ yɛna balaŋ baŋ pou bee di Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan dɔɔ, te bee di Kristo wose man adansɛɛ nɛ. Keŋte wonembukum ŋon gyuuwɔ ka seŋ ɛpo nɔɔman.

english_verse: I looked and saw a beast coming up from the sea. This one had ten horns and seven heads, and a crown was on each of its ten horns. On each of its heads were names that were an insult to God.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te me naa wonembukum ŋolo na ɔ ta lee ɛpo man ɔ ne kɔŋ. Na ɔ dana woo kufu na nyeeya nyetooro, te gyooro kotɔtɔɔse wuɛ woo ŋan pou dɔɔ. Te be ŋmarase mmusuo nombia ŋaale gyakaa nyee kamasɛ dɔɔ.

english_verse: The beast I saw had the body of a leopard, the feet of a bear, and the mouth of a lion. The dragon handed over its own power and throne and great authority to this beast.
translation_verse: Wonembukum ŋon mɔɔ naa nɛ yɛɛ ŋgba mokɔɔte nɛ. Ɔ dana gelase ŋgba ɔsakuru nɛ, te ɔ nɔɔ mɔ yɛɛ ŋgba dinaate nɛ. Keŋte wonembukum gyaŋgbate ŋon moo ɔ doŋ na ɔ gyoorobiiri na ɔ kegyiiri ŋan pou ɔ dana nɛ fa wonembukum kei.

english_verse: One of its heads seemed to have been fatally wounded, but now it was well. Everyone on earth marveled at this beast,
translation_verse: Gyam dinaa daale kyaawɔ kyaa wonembukum ŋon nyee keŋ daale dɔɔ keŋ nafɔ ke gyae ke tale ko e, mɔna gyam kenaŋ ta yem. Wonembukum ŋonaŋ nombia dana gyakoloŋ, mena dɔɔ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ pou sila e.

english_verse: and they worshiped the dragon who had given its authority to the beast. They also worshiped the beast and said, “No one is like this beast! No one can fight against it.”
translation_verse: Balaŋ burum som wonembukum dekpatakpere ŋon, nawolo nyi na ɔ ta mo ɔ doŋ fa wonembukum kei. Ba som wonembukum kei te be kɛlɛɛ e nyiaa, “Amɔte kyaa la ŋgba wonembukum kei nɛ, te amɔte mɔ ne gyae la waa tale yoo na e?”

english_verse: The beast cursed God, and it cursed the name of God. It even cursed the place where God lives, as well as everyone who lives in heaven with God.
translation_verse: Te wonembukum ŋon gyuusu ɔ nɔɔ kolosi mmusuo nombia tia Wurubuarɛ wɔlɛɛ ɔ yele, na ɔ deni dinaa keŋ na baŋ pou be kyaa Wurubuarɛ man nɛ.

english_verse: It was allowed to fight against God's people and defeat them. It was also given authority over the people of every tribe, nation, language, and race.
translation_verse: Keŋte ba besewɔ fa e doŋ nyi waa mo yoo na Wurubuarɛ balaŋ baŋ na waa di be dɔɔ. Te be faa e doŋ bela nyi waa mo di akpaŋalaŋse, na tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ na deise na loŋtoro kamasɛ balaŋ dɔɔ.

english_verse: The beast was worshiped by everyone whose name wasn't written before the time of creation in the book of the Lamb who was killed.
translation_verse: Tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ burum baa som wonembukum kei. Mena balaŋ kɛwɔ yɛna balaŋ baŋ ba te ŋmarase ba yela do nyeedoŋ tɔne keŋ man ya nɛ. Nyeedoŋ tɔne kenaŋ yɛɛ Nambu ŋon ɔ ba mo ɔ wose fa kedeesa leenaŋ tɛɛle kei kewalaŋ nɛ wui.

english_verse: If you are doomed to be captured, you will be captured. If you are doomed to be killed by a sword, you will be killed by a sword. This means God's people must learn to endure and be faithful!
translation_verse: Akpaa yɛɛ nyi baa kyaŋ ŋolo yoo man na, yoo man te baa taŋ kyaŋ e. Te akpaa yɛɛ nyi ŋolo waa yeŋ sila nɔɔman na, sila nɔɔman te waa taŋ yeŋ. “Kei nawolo baa nyi, kaboena nyi Wurubuarɛ balaŋ baa nyiŋ konɔɔ na baa seŋ keŋkeŋ ba kelɛɛdi man.” Wonembukum ŋon ɔ be bɛɛ lee tɛɛle man nɛ

english_verse: I now saw another beast. This one came out of the ground. It had two horns like a lamb, but spoke like a dragon.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te ma besewɔ naa wonembukum ŋolo te bɛɛ lee tɛɛle man bela. Na ɔ dana woo ala ŋgba nam nɛ, mɔna ɔ kekolosi yɛɛ ŋgba wonembukum dekpatakpere ŋon nɛ.

english_verse: It worked for the beast whose fatal wound had been healed. And it used all its authority to force the earth and its people to worship that beast.
translation_verse: Doŋ keŋ wonembukum gyaŋgbate ŋon dana nɛ, keŋ te balate kei mɔ dana ɔ ne yɛɛ ɔ tom ɔ dɔɔ ŋon yele man. Te ɔ yela tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ pou som wonembukum gyaŋgbate ŋon ɔ dana gyaŋtare dinaa ɔ nyeeman nɛ.

english_verse: It worked mighty miracles, and while people watched, it even made fire come down from the sky.
translation_verse: Wonembukum kei mɔ yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia dinaana. Ɔ yela boalaŋ gba koraŋ leewɔ adido kɔŋ kaa yala tɛɛle man te balaŋ pou naawɔ.

english_verse: This second beast fooled people on earth by working miracles for the first one. Then it talked them into making an idol in the form of the beast that did not die after being wounded by a sword.
translation_verse: Lee gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan ɔ be yɛɛ wonembukum gyaŋgbate ŋon yele man nɛ dɔɔ, ɔ talewɔ beo tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ pou. Te ɔ ŋere wɔ te be maa bane ŋgba wonembukum gyaŋgbate ŋon, bɔɔ mo yoosila gooli e ɔ nyeeman yeŋ yeŋ te ɔ ta yem ya nɛ.

english_verse: It was allowed to put breath into the idol, so it could speak. Everyone who refused to worship the idol of the beast was put to death.
translation_verse: Be faa wonembukum balate kei doŋ nyi waa fa bane ŋon bɔɔ maa ŋgba wonembukum gyaŋgbate ŋon nɛ nyeedoŋ na waa tale kolosi, na waa yela baa koe baŋ pou bɔɔ bɛɛ nyi bɔɔ som bane ŋonaŋ ya nɛ.

english_verse: All people were forced to put a mark on their right hand or forehead. Whether they were powerful or weak, rich or poor, free people or slaves,
translation_verse: Te mena wonembukum kei besewɔ ŋere walaŋ kamasɛ, balaŋ baŋ be dana yele na balaŋ yakaa nyibaŋ, na baŋ be dana kɔba na ayematena na gyoorobia na gbeŋa pou yela ba doo wɔ nso be dunoluŋ nyiŋmaase man na be tirinɔɔse dɔɔ.

english_verse: they all had to have this mark, or else they could not buy or sell anything. This mark stood for the name of the beast and for the number of its name.
translation_verse: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ be dana wonembukum gyaŋgbate ŋon nso keŋ ya nɛ, ɔtenate be dana gbɛɛ nyi waa yɔɔ kolo yaa waa lɛɛ kolo ya. Nso kei yɛna wonembukum ŋon yele, yaa ɔ yele keŋ nɔmba.

english_verse: Then I heard a sound from heaven that was like a roaring flood or loud thunder or even like the music of harps.
translation_verse: Keŋte me nyii woya ŋaale lee adido ŋgba boo wuu la waale ne lam nɛ, te ka besewɔ wii ŋgba ɔga fae la nɛ. Woya ŋan mɔɔ nyii nɛ, ŋe yɛɛ ŋgba baale ne gyɔɔ na adolɛɛse nɛ.

english_verse: And a new song was being sung in front of God's throne and in front of the four living creatures and the elders. No one could learn that song, except the
translation_verse: Mena balaŋ kɛwɔ seŋawɔ Wurubuarɛ gyooro gbeli keŋ, na wonise banaara baŋ na kegyiise baŋ siaman kpee nɔɔneŋ wɔle. Te ŋolo te tale kasee nɔɔneŋ kenaŋ kpu na balaŋ kakpoŋse kufu bala akpeŋ kufu ala (

english_verse: They never tell lies, and they are innocent. The Messages of the Three Angels
translation_verse: Be te di dekara see korakora ya, te nombiikum kamasɛ mɔ be mataa na wɔ ya. Wurubuarɛ kpilala batooro baŋ

english_verse: I saw another angel. This one was flying across the sky and had the eternal good news to announce to the people of every race, tribe, language, and nation on earth.
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle te ma besewɔ naa Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋolo na ɔ ne furufu feliŋ man adido. Na ɔ dana Kristo nombia kpaakpaa ŋan ŋe kyaa kekpaakekpaa nɛ waa kolosi fa deise, na akpaŋalaŋse na tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ pou.

english_verse: The angel shouted, “Worship and honor God! The time has come for him to judge everyone. Kneel down before the one who created heaven and earth, the oceans, and every stream.”
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ kolosiwɔ keŋkeŋ nyiaa, “Ɛ yee Wurubuarɛ na ɛ ka lese ɔ yele, nawolo nyi ɔ nombia kedi debaŋ keŋ te lii. Ɛ som Wurubuarɛ, nawolo nyi ŋon yɛɛna adido na ateta na ɛpo na loŋse pou.”

english_verse: A second angel followed and said, “The great city of Babylon has fallen! This is the city that made all nations drunk and immoral. Now God is angry, and Babylon has fallen.”
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ kpilale balate ŋolo mɔ kɔŋawɔ kaa yako nyiaa, “Babilɔn donɔɔ dinaa keŋ te yɛlɛɛ. Ɔ te yale, nawolo nyi ɔ ta mo ɔ kafoŋ nombia kyaŋee tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ pou. Ɔ kafoŋ kei yɛɛ yaa ŋgba soloŋ doŋte te ɔ moowɔ fa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ te be nyɔɔwɔ dɛɛse nɛ.”

english_verse: Finally, a third angel came and shouted: Here is what will happen if you worship the beast and the idol and have the mark of the beast on your hand or forehead.
translation_verse: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ kpilale batoorote ŋolo mɔ kaa yakowɔ keŋkeŋ nyiaa, “Akpaa ŋolo da som wonembukum ŋon, yaa bane ŋon bɔɔ maa ŋgba ŋon nɛ, yaa ɔ sɛɛ te ba do e nso ɔ nyiŋmaa man yaa ɔ tirinɔɔ dɔɔ nɛ, be gyae ke de fa e ya.

english_verse: You will have to drink the wine that God gives to everyone who makes him angry. You will feel his mighty anger, and you will be tortured with fire and burning sulfur, while the holy angels and the Lamb look on.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ waa gyeŋ baŋ ɔtenate dɔɔ na waa wɔŋ ɔ deŋele nideli. Gyae ke yɛɛ ŋgba Wurubuarɛ gbagba moona soloŋ doŋte do leenɔɔ keŋ nawolo nyi ɔ ta gyeŋ baŋ nɛ man fa e. Mena walaŋ ŋonaŋ waa naa diyem nideli boalaŋ keŋ ne kpaa ŋgba ba moo kpatakpela bo do ke man nɛ Wurubuarɛ kpilala na Nambu ŋon siaman.

english_verse: If you worship the beast and the idol and accept the mark of its name, you will be tortured day and night. The smoke from your torture will go up forever and ever, and you will never be able to rest.
translation_verse: Boalaŋ keŋ man mena walaŋ ŋonaŋ waa yeyɛɛ nɛ, ke nyɔɔse gyae ka gyeŋ gyu adido kekpaakekpaa. Te balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ som wonembukum ŋon, na bane ŋon bɔɔ maa ŋgba wonembukum ŋonaŋ nɛ, na baŋ pou bɔɔ do wɔ wonembukum ŋonaŋ nso nɛ, bɔɔ gyae baa fɛɛfo korakora ya. Baa naa diyem nideli nelim na ka weese.”

english_verse: God's people must learn to endure. They must also obey his commands and have faith in Jesus.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ kaboena nyi Wurubuarɛ balaŋ baŋ bee di Wurubuarɛ mmaraase dɔɔ, te be silana Yesu anokoare man nɛ, baa nyiŋ konɔɔ na baa seŋ keŋkeŋ gbɛɛ kamasɛ man.

english_verse: Then I heard a voice from heaven say, “Put this in writing. From now on, the Lord will bless everyone who has faith in him when they die.” The Spirit answered, “Yes, they will rest from their hard work, and they will be rewarded for what they have done.” The Earth Is Harvested
translation_verse: Keŋte me nyii woya ŋaale lee adido bela nyiaa, “Ŋmarase see nyiaa, ‘Balaŋ baŋ pou baa yeŋ de Gbeŋgyoo yele man lee gyɛŋ wee kei ne gyu nɛ, baŋ na nyeebam.’ ” Keŋte Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon mɔ sɛɛwɔ nyiaa, “Ayi, ke yɛɛ ampaŋ. Baa fɛɛfo lee be kaale pou man, nawolo nyi be nombia ŋan bɔɔ yɛɛ nɛ ne gyae la ke sila wɔ.”

english_verse: I looked and saw a bright cloud, and someone who seemed to be the Son of Man was sitting on the cloud. He wore a gold crown on his head and held a sharp sickle in his hand.
translation_verse: Ma besewɔ gɛɛse kɛɛ na, te me naa ŋɔma wolomɛɛ ŋaale. Te me naa ŋolo kyaa ŋɔma ŋenaŋ dɔɔ ŋgba “Deniwalaŋ” nɛ. Ɔ wuuna gyooro kotɔtɔɔ ɔ nyee dɔɔ, te ɔ dana sakawa keŋ kɛɛ bee baŋbaŋ nɛ ɔ nyiŋmaa man.

english_verse: The one on the cloud swung his sickle and harvested the crops.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ walaŋ ŋon ɔ kyaa ŋɔma ŋan dɔɔ nɛ, fuŋa ɔ sakawa keŋ kɛrɛɛ abɔɔ ŋan pou ŋɔɔ yɛɛ tɛɛle dɔɔ nɛ.

english_verse: Another angel with a sharp sickle then came out of the temple in heaven.
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle te Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋolo mɔ besewɔ lee Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man adido bela. Na ŋon mɔ dana sakawa baŋbaŋ daale.

english_verse: After this, an angel with power over fire came from the altar and shouted to the angel who had the sickle. He said, “All grapes on earth are ripe! Harvest them with your sharp sickle.”
translation_verse: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋolo mɔ leewɔ botɔɔ keŋ bee deesi fa Wurubuarɛ nɛ man kɔŋ. Wurubuarɛ kpilale kei ne kɛɛ na kedeesa boalaŋ keŋ dɔɔ. Te ɔ fae yeesa keŋkeŋ yako walaŋ ŋon ɔ dana sakawa baŋbaŋ keŋ nyiaa, “Mo n sakawa keŋ na n ke kɔ gerepo saase ŋan pou ŋɔɔ yɛɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ lee ŋe woŋse ŋan man.”

english_verse: The angel swung his sickle on earth and cut off its grapes. He threw them into a pit where they were trampled on as a sign of God's anger.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon mɔ fuŋa ɔ sakawa keŋ kɔ gerepo saase ŋan pou lee ŋe woŋse man, te ɔ fuŋa ŋa do bɔɔ dinaa keŋ man bɛɛ ŋmaase bia ŋan lese ŋa loŋ mo yɛɛ soloŋ nɛ. Mena bɔɔ kei seŋɛɛ fa Wurubuarɛ baŋ kum keŋ.

english_verse: The pit was outside the city, and when the grapes were mashed, blood flowed out. The blood turned into a river that was about
translation_verse: Keŋte be sakatee gerepo bia ŋan botɔɔ ŋmaase ŋa lese ŋa loŋ donɔɔ keŋ nɔɔman. Te loŋ keŋ ba lee ŋe man nɛ bese fatabo te ŋe laŋawɔ ŋgba boo nɛ. Fatabo kenaŋ kelaŋ dɔɔloŋ man gyae ke yɛɛ ŋgba maelese sɔŋonoŋse ala nɛ (

english_verse: After this, I looked at the sky and saw something else that was strange and important. Seven angels were bringing the seven last terrible troubles. When these are ended, God will no longer be angry.
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle te ma besewɔ naa gyakoloŋ nombii dinaa daale Wurubuarɛ man adido bela. Me naa Wurubuarɛ kpilala nyetooro baale na be dana kayeyɛɛ nombia nyetooro. Kayeyɛɛ nombia kɛŋa yɛna Wurubuarɛ balaŋ deŋele kewɔŋ ɔtomante keŋ. Nawolo nyi ŋa de kaa lam na, Wurubuarɛ mɔ wɔe gyae ke fɛɛ.

english_verse: Then I saw something that looked like a glass sea mixed with fire, and people were standing on it. They were the ones who had defeated the beast and the idol and the number that tells the name of the beast. God had given them harps,
translation_verse: Keŋte me naa kolo gyooro gbeli keŋ siaman ŋgba ɛpo loŋ nɛ. Na ke yɛɛ geleŋgeleŋ te kɛɛ ŋalakee ŋgba diiku nɛ, na boalaŋ mɔ kyakalɛɛ ke man. Te me naa balaŋ baale seŋɛɛ ɛpo kenaŋ nɔɔman. Balaŋ benaŋ yɛna balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ yoo di wonembukum ŋon na bane ŋon bɔɔ maa wonembukum ŋonaŋ dinɔɔ man, te ɔ nɔmba seŋɛɛ fa ɔ yele dɔɔ nɛ. Na be dana adolɛɛse ŋan Wurubuarɛ be fa wɔ nɛ.

english_verse: and they were singing the song his servant Moses and the Lamb had sung. They were singing, “Lord God All-Powerful, you have done great and marvelous things. You are the ruler of all nations, and you do what is right and fair.
translation_verse: Na bee kpee Wurubuarɛ tobaale Mosesi nɔɔneŋ, na Nambu ŋon nɔɔneŋ nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ doŋ pou nyee, n nombia ŋan n ne yɛɛ nɛ yɛɛ nombia dinaana ŋa, te ŋe dana gyakoloŋ. Nyaŋ yɛna tɛɛle kei dɔɔ pou gyoo. N nombia doo na ŋe gbɛɛ, te ŋe yɛɛ anokoare mɔ.

english_verse: After this, I noticed something else in heaven. The sacred tent used for a temple was open.
translation_verse: Kei wɔle te me kɛɛ adido na, te me naawɔ na Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa tɔɔmante keŋ ne di adansɛɛ nyi Wurubuarɛ kyaa botɔɔ nɛ ta toro.

english_verse: And the seven angels who were bringing the terrible troubles were coming out of it. They were dressed in robes of pure white linen and wore belts made of pure gold.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te me naa Wurubuarɛ kpilala nyetooro baŋ be dana kayeyɛɛ nombia nyetooro ŋan nɛ, ta lee Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man. Ba doona akaralɛɛ kegbase wurikyim ŋan ŋɛɛ ŋalakee nɛ, te ba moo kɔba yayam abɔsɔɔse kpaŋ karase be konɔɔse man.

english_verse: One of the four living creatures gave each of the seven angels a bowl made of gold. These bowls were filled with the anger of God who lives forever and ever.
translation_verse: Keŋte wonise banaara baŋ man ŋolo moo kɔba yayam nyefalɛɛ nyetooro fa Wurubuarɛ kpilala nyetooro baŋ. Abɔɔ ŋan doo nyefalɛɛ ŋan man nɛ, yɛɛ kayeyɛɛ kumɛɛ ŋan Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa kekpaakekpaa nɛ waa mo baŋ mo ŋa do tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ nɛ.

english_verse: The temple quickly filled with smoke from the glory and power of God. No one could enter it until the seven angels had finished pouring out the seven last troubles.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ gyoorobiiri na ɔ doŋ yela nyɔɔse kaa wulu Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man. Mena dɔɔ ŋolo te tale gyoo Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man ya, kelii debaŋ keŋ kayeyɛɛ nombia nyetooro ŋan Wurubuarɛ ba mo fa ɔ kpilala nyetooro baŋ nɛ taŋawɔ.

english_verse: From the temple I heard a voice shout to the seven angels, “Go and empty the seven bowls of God's anger on the earth.”
translation_verse: Keŋte me nyii woya ŋaale Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man ne yako Wurubuarɛ kpilala nyetooro baŋ keŋkeŋ nyiaa, “Ɛ gyu ke kyɛɛkee Wurubuarɛ baŋ kumɛɛ ŋan ŋe doo ɛ nyefalɛɛ nyetooro ŋan man nɛ woli tɛɛle dɔɔ.”

english_verse: The first angel emptied his bowl on the earth. At once ugly and painful sores broke out on everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ kpilale gyaŋgbate ŋon gyuuwɔ ke kyɛɛkee kpene keŋ doo ɔ nyefale keŋ man nɛ woli tɛɛle dɔɔ. Ɔ ke kyɛɛkee ke woli tɛɛle dɔɔ pɛ, te gyamɛɛ kumɛɛ ŋaale kaa gyɔɔ balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ do wɔ wonembukum ŋon nso, te bɛɛ som bane ŋon bɔɔ maa wonembukum ŋonaŋ dinɔɔ man nɛ.

english_verse: The second angel emptied his bowl on the sea. At once the sea turned into blood like that of a dead person, and every living thing in the sea died.
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle te Wurubuarɛ kpilale balate ŋon mɔ kyɛɛkee kpene keŋ doo ɔ nyefale keŋ man nɛ do ɛpo man. Botɔɔ te ɛpo loŋ keŋ kyɛɛkeewɔ yɛɛ ŋgba walaŋ deyeŋe fatabo nɛ, te wonembu kamasɛ ŋon ɔ doo ɛpo man nɛ yeŋawɔ.

english_verse: The third angel emptied his bowl into the rivers and streams. At once they turned to blood.
translation_verse: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ kpilale batoorote ŋon mɔ kyɛɛkee kpene keŋ doo ɔ nyefale man nɛ do booneŋ na loŋse man, te ŋe popou bese fatabose.

english_verse: Then I heard the angel, who has power over water, say, “You have always been, and you always will be the holy God. You had the right to judge in this way.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te me nyii Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon ɔ ne kɛɛ loŋse ŋan dɔɔ nɛ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ ŋon n wulaa n kyaa lee kewalaŋ keŋ, te nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ ŋon n kyaa debaŋ kei. Nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ ŋon ayimɛɛ kamasɛ be doo n man ya. Te n deŋele kewɔŋ keŋ n ne wɔŋ balaŋ nɛ, doo na ke gbɛɛ.

english_verse: They poured out the blood of your people and your prophets. So you gave them blood to drink as they deserve!”
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi ba koe n balaŋ na nya akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ. Mena dɔɔ te n moo fatabo fa wɔ nyi baa nyɔɔ, ŋgba mena keŋ kaboena wɔ nɛ.”

english_verse: After this, I heard the altar shout, “Yes, Lord God All-Powerful, your judgments are honest and fair.”
translation_verse: Keŋte me nyii ŋolo kolosiwɔ lee botɔɔ keŋ bee deesi nɛ nyiaa, “Ampaŋ, de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ doŋ pou nyee, n nombia kedi yɛɛ anokoare te ke doo na ke gbɛɛ.”

english_verse: The fourth angel emptied his bowl on the sun, and it began to scorch people like fire.
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle te Wurubuarɛ kpilale banaarate ŋon mɔ kyɛɛkee kpene keŋ doo ɔ nyefale keŋ man nɛ woli weese dɔɔ. Te be faa weese doŋ nyi waa nyakee balaŋ ŋgba boalaŋ nɛ.

english_verse: Everyone was scorched by its great heat, and all of them cursed the name of God who had power over these terrible troubles. But no one turned to God and praised him.
translation_verse: Mena dɔɔ weese ŋon nyakee balaŋ baŋ nideli, te be falaa Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ dana doŋ mena kayeyɛɛ nombia ŋan dɔɔ nɛ yele. Mɔna be bɛe ba te kyɛɛkee ba agyueŋ na baa tina be nombiakumɛɛ keyɛɛ yela, na baa lese Wurubuarɛ yele ya.

english_verse: The fifth angel emptied his bowl on the throne of the beast. At once darkness covered its kingdom, and its people began biting their tongues in pain.
translation_verse: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ kpilale banoŋte ŋon mɔ kyɛɛkee kpene keŋ doo ɔ nyefale keŋ man nɛ woli wonembukum ŋon gyooro gbeli keŋ dɔɔ. Botɔɔ te ditiŋtɛɛ gyoo wonembukum ŋon gyoori keŋ pou. Kayeyɛɛ keŋ be kɔŋ balaŋ dɔɔ nɛ, yela be nyii weeleŋ nideli te be tao be kela.

english_verse: And because of their painful sores, they cursed the God who rules in heaven. But still they did not stop doing evil things.
translation_verse: Be gyamɛɛ ŋan bɔɔ nyim na weeleŋ keŋ bɔɔ nyii nɛ, yela be falaa Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ. Mɔna be bɛe ba te kyɛɛkee ba agyueŋ na baa tina be nombiakumɛɛ keyɛɛ yela, na baa kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ ya.

english_verse: The sixth angel emptied his bowl on the great Euphrates River, and it completely dried up to make a road for the kings from the east.
translation_verse: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ kpilale loorote ŋon mɔ kyɛɛkee kpene keŋ doo ɔ nyefale keŋ man nɛ, do boo dinaa keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi Efurate nɛ man. Te loŋ keŋ pou yoowɔ, na gbɛɛ ke doo fa gyooneŋ baŋ baa lee weese dɛɛlee man kɔŋ nɛ.

english_verse: An evil spirit that looked like a frog came out of the mouth of the dragon. One also came out of the mouth of the beast, and another out of the mouth of the false prophet.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te me naa feliŋkumɛɛ atooro ŋaale na ŋe yɛɛ ŋgba penlase nɛ. Ŋa lee wonembukum dekpatakpere ŋon na wonembukum gyaŋgbate ŋon, na wonembukum balate dekarate ŋon nɔɔse man.

english_verse: These evil spirits had the power to work miracles. They went to every king on earth, to bring them together for a war against God All-Powerful. But that will be the day of God's great victory.
translation_verse: Mena feliŋkumɛɛ kɛwɔ yɛɛ ɔbɔnsam tewulɛɛwɔ, te be dana doŋ keŋ bɛɛ tale yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia dinaana. Be dɔŋ tɛɛle kei gyooneŋ pou dɔɔ bee yilaa wɔ, na be kaa yoo na Wurubuarɛ doŋte ŋon ɔ wee dinaa keŋ man.

english_verse: Remember Christ says, “When I come, it will surprise you like a thief! But God will bless you, if you are awake and ready. Then you won't have to walk around naked and be ashamed.”
translation_verse: De Gbeŋgyoo kpa nyiaa, “Ɛ tɔɔse nyi maa kɔŋ kaa futaa ŋon bo ŋgba ŋmɛɛlate nɛ! Mena dɔɔ walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon waa kyaa gyee keŋ ɔ be wolee ɔ wose ya, te waa desina ɔ wose keŋ ɔ be tɛɛ diteŋteli na desɛŋ balaŋ man mena wee kenaŋ ya nɛ, ŋon na nyeebam.”

english_verse: Those armies came together in a place that in Hebrew is called Armagedon.
translation_verse: Keŋte feliŋkumɛɛ ŋan yilaa gyooneŋ baŋ, te be gyuuwɔ kegyaŋee botɔɔ keŋ bɛɛ baake ke Gyudatena dei man nyi Amagedɔn nɛ.

english_verse: As soon as the seventh angel emptied his bowl in the air, a loud voice from the throne in the temple shouted, “It's done!”
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle te Wurubuarɛ kpilale nyetoorote ŋon mɔ kyɛɛkee kpene keŋ doo ɔ nyefale keŋ man nɛ woli feliŋ man. Botɔɔ te me nyii yeesa keŋkeŋ ŋaale lee gyooro gbeli keŋ ke doo Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man nɛ nyiaa, “Nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ te taŋ!”

english_verse: The great city of Babylon split into three parts, and the cities of other nations fell. So God made Babylon drink from the wine cup filled with his anger.
translation_verse: Keŋte donɔɔ dinaa keŋ kpasewɔ akpeŋ atooro, te donɔɔse dinaana ŋan pou ŋe kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ yɛlɛɛwɔ. Te Wurubuarɛ tɔɔse nombiakumɛɛ ŋan Babilɔntena be yɛɛ nɛ, mena dɔɔ ɔ gyeŋa baŋ kum do wɔ nideli ŋgba mena keŋ kaboena nɛ.

english_verse: Every island ran away, and the mountains disappeared.
translation_verse: Te donɔɔse ŋan pou loŋ be kilisi ŋa gyaŋee nɛ, na bulase pou ŋaalaawɔ.

english_verse: One of the seven angels who had emptied the bowls came over and said to me, “Come on! I will show you how God will punish that shameless prostitute who sits on many oceans.
translation_verse: Kenaŋ wɔle te Wurubuarɛ kpilala nyetooro baŋ be dana nyefalɛɛ nyetooro ŋan nɛ, be man ŋolo kɔŋawɔ me gyaŋ kaa yako maŋ nyiaa, “Kɔŋ na maa wola neŋ mena keŋ maa wɔŋ futooli alo ŋon deŋele. Alo ŋonaŋ yɛna donɔɔ dinaa keŋ bɔɔ baŋ ke booneŋ burum nɔɔman nɛ.”

english_verse: Every king on earth has slept with her, and her shameless ways are like wine that has made everyone on earth drunk.”
translation_verse: Tɛɛle kei dɔɔ gyooneŋ ta gyem tisi ɔ dɔɔ ke yɛɛ kafoŋ nombia na e. Ɔ ta mo ɔ kafoŋ nombia wɔlɛɛ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ pou nyee, ŋgba mena keŋ soloŋ ne wɔlɛɛ soloŋ nyɔɔra nyeeya nɛ.

english_verse: With the help of the Spirit, the angel took me into a desert, where I saw a woman sitting on a red beast. The beast was covered with names that were an insult to God, and it had seven heads and ten horns.
translation_verse: Keŋte Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon kaa gyoowɔ me man nideli, te Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon moo maŋ gyu depampaa dinaa daale man. Botɔɔ te me naa alo ŋolo na ɔ kyaa wonembukum yayam ŋolo dɔɔ. Ba te ŋmarase mmusuo yela ŋan ŋɛɛ tia Wurubuarɛ nɛ gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ pou. Wonembukum ŋonaŋ dana nyeeya nyetooro na woo kufu.

english_verse: The woman was dressed in purple and scarlet robes, and she wore jewelry made of gold, precious stones, and pearls. In her hand she held a gold cup filled with the filthy and nasty things she had done.
translation_verse: Na alo ŋonaŋ doona kanyaŋ yayam na agoo abɔɔ. Te ɔ moo kɔba yayam abɔɔ na kegyaŋase kpookpoo kpaakpaa nyiŋan ŋan ŋɛɛ ŋalakee te ŋe dana koyadoŋ nɛ mia desina ɔ wose. Te ɔ dana kɔba yayam kuruwaa keŋ ɔ nombia nkaraŋkaraŋ na ɔ futooli nombia ŋan be wulu ke man nɛ ɔ nyiŋmaa man.

english_verse: On her forehead a mysterious name was written: I AM THE GREAT CITY OF BABYLON, THE MOTHER OF EVERY IMMORAL AND FILTHY THING ON EARTH.
translation_verse: Ba te ŋmarase yele daale keŋ walaŋ na walaŋ ba gyeŋ ka asɛɛ ya nɛ gyakaa ɔ tirinɔɔ dɔɔ nyiaa, “babilɔn donɔɔ dinaa, futooli ala pou be naa, tɛɛle kei nombia nkaraŋkaraŋ pou nyee.”

english_verse: I could tell that the woman was drunk on the blood of God's people who had given their lives for Jesus. This surprising sight amazed me,
translation_verse: Keŋte me naawɔ na, alo ŋon te nyɔɔ Wurubuarɛ balaŋ fatabo baa dɛɛse ŋgba soloŋ te ɔ nyɔɔwɔ nɛ. Balaŋ kɛwɔ yɛna baŋ bɔɔ koe wɔ, keŋ bɔɔ mo be konɔɔ pou sila Yesu nɛ dɔɔ. Ma kena alo ŋonaŋ nɛ, ma wose gbirisi maŋ nideli.

english_verse: and the angel said: Why are you so amazed? I will explain the mystery about this woman and about the beast she is sitting on, with its seven heads and ten horns.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon bɔɔse maŋ nyiaa, “Woŋ dɔɔ te n wose gbirisi neŋ? Yela maa lese alo ŋon na wonembukum ŋon ɔ dana nyeeya nyetooro na woo kufu, te alo ŋon kyaa ɔ dɔɔ nɛ nombia aweeseŋ ŋan man wola neŋ.

english_verse: The beast you saw is one that used to be and no longer is. It will come back from the deep pit, but only to be destroyed. Everyone on earth whose names were not written in the book of life before the time of creation will be amazed. They will see this beast that used to be and no longer is, but will be once more.
translation_verse: Wonembukum ŋon n be naa nɛ nafɔ na ɔ kyaa, mɔna nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ ɔ be kyaa bela ya. Waa lee bɔɔ kyilimkyilim keŋ ke taŋ ɔto ya nɛ man kɔŋ, na ɔ kaa yeŋ leki. Nyi akpaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ balaŋ baŋ pou ba te ŋmarase ba yela do Wurubuarɛ nyeedoŋ tɔne keŋ ke kyaa leenaŋ kewalaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yɛɛ tɛɛle kei ya nɛ de naa wonembukum ŋon na, gyakoloŋ gyae ke kyaŋ wɔ nideli. Nawolo nyi nafɔ na wonembukum kei kyaa, te nɛnɛɛ dɔɔ ɔ be kyaa ya. Mɔna waa bese kaa lese ɔ wose wola bela.

english_verse: Anyone with wisdom can figure this out. The seven heads that the woman is sitting on stand for seven hills. These heads are also seven kings.
translation_verse: Walaŋ ŋon ɔ dana nyansa te ɔ gyeŋ nombia nɛ, ŋon ne gyae la waa tale nyii nombia kɛŋa man nideli. Wonembukum ŋon nyeeya nyetooro ŋan seŋɛɛ fa bulasebia nyetooro, te bulasebia ŋenaŋ dɔɔ te alo ŋon ne kyaa. Bulasebia ŋenaŋ mɔ seŋɛɛ fa gyooneŋ nyetooro.

english_verse: Five of the kings are dead. One is ruling now, and the other one has not yet come. But when he does, he will rule for only a little while.
translation_verse: Gyooneŋ kɛwɔ man banoŋ ta yekee. Loorote ŋon gyakaa ɔ ne di gyoori, te nyetoorote ŋon te kɔŋa ta ya. Akpaa nyetoorote ŋon de kɔŋ nɛ, waa di gyoori keŋ bo debaŋ kyomii na baa gegi e.

english_verse: You also saw a beast that used to be and no longer is. That beast is one of the seven kings who will return as the eighth king, but only to be destroyed.
translation_verse: Wonembukum ŋon ɔ be kyaa kaalaŋ te nɛnɛɛ ɔ be kyaa bela ya nɛ, ŋon yɛna gyoo gyanaarate ŋon te ɔ kpuɛ gyooneŋ nyetooro baŋ man. Te ŋon dɔɔ ɔ ne gyu baa ɔ kaa yeŋ leki.

english_verse: The ten horns you saw are ten more kings, who have not yet come into power, and they will rule with the beast for only a short time.
translation_verse: Woo kufu ŋan n be naa nɛ, ŋan mɔ seŋɛɛ fa gyooneŋ kufu, mɔna be te dii ta gyoori ya. Mɔ baa fa gyooneŋ kufu benaŋ na wonembukum ŋon doŋ, na baa di gyoori debaŋ dokoloŋ bo.

english_verse: These kings will go to war against the Lamb. But he will defeat them, because he is Lord over all lords and King over all kings. His followers are chosen and special and faithful.
translation_verse: Baa yoo na Nambu ŋon, mɔna Nambu ŋon na ɔ balaŋ baŋ ɔ be baake wɔ mo wɔ yɛɛ ɔ wui, te be silana e anokoare man nɛ baa di ɔ dɔɔ. Nawolo nyi ŋon yɛna gbeŋgyoona pou Gbeŋgyoo na gyooneŋ pou Gyoo.”

english_verse: The oceans you saw the prostitute sitting on are crowds of people from all races and languages.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon yako maŋ bela nyiaa, “Booneŋ ŋan n be naa keŋ futooli alo ŋon kyaa ŋe gyaŋ nɛ, seŋɛɛ fa balaŋ kpookpoo baŋ be kyaa tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ.

english_verse: The ten horns and the beast will start hating the shameless woman. They will strip off her clothes and leave her naked. Then they will eat her flesh and throw the rest of her body into a fire.
translation_verse: Te wonembukum ŋon na woo kufu ŋan n be naa nɛ, baa kɔla futooli alo ŋon nombia. Baa wɔlɛɛ e nɛ kyekpakyekpakyekpa, na baa tina e yela diteŋteli. Baa tao e, na baa too akaŋ keŋ.

english_verse: God is the one who made these kings all think alike and decide to give their power to the beast. And they will do this until what God has said comes true.
translation_verse: Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ yele la te gyooneŋ kufu baŋ sɛɛwɔ, te be yɛɛ ŋon Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii. Mena dɔɔ te ba moo be doŋ pou fa wonembukum ŋon nyi waa di gyoori kelii debaŋ keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan gyae ke kɔŋ mena nɛ.

english_verse: The woman you saw is the great city that rules over all kings on earth.
translation_verse: Alo ŋon n be naa nɛ, ŋon yɛna donɔɔ dinaa keŋ ke gyoo ne di doŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ gyooneŋ pou dɔɔ nɛ.”

english_verse: I saw another angel come from heaven. This one had great power, and the earth was bright because of his glory.
translation_verse: Kei wɔle nɛ, te ma besewɔ naa Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋolo te tisi lee adido ɔ ne kɔŋ. Na ɔ dana doŋ te ɔ gyoorobiiri ŋalakeewɔ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ pou.

english_verse: The angel shouted, “Fallen! Powerful Babylon has fallen and is now the home of demons. It is the den of every filthy spirit and of all unclean birds, and every dirty and hated animal.
translation_verse: Keŋte ɔ fae yeesa keŋkeŋ nyiaa, “Babilɔn donɔɔ te yɛlɛɛ! Babilɔn donɔɔ dinaa keŋ te yɛlɛɛ. Botɔɔ ta bese ɔbɔnsam na ɔ tewulɛɛ, na feliŋkumɛɛ na gyebuse kumɛɛ, na gyebuse nkaraŋkaraŋ pou dekyae.

english_verse: Then I heard another voice from heaven shout, “My people, you must escape from Babylon. Don't take part in her sins and share her punishment.
translation_verse: Keŋte me nyii woya ŋaale lee adido bela nyiaa, “Me balaŋ, ɛ koro lee Babilɔn donɔɔ dinaa kenaŋ man. Na ɛmɛɛ mɔ be nyiŋ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛ ŋan doo donɔɔ kenaŋ man, na Wurubuarɛ ke wɔŋ ɛ deŋele ŋgba mena keŋ waa wɔŋ mena balaŋ benaŋ deŋele nɛ ya.

english_verse: Her sins are piled as high as heaven. God has remembered the evil she has done.
translation_verse: Wurubuarɛ te tɔɔse ɔ nombiakumɛɛ ŋan, nawolo nyi ŋe ta boo nideli keŋ nyi ɔ be tale wolee ŋa ya.

english_verse: Treat her as she has treated others. Make her pay double for what she has done. Make her drink twice as much of what she mixed for others.
translation_verse: Nombiakumɛɛ ŋan ɔ ba mo yɛɛ balaŋ nɛ, ɛ mo ŋe dinɔɔ akpeŋ ala tɔ e kom. Te soloŋ keŋ ɔ be fa balaŋ be nyɔɔ nɛ, ɛ mo keŋ ke dana doŋ akpeŋ ala kela ɔ wui keŋ nɛ fa e waa nyɔɔ.

english_verse: That woman honored herself with a life of luxury. Reward her now with suffering and pain. “Deep in her heart Babylon said, ‘I am the queen! Never will I be a widow or know what it means to be sad.’
translation_verse: Mena keŋ ɔ be di gyoŋ te ɔ ba lese ɔ gyoorobiiri wola nɛ, mena mɔ te ɛ naase e diyem na ɛ ke yela waa yeyɛɛ nideli. Nawolo nyi ɔ gyakaa ɔ ne gyɔ ɔ konɔɔ yako nyi, ‘Me yɛɛ ala gyoo e, ma te yɛɛ lialo e ya. Te mɔɔ gyae maa di walaŋsa mɔ korakora ya.’

english_verse: And so, in a single day she will suffer the pain of sorrow, hunger, and death. Fire will destroy her dead body, because her judge is the powerful Lord God.”
translation_verse: Lee mena dɔɔ weese dokoloŋ kooŋ man te kayeyɛɛ dinaa keŋ gyae kekɔŋ ɔ dɔɔ. Kayeyɛɛ kenaŋ yɛna yeŋ, na walaŋsa kedi na tanam dinaa. Kenaŋ wɔle koraŋ na, baa too e kekpaa. Nawolo nyi de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ ŋon waa wɔŋ ɔ deŋele nɛ, ɔ dana doŋ.”

english_verse: Every king on earth who slept with her and shared in her luxury will mourn. They will weep, when they see the smoke from that fire.
translation_verse: Tɛɛle kei dɔɔ gyooneŋ baŋ bɔɔ gyem tisi ɔ dɔɔ yɛɛ kafoŋ nombia na e, te bɔɔ di gyoŋ kamasɛ kpu na e nɛ, akpaa be naa boalaŋ keŋ ne kpa e nɛ nyɔɔse na, baa wii na baa di ɔ walaŋsa nideli.

english_verse: Her sufferings will frighten them, and they will stand at a distance and say, “Pity that great and powerful city! Pity Babylon! In a single hour her judgment has come.”
translation_verse: Diyem keŋ gyae kekɔŋ Babilɔn donɔɔ keŋ dɔɔ nɛ, gyae ke yɛɛ gyooneŋ baŋ gyakoloŋ. Mena dɔɔ baa seŋ lemlem wii na baa yako nyiaa, “Babilɔn donɔɔ laako! Babilɔn donɔɔ dinaa, nyaŋ na gyerebi! Nyaŋ ŋon nafɔ n dana doŋ nɛ, na debaŋ dokoloŋ kooŋ kei yaa be taŋ wɔlɛɛ neŋ nɛ?”

english_verse: Every merchant on earth will mourn, because there is no one to buy their goods.
translation_verse: Tɛɛle kei dɔɔ kebudiira baŋ mɔ baa wii na baa di walaŋsa ɔ dɔɔ. Nawolo nyi ŋolo bɛɛ tekaa lɛɛ ba abɔɔ bela ya.

english_verse: No one will buy their cinnamon, spices, incense, myrrh, frankincense, wine, olive oil, fine flour, wheat, cattle, sheep, horses, chariots, slaves, and other humans.
translation_verse: Ŋolo bɛɛ tekaa lɛɛ ba abɔɔ buruburu ŋan ŋe dana ofoŋ te bɛɛ mo saa weenɛɛ, na adudo na oliifi na pɛperɛ na soloŋ na nuŋ na biliŋ na nunkyuese na namense na dɛɛkyɔŋse na ŋe kɔtase, na gbeŋa na baŋ bɔɔ kyaŋ wɔ yoo man nɛ ya.

english_verse: Babylon, the things your heart desired have all escaped from you. Every luxury and all your glory will be lost forever. You will never get them back.
translation_verse: Kebudiira baŋ baa yako nyiaa, “Abɔɔ kpaakpaa ŋan pou n be yase n sia gyae nɛ, ŋe popou ta lee n nyiŋmaa man. Mena keŋ n be nyiŋ n wose, na yele keŋ n be lɛɛ nɛ pou ta yoo kekpaakekpaa.”

english_verse: The merchants had become rich because of her. But when they saw her sufferings, they were terrified. They stood at a distance, crying and mourning.
translation_verse: Nombia ŋan gyae ke kɔŋ Babilɔn donɔɔ keŋ dɔɔ nɛ, gyae ke yɛɛ kebudiira baŋ bɔɔ di kebu donɔɔ kenaŋ man nyiŋ ba wose nɛ gyakoloŋ. Mena dɔɔ baa seŋ lemlem wii, na baa di walaŋsa.

english_verse: Then they shouted, “Pity the great city of Babylon! She dressed in fine linen and wore purple and scarlet cloth. She had jewelry made of gold and precious stones and pearls.
translation_verse: Baa wii yako nyiaa, “O Babilɔn donɔɔ dinaa laako! O Babilɔn donɔɔ dinaa, nyaŋ na gyerebi. Nafɔ n ne kaa akaralɛɛ na abɔɔ yayama na agoose bo. Nafɔ kɔba yayam abɔɔ, na boya kpaakpaa nyiŋan abɔɔ, na kegyaŋase kpaakpaa nyiŋan ŋan ne ŋalakee nɛ abɔɔ te n na doo.

english_verse: Yet in a single hour her riches disappeared.” Every ship captain and passenger and sailor stood at a distance, together with everyone who does business by traveling on the sea.
translation_verse: Mɔna debaŋ dokoloŋ kei man na, nya abɔɔ ŋan pou te ŋaalaa.” Kegyiise baŋ bɛɛ kɛɛ agbele dinaana dɔɔ, na baŋ pou bɛɛ kyaa ŋe man, na baŋ pou bɛɛ yɛɛ tom ŋe man, na kebudiira baŋ pou bɛɛ tɛɛ ɛpo dɔɔ di kebu nɛ, baa seŋ lemlem na bɛɛ kɛɛ donɔɔ kenaŋ.

english_verse: When they saw the smoke from her fire, they shouted, “This was the greatest city ever!”
translation_verse: Baa seŋ botɔɔ na baa kɛɛ donɔɔ keŋ kekpabii. Be naa ke kɛɛ kpaa keŋ nɛ baa yako nyiaa, “Donɔɔ woŋti kyaa kaalaŋ, keŋ kelii mena donɔɔ dinaa kei.”

english_verse: They cried loudly, and in their sorrow they threw dust on their heads, as they said, “Pity the great city of Babylon! Everyone who sailed the seas became rich from her treasures. But in a single hour the city was destroyed.
translation_verse: Baa kyaa ŋgba liala nɛ na baa wii waraŋase man nyiaa, “O Babilɔn donɔɔ dinaa laako! O Babilɔn donɔɔ dinaa, nyaŋ na gyerebi! Nyaŋ dɔɔ te kebudiira baŋ pou bɔɔ mo agbele dinaana ta ɛpo dɔɔ di kebu nɛ, nyiŋa ba wose. Mɔna debaŋ dokoloŋ kooŋ man na, nya abɔɔ ŋan n be nyim nɛ pou te ŋaalaa.

english_verse: A powerful angel then picked up a huge stone and threw it into the sea. The angel said, “This is how the great city of Babylon will be thrown down, never to rise again.
translation_verse: Botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ kpilale Dekpatakpere ŋolo moo boe dinaa dinaa daale, te ɔ fuŋa ke ka do ɛpo man te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Nnɛ te baa mo doŋ wɔlɛɛ Babilɔn donɔɔ dinaa keŋ, te ŋolo na ŋolo be gyae waa tekaa naa ke bela ya.

english_verse: The music of harps and singers and of flutes and trumpets will no longer be heard. No workers will ever set up shop in that city, and the sound of grinding grain will be silenced forever.
translation_verse: Ŋolo mɔ be gyae waa tekaa nyii adolɛɛ gyɔɔra na nɔɔneŋ kpeera yaa keferee na kaberɛ wolala nkaŋ donɔɔ dinaa kei man bela ya. Ŋolo be gyae waa tekaa naa tom kamasɛ yɛɛre botɔɔ bela ya. Mena mɔ te bɔɔ gyae baa tekaa nyii nyi ŋolo ne naŋ kolo boe dɔɔ bela ya.

english_verse: Lamps will no longer shine anywhere in Babylon, and couples will never again say wedding vows there. Her merchants ruled the earth, and by her witchcraft she fooled all nations.
